raw_text: ALLEN, ARTHUR AUGUSTUS.—B.1890; ed. Christ Hospital; examr., Exchequer and Audit dept., Nov., 1911; asst. audr., Nigeria, June, 1914; seconded, rly. constrn., Oct., 1922; July, 1929; senr. asst. audr., Nigeria, Nov., 1930.

ALLEN, BERTRAM WEDGWOOD.—B. 1888; ed. Repton and King's Coll., Lond.; pol. probationer, S.S., Nov., 1909; asst. supt. pol., Aug., 1914; dep. comsrr., pol., Muar, Johore, June, 1918; asst. supt., pol., Penang, June, 1920; asst. comsrr., pol., Kedah, Jan., 1923; supt., pol., S.S., July, 1928; ch. pol. offr., N. Sembilan, Oct., 1929; comdt., pol., depot, Singapore, Dec., 1930; ch. pol. offr., Penang, 1932.
ALLEN, CONWAY PHILLIPS, M.B., Ch.B. (Glaz.), D.T.M. & H. (Liverpool)—B. 1901; anaesthetist, F.M.S., July, 1927; med. offr. in ch. offrs., in addn., Aug., 1932; surg., J. Bahru, Nov., 1936.
ALLEN, EMILY MAY.—Ed. St. Etheldreda Coll., Norwich; temp. mistress, kindergarten schl., Tamale, Gold Coast, 1927; mistress, 1928; inspr., schls., 1933.

ALLEN, FREDERICK HAROLD, M.I.Mech.E., M.Inst. C.E.—B. 1890; engnr., asst., Port of London Authority, 1910-19; dist. engnr., Br. Guiana, 1919; dist. engnr., Iorin, Nigeria, 1923; exec. engnr., 1924; native adminstr. engnr., 1925; mech. engnr., Br. Guiana, 1926; exec. engnr., P.W.D., Jan., 1930; ag. D.P.W. and M.L.C., May to Nov., 1934; i.c. pub. wks., accorded title dep. D.P.W., Dec., 1936.

ALLEN, GEOFFREY CARL.—B. 1896; asst. clk., G.P.O., Lond., Jan., 1914; war serv., 1914-1919; asst. survr., G.P.O., U.K., Dec., 1919; supt., P. & T., S.S., Jan., 1925; perm. asst. to sec. for postal affrs., Malaya, Feb., 1930; ag. contr., Penang & P.W., Nov., 1936.

ALLEN, GEORGE VANCE, M.D., M.B., B.Ch., B.A.O. (Hons.), D.T.M. and H.—B. 1894; asst. bacteriologist, Kenya, Feb., 1921; senr. bacteriologist, Sept., 1925; bacteriologist, F.M.S., Nov., 1927; ag. prin., med. coll., Singapore, June, 1929; prin., Mar., 1930; pres., Raffles Coll. in addn., Apr., 1932; mem. dental bd. S.S., Sept., 1933; mem., pharmacy bd., 1934; offl. del., 9th cong. Far Eastern Assoc. of Trop. Med., Nanking, Sept., 1934.

ALLEN, H.—Cashier and acct., E.A.P., July, 1914; on active serv., E. Africa, 1914-16 and 1917-18; tempy. asst. dist. comsrr., E.A.P., Apr., 1919; asst. treas., Uganda, Aug., 1920; asst. treas., Zanzibar, Dec., 1930; ag. treas., in 1932, 1933 and 1934; dep. treas., Uganda, 1935; dep. currency offr., 1936; acct.-gen., 1937.

ALLEN, HON. SIR JAMES, G.C.M.G. (1926), K.C.B. (1917), M.A., Camb.—B. 1855; entd. New Zealand Parlmt., 1887; lieut.-col. in command Otago Div., New Zealand Garrison Art., 1902; min. of defence, 1912; min. of finance, min. of stamp duties and min. of educn., 1912-15; min. of finance and min. of external affairs, 1919-20; high comsrr. in England, 1920-26; M.L.C., 1927.
ALLEN, LEONARD GILBERT.—B. 1901; entered C.O., 1917; apptd., after exam., asst. clk., 23rd Apr., 1919; cler. offr., 1st Jan., 1920; higher grade cler. offr., 17th Sept., 1929.

ALLEN, LUCIEN ARTHUR, O.B.E. (1931).—B. 1888; ed. Merchant Taylors' Schl. and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; B.A., 1910; cadet, F.M.S., Dec., 1912; passed cadet, June, 1915; asst. dist. offr., Kuantan, Sept., 1918 to Aug., 1919; asst. dist. offr., Lower Perak, June, 1920; Brit. rs., Brunei, Mar., 1921; asst. dist. offr., Ipoh, Jan., 1924; dep. pub. pros., N. Sembilan and Pahang, Dec., 1924; offr., cls. II, Dec., 1929; ag. dist. offr., Klang, Nov., 1931; offr., cls. I B, 1932; do., Kinta, Mar., 1933; comsrr., lands and mines, Johore, Mar., 1934; ag. gen. advr., Johore, 1937.
ALLEN, PERCY DE VERE.—B. 1891; ed. Melbourne, Australia; on mily. serv., E. Africa, Egypt and France, 1914-19; lab. inspr., Kenya, Apr., 1925; lab. offr., Jan., 1929; prin. lab. offr., 1936.

ALLEN, COL. SIR STEPHEN SHEPHERD, K.B.E. (1933), C.M.G. (1919), D.S.O. (1917), with bar (1918); Volunteer serv., Cambridge Univ., 1901-04 and in N.Z., 1911-15; left N.Z. with 6th reinforcements N.Z. Exped. Force, with rank of major, 1915; lieut. col., Jan., 1917; in commd., 2nd batt., Auckland Regt., Dec., 1916 to June, 1919; comdr., Auckland Infy. Brig. (T.F.), Feb., 1920 to May, 1925; col., Feb., 1920; in commd., Hauraki Regt., Sept., 1926 to Apr., 1928; R. of O., Apr., 1928; admtr., W. Samoa, 1928-31; chmn., transport co-ordination bd., 1934-36; four times ment. in desps.; hon. A.D.C. to gov.-genl., N.Z., Jan., 1925 to May, 1928.
ALLEN, WILLIAM RUBIN, B.A.—B. 1895; war serv., 1914-18; ret. with rank of capt.; inspr., schls., Gold Coast, Jan., 1929.

ALLEYNE, JAMES DOUGLAS, M.B.E. (1935), M.D. (Tuft's Univ., U.S.A.)—ag. pol. med. offr., dist. A., Barbados and visiting physician, leper asylum, 1917; asst. port health offr., 1919; ag. supt., lunatic asylum, 1919; port health offr., 1921; ex officio mem., quarantine board.

ALLIES, E. J.—B. 1891; apptd. asst. clk., Scottish Educn. Dept., 1910; apptd. after compet. exam. clk. of the 2nd divn. of the civ. serv. to National Insurance Audit Dept., 1913; on mily. serv., 1917-19; assigned to the C.O., Aug., 1919; cler. offr., higher grade, 1st Apr., 1922; staff offr., 3rd Mar., 1937.

ALLISON, HERBERT BRUCE.—B. 1893; ed. Neison Schl., Paisley and R. Tech. Coll., Glas.; asst. engnr., F.M.S.rlys., Aug., 1919; dist. engnr., grade II, Aug., 1924; do., grade I, Aug., 1928; ag. senr. dist. engnr., K. Lumpur, Feb., 1936.
ALLISON, J. S., M.A., LL.B.—B. 1879; ed. Glenalmond and Edinburgh Univ.; M.A. (Edin.); LL.B. (Cape); Capelaw dep., 1903; Union dep. of natire affrs., 1918; under sec. for natire affrs., 1929.
ALLMAN, GEORGE FRANCIS.—B. 1878; under sec. and perm. head, lands dept., N.S.W., Aug., 1934.

ALLSOP, HERBERT HENRY.—B. 1885; German East Africa campaign, 1914-16; asst. polit. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Nov., 1916; 1st grade admstive offr., Aug., 1922; ag. dep. prov. comsrr. (W. Prov.), Oct., 1934—Dec., 1935.

ALTSON, RALPH ABBY, B.Sc., A.R.C.S.—B. 1894; Bedford Sch. and Royal Coll. of Sci., London; asst. botanist and mycologist, dept. of agr., B. Guiana, Feb., 1923; botanist and mycologist, Jan., 1928; asst. mycologist, agrl. dept., F.M.S., Apr., 1928; del. to 2nd Internat. Congr. for Microbiology, Lond., July, 1936; ag. mycologist, July, 1937; title changed, plant pathologist, Jan., 1938.

ALUWIHAIRE, RICHARD.—B. 1895; on mily. serv., 1915; cadet, Ceylon, Oct., 1920; office astt. to dir., agr., Feb., 1922; ag. pol. mag., Bandawamuwa, Oct., 1923; pol. mag., Pt. Pedro, Nov., 1923; ditto, Panadure Nov., 1924; 2nd landing survr., cust., Dec., 1926; comanr., requests, Kandy, and addl. dist. judge, Kegalla, Nov., 1928; asst. atttmt. offr., Oct., 1929; dist. judge, Nuwara Eliya, Oct., 1931; contr., finance and supply, Mar., 1934; asst. govt. agt., Kegalla, Oct., 1937.

ALVAPILLAI, KOVINADAPILLAI, B.A. (Lond.)—B. 1905; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., 1929; attd., Anuradhapura kach., Jan., 1929; attd., Matara kach., Sept., 1929; attd. office asst., Puttalam kach., Oct., 1930; office asst., Galle kach., Mar., 1934; comanr., requests and pol. mag., Jaffna, Mar., 1935; asst. govt. agt., Badulla, Apr., 1936; do., Kandy, Mar., 1937; sec. to min. for educn., June, 1937.

AMBLEDER, CAPT. GOODMAN, M.C., V.D., F.R.G.S.—B. 1885; ed. Steyning Grammar Schil., Brighton Grammar Schil. and St. Mark's Coll., Cheltenham; asst. mast., Victoria Inst., K. Lumpur, F.M.S., Jan., 1910; war serv., France and Gallipoli, 1914-19; rejoined Victoria Inst., 1919; sec., Selangor cadets with rank of capt., Mar., 1924; Col. Aux. Forces Long Serv. Med., Mar., 1924; headmast., Outram schil., S'pore, Apr., 1926; lieut., S.V.C., May, 1926; capt., Aug., 1930; transfd. to S.S.V.F., R. of O.; senr. normal instr., S'pore, Dec., 1931; headmast., Outram schil., Nov., 1932.

AMBLEDER, LLOYD EDWARD.—B. 1894; clk., postal dept., Fiji, June, 1909; 4th cls. clk., lands dept., Aug., 1913; served with 1st Batt., K.R.R.C., France, 1916-17; Indian Army Res. (command., Oct., 1917); Baluchistan, 1917-19; 3rd cls. clk., col. sec.'s office, Nov., 1916; attd., lands dept., Fiji, Sept., 1919; ag. 2nd cls. clk. and sec., mining bd., Oct., 1919-Oct., 1920; ag. sub-inspr. constab. (during Indian strike), Feb., 1920; ag. 1st cls. clk. and clk., exec. and leg. couns., Jan. to Apr., 1921; regy. clk., Western Pacific high comm., May, 1921; ag. ch. clk., Sept., 1922 to Feb., 1923; 1st cls. clk., Jan., 1927; ag. ch. clk. and acctnt., on various occasions, 1923-31; ag. sec., Western Pacific high comm., June to Aug., 1931; ch. clk. and acct., Jan., 1932.
AMERASINGHE, HAMILTON SHIRLEY, B.A. (hons.) Lond.—B. 1913; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Jan., 1937.

AMERASINGHE, SAMSON FELIX, B.A., Cantab.—B. 1902; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., July, 1926; attd., Kalutara kach., Aug., 1926; office asst. govt. agt., E. Prov., Dec., 1927; pol. mag., Dandagamuwawa, Sept., 1928; ditto, Negombo, Dec., 1928; office asst. to govt. agt., Uva, June, 1930; office asst. to govt. agt., N.W. Prov., June, 1932; pol. mag., Kandy, Nov., 1932; addnl. asst. govt agt., Colombo, Oct., 1935.

AMERY, Lieut.-Col., L.C.M.S.—B. 1873; ed. at Harrow and Balliol Coll., Oxford; late Fellow of All Souls, Oxford; Ouseley Scholar of Imp. Inst. in Turkish; called to the bar, Inner Temple, 1902; on editorial staff of The Times, 1899-1909; served during the war in Flanders, Salonika, and the Near East; on the staff of the Impl. War Coun. at Versailles, and as ass. sec. to the War Cabinet and Impl. Cabinet to Jan., 1919; under-sec. of state for the colonies, 14th Jan., 1919; acted as sec. of state during Lord Milner's absence in Egypt, Nov., 1919 to Mar., 1920; First Lord of the Admvr., Oct., 1922; S. of S. for the Colonies, 7th Nov., 1924; also S. of S. for Dominion affrs., 9th July, 1925 to June, 1929; capt. 14th Isth B'harn Batt., Royal Warwickshire Regt., 1914; lieut.-col. on general staff, 1917; M.P. (U) for Birmingham, May, 1911; ditto Sparkbrook div., Dec., 1918; edited "The Times History of the South African War"; author of "Problem of the Army," 1903; "Fundamental Fallacies of Free Trade," 1906; "The Great Question" (with J. M. Robertson, M.P.), 1909; "Union and Strength," 1912.

AMERY, WILLIAM BANKES, C.B.E. (1920).—B. 1883; ed. Christ's Hosp.; ent. cust. and excise dept., 1901; asst. sec. (temp.) war trade dept., 1916; estab. offr., ministry of transport, 1919; finance offr. (with rank of prin.) overseas settlement dept., D.O., 1922; rep. of the U.K. govt. in the Commonwealth of Australia under the Empire Settlement Act, 1925; prin., O.S.D., 1929; temp. attd., D.O., 1932.

AMES, CECIL GERAIT.—B. 1897; ed. Elstree Prep. Schil. and Dover Coll.; (school); 5th Somerset Light Inf., 1915-19; Law Soc.'s final exam. (hons.), 1921; Nigerian adminstr. serv., 1922; ag. ch. registr., sup. ct., Nov., 1932-July, 1933; admitted, solr., Nov., 1933; attd. judi. dept. Jan.-Mar., 1934; asst. judge, high ct., Apr., 1934.
AMHERST, HON. HUMPHREY WILLIAM.—B. 1903; ed. Charterhouse and R.M.C., Sandhurst; regular army, 43rd Light Inf., 1924-26; cadet, Gold Coast, 1929; asst. dist. commr., 1930.

ANDERSON, CHARLES BUXTON, I.S.O., M.I.C.E., M.I.E. (Aust.).—B. 1879; joined engnr. dept., pub. serv., S. Australia, 1898; successively draughtsman, survr., res. engr., rlys., supt., rlys., ag. ch. engr., gen., supt., rlys.; S. Aust. rlys. commr., 1930.
ANDERSON, LIKUT.-COL. E. L. B., O.B.E. (1936). D.S.O.—Asst. dist. comanr., E.A.P., Oct., 1919; pvt. sec. to ag. gov., 1925; dist. offr., 1931.

ANDERSON, JAMES HENRY (Lieut. R.A.K.O. Seaforth Hrs.).—B. 1895; 2nd cls. clk., treas., Nyasaland, 1920; transfd. to secretariat, 1922; senr. clk., 1924; ag. ch. clk., secretariat and edr., Govt. Gazette, 1927; asst. treas., Gold Coast, Aug., 1928; ag. dep. treas., Mar.-July, 1935 and May-July, 1937.
ANDERSON, STANLEY LLEWELLYN.—B. 1885; under sec., ch. secr's. dept. and dept. of social services, N.S.W., 1930.

ANDERSON, WILLIAM JOHN.—B. 1895; ed. Kilmarock Acad. and Tech. Coll.; active serv. (France) 1914-19; dep. storekeeper, Hongkong, Feb., 1925; ag. supt., accts. and stores, P.W.D., 1926; supt., accts. and stores, Jan., 1928; contr., stores, 1928.

ANDREWES, LANCASTER RUGGLES.—Ed. Fort Street, Sydney; war serv., 1915-18; admitted solr., sup. ct., London, 1924; asst. crown solr., Hong Kong, 1928; J.P., 1931; ag. dep. regar. in 1932-35; dep. regar., sup. ct., 1935; ag. regar., sup. ct., offl. admistr., offl. trustee and regar., coys. in 1937; offl. recvr. and regar., trade mark and patents, Oct., 1937.

ANDREWS, HARRY THOMSON.—B. 1897; ed. Pretoria Univ.; P.M.G.'s office, 1913; on mily. serv., 1917-19; called to bar, Transvaal Prov. divn.; advoc., 1927; pvt. secy., min. of lab. and defence, 1926-30; professional asst., dept. of just., 1930; polit. secy., S. Africa House, London, 1930-35; mem., Imp. shipping comitc., I.C.C.—Inter-Colonial Council. Imigrts.—Immigrants. Imigrn.—Immigration. Impl.—Imperial. Ind.—India. Inf.—Inferior. In rev.—Inland revenue. Inspr.—Inspector. Institn.—Institution. Instr.—Instruction. Internat.—International. Interp.—Interpreter. I.S.O.—Imperial Service Order. I.Y.—Imperial Yeomanry. J.M.F.—Johore Military Forces. J.V.R.—Johore Volunteer Rifles. K.B.E.—Knight Commander of the Order of the British Empire. K.C.B.—Knight Commander of the Order of the Bath. K.C.I.E.—Knight Commander of the Order of the Indian Empire. K.C.M.G.—Knight Commander of the Order of St. Michael and St. George. K.C.S.I.—Knight Commander of the Star of India. K.C.V.O.—Knight Commander of the Victorian Order. K.G.—Knight of the Most Noble Order of the Garter Knt., Knt. Bach., or K.B.—Knight Bachelor. K.U.R. & H.—Kenya and Uganda Railways and Harbours. L. of N.—League of Nations Legis. Assem.—Legislative Assembly. Librn.—Librarian. Mag.—Magistrate. Man.—Manager. M.B.E.—Member of the Order of the British Empire. M.C.S.—Malay Civil Service. M.E.C.—Member of the Executive Council. M.E.O.—Malayan Establishment Office. M.F.C.—Member of the Federal Council, Federated Malay States. M.H.A.—Member of the House of Assembly. Mil. Acad.—Military Academy. M.I.M.E.—Member of the Institute of Mining Engineers. Min.—Minister. M.L.A.—Member of the Legislative Assembly. M.L.C.—Member of the Legislative Council. M.S.G.—Malay States Guides. M.V.I.—Malay Volunteer Infantry. M.V.O.—Member of the Victorian Order. Mun. Coun.—Municipal Council. N.W.M.P.—North West Mounted Police. O.A.G.—Officer Administering the Government. O.B.E.—Officer of the Order of the British Empire. Offl.—Official. Offr.—Officer. O.F.S.—Orange Free State. O.R.C.—Orange River Colony. Organizn.—Organization. Parlmt.—Parliament. Parly.—Parliamentary. P.C.—Privy Council. P.E.I.—Prince Edward Island. Pett. Sess.—Petty Sessions. P.M.G.—Postmaster-General. P.M.O.—Principal Medical Officer. Pol. Mag.—Police Magistrate. Postmr.—Postmaster. Pres.—President. Prof.—Professor. Prot.—Protector. Pub. Wks.—Public Works. P.W.—Province of Wellesley. P.W.D.—Public Works Department. Qrtmnr.—Quartermaster. Rec.-Gen.—Receiver-General. Registr.—Registrar. Rea.—Resident. Resig.—Resigned. Resign.—Resignation. Ret.—Retired. Retg.—Retiring. R.I.C.—Royal Irish Constabulary. Rlys.—Railways. R.M.—Resident Magistrate. R.M.A.—Royal Marine Artillery. R.N.R.—Royal Naval Reserve. Sask.—Saskatchewan. Schl.—School. Sergt.—Sergeant. Sess.—Session. S.J.P.—Stipendiary Justice of the Peace. S. Leone—Sierra Leone. Solr.—Solicitor. S. of S.—Secretary of State. S.S.—Straits Settlements. Statn.—Station. Stip.—Stipendiary. St. M. and St. G.—St. Michael and St. George. Sup. Ct.—Supreme Court. Super. Ct.—Superior Court. Supt.—Superintendent. Surg.—Surgeon. Survr.—Surveyor. Tem.—Temple. Transfd.—Transferred. Treas.—Treasurer. Treasy.—Treasury. U.K.—United Kingdom. U.M.S.—Unfederated Malay States. U.S.A.—United States of America. V.-A.Ct.—Vice-Admiralty Court. W.A.M.S.—West African Medical Staff. Wt. Offr.—Warrant Officer.

ARCHER, NORMAN E.—B. 1892; ed. R.N. Colls., Osborne and Dartmouth; midshipman in Mediterranean and Home Fleets, 1909-12; sub-lieut. (six 1st clas. certifs.), Sept., 1912; lieut., June, 1913; H.M.S. Warrior in Mediterranean and Grand Fleets, 1912-15; qualified, specialist course, as torpedo lieut., 1915; served with Russian Navy in Black Sea, &c., 1916-17 (Order of St. Stanislas, 3rd clas.); passed exam. as Russian interpreter, 1918; intell. divn. admly., H.M.S. Lowestoft in Adriatic, minelaying squadron, Grand Fleet, and H.M.S. Malaya, 1917-20; apptd. under reconstruction scheme asst. prin., min. of lab., Sept., 1920; transf. to C.O., Dec., 1921; pvt. sec. to perm. under sec. for Dom. affrs., Sept., 1927; seconded as sec. to Br. economic mission to Australia and visited Australia, N.Z. and Canada, 1928-29; pvt. sec. to partly under sec. for Dom. affrs., June, 1929; prin., D.O., 4th Sept., 1929; seconded as sec., office of high comnr. in Canada for H.M.G. in U.K., 16th Apr., 1932; ag. high comnr., Sept., 1934 to Jan., 1935.
ARCHER, W. S.—B. 1907; ed. the Lodge Schi., Barbados, B.W.I.; chief clk., admr's office, D'ca., 1931; prinl., C.S.O., Antigua and clk to exec. and leg. couns., Leeward Is., 1932.

ARCHIBALD, EDGAR S., B.A., B.S.A.—B. 1889; recd. pymy. educn. at Yarmouth pub. schl. and Yarmouth acad.; grad. Acadia Univ., 1903; also grad. from the Nova Scotia agric. coll. in 1906; recd. deg. of Bach. of Scien. Agric. from the Ontario agric. coll., 1908; inst. of agric. and expmtlst. at the Nova Scotia agric. coll., 1908; prof. of agric. and farm supt. of the same coll. in 1910; endd. the serv. of the Fed. Dept. of Agric. in June, 1912, as Dominion animal husbandman at the Cent. Expmtl. Farm, Ottawa; ag. dir. of Expmtl. Farms, 1918-1919; dir., June, 1919.
ARCHIBALD, SIR ROBERT, KT., C.M.G., D.S.O., M.D.—Med. supt., leper attlt., Chacachacare, Trinidad, Mar., 1937.
ARDON, HAROLD GRAHAM.—B. 1892; mily. serv., 1916-21; educn. dept., Gold Coast, 1922; transf. polit. serv. as asst. dist. comnr., 1925; asst. col. sec., 1930; dist. comnr., 1932.

ARGYLE, STANLEY SEYMOUR, K.B.E. (1930), M.B., B.S., M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.)—B. 1867; ed. Trinity Coll., Melbourne Univ.; lieut.-col., A.A.M.C., A.I.F. in European war; M.L.A., Victoria since 1920; ch. sec. in four ministries, 1923-29; premier and treas., Victoria, May, 1932-Apr., 1935; leader of opposition since Apr., 1935.

ARMSTRONG, ANDREW CLARENCE FRANCIS.—B. 1907; ed. St. Edmunds Schi., Ware and Christ's Coll., Cambridge, B.A.; cadet, Gilbert and Ellice Is., 1929; dep. comnr., 1931; admstv. offr., 1932; ag. asst. sec., W. Pac. high comnr. (on secondment), 1938.

ARMSTRONG, ARTHUR LEOPOLD.—B. 1888; cadet, Fiji, Apr., 1915; asst. sec. for native affrs., Jan., 1920; resigned, Oct., 1920; dist. comnr. and prov. comnr., Dec., 1921; under sec. for native affrs., Oct., 1922; asst. sec., do., Jan., 1927; ag. sec., do., on various occasions, 1927-34; ag. col. sec. (conjoint), June-Aug., 1930; nom. mem., leg. coun. and mem. exec. coun. for various periods, 1929-32; 1st asst. col. sec., Mar., 1932; ag. col. sec., May-June, 1933 and in 1935; gov't. dep., June, 1933 and Sept., 1935; ag. sec., native affrs. in 1934; prin. asst. col. sec., 1936; offl. M.L.C., 1937; agt. and consul, Tonga, 1937.

ARMSTRONG, GEORGE JAMES, M.B.E. (1938).—B. 1901; ed. St. Andrew's Coll., Grahamstown; clk., Basutoland, grade II., 1920; grade I., 1927; sub-inspr., Basutoland Mounted Pol., 1929; asst. dist. comnr., 1936; seconded to secrt., 1936 and 1937; mast., res. comnr's. ct. in addn., 1937; dist. comnr., 1938.
ARMSTRONG, HON. HUBERT THOMAS.—B. 1878; ed. Public Schi., Bulls, N.Z.; memb., N.Z. H. of R. since 1922; min. of lab. and min. of immigrn., 1935; rep. N.Z., Internat. lab. conf., Geneva, 1937.
ARMSTRONG, CAPTAIN JOHN ANDREW.—B. 1892; asst. dist. comnr., Gold Coast, 1924; dist. comnr., 1932.
ARMSTRONG, ROBERT.—B. 1906; clk. Swaziland, 1925; asst. dist. comnr., 1931; dist. comnr., 1936.

ARDNT, CARL EVAN.—B. 1896; on mily. serv., Jan., 1916; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Oct., 1920; attd. Anuradhapura kach., Oct., 1920; ag. pol. mag., Negombo, Aug., 1922; extra office asst., Kandy kachcheri, Nov., 1923; off. asst., Puttalam kach., Jan., 1925; dep. rubber contr., Apr., 1926; asst. gvt. agt., Kurunegala, Sept., 1928; asst. gvt. agt., Mannar, Nov., 1929; asst. sttlm. offr., May, 1930; dep. fiscal, Colombo, Feb., 1931 and July, 1932; admstv. sec., income tax dept., Feb., 1932; asst. gvt. agt., Colombo, Dec., 1933; do., Kegalla, Apr., 1937; registr., motor cars, 1938.

ARNOLD, 1ST BARON (creat. 1924), RT. HON. SYDNEY ARNOLD.—B. 1878; was priv. sec. to the pres. of the bd. of educn. and to the finan. sec. to the treasy., 1914; lib. M.P. for Holmfirth and later for Penistone, 1912-21; partly under sec. for the cols., Jan., 1924 to 7th Nov., 1924.

ARNOLD, LESLIE WILLIAM, B.A., B.Sc. (Econ.) (Lond.).—B. 1891; Free Schi., Penang, June, 1914; 2nd lieut., R.G.A., Nov., 1915; headmast., govt., eng. schi., Penang, Jan., 1927; do., Hutchings schi., Penang, Jan., 1928; do., high schi., Malacca, Dec., 1930; senr. eduon. offr., grade A, 1934; headmast., Free Schi., Penang, Aug., 1934.

ARNOLD, WILLIAM ERIC.—B. 1882; ed. Napier public schi. and Napier high schi., N.Z.; entd. N.Z. govt. life insurance dept., 1901; dist. man., 1920; senr. clk., actuarial br., 1925; office examt., 1927; acctnt., 1929; sec. and dep. comnr., 1932; comnr., 1933.

ARTHUR, OSWALD RAYNER, B.A. (Cantab.).—B. 1905; schol. of Charterhouse Schi. and Corpus Christi Coll., Cambridge; cadet, Nigeris, 1928; asst. dist. offr., 1931; admstv. offr. (asst. comnr.), Cyprus, July, 1937; comnr., Dec., 1937.

ARUMUGUM, JOSEPH NALLIAH, B.Sc.(Lond.).—B. 1896; jnr. demstr., govt. tech. schi., Ceylon, Sept., 1917; ag. sent., demstr., Univ. Coll., Oct. 1921; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Dec., 1921; attd., Anuradhapura kach., Dec., 1921; itg. pol. mag., W. BAYNES, THOMAS EDWIN PRECIVAL, O.B.E. (1932).—B. 1884; ed. St. Kitts Grammar Schl. and Queen's Royal Coll., Trinidad; clerical astt., col. sec.'s off., Trinidad, 1903; 6th clk., C.S.O., 1903; 5th clk., C.S.O., 1906; 1st clk., post office, Antigua, 1907; 3rd clk., C.S.O., 1911; ag. clk. to admstr., Dominica, 1912; 2nd clk., C.S.O., clk. of exec. and legis. couns., Antigua, and supt. of govt. printing office, 1912; clk. to admstr., and clk. to exec. and legis. couns., Dominica, 1919; offr. in ch. (later, warden), Dist. dist., 1924; temp. mem., leg. coun., Dominica, Feb., 1926; O.A.G., Montserrat, in Mar., 1926 and Apr., 1928; O.A.G., Virgin Is., Aug. to Nov., 1926; offl. mem., leg. coun., Dominica in 1927 and 1928; ag. astt. col. sec., Leeward Is., Apr.-Sept., 1927; treas., Dominica, June, 1928; offl. mem., exec. coun., Dominica, June, 1928; O.A.G., Montserrat, June-Sept., 1929; O.A.G., Dominica various occasions, 1929-36; commr., Montserrat, 1932.

BAZELL, CHARLES—B. 1885; ed. Christ's Hosp. and Keble Coll., Oxford (scholar 1904-08); B.A. 1908 (2nd hon. mods., 2nd lit. hum.); astt. mast., Raffles Inst., S'pore, 1914-17 and 1922; headmast., Malay Coll., K. Kangsar, 1923.

BEAN, EDGAR LAYTON, C.M.G., M.A., LL.B.—B. 1893; ed. Scotch Coll., W. Australia, Adelaide University and Merton Coll., Oxford; joined public serv., S. Australia, 1920; crown solr.'s office; assoo. to Mr. Just. Poole, 1921-22; parly. draftsman since 1924.

BEARBLOCK, ARTHUR, M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.), D.T.M. & H. (Eng.), Certif. L.S.T.M. (with dist.).—B. 1898; ed. Univ. Coll. Schi. and Univ. Coll. Hosp.; war serv., 1917-18; M.O., Malaya, Mar., 1928. Badulla, Nov., 1932; asst. govt. agt., Anuradhapura, Feb., 1936; pol. mag., Colombo, Jan., 1936; adml. asst. govt. agt., Kalutara, Dec., 1937; ag. asst. govt. agt., do., June, 1938.

ATHLONE, SAMUEL LAUCHLAND.—B. 1885; ent. civ. serv., Leeward Is., Sept., 1901; clk. to atty.-gen., Leeward Is., Aug., 1906; sec. to legal affairs inquiry commr., Montserrat, 1911; ag. regis., Montserrat circuit, Feb.-Mar., 1915; deputy coroner, dists. "A" and "B," Antigua, Oct., 1917; mag., dist. B, Antigua, July, 1924; mag., dists. "A" and "B," 1930.

ATHLONE, FIRST EARL OF (created 1917), ALEXANDER CAMBRIDGE, K.G. (1928), G.C.B. (1911), G.C.V.O. (1904), C.M.G. (1917), D.S.O. (1900).—Served in Matabele campaign, 1896-7; S. African war, 1899-1900; European war, 1914-18; maj., 2nd Life Gds., 1911-19; brevet-offr., 1919; G.S.O., 1st grade, 1915; ret. with hon. rank of brig.-gen., 1919; personal A.D.C. to the King from 1919; gov. gen. and commdr-in-chief, Union of S. Africa, 1923-30; chan. of the Order of St. Michael and St. George (1934); Grand Cordon of Order of Leopold of Belgium, Croix de Guerre (Belgium); G.O. Leg. Hon. and Croix de Guerre (France); Knt. of Justice of St. John of Jerusalem.
BAILWAD, A. N.—Asst. dist. commnr., Kenya, Jan., 1920; junr. sec., native affrs. dept., 1926; priv. sec. to ag. gov., 1927 and 1928; sec., gov.'s confce., 1929.

BANNERMAN, CHARLES EDWARD WOOLHOUSE, O.B.E.(1924).—B. 1884; barrister-at-law, Middle Temple, Nov., 1913; admitted to Gold Coast bar as solr. and advoc., Jan., 1914; practised before sup. ct., G. Coast up to 31st July, 1919; pol. mag., sup. ct. 1st Aug., 1919; senr. pol. mag., 1920; ag. puisne judge, sup. ct., on various occasions, 1925-1931; circuit judge, B. 1880; 2nd lieut., Bucks. L.Y., 1905-8; temp. capt., Suffolk yeo. and adjutant, 1916; served in Great War, 1914-18; L.O.C., 1907-10; M.P., Cheltenham, 1910 and Dover, 1913-20; J.P., Co. Kilkenny; gov.-gen. of the Dominion of Canada, 1931-35.

BEST, Capt. Charles Cairn, M.C., L.S., M.N.Z.I.S.—B. 1894; ed. Auckland Gram. Schl. and Auckland Univ.; survey dept., N.Z., 1914-23; surv.-on-agr., Selangor, Aug., 1923; asst. supt., trig. surv., Sept., 1927; asst. supt., surv., June, 1928.

BEST, Hon. Sir Robert Wallace, K.C.M.G. (1908).—B. 1856; vice-pres. of the exec. coun., Australian Commonwealth; leader of the Deakin govt. in the Senate, Feb., 1907; min. for trade and customs, C. of A., 1909; now retd.

BEST, Sir Thomas Alexander Vans, K.C.M.G. (1932), K.B.E. (1926), C.M.G. (1918), C.B.E. (1918).—B. 1870; ed. Cheltenham Coll. and Magdalen Coll., Oxford; B.A., 1894; Taylorian Univ. scholar; gold medalist of French Ministry of pub. instruction; asst., B. Cent. Africa admin., 1896; judicial offr., Mar., 1898; ag. ch. judicial offr., Nov., 1899, to Mar., 1900; ag. vice-consul, Chinde, Aug., 1902, to Apr., 1903; ag. dep. commr., Apr. to Dec., 1903, and June, 1905, to Jan., 1906; dist. rea., 1st cls., Apr., 1906; col. sec., Falkland Is., Oct., 1909; ag. gov., Dec., 1909, to Dec., 1910; attended Impl. Educn. Conf., 1911; col. sec., Leeward Is., Jan., 1913; ag. gov., May to Sept., 1914, Apr. to Aug., 1915, and from Jan., 1916, to Mar., 1919; reed. thanks of H.M.'s Govt. for services during disturbances in Antigua in Mar., 1918; col. sec., Trinidad, Aug., 1919; ag. gov., June-Aug., 1920, June, 1921 to Jan., 1922, June to Oct., 1923 and from Aug., 1924; lieut.-gov., Malta, 1925; O.A.G. for various periods in 1926, 1927, 1928 and 1929; gov., Windward Is., Sept., 1930; ret., 1933.
BEVERIDGE, Ian Alfred, B.Sc.—B. 1908; ed. George Watson's Coll. and Edinburgh Univ.; post grad. course, Imp. Forestry Inst., Oxford, 1929; ast. cons. of forests, Gold Coast, 1930.

BEVIR, Anthony, M.A.—B. 1895; scholar of Eton, 1909, and of Hertford Coll., Oxford, 1914; M.A., 1923; served with 7th Batt., King's Liverpool Regt., July, 1915 to Apr., 1919; twice ment. in despatches; ast. prin., C.O., 9th Feb., 1921; accompanied Mr. Ormsby-Gore on his visit to the West African Colonies, Jan.-May, 1926; pvt. sec. to Mr. Ormsby-Gore, Feb., 1926; prnt., 1st Apr., 1929; sec., C.O. conf., 1930.
BEVIS, James Henry.—B. 1904; ed. Univ. Coll. and Inst. of Educn., London, B.Sc., hon. botany, dipl. educn.; ast. mast., Queen's Coll., Br. Guiana, 1927.
BEWES, George Price Hope.—B. 1892; ed., Clifton Coll. and Royal Naval Coll., Osborne; ast. dist. commr., Gold Coast, 25th Aug., 1920.
BEYERS, Brig.-Gen. Leonard.—B. 1894; perm. staff, Union defence forces, 1914-32; dir., prisons, Union of S. Africa, Sept., 1932.

BHARUCHA, Navroji Manekji, B.A., Cantab.—B. 1890; cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., Dec., 1913; addnl. pol. mag., Kurunegala, June, 1914; ag. pol. mag., Kurunegala, Apr., 1916; office asst., Batticaloa kach., Jan., 1917; pol. mag., Nov., 1917; dist. judge, 1922; ast. attm't. offr., May, 1929; dist. judge, 1927; offr. cls. I, gde. II, Oct., 1937.

BARTON, MAJOR PERCIVAL FULLER, O.B.E. (1935), V.D.—Ast. treas., G. Coast, Mar., 1907; attd. col. sec.'s office, Oct.-Nov., 1907 and Sept.-Nov., 1910; senr. ast. treas., Nov., 1908; ch. ast. treas., Apr., 1917; ag. treas. and currency offr., July-Oct., 1919 and Dec., 1920 to Aug., 1921; dep. treas., Jan., 1920; commanded G. Coast Volunteers, 1914 and during War; ment. in desps., Aug., 1917; col. treas. and currency offr., Sierra Leone, Aug., 1922; regis., sup., Malacca, 1920; passed cadet, June, 1921; coroner, Malacca, Mar., 1923; collr., land rev., May, 1923; offr., cls. V, May, 1923; priv. sec. to O.A.G., S.S., May, 1925; asst. dist. offr., Tapah, June, 1926; ditto, Kroh, Feb., 1927; offr., cls. IV, May, 1927; asst. adv., Trengganu, Nov., 1927; res., Labuan, Feb., 1930; offr., cls. III, May, 1932; ag. Br. res., Brunei, Jan., 1937; offr., cls. II, May, 1937.

BLACK, THOMAS.—B. 1894; war serv., 1914-19; 8th Hussars (France); supt., acts. and corresp., port development dept., Hong Kong, 1925; acctnt., treas., 1926; sec., exchange fund advy. comttee., in addn., 1936; acct., gen., 1937, and sec., exchange fund adv. comttee., in addition.

BLACK, WILLIAM JOHN.—B. 1872; ed. pub. and high schls., Ont.; grad. in agr., Toronto Univ.; editor, "Farmers Advocate," Winnipeg, 1902-04; dep. min., agr., Manitoba, 1904-06; pres., Manitoba Agr. Coll., 1905-15; sec., economic and development comn. of Canada, 1915-16; comn. of agr., Canada, 1916-18; chmn., soldier stitlt., bd., 1918-20; dep. min., immigr. and colonization, 1921; man., colonisation dept., Canadian National Rlys., London, 1923; ch. colonization dept., C.N. rlys., Montreal, 1924.

BLACKABY, ERNEST JAMES, M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.)—B. 1902; ed. St. Bartholomew's Hosp., London; house surg., R. Infirmary, Sunderland, Feb., 1924; res. surg., offr., dist. infirmary, Ashton-under-Lyne, 1924; house surg., London Look Hosp., 1925; asst. med. offr., Tonbridge Infirmary, 1926; dist. med. offr., Cyprus, July, 1928; ag. surg. spec., in 1929 and 1930; med. offr., E.A.M.S., Nyassaland, 1931; med. offr., Zanzibar, 1936.

BLACKALL, HENRY WILLIAM BUTLER, K.C., B.A., LL.B. (1st place).—B. 1889; ed. Stonyhurst and Trinity Coll., Dublin; Victoria prizeman, jurisprudence and internat. law; 1st of 1st cls. hons., mod. history; John Brooke scholar, King's Inns; called to Irish Bar, 1912; active serv. European War; crown counsel, R.A.F., Oct., 1919; M.L.C., Apr., 1920; crown counsel, Nigeria, May, 1923; ag. solr.-gen., on various occasions, 1923-31; mem. comn. on riots, S.E. Provs., 1930; atty.-gen., Cyprus, Jan., 1932; atty.-gen., Gold Coast, 1936.

BLACKWELL, BERNARD FLETCHER.—B. 1906; ed. Woodford Acad., N.S.W.; R. Australian Navy, 1924-30 (paymr.-lieut.); ret., 1930; corres. clk., New Hebrides, Sept., 1931; ag. acct., in 1933, 1935, 1936, 1937 and 1938; ag. asst. to commdt., pol. (Br. divn.), Aug., 1936.

BLACKWELL, KENNETH RAY.—B. 1896; ed. Shebbeare Coll., N. Devon; war serv., R.F.A., July, 1915-Nov., 1919; cadet, F.M.S., Mar., 1921; att'chd., Pahang secr., Apr., 1921; asst. supt. chandu monop., Ipoh, June, 1922; supvr., cust., Johore, Aug., 1923; offr., cls. V, asst. dist. offr., Gopang and Lampang, Nov., 1923; passed inter. L.A.A., Dec., 1925; asst. dist. offr., Kroh and asst. supvr., cust., Jan., 1926; offr., cls. IV, Nov., 1927; ag. supt., convict estabmt., F.M.S., in addn., Feb., 1928; offr., cls. III, Nov., 1932; state treas., Perak, Feb., 1934; chmn., sany. bd., Larut in addn., Oct.-Nov., 1934; ag. sec., res., Perak, May, 1935; state treas., Perak, Aug., 1935; ag. off., assignee, and regis., coys., F.M.S., Nov., 1937.
BLACKWOOD, A. Mc.A., M.B., Ch. B. (Glasgow).—B. 1885; lieut., R.A.M.C., May, 1917; capt., 1918; med. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Apr., 1919; senr. med. offr., Jan., Nov., 1932.

BLAINE, CHARLES HERBERT.—Ed. St. Andrews Coll., Grahamstown, and Dover Coll., (Eng.); served, S.A. War, 1901-02; Great War, 1914-18; ment. in deeps., clk., ot., Christiansa, Transvaal, civ. serv., 1902; pub. pros., mag.'s office, Johannesburg, 1906; 1st clk. to A.R.M., Germiston, Jan., 1908; ditto and asst. mag., Krugersdorp, 1909; ditto, Barberton, 1912; senr. clk. and senr. pub. pros., Johannesburg, 1919; addnl. mag., Roodepoort, 1920; ditto, Durban, 1923; ditto, Johannesburg, 1924; 1st gde. mag. and native comntr., Marico at Zeerust, 1929; senr. gde. mag., Zoutpansberg at Louis Trichardt and native comntr., 1931; under sec., justice, Pretoria, July, 1934; sec., just., 1936.

BLAIR, HON. ARCHIBALD WILLIAM.—B. 1878; ed. Wellington Coll. (N.Z.), and Canterbury and Victoria Univ. Colls.; judges associate, 1893-98; admitted barrister and solr., 1899; experience in legal offices, 1898-1910; in practice, 1910-28; judge, N.Z. sup. ct., 1928.
BLAIR, HAROLD ARTHUR.—B. 1902; ed. Lancing and St. Edmund Hall, Oxford; cadet, Gold Coast, Apr., 1928; asst. dist. comntr., Nov., 1928.

BLAIR, HON. SIR JAMES WILLIAM, K.C.M.G. (1935), KT. BACH. (1930)—B. 1871; called to bar, 1894; M.I.A., Queensland, for Ipswich, 1902-15; atty.-gen. in Morgan and Kidston ministries, 1903-7; also sec. for mines for most of that period; sec., pub. instr., Denham ministry, 1912-15; puisne judge, Apr., 1922; ch. just., Queensland, 1925.

BLAKE, GEORGE STANFIELD, B.Sc. Geol., Lond., A.R.S.M. Lond., M.I.M.M.—B. 1876; ed. R. Sch. of Mines, London; on teaching staff, 1896-97; Imp. Inst. (mineral survey of crown cols.), 1901; Newfoundland and Canada, 1909-15; munitions inspect., Eng., 1916-19; geol. staff, Imp. Coll. of Sci., 1920-22; geol. adv., Palestine govt., 1922.

BLAKELY, DONALD QUENTIN.—B. 1875; clk., atty.-gen.'s off., B. Honduras, Apr., 1898; ag. regis. gen.-gen., May, 1906, to Mar., 1907; 1st cls. clk., regis.-gen.'s office, Apr., 1907; ag. regis.-gen., Oct., 1908, to May, 1909; comntr. of sup. ct., B. Hond., Mar., 1908; ag. regis.-gen., Oct., 1908, to May, 1909, and Aug., 1912.

BLAKEMORE, WILLIAM LESLIE, M.B., Ch. B. (Birm.), M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.O.P. (Lond.), D.P.H. (Lond.), D.T.M. & H. (L'pool).—B. 1901; ed. Wolverhampton Gram. Schl. and Birm. Univ.; M.O., Malaya, July, 1928.

BLAND, CHARLES HEBER.—B. 1886; ed. pub. and high schls., Pembroke, Ont., and Queen's Univ., Kingston, Ont.; B.A. (1907); ent. Canadian civ. serv. at first compet. exam., 1909; served with Can. Field Arty., 1917-19; asst. sec., civ. serv. comntr., 1919; ch. examnr., 1921; civ. ser. comntr., 1933; chmn., 1935.

BLANDFORD, HON. SYDNEY D.—B. 1868; M.H.A., Newfoundland; elected for Bonavista, 1904, 1908 and 1909; M.E.C. and min. of agric. and mines, 1909; M.L.C. and min. of agric. and mines, 1914; sheriff of Newfoundland, 1917.

BLANDY, CAPT. R. D.—B. 1891; ed. Victoria Coll., Jersey; ent. Indian pol., 1911; Indian Army, R. of O., Apr., 1915; active serv., N.W. Frontier and Mesopotamia; returned Indian pol., Jan., 1919; dist. supt., pol., 1920-24; ch. of international pol., Tangier, July, 1928; asst. to res. comntr., New Hebrides, Sept., 1929; ag. res. comntr., New Hebrides, for various periods, 1930-38; attd. C.O., May, 1933.

BLANKENBERG, Sir B. A., K.B.E. (1920), O.B.E. (1918).—Rhodesian service, 1895 to 1902; conf. clk. to lieut.-gov., Transvaal, 1902; ag. priv. sec. to lieut.-gov., Aug., 1902; ch. clk. to agt.-gen. in London, 1st June, 1907; ch. clk. to high comsnr. for Union of South Africa, London, 1910; asst. sec. to high comsnr., 1912; sec. to high comsnr., 1919; ag. high comsnr., 1920; ret., 31st Mar., 1926.
twin_text: BLANKENBERG, Sir R. A., K.B.E. (1920), O.B.E. (1918).—Rhodesian service, 1896 to 1902; conf. clk. to lieut.-gov., Transvaal, 1902; ag. priv. sec. to lieut.-gov., Aug., 1902; ch. clk. to agt. gen. in London, 1st June, 1907; ch. clk. to high commr. for Union of South Africa, London, 1910; asst. sec. to high commr., 1912; sec. to high commr., 1919; ag. high commr., 1920; ret., 31st Mar., 1926.

raw_text: BLAXTER, KENNETH WILLIAM.—B. 1895; ed. Malvern and Magdalene Coll., Cambridge (schol.); mily. serv., France and Italy, 1915-19; asst. prin., miny. of transport, 1920; sec., royal comsnr. on compensation for suffering and damage by enemy action, 1923; asst. prin., C.O., 1924; pvt. sec. to permit under-sec. of state for the cols., 10th Dec., 1928; pvt. sec. to partly. under sec. of state for cols., 17th Sept., 1929; prin., 1st Oct., 1930.
twin_text: BLAXTER, KENNETH WILLIAM.—B. 1885; ed. Malvern and Magdalene Coll., Cambridge (schol.); milly. serv., France and Italy, 1915-19; asst. prin., miny. of transport, 1920; sec., royal commn. on compensation for suffering and damage by enemy action, 1923; asst. prin., C.O., 1924; prte. sec. to permit, under-sec. of state for the cols., 10th Dec., 1928; pvt. sec. to parly. under sec. of state for cols., 17th Sept., 1929; prin., 1st Oct., 1930.

raw_text: BLEDISLOE, 1st Viscount (cr. 1935) Rt. Hon. Sir CHARLES BATHURST, P.C. (1926), G.C.M.G. (1930); K.B.E., (1917).—B. 1867; ed. Sherborne, Eton, Univ. Coll., Oxford and Royal Agr. Coll., Cirencester (gold medallist and late chmn. of govs.); chancery barrister and conveyancer, 1894-1910; M.P. South Wilts Divn., 1910-18; mem. of coun. of Duchy of Lancaster; parly. sec. to miny. of food, 1916-17; chmn., R. comsnr. on sugar supply, and dir., sugar distribution, 1917-19; parly. sec. to miny. of agr., 1924-28; chmn., R. comsnr., land drainage, England and Wales, 1921; gov.-gen. and commdr-in-chief, New Zealand, 1930-35; chmn., N. Rhodesia R. comsnr., 1938.
twin_text: BLEDISLOE, 1st VISCOUNT (1935) Rt. Hon. SIR CHARLES BATHURST, P.C. (1926), G.C.M.G. (1930); K.B.E. (1917).—B. 1867; ed. Sherborne, Eton, Univ. Coll., Oxford and Royal Agrl. Coll., Cirencester (gold medallist and late chmn. of govt.); chancery barrister and conveyancer, 1894-1910; M.P. South Wilts Divn., 1910-18; mem. of coun. of Duchy of Lancaster; parly. sec. to miny. of food, 1916-17; chmn., R. commn. on sugar supply and dir., sugar distribution, 1917-19; parly. sec. to miny. of agr., 1924-28; chmn., R. commn., land drainage, England and Wales, 1927; gov.-gen. and commdr.-in-chief, New Zealand, 1930-35; chmn., N. Rhodesia R. commn., 1938.

raw_text: BLELOCH, IAN WILLIAM, M.A. (Hon.) Edin.—B. 1901; ed. Dunfermline High Schl. and Edin. Univ.; cadet, M.C.S., Jan., 1926; offr., cl. V, 1929; offr. cl. IV, Jan., 1933; off. cl. III, Jan., 1938; sec., res., N. Sembilan, Jan., 1938.
twin_text: BLELOCH, IAN WILLIAM, M.A. (Hon.) Edin.—B. 1901; ed. Dunfermline High Schl. and Edin. Univ.; cadet, M.C.S., Jan., 1926; offr., cl. V, 1929; offr., cl. IV, Jan., 1933; offr., cl. III, Jan., 1938; sec., res., N. Sembilan, Jan., 1938.

raw_text: BLOFORD, THOMAS GUEST.—B. 1902; ed. Hilton Coll., Natal and Bedford Schl.; supt. power stations, elec. dept., Ceylon, 1928; ast. engr., elec. dept., F.M.S., 1933; inspr., machinery, mines dept., Gold Coast, 1936.

BLONDIN, Lieut. Col. THE HON. PIERRE EDOUARD.—B. 1874; ed. Seminaire de Nicolet and Laval Univ., Montreal; 1st cl. to H. of C., Canada, g.e. 1908; re-el., g.e., 1911; el. dep. speaker, 1911; min. of inland rev., 1914; sec. of state, 1915; P.M.G., Canada, 1917; overseas, 1917-18; apptd. to senate, 1918; re-apptd. P.M.G., 1920; speaker of senate, 1930.
twin_text: BLONDIN, Lieut. Col. the Hon. Pierre EDOUARD.—B. 1874; ed. Seminaire de Nicolet and Laval Univ., Montreal; 1st el. to H. of C., Canada, g.e. 1908; re-el., g.e., 1911; el. dep. speaker, 1911; min. of inland rev., 1914; sec. of state, 1915; P.M.G., Canada, 1917; overseas, 1917-18; apptd. to senate, 1918; re-apptd. P.M.G., 1920; speaker of senate, 1930.

raw_text: BLOOD, HILARY RUDOLPH ROBERT, C.M.G. (1934), M.A. (Glas.).—B. 1893; on mily. serv., Oct., 1914-1919; tempy. employed at C.O., Mar.-Dec., 1919, Aug., 1924 to May, 1925 and Aug., 1929 to Feb., 1930; cadet, Ceylon, Jan., 1920; attd. to Colombo Kach., Feb., 1920; Anuradahapura Kach., Mar., 1920; ag. office asst., Anuradahapura Kach., Nov., 1920; attd., Kandy Kach., Apr., 1921; office asst., govt. agt., Cent. Prov., June, 1921; 4th asst. col. sec., Apr., 1922; 3rd asst. col. sec., June, 1925; dist. judge, Badulla, Oct., 1926; asst. govt. agt., Trincomalee, Apr., 1928; pol. mag., Colombo, Mar., 1930; col. sec. and registr. gen., Grenada, 1930; O.A.G., Windward Is., June to Aug., 1930; admstd. govt., Grenada, on several occasions; col. sec., Sierra Leone, Oct., 1934; ag. gov., in 1934, 1936 and 1937; gov.'s dep. on several occasions, 1935; ag. gov., 1938.
twin_text: BLOOD, HILARY RUDOLPH ROBERT, C.M.G. (1934), M.A. (Glas.).—B. 1893; on mily. serv., Oct., 1914-1919; temp. employed at C.O., Mar.-Dec., 1919, Aug., 1924 to May, 1925 and Aug., 1929 to Feb., 1930; cadet, Ceylon, Jan., 1920; attd. to Colombo Kach., Feb., 1920; Anuradhapura Kach., Mar., 1920; ag. office ast., Anuradhapura Kach., Nov., 1920; attd., Kandy Kach., Apr., 1921; office ast., govt. agt., Cent. Prov., June, 1921; 4th ast. col. sec., Apr., 1922; 3rd ast. col. sec., June, 1925; dist. judge, Badulla, Oct., 1926; ast. govt. agt., Trincomalee, Apr., 1928; pol. mag., Colombo, Mar., 1930; col. sec. and regnr. gen., Grenada, 1930; O.A.G., Windward Is., June to Aug., 1930; admst. govt., Grenada, on several occasions; col. sec., Sierra Leone, Oct., 1934; ag. gov., in 1934, 1936 and 1937; gov.'s dep. on several occasions, 1936; ag. gov., 1938.

raw_text: BLOUNT, AUSTIN ERNEST, C.M.G.—B. 1870; priv. sec. to Rt. Hon. Sir Chas. Tupper, Bart., 1896-1901; priv. sec. to Rt. Hon. Sir Robt. L. Borden, prime min. of Canada, 1901-1917; clk. of parlms. and clk. of senate, 1918.
twin_text: BLOUNT, AUSTIN ERNEST, C.M.G.—B. 1870; priv. sec. to Rt. Hon. Sir Chas. Tupper, Bart., 1898-1901; priv. sec. to Rt. Hon. Sir Robt. L. Borden, prime min. of Canada, 1901-1917; clk. of parliment. and clk. of senate, 1918.

raw_text: BLUMER, Rev. REGINALD CHARLES, M.A., B.Sc., Dipl. Ed.—B. 1889; ed. Sydney High Schl. and Sydney Univ.; Caird schol. in chem.; B.A., 1910, 1st cls. hon., Latin, 2nd cls. hon., matha.; B.Sc., 1910; M.A., 1912; dipl.ed., 1921; deacon, 1912; priest, 1913; curate, St. John's, Parramatta, 1912-17; curate, Holy Trinity, Dulwich Hill, 1918; asst. mast., Trinity Coll., Kandy, Ceylon, 1921-25; vice-prin., Achimota, Gold Coast, 1926.
twin_text: BLUMER, REV. REGINALD CHARLES, M.A., B.Sc., Dipl. Ed.—B. 1889; ed. Sydney High Schl. and Sydney Univ.; Caird schol. in chem., B.A., 1910, 1st cl. hona., Latin, 2nd cl. hona., matha., B.Sc., 1910; M.A., 1912; dipl., ed., 1921; deacon, 1912; priest, 1913; curate, St. John's, Parramatta, 1912-17; curate, Holy Trinity, Dulwich Hill, 1918; asst. mast., Trinity Coll., Kandy, Ceylon, 1921-26; vice-prin., Achimota, Gold Coast, 1925.

Botha, 1916-19, and attended Paris Pccce Confere., 1919; pvt. sec. to Gen. Smuts, 1919-21; asst. crown pros., Johannesburg, 1922; 1st prof. asst. to atty.-gen., Pretoria, 1924-27; law adviser to crown, Jan., 1927.
BRETT, R. A.—B. 1880; apptd. to emigrants information office, 2nd July, 1906; oversea office, 1919.
twin_text: BRETT, E. A.—B. 1880; apptd. to emigrants information office, 2nd July, 1906; overseer office, 1919.

raw_text: BRIDGES, Likute-Gen. Sir Tom, K.C.M.G. (1919), C.B. (1918), D.S.O. (1904), F.R.G.S.—

BOYD, EDMUND BLAIKIE, C.M.G. (1935), C.V.O., (1938).—B. 1894; ed. Aberdeen Gram. Schol. and Univ. of Aberdeen; M.A. 1916; 1st cls. hons. classics; on military service, 1916; with 60th siege batt., R.G.A., France and Belgium, Nov., 1917, to June, 1919; 2nd lieut., Oct., 1917; lieut., Apr., 1919; temporary clk. in C.O., Sept., 1919; appd. by civ. ser. comms. under reconstrn. scheme, 2nd cls. clk., C.O., Jan., 1920; ast. prin., 1st Apr., 1920; priv. sec. to perm. under-sec., May to Sept., 1921; sec. inter-dept. comttee., income tax in ola., 1922; priv. sec. to partly. under-sec. (Mr. Wood, now Viscount Halifax), 4th Apr., 1922; (Mr. Ormsby Gore), 31st Oct., 1922; sec. Imp. Inst. comttee. of enquiry, 1923; priv. sec. to Lord Arnold, 26th Jan., 1924; ag. prin., 12th Dec., 1924; prin. 2nd Nov., 1925; sec., C.O. confce., 1927; sec., col. development advisory comttee., 1929; ptve. sec. to Lord Passfield as S. of S. for Doma. affra. and for the Cola., 1st Apr., 1930; pvt. sec. to successive secs. of state for the Cola. (Mr. J. H. Thomas, 26th Aug., 1931, Sir P. Cunliffe-Lister (now Lord Swinton), 9th Nov., 1931, Mr. Malcolm MacDonald, 7th June, 1935, Mr. J. H. Thomas, 22nd Nov., 1935, Mr. Ormsby Gore, 29th May, 1936); asst. sec., 19th Aug., 1937.

BOYD, PETER FERGUSON.—B. 1893; telegraphist, Union of S. Africa, Sept., 1906 to Dec., 1911; postal asst., N. Rhodesia, June, 1912; ag. ch. asst., Nov., 1916 to Apr., 1917; acctnt., post office, Apr., 1920; ag. sec., Jan., 1925 to Apr., 1926; ag. dep. P.M.G. Jan.-Sept., 1927; ag. P.M.G., Sept.-Dec., 1927; col. postmtr. and super. tels., Fiji, Jan., 1928; title changed to P.M.G., 1930; nom. mem., leg. coun., Oct., 1929; nom. comnr., currenctv., Feb., 1930.

BOYD, ROBERT.—B. 1890; ed. Campbell Coll., Belfast, and Trinity Coll., Dublin; schol. (1912), B.A.(1913); cadet, F.M.S., Nov., 1913; on war serv., 1915-19; asst. contrlr., labr., Nov., 1919; offr., cls. IV., Nov., 1920; asst. dist. offr., Mar., 1923; regiar., co-op. societies, May, 1923; asst. offr. in ch., co-op. socs., S.S., Nov., 1924; offr., cls. III., Nov., 1925; asst. dir., co-opern., F.M.S., and S.S., Dec., 1927; on sp. deputation to India, Nov., 1928; ag. dir., co-opern., F.M.S. and S.S., June, 1930 and Apr., 1933; offr., cls. II., Nov., 1930; dir., co-opern., S.S. and F.M.S., Dec., 1933.

BOYD-SHANNON, GODFREY ECCLESTON.—B. 1907; ed. Wellington and Cambridge; schol., St. John's Coll., 1926; 1st cls. class. tripos, pt. I., 1928, pt. II., 1929; Browne med., 1928; B.A., 1929; apptd. after compet. exam., asst. prin., D.O., 27th Oct., 1930.

BOYDELL, HON. THOMAS.—Served apprenticeship as engrnr. at R. & W. Hawthorns & Leallies, St. Peters, nr. Newcastle-on-Tyne; came to S. Africa, 1903; worked as a fitter in the Natal harbour wks. and also in the S.A. rly. workshops, Durban; stood unsuccessfully as offl. cand. for S. African Labour Party for the Greyville divn. of Durban at the first Unparly. elections, 1910; contested by-elect. for same const., 1912; re-elec., 1915-20, 21 and 24; dep. leader of the S. African Labour Party in parl.; chmn., parly. labour caucus; national organiser of party; min. of posts and tels. and pub. wks. in Hertzog cabinet, June, 1924; min. of lab., 1925-29; nominated senator, Sept., 1929.
BOYES, JOHN HENRY.—B. 1886; ed. Prince Albert Coll., Auckland; joined N.Z. pensions dept., 1903; regiar., pensions, 1910; asst. comnr., 1919; comnr., 1929; joint pub. serv. comnr., 1936.

BOYLE, SIR ALEXANDER GEORGE, K.C.M.G. (1921), C.M.G. (1908), C.B.E. (1919).—B. 1872; ed. at Charterhouse, Clifton Coll. and R.I.E.C., Cooper's Hill; priv. sec. to senior puisne judge, S. Stiltna., 1893; resig., 1895; asst. treas., Uganda Prot., Sept., 1895; ag. sec. to H.M. Comnr., Uganda Prot., Feb., 1896, to Apr., 1898; ag. treas., Mar., 1899, to Feb., 1900; dep. treas., Apr., 1900; ag. sec. to admstrn., May, 1901 to Jan., 1902; apptd. collr. with seniority of 18th Sept., 1895, 1st Apr., 1902; asst. sec., Apr., 1902; ag. sub-comnr., E.Prov., 1st Nov., 1903; sub-comnr., Jan., 1905; ag. dep. comnr., Apr. to Oct., 1907; ag. ch. sec., Apr., 1909; adminstr.govt., Aug., 1909; col. sec., S. Nigeria, 1910; ret., 1920.

BOYSE, V. H. O.B.E. (1933), M.B.E. (1929).—B. 1883; apptd., after compet. exam., clk. of 2nd div., and assigned to C.O., 17th July, 1902; on military serv. from 26th July, 1917 to 5th Feb., 1919; cler. offr., higher grade, 1st Apr., 1922; asst. acctnt., June, 1922; sent on sp. mission to Iraq to advise on accounting methods, Nov., 1922 to Feb., 1923; ag. as finan. sec. to high comnr., Iraq., Mar.-Dec., 1924; asst., 1st Sept., 1930; acct. to exec. coun., Imp. Agrl. Bureau and Oversea Mech. Transp. Coun.; accompanied U.K. delegation to Imp. Economic Confce., Ottawa, 1932; ag. prin., 1st Apr., 1937.

BRACE, IVOR LLWELLYN, B.L.—B. 1898; called to bar, Gray's Inn, 1921; pol. mag., Gold Coast, 1930; ditto, Nigeria, 1931; crown coun., 1932; ag. solr.-gen., July-Oct., 1937; asst. judge, prot. et al., 1937.

BRADFORD, SIR JOHN ROSE, Bart. (1931), K.C.M.G. (1911); M.D., D.Sc., F.R.C.P., F.R.S.—B. 1863; ed. Univ. Coll. Schl., Coll. and Hosp. George Henry Lewes student and Grocer research schl.; late mem. of Senate, Univ. of London; physician to Univ. Coll. Hosp.; Holme lecturer on clinical medicine to Univ. Coll. hosp. med. schl.; sec. of the Royal Society; sen. med. adviser to C.O., 1912; resigned, 1924.
BRADLEY, ALEXANDER WILLIAM, M.A. (Cantab.).—B. 1903; ed. Malvern and Clare Coll., Camb.; B.A., 1925, M.A., 1928; cadet, Uganda, 1930; asst. dist. offr., 1932; transfd. to Somaliland, 1937.
BRADLEY, CHARLES HUGH GRANVILLE.—B. 1901; ed. Wellington Coll.; clk., col. audit dept., June, 1925; asst. audr., Hongkong, Sept., 1925; do., Uganda, 1930; serv. asst. audr., 1936.

BRADLEY, JOHN THOMAS, O.B.E. (1933), L.R.C.P.E., L.R.C.S.E., L.F.P. and S.G.; M.D. (Brux.)—B. 1870; ed. at Queen's Coll., Belfast, and Lond. Schl. Trop. Med. (1901); undergrad. in arts, Royal Univ., Ireland; prizeman in med., midwifery, and diseases of women and children, Queen's Coll., Belfast, 1897-8; gold medallist in practical midwifery, Clifton st. Mater. Hosp.; 1st prizeman in med. and surg. diseases of children, Children's Hosp., Queen st., Belfast; late asst. med. offr., Glamorgan Co. Asylum, 1901; asst. med. offr., Seychelles, Aug., 1901; J.P. and chmn. of loc. bd. of health, 1st and 2nd div., South Mahé; med. supt., lunatic asylum, Seychelles, Mar., 1906; ch. med. offr., 1924.

BRADSTREET, FREDERICK GEORGE.—B. 1884; ent. C.A.'s office, June, 1902; asst. head, checking and insurance dept., May, 1919; do., gen. dept., Jan., 1924; sec., Zambesi Bdg. interdeptl. comte., 1928-31; dep. head, finance dept., Mar., 1931; dep. head, gen. dept. and dep. ch. clk., 1933; head, appts. and passages dept., 1935.

BRADY, LIEUT.-COL. JOHN BANKS, D.S.O.—Head mast. Grey Coll. Schl., Dec., 1901; instr. of schl., O.R.C., Feb., 1904; instr. of schl., Rhodesia, Aug., 1909; on active serv., 1914-20; commanded 4th and 18th batt., K.R.R.C.; ment. in desps.; croix de guerre with palm leaves; ag. dir. of educn., S. Rhodesia, 1920-21.

BRAGG, ALGERNON.—B. 1890; col. audit dept., Apr., 1915; asst. audr., Nigeria, May, 1915; mily. audit, E. African Expedy. Force, 1916-19; asst. audr., Uganda, 9th May, 1919; senr. asst. audr., Uganda, Sept., 1924; ag. audr., Uganda, for various periods, 1926-27; prin. asst. audr., Nov., 1928; ag. audr., May to Nov., 1929 and Mar.-Nov., 1933; dep. audr., Kenya, 1931; audr., Tanganyika Territory, 1935; do., Kenya, 1938.
BRAILSFORD, H. E.—Asst. dist. comnr., B.A.P., Jan., 1917; dist. offr., Kenya, 1921.

BRENNER, George Gustav Radloff, M.A. (Edin.), LL.B. (Lond.), Barrister-at-law (Middle Temple).—B. 1883; ed. Grey Coll., Bloemfontein; pte. sec. to Gen. Botha, 1916-19, and attended Paris Peace Conferee., 1919; pte. sec. to Gen. Smuts, 1919-21; asst. crown pros., Johannesburg, 1922; 1st prof. asst. to atty.-gen., Pretoria, 1924-27; law adviser to crown, Jan., 1927.

BRIDGES, Lieut.-Gen. Sir Tom, K.C.M.G. (1919), Q.B. (1918), D.S.O. (1904), F.R.G.S.— B. 1872; Grenadier Guards; served S. African War, 1899-1901; Somaliland, 1902-4; European War, 1914-15; mil. attache at the Hague, Brussels, Copenhagen and Christiania, 1910-14; lieut.-col., 4th Hussars, 1914-16; brev., col., 1916; maj.-gen., temp. lieut.-col., 1917; col., Dragoon Gds., 1920; commdr., Br. mly. mission to Smyrna, 1920; gov., S. Australia, 1922-1927.

BRIDGES, MAJOR WILLIAM FRANCIS NOEL, D.S.O.—B. 1890; ed. Melbourne C. of E. Gram. Schl. and Trin. Coll., Melbourne Univ.; surr.-on-agreement, F.M.S., Sept., 1912; served A.L.F. Gallipoli and France, 1915-18; asst. supt., surveys, F.M.S., Jan., 1919; supt., surveys, F.M.S., Oct., 1927; addl. surr.-gen.'s office, Jan., 1928; ag. asst. surr. gen., F.M.S. and S.S., June, 1929 and Aug., 1930 to Feb., 1931; asst. surr.-gen., Dec., 1931; dep. surr.-gen., June, 1933; seconded as dep. contr., rubber, May-Aug., 1934; surr.-gen., Malaya, 1937.

BRIERCLIFFE, RUPERT, C.M.G. (1936), O.B.E., M.B., Ch.B., B.Sc., D.P.H. (Mano.), D.T.M. and H. (Eng.)—B. 1889; dep. dir., health, Palestine, 1920; dir., med. and san. services, Ceylon, July, 1930; dir., med. services, Nigeria, 1936.
BRIERLEY, FRANK NORRIS.—B. 1887; ent. Trinidad civ. serv., June, 1906; warder, county of Caroni, Mar., 1933; do., E. counties, Apr., 1936.
BRIND, WALTER GERARD, D.F.C., M.Sc. (Hon.), (N.U.L.), B.E. (N.U.L.).—B. 1897; transf. from S. Rhodesian govt. as govt. engnr., Bechuanaland Prot., 1928; D.P.W., 1933.

BRINK, LIEUT.-GEN., ANDRIES JACOB KESTEN, D.T.D., D.S.O.—Joined civ. serv., S. African Repub., 1896; served Mpufo (native) campaign, 1896; Zoutpanberg and Anglo-Boer War, 1899-1920; elected veldt cornet, S. African Repub. forces, 1901; capt., permanent force (staff), Union defence forces, 1912; promoted major, and served as dist. staff offr., No. 6 and later as dist. staff offr., No. 7 mil. dist., 1914; brig.-major, 1st mounted bty., German S.W.A. campaign, 1915; temp. lieut.-col., Mar., 1915; dist. staff offr., No. 6 mil. dist., with rank of temp. lieut.-col., whilst so empd., Aug., 1915; brev. lieut.-col., Sept., 1915; brig.-major, 2nd mounted bty., E. Africa, Mar., 1916; gen. staff offr. with rank of col. headqrs., 3rd E. African divn., Apr., 1916; resumed duty as dist. staff offr. No. 6 mil. dist. Dec., 1916; transf. to defence headqrs., and assumed duty as asst. adjt.-gen., defence headqrs., Sept., 1917; ag. ch. of gen. staff and adjt.-gen., with temp. rank of col., Nov., 1919; lieut.-col., 1st grade staff offr., Nov., 1919; brig.-gen., and ch. of gen. staff, Sept., 1920; combined post of ch. of gen. staff and sec. for defence, 1st Oct., 1922; maj.-gen., Nov., 1929; sec. for defence and G.O.C., Union Defence Forces, May, 1933; dir., civ. aviation, Nov., 1934; transf., R. of O., Apr., 1937; commdt.-in-ch., Burger commandos, Apr., 1937; 1914-15 Star, Br. War Med., Victory Med.; D.S.O., (1917), French Croix de Guerre; men. in deeps., for serv. in G.S.W.A. campaign (1918), medal, Anglo-Boer oorlog.

BRINK, FRANCOIS JOHANNES.—B. 1889; ed. Riebeek West Pub. Schl. and S.A. Coll., Cape Town; war serv., 1914-16; Union of S. Africa pub. serv. as asst. inspr., mines, Brakpan, 1925; dep. inspr., mines, Germiston, 1929; inspr., mines and mining commr., Bloemfontein, 1934; inspr., mines, Transval, 1935.

BRINK, COLONEL GEORGE EDWIN, D.S.O.—B. 1889; served Great War, German S.W. Africa campaign as staff offr., headqrs., Southern Force, 1914-15; German E. Africa campaign as staff capt. and brig. major, 1st S.A. Mounted Brigade, 1916-17 and on G.H.Q. staff, 1917-18; graduated, Staff Coll., Camberley, 1921; commdt., S.A. Milv. Coll., Roberts Heights, 1932; O.C., troops, Roberts Heights, 1933; O.C., Cape commd., 1933; dir., army training, Union defence forces, Pretoria, 1937; Croix de Guerre (French); ment. in desp. (3 times).

BRINSLEY, GEORGE GERARD.—B. 1902; temp. asst. conserv., forests, Trinidad, 1921; forest survr., Br. Guiana, Oct., 1928; survr., lands dept., Jamaica, Jan., 1932; addl. P.W.D. as supervisor, Falmouth harbr. improvement, Oct., 1934; do., Kingston and Port Antonio harbrs. improvement, Sept., 1936.
BROCK, DENIS GEORGE, B.A.—B. 1916; ed. Latymer Upper Schl. and Sidney Sussex Coll., Cambridge (schol., 1934-38); hist. tripes, B.A., 1937; apptd. after compet. exam., asst. prin., D.O., Oct., 1938.

BROCKMAN, SIR EDW. LEWIS, K.C.M.G. (1913), C.M.G. (1908).—B. 1865; cadet, Straits Stltms., 1886; passed in Malay, 1888; 3rd mag., Penang June, 1890; dist. offr., Bukit Mertajam, Mar., 1892; 2nd asst. col. sec., June, 1896; also ag. coll. land rev., Singapore, June, 1896; collr. land rev., Malacca, 1898; commr., court of requests, Singapore, Aug., 1902; asst. col. sec. and clk. of coun., Sept., 1903; ag. res. gen., May, 1907; confirmed as fed. sec., continuing to act also as res. gen., July, 1907 to Feb., 1908; ag. res., Perak Apr., 1908; res., Pahang, Nov., 1908; res., Negri Sembilan, 1st Jan., 1910; col. sec., S. Stltms., 1910; chief sec., F.M.S., 1911; ret., Sept., 1920; apptd. agt., Malay States Infra. Agency, Nov., 1920.

BROEKEMA, ALBERTONIE HERMAN, K.C., B.A., LL.B.—B. 1895; ed. Boys' High Schl., Stellenbosch; awarded Rhodes scholarship, Univ. of Stellenbosch; called to bar, Cape Town, 1924; senr. asst. to atty. gen., Cape Town, 1931; law advr. dept. of just., Feb., 1933; chmn. disciplinary appl. bd., dept. of S.A. rlys. and harbrs., Apr., 1933; legal advr. to dept. of prime min. and of external affrs., Sept., 1933; mem., S.W. Africa column., 1935; K.C., 1937; attended Impl. Conf., as law advr., 1937; sec. for defence, Sept., 1937.

BROMLEY, Rear-Adml. ARTHUR, C.M.G. (1919).—B. 1876; ed. Farnborough Schl.; H.M.S. Britannia; joined navy, 1892; lieut., 1896; commdr., 1908; capt., 1915; served European War, 1914-18; H.M.S. Courageous, 1916-19; flag capt. to Vice-Adml. Napier commdg. light cruiser force; ret. list, 1922; rear adm., ret., 1926; a gentlemen usher to the King since 1927; pvt. sec. (ceremonial and reception) to the S. of S. (Lord Passfield), 1931.

BROODBANK, ALFRED JOHN BERTIE.—B. 1888; dist. treas., N.Borneo serv., Aug., 1921 to Aug., 1931; asst. audr., in addn., Dec., 1924 to Apr., 1926, and Oct., 1928 to July, 1929; ag. audr., Aug., 1931 to May, 1932; dist. treas., May, 1932; asst. commr. cust., in addn., Mar., 1933; ag. commr. cust. and excise, Apl., 1934; commr. cust. and excise, Apl., 1935.
BROOKE, Neville John.—B. 1891; barrister-at-law, Middle Temple; asst. dist. offr., Nigeria, Jan., 1915; seconded to C.O., 1928; census offr., N. Provs., 1930; res., 1930; judge, high ct., Apr., 1934.

BROOKES, VICTOR S.—B. 1888; passed senr. Cambridge local exam., 1906; apptd. copyist, col. sec.'s office, Antigua, 1906; cashier, St. John's savings bank, Sept., 1908; acctnt., ditte, June, 1909; ag. acctnt., treasy. dept., June to Sept., 1909; clk. to comsrr. and exec. coun., Virgin Is., Apl., 1911; rev. offr., St. Kitts, Nov., 1914; acctnt., treasy., St. Kitts; ag. audit clk., St. Kitts-Nevis, 1918; ag. treas. and ag. mem. exec. and leg. couns., Aug.-Oct., 1934.

BROOKS, ARTHUR CECIL, M.C., B.Sc.(N.U.I.), A.R.C.S.Q.I., A.I.C.—B. 1896; ed. St. And Coll., Dublin, R. Coll. of Sci. for Ireland and Birm. Univ.; astt. gvt. analyst, S.S., July, 1924; dep. gvt. analyst, Penang, Oct., 1936; ar. astt. analyst, S'pore, Oct., 1937.

BROOKS, B. L.—B. 1900; ed. Charterhouse; Artists Rifles; comand., Guards' Machine Gun Regt., 1920; passed inter. exam., Surry's Inst.; dipl., forestry, Edinburgh, 1922; astt. conserv., forests, Uganda, 1922; seconded to admstrn., 1924; dep. conserv., forests, Trinidad, 1925; ag. conserv., forests in 1925, 1928, 1931 and 1933; conserv., forests, May, 1934.

BROWN, CHARLES CUTHBERT, B.A.—B. 1888; ed. Marlborough and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; schlr., 1909; Ball schlr., 1908; int. cls. clasm. trip., 1910; cadet, F.M.S., 1911; astt. dist. offr., Brunei, 1912-13; astt. sec. to res., Perak, 1913-15; 3rd astt. sec., Fed. secretariat and priv. sec. to ch. sec., 1915-19; astt. dist. offr., Kuala Kangsar, Oct., 1919; dist. offr., Nov., 1922; offr., cls. III, Nov., 1923; offr., cls. II, Nov., 1928; offr., cls. IB, Apr., 1931; Br. advr., Trengganu, June, 1933; Br. res., Pahang, Sept., 1935.

BROWN, CAPT. ERIC GORDON, B.Sc., A.M.I.C.E., Chtd. Civ. Engnr.—B. 1890; ed. Burton-on-Trent Gram. Schl., Malvern Coll., and B'mgham Univ.; astt. engnr., F.M.S. rly., Oct., 1919; dist. engnr., grade II., Oct., 1924; ditto, grade I., Oct., 1928; dist. engnr., Jan., 1936.

BROWN, FRANK LESLIE, M.C.—B.1896; ed. Wilson Gram. Sch.; served in France, 1916-19; lieut., M.C., bar (ment. in desps.); prob., N Rhodesia, Oct., 1919; astt. nativ. comsrr., Feb., 1922; nativ. comsrr., Oct., 1926; dist. offr., grade III, Apr., 1929; do., grade II, Apr., 1931; sec. as astt. sec., Aug., 1931; astt. col. sec., Jamaica, Aug., 1935.

BROWN, GEORGE PRITCHARD.—B. 1885; served with E.
