BULMAN, JOSEPH WAUGH, A.M.I.Loco.E.—B. 1893; ed. Petershill Pub. Schl., Glasgow; served in Navy, 1914-19; loco. foreman, Cyprus govt. rly., Nov., 1919; ast. loco. supt., Oct., 1927; supt., rly., July, 1935.

BUNBURY, CECIL MOLESWORTH.—Ed. privately; employed at Crown Bridge Works, W. Bromwich, 1898-1898; Great Central, Great Northern and City Rlyws., 1898-1900; served in S. African War, 1900-1902 (medal with 4 clasps); Central S. African Rlyws., 1902-1904; ast. engr., Uganda Rlyw., 15th Jan., 1906; maintenance engr., Jan., 1925; ag. ch. engr., 1925.

BUNTING, BENJAMIN, N.D.A., F.L.S.—B. 1889; ast. agriculturist, F.M.S., Mar., 1914; ag. do., Dec., 1914; agriculturist, Man., 1920; on sp. duty in Ceylon, Jan.-Feb., 1930; ag. ch. research offr., K. Lumpur, Nov., 1936.
BUNTING, ROBERT HUGH, F.L.S.—Ast. dir. of agriculture, Sierra Leone, 1913-15; ast. dir. of agrio. and govt. mycologist, Gold Coast, Jan., 1915; ag. dir. of agrio. on several occasions.
BURGE, G. H. K.—B. 1890; cadet, Gilbert and Ellice Islands, Jan., 1915.

BURGESS, CLAUDE BRAMALL.—B. 1910; ed. Epworth Coll. and Christ Ch., Oxford, B.A. (mod. greats); cadet, Hong Kong, Oct., 1932; ast. to sec. for Chinese affrs., Feb., 1935; ast. to supt. of impts. and expts., Aug., 1935; admstive ast. urban coun., Mar., 1936; pol. mag., June, 1936; 3rd ast. col. sec., Sept., 1937; dist. offr., North, Dec., 1937; 2nd ast. col. sec., and dep. clk. of councils, June-July, 1938; supt., inland rev., Aug., 1938.

BURKE, LEONARD GEOIL (B. Econ.).—Learner, Capetown, 1913; ast. staff br., Pretoria, 1913; 3rd grade clk., do., 1914; 2nd grade clk., do., 1918; 1st grade clk., staff sect. (N. area), 1924; senr. clk., staff br. Pretoria, 1928; prin. clk., postal br., Pretoria, 1932; ch. clk., do., 1934; trans. to office of pub. serv. commn., 1934; pub. serv. inspr., grade II, 1934; ast. sec. (P.S.C.), 1935; under-sec., staff. posts. and tels. dept., Pretoria, 1937.

BURKE-GAFFNEY, E.M.O.D.—B.1887; ed. Belvedere Coll. and Univ. Coll., Dublin; ast. engr., F.M.S., P.W.D., Jan., 1920; exec. engr., Jan., 1929; senr. exec. engr., Feb., 1936; ast. state engr., Kedah, Dec., 1937.
BURKE-GAFFNEY, H.J. O'D., M.D., B.Ch., B.A.O. (Dub.).—B. 1901; med. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Aug., 1929; ast. bacteriologist, 1929; senr. pathologist, 1935.
BURNER, ERIC ANDERSON.—Ed. at Felsted Schl.; command., R.F.A., France, Belgium and Germany, 1915-18; ast. dist. comsnr., Gold Coast, 1920; dist. comsnr., 1924.

BURNETT, BERTRAM DAVIDSON.—B. 1891; ed. Port Elphinston Pub. Schl. and Inverurie Acad.; war serv., 6th Batt. Gordon Highlanders, 1914-19; foreman of wks., 2nd grade, P.W.D., Gold Coast, Feb., 1920; headmast., junr. trade schl., Apr., 1924.

BURNETT, CAPT. FREDERICK, M.C., M.A.—B. 1890; ed. Ashwater Schl., Devon, and Oxford Univ.; war serv., R.E., France, 1914-19 (M.C. and bar); divl. agril. offr., Ceylon, 1925; ast. regis., co-op. socs., 1926; vice-prin. agril. coll., Peradeniya, 1926; dep. dir., agr., Br. Guiana, 1930; rep. col. at confce. of dirs. of agr., London, 1938.

BURNS, SIR ALAN CUTHBERT MAXWELL, K.C.M.G. (1936), C.M.G. (1927).—B. 1887; apptd. to treasry. and cust. dept., St. Kitts, Leeward Is., Mar., 1906; clk. to mag., Dec., 1909; ag. mag., Anguilla, 1910; dep. coroner and J.P., St. Kitts, 1911; ag. clk. and priv. sec. to admstr., Dominica, 1912; supervisor of cust., S. Nigeria, Nov., 1912; ast. sec., Nigeria, Jur.

BURTON, ROBERT HILLHOUSE.—B.1896; apptd. after compet. exam., clk. of the 2nd div. of the civ. ser. and assigned to C.O., 15th Mar., 1915; cler. offr., higher grade, 30th Apr., 1923; staff offr., 1st Oct. 1936.
BURTON, GERALD JOHN LLOYD, M.C., B.A.—Plant breeder, agril. dept., Kenya, Jan., 1921; senr. plant breeder and experimentalist, 1935.

BURTON, WILLIAM ELLIOT.—M.D., C.M., McGill Univ., 1910; ag. temporary res. surg., Barbados general hosp., 1910; ag. med. offr., St. James' parish, Barbados; ag. visiting physician, leper asylum; ag. surg. to police, dist. II., Barbados, 1911; mag. and med. offr., Anguilla, Leeward Is., 1912; inspr. public health, St. Kitts-Nevis, Aug., 1918.

BUSHE, SIR HENRY GRATTAN, K.C.M.G. (1936), C.B. (1932), C.M.G. (1927)—B. 1886; called to the bar, 1909; Western circuit, 1910-11; S. Eastern circuit, 1911; Herts and Essex sessions, 1916; sec. to royal comm. on the contracts between the War Office and Sir John Jackson, Ltd., 1916-17; ag. legal asst., C.O., Nov., 1917; legal asst., 1st Jan., 1919; legal adviser to D.O. and C.O., 1st Apr., 1931.

BUTCHER, JAMES WILLIAM.—B. 1889; ed. N.Z. state schls. and Waitaki Boys' High Schl.; ent. regisr. gen.'s dept., 1906; statistical branch, 1912; ch. compiler, census and statistics office, 1915; asst. govt. statistician, 1929; govt. statistician, 1932; N.Z. rep. at Br. Empire Conf. of statisticians, Ottawa, 1935; mem., Internat. Statistical Inst.

BUTCHER, PERCY GEORGE, B.A. (hons. Oxon).—B. 1890; ed. Perse Schl., Cambridge and Exeter Coll., Oxford; war serv., Devonshire Regt., 1915-19; France, 1916-19; admstv. offr., cls. IV., Nigeria, Aug., 1919; Arabic dipl. Schl. of Oriental Studies, 1927; ag. res., Bornu Prov., various periods since 1928; dep. res., Aug. 1934; admstv. offr., cls. I., 1936.
BUTLER, CHARLES OWEN.—B. 1896; on mily. serv., 1914-19; asst. dist. commnr., Gold Coast, 1921; dist. commnr., 1924; asst. col. sec., 1928; ag. senr. asst. col. sec., Apr.-Sept., 1932.

BUTLER, CHARLES RICH.—B. 1886; ed. Trinity Coll., Dublin, and at the Sorbonne, Paris; B.A.; supt., educn. dept., Nigeria, Apr., 1914; attached, Nigeria Regt., 1917-18; ch. inspr., S. Provs., 1930; ag. asst. dir., educn., S. Provs., 1932 and 1933; prin., Higher Coll., Yaba, 1933.
BUTLER, ERNEST CECIL.—B. 1900; clk., Basutoland, 1920; sub.-inspr., B.M. pol., 1927; war serv., Apr., 1917-Mar., 1919; asst. dist. commnr., 1937; dist. commnr., 1938.

BUTLER, FREDERICK BERRY LEWIS, F.L.S.—B. 1890; ed. Shaw Schl. (Berks); ent. R. Botanic Gardens, Kew, 1912; served in European War, 1915-19; agrl. instr., E.A.P., 1919; man., Mazeras and Kibos experimental farms, E.A.P., 1921; agrl. supervisor, Kenya, 1923; grader and inspr., Kenya, 1925; ch. grader and inspr. of produce, Cyprus, Jan., 1935.

BUTLER, G. G., M.B.E., B.A., M.D., B.C., Cantab. (hons. nat. sci. tripes), M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.), certif., L.S.T.M. (with distinc- tion).—Apptd., W.A.M.S., Sierra Leone, Apl., 1911; seconded for sp. investigation under yellow fever commr., May, 1913 to June, 1914; served Cameroons Expdy. Force, Jan., 1916 to Apl., 1916; temp.-capt. (gen. list) Jan. to Apl., 1916; seconded for serv. with R.A.M.C., 1916-19; dir. of laboratory, Tanganyika Territory, Jan., 1920; sec. to S. of S. for the cols. (Mr. Ormsby Gore), 31st May, 1937; pvt. sec. to perm. U.S. of S. for the cols. (Sir Cosmo Parkinson), 24th Dec., 1937.

CARTER, CYRIL WALTER, M.Inst. C.E., M. Int. Nav. C.—B. 1900; ed. Berkhampstead Sch. and Crystal Palace Engng. Sch.; mily. serv., 1918-19; seconded to Tanganyika Govt., 1927; dep. res. engr., Egyptian govt., 1929; exec. engr.-in-ch., harb. wks., Tanganyika, 1930; senr. asst. engr., harbr. dept., Nigeria, 1934; ag. port engr., 1935; port engr., 1935.

CARTER, EDWARD STANLEY.—B. 1888; ed. Carpenter's Co. Tech. Inst.; war serv., 1914-19; asst. engr., P.W.D., Hong Kong, 1920; seconded, Kowloon-Canton Rly., 1926-27 and in 1929; ag. mgr. and ch. engr., do., 1938.
CARTER, HUGH CLAY, M.C., M.E.I. (Canada).—B. 1884; ed. St. Albans Sch.; asst. engr., Br. Honduras, Jan., 1921; ag. dir., pub. wks., Apr., to Dec., 1923; D.P.W., Br. Honduras, May, 1924.
CARTER, WILLIAM HOWARD.—B. 1887; asst. insp't., schls., Barbados, 1934.

CARTER, SIR WILLIAM MORRIS, KT. B.A.G. (1919), C.B.E. (1918), B.A., B.C.L.—Ed. at King's schl., Canterbury, and Brasenose Coll., Oxford; open exhib., in mod. hist., Jan., 1892; class. mods. (honours), 1894; 2nd cl. hons. juris., 1896; certif. of honour, bar exam., 1899; awarded spec. prize in English constitutional law and legal hist., by coun. of legal educn., 1899; called to the bar, Linc.'s Inn, Nov., 1899; regis. and prim. regis. of documents, E. Africa Prot., Jan., 1902; ag. town mag., Mombasa, Apl., 1902; mag., Ist Oct., 1902; judge of H.M. high ct. of Uganda and H.B.M. ct. of appeal for E. Africa, Nov., 1903; ag. prin. judge and legal adviser, Apl., to Sept., 1904; ag. prin. judge, Mar. to Oct., 1907, Aug. to Dec., 1909, and Aug., 1911, to Jan., 1912; apptd. commr. to enquire into native land tenure, 1906; mem. of comtes., to consider land legislation, 1906; and to draft land legislation, 1909; president, native land settm. comtes., 1911; chief just., Uganda Prot., July, 1912; joint compiler of first edition of laws of Uganda Prot., 1909; ch. just., Tanganyika Territory, 12th Oct., 1920; ret. 1924; ohmn., cotton enquiry coman., Uganda, 1929.

CARTMEL-ROBINSON, HAROLD FRANCIS, O.B.E. (1933).—B. 1889; ed. St. Paul's Schl. and Merton Coll., Oxford; probtnr., N. Rhodesia, Sept., 1912; asst. native commr., 1914; war serv., 1917-19; native commr., 1918; asst. mag., 1926; ag. prov. commr. for short periods in 1928, 1931, 1932 and 1933; prov. commr., 1934; ag. senr. prov. commr., 1935.

CARTWRIGHT, CYRIL GEORGE FOX.—B. 1911; ed. Winchester Coll. and Balliol Coll., Oxford; B.A.; cadet, Gilbert and Ellise Ic., 1934; dep. oomsnr. (temp. and provnl.), 1934; ag. sec. to govt., 1936; sec. to govt., Seychelles (seconded), 1938.
CARVER, J. E. A.—Asst. conserv., forests, Nyasaland Prot., Sept., 1921; conserv., forests, Mauritius, Sept., 1938.
CASEY, NORMAN RANDRETH, M.B.E. (1934).—B. 1882; ent. Fiji civ. serv., 1908; gaoler, 1917; ag. supt., 1918; ag. impr.-gen., prisons, 1920; supt., prisons, 1923; off., M.L.C., 1937.

CASEY, HON. RICHARD GARDINER, D.S.O. M.C., M.A., M.P.—Polit. liaison offr. in London between Br. and Australian Govts., 1924-31; M.P., Corio, H. of R. of C'wealth of Australia, since 1931; asst. treas., 1933-35; treas., Lyons ministries, 1935 and 1937; also min. in ch. of development and industr. resch.

CASELY-HAYFORD, ARCHIBALD, M.A. (Cantab.)—B. 1898; ed. Colet Court (St. Paul's Prep. Schl., Lond.), Richmond Coll., Cape Coast, Dulwich Coll., and Clare Coll., Cambridge, called to bar, Inner Temple, 1921; practised before sup. ct., Gold Coast, 1921-36; dist. mag., Gold Coast, Dec., 1936.

CASGRAIN, HON. PIERRE FRANCOIS, B.A., LL.M., K.C.—B. 1868; ed. St. Mary's Coll. and Laval Univ.; practised law, Montreal, since 1912; ol. to Canadian H. of C., g. el., 1917; re-el., g. el., 1921, 1926, 1926, 1930 and 1936; ch. lib. whip, 1925-36; ol. speaker, H. of C., Feb., 1936.
CASHIN, HON. SIR MICHAEL PATRICK, K.B.E. (1918).—B. 1886; M.H.A., Newfoundland; mem. for Ferryland since 1893; mem. exec. coun. and min. of finance and customs, 1909; min. of finance, Jan., 1918.
CASPERSZ, Maxim Lucian Dido, B.Sc. (Hons.) Lond.—B. 1912; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Dec., 1935; office ast., Nov., 1936; offr. cls. II Dec., 1937.

CASTILLO, ROGER—B. 1883; ed. Flores's Coll. and govt. lyceum, Malta; ent. Malta civ. serv., Feb., 1901; ch. clk., P.W.D. and compt., industrial property, Mar., 1930; asst. sec. to govt., July 1937; sec. to govt., May, 1938.
CASTONGUAY, JULES.—B. 1882; asst. in office of clk. of crown in chancery for Canada, 1908; asst. ch. electoral offr., 1920; ch. electoral offr., 1927.

CATCHPOLE, CAPT. E. K.—B. 1889; Rhodesian pol., 1910-15; K.A.R., 1915-20; asst. supt., pol., Kenya, Dec., 1920; supt., 1928; ag. asst. commr., Oct., 1933-Aug., 1934; serv. brother of St. John of Jerusalem, 1938.

CATHIE, BERNARD DALE AUSTIN.—Ed. at Cranleigh and Lille; Gold Coast Regt., 1916-18; called to bar, Middle Temple, June, 1926; polit. serv., Gold Coast, 1921-35; ag.pol.mag., Kumasi, 1933; regist., sup. ct. and clk. of arraigns, Gibraltar, March, 1937.

CATOR, GEOFFREY EDMUND, C.M.G. (1936); B.A.—B. 1884; ed. King's schl., Bruton and Selwyn Col., Camb. (Scholar); cadet, F.M.S., Nov., 1907; passed cadet, June, 1909; offr. cls. V. Mar., 1911; ag. res., Labuan, in addn., Feb., 1917; offr. cls. IV., Jan., 1918; offr. cls. III., Nov., 1919; offr. cls. II., Nov., 1924; mem. stand. advary. comtes. on use opium, Malaya, Feb., 1926; temy. mem., legis. council, S.S., May, 1927; offr. cls. IB, dist. offr. Klang, Jan., 1928; ag. Br. res., Perak, in addn., June-July, 1928; offr. cls. I A, June, 1930; supt., govt. monopolies, S.S., 1930; M.L.C.; Br. res., Selangor, July, 1932; Br. res., Perak, Dec., 1933.
CATTANACH, A.—B. 1880; on mily. serv., Feb., 1915 to Feb., 1918; asst. cashier and cashier, disposal and liquidation coman., 12th May, 1919 to 22nd July, 1922; cler. offr., C.O., 24th July, 1922.

CAVENAUGH, HERBERT JAMES LAWRENCE.—Ed. Ardingly Coll. and King's Coll., London; addt., R.I.C., Dublin for training, May, 1908; sub-inspr., Trinidad constab., Dec., 1908; serv. with H.M. Forces, Sept., 1915, June, 1919; capt.; G.S. and Victory Meds., Order of the Nile (4th cl.); ag. staff offr., local forces, Aug. and Sept., 1919; ag. impr., Trinidad constab., Sept. to May, 1920; supt. of pol., Br. Honduras, 1st June, 1920; ag. dist. coman., Belize, Mar.-May, 1921; ag. supt., prisons in addition to substantive appt., Sept., 1924 to S'pore and astt. contrlr., rubber in addn., Aug., 1926; ditto, Penang, Mar., 1927; offr., cls. IV, Apr., 1928; dist. offr., Alor Gajah, Dec., 1928; astt. dir., co-opern., S.S. and F.M.S., Dec., 1929; ag. astt. advr., B. Pahat, Apr., 1931; offr., cls. III, Apr., 1933; mag., Seremban, May, 1935; do., K. Lumpur, Dec., 1936; sec. to res., N. Sembilan, Nov., 1936; leg. advr., Trengganu, Sept., 1938.
CALWELL, H. G., B.A., M.B., B.Ch., B.A.O. (Hons.) (Belf.), D.T.M. & H. (Lond.) (Gold Med., Diseases of Children, Belfast).—B. 1901; med. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Apr., 1931.

CAMERON, Geoffrey Valentine, B.A. (Oxon), 1st cls. hons. jurisprudence, barrister-atlaw and former solr.—B. 1904; advot.-gen., St. Lucia, Sept., 1934; legal advr., Aden, 1937; ag. ch. just., supreme ct., May-Sept., 1938.

CAMERON, MAJOR SIR MAURICE ALEXANDER, K.C.M.G. (1914); C.M.G. (1900), late R.E.—B. 1855; first comm., in Roy. Engrs., Aug., 1874; dept. col. engr., S. Stittima, Dec., 1883, to Apr., 1892; during which he acted about two years and a half as col. engr., and survr., gen., and was a mem. of exec. and legis. couns.; apptd. one of the crown agts. for the colonies, 1895-1920.
CAMERON, NORMAN EUSTACE.—B. 1903; ed. Queen's Coll., Br. Guiana, and Fitzwilliam Hall, Camb. (Br. Guiana school, 1921). M.A. (Cantab); asst. mast., Queen's Coll., Br. Guiana, 1934.
CAMILIERI, LUIGI A., LL.D.—B. 1892; matric., Malta Univ., 1907; LL.D., 1913; ed. for legis. assm., 1921; mag., 1924; judge, 1930.

CAMMELL, MR. BERNARD.—B. 1898; ed. Malvern Coll. and Royal Milly Acad., Woolwich, 1915; R. Arty., 1915-21; active serv. (France), 1916-18; asst. dist. comsnr., Nyassaland Prot., 1927-30; asst. treas., 1930-36; seconded, Somaliland Prot., 1936.

CAMP, ISAAC NEWTON, M.A. (Harvard).—B. 1890; sttlmt. offr., milly. serv., 1917-20; Br. rep., Es-salt, Trans-Jordan, 1920; dep. ch. br. rep., Trans Jordan, 1921; pres., demarcation commn., Beisan, 1922; admstve. offr. (seconded to sttlmt.), 1927; land sttlmt. offr., 1936.

CAMPBELL, ARCHIBALD, A.M.I. Mech.E.—B. 1891; ed. Royal Tech. Coll., Glasgow; N.B. Ely. Co., 1906-11; engrg. design dept., C.A., 1914; war serv., 1916-18; asst. engr., 1921; dep. ch. engr., (mech.), 1935.
CAMPBELL, COLIN BRUCE, B.A., M.C.—B. 1890; ed. Leatherhead Schol. and Lincoln Coll., Oxford; supt., educn. dept., N. Provs., Nigeria, May, 1921.

CAMPBELL, DAVID CALLENDER.—B. 1891; ed. Foyle Coll., Derry and Edin. Univ.; asst. sec., Tanganyika, Dec., 1919; asst. ch. sec., 1933; dep. ch. sec., Uganda, Feb., 1936; ag. ch. sec., July-Dec., 1937 and Mar.-Nov., 1938.
CAMPBELL, HAROLD ARTHUR, A.M.Inst.C.E.—B. 1891; war serv. Canadian Engrs., France, 1916-18; asst. engr., P.W.D., Kenya, 1919; do., Nigeria, 1922; do., Kenya, 1926; exec. engr., 1929.

CAMPBELL, JAMES EDWARD FRANCIS, C.B.E. (1937), M.A.—B. 1879; ed. Christ Church, Oxford; double 1st cls. hons.; called to bar, Middle Temple; Order of the Nile; served throughout great war; major, R. Artillery, 1916-18; ent. Palestine admtn., Mar., 1919; asst. dist. gov., Jerusalem-Jaffa dist., 1920; dist., comsnr., Jessusalem dist., 1932.

CAMPBELL, J. M., M.B., Ch. B. (Ed.), D.P.H. (Camb.), D.T.M. (Liv.) (Alan Milne med.).—B. 1897; med. offr., Kenya, Nov., 1923; senr. health offr., Tanganyika Territory, Nov., 1930; D.D.M.S., N. Rhodesia, 1938.

CAMPBELL, LACHLAN BAIN, M.C. (1918), A.M.I.C.E. (Lond.).—B. 1882; ed. Auckland Grammar Schol. and Canterbury Univ. Coll., N.Z.; 30 yrs' serv. in N.Z. pub. wks. dept.; comsnr., borough of Napier (during restoration period following earthquake), 1931-33; sec., marine, 1933.

CAMPBELL, OWEN NEIL.—B. 1834; ed. Auckland gram. schol. and Auckland univ. coll., N.Z.; ent. N.Z. lands and survey dept., 1901; served overseas as lieut., N.Z. Engrs., 1916-19; ch. drainage engr., lands dept., 1922; (dist.) comsnr. of crown lands, 1927; dep. under-sec., lands and survey dept., 1934; native trustees and under-sec., native dept., 1935.

CAMPBELL, S. McNEILL.—B. 1893; ed. Hamilton Acad., and Glasgow Univ.; M.A. (1st cls. hons. Classics) 1915; G.U.O.T.C., Sept., 1914; on military service, Jan., 1916 to Mar., 1919, with Cameronians (Scottish Rifles) 52nd Div. and R.A.F., Egypt and Palestine; lieu.t., July, 1917; capt. R.A.F., Nov., 1918; temporary olk., in C.O., Apr., 1919; apptd. by civ. serv. comsnr., 2nd cl. olk., C.O., Jan., 1920; asst. prin., 1st Apr., 1920; priv. sec. to perm. under sec. (Sir J. Masteron Smith), Apr., 1922 to Nov., 1923; ag. prin., 3rd Oct., 1923; prin., 1st Oct., 1926; sec., W. Indies Closer Union Comn., Nov., 1929-Mar., 1933.

CAMPIGLI, Lieut.-Col. GEORGE MURRAY (R. of O.), M.C., M.Inst.T.—B. 1890; ed. pvtly.; Vict. state rly., 1906-14; served in Australian Forces in Gallipoli and Palestine, 1914-18; ment. in desps.; capt. (staff capt.), May, 1917; dep. asst. dir., rly. traffic, 1918; asst. dir., ditto, 1919; offr.-in-ch., Palestine milly. rly., 1919; transf'd to civ. govt. as asst. gen. man. Palestine rly., 1920; lieut.-col., R. of O., July, 1921; ag. gen. man. in 1922, 1924, 1926-1927-28; mem., advisory bd. to Palestine govt. for commerce and industry, Mar., 1928.

CAMPION, Lieut.-Col. Sir WILLIAM ROBERT, K.C.M.G. (1924), D.S.O. (1917), T.D. (1914).—B. 1870; ed. Eton and New Coll., Oxford; lieut.-col., 4th Batt., R. Sussex Regt., 1915; col., 1924; M.I., Lewes divn., Sussex, 1910-24; gov., W. Australia, 1924-31.

CAMSHELL, CHARLES, C.M.G. (1935), R.A., B.Sc., LL.D., F.R.S.C., F.G.S.A.—B. 1876; ed., St. John's Coll., Winnipeg; grad., Man. Univ., 1894; post grad., geol., of Queen's Univ., Kingston, Harvard, and Massachusetts Inst. of Tech., Boston; geol., Algoma Cent. Rly., 1901; geol., Canadian Northern Rly., 1902-03; geol., geological surv. of Can., 1904; geol., investigations and explns. in Br. Columbia and N. Can., 1904-20; in charge of B.C. and Yukon br. of the geol. surv., 1918; dep. min., federal dept. of mines, 1920; apptd., Dom. fuel bd., 1921.

CANNON, HON. LAWRENCE ARTHUR DUMOLIN, B.A., LL.L., LL.D.—B. 1877; ed. Commercial Coll., Arthabaskaville, Que. (Can.), Quebec Seminary and Laval Univ.; gov.-genl.'s med., A.B., 1896; LL.L., 1899; LL.D., 1927; admitted to bar, 1899; K.C., 1910; M.L.A. (Que.), 1916 and 1919; mem. of coman. to revise and consolidate statutes of Quebec, 1923; batonnier, Quebec bar, 1924; hon. sec., Canadian Bar Asso., 1927; judge, King's bench (Que.), 1927; judge, sup. ct., Canada, 1930.
CANSDALE, GEORGE SOPER, B.A., B.Sc. (Oxon).—B. 1909; B.A. Forestry (with distinc.), 1932; B.Sc., 1934; asst. cons. of forests, Gold Coast, 1934.

CAPETOWN, ARCHBISHOP OF, THE MOST REV. FRANCIS ROBINSON PHELPS, D.D., Ed. Keble Coll. Oxford; B.A. 1886; M.A.; 1889; D.D., 1916; bishop of Grahamstown, 1915; archbishop of Capetown and metropolitan of the church of the province of S. Africa, 1931.
CAPNER, ARCHIBALD NORMAN, A.M.I. Mech.E.—B. 1891; ed. Chester Coll.; on war serv., 1914-19; mech. and elec. foreman, P.W.D., Palestine, Aug., 1922; elec. and mech. engr., P.W.D., Cyprus, July, 1928.
CARBERRY, JOHN EDWARD DOXTON, LL.B. (McGill).—B. 1893; served in Great War, 1915-19; called to bar, Middle Temple, 1925; olk. ots.

CHRISTOFFELSZ, ARTHUR ERIC, B.A., LL.B., Cantab., barrister-at-law (Grays Inn).—B. 1890; cadet, local div., Ceylon civ. serv., Mar., 1915; extra office asst., Jaffna kach., and pol. mag., Jaffna, Jan., 1917; office asst., Badulla kach., Mar., 1919; pol. mag., Dandagamuwa, Oct., 1920; ditto, Kurunegala, Sept., 1921; asst. sttlmt. offr., Oct., 1926; asst. sttlmt. offr., Dec., 1927; asst. govt. agt., Kegalla, June, 1931; do., Puttalam and Chilaw, Sept., 1933; agt. govt. agt., N.W.P., Mar., 1937.

raw_text: CHURCHILL, RIGHT HON. WINSTON LEONARD SPENCER, P.C. (1907).—M.P. for Oldham, 1900-6; for N.W. Manchester, 1906-8; for Dundee, 1908; late lieut. 4th Queen's Own Hussars; ed. Harrow, Sandhurst; entd. army, 1895; served with Spanish forces in Cuba, 1896 (1st cls. (Spanish) Order of Military Merit); served, attached 31st Punjab Infantry, with Malakand field force, 1897; present at operations in Bajaur, including actions of 16th and 30th Sept. (deepa. medal with clasp); served as orderly offr. to Sir W. Lockhart with Tirah exped. force, 1898 (clasp); served, attached 21st Lancers, with Nile exped. force, 1898; present at battle of Khartoum (medal with clasp); contested Oldham (C.) 1899; served as lieut. South Africa Light Horse; acted as correspdt., "Morning Post," South Africa, 1899-1900; taken prisoner, action 15th Nov., but escaped 12th Dec.; present at actions of Acton Homes, Venter's Spruit, Hussar Hill, Cingolo, Monte Cristo, and at battles of Spion Kop, Vaal Krantz, and Pieters; also operations round De-wetdorp, April, 1900; passage of Sand River, 15th May; engagements of Johannesburg and Diamond Hill, and capture of Pretoria (medal with six clasps); parly. under-sec. of state for the Colonies, Dec., 1905; visited Uganda and E. Africa, 1907; pres. of Bd. of T., 1908; Homese., 1910-11; first lord of the Admiralty, 1911; chancellor of the Duchy of Lancaster, 1915; min. of munitions, 1917; sec. of state, War Office and Air Ministry, Jan., 1919; S. of S. for the Colonies, 14th Feb., 1921 to 25th Oct., 1922; chancellor of the exchequer, 7th Nov., 1924; author of "The Story of the Malakand Field Force"; "The River War"; "Savrola"; "London to Ladysmith, via Pretoria"; "Ian Hamilton's March"; "Life of Lord Randolph Churchill."

(1900).—B. 1866; joined Perak service, 1883; passed in Malay, Feb., 1885; collr. land rev., Kuala Kangsar, Mar., 1885; on special service to Palang, 1887 (recd. thanks of S. of S.); ag. govt. agt. there, 1887-8; supr., Ulu Pahang, 1889; ag. Br. res. Pahang, 1890 to 1891, and from Feb., 1893; took a leading part in suppression of Pahang rebellion, 1892; sec. to govt., Selangor, Dec., 1894, but continued to act as res. of Pahang; visited Cocos-Keeling Is. as special comsnr., June, 1894; led armed exped. into native states of Tréngganu and Kelantan to effect capture of fugitive rebel leaders, Mar.-June, 1895 (recd. thanks S. of S.); res., Pahang, July, 1896; gov. of North Borneo and Labuan, 1899 to 1901; Br. res., Pahang, 1901; ag. col. sec., Trinidad and Tobago, Sept., 1903, confirmed, Sept., 1904; sent on deputation to B. Guiana in Nov., 1905, to inquire into municipal institutions; admtd. govt., Mar. to Aug., 1904, and from Apr. to Oct., 1906; col. sec., Ceylon, May, 1907; admtd. govt., on various occasions, 1907-12; gov., G. Coast, Dec., 1912; gov., Nigeria, July, 1919; gov. of Ceylon, Nov., 1925; gov. S. Stltnts. and high comsnr., Malay States, June, 1927; ret., 1929; concluded with M. Noufflard, the lieutenant-governor of Dahomey, in Aug., 1914 an agreement for the temporary partition between the British and French Goyta, and for the provisional administration of Togoland; admstd. Br. sphere of occupation from Aug., 1914, to Aug., 1919; chmn., comtes. on W. African trade and taxation, Oct.-Nov., 1921 (received thanks of S. of S.); is part author, with Sir Frank Swettenham, of a Malayan dictionary, and author of more than a dozen books, mostly on Malayan subjects, and of a translation of the penal code into Malay; also of a history of the G. Coast regt., in the E. African campaign.

CLUTTERBUCK, P.A., M.C.—B. 1897; schol. of Malvern Coll. and of Pembroke Coll., Cambridge, 1915; Coldstream Guards, 1916-19; capt. and adjt., 3rd batt., Oct., 1917; M.C. and ment. in desps., 1918; apptd. under reconstruction scheme, asst. prin., G.P.O., 1st Sept., 1919; transfd. to C.O., 13th Feb., 1922; sec., managing comtee, Imperial Bureau of Mycology, 1923-25; addnl. pte. sec. to parly. under sec. (Lord Arnold), June-Oct., 1924; sec., Milner-Lovat agrl. comtee., 1924-25; sec., Donoughmore coman. on Ceylon constitution and visited Ceylon, 1927-28; pte. sec. to perm. under sec. for Doms. affrs., 23rd Aug., 1928; prin., Doms. Office, 14th Nov., 1929; U.K. delegn. to L. of N. assembly, 1929, 1930 and 1931; negotiations with Egypt, 1930; reparations conferees., Lond., 1931 and Lausanne, 1933; sec., Newfoundland Royal Coman. and visited Newfoundland (twice) and Canada, 1933; further visit to Newfoundland, Sept.-Oct., 1938.

raw_text: CLUVER, COL. EUSTRACE HENRY, M.A., M.D., B.Ch., D.P.H.—B. 1894; ed. at Victoria Coll., Stellenbosch, S. Africa, Hartford Coll., Oxford and King's Coll. Hosp., London; Rhodes schol. for Stellenbosch; Burrey Yeo. schol., King's Coll.; senr. demy, Magdalen Coll., Oxford; 1st cls., final hons., nat. science (physiology); served with 1st S. African field ambulance in France; lecturer in geology, Victoria Coll., Stellenbosch, 1914; prof. of physiology, Univ. of Witwatersrand, Johannesburg, 1919-26; asst. health offr., Union of S. E. of O., with rank of maj., S.A.M.C., 1929; lieut.-col., R. of O., 1932; D.D.M.S. defence headqrs., 1933; D.M.S. with temp. rank of col., 1934; comdr., Order of Hosp. of St. John of Jerusalem, 1935; col., 1937; assoc. edr., Journal of Industrial Hygiene (America); published many papers in tech. jnl.s. on subject of physiology and hygiene.

M.C.—B. 1878; ed. privately; mem. of New Zealand parl. since 1911; min. of justice, 1919-20; P.M.G., 1919-25; min. of pub. wks., 1920-26; min., native affrs., 1921-1928; min. rly., 1923-1928; prime min., 1925-1928; min., external affrs., 1928; min., mines, 1928; min., agr., 1928; ret. from office, 1928; joined coalition miny., 1931; min., pub. wks., 1931-33; min., transport, 1931-35; min., finance, 1933-35; min., cust., 1933-35; N.Z. rep. at Imp. raw_text: CILLIERS, CHARL. ANDRIES, B.A.—B. 1893; ed. Victoria Coll., Stellenbosch (now Univ. of Stellenbosch); clk., defence dept., Union of S.A., 1913; pvt. sec. to min. of lands, 1916; ditto, to min. of defence, 1917; ditto, to min. of agr., 1921; under-sec. for pub. wks., 1934.

CLAIRMONTE, FREDERICK ARCHIBALD CONRAD.—B. 1886; ed. Harrison Coll., Codrington Coll., and Ontario Business Coll.; ent. civ. serv., Barbados, 1909; war serv., 1916-19; clk., pol. mag., cts., dist. "A," 1927; ag. pol. mag., various dists., on several occasions, 1928-31; comm., income tax, 1935.
CLEMMY, A.V., M.A., M.B., B.Ch. (Oxf.), M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P., M.M.S.A.—B. 1901; med. offr., Tanganyika Territory, July, 1928.

CLIPSHEAM, PHILIP RICHARD.—B. 1903; apptd. after compet. exam., cler. offr., C.O., 30th Sept., 1920; seconded as regis. on staff of high commr. in Canada for H.M.'s govt. in Gt. Britain, 1932; rtnd. to C.O. as cler. offr., higher grade, 4th Dec., 1937.

CLORTE, PHILIP ALBERT (B.Sc., Cape).—B. 1852; ed. S.A. Coll., Cape Town, S.A.; Schl. of Mines, Kimberley and Johannesburg; B.K.F., France, S.A. Heavy Arty., 1917-18; asst. inspr., mines, 1927; asst. gen. man., state alluvial mines, Namaqualand, 1929; inspr., mining leases, Union of S.A., 1934.

CLURIE, REGINALD MONTAGU.—B. 1891; ed. Clifton Coll. and Balliol Coll., Oxford; B.A. (hons. schl., jurisprudence); barrister-at-law, Inner Temple, 1915; served Great War, 1914-15; res. mag., Jamaica, Mar., 1932; crown coun., S. Stilts., 1937.
COBB, MAURICE LAKE, M.C., A.M.I.C.E.—B. 1894; ed. Tarlidge Schol. and Univ. Coll., Lond.; asst. engr. Nigerian rly. capital wks., 1921; do.; open lines, 1936; ch. engr., do., 1938.

COBON, HUGH HORACE, A.S. (Australasia), M.Q.I.S.—B. 1892; Aus. Imp. Focs., 1915-17; authd. survr., Queensland, 1920; survr., F.M.S. rlys., Nov., 1920; survr., Penang, Sept., 1921; do., Kedah, Feb., 1924; do., F.M.S., July, 1925; asst. supt., Johore, Feb., 1930; do., F.M.S., Oct., 1933; ag. supt., Johore, June, 1934; ag. asst. survr.-gen., F.M.S., Nov., 1937.

COCHRANE, EDGAR, M.B., Ch.B. (Glasgow), D.P.H. (1831).—B. 1897; on war serv., 1915-19; ag. dist. surg., Jesselton, Br. N. Borneo, 1925-26; asst. govt. med. offr. of health and dep. port health offr., Br. Guiana, 1932; med. offr., Gold Coast, 1936; senr. med. offr., Grenada, 1938.

COCKAYNE, ALFRED HYDE, L.S.O. (1837).—B. 1880; asst. biologist, N.Z. agrl. dept., 1904; biologist, 1909; dir., fields div., 1923, also dir., plant research st., 1927; asst. dir. gen., agr., 1929; dir.-gen., 1936.

COCKMAN, HERBERT JAMES, D.F.C.—B. 1898; ed. St. Paul's Schol. and Merton Coll., Oxford; cadet, F.M.S., Dec., 1921; attd., Fedl. secrt., Jan., 1922; offr. cls. V, Dec., 1924; offr. cls. IV, dist. offr., Pekan, Dec., 1928; offr. cls. III, dist. offr., Lipis, Dec., 1933; ch. asst. dist. offr., Kinta, Nov., 1934.

COCKRAM, BEN.—B. 1903; ed. Victoria, Taunton's Schol. and Oxford; hist. schol., Queen's Coll., 1921-25; 1st cls., mod. hist., 1925; Goldsmith's sen. student, 1926; 1st cls., med. and mod. hist., London, 1923; fellow, Michigan, 1925-26; Ph.D., 1926; apptd. after compet. exam. asst. prin., Dominions Office, 4th Nov., 1926; pte. sec. to the party, under sec. for dom. affrs. (Mr. Lunn), 1st Oct., 1930; pte. sec. to perm. under sec. of state for Dominion affrs., 22nd June, 1931.; prin., 21st Nov., 1934.

COCKS, HUGH CROLL, B.A. (Oxon.).—B. 1904; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Dec., 1927; attd. Colombo kach., Jan., 1928; attd. secretariat, May, 1928; office asst., Nuwara Eliya kach., Jan., 1929; office asst. to govt. agt., Uva, May, 1930; asst. attit. offr., June, 1930; asst. govt. agt., Kurunegala, Mar., 1936; do., Kandy, Sept., 1937.

COE, THOMAS PEROWNE, M.C.—B. 1887; ed. King Edward VI Schol., Norwich and Brasenose Coll., Oxford; (schol.) 2nd cls. clas. mods., 1908, 3rd cls. lit. hum., 1910; R.A., 1910; cadet, F.M.S., Nov., 1910; passed cadet, Dec., 1912; offr., cls. V, Dec., 1913; on war serv. with Norfolk Regt., 1915-18; offr., cls. IV, Jan., 1919; demob., Feb., 1919; 2nd asst. sec., Fed. secretariat, Nov., 1919; ag. priv. sec. to ch. sec. in add., Nov., 1919 to June, 1920; 2nd-lent. M.S.V.R., Apr., 1921; ag. capt., Nov., 1921; capt., Aug., 1922; supernmy. offr., cls. III, Nov., 1922; major, M.S.V.R., Jan., 1923; dist. offr., July, 1924; offr., cls. II., Nov., 1927; sec. to res., Perak; asst. adviser, Kelantan, Sept., 1928; ag. gen. advr., Johore, Mar., 1930; offr. cls. I B; asst. adviser, Muar, Johore, Feb., 1930; ag. Br. advr., Kelantar, May, 1931; ag. sec., postal affrs., S.S. and F.M.S., Mar., 1933; ag. dir.-gen., posts and tels., Malaya, Jan., 1934; offr. cls. I A., Jan., 1936.
COGHILLAN, B.A., M.D., B.Ch., B.A.O. (Dub.), D.T.M. (Liv.).—B. 1899; I.F.S., A.M.C., 1922-23; med. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Apr., 1926.

COHEN, ANDREW BENJAMIN.—B. 1909; ed. Malvern and Trinity Coll., Cambridge: 1st cls., 1st pt., class. tripods, 1st cls., 2nd pt., class. tripods; apptd. after compet. exam., asst. prin., inland rev., Oct., 1932; transf. to C.O., 2nd Oct., 1933; ag. prin., 14th Dec., 1937.

COLK, ARNOLD KIELTAND.—B. 1884; sec. to bd. of educn., Bahamas, 1907; ag. recs. justice, Rum Cay, 1908; ag. inspr. and genl. supt. of schools, 1910, 1911, 1913; 3rd cls., treas., dept., 1913; 1st cls., P.O., 1914; ch. cls. and asst. examr. offr., cust. dept., Aug., 1921; ag. contrlr., cust., in 1922, 1923, 1924 and 1925; asm. contr., and examining offr., Dec., 1923; surr., shipping, Mat., 1925; ag. contr. cust. and regir., shipping from Apr., 1926; ag. harbour., Mar. to Apr., 1927; comtr., cust., Sept., 1928; M.L.C., Dec., 1931; del., W. Indies cust. confce., Bermuda, 1934.

COLEBATCH, HON. SIR HAL PATENHALL, KT. BACH. (1927).—B. 1872; ed. Goolwa, S. Australia; M.L.C. for E. Prov., W. Australia, May, 1912; col. sec., min. for educn., min. of pub. health and leader of legis. coun., 1916-19; premier, W. Australia for short period, 1919; min. of educn. and pub. health, 17th May, 1919; agt.-gen. for W. Australia, 1923-27; mem., Australian constitution royal comm., 1927-29; C'wealth senator for W. Australia, 1929-33; agt.-gen. for W.A., since 1933.

COLEMAN, EPHEMIA HERBERT, K.C.—B. 1890; ed. Pub. and High Schls., Arnprior and Carleton Place, Ont., and Univ. of Manitoba; sec.-treas., Canadian Bar Assoc., 1919-33; dean, Man. Law Sch., 1929-33; bencher, Law Soc. of Man., 1928-33; under-sect. of state, Canada, 1933.

COLEMAN-DOSCAS, APOLLO ERNEST, Dip. Agr., W.A. Scotch Coll., Claremont, W.A.; Univ. of W.A.—B. 1893; asst. agrl. inspr., F.M.S., July, 1920; inspr. agr., Johore, Aug., 1922; A.F.O., Malacca, Apr., 1925; do., F.M.S. (Perak South), Jan., 1926; ag. agr. offr., Perak, Sept., 1931; A.F.O., N. Sembilan, Sept., 1933; state agrl. offr., Johore, Feb., 1935; ag. ch. field offr., July, 1938.

COLEY, CAPT. C. F. (Reg. Army, R. of O.)—B. 1897; ed. Oxford Univ. and Université de Paris; served with Duke of Cornwall's Light Infy., Devon Regt., and R. Corps of Signals in European War, 1914-18; diplomatic serv., 1918; cadet, Nigerian admstive serv., Mar., 1926; ag. asst. dist. offr., Apr., 1926.

COLLIER, C. W. H.—B. 1892; ed. Queen's Coll., Br. Guiana; cler. asst., immigrn. dept., Br. Guiana, 1909; sec. to various col. comtees, and gradually promoted to 1st cls. offr., 1920; immigrn. agt., 1926; sec., colonization (500 family scheme) comtes., 1927; senr. immigrn. agt. and immigrn. agt., Demerara county, 1928; ag. immigrn. agt., 1929; senr. clsk., col. sec.'s office, 1930; prin. clsk. and clsk., exec. and leg. couns., 1932; asst. col. sec., 1934.

COLLIER, HON. PHILIP.—B. 1873; M.L.A. for Boulder, W. Australia, since 1905; min. for mines and rlys. in Scaddan govt., 1911-14; and of mines and water supply, 1914-16; leader, parly. labour party, May, 1917; premier, W. Australia, 1924-1930 and 1933-36; resigned from ministry, 1936.

COLLINS, CHARLES HENRY, B.A., Lond.—B. 1887; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Nov., 1910; pol. mag., Puttalam, Sept., 1912; office ast., Kandy kachcheri, July, 1913; ast. land settm. offr., May, 1915; ast. land settm. offr., Sept., 1915; ag. ast. govt. ast., Puttalam, kach., Sept., 1916; office ast., Colombo, kach., Oct., 1916; 2nd ast. col. sec., Oct., 1917; 3rd ast. col. sec., Sept., 1920; 2nd ast. col. sec., Jan., 1922; prin. ast. col. sec., Feb., 1924; addnl. ast. col. sec., Oct., 1925; dep. collr. cust., Dec., 1926; ag. dep. ch. sec., Dec., 1932; govt. agt., Sabaragamuwa, Feb., 1933; dep. finan. secr., Feb., 1934; offr. cls. I., grade I., 1935; ag. finan. sec. in 1935, 1936 and 1937.

COLLINS, F.—B. 1882; elk., Bri. tel. serv., 1889; elk., Cape post and tel. serv., 1900; engr. ast., 1911; inspr., traffic, 1912; traffic supt., 1920; chf. supt., 1923; chf. elk., 1925; contr. teis. and telephones, 1933; under sec., iels., 1936.

COLLINS, FRANCIS RICHARD.—B. 1896; war serv. 1915-20; Inns of Court O.T.C. and 2nd Bn. The Dorsetshire Regt. served in India, Palestine, Syria, Egypt and the Sudan; Col. Audit Dept., 1926; ast. audr., Kenya, 1926; C.A.D. cent. office Lond., 1933; senr. ast. audr., G. Coast, 1937.
COLLINS, JOHN VICTOR, M.A., Sc.M. (Dublin), F.I.C.—B. 1897; ast. govt. analyst, Ceylon, Jan., 1925; dep. govt. analyst, Oct., 1927; govt. analyst, May, 1934.

COLLINS, W. H., Ph. D., F.R.S.C.—B. 1878; ed. Univ. of Toronto, 1904; Heidelberg Univ., 1907; Chicago Univ., 1909; Univ. of Wisconsin, 1911; ast. geologist, geol. surv. of Can., 1905; jun. geol., 1912; geol., 1914; fellow, Royal Soc. of Can., 1918; dir., geol. surv. of Can., 1920; ex-off. mem., Can. Inst. Min. Met.; mem., Geol. Soc. of America, 1920; has pub. numerous papers and memoirs on pre-cambrian geol. and petrology.

COLLINS, COL. COMMDT. THE HON., WILLIAM RICHARD, D.T.D., D.S.O.—B. 1876; ed. Pretoria Staats Gymnasium; joined Repub. civ. serv., 1894; Anglo-Boer War, 1899-1902; practised at side bar, Ermelo, 1902-38; mem., Ermelo, Transvaal parl., 1907-10; Union parl., 1916-38; Wakkerstroom, 1938; serv. during Rebellion and German S.W. Africa, 1914-15; min. of agr. and forestry, Nov., 1938.

COLLYMORE, ERNEST ALLAN, M.A. (Oxon.), K.C. (1933)—B. 1893; ed. Harrison Coll., Barbados and Merton Coll., Oxford; 2nd cls. hon. final schl. of jurisprudence, 1914; barrister-at-law, Inner Temple, 1920; serv. in E. Lancs. Regt., 1914-19; capt., Oct., 1915; capt., Barbados Vols. and O.C., cadet corps of the island, 1921-26; sen. mag., Bridgetown and dist. A, June, 1925; recvr. of wrecks, Aug., 1925; ag. judge, ast. ct. of appeal in 1926 and 1928; stip. and circuit mag., Bahamas and coroner for New Providence, July, 1927; ag. atty.-gen. in 1927 and 1928; atty.-gen., Barbados, June, 1928; ch. just., July, 1936.

COLTON, WILLIAM DOUGLAS.—B. 1900; ed. Cheltenham Coll.; treasy. elk. N. Rhodesia, Mar., 1927; ast. treas., Apr., 1930; ag. dep. treas., Sept., 1937; dep. ast. gen., Jan., 1938; ag. ast. gen., Jan., 1938.

COMBE, SIR RALPH MOLINEUX, KT. BACH. (1920). Ed. at Haileybury Coll. and Exeter Coll., Oxford; B.A., 1894; called to the bar, Inner Tem., 1897; Crown advocate, E. Africa Prot. 4th Aug., 1905; mem. of exec. and legis. couns., 1907; atty.-gen. E.A.P., 1912; atty.-gen., Nigeria, 1913; ch. just., Nigeria, 1918; ret., Mar., 1929.

COMMISSIONG, THOMAS BERTRAND.—B. 1893; 3rd elk., col. sec.'s office, Grenada, Apr., 1909; 2nd elk., gov't's office, June, 1915; 2nd elk., col. sec.'s office, Dec., 1919; ag. ch. elk., col. sec.'s office, elk. to exec. coun., med. regis. and man., govt. printing dept., May, 1921; ast. col. sec., Grenada, Jan., 1928.
CONEY, JAMES SHARPE.—B. 1884; G.W.R. 1900-03; Cape govt. rlys., 1903-10; S. African rlys. & harbrs., 1910-27; supt. (admistr.), K.U.R. Rlys. and H., 1927; ch. ast. to gen. man., 1929.

CONIBEAR, JAMES HANFORD.—Reg. army, 1897-1919; sub.-inpr., pol. and keeper, prisons, St. Lucia, Nov., 1920; ag. ch., pol. and impr. prisons, 1922; prison supt., Gold Coast, Jan., 1924; lieut., G.C. Volunteers, Oct., 1924; ag. adjt., 1924-26; capt., G.C. Defence Force, Mar., 1933; supt., grade I., Sept., 1934; senr. prison supt., Jan., 1935; ag. dir., prisons, June-Sept., 1935.
CONN, ALEXANDER ABERDEIN, M.I.Mar.E., A.M.I. Mech.E.—B. 1895; appren. and marine engr., 1911-24; 2nd engr., K.U.R. & H., 1924; engr.-in-charge, 1932; senr. marine engr., 1933.
CONNELL, W. K., M.B., Ch.B. (Glas.), F.R.C.S. (Eng.), D.T.M. & H. (Lond.).—B. 1893; mily. serv., R.A.M.C., 1916-20; med. offr., Tanganyika Territory, July, 1924; senr. surg. specialist, 1934.

CONNOLLY, HON. SIR JAMES DANIEL, KT. BACH. (1920).—B. 1870; ed. Warwick Pub. Schl. and Catholic Schl.; M.L.C., Western Australia, 1902; col. sec. and min. of com. and lab., 1906; col. sec. and min. of pub. health, 1910; leader of leg. coun. from 1906 to 1911; M.L.A. for Perth, 1914; agt. gen. in London for Western Australia, 1917-23; agt. gen. in London for Malta, 1930.

CONRADIE, DAVID GIDEON, B.A., LL.D.—B. 1879; ed. Prince Alfred Hamlet and Cores Schls., Stellenbosch and Trinity Coll., Dublin; sen. mod. and gold medal, LL.D., 1906; advoc., Cape Town and Bloemfontein, 1907-09; sec., educn. dept., O.F.S., 1909-15; atty., Reitz and Bloemfontein, 1915-33; mem., prov. and prov. exec., 1920-26; M.P., Bethlehem and Lindley, 1926-33; admstr., S. W. Africa, 1933.

CONRADIE, JOHANNES HENDRIK.—B. 1872; ed. Katzidzorp, Robertson, ptye. study, later Stellenbosch; schlmaster., Christiana, 1889; Boshof, 1890-95 and Grey Coll. Schl., 1896-1900; admitted as atty.-at-law, 1905; finan. sec., Dutch Reformed Ch., Cape Prov., 1905-12; supt., Kakamas lab. col., 1912-1920; organising secr., Nationalist Party, Cape Prov., 1921-29; mem., leg. assm. for Gordonia, 1924-29; admstr., Cape Prov., Sept., 1929.

CONWAY, WILLIAM KWAN.—B. 1893; ed. High Schl., Dundee; govt. travelling schl. master, 10th Dec., 1913; 2nd elk., C.S.O., Mar., 1916; ag. elk. and priv. sec. to govt. of Falkland Is., June, 1916 to Apr., 1917 and Sept., 1917 to Apr., 1920; collr., cust., C. Coast, Oct., 1920; senr. collr., cust., June, 1928; ast. comptlr., customs, 1938.
COOK, James, L.R.C.P. & S. (Edin.), L.R.F.P. & S. (Glas.), F.R.C.S. (Edin.), D.P.H., Durham—B. 1904; med. asst., col. hosp., Port of Spain, Trinidad, Oct., 1936.

COOK, Rt. Hon. Sir Joseph, P.C. (1914); G.C.M.G. (1918)—B. 1860; mem. of H. R., Commonwealth of Australia, since 1901; postmr.-gen., N.S. Wales, 1894-1898; min. for mines and agric., 1898-9; min. of defence, C. of A., June, 1909; prime minister, C. of A., June, 1913-Sept., 1914; min. for the Navy, 1917; ditto, from 10th Jan., 1918; high commr. in London for C. of A., Nov., 1921; ret., 1927.
COOKE, Edgar Henry.—B. 1889; war serv., 1916-17; probr., N. Rhodesia, 1918; asst. nat. commr., 1919; nat. commr., 1924; asst. mag., 1928; dist. commr., 1933; ag. prov. commr., 1936 and 1938.
COOKE, Eleanor Margaret.—B. 1891; matron, Achimota Coll., Gold Coast, May, 1928.

COOKE, William Ernest, M.A., F.R.A.S.—Cadet, Adelaide observatory, 1878; B.A. Adel. Univ., 1883; ast. astronomer, Adel. obser., 1884; M.A., Adel. Univ., 1889; govt. astronomer, W.A., 1896; govt. astronomer, New South Wales, 1912-26; prof. of astronomy at Sydney Univ.

COOLS-LARTIGUE, Alexander Raphael—B. 1899; ed. at St. Mary's Coll., St. Lucia, and Dominica Grammar Schl.; called to bar, Gray's Inn, 1924; admitted to bar, Leeward Is., 1924; practised at bar, 1924-35; ag. crown atty. and mag., dist. "F," and mem. exec. and leg. couns., Dominica, Oct. 1931 to Jan. 1932; ag. crown atty., Dominica, and mem. exec. and leg. couns., in 1933-4-5; visiting just. of prisons and J.P., 1934; crown atty., and mag., dist. "E," and mem. exec. and leg. couns., Dominica, Jan., 1935; commr., sup. ct., Leeward Is., June, 1935; ag. atty.-gen., Dec., 1936-Feb., 1937; ag. admstr., Dominica, Nov., 1937 and Mar., 1938; atty.-gen., St. Lucia, Apr., 1938.

COOLS-LARTIGUE, Louis.—B. 1903; ed. Dominica Grammar Schl.; ast. mast., Dominica Grammar Schl., Mar., 1923-Aug., 1924; 2nd clk., admr's. office, Dec., 1924; junr. clk., do., Aug., 1927; senr. clk., reglar's. office, Apr., 1929; prin. admr's. office as ch. clk. and clk. of couns., etc., June, 1932.

COOLS-LARTIGUE, Theodore.—B. 1867; ed. at Mount Saint Mary's Coll., Derbyshire; called to the bar, Gray's Inn, 1889; admitted to the bar, St. Lucia, 1889; ditto, Leeward Is., 1894; ag. reglar., St. Lucia, 1891; ag. mag. in 1903-4-5; ag. reglar., St. Lucia, 1906-7; ag. mag., 1911; elective mem., Castries town bd., 1903-1912; reglar. and prov. marshal, Dominica, and also supplementary mag., Roseau, Dominica, Feb., 1912; offl. mem. leg. coun., Dominica, visiting just. of Dominica prison and J.P., 1912; govt. mem., Roseau town bd., 1914; valuation commr. for assessing of taxes, 1916; ag. crown atty. and mem. exec. coun., 1916-19; crown atty. and reglar., sup. ct., Oct., 1919; ret., 1929; re-apptd. ag. addnl. mag., dist. E, 1930.

COOMARASWAMY, Chinnappah.—B. 1887; ed. Hindu Coll., Jaffna, and Royal Coll., Colombo; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., April, 1910; extra office ast., Batticaloa kach., May, 1912; pol. mag., Matara, May, 1913; do., Puttalam, June, 1913; do., Chilaw, Feb., 1914; do., Point Pedro, June, 1915; eg. dist. judge, Batticaloa, Feb., 1918; ditto, Chilaw and Puttalam, Mar., 1920; addnl. ast. govt. agt., Chilaw and Puttalam, in addn., Aug., 1920; commr. of requests, Colombo, May, 1922; dep. comsnr., stamps, May, 1925; dist. judge, Kalutara, Apr., 1926; dep. comsnr., stamps, May, 1926; reglar., motor cars, Jan., 1928; ag. reglar.-gen., Sept., 1931; dist. judge, Kurunegala, June, 1932; do., Jaffna, Oct., 1933.

COOMARASWAMY, Veluppillai.—B. 1892; Cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Mar., 1913; ag. addnl. office ast., Anuradhapura Kach., Nov., 1913; ag. pol. mag., Puttalam, Feb., 1914; pol. mag., Negombo, July, 1917; ditto, Dandagamuwa, July, 1918; office ast. to govt. agt., S. Prov., Feb., 1919; pol. mag., Panadura, Oct., 1920; pol. mag., W. P., July, 1923; dist. judge, Kegalla, June, 1924; ast. govt. agt., Hambantota, July, 1927; ast. govt. agt., Puttalam and Chilaw, Jan., 1931; clk. state coun. and sec., bd. of ministers, July, 1932; offr., cls. I, grade II, Jan., 1933; reglar.-gen. and dir., comm. intelligence, Apr., 1936; ag. commr. of lands, Oct., 1937; ag. conserv., forests, Jan., 1938.

COOMBES, H. P.—B. 1894; olk., govt. sec's. office, Nyassaland, May, 1917; senr. clk., Aug., 1923; ag. ast. sec. and clk., exec. and leg. couns., Dec., 1926 to Mar., 1927; ag. senr. ast. sec. and clk., exec. and leg. couns., Apr. to Dec., 1927; ag. office supt., secretariat and clk., exec. and leg. couns., in 1929 and 1930; office supt., secretariat, Apr., 1931; clk., exec. and leg. couns., Apr., 1931 to Dec., 1933.

COOPE, Arthur Egerton.—B. 1888; ed. Fonthill Schl., St. Edward's Schl., and Keble Coll., Oxford; B.A. Oxon.; cadet, F.M.S., Nov., 1911; passed cadet, June, 1913; offr., cls. V, Dec., 1914; ast. dist. offr., May, 1915; offr., cls. IV, Jan., 1919; ast. dist. offr., Apr., 1919; dist. offr., Mar., 1921; offr., cls. III, Nov., 1923; offg. ast. advr. lands, Kodiah, May, 1924; town planning admr. and state valuer, Ipoh, Oct., 1928; offr., cls. II, Nov., 1928; ag. ast. dist. offr., Ipoh, in addn., Apr. to May, 1929; ag. mag., Seremban, Sept., 1929; ag. chmn., sany. bd. in addn., ast. adviser, Muar, Johore, Mar., 1930; mag. K. Lumpur, July, 1931; offr., cls. I.B, Nov., 1931; ag. legal advr. and judl. commr., Kelantan, Mar., 1933; commr., trade and cust., Johore, Dec., 1934; contr. rubber, Malaya, Mar., 1935; ag. br. advr., Trengganu, Dec., 1937.

COOPER, Evans Gustavus.—B. 1875; ast. clk. of courts, Jamaica, 1893; called to the bar, Gray's Inn 1912; clerk of cts., Hanover, Dec., 1912; J.P., 1913; ag. res. mag. on various occasions; supernny. res. mag., June, 1923; res. mag., June, 1924.

COOPER, Comdr. Guy Edward, R.N. (ret.),—B. 1891; ed. R.N.; Great War, ment. in despatches; called to bar, Inner Temple, 1923; legal adviser, Seychelles, Apr., 1928; ag. ch. just., May to Nov., 1928 and Nov., 1930 to May, 1932; pol. mag., Gold Coast, Nov., 1932; title changed to dist. mag., 1935; attd. legal advry. staff, C.O., July-Aug., 1936.

COOPER, William Gerald Groves, Ph.D. (Lond.), B.Sc. (Lond.), A.R.C.Sc., F.G.S., A.Inst. M.M.—B. 1890; ed. Queen Elizabeth's Grammar Schl., Crediton, Devon, Battersea Polytechnic Inst. and Imp. Coll. of Sc. and Technology, 1911-14; res. engnr. on location and constrn. of Canadian Northern Ontario Rly., 1911-14; war serv., 1914-18; geologist, Gold Coast geol. survey, Nov., 1919; ast. dir., geol. surv., 1937.

COORAY, Edmund Joseph, B.A., LL.B. (Lond.), Barrister-at-Law, Lincoln's Inn.—B. 1907; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Oct., 1931; astd. Colombo kach., Nov., 1931; office ast., Ratnapura kach., Mar., 1934; do., Mannar and... Mullaitivu kach., Mar., 1935; do., Kegalla, kach., Nov., 1935; addt. dept. of co-op. socs., Oct., 1936; ag. dep. regis., Mar., 1938.

CORBELL, ROBERT LESLIE, B.Sc., M.R.C.V.S.—B. 1895; ed. Ipswich Schl. and Royal Vety. Coll., London; demonstr. of path. and bact., Royal Vety. Coll., 1923; res. asst., Inst. of Animal Path., Camb., 1924; R.A.V.O., 1915; srgt. dresser, transport corps for serv. in Palestine, 1919; vety. res. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Feb., 1928; seconded to Siam, 1937.

CORBETT, ALAN FREDERICK.—B. 1878; ed. K.H.S., Coventry, and St. John's Coll., Oxford; B.A., 1902; barrister-at-law (Inner Temple), 1903; advoc., sup. ct., Cape Colony, 1904; sup. ct. (T.P.D.) Union of S.A., 1919; clk., res. mag., Worcester, July, 1903; clk., mast. sup. ct., Cape, Jan., 1905; clk., res. mag., Beaufort West, Dec., 1905; clk., inland comnr., Kimberley, May, 1906; clk., inland rev. dept., Apr., 1911; ag. recr. of rev., Germiston, Oct., 1915; senr. clk., inland rev. dept., Nov., 1916; prin. clk., inland rev. dept., Jan., 1921; ast. comnr. for inland rev., Apr., 1929; comnr. for inland rev., Sept., 1929.

CORBETT, GEORGE HAMBLIN, B.Sc., F.R.E.S.—B. 1887; ed. King Edwards Schls., Bath, and Edin.; Cornell and Harvard Univs.; govt. entomologist, F.M.S., Apr., 1920; deleg. of F.M.S. and S.S. govts. at 2nd Imp. Entomological Conf., London, June, 1925; consltg. entomologist, Inst. Med. Resch., and Rubber Resch. Inst., in addn., June, 1930-Aug., 1932 and Mar., 1935.

CORDEAUX, MAJOR SIR HARRY EDWARD SPILLER, C.M.G. (1921), C.B. (1904), C.M.G. (1902).—Ed. at Cheltenham Coll., and St. John's Coll., Camb.; minor scholarship, 1889; class. tripso., 1892; passed in Hindustani and Persian, 1895; Somali, 1899; B.A., 1892; entd. Indian Army, 1894; probationer, Bombay polit. dept. as 4th ast. polit. res., Aden, Apr., 1895; ast. res., Berbera, July, 1898; vice-consul, Berbera, Oct., 1898; consul, Nov., 1902; admtd. govt. of Somaliland Prot., in 1900, 1902, 1903 and 1904; served in operations against the Somali Mullah 1902-04, as polit. offr. (ment. in desps., medal and 3 clasps); dep. comnr., Somaliland Prot., 1904; admtd. govt., June, 1905; comnr. and comdr.-in-chief, 15th May, 1906; gov., Uganda, 1st Feb., 1910; gov., St. Helena, 22nd Nov., 1911; assumed govt., 21st Feb., 1912; gov., Bahamas, 1920; ret. 1926.

CORDOUMAN, JEAN ALBERT.—B. 1887; clk., cust., Durban, 1904; Pretoria, 1911; 1st grade clk., 1916; senr. clk., 1919; prin. clk., 1921; acot., 1922; ch. clk., cust. and excise, Pretoria, 1930; ch. inap., cust. and excise, 1933; dep. comnr., 1933.

COREA, CARLTON VICTOR DEMETRIUS SENEWIRATNE.—B. 1903; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Feb., 1927; attd., Matara kach., Mar., 1927; ag. office asst., Kegalle kach., Nov., 1928; pol. mag., Pt. Pedro, Mar., 1930; office asst., Galle kach., Nov., 1931; ast. govt. agt., Badulla, June, 1933; ast. settlmt. offr., Dec., 1934.
CORNELL, CHARLES ALBERT.—Called to bar, Gray's Inn, 1928; ast. dist. comnr., Kenya, Feb., 1921; ag. dist. comnr., 1925; dist. comnr., 1927.

CORNEY, LEONARD GEORGE.—B. 1886; B.A. Cantab., class. exhibtn., St. John's Coll., Camb.; class. tripso., 1908; examnr., col. audit branch of E. and A. Dept., Feb., 1910; ast. audr., Gold Coast, Aug., 1910; ag. audr., July, 1913, July to Dec., 1914, and Apl. to Oct., 1916; ag. ast. col. sec., Dec., 1916 to Jan., 1917; seconded to G. Coast Regt., Apl., 1917 to Jan., 1918; senr. ast. audr., Jan., 1919; ag. audr., Jan. to Oct., 1919; dep. audr., Jan., 1920; ast. dir. cent. office (Col. Audit Dept.), Feb., 1930; dep. audr., S.S. and F.M.S., Dec., 1931; ag. audr., S.S. and F.M.S., Apr., 1932; audr., do., 1936.

CORNISH, HENRY HAVLOCK, K.C., M.A. (double 1st cls. hons.), LL.M.—B. 1887; ed. Kaitangata Schl., Otago Boys' High Schl., Otago Univ. and Victoria Univ. Coll., N.Z.; pvt. legal practice many years; pres., Wellington dist. law socty., 1926; mem., N.Z. law socty.; coun. of law reporting, rules comttee, etc.; prof., English and N.Z. law, Victoria Univ. Coll., 1930-34; solr.-gen., N.Z., 1934.

CORNWALLIS, COL. SIR K., K.B.E. (1929), C.M.G. (1926), C.B.E., Mily. (1919), D.S.O.(1917), Commander of the Order of King George I. of Greece, Order of Nahdhah (2nd cls.), Order of the Nile (3rd cls.), Order of Majidie (4th cls.), three times mentioned in desps.—Sudan civ. serv., 1906-14; Egyptian civ. serv., 1914; dir., Arab Bureau, Cairo, 1916-19; dep. ch. polit. offr., Egyptian Expedy. Force, 1918; ast. ch. polit. offr., ditto, 1919; attd. to Amir Faisal in London, 1919; attd. to F.O., 1920-21; accompanied Amir Faisal to 'Iraq and seconded to 'Iraq govt. for two years, June, 1921; adviser to min. of interior, 'Iraq, September, 1921.

CORRIE, OWEN CECIL KIRKPATRICK, M.C.—B. 1882; ed. Monckton Combe Schl. and Trinity Coll., Cambridge (maths. exhib.); on active serv. in France, Belgium and Palestine with N. Somerset Yeomanry and R.F.A. (T.F.), 1916-19; M.C. and ment. in desps.; judl. offr., Nablus, O.E.T.A. (S.), Mar., 1919; vice-pres., ct. of appeal, O.E.T.A. (S.), Oct., 1919; vice-pres., ct. of appeal, Palestine, July, 1920; senr. Br. judge, sup. ct., Sept., 1924; ag. ch. just. on various occasions, 1922-26; ch. just., Fiji and ch. judl. comnr., W. Pacific, 1936.

CORRY, WILFRED CHARLES STEWART, B.A. (Oxon).—B. 1900; ed. Cheltenham Coll. and Wadham Coll., Oxford; cadet, F.M.S., Dec., 1923; attchd. secr., Taiping, Jan., 1924; attchd. land office, J. Bahru, Apr., 1924; passed cadet, Jan., 1926; offr. cls. V, Dec., 1926; ast. collr., land rev., K. Lumpor, Mar., 1927; offr. cls. IV, Dec., 1930; pr. sec. to govr. S.S. and high comnr., Malay States, July, 1931; offr. cls. III, Dec., 1935; ag. 1st ast. sec., S.S., July, 1936; ch. ast. dist. offr., Kinta, Nov., 1937.

CORSEK, LIEUT. E. E.—Ed. Shrewsbury; 2nd lieut., 4th Batt. King's Shropshire Light Infantry (T.), Aug., 1914; lieut., June, 1916; served in France, 1917-18; ast. dist. comnr., G. Coast, 3rd Dec., 1919.

CORSON, JAMES FREDERICK, O.B.E. (1935), M.B.E., M.D., Ch.B. (Manch.), D.P.H., D.T.M. & H. (Cantab.).—B. 1878; W.A.M.S. (Gold Coast), Jan., 1913; pathologist, Dec., 1920; Nigeria, May, 1923; Tanganyika Territory, 1925.

COSTAR, NORMAN EDGAR.—B. 1909; ed. Battersea Grammar Schl. and Jesus Coll., Cambridge; hist. schol., 1928-32; cls. I divn. 1, hist. tripso., pts. I and II; B.A., 1931; Kellen prize-man, 1931; apptd. after compet. exam., ast. prin., C.O., Apr., 1932; pvt. sec. to permitt. under sec., D.O. (Sir Ed. Harding), 21st Oct., 1935.
COSTLEY-WHITE, C. G.—B. 1913; ed. Eton and Balliol Coll., Oxford; 2nd cls. mod. greats, 1936; apptd. after compet. exam., ass't prim., C.O., 4th Oct., 1937.

COTCHING, HOUGHTON ALDGATE, A.M.I.Mech.E.—B. 1887; serv. in mech. dept., N.E. Ry. Coy., 1903-15; on mil. serv., 1915; seconded for duty with R.E. (inland waterways and docks), 1917; major, D.A.D.R.T. and sent to Chemin de Fer du Hedjar as loco. supt., 1919; ch. mech. engr., Palestine rly., 1920.

COULSON, NORMAN.—B. 1891; ed. Charterhouse and Pembroke Coll., Camb.; (M.A., hons. in cls. and mod. hist.); 2nd lieut., 10th Res. Regt., Cav., Sept., 1914; att'd. 8th (K.R.I.) Hussars, Sept., 1915; (France and army of occupation); cadet F.M.S., Apr., 1920; passed cadet, June 1922; att'd. to treasy., Penang on sp. duty, Sept., 1922; dist. offr., Dinlang, Apr., 1923; dist. offr., Raub, Feb., 1923; seconded for serv. in Kelantan, Aug., 1926; offr., cls. III., Feb., 1928, supt., lands, Kelantan, Apr., 1928; mag., Seremban, Mar., 1930; ag. sec. to res., Selangor, June, 1931; offr., cls. II., Feb., 1933; ass't treas., Penang, Apr., 1934; dist. offr., Seremban, June, 1937.

COURT, PHILIP FRANCIS SHELSEY, F.R.C.S. (Ed.), M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.)—B. 1900; med. offr., med. dept., Hong Kong; med. offr. in ch., Kowloon and New Territories, Oct., 1930; ditto, Victoria Hosp., May, 1932; ass't med. offr., Victoria Hosp., May, 1932; med. offr. in ch., Kowloon Hosp., Mar., 1933; do., Victoria hosp., Feb., 1936; M.O., Queen Mary Hosp., June, 1937.

COUSIN, D. P.—B. 1889; Brunel medallist, London Schl. of Economics, 1913; war serv., 1914-19; served in Salonica and Palestine (twice ment. in desps.); dist. storekeeper, Tanganyika rly., 1920; dep. ch. storekeeper, 1932; ch. storekeeper, 1934; stores supt., K.U.R. and H., 1934.

COUTTS, JAMES MAGNUS.—B. 1896; ed. Heriot's Schl., Schl. of Medicine and Univ., Edinburgh; L.D.S., R.C.S. (Edin.), 1924; served great war with Cameron Highlanders, 1915-19; M.M., 1916; dental surg., Falkland Is., Oct., 1925; ag. loc. audr., Apr., 1926-Apr., 1927; lieut., Falkland Is. Def. Force, 1927; capt., 1929; ag. O.C., May to Nov., 1928; 2nd in commd., 1927-31; dental offr., Straits Settlements, Aug., 1931; dent. offr., S.S. and dent. surg., S'pore, Jan., 1934; sec., dent. bd., S.S. in addn., Feb., 1934.

OOWGILL, JOHN VINCENT.—B. 1888; ed. Durham Schl. and All Souls Coll., Oxford (bible-clerk, 1907-11), B.A., 1911; cadet, F. M.S., 1911; studied Chinese, 1912-14; on mil'y. serv., 1915-19; M.C., 1917, ment. in desps., 1919; dist. offr., Dec., 1919; ass't advr., Trenegau, Nov., 1923; town planning admstr. and state valuer, Ipoh, June, 1926; collr., land rev., K. Lumpur and regiar. titles, Selangor, Nov., 1927; ag. sec. to H. comsnr., June, 1928; offr., cls. II., Nov., 1928; ag. dist. offr., Kinta and dist. supt., prisons, July, 1930; offr., cls. II., 1931; mem., Perak coun. of state, Jan., 1932; ag. sec. to res., Perak Mar., 1932; chmn., sany. bd., K. Lumpur, Feb., 1934; ag. Br. res., Palang, Mar., 1937; ag. Br. res., N. Sembilan, Feb., 1938.
OWIN, GEORGE STEWART, M.R.C.V.S.—B. 1903; ed. Nat. Elementary Schl. and Royal (Dick) Vety. Coll., Edinburgh; vety. offr., Tanganyika Territory, 1925.

OWPER, HERBERT WELLESLEY.—B. 1883; ed. at St. Paul's School, London, Hertford Coll.; Oxford, and in Germany, France and Italy; ass't res., N. Nigeria, Nov., 1910; ag. res., 1916; 2nd cls. dist. offr., Dec., 1918.
COX, ALFRED JAMES, B.Sq. For. (Edin.)—B. 1902; ass't cons. of forests, Gold Coast, Jan., 1926; sec', ass't cons. of forests, July, 1937.

COX, ARTHUR HENRY, C.M.G. (1938).—B. 1888; ed. Felsted and Corpus Christi Coll., Cambridge; B.A., 1910; ass't dist. comnr., Uganda, Nov., 1911; A.D.C. to gov., 1918-19; 1st gr. admstr. offr., Jan., 1922; dep. prov. comnr., Dec., 1928; ag. prov. comnr., July, 1929 and June, 1932; ass't ch. sec., Uganda, Mar., 1930; ag. dep. ch. sec., Mar., 1930 to Oct., 1931; prov. comnr., Dec., 1932.

COX, CHARLES EDWARD LANGTON.—B. 1889; entd. civ. serv., B. Guiana, 1907; 2nd cls. clk., col. sec.'s office; called to the bar, 1918; stip. mag. for the colony; reviving barrister, 1919; served in France as lieut., R.G.A. (S.R.); injured at Loos, Sept., 1916; invalided out of army, Nov., 1917; served under Admiralty as A.T.S.O., mining depot, Grangemouth, Mar. to Dec., 1918; extra A.D.C. to Sir W. Egerton, K.C.M.G., 1913-15; lieut., artillery coy., B. Guiana Militia, 1908; capt., 1919; pol. mag., St. Vincent, 1920-23; ag. atty.-gen. and ch. just., St. Vincent; crown atty. and regir., sup. ct., Dominica, 1923-25; mem., exec. coun., income tax comnr., offl. recr., visiting just., prison, Dominica; pres., dist. ct., Cyprus, 1925; ag. bulsine judge, sup. ct., for various periods, 1928-36.

COX, LIEUT.-COL. SIR C. H. F., K.C.M.G. (1937), C.M.G. (1927), D.S.O. (1917).—Ed. Rugby and R. Mil'y. Acad., Woolwich; command. R.H. and R.F.A., 6th Jan., 1900; served S. African War; served in India, 1905-08; seconded Sudan civ. serv., 1913-15; served in Great War, 1915-18; four times ment. in desps., D.S.O., 1917, bar, 1918; brevet lieut.-col., 1st Jan., 1919; ass't admstr. offr., Palestine, Apr., 1919; mil. gov., Nablus, Mar., 1920; ditto, Galilee, Aug., 1920; dist. gov., Samarai, July, 1922; ret'd from Army with rank of lieut.-col., Jan., 1923; ch. R. res. Trans.-Jordan, Apr., 1924; ret., 1939.

COX, HERBERT CHARLES FAHIE, K.C. (1936)—Br. Guiana pol. 1913-19; called to bar, 1915; on war serv., France, 1917-19; ass't to atty.-gen., Br. Guiana, 1919; ag. mag. on various occasions; atty.-gen., Bahamas, 1925; M.L.C., 1926; ag. ch. just., May-Sept., 1926, Mar.-Oct., 1927 and from June to Sept., 1929; chmn., bd. of health, 1926-27; chmn. water and sewage bd., 1928-29; dep. gov., Mar., 1929; dep. admstr., June and July, 1929; atty.-gen., Gibraltar, Dec., 1929; solr.-gen., Nigeria, 1933; ag. atty.-gen., Mar.-Aug., 1934; atty.-gen., 1935.
COX, HERBERT WILLIAM, F.I.A.C.—B. 1898; olk., K.U.R. and H., 1922; ass't acctnt., 1925; port acctnt., 1931; senr. ass't acctnt., 1933.

COX, LIONEL, A.S.A.—B. 1890; ed. St. Stephens Gram. Schl., Dublin; acctnt., govt. opium shops, S'pore, Feb., 1926; offr. i.c., govt. opium shops, S'pore, July, 1934; acctnt., med. dept., S.S., Sept., 1935; ag. acct.-gen., S.S., Mar., 1938.

CRABB, RICHARD BRUCE—B.1891; ed. Cranleigh Schl.; ass't dist. comnr., Gold Coast, 1915; served European War, East Surrey Regt., 1916-18; dist. comnr., 1922; ass't col. sec., 1923; called to bar, Inner Temple, 1926; ag. clk., couns. in 1927 and 1928; ag. prin. ass't col. sec., Apr. to Sept., 1932; senr. ass't col. sec., Sept., 1932.
CRAGG, WILLIAM GILLIAT, D.S.O. (1918), Major, R. of O. Loyal Regt.—B. 1883; ed. practised in London and South-Eastern circuit, 1933-37; dist. mag., Gold Coast, Dec., 1937.

D'ANDRADE, WILLIAM ALBERT.—B. 1884; cler. asst., surg. genl.'s office, Br. Guiana, 1903; 6th cls. offr., cust., 1903; 5th ditto, 1906; 4th ditto, 1913; 3rd ditto, 1921; 2nd ditto, 1925; ch. clk., cust., 1925; compt., cust., 1927.

DANDURAND, Hon. Senator RAUL, K.C., LL.D., P.C.—B. 1861; ed. Montreal Coll. and Laval Univ.; LL.B., 1882; LL.D., 1909 and hon. LL.D., McGill, Toronto, and Queen's Univ.; pres., Univ. of Montreal, Feb., 1934; called to Quebec bar, 1883; K.C., 1898; called to senate of Canada by Lord Aberdeen, 1896; speaker of senate, 1905-9; P.C., 1909; min. of state and leader of King govt. in senate, 1921, also in 2nd King govt., 1926, and in 3rd King govt., 1935; leader of lib. party, senate, 1930; served as R. commr., on emigration, Quebec, 1910; el. pres., 6th assem., L. of N., Geneva, 1925; del. of Canada to coun., L. of N., 1927-30; apptd. by pres. of U.S.A., arbitr. under treaty of peace with Brazil.

DANEEL, JOSHUA, M.B., Ch.B. (Edin.), F.R.C.S. (Edin.).—B. 1884; ed. Victoria Coll., Stellenbosch and Edin. Univ.; admitted F.R.C.S., Edin., 1922; med. supt., Rietfontein hosp., Union of S. Africa, 1927.

DANGERFIELD, PAUL, F.R.I.B.A., M.C., Chev. Leg. d'Honneur.—B. 1894; ed. St. Albans schl.; archt. educn.; articled, 1910-13, and A.A. schl., 1919-20; war serv., B.E.F., 1914-19; govt. archt., Kenya, 1926.

DANIEL, EDWARD WELLEY, B.A. (Lond.), F.R.S.A., A.R.Hist.S., M.R.S.T.—B. 1895; ed. St. Paul's Coll., Cheltenham; war serv., 1914-18; serv. with army of occupation, 1919-20; Trinidad local forces, 1927-32; asst. mast., high schl. for boys, Hereford, 1921-27; prin. and lect. in pedagogy, govt. training coll. for teachers, Trinidad, 1927; sec. for external exams., 1927; asst. dir., educn. and ch. inspr., schls., 1934; ag. dir., educn., chmn., bd. of educn. and temp. mem., leg. coun., Mar.-Oct., 1936; author, "West Indian Histories."

DANIEL, FRANCIS DE FORREST.—B. 1886; ed. Marlborough Coll.; admitted solr., 1910; asst. acctnt., Lagos rly., July, 1912; transf., cust. dept., May, 1913; asst. dist. offr., N. Provs., Nigeria, July, 1914; res., Mar., 1930; staff grade, 1937.

DANIEL, Lieut. Col. Hector Cyril, M.C., A.F.C.—B. 1898; serv. with R.A.F. in Great War, France, 1917-18; O.C., Flying Tr. Schl., Roberts Heights, S. Africa, 1933; course at senr. officers schl., Sheerness, 1935; O.C. aircraft and arrty. depot, Roberts Heights, 1936; dir., air and techn. services, Union Defence Forces, 1937.

DANIEL, REGINALD YOUNG, M.A. (Oxon).—B. 1894; on mily. serv., 1914; cadet, Ceylon, civ. serv., Oct., 1920; ag. office asst. to asst. govt. azt., Matara, Sept., 1921; ag. pol. mag., Gallo, Jan., 1922; office asst. Hambantota kach., Mar., 1922; ditto to govt. azt., N. Prov., Nov., 1922; dist. judge, Anuradhapura, Dec., 1925; pol. mag., Kalutara, Dec., 1926; pol. mag., Kandy, Apr., 1928; addnl. pol. mag., Colombo, Aug., 1930; dist. judge, Nuwara Eliva, Sept., 1930; do., Ratnapura, Oct., 1931; asst. govt. azt., Mannar, Mar., 1933; do., Matale, Oct., 1934; asst. sttlmt. offr., Nov., 1937; ag. govt. azt., N.W.P., Feb., 1938.
DANKS, Frank SCOBIE, B.S. (Forestry), Dipl. in Agr.—B. 1896; on war serv., 1915-19; asst. conserv., forests, 1st gr., Cyprus, Oct., 1926.
DANKS, William Barr CLELLAND, B.Sc., M.R.C.V.S.—B. 1900; ed. Hamilton Acad., R.M.C., Sandhurst and R. "Dick" Vety Coll., Edinburgh; vety. offr., Kenya, 1927; vety. resch. offr., 1930.

DANNATT, Robert Malcolm, M.B., B.S. (Lond.), F.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.), F.R.C.S. (Edin.).—B. 1891; med. offr., F.M.S., Oct., 1925; surgn., F.M.S., Mar., 1926; do., Selangor, Nov., 1933; ag. senr. surg., S'pore, Aug., 1935; res. surg., col. hosp., Grenada, 1937.

DARBY, Capt. GEORGE, O.B.E., M.C.—B. 1884; active serv., France, Belgium, N. and S. Russia; four times ment. in desps.; Order of St. Anne (3rd cls. with swords) and St. Stanislav (2nd cls. with swords) of Russia; adm. offr., cls. IV, Nigeria, 1921; asst. sec., Nigerian secr., 1922; pvt. sec. to gov., 1925; land offr., 1928; ag. commr., lands, June to July, 1934; prim. asst., land and survey dept., Apr. 1937; ag. dep. commr., lands and surrv.-gen., Apr.-May, 1937, and Sept.-Feb., 1937-38.

DARRELL, Arthur LLEWELLYN.—B. 1883; ed. Harrison Coll., Barbados; asst. clk. to regisr., St. Vincent, 1900; 3rd clk. treas., 1901; 2nd clk. to gov., Windward Is. (Grenada), 1909; pvt. sec. and A.D.C. to gov., Windward Is., 1913; treas. asst., 3rd grade, Nigeria, 1914; treas. asst. 2nd grade, 1919; agr. asst. treas., 1921; ag. treas. in 1928, 1933 and 1935; dep. treas., 1934; treas., Dominica, 1936.

DARTFORD, Gerald Percy.—B. 1908; ed. Trent Coll., Derbyshire and Lond. Univ., B.A. (Lond.), hona. hist., 1930; hon. geog., 1936; asst. mast., Queen's Coll., Br. Guiana, 1932; 2nd lieut., Br. Guiana Militia, 1935; lieut., 1938.

DASH, Prof. John Sydney, B.Sc. (Agr.), McGill Univ.—B. 1887; various posts, dept. of agr., Barbados, 1905-09; plant pathologist, Br. Columbia, 1913; asst. supt., agr., Barbados, 1914-18; dir., station agronomique, Guadeloupe, F.W.I., 1918-21; tobacco specialist and botanist, seed br., dept. of agr., Canada, 1921-22; prof. of agr., Impl. Coll. Trop. Agr., Trinidad, 1922-27; dir. agr., Br. Guiana, 1927; M.E.C. and M.L.C., 1928; pres., Royal Agr. and Comm. Soc., 1928-29; chmn., various agrl. comtees., 1928-33; spec. rep. for govt. on behalf Rice Marketing Bd. on rice export, Trinidad, 1936; spec. invest. of Central Coffee Bd., Suriname, 1937; pres., Boy Scouts Assoc., 1938; rep. col., various agrl. confs., 1930-38.

DAUBNEY, Robert, O.B.E., M.Sc., M.R.C.V.S.—B. 1891; ed. Manchester Grammar Schl. and Liverpool Univ.; mil. serv., 1914-19; resch. asst., R. Vety. Coll., 1913-14; min. of agric. and fisheries, 1919-25; asst. ch. vety. resch. offr., Kenya, 1925; ch. vety. resch. offr., 1932; dir., vety. services, 1937.
DAVENPORT, Cecil Thornhill, M.A.—B. 1892; ed. Malvern and Oriel Coll., Oxford; asst. dist. commr., Kenya, Jan., 1920; dist. offr., 1923.
DAVEY, Peter CHRISTMAS.—B. 1914; ed. Eastbourne Coll.; A.D.C. to gov. of Aden, Mar., 1936; polit. offr., Aden, Oct., 1938.
DAVIDSON, Hon. Colin George Watt, K.C.—B. 1878; judge, sup. ct., N. S. Wales, Feb., 1927.

DAVIDSON, Gerald Larcom Dean.—B. 1898; on mily. serv., Sept., 1917; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Nov., 1921; attld. Colombo kach., Dec., 1921; attld., Badulla kach., Jan., 1922; office asst., Ratnapura kach., Oct., 1924; ditto, Kuruenagala kach., Oct., 1926; pol. mag. Gampola,

CRERAR, HON. THOMAS ALEXANDER.—Ed., pub. schls. and Collegiate, Portage la Prairie, Manitoba; teacher, farmer, grain elevator operator; invited to enter Union govt., 1917; P.C., Canada, 1917; min. of agr., 1917-19; M.P., Marquette, Manitoba, 1917-25; leader, progressive party, 1921-22; M.P., Brandon, 1930; min. of rlys. and canals, 1929-30; defeated, g.e. 1930; re-el., g.e. 1935 as M.P. for Churchill; min. of mines, min. of immigrn. and colonization, min. of interior, and supt. gen., Indian affrs. in Mackenzie King cabinet, Oct., 1935.

CRESSALL, PAUL EWART FRANCIS, M.C.—B. 1893; ed. Cranleigh Schl.; ent. civ. serv., B. Guiana, 1911; cler. ast., secretariat and sub-inspr., B. Guiana pol., 1912; ag. dist. inspr., 1914-16; 2nd lieut., B. Guiana Militia Infy., 1914; served in B.W.I. Regt. as capt., E. Africa and Palestine, 1915-19; dist. inspr., B. Guiana pol., 1920; J.P. and coroner, 1920; ag. county inspr., 1920; passed final law exam., 1921; capt., B. Guiana local forces, 1922; ag. staff offr., B. Guiana local forces, 1922; called to the bar, Gray's Inn, 1923; county inspr., pol., 1923; commr., for oaths and revising barrister, 1924; stip. mag., Apr., 1924; ag. stip. mag., Georgetown, Aug., 1924 to Dec., 1924 and July to Dec., 1926; chmn., comm. of enquiry into Onderneeming indus. schl., Sept., 1925; ag. chmn., soldiers' pensions bd., 1925; chmn., ditto, Nov., 1926; senr. stip. mag., Nov., 1930; senr. Br. mag., Palestine, June, 1931; ag. pres., Nablus dist. ct., May, 1933; do., Jerusalem dist. ct., June, 1933; chmn., legal bd., June, 1933; relieving pres., dist. ct., Aug., 1934; pres., dist. ct., 1936.

CRESWELL, COL. THE HON. FREDERIC HUGH PAGE, D.S.O.—B. 1866; ed. Bruce Castle, Derby Schl., and R. Schl. of Mines (grad. 1st cls.); arrived in S. Africa, May, 1893; mininig in Rhodesia, 1893-94; man., Durban Roodepoort Deep Mine, 1895-99; served in Imp. Light Horse during Anglo-Boer war; man., Village Main Reef, 1902-03; supported agitation for expulsion of Chinese labour; elec. to first Union parl., 1910, and led Labour Party; 2nd in commd., Rand Rifles, S. W. Africa, 1914; 2nd in commd., subsequently commdg., 8th S. A. Infy. Brig., German E. Africa campaign; mem. for Denver; min. of defence and labour, 1st and 2nd Hertzog minv., June, 1924 to Mar., 1933; M.P. for Bellville, May, 1933.

CREWE, 1ST MARQUESS OF (cr. 1911) — ROBERT OFFLEY ASHBURTON CREWE-MILNES, K.G., P.C., M.A., F.S.A.—B. 1858; Baron Houghton of Great Houghton in the county of York, 1863; Earl of Crewe (cr. 1895); Earl of Madeley (cr. 1911); ed. at Harrow and Trin. Coll., Camb.; asst. priv. sec. to sec. for foreign affairs (Earl Granville), 1883-84; lord-in-waiting to Queen Victoria, 1886; lord-lieut. of Ireland, 1892-95; lord pres. of the coun., 1905-08; S. of S. for the Colonies, Apr., 1908; Lord Privy Seal (in conjunction with Secretaryship of State), 1908; S. of S. for India, 1910-15; accompanied H.M. King George V. on the occasion of the Royal visit to India, 1911; lord pres. of the coun., 1915.

CREWE, EDWIN CHARLES.—B. 1886, 2nd divn. clk., G.P.O. Lond., 1905; ch. clk. and acctnt., P. & T., Gold Co.t. Dec., 1911; acctnt., 1913; ch. acctnt., Jan., 1920; dep. P.M.G., Apr., 1929; P.M.G., Nigeria, Aug., 1936.
CRICHLLOW, NATHANIEL.—M.B., Ch.B.; med. offr., Solomon Islands, Oct., 1914; ag. senr. med. offr. on several occasions; travelling med. offr., July, 1927.
CRISP, HON. SIR HAROLD, K.B., (1938).—B. 1874; called to bar, 1896; puisne judge, Tasmania, Aug., 1914; admstr. of govt., 1930; ch. just., 1937.

CROCKETT, HON. OSWALD SMITH, LL.D.—B. 1868; ed. pub. and high schls., Fredericton, N.B., Canada, and Univ. of New Brunswick; B.A., 1886; atty., sup. ct., N.B., 1891; called to bar, 1892; M.P. (Can.) for York County, N.B., 1904-11; judge, king's bench div., sup. ct., N.B., 1913; judge, sup. ct., Canada, 1932.

CRONEBN, BRIAN.—Ed. Dulwich Coll.; served B.S.A. pol. and Bechuanaand Prot. pol., 1911; 2nd grade clk., 1921; ag. sub-inspr., 1922; ag. ast. res. mag., 1926; passed 2nd grade Seeawana exam., 1923; ag. res. mag., 1927; 1st grade clk., Apr., 1928; sub-inspr., Bechuanaand Prot. pol., 1929.

CROOK, WALLER VERNON.—B. 1900; ed. Marlborough Coll., and R. Mily. Coll., Sandhurst; comand, Reg. Army, East Surrey Regt., Dec., 1918; served N. Russia, 1919; seconded under C.O., Mar., 1926; cadet, Uganda, 1926; ast. dist. offr., Uganda, June, 1928, and placed on Reg. Army, R. of O.; dist. offr., 1938.

CROSBIE, HON. SIR JOHN C., K.B.E. (1919).—M.H.A., Bay de Verde dist., Newfoundland, 1908; M.E.C., 1909; mem. defence comtee., 1915; chrmn., tonnage comtee, 1917; min. of militia, 1917; min. of shipping, 1918.

CROSBIE, ROBERT EDWARD HAROLD, O.B.E. (1927).—B. 1886; ed. Cheltenham and Pembroke Coll., Cambridge (schol.); B.A. (clam. tripos, hons. cl. II), 1908; Egyptian civ. serv., 1910-19; seconded for milly. serv. wid. E.E.F., 1915; apptd. to O.E.T.A. (S.), 1918; dep. milly. gov., Brisban, 1919; dep. milly. gov., Tiberias, 1920; dep. dist. gov., Jenin, 1920; ast. gov. (continuing as ast. dist. commr.), S. dist., 1922; passed advanced Arabic exam., 1924; dist. commr., Southern dist., 1931.

CROSS, GRAHAM.—Clk., native ct., Johannesburg, May, 1902; ast. res. mag. and ast. mag., native ct., Witwatersrand dist., Aug., 1903; mag., Nov., 1915; under sec. for lands, Sept., 1923; sec. for lands, Aug., 1932; chmn., pub. serv. comm., Jan., 1934.

CROSS, MATTHEW ARNOLD.—B. 1881; clk., deeds, Transvaal, Mar., 1902; examr. deeds, July, 1904; 2nd grade examr., deeds, Pretoria, Oct., 1919; 1st gr. ditto, Nov., 1919; ch. examr., July, 1922; ast. registr., deeds, Bloemfontein, Nov., 1927; ditto, Pretoria, Oct., 1928; registr., deeds, Bloemfontein, Apr., 1933; do., Pretoria, Feb., 1934; ch. registr., deeds, Union and registr., deeds, Transvaal, July, 1937.

CROSSE-CROSSE, WILLIAM WICKHAM REDDER.—B. 1899; ed. Blundelle schl.; 2nd lieut. Welsh Guards, 1918; lt., 1920; served with 1st and 2nd K.A.R., in Tanganyika Territory, 1922-24; R. of O., (Welsh Guards), 1924; cadet, Uganda Prot., 1924; ast. dist. offr., 1926; ag. lab. commr., 1929; temp. ast. prin., C.O., 1930-32; dist. offr., 1936.

CROSTHWAITE, F. D., M.B., Ch.B., Edin. Univ., D.P.H. Camb., L.M., Dublin.—B. 1879; ed. Tonbridge Schl., Edinburgh Univ. and Middlesex Hosp. Schl., London Univ.; ast. physician, ment. hosp. serv., Union S. Africa, 1913; phys. supt., Queenstown ment. hosp., 1922; do., Pretoria ment. hosp., 1928; do., Witrand Instn. for Feebleminded, 1932.
CROUCHER, HERBERT HARRY, B.Sc. (Lond.), A.I.C.—B. 1904; ed. Reading Univ.; lect. in and ag. high. comsnr., Trans-Jordan, July-Sept., 1930; gov. and comindr.-in-ch., St. Helena, 1932.

DAVIS, THOMAS ARTHUR WARBEN.—B. 1899; ed. Haverfordwest and Keble Coll., Oxford; Indian army, 1917-22; dipl., forestry (Oxon), 1923; asst. conserv., forests, Br. Guiana, 1925; ag. conserv., forests, various periods, 1930-36.

DIX, CHARLES CABRY, C.M.G., D.S.O., R.N. (ret.).—Ed. R.N. Schl., Etham and H.M.S. "Britannia"; R.N., 1898-1919; harbmr., Barbados, 1926; harbmr., Kingston, Jamaica, 1927; lec. and demonstr. of chem., Oxford, 1884-86; prof. of chem., Pharm. Soc., 1886-96; lect. on chem., St. Thomas' Hosp., 1892-1900; sec., chem. soc., 1903-5; vice-pres., 1903-6; pres. section of chem. and agric. science, Brit. Assoc., 1906; mem. of coun., Royal Soc., 1904-7; mem. of adv. comte., trop. agric., C.O., 1908, vice-pres., Internat. assoc. of trop. agric., 1907; pres., 1910; pres., Internat. Congress trop. agric., London, 1914; mem. comm. intell. comte. Bd. of T., 1900-6; dir., scientific and tech. dept., Impl. Inst., 1896-1903; dir., Impl. Inst., from 1903 to 1923; reported on agric. resources of Cyprus for C.O. in 1904, and of Asia Minor in 1907 (reports presented to parlmt.); visited Ceylon at request of colonial govt., 1910, and again in 1913; visited Newfoundland at request of colonial govt., 1914; arranged and supervised govt. min. surveys in Ceylon, N. and S. Nigeria and Nyasaland (reports presented to parlmt.); author of numerous scientific papers in the Phil. Trans. and Proceedings of Royal Soc., chem. soc., etc., and of tech. reports relating to the utilisation of the resources of the colonies, including "Cotton Cultivation in the Brit. Empire and in Egypt," 1904, "Brit. Cotton Cultivation," 1908; edited "Tech. Reports and Scientific Papers," Impl. Inst., 1903; "Selected Reports," Impl. Inst., 1909 (presented to Parlmt.); "Reports on work of Impl. Inst., 1906-14" (presented to Parlmt.): "Report on present position of Cotton Cultivation," 1910; editor, Impl. Inst. Handbooks on Commercial Resources of Tropics; author of article, "Rubber," in last edition "Ency. Brit."

(1915), K.C.—B. 1869; ed. in England and Canada; called to the bar, Ontario, 1891; counsel, railway comttee., Ontario legislature, 1902; counsel for City of Toronto, 1910; Ontario gov't. comsnr. on Toronto Power Coman., 1911; ch. comsnr., bd. of railway comanars., Canada, 1912; resig. to assume portfolio of min. of finance in Union Gov't., Aug., 1919; elec. by acclamation for Kingston, Oct., 1919; re-el. for York West, g.e., Dec., 1921; resig. portfolio on defeat of admtn., Dec., 1921; chmn., bd. of liquor comsnr., Ontario, 1928.

DRAYTON, ROBERT HARRY, LL.B. (hons.) Lond.—B. 1892; solr. (Eng.); lieut., Devonshire Regt. and Machine Gun Corps, 1914-1918; serv. in France, Jan., 1916 to Sept., 1917; min. of reconstrn. and Lord Chancellor's dept., 1919; treas. solr.'s office, 1919-20; asst. legal sec., gov't. of Palestine (continuing as asst. atty.-gen.), Nov., 1920; ag. legal sec., Aug., 1921 to Oct., 1921; mem. of advisory and exec. couns., Apr. to July, 1923 and July to Sept., 1924; title changed to solr.-gen., 1925; rep. Palestine at internat. arbitrn. on partition of Ottoman pub. debt, Geneva, 1925; mem., finan. comsnr., orthodox patriarchate of Jerusalem, 1924-28; one of counsel rep. gov't. in parly. re disturbances of 1929; ag. atty.-gen. on several occasions, 1922-31; legal draftsmn., Jan., 1932; called to bar, 1934; comsnr. for revision of laws of Palestine, Sept., 1934; atty.-gen., Tanganyika Territory, Sept., 1934; ag. gov', Apr.-July, 1938.

Watson's Coll., Edin.; apptd., after compet. exam., olk., 2nd div., civ. serv. and assigned to B. of T., 1910; apptd., after compet. exam., olk., 3rd cls., Crown Agents' office, Feb., 1912; on mil. serv., 1914-19 (France, adjutant, Behar Light Horse Volunteers, India, Apr., 1892 to Apr., 1897; 1st and 5th Dragoon Guards and Imperial Light Horse, Apr., 1897 to Oct., 1900; Hazara (N.W. Frontier, India), 1888, asst. prov. marshal (ment. in desp., medal and clasp); commanded Imperial Light Horse, S. African war, Nov., 1899 to June, 1900; dist. comsnt., Potchefstroom and Krugersdorp dists., June to Oct., 1900; commanded "A" division, S.A.C., Oct., 1900, to end of war (ment. in desp., Queen's medal, 4 clasps, King's medal, 2 clasps); substantive lt.-col. in the army, dated 29th Nov., 1900; commdt., Transvaal volunteers; left the service, 1906; A.A.G., Northern com., India, 1906-7; chief constable, metrop. police, 1907-12; commdt.-gen., Rhodesian forces, 1912; ret. 1st Apr., 1923.

Congress trop. agric., London, 1914; mem. comm. intell. comte., Bd. of T., 1900-6; dir., scientific and tech. dept., Impl. Inst., 1896-1903; dir., Impl. Inst., from 1903 to 1923; reported on agric. resources of Cyprus for C.O. in 1904, and of Asia Minor in 1907 (reports presented to parlmt.); visited Ceylon at request of colonial govt., 1910, and again in 1913; visited Newfoundland at request of colonial govt., 1914; arranged and supervised govt. min. surveys in Ceylon, N. and S. Nigeria and Nyasaland (reports presented to parlmt.); author of numerous scientific papers in the Phil. Trans. and Proceedings of Royal Soc., chem. soc., etc., and of tech. reports relating to the utilisation of the resources of the colonies, including "Cotton Cultivation in the Brit. Empire and in Egypt," 1904, "Brit. Cotton Cultivation," 1908; edited "Tech. Reports and Scientific Papers," Impl. Inst., 1903; "Selected Reports," Impl. Inst., 1909 (presented to Parlmt.); "Report on work of Impl. Inst., 1906-14" (presented to Parlmt.); "Report on present position of Cotton Cultivation," 1910; editor, Impl. Inst. Handbooks on Commercial Resources of Tropics; author of article, "Rubber," in last edition "Ency. Brit."
