Fiji civ. serv., 1898; lieut., armed native constab., 1901; certif., musketry and machine gun, Hythe, 1906; called to the bar, Inner Temple, 1907; inspr., Fiji constab., 1907; barrister and solr., sup. ct., Fiji, 1908; A.D.C. to ag. gov., for various periods, 1908-10; 2nd grade dist. comsnr., May, 1912; 2nd in comd. local forces with rank of major, Aug., 1914; ment. by Army Council for services in 1914 in connection with defence of Fiji; capt., E. Surrey Regt., Mar., 1915; coy. comdr., K.A.R., E. Africa, 1916-19; 1st grade dist. comsnr., Fiji, Jan., 1916; regis. gen., regis., sup. ct., comsnr. stamps, and pub. trustee, Fiji, Jan., 1920; cust., enemy property and contr. L.C.O., Fiji and W. Pacific, Feb., 1920; ag. atty.-gen., May, 1920 to May, 1921; comsnr. for the revision of the laws of Fiji, 1920 (not completed); atty.-gen., B. Honduras, July 1921; ag. ch. just. in 1921, 1922 and 1923; chmn., comsn. to enquire into outbreak of yellow fever, 1921; chmn., retrenchment comte, 1922; chmn., franchise coman., 1923; revd. and consol. laws, B. Hond., 1924; attv.-gen., Zanzibar, Nov., 1924; atty.-gen., Tanganyika Territory, 1929; judge, high ct., N. Rhodesia, Nov., 1934; title changed to ch. just., Feb., 1938.
FRANCIS, JOSIAH.—Served European war with A.I.F., 1916-19; M.H.R., C'wealth of Australia since 1922; hon. min., Lyons' ministry, 1932.

FRANCIS, OLIVER MICHAEL, M.D., C.M. (McGill), L.M.S. (Nova Scotia). D.T.M. & H. (Eng.), D.P.H. (Lond.)—B. 1885; med. offr., 5th dist., St. Lucia, Jan., 1919; gov't. M.O., Br. Guiana, 1921; supt., G'town prison, 1932; ag. asst. govt. M.O.H., 1935; ag. govt. M.O.H., 1936; 2nd asst. G.M.O.H., 1937.
FRASER, ALASTAIR GARDEN, B.A.—B. 1902; ed. Rugby and Queen's Coll., Oxford; B.A., 1924; 3rd cls. hons., phil., polit., and econ.; asst. mast., Achimota, Gold Coast, 1924.

FRASER, REV. ALEXANDER GARDEN, C.R.E. (1930), M.A.—B. 1873; ed. Merchiston Castle, Edin., Edin. Univ. and Trinity Coll., Oxford; deacon, 1913; priest, 1915; C.M.S., Uganda, 1900-03; prin., Trinity Coll., Kandy, Ceylon, 1904; chaplain with 3rd divn., France, 1917-18; chaplain to the forces; prin., Prince of Wales Coll., Achimota, Gold Coast, 1924.

FRASER, D. M.—B.1890; Br. post office, 1905; post. clk. and telegraphist, Kenya and Uganda, 1913; postmtr., Tanganyika, 1917; ch. acct., posts and tels., 1919; asst. P.M.G., Kenya, Uganda, Tanganyika, 1933; ret., 1934; asst. P.M.G., Trinidad, 1936.
FRASER, H.—Ed. Grammar Schl., Inveraray; George Watson's Coll., Edinburgh and Edinburgh Univ.; B.Sc., 1925; asst. conservator of forest, Tanganyika Territory, 1925.

FRASER, HUGH, B.A.—B.1890; ed. Wellington and Exeter Coll., Oxford(schol.); cadet, F.M.S., 1913; ag. priv. sec. to ch. sec., Apr., 1914-Feb., 1915; ag. supervisor of cust., Port Dickson, May, 1915; ditto, Port Swettenham, Jan., 1916; passed cadet, Dec., 1916; offr., cls. V., Dec., 1916; 3rd asst. sec., Fed. acct., Aug., 1917, priv. sec. to ch. sec. in addn., Sept., 1917; dist. offr., Jelebu, Feb., 1918; asst. dist. offr., Ipoh, Sept., 1920; offr., cls. IV., Nov., 1920; 3rd asst. sec. to govt., F.M.S., Dec., 1920; dist. offr., Kuantan, Sept., 1924; offr., cls. III., Nov., 1926; ag. asst. advr., Kedah, Mar., 1927; ag. temp. legal adviser, Kedah in addn., Apr. to Dec., 1927; ag. asst. treas., F.M.S. and state treas., Selangor, June, 1929; offr., cls. II., Nov., 1930; dist. offr., Larut and regiar., titles, Perak Nov., 1930; offig. mag., K. Lumpur, Nov., 1932; ag. sec. to res., Perak Mar., 1933; offr. cls. I.B., 1934; Malayan estab. offr., Mar., 1936; sec., high comsnr. in addn., Aug., 1936; ag. under-sec. to govt., F.M.S., Jan., 1937; under-sec. to govt., F.M.S., Apr., 1937; ag. Fed. sec., F.M.S., Apr., 1937.

FRASER, JOHN ALEXANDER, M.C., Barrister-at-Law.—B. 1896; ed. Trinity Academy, Leith, and Edin. Univ.; on milly. serv., 1915-19; cadet, Hong Kong, 1919; attd. to sany. dept., 1921; 3rd asst. to sec. for Chinese affrs., 1921; 2nd pol. mag., Apr., 1922; dist. offr. (North), June, 1922; passed in Cantonese, 1922; asst. head, sany. dept. and sec. to sany. bd., 1922; dist. offr., South, 1924; asst. supt., impts. and expts., 1925; dist. offr., North, Feb., 1926; dist. offr., North and South Sept., 1928; called to bar, Middle Temple, June, 1931; pol. mag., Kowloon, July, 1931; ag. asst. atty.-gen., Oct., 1932; ag. crown solr., Aug., 1936 and Feb., 1937; attd. A.G.'s dept., Sept., 1937 and Aug., 1937; ag. puisne judge, Sept., 1936; temp. addnl. judge, Oct. 1936; cadet offr., cls. I, Dec., 1936; ag. 1st mag., Jan., 1937; ag. atty.-gen., June, 1937 and Feb., 1938; editr. of "Ordnances and Regulations of Hong Kong" (1937 edn.).

FRASER, SIR JOHN GEORGE, KT. BACH. (1924), C.M.G. (1913).—B. 1884; cadet Colonial civ. ser., Oct., 1887; ag. dist. judge, d.c., Badulla, Feb., 1894; asst. govt. agt., Chilaw, Dec., 1899; land settm. offr., Aug., 1901; ag. govt. agt., W. Prov., 4th Jan., 1912; ag. controller of revenue, in 1911, 1912, 1913, 1915 and 1916; govt. agt., W. Prov., July, 1914; M.L.C.; ret., 1924.

FRASER, HON. PETER.—B. 1884; ed. Board Schl. and Workers' Educnl. Asso; mem. of N.Z. H. of R. since 1918; represented N.Z. at Empire mission to Cairo under F.O., 24th Jan. to 14th Mar., 1924; ch. just., Trinidad, 1924; ch. just., Ceylon, 1926; ret., Aug., 1930; passed exam. in modern Greek, Dec. 1903 and in Turkish, 1918; joint commr. for compiling revised edition of Statute Laws of Cyprus (published 1907), for which recd. thanks of legis. coun. and S. of S.; joint commr. under reprint of statutes law, 1913; edition published, Feb., 1914; commr. for laws, under reprint of statutes law, 1922; edition published, 1924; compiler of "The Mukhtar's Handbook," 1911; published "Ottoman Land Laws," 1919.

FLETCHER, SIR ARTHUR GEORGE MURCHISON, K.C.M.G. (1930), KT. BACH (1929), C.M.G. (1922), C.B.E. (1919).—Ed. Cheltenham Coll. and Trin. Coll., Oxford (exhib.); cadet, Hong Kong, 1901; seconded for famine relief work in Kwang Si, May-June, 1903; ag. astt. registr. gen., 1903; J.P., 1904; ag. astt. col. sec. and clk. of couns., 1905-8-9; priv. sec. to O.A.G., 1907; dep. offr. recvr., 1909; astt. to atty.-gen., 1909; regiar., trade marks and letters patent, 1910; ag. assessor of rates, 1911; ag. dep. regiar., 1911; priv. sec. to O.A.G., 1912; offr. recvr., 1912; ag. astt. col. sec. and clk. of couns., 1912; ag. treasur., 1913; astt. col. sec. and clk. of couns., Aug., 1913; priv. sec. to O.A.G., and to gov., Aug.-Dec., 1913; ag. col. sec. and mem. of exec. and leg. couns. in 1916, 1917, 1918, 1919, 1920 and 1922; chmn., shipping control comttee., 1917-19; ag. col. sec. and mem., exec. and leg. couns., 1923 and 1928; col. sec., Ceylon, Jan., 1926; O.A.G., May-Aug., 1927; gov., Fiji and high commr., W. Pacific, 1929; gov. and commr.-in-ch., Trinidad and Tobago, 1936; resigned, 1938.

FLETCHER COOKE, JOHN.—B. 1911; Barham schol., Malvern Coll., 1926; senr. exhib., St. Edmund Hall, Oxford, 1929; Kitchener schol.; 1st cls. philosophy, politics, economics, 1932; apptd. after compet. exam., ast. prin., C.O., 1934; offr., cls. V, Malayan civ. serv., 1937.
FLETCHER, REGINALD H.—Joined Jamaica civ. serv., post office dept., by compet. exam., 1890; 1st cls. clk., 1908; ch. clk., 1915; ast. postmr., 1921; postmr., Jamaica, 1925.

FLEURET, ARTHUR ISADORE.—B. 1899; mesgr. Defence Force, May-Oct., 1935; adjt., do., May-Dec., 1935; capt., do., May, 1937; ag. col. postmr., July, 1936 to Feb., 1936; ast. col. sec., Jan., 1937.

FLINN, MAJOR WILLIAM HENRY, O.B.E. (1919).—B. 1896; ed. Commr., 14th Apr., 1921; ag. clk., exec. coun., Feb.-Mar., 1922; passed exam. in mod. FLIPPANOE, FREDERICK, F.L.S.—B. 1891; ed. Stoke schl., Surrey; entd. R. Botanic Gardens, Kew, 1913; war serv., France 1916-19; ast. curator, Botanic Gardens, S.S. (Singapore), 1919; local head of dept., Waterfall Gardens, Penang, 1921-37; supt., hot. and forestry dept., Hong Kong, 1937.

FLOOD, J. E. W., C.M.G. (1933)—B. 1886; ed. at Portora Royal Schol., Enniskillen, and Trin. FLOUD, SIR FRANCIS LEWIS CASTLE, K.C.B. (1921), C.B. (1918)—B. 1875; ed. FORANDER, CLARENCE EUGENE.—B. 1893; gen. cler. serv., Ceylon, 1912; salt advr. to govt., Aug., 1934.

HATTIE SPENCER.—Served, A.I.F., Great War, 1914-15, el., senate, Commonwealth of Australia, 1917; mem., standing comttee. on pub. wks., 1920-23; joint comttee. pub. accts., 1924-26; mem., Commonwealth delegn. FOLLOWS, CHARLES GEOFFREY SHIELD.—B. 1896; ed. FORBES, ALASTAIR GRANVILLE.—B. 1908; ed. Blundell's Schol. and Clare Coll., Cambridge, B.A.; called to bar, Gray's Inn, 1932; mag. dist. "F" and govt. offr., E. dist. Dominica, 1936; ag. crown atty., in 1936 and 1937; mag. dist. "E," May, 1937; J.P., Dec., 1936 and visiting just. of prison.

FORBES, Rt. Hon. GEORGE WILLIAM, P.C. (1930).—B. 1889; ed. FORBES, JOHN, B.Sc., A.M.I.C.E., Chart. Civ. Engr.—B. 1902; engr., P.W.D., Hong Kong, 1929.

Rhodesia, 1913-19; Transvaal Border Patrola, 1913; called to the bar, Lincoln's Inn, 1921; atty.-gen., St. Lucia, 1923; mem., exec. and leg. councils; chmn., agril. comsrr., 1924; ag. admstr., St. Lucia, Dec., 1924; ag. ch. just., St. Vincent, July, 1926; res. mag., Jamaica, Apr., 1927; asst. judge, Zanzibar, Aug., 1934; ag. ch. just., Sept., 1936-Mar., 1936; mem., ct. of appl. for Eastern Africa; chmn., riot enquiry coms., Feb., 1936; ag. ch. just., May-Nov., 1938.

FRISBY, ALFRED WILLIAM.—B. 1897; ed. Wadham Coll., Oxford; B.A. (Oxon.); European mast., Penang, Aug., 1923; headmast., Old Victoria Inst., K. Lumpur, Aug., 1929; inspr., schls., Pahang, Jan., 1930; ag. inspr., schls., Malacca, Apr., 1933 and Oct., 1936.

FRITH, GEOFFREY HAMMOND, O.B.E. (1938).—Served in the Great War; just. of peace, Turks and Caicos Is., 1919; ch. clk. to comsrr., clk. to leg. bdr., sec. to bd. of eduon., 1920; sub-inspr., pol., 1922; asst. treas., collr. of cust., postmtr., gen. man. of savings bank, regis. of shipping, 1923; ag. asst. comsrr., mag., etc., 1927; ag. comsrr. and judge, grand ct., Cayman Islands, 1929; sub. treas., etc., Tobago, 1931; treas., St. Lucia, 1933; ag. col. sec., Oct., 1933-Feb., 1936; admstr., various occasions, 1936-38.

FUAD, MUSTAFA.—B. 1888; barrister-at-law, Gray's Inn, 1922; ed. Turkish Schls., St. Paul's Schl., and English Schl., Nicosia, Cyprus; clk., treasy., Cyprus, 1905; inspr., pol. and dep. coroner, 1906; judge, dist. ct., Limassol, 1913; judge, dist. ct., Nicosia, 1916; mem., legal bd., 1916; puisne judge, sup. ct., 1927; pres., sheri. ct. of appeal, 1927.

FULLER, HON. SIR GEORGE WARBURTON, K.C.M.G. (1919).—M.L.A., Kiama, N.S.W. 1889-94; elected to first H. of B., C. of A., 1901; re-elected, 1903 and 1906; min. of home affairs, C. of A., June, 1909 to Apr., 1910; M.L.A., Wollondilly, N.S.W., 1916-28; col. sec., N.S.W., Nov., 1916-20; premier, 1922-26; agt.-gen., N.S.W., 1928-31.
FULTON, ADAM, M.R.C.V.S.—B.1897; lieut., May, 1915; capt., 1916; on active serv., Gallipoli, Egypt and Suez, France and Italy, 1915-18; demob., Jan., 1919; vety. offr., Gold Coast, May, 1926.

FURNESS, SIR ROBERT HOWARD, K.T. BACH. (1929).—B. 1880; ed. King William's Coll., Isle of Man; solr. (hons.), 1902; practised at bar of B. Honduras, 1906-13; regis.-gen., B. Honduras, 1913; served with B. W. I. Regt., 1915-19; called to bar, Lincoln's Inn, 1919; mag., Tanganyika Territory, Jan., 1920; senr. mag., Apr., 1921; solr. gen., Trinidad, Nov., 1923; ch. just., Barbados, Oct., 1926; do., Jamaica, 1936.

FURNNESS-SMITH, CECIL, Barrister-at-Law.—B. 1890; ed. Birkenhead Schl. and St. John's Coll., Camb.; B.A., 1912; LL.B., 1913; astt. dist. comsrr., G. Coast, Apr., 1914; dist. comsrr., 1921; called to bar, Inner Temple, 1922; crown coun., Mar., 1925; ag. solr.-gen., May-Oct., 1927; ag. circuit judge, 1929; solr.-gen., Tanganyika Territory, Aug., 1932; ag. atty.-gen. in 1933; atty.-gen., Zanzibar, Mar., 1936.

FURSE, MAJOR R. D., C.M.G. (1935), D.S.O.—B. 1887; ed. Eton and Balliol Coll., Oxford, B.A.; astt. priv. sec. to sec. of state (Mr. Lewis now Lord Harcourt), Dec., 1910 to Aug., 1914; on active service in 1st King Edward's Horse, K.O.
GITTENS, Darnley Erroll Wyndham.—B. 1890; ent. civ. serv., Barbados, 1913; income tax commr., 1931; col. treas., 1935.

GITTINS, John Wansborough, B.A.—B. 1906; ed. Blundell's and Exeter Coll., Oxford; cadet, Gold Coast, 1928; resigned (ill-health), 1929; cadet, Fiji, 1930; passed cadet, 1932; dist. commr., 1933; clk. to couns., 1933; aast. sec., secr., 1937; re-designated dist. offr., 1938.
GLASHAN, H. W.—B. 1884; ed. Aberdeen Univ.; M.B., Ch.B., 1907; A.M.O., Pietermaritzburg ment. hosp., 1909; supt., Fort Beaufort ment. hosp., 1921; supt., Pietermaritzburg ment. hosp., 1931.

GLEN, James Alexander, M.A.—Ed. Trinity Coll., Dublin; 2nd lieut., R.G.A., Dec. 1915; lieut., July 1917; ag. capt., Nov., 1918; served in France and Palestine; demob., Jan., 1919; aast. dir. of edn., 'Iraq, Apr., 1920; ag. adviser, Min. of educn., Apr., 1922.

GLENWAY, Vincent G., C.M.G. (1937), O.B.E. (1929), M.A. Oxon. (hons. nat. sci.), F.G.S., Diploma, Forestry.—Aast. dist. commr., E.A.P., Dec., 1913; ag. dist. commr., N. Frontier, Jan., 1914 to June, 1919; offr. in charge, Somali disarmament, N. Frontier dist., Mar., 1919 (A.G.S. Med.); seconded as dist. commr., Br. Somaliland, Mar.-Sept., 1920; aast. dist. commr., Oct., 1922; ag. dist. commr., Sept., 1922; dist. commr., 1926; Abyssinian mission, 1927-28; ag. prov. commr., 1930-31, and from Mar., 1933; senr. dist. commr., 1934; prov. commr., 1935; gov. and comdr.-in-ch., Somaliland, 1939.

GLOVER, William Edward, M.B., Ch.B. (Aberdeen), D.T.M. and H. (Cambridge), cert. of Lond., S.T.M. (with distinc.).—B.1888; ed. Westminster Schl. and Aberdeen Univ.; W.A.M.S., 1913; temp. capt. in Army (gen. list), July, 1915 to Mar., 1916; seconded for serv., R.A.M.C., Sept., 1916 to Sept., 1919; S.M.O., Nigeria, Jan., 1928; D.D.M.S., Dec., 1935.

GODBOLT, S.B.—B. 1884; clk., treasy., Cape 1901; Transvaal, 1905; Union of S. Africa, 1910; prin. clk., treasy., Union of S. Africa, 1920; ch. clk., grade II, 1924; ditto, grade I, 1928; commrs., pensions, Julv., 1932.
GODDARD, Harry Frederick.—B. 1902; apptd. after compet. exam., cler. offr., C.O., 4th May, 1920.
GODDARD, Major Theodore Allan.—B. 1883; inspr., pol., Barbados, 1922; aast. sup't, fire brig. (conjunct), 1923; dep. commr., pol., 1936.

GODDEN, Henry James, M.B.E. (1929)—B. 1877; telegraphist, Basutoland, 1902; postmr., 1907; clk. to aast. commr., 1917; ag. aast. commr., 1922; passed 1st grade Sesuto, 1922; ch. clk., mast. of ct. and regist., 1924.

GODFREY, Sir Joseph Ed., Kt. Bach. (1914).—B. 1858; M.B. and C.M., Edin., 1882; gov. med. offr., B. Guiana, Feb., 1883; med. inspr. of estates hospitals, May, 1898; deputy regist. of births, deaths and marriages, 1898; deputy chmn., cent. bd. of health, 1902; acted on sev. occasions as surg.-gen., regist.-gen., and chmn. of cent. bd. of health; apptd. surg.-gen., regist.-gen. of b. d. and m., chmn. cent. bd. of health, and offl. mem. of ct. of policy, and comb. ct., July, 1904; dep. chmn. of bd. of agric., 1905; chmn. of poor law commrs., 1906; del. for B. Guiana and Bahamas to Internat. Leprosy Conf., Bergen, 1909.

GODELEY, Lieut.-Gen. Sir Alexander John, K.C.B., K.C.M.G.—B. 1867; lieut., Royal Dublin Fusiliers, 1886; adjutant, 1889; capt., 1895; major, 1897; colonel, 1905; apptd. comdt. New Zealand Forces, 1910, with local rank of major-general; comdg. New Zealand expeditionary force in Egypt and at Dardanelles, 1914-15; gazetted major-general, 1915; temp. lieut.-gen. in command of an Anzac army corps, France, 1916; lieut.-gen., 1918; gov. and comdr.-in-ch., Gibraltar, 1928; foreign decorations: Legion of Honour, Croix de Commandeur (1917); Order of the Crown, Grand Officier (Belgian) 1917; Order of the White Eagle (Serbian), 1917; Croix de Guerre (Belgian), 1918.

GODSALL, Walter Douglas, B.A. (Oxon.).—B. 1901; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Dec., 1923; attd. N'Eliai kach., Jan., 1924; ditto, Badulla kach., Aug., 1924; ditto, Kegalla kach., Mar., 1926; office ast., Ratnapura kach., Dec., 1926; ditto, Galle kach., Mar., 1929; ag. ast. govt. ast., Kandy, Jan., 1930; do., Galle, May, 1934; temp. attd. C.O., Sept., 1933 to Feb., 1934; addnl. ast. govt. ast., Matara, maddn., Oct., 1934; ast. contr., estab., Mar., 1935; contr., estab. in 1935 and 1937.

GODWIN-AUSTEN, Robert Annesley, F.R.G.S.—B. 1885; ed. St. Lawrence Coll., Ramsgate; employed on rly. and land surveys, Canada, 1910-14; served with line and field survey units, France, 1915-19; ment. in desps.; rly. survey, Persia, 1920-21; ast. survr., N. Rhodesia, 1922; dir., roads, 1927; dep. dir., surveys, Tanganyika, 1928; dir., land regist., and surveys, Cyprus, Sept., 1933; Br. del. of Evkaf, Mar., 1936.

GOLDBURG, Joseph.—B. 1886; entd. crown agents' office, 1902; ast. head, gen. dept., 1919; ast. head, finance dept., 1922; ast. head, shipping dept., 1925; ast. head, finance dept., 1930; dep. head, finance dept., May, 1933; astt., W. African Currency bd., 1912-33.
GOLDSCHMIDT, Lt.-Col. G. T., D.L.I.—Offr. cmdg., troops and Mauritius territorial force, Nov., 1937.

GOLDSWAIN, Clement Tyson.—Ed. Publ. Schl., Grahamstown; clk., postal dept., Dec., 1896; clk., Prime Minister's office, Capetown, 1902; clk., mag's. office, Prieska, July, 1906; De Aar, 1908; Dordrecht, 1911; Mossel Bay, 1918; ast. mag., Malmsbury, 1920; mag., Durbanville, 1920; Bellville, 1926; Robertson, 1929; Lydenburg, 1931; Standerton, 1933; Malmsbury, 1934; Kimberley, 1937.

GOLLAN, Sir Henry Cowper, Kt. Bach. (1921), C.B.E. (1918).—B. 1868; ed. at Charterhouse; M.A., Edin. (1887); called to the bar, Mid. Temp., Jan., 1891, N. circuit; priv. sec. to Col. Lugard, commr. and comdt., W.A.F.F., Sept. 1st, 1899; priv. sec. to H.C. of Nor. Nig., Jan.-Sept., 1900; ag. sec. to the admnstr., June-Aug., 1900; atty.-gen. of Nor. Nig., Sept., 1900; ag. ch. just., Nov., 1900-Apr., 1901; ch. just., Nov., 1901; prepared criminal code for N. Nigeria, 1904; compiled proc. of N. Nigeria, 1905; ch. just., Bermuda, 1904; prepared Cts. Consolidating Act, Bermuda, Dec., 1905; chmn. of produce comm., 1905 and of aliens comm., 1906; chmn. of bd. of educn., Bermuda, July, 1907; atty.-gen., Trinidad, 1911; atty.-gen., Ceylon, 1918; ch. just., Hong Kong, 1924; chmn., salaries comm., 1928-29; ret., Aug., 1930.

GOMES, Stanley Eugene, B.A.—B. 1901; ed. Jesus Coll. (Camb.); called to bar (Gray's Inn), 1924; ag. S.M., Br. Guiana, 1927 and 1928; S.M., 1929; ag. ast. atty. gen., 1929-33; ast. atty. gen., 1933; ag. atty. gen., 1934, 1936 and 1939. as premier, 1929; re-elect., g.e., 1934; premier and prov. treas., Sask., 1934; resigned as premier when apptd. min., agr. in Rt. Hon. W. L. Mackenzie King's cabinet, 1935; first elec., H. of C., Canada at by-elec., Jan., 1936.

GARRAN, SIR ROBERT RANDOLPH, K.C.M.G. (1920), KT. BACH. (1917), C.M.G. (1901).—B. 1867; barrister-at-law; sec. to drafting comtes. of Australian Fed. Convention, 1897-8; sec. to atty-gen.'s dept., C. of A., Jan., 1901, and parl. draftsman; solr.-gen., 1916-32; ohmn., Canberra univ.; author of "The Coming Commonwealth," 1897, and joint author (with the Hon. Sir John Quick) of "The Annotated Constitution of the Austn. Commonwealth," 1901.

GARVY, RONALD HERBERT, M.A. (Cantab.), F.R.A.I.—B. 1903; ed. Trent Coll. and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; cadet, Fiji and Western Pacific services, Nov., 1926; astt. dist. offr., Mala, Apr., 1929; dist. offr., Oct., 1930; astt. sec., W. Pacific high comm., Aug., 1932; ag. sec. in 1932, 1933, 1934 and 1938; ag. resdt. comsnr., Gilbert and Ellice Is. (on secondment), 1938.

GEERING, SIDNEY.—Temp. asst. to Irrig. engrnr., P.W.D., B. Guiana, Aug., 1916 to Oct., 1918; ag. dist. engrnr., Oct., 1918 to Aug., 1920; 1st cls. overseer, July, 1919; clk. of works, Gambia, Aug., 1920.

GELDENHUYSEN, FRANS EDUARD.—B. 1889; B.A., 1910, Victoria Coll., Stellenbosch, S.A.; studied in U.S.A. at Univs. of Cornell, Wisconsin, Illinois and California; B.Sc., Cornell Univ., 1913 and Ph.D., 1917; teacher of nature study and agrl. science, Grey Coll. Sch., 1918; edr., "Die Landbou-weekblad," Bloemfontein, S.A., 1919; first ch., divn. of agrl. economics and marketing, dept. of agrl., S.A., 1926; under-sec. for agrl., 1926; dir., forestry, 1931; comm., coun., Rome, Oct., 1934.
GERMONT, ROBERT CHARLES, M.D. (Lans.), M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.).—B. 1887; med. offr., Botabelo leper settlmt., Basutoland, 1933.
GIBBON, JOHN FRANK, M.B., C.M. (Edin.)—B. 1869; entd. med. serv., Trinidad, Jan., 1899; dist. med. offr., Jan., 1916.
GRANNUM, FRANK NEVILLE, M.B., Ch.B., D.T.M. & H.—B. 1896; ed. Harrison Coll., Barbados and Edin. Univ.; pol.med.offr., dist. "F," Barbados, 1931; sanitation offr., 1936.
GRANT, DONALD KEITH SHAPTBURY, O.B.E. (1939).—Asst. conservator of forests, E.A.P., Dec., 1912; conservator of forests, Tanganyika Territory, Dec., 1920.

GRANT, EZRAEL AUGUSTUS.—Ent. British Honduras civ. serv. after compet. exam., as copyist to survey dept., Nov., 1899; 3rd cls.clk.(asst. keeper), King's warehouse, Feb., 1906; 2nd cls.clk., P.W.D., Apr., 1907; on active serv.with B.W.I. Regt. during war, Apr., 1916 to July, 1919; ag. 1st cls.clk., P.W.D., Aug., 1919 to Mar., 1920; acct., P.W.D., Apr., 1920; ag.ch.clk.and storekeeper, P.W.D., Apr., 1921; ag.asst.inspr., schls., during 1921 to 1923; asst.inspr., schls., provisionally, Apr., 1923; barrister-at-law, Inner Temple, 1928; dist.comsnr., Orange Walk, Nov., 1928; ag. atty.-gen., Apr. to May, 1931; ag.dist.comsnr., Belize and offl.admstr.and offl.recr.from Apr., 1931; dist.comsnr., N.dist., 1938.

GRANT, RICHARD WILLIAM, M.C.—B. 1897; ed. Jones' Schl. Mon. and Royal Mil. Acad., Woolwich; cadet, S.S., Nov., 1920; attd., col.sec.'s office, Dec., 1920; offr., cls.V, dist.offr., N.Tchab., Nov., 1923; offr., cls.IV, Nov., 1927; dist.offr., Bentong, Dec., 1929; asst.advr., K.Tinggi, Mar., 1930; offr., cls.III, Nov., 1932; asst.advr., Segamat, Oct., 1933; ag.1st asst.sec.(A), S.S., 1935; pres., town bd., J.Bahru, Feb., 1937; offr., cls.II, Nov., 1937.

GRANTHAM, ALEXANDER WILLIAM GEORGE HERDER, M.A. (Oxford), Barrister-at-Law.—B. 1899; cadet, Hong-Kong, Nov., 1922; attd., C.S.O., 1925; 2nd pol.mag., Sept., 1929; sec.retrenchment comsn., July, 1930; asst.to P.M.G.in addn., July, 1930; dist.offr., South,in addn., Dec., 1930; extra asst.col.sec., and sec.to harbr. advisory comte., 1931; 1st pol.mag.in addn., 1932; 2nd asst.col.sec.and dep.clk.of comns., June, 1933; Imp.Def.Coll., 1934; extra asst.col.sec., Apr., 1935; col.sec., Bermuda, 1935; ag.gov.in 1936 and 1937; col.sec., Jamaica, 1938.

GRANTHAM, WILLIAM M.A.—B. 1898; tempy.asst.engnr., P.W.D., Perak, F.M.S., Feb., 1924; asst.engnr., P.W.D., F.M.S., Feb., 1924; asst.drng.e. and irrig.engnr., Jan., 1932; drng.e. and irrig.engnr., Krian, Feb., 1933; sr.drng.e. and irrig.engnr., Malay States, May, 1936; ag.asst.advr., drng.e. and irrig., Malay States, July, 1936.
GRAVER, GEORGE STANLEY, A.M.I.C.E.—B. 1898; ed. Devonport High Schl.; war serv., 1917-19; engnr., P.W.D., Hong Kong, 1923; ag.exec.engnr., May-Dec., 1933.

GRAY, CHAS. EDGAR.—B. 1904; cler.asst., offl.recr.'s office, Br. Guiana, 1919; 6th cls.offr., col.secr.'s office, 1921; 5th cls.offr., 1924; 4th cls.offr., 1926; sec.to prisons comte., 1928; 2nd cls.elk., 1933; ag.prin.clk.and elk., exec.and leg.couns.in 1932 and 1933; cls.I.clk., 1934; sec., floods investigation comte., 1934; ag.pvte.sec.and A.D.C.to O.A.G., 1934 and 1935; asst.dist.comsnr., 1936.

GRAY, GEOFFREY LEICESTER.—B. 1905; cadet, N.Borneo, Jan., 1925; asst.prot., lab., May, 1925; ag.dep.prot., lab.and asst.secr.for Chinese affrs., Oct., 1928; ag.dist.offr., Aug., 1932; dist.offr., Jan., 1934; under-secr., July, 1935; mag., 1st cls., Oct., 1937; gov't sec., Feb., 1938; adml.secr.judge, Mar., 1938; ag.judge, high cls., July, 1938.

GRAY, JOHN, B.A., M.D., B.Ch., B.A.O.(Dub.), L.M., Rot.Hosp.(Dub.), certif.L.S.T.M.—B. 1882; med.offr., gen.hosp., Singapore, Apr., 1909; grade II, F.M.S., Apr., 1910; sr.surg., class III, Oct., 1913; ch.med.offr., Malacca, Jan., 1919; ch.med.offr., Singapore, Nov., 1920; ag.prin.civil med.offr., S.S., and tempy.M.L.C., for various periods, 1923-31; state surg., Kedah, Oct., 1931; senr.med.offr., St.Helena, 1936.

GRAY, JOHN MACFARLANE, L.R.C.P., L.R.C.S.(I), L.M.(Rot.), D.P.H.(Univ.Dub.)—B. 1898; health offr., med.dept., Hong Kong, 1933; med.offr., Uganda, 1925-33; ag.M.O.H., 1934; ag.venerable diseases offr., 1936; H.O.urban coun., 1936; surgeon sub-lt., R.N.V.R., 1917-18.

GRAY, JOHN MILNER.—B. 1889; ed. Perse Schl.and King's Coll., Cambridge, M.A.; admitted solr., sup.ct., 1923; called to bar, Gray's Inn, Apr., 1932; war serv.with B.E.F., France (twice wounded); asst.dist.comsnr., Uganda Prot., 1920; mag., 1924; ag.solr.gen., 1929; ag.puisne judge, 1933; judge, sup.ct., Gambia, 1934; O.A.G., May, 1935.

GRAY, R.—B. 1880; clk., treasy., 1902; clk., rev.dept., 1904; dep.recr., rev., Lichtenburg, 1905; clk., rev.dept., Jo'burg, 1908; Pretoria, 1912; prin.clk., 1921; survr., 1928; acctnt., 1929; recr., rev., E.London, 1931; do., Pretoria, 1932; do., Durban, 1933; do., Cape Town, June, 1938.

GRAY, THEODORE GRANT, C.M.G.(1938), M.B., Ch.B. (Aberdeen), M.P.C.—B. 1884; ed. Aberdeen Grammar Schl.and Aberdeen Univ.; asst.med.offr., 1911; med.supt., Nelson (N.Z.) mental hosp., 1920; med.supt., Auckland mental hosp., 1924; dep.inspr.gen.mental hosp., N.Z., 1925; dir.gen., 1927.

GRAY, MAJOR WILLIAM BAIN, C.B.E.(1935).—B. 1886; ed. George Watson's Coll., Edinburgh; Univ.of Edinburgh; M.A., 1st cls.hons., Gladstone Prize and Ph.D., and New Coll., Oxford, B.Litt.; some time Carnegie Research Fellow; lect., Tech.Coll., Brighton; examnr., Univ.of Edinburgh, Oxford and Cambridge joint bd.and Oxford local exams.; 2nd lieut., 6th Batt., the Royal Scots, Feb., 1912; mob., Aug., 1914; adjt., Jan.-Dec., 1915; capt., Aug., 1916; gen.staff, Aldershot commd., Dec., 1918-Apr., 1921; major, Army Educln.Corps, Nov., 1920; major, R.A.R.O., Mar., 1923; comsnr., educn.Br.Guiana, Oct., 1924;(appt.altered to dir., Nov., 1927); mem., educn.comsn., Nov., 1924; chmn., Georgetown public free liby., May, 1926; mem., ct.of policy and combined ct., June, 1927; chmn.diamond and gold industries comsn., Sept., 1927; mem.first exec.and leg.couns.underreformed constitution, July, 1928; ag.col.sec.and gov't.dep.on various occasions; chmn.primary educn.comte., 1938; admnr.and col.sec.St.Vincent.Windward Islds., 1938.

GREAVES, ARTHUR WILLIAM.—B. 1898; ed. King Edward's High Schl., B'ham; insp.pol., K.Lumpur, July, 1919; 1st inspr., cent.stn., K.Lumpur, June, 1923; asst.supvr., cust., F.M.S., July, 1923; aspt., cust., prev.br., F.M.S., Nov., 1923; aspt.trade and cust., Nov., 1931; aspt., cust.and excise, Ipoh, Nov., 1935; ag.dep.comsnr., Perak, Feb., 1936.

GREAVES, AUBREY VERNON, M.B. (Tor.), M.C.P. & S. (Ont.), D.T.M. (L'pool).—B. 1886; ed. Harrison Coll., Barbados and Univ. of Toronto; Milne med. in trop. med. Univ. of L'pool; on active serv. as capt., Canadian A.M.C., 1916-19; govt. bacteriologist and asst. med. offr. of health and med. offr. in charge V.D. clinic and infant welfare clinic in addn., Bahamas, 1926; sent to Miami, Florida, to investigate epidemic of small-pox, 1926; ag. ch. med. offr., Apr.-Dec., 1927; bacteriologist, Bahamas, 1928; asst. bacteriologist, Hong Kong, 1929; bacteriologist, 1931; lect. in forensic med. and toxicology, Univ. of Hong Kong.

Univ. of L'pool; on active serv. as capt., Canadian A.M.C., 1916-19; gov't. bacteriologist and asst. med. offr. of health and med. offr. in charge V.D. clinic and infant welfare clinic in addn., Bahamas, 1926; sent to Miami, Florida, to investigate epidemic of small-pox, 1926; ag. ch. med. offr., Apr.-Dec., 1927; bacteriologist, Bahamas, 1928; asst. bacteriologist, Hong Kong, 1929; bacteriologist, 1931; lect. in forensic med. and toxicology, Univ. of Hong Kong.

Tem., 1880; ag. solr.-gen., Barbados, Aug., 1884, to July, 1885, and July, 1886, to Jan., 1887, when confirmed; J.P., Sept., 1885; escheator-gen., 1887; Q.C. for Barbados, 1890; mem. exec. coun., 1890; acted as atty.-gen., July to Dec., 1897, and June to Dec., 1891; mem. of house of assem. and of bd. of educ., 1888-1902; atty.-gen., Mar., 1896; ch. just., Apr., 1902; ret.

raw_text: GRECH, WYNHAM, LL.D. (Malta), 1913.—Called to the bar (Grays Inn), 1916; 2nd lieut., R.F.C., 1916; capt., R.A.F., 1918; sp. serv., Malta and Rome, 1917; attd., Italian Sup. Comd., 1918-19 (Chevalier, Crown of Italy); transf., unemployed list (R.A.F.), 1919; asst. legal adviser, Seychelles, 1921; ag. legal adviser and crown pros., 14th Mar., 1921 to 25th Sept., 1922 and in 1924; legal adviser and crown pros., 1924; ag. ch. just., Dec., 1923 to Jan., 1924, Mar.-Oct., 1925 and Apr. to July, 1927.

Comd., 1918-19 (Chevalier, Crown of Italy); transf. unemployed list (B.A.F.), 1919; asst. legal adviser, Seychelles, 1921; ag. legal adviser and crown pros., 14th Mar., 1921 to 25th Sept., 1922 and in 1924; legal adviser and crown pros., 1924; ag. ch. just., Dec., 1923 to Jan., 1924, Mar.-Oct., 1925 and Apr. to July, 1927.

Clifton Coll. and Balliol Coll., Oxford; 1st cls., hon. schl. mod. languages (French) 1912; pvt., 28th County of London Regt., Aug., 1914; lieut., R.A.O.C., Nov., 1915; capt., May, 1916; major, May, 1918; D.A.D.O.S. 15th (Scottish) Divn.; ment. in despe.; attd. mission to Lithuania, Oct., 1919; junr. examr., clearing office (enemy debts) B. of T., Sept., 1920; senr. examr., Jan., 1921; asst. claims clk., Jan., 1922; attd. to govt. agt., Mixed Arbitral Tribunal, July, 1928; temp. admstve. offr., D.O., Nov., 1929; C.O., Feb., 1930; asst. pty. sec. to S. of S. for the Cols., Oct., 1930; asst. prin., 1st Oct., 1934; pty. sec. to partly. under sec. of state for cols. (Lord
HASLAM, HAROLD STANLEY, M.C., A.M.Inst. C.E.—B. 1887; ed. Bristol Univ.; sect. engrnr., constrn. dept., F.M.S. rly., May, 1919; divnl. engrnr., July, 1929; dist. engrnr., Aug., 1935.
HAWS, HENRY FRANCIS.—B. 1894; ed. Imp. Coll., Windsor; admitted, solr., 1920; legal advr., K.U.R. & H., 1927.
HAWS, RICHARD BRUNEL, M.B., M.R.C.P. (Lond.), D.T.M. (Lond.).—B. 1893; med. offr., Kedah, June, 1923; prof. of medicine, King Edward VII Med. Coll., Singapore, Apr., 1927.

HAWKINS, GERALD.—B. 1891; B.A., London Univ.; on mily. serv., 1914-20 (capt.); cadet, S.S. Nov., 1920; asst. to land office, Penang, Dec., 1920; dep. collr. of land rev., Penang, Jan., 1921; offr., cls. V., May, 1919; cls. IV., May, 1923; sec., Pahang, Apr., 1926; dist. offr., M., 1928; cls. III., May, 1928; assessor, wild life commn., Aug., 1930; asst. advr., Segamat, May, 1931; offr., cls. II, May, 1933; attd., co-op. socs. dept., Sept., 1934; offr. cls. Ia., 1937.

HAY, ALISTAIR WILLIAM.—B. 1899; ed. Tombridge Schi. and Exeter Coll., Oxford; cadet, S.S., Nov., 1921; offr. cls. V, ag. inspr. Chinese schls., Nov., 1924; passed cadet, June, 1925; 2nd asst. dir., educn. (Chinese), Sept., 1925; offr., cls. IV, Nov., 1925; 3rd mag., S'pore, Jan., 1932; offr. cls. III, asst. prot. Chinese, S'pore, Nov., 1933, do., Malacca, Jan., 1936; ag. prot., Chinese, Penang, Aug., 1937.
HAY, CLIFFORD H., C.M.G., C.B.E., (1928), M.V.O.—Sec., and perm. head, premier's dept., New S. Wales, Mar., 1916; under sec. and P.H., 1924; agt.-genl. for N.S. Wales, London, May, 1938.

HAY, MORTIMER CECIL.—B. 1891; ed. The Schi., Bishop Stortford and St. Catherine's Coll., Cambridge (exhbr.); M.A., Cantab.; cadet, F.M.S., Nov., 1913; passed cadet, July, 1916; offr., cls. V, Dec., 1916; offr., cls. IV, Nov., 1920; dep. contrlr. of lab., Klang, Dec., 1920; dep. contr. lab., Kuala Lumpur, Sept., 1925; offr., cls. III, Nov., 1925; asst. treas., F.M.S. and state treas., Selangor, Jan., 1930; cls. II, Nov., 1930; Br. advr., Perlis, Dec., 1930; ag. legaladvr., Johore, Mar., 1933; cls. B, July, 1934; dist. judge and 1st., mag., Penang, June, 1936; dep. legaladvr., F.M.S., Apr. 1937; dep. commr. of lands, cls. IA., 1938.

HAYCRAFT, SIR THOS. WAGSTAFFE, KT. BACH. (1919).—B.A., St. John's Coll., Oxon; called to the bar, In. Tem., 1885; practised in S.E. circuit and Sussex sessions; examr. of the ct., 1889 to 1899; apptd. arbitrator on bd. of Lond. chain of arbitn., 1897; pres. dist. ct., Larnaca, Cyprus, 1899; pol. mag., Gibraltar, 1911; puisne judge, Mauritius, 1913; ch. just., Grenada, 1916; ch. just., Palestine, 1921; ret., Mar., 1927; author of "Executive Powers in relation to Crime and Disorder."

HAYDEN, JAMES J.—B. 1883; ed. St. Mary's Coll., Dublin; clasped in feudal and English law, Dublin Univ. (dual gold medallist); admitted solr., sup. ct. of judicature, Ireland, 1906; called to Irish bar, 1926; reglar., high ct., Uganda, Jan., 1921; also addnl. dist. mag., 1st cls. admin., rev. authority, reglar. of companies, patents, designs, trade-marks, documents and business names, custodian of enemy property and contr. of local clearing house; mag., Uganda, Mar., 1927; offr. recr. and reglar., trade marks and patents, Hong Kong, Apr., 1934; puisne judge for appl., Nov., 1934; puisne judge, Dec., 1935; ag. ch. just., May-June, 1936; puisne judge, Kenya, Mar., 1937.

HAYDOCK-WILSON, HUGH.—B. 1897; ed. Bedford Modern Schi.; on war serv., Artists Rifles (London R.) and Manchester Regt., 1915-19; ent. civ. serv., Br. Guiana as 4th cls. clk., C.S.O., Apr., 1921; 3rd cls. clk., July, 1924; capt., B. G. Militia (Art.), 1922-25; 2nd cls. clk., July, 1928; pvt. sec. to O.A.G., Oct. to Nov., 1928; hon. A.D.C. to gov. Feb., 1929; sec., colonization comtee., Jan., 1929 to Mar., 1930; ag. prin. clk., C.S.O., Nov., 1930 to Mar., 1931; asst. commr., pol., Nigeria, May, 1931.

HAYES-SADLER, CECIL WILLIAM, M.A. (Oxon.).—B.1892; ed. Eton and Trinity Coll., Oxon; served in France with R. Engnr., (Signals), 1914-18; French Croix de Guerre, 1917; asst. dist. commr., Kenya Col., 1920; ag. senr. asst. sec., 1926; attchd. D.O., 1927; ag. prin. asst. col. sec., 1933; ag. senr. asst. col. sec., 1934, and Mar., 1935 to Dec., 1936; ag. dep. col. sec., Dec., 1936 to Mar., 1938; ag. asst. ch. sec., Oct., 1938.

HAYWOOD, WILLIAM FREDERICK.—B. 1891; clk., G.P.O., 1905-16; postal suupt., 1927; acct. 1928; sec. to comm. of currency, 1932; ag. P.M.G., 1932, 1936 and 1937; nom. commr. of currency, 1932; nom. M.L.C., 1936; offl. M.L.C., 1937.

HEAD, W. G.—B. 1899; apptd. after compet. exam., asst. clk., C.O., Oct., 1916; on mil. ser. from July, 1917, to Oct., 1919; cler. offr., 1st Jan., 1920; higher cler. offr., 19th May, 1925; seconded for service with Br. economic mission to Australia 10th May, 1928 to 3rd Mar., 1929; staff offr., 18th Dec., 1934.

HEAPE, WILLIAM LESLIE.—B. 1896; sec. sec., Somaliland Prot., 1919; attd. for duty, C.O., 1926; resumed subet. aspt., Somaliland, 1926; asst. sec., Tanganyika Territory, 1929; col. sec. and reglar.-gen., Grenada, 1935; admstr., St. Vincent, July-Sept., 1938.

HEARNE, HORACE HECTOR.—B. 1882; barrister-at-law, (Lincoln's Inn); asst. dist. comm., Uganda, Jan., 1916; 1st cls. mag., Aug., 1918; dist. mag., Jan., 1921; senr. mag., Nov., 1924; puisne judge on various occasions, 1925-26 and 1928-30; ag. chan. of diocese, Nov., 1929; mem. H.M.'s ct. of appl. for Eastern Africa, 1930; chan. of diocese, 1932; puisne judge, Tanganyika Territory, 1933; ag. ch. just., 1935 and 1936; puisne just., Caylon, 1937.

HEARST, SIR WILLIAM HOWARD, K.C.M.G. (1917).—B. 1864; ed. pub. schls., Collingwood Coll., Ont., and Toronto Univ.; called to the bar, 1888; K.C., 1908; prominent legal practitioner at Sud. Ste. Marie, Ontario; now head of firm Hearst Rowland and Atkin; has been counsel in all more important civil and criminal cases in Algoma district in recent years; unsuccessful cand. for legis. assembly, Ontario, 1894; elec. at g.e. 1908, 1911, and 1914; min. of lands, forests and mines in Whitney administration, 1911; premier of Ontario, 1914; also min. of agric.; defeated at g.e., Oct., 1919; resign. with his govt., Nov., 1919; mem., internat. joint comtn. (Can. section), 23rd Feb., 1920.

HEATH, A. P.—B. 1897; ed. Uppingham; on mil. serv., Aug., 1915; 2nd lieut., Feb., 1917 to Aug., 1917; temp. clk., Min. of Nat. Serv., Nov., 1917; transf. O.S.O., Mar., 1919; apptd. after compet. exam., cler. offr., O.S.O., Nov., 1921.
HEATH, CHARLES EDWARD.—B. 1890; asst. govt. printer, Gold Coast, 1915; ag. govt. printer, 1920, 1921, 1923 and 1932.
HEATH, HENRY WYLDE EDWARDS.—B. 1912; ed. Dean Close Schi., Cheltenham; sub-inspr., pol., Leeward Is., Sept., 1931; asst. supt., pol., Hong Kong, Jan., 1936.

HEATON, GEORGE HERBERT.—B.1896; ed. privately and Oxford Univ.; lieut., 1/1st Kent Regt. and R.F.C., 1915; H.M. Borstal instn., 1924; supt., prisons, Uganda, 1927; commr., prisons, Tanganyika Territory, 1931; commr., prisons, Kenya, 1938.

HERDEN, GEORGE BRENTNALL, A.M.I.R.E.—B. 1886; ed. Bedford Schi.; ltd. constr. supt. engrn., posts and tels. dept., Nigeria, May, 1909; suptg. tel. engrn., Jan., 1914; asst. engrn.-in-charge, Jan., 1921; P.M.G., July, 1928; served with

HODGSON, DUDLEY HENRY, B.A. (Oxon.), Dip. in Forestry.—B. 1899; asst. conserv., forests, F.M.S., Sept., 1921; conserv. forests, Oct., 1930; ag. dep. dir., forestry, S.S. and F.M.S., in 1931, 1932 1933 and 1936.

HODGSON, JOHN LESLIE WILBERHAM.—B. 1902; ed. Haileybury Coll.; asst. commr., pol., Nigeria, 1928; senr. asst. aspt., pol., 1937; A.D.C. and prte. sec. to gov., 1934-36; asst. dist. offr., Somaliland, 1938.

HODKIN, CAPT. THOMAS WILLIAM, Fellow R. Econ. Soc., Fellow R. Statistical Soc., Fellow Inst. of Commerce, Assoc., Chartered Inst. of Secs., Assoc., Inst. of Bankers.—B. 1885; ed. Manchester Grammar Sch., Manchester Univ. and Trinity Coll., Oxford; dipl. in educn., Oxford; served in France with E. Lancs. Regt., 1914-18; attd. to gen. staff; educn. offr., 66th divn., 1918-19; instr., schl. of educn. for offrs., Catterick, 1919; instr., schl. of instrn. for offrs., Bedford, 1919-20; instr., W.O. schl. of educn., Shorncliffe, 1920-22; brig. educn. offr., 1st Air Defence Brig., Aldershot, 1922; A.E.C., res. of offrs.; examr. for W.O. in bookkeeping and accountancy; commercial master, Zanzibar, 1922; prin., Arab schl., Mombasa, Kenya, 1927.
HODKINSON, ERIC THOMAS, B.Sc., M.B., Ch.B. (W.W. Rand).—B. 1904; ed. King Edward VII Sch. and Univ. of Witwatersrand, temp. med. offr., Basutoland, 1933; med. offr., 1936.

HODSON, SIR ARNOLD WIENHOLT, K.C.M.G. (1932), C.M.G. (1922), Kt. of Just., Order of Hosp. of St. John of Jerusalem.—Ed. Italy, Mulgrave, Castle, and Felsted; in Central Queensland, 1900-1902; joined Australian Commonwealth Horse for service in S. Africa, 1902; in Transvaal, 1902-4; sub.-inspr., Bech. Prot. pol., 1904; specially employed in connection with Damara war (med. and clasp); mem. of Ngamiand mission, 1906; ag. asst. commr., Mar. to May, 1910, and Mar. to Dec., 1911; dist. commr. and 1st cls. mag., Somaliland, 1912; H.B.M. consul for Southern Abyssinia, 1914; ditto, S.W. Abyssinia, 1923; served part time in European War; present at coronation of Empress Zoditow, 1917; 3rd cls. Star of Ethiopia; African Gen. Ser. Med. and Clasp, Jubaland, 1917-18; attd., Abyssinian army in expedition against Tigre, 1919 (med. and clasp); late capt., Royal Sussex Regt. (Territorials); gov. and commdr-in-chief, Falkland Is. and their dependencies, Dec., 1926; ditto, Sierra Leone, May, 1931; ditto, Gold Coast, 1934; author "Trekking the Great Thirst," "Seven Years in Southern Abyssinia," "Where Lion Reign," joint author of "A Practical Galla Grammar."

HODSON, THOMAS ARTHUR.—B. 1882; ed. Cheltenham and Wadham Coll., Oxford (M.A.); cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., Nov., 1905; seconded asst. supt. of pol., Galle, Dec., 1906; asst. supt. of pol., Galle, July, 1908; ditto, Tangalla, May, 1909; ditto, Matare, Dec., 1909; office asst. to govt. agt., S. Prov., Jan., 1910; pol. mag., Kandy, Feb., 1911; asst. govt. agt., Trincomalee, Nov., 1911; dist. judge, Nuwara Eliya, May, 1914; asst. settlmt. offr. and spea. offr. under Waste Lands Orde., June, 1916; on mil. duty, 1917; addtnl. asst. col. sec., Apr., 1919; asst. govt. agt., Kalutara, May, 1919; addnl. asst. dir., eduon., Mar., 1922; asst. govt. agt., Matale, Mar., 1924; govt. agt., Prov. of Sabaragamuwa, Mar., 1925; ditto, N.W. Prov., Oct., 1926; do., Cent. Prov., Jan., 1931; do., Prov. of Uva, Nov., 1931; govt. agt., Cent. Prov., Oct., 1932.

HOEXTER, HON. OSCAR HENDRIK, K.C. (1929), B.A. (S.A.), B.A. (Cantab.), LL.B. (S.A.)—B. 1893; ed. Rouville Sch., Grey Univ., Coll., Bloemfontein and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; admitted to bar, 1918; practd. as adv. of supr. cr. of S.A. at Bloemfontein, O.F.S. till 1938; judge, high cr., S.W. Africa, 1938.

HOFFMANN, WALTER HEKEL.—B. 1901; ed. B.H.S. Rondebosch and Transvaal Univ. Coll., Pretoria; clk., inland rev. dept., 1918; inspr., co-op. soc's., 1927; pr. clk., treasry., 1934; asst. acct., 1936; secy., pub. debt commrs., 1937; prin. acct., agric. dept., 1938.

HOFMEYR, JAN HENDRIK, M.A., D.Sc., M.P.—B. 1894; ed. S.A. Coll., High Sch., S.A. Coll., Cape Town, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; prof. of classics, Univ. Coll., Johannesburg, 1917; prin., univ. of Witwatersrand, 1919; admstr., Transvaal Prov., 1924; M.P., Johannesburg (North), 1929; min. of interior, educn. and pub. health, 1933; min. of mines, educn., lab. and social welfare, 1936; chancellor, Witwatersrand Univ., 1938.

HOGAN, HON. EDMOND JOHN, M.L.A. (Victoria)—B. 1884; M.L.A., W. Australia, 1913; leader, Victorian parly. lab. party, Apr., 1926; premier and treas., Victoria, May, 1927 to Nov., 1928 and from Dec., 1929 to May, 1932; joined country party, 1935; min., agr. and forests, 1935.

HOGAN, GERALD GEORGE.—B.1886; barrister, 1910 (Victoria); practised, Victoria until 1915; served Australian Field Arty., Egypt and France, 1915-19; rank of major on retirement; mem. in desps.; stip. sp. mag., N. Territory, Australia, 1919; dep. judge, sup. ct., N. Territory, 1920; crown law offr., mandated Territory of New Guinea, 1922; M.E.C. and M.L.C., 1933.

HOBGEN, SIDNEY JOHN, M.A.—B. 1898; ed. Highgate Sch. and Oriel Coll., Oxford; supt., educn. dept., N. Provs., Nigeria, Aug., 1931; senr. educn. offr., Palestine, 1933; ag. dep. dir., various occasions, 1934-37; dep. dir., educn., Jan., 1937.

HOGGAN, WILLIAM EDWARD, A.R.C.S., A.M. Inst. C.E., Whitworth Schl.—B. 1880; ed. City of London Sch. and Imp. Coll. of Science; eng. inspr., C.A., 1910; asst. engr., 1919; dep. head, engrg. inspnt. dept., 1920; ch. insp. engr., and head, engrg. inspnt. dept., 1934.
HOGGAN, LIEUT.-COMMDR. C. B., R.D., N.R.N.—Pilot, Kenya, 1924; senr. do., K.U.R. & H., 1936.

HOLDER, FRANK WILFRED, B.A.(Durham)—B. 1897; ed. Harrison Coll. and Codrington Coll., Barbados; called to bar, Middle Temple, 1924; mem., H. of A. and mem., exec. comtee., Barbados; solr.-gen., Oct., 1936.
HOLDING, RICHARD HENRY.—B. 1898; on mil'y. serv., Apr. 1917 to Mar., 1920; temp. clk., O.S.O., Apr. 1920; apptd. after compet. exam., clk. offr., O.S.O., 6th Oct., 1921.

HOLE, COMMDR. GEORGE FRANCIS, R.N.—B. 1887; cadet, H.M.S. "Britannia," 1902; midshipman, 1904; sub-lieut., 1907; lieut., 1909; commanded H.M.S. "Peterel," 1914-15; invalided and pensioned, 1916; asst. master attendant, Colombo, Ceylon, Mar., 1916; offr. in charge, mine-sweeping Colombo, 1917-19; lieut.-commdr., 1917; priv. sec. and ag. A.D.C. to gov., Apr., 1919; master attendant, Colombo and Galle, July, 1922; harbmr., Hong Kong, 1924; commr., 1927; offl. mem., leg. coun., 1929; dir., air services, 1929; head, Hong Kong delegn. to Shipping Confce., Simla, 1931.

HOLGATE, MAXWELL RUSSELL, M.A.(N.Z.).—B. 1896; asst. mast., Raffles Schi., July, 1920; ar. mast., Sultan Idris Training Coll., Perak, Nov., 1922; ag. prin., do., Sept., 1926 and Apr., 1930; asst. inspr. schls., Singapore and Labuan, Nov., 1930; headmast., King Edward VII Schi., Taiping, Dec., 1930; inspr., schls., Malacca, Nov., 1934; senr. educn. offr., A. Malaya, 1937.
HOLLAND, FRANCIS CHAPLIN, B.Sc. (McGill), A.M.I.C.E., Chtd. Civ. Engr.; B. 1890; asst. engrnr., P.W.D., Malaya, May, 1923; ex. engrnr., May, 1932; ag. sr. ex. engrnr., Aug., 1936.

HOLLAND, CAPT. FRANCIS GEORGE LEOPOLD, M.B.E., (1931).—B. 1884; N.Z., educn. dept., 1902-11; Germany, 1912-14; 2nd lieut., R.F.A., 1915; served in Macedonia, 1915-19; capt., 1917; ment. in despatches; headmstr., govt. educn. scheme, Gilbert and Ellice Is., 1920; dep. commr. (temp. and provln.), 1929; asupt., educn., 1931; ag. dist. offr. at various times, 1928-33.
HOLLAND, JOHN JAMES, B.Sc., B.E., M.Inst.C.E., M.I.M. & Cy.E.—B. 1893; ed., Univ. Coll., Galway; asst. engrnr., P.W.D., Nigeria, Aug., 1928; divl. engrnr., P.W.D., Cyprus, Mar., 1930.

HOLLIDAY, ARTHUR ROBERT.—B.1884; atty. and notary, sup. ct. of S. Africa, 1906; asst. dist. commr., Gold Coast Colony, Jan., 1915; seconded for service in Togoland; dist. polit. offr., Misahoe, Oct., 1917; dist. polit. offr., Lome, 1918; ag. dist. commr., Bole, N. Territories, Gold Coast, Oct., 1918; asst. admstr.-gen., Zanzibar, Jan., 1925; ag. admstr.-gen., pub. trustee, regis. of doc. and land offr. on various occasions, 1925-29; admstr.-gen. and offl. recr., Uganda, 1930; admstr.-gen., offl. recr., pub. trustee, regis.-gen., births, deaths and marriages, regis., companies, trade marks, business names, patents, designs and trade unions, Tanganyika Territory, 1936.

HOLLINGSWORTH, LAWRENCE WILLIAM, M.B.E., (1935), B.A. (Lond.), F.R.Hist.S., M.R.S.T.—B. 1896; ed. Sir Walter St. John's Schi., London Univ., and London Day Training Coll.; on active serv., E. Africa, 1916-19; headmstr., teacher's training schi., Zanzibar educn. dept., 1922; prin., govt., secondary schi., 1935; author "Milango ya History" (Vols. I, II and III); "Jiogora ya Unguja na Pemba", "A Short History of the East Coast of Africa", "Njia za Kufundisha," part author "A School History of Zanzibar."

HOLLIS, SIR ALFRED CLAUD, G.C.M.G. (1934), K.C.M.G. (1927), C.M.G. (11), C.B.E. (1919).—B. 1874; asst. collr., E.A.P., Mar., 1897; employed on transport duty under Uganda Mutiny, 1898; collr., June, 1900; ag. Brit. vice-consul for German East Africa, Apr., 1900 to Feb., 1901; ag. sec. to admstr. and priv. sec. to H.M. commr., Feb., 1901 to Apr., 1905; sec. to the admstr., 1st Apr., 1903; Jubaland expdn., 1900-1, and Nandi expdn., 1905-6 (medal and two clasps); sec. for native affairs and M.L.C., June, 1907; on sp. serv. to Uganda, Oct.-Nov., 1909; ag. ch. sec. to govt., Apr. to Dec., 1911, and Feb. to Oct., 1912; Swahili examiner, 1901 to 1912; col. sec., Sierra Leone, Jan., 1913; ag. govt., Oct., 1913 to Feb., 1914, June to Oct., 1915 and Jan. to Mar., 1916; sec. to provisional admtn., German E. Africa (Tanganyika Territory), Nov., 1916; ag. admstr., Feb. to Nov., 1919; ch. sec., Tanganyika Territory, 1919; ag. gov., Feb. to Oct., 1922; Br. res., Zanzibar Jan., 1924; 1st cls., Order of Brilliant Star, Zanzibar, 1929; gov. and commr.-in-ch., Trinidad and Tobago, 6th Jan., 1930; assumed govt., 22nd Mar., 1930; ret., 1936; author of works on the Massai, Nandi and other E.A. tribes.

HOLLOWAY, JOHN EDWARD.—B. 1890; B.A. (hons. hist.), 1910, Univ. C. of G.H. (Victoria Coll.); res. mast., Grey Coll. Schi., Bloemfontein, 1911-13; mem., coun. of examiners, O.F.S., 1912-13; D.Sc. (Econ.) Lond. (Schl. of Economics), 1917; Hutchinson research medallist; lect., Grey Univ. Coll., 1917-19; Transvaal Univ. Coll., 1919-21; prof., economics, T.U.C., 1921-25; dir., census and statistics, Union of S. Africa, 1925; Union rep. at Internat. Conven. on Statistics, Geneva, 1928; adviser, Union govt., Ottawa Econ. Conf., 1932 and World Econ. Conf., London, 1933; economic advt., treas., Jan., 1934; sec. for finance, Sept., 1937.
HOLMES, LEMLIK BENJAMIN, B.A. (Oxon.).—B. 1906; asst. mast., educn. dept., Hong Kong, Nov., 1929.

HOLMES, STEPHEN LEWIS, M.C. (1918).—B. 1896; King's scholar of Westminster, 1909; 2nd lieut., Royal Garrison Art., July, 1915; served in France and Belgium, 1916-19; adjt.; ag. major; twice ment. in despatches; scholar of Christ Church, Oxford, 1919; apptd. under reconstruction scheme, asst. prin., C.O., 7th Feb., 1921; B.A. (lit. hum.) July, 1921; M.A., June, 1930; pvt. sec. to perm. under sec. (Sir S. Wilson), Feb., 1926; asst. pvt. sec. to S. of S. for dorm. affrs. and for cols. (Mr. L. S. Amery), Feb., 1928; accomp. Sir S. Wilson on visit to W. African cols., Aug. to Nov., 1928; prin., D.O., 10th Dec., 1928; U.K. delegation to monetary and econ. conf., London, 1933; Imp. Defence Coll., 1934; U.K. deleg. to London naval conf., 1935; seconded as senr. sec. to high commr. for the U.K. in Canada, Sept., 1936; ag. high commr. for the U.K., Feb., 1937 and Nov., 1937-Feb., 1938.

HOLMES, WILLIAM, B.A., LL.B. (Dublin).—B. 1901; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Dec., 1923; attd. to Badulla kach., Jan., 1924; ditto, N. Eliya kach., Aug., 1924; ditto, secretariat, June, 1925; pol. mag., Gampola, Dec., 1926; asst. pvt. sec. to govt., Mar., 1927; office mast., Kurunegala kach., Sept., 1927; landing survr., cust., Mar., 1929; pvt. sec. to O.A.G., Apr. to Sept., 1930; landing survr., cust., Feb., 1931; dist. judge, Tangalle, Sept., 1932; asst. contr., establs., Apr., 1934; dep. regis., co-op. socs., July, 1934; addnl. asst. govt. agt., Colombo, Oct., 1934; dep. collr., cust., June, 1935; dist. judge, N. Eliya, Nov., 1935; ag. dep. collr., cust., Oct., 1936; asst. govt. agt., Ratnapura, Sept., 1937.

HOLMES, WILLIAM EDWARD, M.A., M.B., Ch.B., B.A.O., D.P.H. (Trinity Coll., Dublin), D.T.M., D.T.H. (L'pool).—B. 1899; ed., St. Patrick's Cath. Gram. Schi., Mountjoy Schi., Dublin and Dublin Univ.; health offr., Malaya, July, 1928.

HOLMES, WILLIAM FREDERICK, B.A. (Sydney), Assoc., R. Coll. of Science (London) in Geology, Assoc., R. Schi. of Mines (London) in Mining Mem., Inst. of Mining and Metallurgy, F.G.S. (1900)—Demonstr., mine surveying, R. Schi. of Mines, 1901; mining engrnr., Dutch R. Indies, S. Africa, Siam and Gold Coast; senr. inspr., mines, Cold Coast, 1914; sec. for mines, 1922.

HOLTUM, RICHARD ERIC, M.A. (Cantab.), F.L.S.—B. 1895; ed., Bootham Schi., York and St. John's Coll., Camb.; asst. dir. gardens, S.S. Mar., 1922; ag. dir., Feb.-Oct., 1923 and in Feb., 1925, dir., May, 1925.

HOME, BRUCE FORDYCE, L.R.C.P. (Edin.), L.R.C.S. (Edin.), L.R.F.P.S. (Glas.), Dip. Psych. (Edin.).—B. 1896; alienist med. offr., Nigeria, Oct., 1927; asst. med. supt., mental hosp., S'pore, Oct., 1931; med. supt., Aug., 1935.

HONE, CAPT. HERBERT RALPH, M.C., K.C., LL.B. (Hons.)—B. 1896; ed. Vardean Schl., Brighton and London Univ.; enlisted, Inns of Court O.T.C., 1915; 2nd lieut., 18th Lond. HUTCHINS, ERNEST ELWYN.—B. 1886; ed. Exeter Schl.; forestry course, Edinburgh Univ., 1903-04; forest dept., S. Africa, 1904; forest de-marcator, Kenya, 1909; asst. conservator, forests, Kenya, 1911; E. Africa Mounted Rifles, 1915-16; asst. polit. offr., German E. Africa, 1916-21; admstive offr., 1st grade (now dist. offr.), Tanganyika Territory, 1921.

HUTCHINSON, GEORGE STUART MENTETH.—B. 1883; ed. United Services Coll., Westward Ho! and Royal Mily. Coll., Sandhurst; 2nd lieut., unattd. list, 1902; Indian Army, 1903; capt., 1911; major, 1917; Burma Mily. Pol., Southern Shan States, 1910-14; Indian Expedy. Force, France, 1915; ment. in despatches, 1915; invalided, 1917; examining offr., Miny. of Pensions, 1917; regional wounds offr. for Scotland, 1919; apptd. under reconstruction scheme, asst. prin., C.O., 1st Apr., 1924; prin., 1st Oct., 1930.

HUTCHISON, PATRICK HAMILTON.—B. 1902; ed. Edinburgh Acad. and Edin. Univ.; M.A., 1924; LL.B., 1925; admitted solv., Feb., 1927; asst. admstr. gen., Tanganyika Territory, 1929; ag. admstr. gen., offr. recr., pub. trustee, regist., companies, trade marks, patents, designs, trade unions, regist. gen., births, deaths, marriages, house tax and estate duty commnr., 1933-34.

HUTT, ALEXANDER MCDONALD BRUCE.—B. 1904; ed. St. Andrew's Coll., Grahamstown, S.A., and Univ. Coll., Oxford; B.A. (English lit.); cadet, Tanganyika Territory, Jan., 1926; asst. dist. offr., Jan., 1928; ag. dist. offr., May, 1932; seconded, secr., Mar., 1935; dist. offr., Jan., 1938.
HUTTON, ARTHUR JAMES SCOTT.—B. 1891; ed. Allen Glen's; asr. asst. archt., M.P.W.S., Feb., 1936; ag. govt. archt., S.S., May, 1936.
HUTTON, THEODORF RALPH, A.M.I.C.E.—B. 1889; asst. engrnr., P.W.D., F.M.S., Oct., 1919; ag. exec. engrnr., various occasions, 1922-27; exec. engrnr., 1928-32; sr. exec. engrnr., Jan., 1936.
HUXHAM, HAROLD JAMES, C.M.G. (1936).—B. 1889; income tax advr., Ceylon govt., Aug.,

HORNYEY, CAPT. HENRY EPTON, O.B.E. F.R.C.V.S., D.V.S.M.—B. 1890; ed. King's Coll. Sch. and R. Vety. Coll., London; lect. in bacteriology to S.E. Agrl. Coll. (Univ. of London), 1912-13; vety. offr., N. Rhodesia, 1913-20; war serv., E. Africa, N. Rhodesia Rifles, 1914-17; S.A.V.C., 1917-19; ment. in desps.; studied physiology and pathology, Univ. Coll. Sch. of Trop. Med., London, Ecole de Medicine, Paris, and Vety. Research Lab., Pretoria; vety. pathologist, Tanganyika Territory, Feb., 1922; dep. dir., vety. services and vety. pathologist, 1930; dir., vety. serv., Jan., 1933; dir., teetze research Sept., 1938.
HOSKYNS-ABRAHALL, THEO.—B. 1896; Epson Coll.; served with H.M. Forces, Franz Salonika, Egypt and Palestine; admstr. of Nigeria, 1921; senr. dist. offr., 1936; admstr. offr., cl. I, 1937.

HOWARD, FREDERICK JAMES, O.B.E.—B. 1883; ed. at Bedford; 2nd divn. clk., W.O., 09; brig.-maj., 1909-12; G.S.O., 3rd grade, 1914; 2nd grade, 1915; 1st grade, 1916; brig.-gen. commdg. 99th Infy. Brig., 1918; commdr-in-ch. allied troops, N. Russia, 1918-19; commd., Imdad force, 1920; N. Persia force, 1921; served S. Africa, 1899-1902 (desp.), Queen's med., 3 clasps, King's med., 2 clasps); European War, 1914-19; commdt., Staff Coll., Camberley, 1922-26; commd. 2nd divn., Aldershot, 1926-28; commdr., Meerut dist., India, 1928-31; lieu., H.M.'s Tower of London, 1931-33; quar.-gen., India, 1933-36; G.O.C.-in-ch., Eastern commd., 1936-38; A.D.C. to King since 1937; commdr.-in-ch., forces in Middle East, 1938; gov. and commdr.-in-ch., Gibraltar, 1938.

IRVINE, FREDERICK ROBERT, M.Sc. Ag., Fellow, Linnean Soc. (1927).—B. 1898; ed. Royal Grammar Sch., Newcastle-on-Tyne; war serv., Gordon Highlanders, France and Belgium, 1916-19; 2nd lieut., R.A.F., 1918; B.Sc., agr., Armstrong Coll., Durham Univ., 1920; teacher of agr. and biology, Achiomota, Gold Coast, 1924.

IRVINE, ROBERT, M.C. (1917).—B. 1894; ed. Dunfermline High Schl.; bd. of agr., Scotland, 1913; served in France, 1915-18; capt., Lancs. Fusiliers, 1918; cadet, F.M.S., Oct., 1919; pvtc. sec. to ch. sec. to govt., Mar., 1921; offr., cls. V., July, 1921; asst. dist. offr., Tampin, Apr., 1922; ag. 2nd mag., K. Lumpur, May, 1923; dist. offr., Jelebu, Nov., 1924; asst. dist. offr., Lower Perak, Jan., 1925; offr., cls. IV., July, 1925; ag. 2nd asst. sec. (A) S.S., May, 1926; ag. 1st asst. sec. (B) S.S. and clk. of couns., Mar., 1928; dist. offr., Tampin, Dec., 1929; offr., cls. III, July, 1930; ag. 1st asst. sec. (A) S.S., Jan., 1934; offr., cls. II, July, 1935; ag. sec. to high commr., June, 1937.

IRVINE, HON. SIR WM. HILL, G.C.M.G. (1936), K.C.M.G. (1914), LL.D., K.C.—B. 1858; called to the bar, Victoria, 1884; premier of Victoria, 1902-4; mem. H. of R., C. of A., since 1906; atty.-gen., C. of A., June, 1913 to Sept. 1914; chief just., Victoria, 1918-35; lieut.-gov., Victoria, May, 1918; ag. gov. from June, 1931 to May, 1934; ret., 1935.

IRVING, FRANCOIS ERNEST.—B. 1885; ed. at St. Andrew's Coll. and Marist Bros. Schl., S. Africa; served in Boer War, 1901-2; German S.W. Africa, with Imp. Light Horse, 1914-15; German E. Africa, 1916; seconded to admtn. of Mafia Is., as finan. offr., Nov., 1916, to May, 1919; asst. cust. dept., Zanzibar, Oct., 1919; ag. asst. compt., cust. for various periods, 1920-22; asst. compt., cust., Dec., 1922; ag. compt., cust. for various periods, 1924-37; senr. cust. offr., 1938.

IRVING, HENRY EDWARD, M.B.E.—Ed. at Charterhouse, and Handelsehreanstalt, Leipzig; trooper, 9th Lancers, 1899-1902; served in S.A. (medal and four clasps); ch. clk. to cent. repatriation bd., O.R.C., Mar., 1902; clk. to col. sec., O.R.C., Oct., 1902; prov. sec.'s dept., 1910; lieut., 16th M.R., 1913; seconded for duty as censor, O.F.S., 1914-19; ment. in desps., 1917; M.H.E., 1918; ch. clk. to prov. sec., 1924; asst. prov. sec., O.F.S. prov. instrn., June, 1935; prov. sec., O.F.S., Nov., 1935.

IRWIN, HON. ROBERT.—B. 1865; ed. Shelburne Acad.; elec. mem., H. of A. of Nova Scotia for Shelburne County, 1906; re-ele., 1911-1916-1920; defeated, 1925; speaker, H. of A., 1917-25; mem. old age pension bd., Nova Scotia, 1933; lieu. gov., Nova Scotia, Apr., 1937.

IRWIN, ROBERT EDWARD.—B. 1899; ed. at Christ's Hosp.; aud. audit dept., Oct., 1925; asst. audr., Tanganyika Territory, Nov., 1925; asst. audr., Gold Coast, Dec., 1928; senr. asst. audr., Kenya, Sept., 1937.
IRWIN, WILLIAM HENRY, B.A. (Dublin).—B. 1907; ed. Trinity Coll., Dublin; called to bar, Inn of ct. of N. Ireland, 1932; practised at bar, 1932-36; dist. mag. Gold Coast, Oct., 1936.

ISAACS, Rt. Hon. Sir ISAAC ALFRED, P.C. (1921), G.C.B. (1937), G.C.M.G. (1932), K.C.M.G. (1928).—B. 1855; called to the bar, Victoria, 1880; Q.C., 1899; M.L.A., Victoria, 1892-1901; solr.-gen., 1893; atty.-gen., 1894-9; and again in 1900; mem. of Aust. Fed. Convention, 1897-8; and of first Federal parlmt.; atty.-gen. in Deakin ministry, 1906-6; judge of High Ct. of Australia, Oct., 1906; ag. ch. just., 1925 and 1927; ch. just., Mar., 1930; gov. gen. of the C'wealth of Australia, Jan., 1931-36.

ISHERWOOD, ALBERT ARTHUR MAGNALL, O.B.E. (1926), M.A. (Oxon.) (hons.).—B. 1889; ed. St. John's schl., Leatherhead, and University Coll., Oxford; jnr. supt., educn.dept., N. Nigeria, July, 1913; Nigerian Land Contingent, 1913; attd. polit. dept. N. Nigeria, 1915-17; polit. offr., Tanganyika adminstr., 8th Oct., 1917; ag. senr. comanr., Feb., 1923 to Jan., 1924; dep. dir., educn., Sept., 1924; ag. dir., educn., Oct., 1924 to Mar., 1925; ditto and mem., exec. and leg. couns., in 1927, 1929, and 1931; Swahili examr.; dir., educn., 1931.
IVES, HON. WILLIAM CARLOS, B.C.L.—B. 1873; ed. pub. schl., Pineher Creek, Alta., Coaticook, Queb., and McGill Univ.; puisne judge, sup.ct., Alta., 1914.

IVEY, COMMDE. A. V. P., R.D., R.N.R.—B. 1894; Royal Navy, 1915; sub. lieut., R.N.R., 1917; lieut. commdr., 1927; commdr. (ret. list), 1935; Cameroons expedy. force, 1915-16; Harwich force destroyers, 1916-17; commanded Torpedo boats, 1918; commanded H.M. river gunboat "Robin" on West River, China, 1919; marine offr., Nigeria, 1920; harb. mast., Port Harcourt, 1926; prin. marine offr., 1930; ag. dir., marine, Oct., 1934 to May, 1935; dir., marine, 1935; el. younger brother, Trinity House, 1937.

IZARD, GODFREY WALLACE.—B. 1891; ed. Uppingham; B.S.A. Co., 1909-14; called to bar, Middle Temple, June, 1914; enlisted, Aug., 1914; invalided; admtnv. offr., Nigeria, Jan., 1915; admtnv. serv., Apr., 1930; dep. comanr., lands, 1930; prin. asst. sec., N. Prova., 1931; sec., N. Prova., May, 1936.

IZARD, H.—Asst. dist. comanr., E. Africa Prot., Feb., 1915; war serv., 1915-19, E. Africa, Egypt, Mespot., Persia; dist. offr., 1921; junr. asst. sec. and clk. of couns., 1923; called to bar (Inner Temple), 1929; senr. dist. comsnr., Kenya, July, 1936; offr.-in-ch., Turkana, Feb., 1937; comanr., mines, Dec., 1937.

JACK, HENRY WALTER, M.B.E. (1930), D.Sc., B.A.—B. 1891; ed. Univ. Coll., Cork (scholar); B.A. (econ.), 1911, B.Sc. (biol.), 1912; agr. inspr., F.M.S., 1914; agr. instr., 1915; ec. botanist, S.S. & F.M.S., 1919; mem., comte., on rubber research, 1923; D.Sc. by research, 1925; rep. of Malaya, Pac. Sc. Cong., Japan, 1926 and in Java, 1929; economic botanist, Mar., 1929; mem., coconut and oil palm research comte., 1929; hon. sec., rice cult. comte., Malaya, 1930; dir. agr., Fiji, 1934; mem., bd. of trustees, Fiji museum; chmn., Luva schl. comte.; conserv., forests, 1936; offl. M.L.C., 1937; publns. on rice, coconuts, oil palm, cotton, rubber, etc. London; vety. offr., Kenya, 1926; vety. resch. offr., 1938; dep. dir. (lab. services), 1937.

HUMPHRYS, LIEUT.-COL. SIR FRANCIS HENRY, G.C.M.G. (1932), G.C.V.O. (1928), K.C.M.G. (1929), K.B.E. (1924), C.I.E. (1920).—B. 1879; ed. Shrewsbury and Christ Church, Oxford; 2nd lieut., Worcestershire Regt., 1900; served S. African War, 1900-01 (Queen's med. 3 clasps); ent. polit. dept., govt. of India, 1903; pte. sec. to ch. comsrr., N.W.F.P., 1904; astt. comsrr., Hazara, 1907; dep. comsrr., Rannu, 1910; Kohat, 1914; polit. agent, N.W. Frontier, 1915; polit. offr. with troops, N.W. Frontier, India, 1917 (deeps.); served European War in Europe as pilot B.A.F., 1918; polit. agt., Khyber, until Mar., 1922; govt. agt., New Hebrides, Mar., 1922.

JAMES, S. I.—B. 1891; ed. St. Paul's Schl.; on mily. serv., Sept., 1914 to Aug., 1919; ent. W.O. (temp.) Nov., 1919; ent. C.O. as clerical offr., July, 1926; ag. cler. offr., higher grade, 10th Mar., 1931; ast. prin., Ist Oct., 1932; ag. prin., 7th Mar., 1938.

JAMES, SIR WALTER HARTWELL, KT. BACH. (1907).—Ed. in Perth at state and high schls., admitted as barrister and solicitor, 1888; member for East Perth, 1894; represented W. Aust. at fed. conv., 1897-8; hon. minister, 1901; K.C., 1902; premier and atty.-gen., 1902; agt.-gen. for W.A., 1904-1906; chan., univ. of W. Australia.

JAMESON, CLAUDE FRANCIS SPURRELL, M.C., A.C.S.M., A.I.M.M.—B. 1891; ed. Wellington Coll. and Camborne Schl. of Mines; war serv., 1914-19; capt., R.E., Jan., 1916; inspr., mines, Selangor, Oct., 1919; ast. warden, mines, F.M.S., Oct., 1926; sr. inspr., mines, Ipoh and Siput, Feb., 1931; do., K. Lumpur, Mar., 1933; ast. warden, mines, Selangor, Apr., 1933; do., N. Sembilan, in addn., June, 1933; warden, mines, Johore, 1936.

JAMIESON, MORRIS, B.Sc. (Lond.), A.R.C.S., A.I.C.—B. 1896; ed. Allan Glen Schl., and Glas. and Lond. Univ. (Royal Coll. of Sc.); astt. gov't. analyst, S.S., Feb., 1922; sr. astt. gov't. analyst, S'pore, Aug., 1929; govt. analyst and dy. comsrr., excise, Oct., 1935.

JANSEN, HON. ERNEST GEORGE, B.A., LL.B., M.P.—B. 1881; ed. Ladysmith Govt. Schl. and Durban High Schl.; leader of Nationalist party in Natal; mem., Natal Univ. Coll. Coun.; chmn., Saamwerk Unie van Natalse Vereeniginge (the first body in S.A. to prescribe exams. in Afrikaans) for 7 years; foundation mem. of Suid Afrikaanse Akademie vir Taal, lettere en kuns; mem., Union H. of A. for Vryheid since 1921; speaker, Union H. of A., July, 1924-July, 1929; min. of native affrs., June, 1929 to Mar., 1933; speaker, H. of A., May, 1933.

JANZS, HERBERT ERIC.—B. 1890; cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., Sept., 1914; ag. office astt., Batticaloa kach., Sept., 1915; office astt., Kegalla kach., Jan., 1917; do., Jaffna kach., Jan., 1918; pol. mag., Kalutara, Mar., 1920; dist. judge, Ratnapura, Jan., 1925; astt. sttlm. offr., Sept., 1928; ag. pol. mag., Colombo, Apr. to June, 1930; ag. sttlm. offr., May., 1931; offr., cls. I, grade II, Jan., 1937; sttlm. offr., Mar., 1938.

JARDINE, SIR DOUGLAS JAMES, K.C.M.G. (1938), C.M.G. (1932), O.B.E. (1918).—B. 1888; ed. at Westminster and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; 2nd cls. classical trip., 1910; B.A., 1910; M.A., 1914; chief sec.'s off., Cyprus, 1910; pvt. sec. to ag. high comsrr., July to Oct., 1911; astt. sec. to govt., 1912; ag. ch. astt. sec. on several occasions, 1912-16; passed in modern Greek, 1912; sec. to the admin., Somaliland, 1916; accompanied mission to Abyssinia on occasion of coronation of the Empress Zauditu (3rd cls. Star of Ethiopia), 1917; in charge H.Q. services, Somaliland Expdy. Force, 1920 (A.G.S. med. and ment. in desg.); senr. astt. sec., Nigerian secretariat, 1921; ag. dep. ch. sec., July to Nov., 1925 and June to Aug., 1926; dep. ch. sec., Tanganyika Territory, May, 1927; ag. ch. sec., June to Nov., 1927 and Apr. to Oct., 1928; ch. sec., Jan., 1929; gov't. dep. on several occasions since 1927; ag. gov't. in 1929, 1931 and 1933; gov't., North Borneo, 1933; accredited rep. to Perit. Mandates Comen., 1929, 1930 and 1931; gov't. and commdr.-in-ch., Sierra Leone, 1937; joint-editor "Hand-book of Cyprus," 1913 and 1919; and author of "Mad Mullah of Somaliland," 1923.

JARDINE, ROBERT FRIER, C.M.G. (1928), O.B.E. (1926).—B. 1894; ed. Downing Coll., Cambridge; served European War in Gallipoli, Egypt and Mesopotamia; polit. offr., Mesopotamia, 1917; astt. divnl. adviser, Mosul prov., under Iraq govt., 1921; admstve. inspr., Mosul prov., 1923; on ap. duty in Europe in connec. with Mosul question, 1925; admstve. inspr., Sulaimani, 1925; do., Mosul prov., 1925; ditto, Basra, 1928; ch. inspr., land regstrn. sec., dept. lands and surveys, Palestine, 1936; author "Grammar of Bahdian Kurmanji (Kurdish)," 1922.

JARRETT, JAMES HENRY, K.C.—B. 1895; ed. Lancing Coll.; barrister-at-law, Gray's Inn; H.A.C., 1914; served European War, 1914-19; astt. dist. comsrr., Uganda, 1919; seconded, judl. dept. as mag., 1922; mag., 1924; astt. atty.-gen., 1926; ag. solr.-gen., May, 1927 to Jan., 1928; crown coun., 1927; atty.-gen., Grenada, 1929; ag. admstr. and ag. ch. just., St. Vincent in 1930 and 1931; atty.-gen., Bahamas, 1933; ag. ch. just. and O.A.G., 1934; dep. gov't., 1935 and 1936; col. sec., Aug., 1935; O.A.G., in 1936-37; admstve. govt., 1938.

JARRETT, NORMAN ROWSTONE.—B. 1889; ed. Highgate and Exeter Coll., Oxford, 3rd cls. class. moda., 1910, 2nd cls. lit. hum., 1912; B.A., 1912; cadet, F.M.S., 1913; passed cadet, July, 1914; offr., cls. V, Feb., 1916; cls. IV, Jan., 1920; dist. offr., Mar., 1921; offr., cls. III, Jan., 1925; offr., cls. II, 1930; astt. sec. to govt., F.M.S., Apr., 1930; ag. sec. to req., Perak, May, 1931; ag. Br. advr., Trengganu, June, 1933 and Dec., 1936; offr., cls. I B, 1934; ag. under sec. to govt., F.M.S., Feb., 1934; Br. advr., Trengganu, 1937.

JARVIS, CYRIL CHESTER.—B. 1884; ed. Victoria Coll., Stellenbosch; Rhodes school., 1904; Wadham Coll., Oxford, B.A. (hon. juris.); LL.B., Cape Univ.; sec., ch. just., Cape Colony, 1907; admitted Cape bar, 1910; ch. clk. to atty.-gen., O.F.S.; prof. astt. to atty.-gen., Cape Prov., 1921; crown pros., Johannesburg, 1926; atty.-gen., Natal, 1933; law advr., 1937.

JAYARATNE, MERENNA FRANCIS DE SILVA, B.A. (Lond.).—B. 1904; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Feb., 1927; atti. Colombo kach., Mar., 1927; office astt., Matale kach., Jan., 1928; pol. mag., Jaffna, Mar., 1930; office astt., Batticaloa kach., Mar., 1933; astt. gov't. agt., Mullaitivu, Oct., 1934; sec. to min. for agr. and lands, Nov., 1936.
JAYASUNDARA, LAYARD, B.A. (Cantab.), B.Sc. (Lond.).—B. 1910; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Mar., 1935; ag. office astt., Nov., 1936; office astt., Apr., 1938.
JAYASURIYA, CHARLES EDWIN PERERA, B.A. (Hons.) Lond.—B. 1912; cadet Ceylon civ. serv., Jan., 1937.

JEFF, CAIT. JOHN.—B. 1896; ed. Glasgow Univ.; cadet, F.M.S., addt Chinese prot., Ipoh, Apr., 1920; passed cadet, Dec., 1922; astt. prot., Chinese, Seremban, Dec., 1923; astt. cust. dept., Jan., 1925; offr., cls. IV, Apr., 1927; ag. prot., Chinese, Perak, Apr., 1928; ditto, N. Sembilan, Jan., 1930; ditto, Selangor and Pahang, May, 1931; offr., cls. III., Apr., 1932; ag. astt. sec., Chinese affrs., S.S., Aug., 1933; do., S.S. and F.M.S., Jan., 1934; do., Malaya in addn., Oct., 1934; offr., cls. II, Apr., 1937.

JEFF, RICHARD HEDLEY ALLISON.—B. 1889; jr. astt. engnr., F.M.S. Rly., June, 1913; dist. engnr., grade III, head office, July, 1915; supernmy. dist. engr., July, 1919; on sp. duty in S. India, Aug., 1919; dist. engr., grade II, K. Lumpur, Jan., 1920; dist. engr., grade I, Jan., 1924; sr. dist. engr., head office, June, 1929; ditto, Johore Bahrn, Feb., 1931; divnl. engr., F.M.S. rly., Aug., 1932; ag. maint. and new wks. engr., Apr.-Dec., 1936; sec., Mui-Tsai Comsn., Hong Kong and Malaya, 1936; ag. asst. sec. for Ch. affrs., Oct., 1937.

JEFFERY, CECIL ALBERT, C.M.G. (1936).—B. 1888; ed. Clyde schl., N.Z.; ent. N.Z. post and tel. dept., 1903; clk., govt. house, 1914; pvte. sec. to various ministers, 1915-26; ch. pvte. sec. to prime min., 1926-35; clk., exec. coun. and sec. to cabinet, 1935; served with N.Z. expd. force; mem., N.Z. deleg. to Imp. Conf., 1937.

JEFFREY, PAT COMMR., A., R.D., R.N.R.—B. 1892; ed. Glas. Univ.; R.N., 1914-19; probationer, N. Rhodesia, 1919; J.P., 1920; clk. of ct. and under-sheriff, Batoka dist., 1920; asst. nat. commr., 1921; nat. commr., 1926; passed higher Chinyanga; asst. dist. offr., Tanganyika Territory 1927; asst. secy., Jan., 1930; ag. asst. ch. sec., 1934-35; clk., exec. and leg. couns., 1935; admstve. offr., 1st gr., Zanzibar, Aug., 1935; asst. secy., clk., leg. coun., edr., gazette and press liaison offr., Aug., 1935; clk., exec. coun., Jan., 1936; ag. asst. ch. sec., Mar., Oct., 1936.

JEFFREYS, MERVIN DAVID WALDEGROVE.—B. 1890; ed. St. George's, Bulawayo and Exeter Coll., Oxford; Rhodes schol., B.A. (Eng.) Oxon, 1914; Ph.D. (Sc.) Lond., dip. anth., Lond., 1930; asst. dist. offr., Nigeria, 1915; lieut. W.A.F.F., 1916; served in E. Africa, 1916-19; dist. offr., 1925; anthropological offr., 1930-31; admstve. offr., clu., II, 1935; ag. asst. judge, 1936.

JEFFREYS, ROBERT SYDNEY.—B. 1888; ed. Cape Town (S. Africa), Bedford Schl. and St. John's Coll., Cambridge, B.A., LL.B. (1909); Barrister-at-law (Gray's Inn, 1910); joined admstn. of N. Rhodesia, June, 1914; asst. native commr., and J.P., July, 1916; resigned to proceed on active serv., Feb., 1918; rejoined N. Rhodesia admstn., May, 1919; native commr., July 1921; seconded to secretariat, Nov., 1921-Dec., 1922; asst. mag., Apr., 1926; dist. offr. (grade II) with powers of mag., Apr., 1928; seconded as asst. to sec. for native affrs., Mar., 1927-Oct., 1928; ag. crown coun., Nov., 1928-Mar., 1929; ag. pol. mag., May, 1930; res. mag., Apr., 1936.

JEFFRIES, CHARLES J., C.M.G. (1937), O.B.E. (1928).—B. 1896; ed. Malvern Coll.; class. demy., Magdalen Coll., Oxford, 1914; 2nd lieut., 3rd Wiltshire Regt., Sept., 1915; served in France, 1916; lieut., 1917; invalided from army, Nov., 1917; tempy. 2nd cls. clk., C.O., 27th Dec., 1917; apptd. under reconstruction scheme, 2nd cls. clk., 16th July, 1919; sec., E. Africa land sttlmt. selection board, 1919; asst. sec., E. Africa currency board; prin., 1st Apr., 1920; hon. sec., Corona Club, since 1921; sec., comitee. on governors' pensions, 1927-28; asst. sec., 1st Oct., 1930.

JEFFRIES, CHARLES WILLIAM, F.R.A.S.—B. 1882; Computer, Royal Observatory, Greenwich, 1897; junr. asst., Royal Observatory, Cape of Good Hope, 1903; 1st asst., Royal Observatory, Hong Kong, 1907; chief asst., ditto, 1912; ag. dir. in 1915, 1920, 1924, 1925 and 1929; dir., 1932.

JELF, ST. ARTHUR SELBORNE, Kt. Bach. (1932), C.M.G. (1927).—B. 1876; ed. Marlborough and Exeter Coll., Oxford (exhri.); 2nd cls. class. mods., 1897; 3rd cls. lit. hum., 1899; B.A., 1899; M.A., 1913; cadet, F.M.S. civ. serv., 1899; held various admstve. and exec. posts in Malay States, 1900-10; asst. gen. adviser to Johore govt., Jan., 1911; offr. of cls. IV., Jan., 1912; chmn., man. bd., Kinta, Perak, Apr., 1914 to Apr., 1917; 2nd lieut., M.S.V.R., Sep., 1914; lieut., M.S.V.R., Jan., 1915; seconded for mil. serv. Desert Provs., Egypt 1920; mil. adviser to Iraq Gov't., Mar., 1921.
