SIR CHARLES WALLACE ALEXANDER NAPIER COCHRANE-BAILLIE, G.C.I.E. (1903), G.C.M.G. (1900), K.C.M.G. (1895).—Only son of 1st baron; born, 1860; dep. lieut. Lanarkshire; lieut. Lanarkshire yeomanry, 1883; M.P. for N. St. Pancras, 1886-90; gov. Queensland, 1895-1901; gov. of Bombay, 1903-7.

LANCASTER, CAPT. FREDERICK GEORGE, M.B.E., M.C.—B. 1890; Regtate pol. force, 1910; served in War, 1914-18; capt., 1917; R.I.C., 1920; dist. instr., R.I.C., Killarney, 1921; dete. instr., pol., Bahamas, 1928.

LANDOR, JOSEPH VICTOR, M.D. (London), B.S., M.R.C.S. (Eng.), D.P.H., D.T.M. & H. (Eng.), M.R.C.P. (London); Certificate of London S.T.M., with distinc.—B. 1897; med. offr., S.S., Aug., 1927; med. offr. i/c. gen. hosp., Jo. Bahru, ag. phys. and radiologist, Johore, in addn., Apr., 1933; ag. prof. of med., Coll. of Med., Spore, June, 1934; phys., gen. hosp., Spore, Apr., 1935; phys. and regsr., gen. hosp., Spore, Jan., 1936.

LANE, CHARLTON ADLERBERT GUSTAVUS.—B. 1890; ed. Winchester and Univ. Coll., Oxford; B.A. (Hons.), 1911; ast. dist. comsrr., E.A.P., 1913; ag. dist. comsrr., 1918; attd., secretariat, 1918; ag. senr. ast. sec., 1919; senr. ast. sec., 1920; dist. comsrr., Zanzibar, 1923; mag., Tanganyika Territory, 1925; res. mag., Kenya, 1929; ag. judge, supt. ct., 1935, 1937 and 1938; puisne judge, Sierra Leone, 1939.

LANE, HENRY WALKER, Assoc. M.Inst.C.E.—B. 1886; ed. Christa Coll., Waitaki High Sch.; & Canterbury Coll., N.Z.; lieut. R.E., France, Egypt and Palestine, 1915-19; ast. engr., P.W.D., Kenya, 1924; senr. ast. engr., 1934; ag. asptg. engr., 1936.

LANG, ERNEST PHILIP HENRY.—B. 1880; Ed. Monmouth; admitted solr., sup. ct., England, 1905; pvt. prac., Hong Kong, 1905-15; war serv., 1915-19 (capt.); ast. crown solr., Hong Kong, Sept., 1928; dep. regisr., sup. ct., Oct., 1928; J.P., 1928; ag. offr. recor. and regisr., trade marks and letters patent in adm., 1930; ag. regisr., offl. adminstr. and regisr., companies in 1931, 1932, 1934 and 1935; regisr., sup. ct., 1935.
LANG, HON. JOHN THOMAS.—M.L.A. of New South Wales from Dec., 1913; treas. in Storey and Dooley govt., 1920-22; premier, N.S.W., 1925-27 and 1930-32.

LANGLEY, CABLETON GEORGE, K.C. (1935).—B. 1885; ed. City of London Sch.; called to bar, Middle Temple, 1913; on mily. serv., Malta, France and Ireland, 1914-21; pvt. practice, Lincoln Inn and Bahamas Is., 1921-31; atty.-gen., Leeward Islands, 1931; comsrr., sup. ct., J.P.; ag. puisne judge, 1931-3; dual appt. atty.-gen. and ag. col. sec., 1934-36; adminstr., Antigua, 1936-38; 2nd puisne judge, B. Guiana, 1937.
LANGSTONE, HON. FRANK.—B. 1881; mem., N.Z. H. of R., 1922-25 and since 1928; min. of lands and comsrr. of state forests, 1936.

LANGWORTHY, HENRY BRUCE.—B. 1896; pol. probr., F.M.S., July, 1920; ast. comsrr., pol., July, 1923; 1st ast. comsrr., Selangor, July, 1925; adjt., Fedl. hdqrs., Oct., 1925 and Feb., 1930; supt., convict estab., Taiping and inspr. prisons, F.M.S., Sept., 1926; ch. pol. offr., B. Pahat, Oct. 1931; ast. sup., pol. S.S., Dec., 1933; dy. comsrr., pol., F.M.S., Feb., 1935; dy. comsrr., pol. and offctg. comsrr., pol., Johore, July, 1935; ag. commr. pol., Johore, Nov., 1936.

LANIER, PAUL EDOUARD MAXIME, M.D., M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P.—Ed. Middlesex Hosp. London, Paris and Lausanne; ast. med. offr., Seychelles and visiting mag. to outlying islands, 1924; resigned; re-ent. col. serv. as res. surg., Seychelles and visiting mag., outlying islands, 1929; sen. med. offr., 1935.

LANKTREE, CHARLES JOSEPH DANE, Barrister-at-Law, Kings Inns, Dublin.—B. 1895; on mil. serv., 1914-19; cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., Oct., 1921; astd. Colombo kach., Nov., 1921; office ast. Kandy kach., Mar., 1924; admstv. sec. to dir. of med. and army. serv., Oct., 1925; addnl. ast. dir., educn., Nov., 1926; admstv. sec. to dir., elec. undertakings, July, 1928; ast. stltm. offr., Jan., 1932; ag. man., elec. undertakings, Apr., 1933; addnl. man., do., July, 1934; ast. govt. agt., Matara, Sept., 1934; do., Trincomalee, Dec., 1936.

LANSDOWN, CHARLES WILLIAM HENRY, B.A., LL.B.—B. 1874; clk., C.G.R., Mar., 1889; clk., C.S.O., Capetown, Oct., 1893; atty.-gen.'s off., Nov., 1894; mag.'s clk., Alwali N., Feb., 1898; pte. sec. to atty.-gen., Aug., 1898; ag. ast. mag., Port Elizabeth, Sept., 1901; ch. clk. and pte. sec. to atty.-gen., Oct., 1901; addnl. legal advsr., July, 1907; prof. ast., atty-gen.'s off., Capetown, Apr., 1912; law advsr., dep. of justice, Union of S. Africa, Aug., 1918; atty-gen., Cape Prov., Dec., 1926; senr. law adviser, 1927; puisne judge, Natal prov. divn., Oct., 1931; judge pres. (E.D. local divn.), 1937.

LAPOINTE, RT. HON. ERNEST, P.C., B.A., LL.B., K.C., LL.D. (Quebec East)—B. 1876; ed. Rimouski Coll. and Laval Univ., Quebec; called to bar, 1898; K.C., 1908; Canadian del. to L. of N., Geneva, 1922; rep. Canada with Rt. Hon. Mackenzie King at Imp. Confce., 1926; negotia- ted and signed, with Hon. Mr. Fielding, treaty with France, 1922; negotiated and signed, on behalf of Canada, treaty with U.S.A. regarding Pacific fisheries; signed four other treaties with U.S.; leader of govt. in H. of C. session of 1926; Can. plenipotentiary at Naval confce., Geneva, June-July, 1927; head of Can. delegn. to Imp. Confce., on Imp. and Dominion legis., 1929; barrister-at-law; elec. by accl. to H. of C., Canada, 1904; re-el. at g.e., 1904, 1908, 1911 and 1917 for same constit.; resigned, 1919 to contest Quepany Prospectus"; partly, candidate, Sheffield (Attercliffe), 1909-10; S. Hackney, 1910.

KINEAR, George, M.D. (Univ. of Manitoba), M.C.P.S. (Sask.), Certif. L.S.T.M. and H.—B.1894; ed. Regina Collegiate Inst., 1st cls. certif., Regina Normal Schol., 1912; prin., Earl Grey and Govan. Pub. Schls. (Sask.), 1913-17; Leeward Is. med. serv., 1926; D.M.C.O., dist. D. Dominica; M.O., Bahamas gen. hosp., 1930; ag. M.O. and bacteriologist, Bahamas gen. hosp., 1931; ag. ch. med. offr., 1932 and 1935-36; S.M.O., Falkland Is., Jan., 1937.

KIRKBRIE, Alan Logan, O.B.E.—B. 1901; adm. offr., Palestine, 1921; seconded to Trans-Jordan as 2nd ast. ch. Br. rep., 1921; finan. advr., 1928; asst. sec. on revision to Palestine, 1934; dist. commr., Palestine, 1938.

KIRKBRIE, Alec Seath, O.B.E. (1932), M.C.—B. 1897; mily. serv., Egypt, Palestine, Hejaz, Trans-Jordan, 1916-21; Br. rep., Ev Salt, Apr., 1921; junr. ast. sec., Palestine, Apr., 1922; ast. sec., Nov., 1926; clk. to exec. and advy. couns., Nov., 1924-Nov., 1927; ast. Br. res., Trans-Jordan, Nov., 1927; Br. res., do., 1939.
KROGH, ANDRE LOUIS, M.D., Ch.B. (Edin.).—B. 1887; gov't. med. offr., Dec., 1920; dist. med. offr., Tobago, 1922; senr. med. offr., Col. Hosp., San Fernando, Trinidad, 1937.

KBUGKR, JOHANNES JACOB.—B. 1888; on active serv., E. Africa and overseas, 1915-19; gov't. printer, 1927; Union R. of O. with rank of major; mem., exec. comtee., nat. industrial coun. of the printing and newspaper industry of S. Africa.

KUIT, ALBERT.—S. Africa Repub. govt. and Z.A.S.M. rly. serv. till Br. annexation; served Jameson raid and Boer war on Boer side; finan. and estate agt., Amersfoort (T'vaal) town coun. J.P. and practically all public bodies, 1902-25; rly. comnr. since 1925; pub. debt. comnr. since 1928; a comnr. of oaths for Union.

KUSCHKE, GEORG ADOLF CARL, (M.A.).—B. 1892; ed. Normal Coll., Johannesburg, Transvaal Univ. Coll. and Univ. of S. Africa (B.A.), Stellenbosch Univ. (M.A.); teacher, Transvaal, 1912; prin., Transvaal, 1921; inspr., schls., Transvaal, 1927; tech. advr., Transvaal educn. dept., 1933; dir., educn., Transvaal, 1934; sec. for social welfare, 1937.

KUSCHKE, HERMANN F. G., B.A.—B. 1891; ed. Johannesburg and Pretoria; head, normal sec., Stoefberg Gedenk Sch., 1916-24; organising inspr., nativ. educn., O.F.S., 1924; ch. inspr., nativ. educn., O.F.S., 1926.

LABORDE, EDWARD DANIEL, O.B.E. (1922), I.S.O. (1903).—B. 1863; 1st clk., audit office, Tobago, Jan., 1882; dep. regiar., ag. confidential clk. to administrator, and clk. of couns., June, 1882; 2nd clk., administrator's office, St. Lucia, Feb., 1883; ch. clk., Sept., 1883; ag. 2nd clk., gov.in-chief's office, Barbados, 1884; ch. clk. to gov., Windward Is., Grenada, 1885; priv. sec. to Gov. Sendall, 1886; ch. of pol. and excise, St. Vincent, 1889; inspr. of prisons, 1889; supervisor of cust. and port offr., 1897; in charge of relief work after hurricane of 1898; specially mentioned in despatches and thanked by S. of S. for services; chmn. town bd., 1899-1901; mem. ex. and legis. couns.; ag. col. sec., Apr. to May, 1901; ch. of pol. and inspr. of prisons, St. Lucia, 1902; has served on several comns. of enquiry in different colonies; ag. admstr., St. Vincent, June to Dec., 1904; ag. admstr., and col. sec., St. Lucia, July to Nov., 1905; ag. col. sec., Oct., 1906, to Jan., 1907; ag. administr. and col. sec., May to Nov. 1907; treas., St. Lucia, 1912; col. treas., Grenada, 1915; ag. col. sec., Grenada, for various periods, 1915-20; admstr., govt., Sept.-Oct., 1915, and Sept. to Oct., 1920.

LACEY, MAJOR ALFRED TRAYERS, O.B.E. (1936).—B. 1892; ed. King Edward VI Gram. Schl., Norwich, and King's Coll., Cambridge; class. tripes; hon. degree, M.A.; supt., educn., Tanganyika Territory, 1924; dir., educn., Nyasaland Prot., 1930; do., Kenya, 1938.

LAFERLA, CAPT. ALBERT V., O.B.E. (1935); LL.D.—B. 1887; ed. Flores's Coll., Malta; matric., Malta Univ., 1903; grad. in law, 1910; lieut., King's Own Malta Regt. of Militia, 1903, capt., 1911; demob., 1920; dir., elem. schls., Malta, Aug., 1920; dir., educn., Nov., 1933.

LA FONTAINE, SYDNEY HUBERT, O.B.E. (1930), D.S.O., M.C. (1916).—Asst. dist. comnr., E.A.P., 1910; district comnr., Kenya, Oct., 1918; 2nd grade prov. comnr., 1931; ag. ch. native comnr., 1933; prov. comnr., grade I, 1934.

LAING, GORDON DACOMB, B.Sc., M.B., Ch.B. (St. And.), D.P.H. (Johannesburg)—B. 1889; ed. Merchiston Schl., Pietermaritzburg, Madras Coll., St. Andrews, and St. Andrews' Univ.; senr. lect. in anatomy, Univ. of Witwatersrand, 1924; asst. pathologist, S. African Inst. for Med. Research, 1927; asst. health offr. for Union of S. Africa, 1928; med. offr., health, Pretoria munic., Nov., 1929; dep. med. offr., health, Johannesburg, 1934; lieut.-col. S.A. Med. Corp., Active Citizen Force; published some papers on anatomical and anthropological subjects in the Journal of the S. African Assocn. for the Advancement of Science and on preventive med. in S.A. Medical Journal.

LAING, MALCOLM BUCHANAN.—B. 1890; ed. Forest Schl., Essex; asst. commissary, B. Guiana, Oct., 1909; mem., ch. clk., immigrn. dept., Apr. 1913; mem., gov't. standing bd. of exams. in Hindi, etc., Nov., 1913; immigrn. agt., Aug., 1920; capt. B. Guiana Arty. (Militia), Jan., 1921; senr. clk., col. sec.'s office, Aug., 1925; clk. to exec. coun. and leg. coun., July, 1928; prvs. sec. to gov., Apr., 1929 to June, 1930; dist. comnr., Jan., 1932; temp. M.L.C., June, 1935; M.L.C., Oct., 1936; comnr. of lab. and local govt., Jan., 1938.

LAKE, FREDERICK JAMES.—B. 1894; 2nd div. clk., G.F.O., 1912; B.E.F., France, 1915; K.A.R., German E. Africa, 1917; exec. offr., G.F.O., 1920; asst. polit. offr., Tanganyika, 1921; asst. dist. offr., 1923; dist. offr., 1932; ag. prov. comnr., Jan.-May, 1938.

LAKE, LIEUT.-COL. MORICE CHALLONER C.M.G. (1936)—B. 1885; ed. Wellington Coll. and Sandhurst; comnd., unattd. list (attd. 1st Battu. Somerset Light Infy.), Jan., 1904; T.A., 1905; regtl. duty, 1905-15; Aden, 1913; served Aden Field Force, 1915-19; offr. in dh., signals, 1915-16; offr. commdg. signal schl., Lucknow, 1916-17; raised and commanded Aden (Amb.) Labour Corpsa, 1917-18; raised and commanded 1st Yemen Infy., 1918-25; sp. duty, W.O. revising mily. rept., Aden Prot., 1926-27; reverted, mily. duty, India, 1927-28; adviser to C.O. and Air Min. on raising levies, etc., in Aden, 1928; polit. offr., Aden, Nov. 1928; ag. 2nd asst. res. and Prot. sec., July to Nov., 1930; polit. secy., May, 1934; ag. ch. comnr., Apr.-Sept., 1936 and May, Oct., 1936; O.A.G., Oct., 1937-Feb., 1938 and Aug.-Dec., 1938.
LAMACQ, FRANK CONSTANT.—B. 1903; apptd. cler. offr., London telephone service, May, 1921; transf'd. to C.O., July, 1923.
LAMB, CYRIL HUBERT, M.I.M.S.—B. 1887; land survr., P.W.D., Hong Kong, 1924.

LAMBERT, C. E.—B. 1900; on mil. serv., May, 1918 to Nov., 1919; temp. clk., cust., 1919 to 1920; R.I.C., 1920 to 1921; temp. clk., Min. of Lab., Apr., 1921 to July, 1922; apptd. after compet. exam., cler. offr., C.O., 9th July, 1923; seconded as regir. on staff of high comnr. in Canada for H.M.'s govt. in Great Britain, 1928-32; returned to C.O., 12th Sept., 1932; asst. prin., 1st Feb., 1937.

LAMBERT, EDGAR BUCHANAN, F.R.G.S.—B. 1889; ed. Dulwich Coll., B. Mily. Coll., Sandhurst and Crystal Palace Engng. Schl.; 2nd lieut., The King's (Liverpool) Regt. 1909-11; land survr., P.W.D., Hong Kong, 1913; 1st cls. do., 1920; asst. supt., surveys, 1934; do., crown lands, 1938.

LAMBERT, WILLIAM OSBORNE, M.I.N.A., M.I.Mar.E.—B. 1887; asst. gov't. marine survr., Hong Kong, 1917; Hong Kong del. to safety of M.A. and LL.B. (with distinction), Edin.; mem. of Lincoln's Inn; asst. res., N. Nigeria, Mar., 1911; res., Aug., 1924; ag. sec., N. Provs., Apr. to Nov., 1927; ag. sec., native affrs., Nov., 1927 to Sept., 1928; staff grade, Mar., 1928; sec., N. Provs., Apr., 1929; gov. and commdr.-in-ch., Seychelles, 1933; do., Leeward Is., 1935.
LEVINKIND, LEOPOLD.—B. 1892; ed. S. African Coll., Cape Town; B.So. (engng.); corp., C.P. Rifles, S.W. Africa campaign; ent. dept., irrig., Union of S. Africa, 1917; hydrographic survr., 1934.

LEWRY, ARTHUR WERNER.—B. 1894; M.A. (Cantab.), barrister-at-law, Inner Temple, 1920; major, R. of O.T.; ed. St. Paul's Schol. and Trinity Hall, Cambridge; served with Middlesex Regt., Flanders, Gallipoli, Egypt and Palestine (severely wounded), 1914-19; pol. mag., Gambia, Jan., 1930; crown coun., Kenya, Oct., 1932; attd. legal advy. staff, C.O., May-June, 1936; solr., gen., Uganda, Aug., 1936; ag. atty.-gen., Sept., 1936-Apr., 1937; comnr. for revision of laws; gov.'s dep., Nov., 1936; chmn., Jinja currency enquiry comsn., 1937; atty.-gen., Jamaica, Feb., 1939.

LEWIN, CLAUD JOHN, M.C., B.Sc.—B. 1898; ed. Sexey's Sch., Univ. Coll., Reading and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; on war serv., 1915-17; supt., agr., Nigeria, Dec., 1922; junr. botanist, 1923; senr. botanist, 1925; ch. agriculturist, N. Rhodesia, Nov., 1930; ag. sec., agr., Dec., 1932; dir. agr., July, 1933; temp. M.L.C., 1935, 1936 and 1937.

LEWIS, ALFRED DALE, B.A. (Cape), M.A. (Cantab.), M.I.M.E., M.Inst.C.E.—B. 1881; P.W.D., India, 1905-07; circle engnr., irrig. dept., Cape and Union of S. Africa, 1907-15; on mily. serv. as major, R.E., in France, Egypt, Mesopotamia, 1915-19; ag. dir. of irrig., 1920; dir. of irrigation, 1921; author of "Irrigation in America."

LEWIS, BERTRAM.—B. 1887; Br. P.O., Seet's office, C. and A.G.'s dept. and London & prov. audit, 1903-17; asst. acctnt., dep. ch. acctnt., Kenya and Uganda, P. and T., 1917-24; ch. acctnt., Palestine, 1924; do., Kenya, Uganda and Tanganyika post serv., 1936; regional dir., P. and T. services, 1937.
LEWIS, CHARLES HENRY.—B. 1890; linotype operator, govt. printing press, Zanzibar, Nov., 1921; ag. govt. printer, Oct., 1922 to May, 1923; asst. govt. printer, Nov., 1923; govt. printer, Feb., 1933.

LEWIS, ERNEST HARRY, C.B.E., C.A. (S.A.).—Ed. King's Coll., Lond.; on war ser. (S.A.), 1899-1900; 1st grade clk., audit dept., Transvaal, 1902; acctnt., educn. dept., 1907; ch. clk. and acctnt., Transvaal prov. admtn., 1910; ch. paymr. and acctnt., S. African pol., 1910; asst. finan. sec., defence dept., 1916; finan. under sec., 1919; finan. advr., S. African pol., 1929.
LEWIS, I. E. G.—B. 1904; ed. Malvern Coll.; solr., sup. ct.; admitted Apr., 1929; advoc. and solr., S. Settlns., 1934-37; regist., high ct., Zanzibar, Dec., 1937.
LEWIS, JOHN VINCENT.—B. 1907; ed. King William's Coll., Isle of Man and Keble Coll., Oxford (B.A.); asst. dist. offr., Tanganyika Territory, July, 1930.

LEWIS, JOHN WILLIAMSON, A.M.I.C.E.—B. 1887; ed. King's schl., Canterbury; Crystal Palace schl. of practical engnr.; jr. asst. engnr., constrn. dept., F.M.S. rly., 1909; asst. engnr., 1912; grade III, 1913; senr. dist. engnr.: Nov., 1923; ditto, Bahru, May, 1930; do., head office, Mar., 1931; maintenance and new wks. engnr., F.M.S. rly., Aug., 1932; ag. ch. engnr., ways and wks., Oct., 1934.

LEWIS, MALCOLM STUART.—B. 1889; H.A.C., 1914-16; R.G.A., 1917-19; treas. asst., Nigeria, 1919; senr. asst. treas., 1934; asst. acct.-gen. and currency offr. 1937, retaining personal designation of senr. asst. treas.; ag. acct.-gen., 1937 and 1938.

LEWTHWAITE, RAYMOND, M.A., D.M., B.Ch. (Oxon.), M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.).—B. 1894; resch. student, Inst. Med. Resch., F.M.S., Dec., 1926; pathologist II, Jan., 1929; pathologist I, Dec., 1931; ag. dir., I.M.R., Aug., 1936.

LEY, MAJOR CUTHBERT HILLYAR, O.B.E. late R.E., F.R.Met.Soc.—B. 1872; comnr. in R. Engnr., 1892-1907; ret. from army, 1907; army serv. in Jamaica, 1896-99; S. African war, 1899-1900 (Tugela Heights and relief of Ladysmith); A.D.C. to inspr.-gen. of fortifications and engnr., 1900-03; geodetic survey of S. Africa, 1903-05; topographical scen., gen. staff, 1906-07; survey of Fiji, 1908; ordnance survey of Gt. Britain, 1912; served during war at Southampton in depts. of fortifications, miny. of munitions, and directorate of arty.; dir., Palestine survey, Dec., 1920.

LEY, HENRY COATES.—B. 1898; clk. to gov., Leeward Is., Nov. 1920; 1st clk. treas. and cust. and shipping master, Falkland Is., Apr., 1924; also postmtr., Apr., 1928; ag. treas. and collr., cust., man., savings bank, recr. of wrecks and registr., shipping, June 1928; ch. acctnt., treas., Cyprus, Sept. 1930; ag. dep. treas. in 1933 and 1934; dep. treas., Nov., 1934; ag. treas., May-Nov., 1936 and May-Sept., 1938.

LEYH, STANLEY GEORGE HOARE. Pay-Cdr. R.D., R.N.R., O.B.E. (Mily.).—B. 1893; ed. East Ham Tech. Coll., A.S.C. (T.F.), 1908-12; Royal Navy, 1913-20; staff of comdr-in-chief, Grand Fleet, 1914-17; admty. war staff (allied naval coun.), 1918-19; staff, comdr-in-chief, China station, 1919-20; librarian, col. sec.'s office, S'pore, Oct., 1920; office asst., col. sec.'s office, S'pore, Jan., 1922; offr.-in-ch., records revision, in addn., Feb., 1924; dep. comnr., excise, S.S., Nov., 1929; ag. senr. do., 1933-34; mem. and sec., Malayan tobacco comtes., 1933; seconded dep. comnr., cust. and excise, F.M.S., Feb., 1935-Mar., 1936; ag. senr., dep. comnr., excise, S.S., June, 1936-Feb., 1937; dep. comnr., excise, Penang, Aug., 1937.

LIESCHING, PERCIVALE, C.M.G. (1932).—B. 1895; ed. Bedford Schl. and Brasenose Coll., Oxford; R.A. 1920; pvte., 1st batt. H.A.C., 1914; 2nd lieut., Rifle Brig., Feb., 1915; lieut., June, 1915; capt., Tank Corps, Oct., 1918; served in France, 1914, 1917-18; E. Africa, 1916; ment. in depts., 1918; apptd. under reconstruction scheme, asst. prin., U.O., 26th July, 1920; pvte. sec. to perm. under sec. for dom. affrs., 6th Aug., 1925; temp. ag. prin. 6th Jan., 1927; prin. 1st. Apr. 1929; accompanied U.K. deleg. to L. of N. Assembly, 1927, to signature of Pact of Paris, 1928 and to Imp. economic confce., Ottawa, 1932; a del. to civ. avia. confce., Wellington, N.Z., 1936; seconded as sec. to H. Cr. for the U.K. in Canada, 1928-32 and as polit. sec. to H. Cr. for the U.K. in the Union of S. Africa, 1933; ag. high comnr. for U.K., Aug.-Dec., 1933; offl. sec. to high comnr. for U.K. in Australia, 1936.

LIGHT, JOSEPH, B.A. (Oxon.).—B. 1898; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Nov., 1921; attdi., Galle kach., Jan., 1922; ditto, Kegalla kach., Feb., bee East; elec., 1919; re-e1., g.e., 1921; min. of marine and fisheries, King cabinet, 1921; min. of just., Jan., 1924, and held position in 2nd King admn.; re-e1. at g.e., 1925, 1926 and 1930; re-signed portfolio with liberal admn., Aug., 1930; re-e1., at g.e., 1936; min. of just., Oct., 1935.

LARTER, LESLIE N. H., B.Sc., A.R.C.S., A.I.C.T.A.—Ed. Imp. Coll. (Lond.), schol. and medallist, graduated (1st cl. hon.) 1929; col. agril. schol., Fitwilliam House, Camb. and Imp. Coll. of Trop. Agr., Trinidad, 1930-32; geneticist, Jamaica, Nov., 1932.

LASCELLES, SIR ALFRED GEORGE, Kt. BACH. (1913), B.A.—Univ. Coll., Oxon; 2nd class mod. hist.; b. 1887; called to the bar, Inner Temple, 1886; acted on several occasions as pres. of dist. ct. in Cyprus and as puisne judge; pres. of dist. ct., Papho, 1892; ditto, Larnaca, 1893; ag. puisne judge, June, 1895; ag. Queen's advoc., Feb., 1896; pres. dist. ct., Nicosia, 1896; Queen's advoc., 1896; atty.-gen. of Ceylon, 1902; ag. ch. just., Mar., 1906; atty.-gen., Oct., 1906; chief just., May, 1911; retired, 1914.

LATCHFORD, HON. FRANCIS ROBERT, B.A., K.C.—B. 1886; ed. Ottawa Univ.; called to the bar, 1886; K.C., 1888; elec. to Ontario legis., 1899; re-elec., 1902; min. of pub. wks., Ontario, 1899; atty.-gen., 1904-5; judge, chancery divn., high ct. of just., Ontario, 1908; ch. just., 2nd divn., ot., sup. ct., 1923.
LATHAM, D. V., B.A., M.B., B.Ch., B.A.O. (Dub.).—B. 1897; war serv., R.N.V.R., 1917-18; med. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Jan., 1926.

LATHAM, Rt. HON. SIR JOHN GRIGG, P.C., G.C.M.G. (1935), C.M.G., K.C., M.A., LL.B., M.P.—B. 1877; ed. State Schls., Scotch Coll., Melbourne and Melbourne Univ.; called to bar, 1904; formerly lecturer, Melbourne Univ.; during European War was lieut.-commdr., Intelligence Branch; attended, with min. for navy, Imp. Confco., 1918; assist. sec., Br. Empire delegation, Peace Confco., Paris, 1919; Br. sec. to inter-allied comm. on Czecho-Slovak affrs. at confco.; M.H.R. (C'wealth) for Kooyong (Vic.); atty.-gen., C. of A., Dec., 1925; a rep. of C'wealth at Imp. Confco., 1926; leader of opposition H. of R., 1929; dep. leader, 1930; atty.-gen. and min. of external affrs. and industry in Lyons' ministry, 1932; leader, goodwill mission to Japan, 1934; resigned from parl., 1934; ch. just., high ct., 1935.
LAURENCE, C. G. W.—B. 1902; apptd., after compet. exam., cler. offr., C.O., 4th May, 1920; cler. offr., higher grade, 30th Nov., 1936.

LAURSENSON, GEORGE LYTTELTON, A.M. Inst.C.E.; A.M.N.Z.Soc.C.E.—B. 1893; ed. Lyttelton dist. high schl.; Wellington Coll., N.Z.; London Univ.; N.Z.P.W.D., 1911; ast. engrnr., 1915; war serv. N.Z. Field Engnr.; res. harbr.-engnr., Westport, 1928; oiv. engrnr., transp. dept., 1930; commnr., transp., 1937.
LAURIE, WILLIAM, M.Inst.M., M.Cy.E.—B. 1890; ast. engrnr., Kedah, Apr., 1920; exec. engrnr., F.M.S., Apr., 1929; ag. senr. exec. engrnr., Nov., 1936.

LAVILLE, LOUIS VICTOR JOSEPH.—B. 1888; ed. at Bedford Schl., and Corpus Christi Coll., Camb. (schol.), 2nd cls. class. and hist. tripes, R.A., Camb. barrister-at-law, Middle Temple; cadet, S. Stlitltn., Dec., 1912; add. to col. secy.'s office, Jan., 1913; passed cadet; supy. officer, cls. V, Jan., 1916; lent to gov't. of Kelantan, June, 1917; offg. dist. offr., Ulu Kelantan, Jan., 1918; supy. offr., IV, Dec., 1919; offg. dist. offr., Bukit Mertajam, July, 1920; offg. collr., income tax, Singapore, Apr., 1922; mag. and asst. regiar., sup. ct., Seremban, Dec., 1923; D.P.P., Perak, July, 1925; sheriff and dep. regiar., sup. ct., S'pore, Nov., 1928; 1st mag. and pres., town bd., Johore Bahru, Mar. and Oct., 1929; cls. II, Dec., 1929; offg. regiar., sup. ct., Penang, Aug., 1931; ag. legal advr. and jud. commr., Kelantan, June, 1932; offr., cls. IB, Nov., 1933; dist. judge, S'pore, Feb., 1935; legal advr., Kedah, May, 1936; ag. judge, Johore, Jan., 1937; ag. European judge, Kedah, June, 1938.
LAW, ALFRED NOEL, M.C.—B. 1895; mil. ser., 1915-18; inspr., dist. adminstr., Palestine, 1920; ag. ast. dist. commr. on various occasions, 1925-30, and once as dist. commr.; ast. sec., 1932.

LAW, SIR CHARLES EWAN, Kt. BACH. (1937).—B. 1884; ed. High Schl., Croydon and Pembroke Coll., Cambridge; Campbell-Foster prizeman, criminal law, Middle Temple, 1903; called to bar, 1906; Burma bar, 1906; staff capt. (temp. Brit. serv.), Burma divn., Mar., 1917; Volunteer Long Service med.(India), Feb., 1919; maj. (temp. Ind. army res. of offrs.) comdg. 15th Mech. Transport Coy., July, 1919; released from serv., rank capt., I.A.R.O., Feb., 1920; crown counsel, Kenya, Jan., 1922; ag. solr.-gen., Mar., 1923 to Oct., 1924; mem., legis. coun., May, 1923 to Oct., 1924; ag. atty.-gen. and mem., exec. coun., Apr., 1924; Swahili (L.S.), Sept., 1923; res. mag., Jamaica, Mar., 1925; ag. judge, Kingston ct. on various occasions, 1926 to 1927; judge, Kingston ct., Feb., 1927; ag. puisne judge, on various occasions 1926, and 1927 to 1929; mem., standing comm. of res. mags., June, 1926; mem., comm. of enquiry into admnstr. of prisons, Sept., 1926; puisne judge, Uganda, Oct., 1930; ag. ch. just., from May, 1932 to Mar., 1933; chan. of diocese, Jan., 1933; mem., comm. of enquiry into admnstr. of just. in crim. matters in E. Africa, Jan., 1933; ch. just., Zanzibar, Mar., 1934; mem., ct. of appl. for Eastern Africa; chmn., agrl. indebtedness comm., Aug., 1934.
LAW, DAVID, Grad.Inst.T.—B. 1901; station mast., K.U.R. and H., 1925; acct., clk., 1926; travelling inspr. of accts., 1926; ast. acctant., 1935.

LAW, Y. P., B.A. (Cantab.).—B. 1888 ast. mast., educn. dept., Hong Kong, 1915; ag. inspr. of vernac. schls., 1915 and 1917; mem., bd. of examra., 1919-22; inspr., vernac. schls., 1920 and 1921; hon. sec., bd. of educn., in addn., 1920-22; inspr., vernac. schls., New Territories, 1922; re-appld. mem., bd. of examra., 1924; ag. sen. inspr., vernac. schls. for urban districts in addn., 1925; inspr., vernac. schls., 1927.
LAWES, GORDON WILLIAM.—B. 1896; pol. probr., F.M.S., June, 1919; ast. commnr. pol., F.M.S., May, 1925; offr., superintending pol. circle, Ipoh, June, 1935; adjut., F.M.S. pol., Aug., 1938.

LAWLEY, HON. SIR ARTHUR, G.C.S.I. (1911); G.C.I.E. (1906); K.C.M.G. (1901).—B. 1860; sec. to adminstr., S. Rhodesia, May, 1896; dep. adminstr., Matabeleland, Nov., 1896; adminstr., Matabeleland, Dec., 1898; ag. adminstr., Mashonaland, July, 1899; gov. of W. Aust., Jan., 1901; lt.-gov., Transvaal, 15th Aug., 1902; ag. gov. of Transvaal and O.R.C. and high commr., 1903; chmn. of ex. coun., pres. of leg. coun., mem. I.O.C.; gov. of Madras, 1906.
LAWLOR, JOHN HENRY, B.A.—B. 1880; ast. mast., Pietermaritzburg Coll., Feb., 1906; ast. inspr., schls., Natal, May, 1910; inspr. of schls., Apr., 1912; ch. inspr., schls., Aug., 1931.

LAWRANCE, MAJOR SIR ARTHUR SALISBURY, K.C.M.G. (1937), K.B.E. (1934), C.M.G. (1931), D.S.O.—Major, late reserve of officers; served with I.Y. in S. Africa, 1900-1902 (Queen's medal with 3 clasps, King's medal with 2 clasps, ment. in despa.); served with 6th Batt., K.A.R., Somaliland, 1905-1911 (African General Serv. medal with clasp "Somaliland 1908-1910," ment. in despa.); asst. res., N. Nigeria, 1st Feb., 1911; dist. commr., Somaliland, Sept., 1914; comdt., Somaliland Camel Corps, Oct., 1913; operns. against Mullah, 1913; ment. in despa.; A.G.S. med. with clasp; ag. consul., Harar, 1915 and 1919; operns. against Mullah, 1920; ment. in despa., D.S.O.; admstr. govt. on various occasions, 1921, 1925-32; sec. to govt., 1926; commr. and commr.-in-ch., June, 1932; gov. and commr.-in-ch., May, 1936; ret., 1939.

LAWRENCE, MAJOR CHARLES TREVOR, O.B.E. (1926)—B. 1878; ed. St. David's, Reigate and at Shrewsbury Schl.; N. Nigeria Regt., 1900-05; A.D.C. to Gov., S. Nigeria, 1906; junr. asst. sec., S. Nigeria, 1910; ag. asst. prov. commr., Cent. Prov., 1912; prty. sec. to gov.-gen., 1914 and 1915; commanded Nigeria Land Cont., 1914-15; 1st asst. sec., S. Provs., 1916; add., Nigeria Regt., 1917-19; sec., petroleum pool bd., 1918-19; ag. sec., S. Provs., 1919, 1920 and 1921; prim. asst. sec., 1921; commr., Br. Empire Exhib., 1923; ag. lieut.-gov., S. Provs., 1927; staff grade, 1928; admstr. of col., 1930.

LAWRENCE, HON. HARRY GORDON, K.C., B.A., LL.B.—B. 1901; ed. Rondebosch Boys' High Schl. and Univ. of S. Africa; called to bar, May, 1926; M.P. since June, 1929; min. of lab., Union of S. Africa, July, 1938.

LAWRENCE, NATHAN COJO, B.A. (London), Teachers' Dipl., Educn. (Lond.). Mem., Inner Temple.—B. 1900; ed. Kpong Methodist Schl., King's Coll., London and Univ. of London; elk., gov.'s office, Gold Coast, July, 1924; resigned, 1930; 2nd divn. teacher, 1937; inspr. schl., 1938.
LAWRIE, EDWIN STEWART, M.B., Ch.B. (Glas.), F.R.C.S. (Edin.), F.R.F.P.S. (Glas.).—B. 1899; ed. G'gow Acad. and G'gow Univ.; M.O., Malaya, Mar., 1928.

LAWSON, HON. SIR HARRY SUTHERLAND WIGHTMAN, K.C.M.G. (1933).—B. 1875; M.L.A. for Castlemaine, Victoria, 1899; sat continuously in State Parlmt. from 1899; commr. of crown lands and survey, Dec., 1913 to Nov., 1915; afterwards atty.-gen., solr. gen. and min. of public instrn., premier and atty.-gen., 1918; premier and min. of agr., 1920-24; now C'wealth senator for Victoria.
LAWSON, HUBERT JAMES, M.B., Ch.B., B.A.O., Cert. L.S.T.M.—B. 1897; ed. Larne Grammar Schl. and Queen's Univ., Belfast; war serv., 1916-17; M.O., Malaya, Mar., 1928; ag. C.M.O., Kelantan, July, 1937.
LAWSON, HUGH, A.M.I.M.E., Ch. Meeh. Engr.—B. 1893; ed. Queen Victoria Schl. and Tynemouth Munici. High Schl.; inspr. of mach., F.M.S., Oct., 1924; regsr., motor vehicles, Perak, in admn., Apr., 1929.

LAXTON, THOMAS CLEMENT.—B. 1879; clk., postmstr.-gen.'s dept., Cape, 1898; treasy., 1901; examr., control and audit office, Cape, 1906, and Union of S.A., 1910; senr. clk., treasy., 1920; prinl. clk., pub. debt commrs., 1932; acct., 1933; sec., 1938.

LEA, LESLIE.—Ed. privately and Univ. Coll. Schl., Gray's Inn, 1922; clk. acct., fencing dept., E. Africa Prot., Sept., 1908; conf. clk. to commr. for lands, 1909; conveyancing clk., land dept., 1910; ag. conveyancer, 1913; asst. regir., crown lands, 1918; musketry instr., K.A.R., 1918 (recd. thanks G.O.O.L. of C.); regir. of titles, 1919; dep. recorder of titles, 1919; ag. prin. regir., crown lands, 1920; dep. regir.-gen., titles and ch. controlling rev. authority, 1920-21; law offr., legal adviser, etc., St. Helena, Aug., 1923; col. audir., mem., bd. of commrs., crown property, Sept., 1923; offr.-in-charge, secretariat, 1925; commr. for preparation of new edn. of laws, 1925.
LEA, WALTER ALBERT, A.M.I.C.E.—B. 1883; ed. at Carpenters' Company's Coll. and West Ham Tech. Inst.; dist. engrnr., Br. Guiana, Dec., 1914; exec. engrnr., P.W.D., Tanganyika Territory, 1929.

LEACH, FRANK, M.C., B.A. (Oxon.)—B. 1889; on mily. serv., July, 1917; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., May, 1921; attd., secretariat, June, 1921; office asst. to asst. govt. agt., Hambantota, Nov., 1922; asst. attit. offr., Oct., 1923; office asst. to dir., med. and sanry. services, Apr., 1929; asst. attit. offr., Jan., 1930; asst. govt. agt.; Badulla, Feb., 1931; do., Hambantota, Apr., 1932; asst. ch. sec. and sec., pub. services comm., Dec., 1935.
LEADBETTER, SIDNEY.—B. 1902; apptd. after compet. exam., cler. offr., C.O., 4th May, 1920; cler. offr., higher grade, 3rd Mar., 1937.
LEAKY, DOUGLAS GRAY BAZZETT.—B. 1907; ed. Weymouth and St. John's Coll., Cambridge, B.A. forestry; asst. conserv., forests, Kenya, Aug., 1932.

LEAKEY, RICHARD JOHN MONTAGUE.—B. 1884; ed. Starnford Schl., prty. tuition and St. John's Coll., Cambridge, B.A. (Cantab.); office asst., Nigerian Rly. open lines, 1923; asst. engrnr., do., capital wks., 1923; asst. engrnr., open lines, 1927; senr. engrnr., do., 1936.

LEATHES, FRANCIS PALLENT.—B. 1888; S. African M. Pol., 1907-10; E.A. campaign, 1914-18; Tanganyika Territory pol., 1916; asst. supt., 1928; ag. dep. commr., several occasions; dep. commr., June, 1936.

LEAVITT, CLYDE.—B. 1878; ed., High Schl., Bellaire, Mich., Univ. of Mich. (B.A., 1901; M.So. For., 1904); ent. U.S. forest serv., assistant asst., 1903; apptd. forest asst., 1904, with successive appts. as ch. of boundaries, asst. ch., office of organization, ch., office of organization, dist. forester, and asst. forester; ch. forester, comm. of conserv., and bd. of rly. commrs., Canada, 1912; ch. fire inspr., bd. of rly. commrs., 1921; mem., soc. of American Foresters; successively pres. and sec., Can. Soc. of Forest Engrs.
LE CHEMINANT, PERCY.—B. 1893; ed. Christ Ch. Schl., Clifton; R.A.M.C., 1914-16; R.E., 1916-19; Midland Rly., 1909-23; asst. acctnt., K.U.R. and H., 1930.
LECK, W. J., M.I.M.E.—B. 1889; ed. St. Bees Schl., Cumberland; colliery man., Nigeria, 1914.

LECKIE, CAPT. PETER MARTIN, L.S. (Vict.).—B. 1890; survr., dept. lands and surveys, Dec., 1907; stat survr., May, 1913; war serv., Sept., 1915-18; survr., Perak, F.M.S., Oct., 1920; do., Kedah, Apr., 1923; asst. supt., surveys, Aug., 1926; ag. senr. asst. supt., surveys, Aug., 1936.

LE CONTE, LOUIS.—B. 1881; mag., lesser dependencies, Mauritius, 1909; do., Mauritius and Rodrigues, Oct., 1913; ag. mast. and regir., sup. ct., 1913-14; ag. prot. of immigrts. and poor law commrs., 1914; war serv. in France and Macedonia, 1916-19; food contr., May 1920; puline judge, July, 1932; ag. ch. judge, sup. ct., Oct., 1933 and March-Ded., 1938.

LEE, FRANK G.—B. 1903; ed. Brentwood Schl. and Downing Coll., Cambridge; 1st cls. English tripso, 1923; 1st cls. historical tripso, part II, 1924; foundation schl., 1924; appkd. to C.O. after compet. exam., 8th Nov., 1926; temp. ag. prin., 26th Sept., 1930; seconded to Nyasaland admnstr., 2nd Apr., 1931; asst. pvt. sec. to S. of S. for the cols., 4th Aug., 1933; seconded to Nyasaland admnstr., 1931-33; visited Bechuanaland Prot. as sec. to finan. comnrr. (Sir A. Pim), 1933; asst. pvt. sec. to S. of S. for the cols., 1933; visited Cyprus as sec. to finan. comnrr. (Sir R. Oakden), 1934; prin. 1934; seconded to Imp. Defence Coll., 1938.

LEE, HARRIE BETTIE, D.S.O., M.C., M.B., B.S. (hons.) Melbourne, D.T.M. and H., engt.; certif. London S.T.M. (with distinct.).—B. 1888; ed. Sydney and Melbourne Univ.; four years serv. with A.I.F. in field as R.M.O.; field ambulance offr. and D.A.D.M.S., 1st Australian Divn., 1914-18; med. offr., W.A.M.S., Nigeria, Jan., 1924; specialist, Nigeria, Aug., 1933.

LEE, JOHN ALEXANDER, D.C.M., (1917).—B. 1891; ed. Albany Street Public Schl., Dunedin; war serv. with N.Z.E.F.; mem., N.Z. H. of R., 1922-28 and since 1931; parly. under sec. to min. of finance, 1936; author of "Children of the Poor," "The Hunted," "Civilian into Soldier" and "Socialism in New Zealand."

LEE, JOHN HENRY BURKILL.—B. 1905; ed. Univ. Coll., Leicester; cadet, Hong Kong, civ. serv., Dec., 1928; asst. treas., Apr., 1931; asst. P.M.G., 1932; 1st clk. and pol. mag., Kowloon, Feb., 1934; asst. dist. offr., North, Jan., 1938; pvt. sec. to O.A.G., May, 1935; do. to gov., Dec., 1935; extra asst. col. sec., Mar., 1936; dep. clk., comm., Aug.-Sept., 1936.

LEE, SYDNEY WILLIAM TIMPSON, M.D., M.B., B.Ch., B.A.O., D.P.H. (Belf.), D.T.M. (L'pool), D.O.M.S. (R.C.P.S. Eng.).—B. 1898; surg. sub. lt., R.N.V.R., 1917-18; M.O.S., Rhodesia, 1921-22, M.O., Uganda, 1922; S.H.O., Uganda, 1930; ag. D.D.S.S., 1930; ag. D.D.M.S., 1934; S.M.O., Zanzibar, 1934; M.O.H., port health offr. and offr. i/c. eye clinic, 1934; ag. D.M.S., 1937.
LEE, HON. SIR W. H., K.C.M.G. (1922), Kt. BACH. (1920).—M.H.A., Tasmania; premier, chief sec., and min. for educn., 1916-22.

LEEMBRUGGEN, EDWARD LESLIE.—B. 1893; regisv. clk., Western Pacific high comn., 1917; corres. clk., Gilbert and Ellies Ics. Col., 1920; sec. to res. comnrr., 1922; sec. to gov't., 1931; ag. admstive offr., Fanning Is., June 1936.

LEES, AUBREY TREVOR OSWALD.—B. 1899; ed. Repton and Royal Milly. Academy, Woolwich; ent. R. Arty., Jan., 1919; served with 2nd R.W. Fuz., Ireland, June, 1920; and with Arab and Kurdish Levies, Iraq and Kurdistan, June, 1922; add. air hd. qrs., Iraq (air staff intell., spec. serv.) June, 1924; admnstr., Zanzibar, Feb., 1926; pvt. sec. to Br. res. and ag. asst. sec., Nov., 1926; clk. of exec. and leg. couns. and edr. of gazette, Sept., 1927; temp. addt., C.O., 1928; admstive offr., Palestine, 1929.
LEFFRAUX, LESLIE.—B. 1886; ed. privately; service with bank of England, 1904-33; gov't. reserve bk. of N.Z., 1934.

LE GEYT, ASHLEY HUBERT, B.A.—B. 1902; ed. Victoria Coll., Jersey and Pembroke Coll., Oxford; cadet, Tanganyika Territory, Oct., 1925; asst. dist. offr., Nov., 1927; asst. sec., Dec., 1927; dist. offr., Nov., 1937.

LEGGE, CYRIL HENRY ETHELWOOD.—B. 1883; joined B. Guiana civ. serv., June, 1901; clk. in surg.-gens. office, cent. bd. of health, cust. and atty.-gens. office; commissary, May, 1906; ag. stip. mag., Mar., 1914 to Dec., 1916; stip. mag., 2nd grade, Dec., 1916; stip. mag., 1st grade, Mar., 1921; passed final bar exam., 1912; called to bar, Lincoln Inn, July, 1922.

LEIGH-CLARE, HARRY JAMES LEIGH, M.C., B.A. (Cantab.).—B. 1891; on milly. serv., Jan., 1915; cadet, Ceylon, Feb., 1920; addl. Colombo kach., Mar., 1920; addl. office of food contrlr., Apr., 1920; ag. office asst. to govt. agt., S. Prov., Mar., 1921; office asst. to govt. agt., Cent. Prov., Apr. and Sept., 1922; addnl. landing survr., cent. Colombo, Oct., 1924; 2nd landing survr., Nov., 1924; dist. judge, Anuradhapura, Dec., 1926; asst. govt. agt., Mannar, Dec., 1927; landing survr., cust. Mar., 1928; addnl. asst. govt. agt., Matara, Feb., 1929; asst. govt. agt., Matara, Feb., 1930; admstive secy. to dir. med. and san. services and sec. to min. of health, Sept., 1939; ag. tea exp. contr., Dec., 1934; dep. colir., cust., Apr., 1935.

LEMARIE, ERNEST JOSEPH, C.M.G. (1934).—B. 1874; ed. Sherbrooke (Que.), Coll.; ent. privy coun. office for Canada, Jan., 1894; priv. sec., prime min., 1904-11; post office dept., 1912-23; clk. of privy coun. for Canada, Aug., 1923.

LEMBERGER, MAURICE.—B. 1900; appkd. after compet. exam., asst. clk., 23rd Apr., 1918; on milly. serv., 26th Aug., 1918 to 27th Feb., 1920; appkd. cler. offr., 1st Jan., 1920; trans. to P.O. stores dept., Mar., 1920; trans. to C.O., 1st Sept., 1923.

LEMPRIERE, WILLIAM HENRY.—B. 1896; ed. Victoria Coll., Jersey; enlisted K.R.R.C., 1916; 2nd lieut., Cheshire Regt., 1917; ag. captn., Schl. of Instrn., E.E.F., 1919-21; demob., 1921; aud. audit dept., Oct., 1921; asst. audr., Gold Coast, Nov., 1921; senr. asst. audr., Kenya, 1934, ag. dep. audr., May-Sept., 1936, audr., B. Guiana, 1937.

LENTON, HENRY JOHN.—Cape telegr. serv., Sept., 1898; clk., staff branch, Jan., 1902; 1st cls. clk., ditto, Apr., 1912; senr. clk., ch. clk.'s office, Apr., 1918; prin. clk., ditto, Dec., 1918; 2nd grade, ch. clk., Apr., 1921; asst. sec., Nov., 1923; ag. P.M.G., May to Oct., 1924 and from July-Dec., 1926; P.M.G., Apr., 1926.
LESLIE, COL. R. B. M.O., Lincolnshire Regt.—Commdt., Ceylon defence force, Mar., 1936.
LESSELS, RAYMOND.—B. 1897; asst. engrnr., posts and tels. dept., Nigeria, Mar., 1924; divnl. engrnr., Apr., 1933; ag. engrnr.-in-ch., 1933, 1934 and 1938.

LESTER, A. R., M.B., B.S. (Bomb.), F.R.F.P.S. (Glas.), D.P.H., D.T.M. and H. (Edin.).—B. 1890; Indian med. serv., 1917-23; milly. serv., India and Persia, 1918-23; med. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Feb., 1924; med. offr. in ch. sp. sociological investgn., Kabama, 1927-31; senr. med. offr., Uganda, 1934.

LETHAM, CHARLES KINGSLEY, B.Sc. (agr.).—B. 1901; ed. S. African Coll. Sohl., Cape Town; B.Sc. (agr.), Transvaal Univ. Coll., Pretoria, Univ. of S. Africa, 1922; dist. agrl. offr., Tanganyika Territory, 1924.

LETHEM, SIR GORDON JAMES, K.O.M.G. (1936), C.M.G. (1934).—B. 1886; ed. at Mill Hill Sohl., Middlesex, and Edin. and Grenoble Univ., ag. govt. agt., Sabaragamuwa, July, 1933; govt. agt., Prov. of Sabaragamuwa, July, 1935; do., Prov. of Uva, Sept., 1937.

LUGARD, 1ST BARON (CR. 1928), RT. HON. SIR FREDERICK JOHN DEALTRY LUGARD, P.C. (1920), G.C.M.G. (1911), K.C.M.G. (1901), C.B. (1896), D.S.O. (1887). — B. 1858; ed. Rossall and Sandhurst; 1st commn., 9th foot, May, 1878; captain, Aug., 1880; major, Aug., 1896; lieut.-col., July, 1899; col., July, 1905; temp. brig.-gen., 1900-07; to India, Sept., 1878; active service, Afghanistan, Sept., 1879, to Sept., 1880, medal; Soudan, Feb. to Nov., 1885; medal, clasp, "Tofrek" Khedive's Star, despatches; Burmah, Oct., 1886, to Aug., 1887; mentioned in despatches, D.S.O. and medal. To Africa, Feb., 1888; commanding expdn. against slave traders; very severely wounded. To East Africa, Nov., 1889, to Oct., 1892; exploration of Sabakh; admnstr. Uganda; went to Borgu in commd. expdn., July, 1894, and made treaties on behalf of Royal Niger Company, returned, Apl., 1895, Feb., 1896, led expedition across Kalahari for Brid Charterland Co.; apptd. commnstr., and commd., West African frontier force, Aug., 1897; high commnstr. for Northern Nigeria, Dec., 1899; resigned Sept., 1906; gov. of Hong Kong, May, 1907; gov., N. and S. Nigeria, Mar., 1912; gov.-gen., Nigeria, Dec., 1913; assumed govt., Jan., 1914; retired, 1919; Br. mem., Permanent Mandates commn. since 1922; author of "The Dual Mandate in Br. Tropical Africa" and other works.
twin_text: LUGARD, 1st Baron (cr. 1928), Rt. Hon. Sir Frederick John Deatly Lugard, P.C. (1920), G.C.M.G. (1911), K.C.M.G. (1901), C.B. (1896), D.S.O. (1887). — B. 1858; ed. Rossall and Sandhurst; 1st coman., 9th foot, May, 1878; captain, Aug., 1885; major, Aug., 1896; lieut.-col., July, 1899; col., July, 1906; temp. brig.-gen., 1900-07; to India, Sept., 1878; active service, Afghanistan, Sept., 1879, to Sept., 1880, medal; Soudan, Feb. to Nov., 1885; medal, clasp, "Tofrek" Khedive's Star, despatches; Burmah, Oct., 1886, to Aug., 1887; mentioned in despatches, D.S.O. and medal. To Africa, Feb., 1888; commanding expdn. against slave traders; very severely wounded. To East Africa, Nov., 1889, to Oct., 1892; exploration of Sabakh; admnstd. Uganda; went to Borgu in comd. expdn., July, 1894, and made treaties on behalf of Royal Niger Company, returned, Apl., 1895, Feb., 1896, led expedition across Kalahari for Brd Charterland Co.; apptd. commr., and comdt., West African frontier force, Aug., 1897; high commr. for Northern Nigeria, Dec., 1899; resigned Sept., 1906; gov. of Hong Kong, May, 1907; gov., N. and S. Nigeria, Mar., 1912; gov.-gen., Nigeria, Dec., 1913; assumed gov., Jan., 1914; retired, 1919; Br. mem., Permanent Mandates commn. since 1922; author of "The Dual Mandate in Br. Tropical Africa" and other works.

raw_text: LUGG, HARRY CAMP.—B. 1882; ed. MACDONNELL, SIR MICHAEL FRANCIS JOSEPH, Kt. Bach. (1929), M.A.—B. 1882; ed. St. Paul’s Schol. and St. John’s Coll., Camb. (scholar), B.A., 1904; pres., Cambridge Union, 1904; called to the bar, Inner Temple, May, 1908; asst. dist. commr., Gold Coast, Jan., 1911; pol. mag. and inspr. of schls., Gambia, Apr., 1913; legal adviser, Gambia, Apr., 1918; mem., exec. and leg. couns.; solr.-gen., Sierra Leone, Aug., 1918; atty.-gen., Apr., 1920; mem., exec. and leg. couns.; ag. ch. just., circuit judge and judge of ct. of appeal on various occasions, 1919-23; ch. just., Palestine, 1927 and ex officio pres., full ct. of appl. of Egypt, 1930; compiler, King's Lynn and Bedfords Schls. and Magdalene Coll., Cambridge (schol.) ; joined Sudan polit. serv., 1905; successively inspr. in provs. of Kordofan, Blue Nile and Khartoum; polit. and intell. offr., expdy. force, Darfur reoccupation, 1916 (three times ment. in desps., D.S.O.); sub-gov., Darfur prov.; asst. civ. sec., Sudan gov.; civ. sec., do., 1926; gov. and commr.-in-ch., Tanganyika Territory, 1934; high commr. and commr.-in-ch. for Palestine and high commr. for Transjordan, 1938; Order of the Nile (2nd cls.), 1930; Burton Memorial Med. of the R. Regt., Aug., 1914; Gallipoli, 1915 (wounded); Mesopotamia, 1916 (wounded); lieut., 9th Batt. Worc. Regt., France, 1917-18 (wounded and prisoner); capt., 1st Batt. Worc. Regt., B.A. (Oxon.), 1919; diploma of forestry (with distinction—placed head of the list), 1920; M.A., 1920; forestry comm., 1920-22; instr., forestry schl., Forest of Dean; asst. commr., national savings commt., 1922; dep. conservator, forests, Trinidad and Tobago, 1923; conservator, forests, 1923; mem., bd. of agr., 1923; conserv., forests, Gold Coast, 1934.

MARSHALL, REGINALD HENRY.—B. 1890; ed. Dulwich; active serv., Aug., 1914 to Mar., 1919; col. audit dept., May, 1919; audr., Seychelles, June, 1919; audr., Rr. Honduras, Aug., 1924; C.A.D., Oct., 1927; dep. audr., Tanganyika Territory, July, 1930; prin. asst. audr., Nigeria, 1937.

MARTIN, FREDERICK JOHN, M.A., Ph.D. (Cantab.), F.I.C.—B. 1891; (nat. sci. tripos pt. 1 and agril. dipl.); war serv., 1914-19; active serv., France, 1915-17; demob., rank of capt. (flight commdr., R.A.F., 1919); senr. asst. gov't. chem., Sudan gov't., 1919-24; agril. chem., Sierra Leone, 1924; asst. dir., agr., 1930; dir., agr., 1934.

MARTIN, JOHN MILLER, B.A.—B. 1904; ed. Edinburgh Acad. and Oxford; class. schol., Corpus Christi Coll., 1923-27; 1st cls. class. mods., 1924; B.A., 1927; apptd. after compet. exam., asst. prin., Doms. Office, 2nd Nov., 1927; seconded to Malayan civ. serv., 1st Dec., 1931; pvt. sec. to perm. under-secr., D.O. (Sir Edward Harding), 21st Nov., 1934; prin., C.O., 21st Oct., 1935; sec., royal comm. on disturbances in Palestine, 1936.

MARTIN, KENNETH SEFTON, B.A. (Oxon).—B. 1888; asst. dist. commr., S. Nigeria, 1911; supervisor, cust., 2nd grade, 1911; 1st grade, 1919; senr. collr., 1929; asst. comptr., 1933; ag. comptr. on various occasions, 1931-37; dep. comptr., 1936.
MARTIN, LEWIS VERNON.—B. 1902; apptd. after compet. exam., cler. offr., C.O., 17th Mar., 1920; cler. offr., higher grade, 1st Jan., 1935.
MARTIN, MISS MARY, M.B.E.—Staff nurse, Botabeblo leper attmnt., Basutoland, 1914; matron, 1937.
MARTIN, HON. WILLIAM LEE.—B. 1870; ed. Waimate High Sch.; mem., N.Z. H. of R., 1927-31 and re-elected, 1935; min. of agr., 1935.

MARTINDALE, MILES HAY, Barrister-at-Law (Middle Temple).—B. 1893; asst. dist. offr., 1919; pvt. sec. to gov', 1920; asst. sec., S.P. secr., 1923; ag. dist. offr., 1924-27; dist. offr., 1928; ag. crown coun. and ag. ch. registr., 1933; asst. judge, high ct., Prot., 1934; judge, Prot., ct., Apr., 1937.

MARTYN, ELDERED BRIDGEMAN, B.A., A.I.C.T.A.—B. 1903; ed. Marlborough Coll., Oriel Coll., Oxford and Imp. Col. Sci. & Tech.; O.O. scholarship courses in mycology, Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge, and Imp. Col. Trop. Agr., Trinidad; botanist mycologist, dept. agr., Br. Guiana, 1928; rep., col. inter. grassland confere., London, 1937; botanist, plant pathologist, and supt. bot. gdns., 1938.

MASON, FRANK REGINALD, Dip. Agr. H.A.A.C.—B. 1899; ed. St. George's Sch., Harpenden; Harper Adams Agr. Coll., Newport and R. Coll. of Sci.; capt., M.S.V.R.; asst. mycologist, F.M.S., Jan., 1922; ag. prin. agril. offr., Johore, Sept., 1933; ag. state agril. offr., Perak, May, 1935; visited Malabar Coast, S. India, to study coir industry, Jan., 1936; ag. state agril. offr., Perak, May, 1936; dep. dir., agr. and fisheries, Palestine, 1937.

MASON, GERALD BOVELL.—Ed. Harrison Coll., Barbados; matric., London Univ., 1890; joined London hosp., 1890; passed prelim. scientific, 1892; M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.), 1896, certif., London Sch. of Trop. Med., 1904; D.P.H., Cambridge, 1917; med. offr., No. 4 dist., Antigua, 1903; ag. commr., Virgin Is., 1904; surg.-lieut., Antigua def. force, 1904; med. and med. offr., Anguilla and dep. judge of sup. ct., Leeward Is., Jan., 1905 to Sept., 1906; med. offr., No. 6 dist., Nevis, 1906; med. offr., dist. "D", and med. dist. "F", Dominica, 1907; med. offr., St. Vincent, 1909-12; temp. capt., R.A.M.C.; med. offr. in charge of troops, Cambridge, 1916-19; founder and hon. sec., W. Indian Club, 1897-8; founder and hon. sec., West India Red Cross Soc'y., 1921-1924; dir., med. and health dept., Bermuda, Aug., 1924.
MASON, HON. HENRY GREATHEAD REY, M.A., LL.B. (N.Z.).—B. 1885; ed. Clyde Quay Sch., Wellington Coll. and Victoria Univ. Coll., N.Z.; mem., N.Z. H. of R. since 1926; atty.-gen. and min. of just., 1935.

MASSEY, HON. VINCENT.—B. 1887; ed. St. Andrew's Coll., Toronto, Univ. of Toronto and Balliol Coll., Oxford, (M.A.); lect., mod. hist., Univ. of Toronto, 1913-15; in ch. musketry, mil. dist. No. 2 (Canada), 1915-18 (ment.); temp. lieut. col., 1916; assoc. sec., war comttee. of cabinet (Ottawa), Jan.-Dec., 1918; sec., govt. repatriation comttee. and later dir., 1918-19; minr. without portfolio in Dominion cabinet, 1925; attended Imp. Conf., London, 1926; H.M. envoy extraordinary and min. plenipotentiary for Canada to U.S.A., 1926-30; high commnr. for Canada in London, 1935.
MASSON, ALEXANDER HERBERT LARNACH.—B. 1901; called to bar, Middle Temple, 1926; 2nd mag., Victoria, Jan., 1938; mag. county St. Patrick, Trinidad, Nov., 1938.

MASSON, GERALD GUSTAVE.—B. 1888; dipl. of agr., Roseworthy Agr. Coll., S. Australia; served in Great War with 9th Australian Light Horse Regt., 1915-19; Gallipoli, Sinai, Palestine and Syria; ment. in despa.; inspr., agr., occupied enemy territory admstr., 1919; inspr., agr., N. Palestine, 1920; inspr., agr. and forests, 1924; ch. agr. offr., 1930; sec., comttee. of agro. nomy, gen. agr. coun., Palestine.

MASTEN, HON. CORNELIUS ARTHUR.—B. 1857, Quebec; educ. parish dist. schl., La Colle; La Colle Academy; Victoria Univ., Coburg, Ont. (graduated with hon., May, 1879); mathematical master, high schl., Orillia, Ont., 1879-1880; entered law soc. of Upper Canada, Oct., 1880; called to the bar, 1883; K.C. (Ont.), and bencher of the law soc. of Upper Canada, 1908; commr., to enquire into the Insurance situation in Ont. with a view to improvements in the law; pres., Alumni Assoc. of Toronto Univ.; chmn., exec. comttee. of the speaker's patriotic league; author of "Masten Company Law in Canada"; justice of the sup. ct. of Ontario, Nov., 1915.

MASTERTON-SMITH, PRECIVAL FRANK, M.C.—B. 1895; ed. Christ's Hosp. and Wadham Coll., Oxford (hons. mod. hist.); B.A., 1921; R. Fusiliers, 1914-16; 60th Rifles, 1916-19; M.C., 1917; capt., res. of Offrs., 60th Rifles; adminis. offr., Nigeria, 25th Jan., 1922.

MATEER, EDWARD JOHN.—Ed. Foyle Coll. and Taunton Schl.; asst. treas., Uganda, July 1920; senr. asst. treas., 1931; prin. asst. treas., 1933; prin. asst., actg.-gen., 1937; ag. acct.-gen., Feb.-May, 1938.

MATHER, NORMAN FREDERICK HUGH, B.A. (Oxon).—B. 1890; ed. Merchant Taylor's Schl., and Hertford Coll., Oxford (schol.); cadet, F.M.S., Nov., 1913; served in Rifle Brig., Dec., 1915-Sept., 1919; asst. sec. to res., Selangor, Nov., 1919; asst. dist. offr., Mar., 1921; regsr., war serv. land grant scheme, S. Feb., 1922; sec. to res., Pahang, Dec., 1923; mag., Seremban, July, 1925; offr., cls. III, Nov., 1925; chmn., sany. bd., Seremban, Aug., 1926; cls. II, Nov., 1930; asst. advr., Segamat, May 1928; dist. offr., Larut and regsr. titles, Perak, Mar., 1932; chmn., govt. rice mill in addn., May, 1933; ag. commnr., trade and cust., Johore, Sept., 1936; offr. cls. I B, Sept., 1936.

MATHERS, FREDERICK FRANCIS, K.C., LL.B.—B. 1871; ed. schls. in Halifax, Nova Scotia, Dalhousie law schl. and Harvard law schl.; called to the bar, 1892; dep. prov. sec. and clk. of exec. coun., N.S., 1902; also regsr. of joint stock companies, 1909; now deputy atty.-gen.

MATHESON, CAPT. C. L. T., M.C.—Commissioned in Royal Engineers, 1909; asst. dir. of surv., G. Coast, 1914; Togoland campaign, dir., Cameroon, 1914-15; France with Field Coy. R.E., 1916-17; W.O., 1918; dep. dir. topographical surveys, G. Coast, 1920.

MATHEW, CHARLES.—B. 1903; ed. Downside schl. and Trinity Coll., Oxford; barrister-at-law, Lincoln's Inn, 1927; cadet, admstr., Nigeria, 1929; asst. dist. offr., 1932; ag. pol. mag., crown coun., and offr. admstr. on various occasions, 1930-33; mag., Uganda, 1933; ch. mag., Palestine, but did not proceed; crown coun., Uganda, 1936; ag. solr.-gen., Nov., 1935-Dec., 1936; judcl. advr., Buganda, 1938.

MATHEWS, ARNOLD BURNETT.—B. 1898; ed. Shrewsbury (schl.) and Magdalene Coll., Cambridge (cls. schl.); sub-lieut., R.N.V.R., 1917-19; 2nd cls., Pt. I, classical tripes, 1920; M.A.; 2nd cls., anthropological tripes, 1922; asst. dist. offr., N.P. Nigeria, Jan., 1928; asst. sec., N.P. secretariat, Feb., 1925; asst. sec., Nigerian secretariat, Aug., 1927; clk. to exec. and leg. couns., Apr.-Sept., 1928; attd. C.O., July, 1929; asst. sec., Nigerian secret., Sept., 1929; asst. dist. offr., N. Prov., Aug., 1930; dist. offr. Jan., 1931; ap. duty reorganization pagan admstr., 1933-36; ch. asst. col. sec., Sierra Leone, July, 1935; ag. col. sec., various periods, 1936-38; gov't. dep. on several occasions.

MATHIESON, HON. JOHN ALEXANDER, K.C.—B. 1863; ed. Harrington Gram. Schl., Prince of Wales' Coll., Taught Schl., Desable Gram. Schl., P.E.I., read law with McLean and Macdonald; called to the P.E.I. bar, 1893; K.C., 1905; el. to legislature for King's county, 1900; re-el., 1904, 1908 and 1912; leader of the opposition, 1903; premier and atty.-gen., 2nd Dec., 1911; ch. just., P.E.I., 13th June, 1917.

MATHISON, JOSEPH GEORGE, B.L. (Glasgow)—B. 1907; asst. admstr.-gen., Zanzibar, 1930; ag. munic. and land offr., Apr., 1934; munic. and land offr., Sept., 1934; ag. admstr.-gen., pub. trustee, registr. docs. in addn., Apr., 1935; asst. admstr.-gen. and dep. offr. recr., Uganda, 1936.
MATTHEWS, HON. ALBERT, LL.D.—B. 1873; chmn., bd. of govs., McMaster Univ., Toronto; lieut.-gov., Ontario, Nov., 1937.

MATTHEWS, LESLIE GARD.—Ed. Plymouth, Oxford Univ., O.T.C., 1915; O.A.S., France, 1916 and 1917, Lane. Fusiliers; Mesopotamia, 1917-21, attd., Indian Army and Iraq Levis; Arab rebellion, Iraq, 1920 (med. and clasp); asst. treas., Somaliland, June, 1921; dist. pol. offr., Somaliland, Feb., 1923; asst. commnr., pol., Nigeria, 1926.

MAUDE, HENRY EVANS, M.B.E. (1939).—B. 1906; ed. Highgate Schol. and Jesus Coll., Cambridge; B.A., 1929; M.A., 1933; cadet, Gilbert and Ellice Is., 1929; dep. commnr., 1931; ag. native lands commnr., 1931; admstr. offr., 1932; ag. sec. to govt., Mar.-June, 1933; nat. lands commnr., Gilbert Is., 1935; rep. Gilbert & Ellice Is. at confce. and seminar on educn. in Pacific countries, Honolulu, July-Aug., 1936; asst. dist. offr., Zanzibar, Sept., 1936; admstr. offr., Gilbert & Ellice Is., 1937.

MALAN, FRANCOIS STEPHANUS.—B. 1882; ed. Normal Coll., Cape Town and Victoria Coll., Stellenbosch, B.A.; inspr. schls., Transvaal, 1916; prin., Normal Coll., Ermelo, Transvaal, 1919; sec., Transvaal educn. dept., 1924; inspr., schls., Transvaal, 1935.

MALAN, HENDRIK JACOBUS, B.A. (Cape).—B. 1879; Cape civ. serv., 1900; war serv., capt., Pretoria Rgt., S.W.A., 1914-15; asst. mag., Johannesburg, 1918; mag., Britstown, 1919; Ladysmith, 1922; Johannesburg, 1927; Senekal, 1930; Potchefstroom, 1933; Pietermaritzburg, 1936; Port Elizabeth, Oct., 1936.

MALAN, WOUTER DE VOS.—B. 1892; ed. Boys High Schl., Huguenot Coll., Wellington, Victoria Coll., Stellenbosch and Teachers' Coll., Columbia Univ., New York; B.A. lit. and phil., 1911; B.A. (hons.) hist., 1916; Ph.D., Col. Univ., 1923; princ., Ceres High Schl., 1924-28; prof., educn., Univ. of Stellenbosch, 1929-30; prof. asst. to aspt.-gen., educn., Cape Prov., 1930; supt.-gen., educn., Cape Prov., 1934.

MALHERBE, DAVID GABRIEL, B.Sc. (Min. Eng.).—B. 1887; ed. Potchefstroom Coll. (High Schl.), Victoria Coll., Stellenbosch, and Transvaal Univ. Coll., Johannesburg; asst. inspr., mines, 1920; inspr., min., 1933; asst. govt. mines engnr., 1937.

MALHERBE, ERNEST GIDEON.—B. 1886; ed. Stellenbosch Univ. and Columbia Univ.; B.A. Hon., M.A. (phil.) (Cape), M.A. and Ph.D. (educn.) Columbia; fellow, Teacher's College, Columbia Univ., 1923-24; teacher, Cape Town Training Coll.; lect., educn. psychology, Univ. of Stellenbosch; senr. lect., educn., Univ. of Cape Town; ch. investigator educn. sec., Carnegie Poor White Commn. of research, 1928-32; dir., National Bureau for Educn. and Social Research, 1929; author publications on educn. subjects.

MALHERBE, JOZUA FRANCOIS.—B.1880; clk., dept. of just., Transvaal, 1900; clk., commr. of pol., 1902; 1st gde. clk. and A.R.M., dept. of just., 1908; pub. pros., Kimberley, 1912; senr. clk., dep. of just., 1920; mag., Wynberg, 1921; addt. mag., Pretoria, 1925; mem., wage bd., 1926; pub. serv. inspr., 1930; workmen's compensation commn., 1934.

MALLARD, FRANCIS ALLEN, F.R.I.B.A.—B. 1896; war serv., Aug., 1915-Dec., 1918; asst. archt., P.W.D., F.M.S., Jan., 1921; asst. archt., Johore, Aug., 1931; asst. archt., S'pore., Dec., 1934; exec. archt., cent. off., P.W.D., Feb., 1938; a st. archt., S'pore., Mar., 1936.
MALONE, DENIS KRON.—B. 1903; ed. Cooper's Company Schl.; cler. offr., C.O., 29th Mar., 1921; cler. offr., higher grade, 13th July, 1937.
MALPAS, ARCHIBALD HENRY, M.M., F.L.S., F.Z.S.—B. 1884; asst. marine biologist, Ceylon, July, 1920; dir., Colombo museum, and marine biologist, May, 1934.

MANDERS, RICHARD HENRY DAVIN, B.A. (Cantab).—B. 1904; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Jan., 1927; attd. secretariat, Feb., 1927; attd., Kandy kach., Oct., 1927; office astt., ditto schls., N. Sembilan, Jan., 1926; asst. inspr., schls., S'pore, Oct., 1930; senr. educn. offr. (B), Nov., 1931; ag. prin., Raffles Instn., Oct., 1936; ag. inspr., schls., Penang, Dec., 1936; senr. inspr., schla., Selangor, Dec., 1937.
MEADEN, HENRY, M.B.E. (1931).—B. 1882; asst. warden, Tobago, 1919; asst. warden, St. Patrick, 1922; mag. warden, Tobago, 1st Apr., 1926; warden, St. George and prot., immigrts., 1937.

MEDWAY, F. W.—Imp. serv. (acctn.-gen.'s dept., G.P.O.), 1901-2; clk., G.P.O., Bloemfontein, Nov., 1902; clk., treasy., O.F.S., May, 1908; senr. clk., treasy., Union of S. Africa, Apr., 1912; senr. clk., inland rev. dept., Dec., 1916; prin. clk., Apr., 1917; ch. clk., April, 1928; recr., Pietermaritzburg, Apr., 1933; do., Durban, June, 1938.

MEGARRY, THOMAS, B.A.—B. 1898; ed. Royal Academical Inst., Belfast and Trinity Coll., Dublin; B.A. (1st cls. hona., classics and mental and moral philosophy); cadet, Hong Kong, Nov., 1922; passed cadet, Dec., 1924; asst. to sec., Chinese affrs., 1925; asst. head, sany. dept., Mar., 1928 3rd asst. col. sec., June, 1928; sec., currency comtce. in addn., 1930; mag. and dist. offr., N. district, New Territories, Mar., 1931; head, sany. dept. and pres., sany. bd., Jan., 1933; dist. offr., N. dist., New Territories, Jan., 1934; 2nd asst. col. sec. and dep. clk., couns., Dec., 1936; supt., impts. and expts., Feb., 1937; prin. asst. col. sec. and clk., couns., Nov., 1937.

MEGAW, ARTHUR HUBERT STANLEY, M.A. (Cantab.).—B. 1910; ed. Campbell coll., Belfast and Peterhouse, Cambridge; Walston stud., 1931; asst. dir., Br. schl. of archaeology, Athens, Oct., 1935; ag. dir., May, 1936; dir. antiquities., Cyprus, July, 1936.

MEGSON, JOHN, M.B.E. (1937)—B. 1895; apptd. after compet. exam. clk. of the 2nd div. of the civ. serv.; temporarily assigned to Board of Agric., July, 1914; Home Office, Aug., 1914; on military service, Nov., 1915 to Mar., 1919; Home Office, Mar.-Apr., 1919; assigned to C.O., Apl., 1919; cler. offr.; higher grade, 9th Jan., 1923; staff offr., 13th Oct., 1930.

MEIGHEN, Rt. Hon. ARTHUR, P.C., K.C.—B. 1874; ed. St. Mary's Coll. Inst. and Toronto Univ.; B.A., 1896; 1st cls. to H. of C., Canada, g.e., 1908; re-el., 1911, 1913 and 1917; defeated, 1921; el. by-el., 1922; re-el., 1925; solr.-gen., 1913; re-el. accl., 1913; p.c. (Can.), 1915; sec. of state and min. of mines (Can.), 1917; later, same year, min. of interior and supt. gen., Ind. affrs.; prime min. of Canada, 1920; p.c. (Eng.), 1920; prime min., Can., 1926; def., 1927; mem., senate, 1932; min. without portfolio in B-nnett cabinet and govt. leader in senate, 1932; leader of oppoan., senate, 1925.

MELLISH, HON. HUMPHREY.—B. 1862; ed. Mount Mellick Schl., Prince of Wales Coll., Charlottetown, 1876-79 (holder of Govt. scholarship for County of Queen's P.E.I., 1876-78; medallist, classics and maths., 1879); Dalhousie Univ. (B.A., 1882, L.I.B.); matriculant, Univ. of Lond., hons. div., 1883; called to N.S. bar, 1890; K.C., 1904; practised law, 1901-1918; pres., N.S. Barristers' Soc., 1912-13; agt., Canadian min. of just. in N.S., 1903-04; judge, sup. ct., N.S., 1918, also surrogate, and dep. local judge in admty., for N.S. dist., 1920; local judge in admty., 1921.

MELVILLE, EUGENE.—B. 1911; ed. Queen's Park Schl., Glasgow and United Coll., St. Andrews; Harkness res. schol., 1929-33; 1st cls. hons., classics, 1933; 1st cls. hons., econa. and moral philosophy, 1935; asst. inspr., taxes, 1936; apptd., after compet. exam., asst. prin., C.O., Jan., 1936.

MENZIES, Rt. Hon. ROBERT GORDON, P.C. (1937), K.C., LL.M., M.P.-M.L.C., Victoria, 1928-32; min. without portfolio, 1928-29; M.L.A. for Nunawading, Victoria, 1932-34; atty.-gen., min. for rlys. and dep. premier; el. M.H.R. of C'wealth of Australia for Kooyong (Vic.), 1934; atty.-gen. and min. for industry, Lyons miny., 1934 and 1937; mem., ministerial delegn. to England, 1935; visited England on behalf of C'wealth govt., 1936 and 1938.

MERCIECA, PROF. SIR ARTURO, Kt. Bach. (1926), M.A., LL.D.—B. 1878; matric., Malta Univ., 1894; M.A., 1897; LL.D., 1901; el. for coun. of govt., 1907-10; asst. crown advoc., 1915; prof. of law, 1915; crown advoc., 1919; judge, 1921; ch. just., 1924; ex. in law, 1921.
MERRET, JOSEPH.—B. 1888; asct. Sydney metrop. meat industry bd., N.S.W., 1916; sec. and treas., 1931; commr., metrop. meat industry, 1933.

MERRICK, H. F. C.—B. 1889; ed. Battersea Gram. Schl. and London Univ.; asst. audr., S. Nigeria, 1913; ditto, Sierra Leone, 1914; audr., Malta, 1920-22; clk., col. audit dept., 1922; audr., Gibraltar, May, 1927.

MERRICK, JOHN EDWARD SIRGFBND, C.M.G. (1937), O.B.E. (1931).—B. 1888; ed. Winchester and Univ. Coll., Oxford; B.A. 1911; asst. dist. commr., Uganda Prot., 1912; 3rd asst. sec., 1913; 2nd asst. sec., 1916; senr. asst. sec., E. Africa Prot., Nov., 1919; asst. col. sec. (2nd grade), Kenya, 1926; ag. asst. col. sec. (1st grade) in 1925 and 1926; prin. asst. col. sec., 1927; ag. col. sec. for various periods, 1928-32; dep. ch. sec., Uganda, 1932; ag. ch. sec. in 1933, 1934 and 1936; ch. sec., Dec., 1933; ag. gov., July-Dec., 1937.

MESSERFY, GEORGE FORSHAW.—B. 1889; ed. Minto House Schl., Queen's Coll., Br. Guiana, and Victoria Coll., Jersey; ag. surr.'s ast., lands and mines, Br. Guiana, 1910; 5th cls. offr., do., 1911; 4th cls. offr., 1915; land offr. and dist. commissary, taxation, Demerara River, 1916; ag. ch. clk., lands and mines, 1926; supt., lands, 1930; ag. sec. and acctnt., do., on various occasions; seconded to col. sec., 1935; sec., coronation comtce., 1936; sec. and acctnt., land and mines dept., 1937.

METCALFE, CHARLES CAMPBELL, M.R.E. (1931), M.C.—B. 1880; ed. Elizabeth Coll., Guernsey; clk., W.O., 1901; clk., treasy., Nyassaland, 1911; K.A.R., 1914-22; ag. chief transport offr., Nyassaland, Apr., 1922 to Feb., 1923; ch. transport offr., Feb., 1923.
METIVIER, CAPT. HARRY VINCENT MERCER, O.B.E., B.Sc., M.R.C.V.S.—Govt. vety. surg. examr. of animals, Trinidad, Dec., 1920; senr. vety. offr., 1937.

METTAM, RICHARD WILLIAM MORISON.—B. 1895; prof. of bacteriology, Royal Vety. Coll., Dublin, Ireland; M.R.C.V.S., 1917; lieut. R.A.V.C., Aug., 1917; served in France and Flanders, 1917-1918; capt., 1918; Rhine Army, 1919; vety. research offr., dept. of agr., Onderstepoort, Pretoria, Jan., 1920; prof. of anatomy and embryology, Univ. of Witwatersrand, 1922-26; prof. of anatomy and embryology, Transvaal Univ. Coll., Pretoria, 1926-27; ag. asst. ch. vety. research lab., Kabeta, Kenya, 1927-28; vety. pathologist, Nigeria, 1937.

METZGEN, MONRAD SIGFRID, M.B.E.(1932).—B. 1893; temp. clk. to keeper of prisons, B. Hond., Mar., 1910; transf. to treas. dept., Aug., 1910; temp. asst. keeper of King's warehouses, Sept., 1910; 3rd grade cust. and excise offr., Aug., 1911; 2nd grade ditto, Jan., 1918; 2nd lieut., B. Hond. Territorial Force, June, 1918; internal rev. offr., Mar., 1920; seconded to income tax dept., June, 1921; prin. offr., income tax, Aug., 1922; offr. of eust., Nov., 1922; sec. to select comtee. to enquire into cost of living to civ. servants, Mar., 1923; ag. audr. in 1923 and 1924; ag. dist. comsnr., Orange Walk, June-July, 1923; ch. income tax collr., Jan., 1924; dist. comsnr., 1935; joint compiler, Br. Hond. Handbook.
twin_text: METZGEN, MONRAD SIGFRED, M.B.E. (1932).—B. 3.1893; temp. clk. to keeper of prisons, B. Hond., Mar., 1910; transfd. to treas.dept., Aug., 1910; temp. asst. keeper of King's warehouses, Sept., 1910; 3rd grade cust. and excise offr., Aug., 1911; 2nd grade ditto, Jan., 1918; 2nd lieut., B. Hond. Territorial Force, June, 1918; internal rev. offr., Mar., 1920; seconded to income tax dept., June, 1921; prin. offr. income tax, Aug., 1922; offr. of cust., Nov., 1922; sec. to select comtees to enquire into cost of living to civ. servants, Mar., 1923; ag. audr. in 1923 and 1924; ag. dist. commnr., Orange Walk, June-July, 1923; ch. income tax collr., Jan., 1924; dist. commnr., 1935; joint compiler, Br. Hond. Handbook.

raw_text: MEYER, DAVID DE WAAL.—B. 1897; ed. Grey Coll. Schl. and Grey Univ., Bloemfontein, O.F.S.; M.A., 1919; dipl. comm. economics, Rotterdam, 1922; pvt. sec., to min. of mines and industries, Union of S. Africa, 1924; in ch. external trade divn., dept. of mines and industries, 1926; mem. gov't. econ. advry. and tariff bd., 1930; trade comsnr., Montreal, 1932; accredited rep. of Union in Canada, 1938.
twin_text: MEYER, DAVID DE WAAL.—B. 1897; ed. Grey Coll. MONTANARO GAUCI, ANTHONY J., LL.D.—B. 1898; matric. Malta Univ., 1915; LL.D., 1922; cl. for leg. assem., 1927; dep. speaker, leg. assem., 1927; speaker, do., 1929; judge, 1930.

MONTGOMERY, H. R., C.M.G. (1936).—Asst. dist. comanr., E.A.P., May, 1908; dist. comanr., Apr. 1914; ag. senr. comanr., Kenya, 1926; senr. comanr., 2nd grade, 1929; 1st prov. comanr., 1932; ch. native comanr., 1934.

MOODY, SYDNEY, O.B.E. (1932)—B. 1889; ed. King's Schl., Pontefract, Leeds Univ. and Lincoln Coll., Oxford (Hist. Schol.), 2nd cls. hist. finals, 1914; pvt., Pub. Schls. Batt., R. Fusiliers, 1914; 2nd lieut., E. York. Regt., France, 1915; lieut., King's Liverpool Rgt., Egypt, 1916; lieut., Herefordshire Regt., Palestine, 1917-1918; held various appts. in O.E.T.A. (S.) MOONESINGHE, NALIN, B.A. (Oxon.).—B. 1899; cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., May, 1924; attd., Anuradhapura kach., June, 1924; do., Batticaloa kach., Jan., 1925; do., Badulla kach., June, 1925; pol. mag., Pt. Pedro, Nov., 1926; ditto. Puttalam, May, 1927; ditto, Jaffna, Aug., 1929; office asst., Galle kach., Nov., 1930; pol. mag., Pt. MOOR, HERBERT WILLIAM.—B. 1891; obtained higher standard certif. in Forestry at Imperial Research Inst. and Coll., Dehra-Dun, India, 1914; with medals for forest engineering and sylviculture; probationer, Indian provincial forest service, 1914; passed exams. in Indian land revenue systems, forest law and accounts, and Hindustani by the higher standard, 1915; permanent extra astt. conservator, 1916; transfd. to Trinidad as deputy conserv., forests, Jan., 1918; astt. conserv., forests, Gold Coast, Jan., 1923; prin. forest offr., Apr., 1926.

MOREAU, REGINALD ERNEST, C.M.Z.S., M.B.O.U.—B. 1897; 2nd. div. elk., W.O., 1914; exec. offr. gr. II, army audit office, H.Q., B.T.R., Cairo, 1920; sec. & librarian, E. African agr. research station, Amari, Tanganyika Territory, 1928, also in ch. ornithological research.
MORETON, JAMES REGINALD.—B. 1905; ed. Northwich Grammar Schl. and Jesus Coll., Cambridge; cadet, Hong Kong, Feb., 1928.

MORGAN, MAJOR KENNETH SAYCE.—B. 1886; ed. City of Lond. Sch.; astt. supt., sp. br., Singapore, Feb., 1936.

MULOCK, Rt. Hon. Sir WILLIAM, P.C. (1925), K.C.M.G. MURISON, Sir JAMES WILLIAM, K.T. BACH. (1919), B.A., LL.B., K.C.—B. 1872; ed. privately and at Trin. Hall, Camb.; senr. class. scholr., 1891; 1st div., 2nd cls. class. triplos, 1894; 3rd cls. law triplos, Part II., 1895; Latham English prize, 1895; B.A., LL.B., 1895; called to Bar, Middle Temple, Apr., 1896; devilled and practised at the Chancery Bar, 1896-1902; regis., E.A.P., Oct., 1902; town mag., Mombasa, Jan., 1904; 2nd astt. judge, H.B.M. et. for Zanzibar, Apr., 1904; sen. astt. judge, Mar., 1905; ch. judge, Zanzibar, 20th Sept., 1914; mem., of ct. of appl. for Eastern Africa, 1905-1910, and from 1914 upon transfer of Zanzibar to C.O.; judge, sup. ct. of H.H. Sultan of Zanzibar from 1908; arbitrator in dispute between Zanzibar and E.A.P. govts. as to H.H. mainland properties, 1911; recd. thanks of S. of S. for conversion of applied Indian Acts into Zanzibar Decrees, 1917; judge prize ct. Zanzibar from 1914; passed govt. exam. in Hindustani, 1903, and in Swahili, 1904; examr. in Swahili to the Zanzibar Govt.; joint edr., Zanzibar Law Repts., 1888-1918; astt.-gen., S. Stlitms., 1919; ag. col. sec., June, 1920 to May, 1921; ch. just., S. Stlitms., July, 1925; ret., 1933; commr. to Palestine to enquire into Jewish-Arab riots, 1934; edited the Revised Laws of the S. Stlitms., 1926; author of "Swahili Translations," 1911.

MURPHY, JOHN PELLY.—B. 1909; ed. Mount St. Mary's Coll., Chesterfield, B.A., LL.B., (T.C.D.); solr., high ct. of just., Ireland, 1932; junr. legal offr., Hong Kong, Apr., 1936; ag. dep. regtr., sup. ct., Apr., 1936; ag. ast. land offr., Dec., 1936; ag. dep. regtr., sup. ct., Feb., 1937.
MURPHY, JOSEPH.—B. 1892; ed. Stokane Sohl., Sligo; R.I.C., 1913-23; Jamaica constab., Apr., 1922; sub-inspr., Apr., 1926; 3rd cls. inspr., Apr., 1931; King's pol. med., 1926.

MURRAY, HON. SIR GEORGE JOHN ROBERT, K.C.M.G. (1917), B.A., LL.M.—B. 1863; judge of sup. ct., S. Australia, 1912; chief just., Jan., 1916 and lieut.-gov., Apr., 1916.

MURRAY, SIR J. H. P., K.C.M.G. (1925), C.M.G. (1914).—M.A., Oxon.; ed. at Sydney gram. schl. and won Knox prize, Fairfax prize, senr. univ. prize, and John West medal; went to Brighton Coll., Germany, and Univ. Coll., London; entd. Oxford Univ. and gained foundation scholarship to Magdalen Coll., 1890; 1st cls. class. moda., 1892; 1st cls. Lit. Hum., 1894; M.A., 1885; called to the bar, Inner Temple, 1886; practised as a barrister in Australia until apptd. crown prosecutor, N.S. Wales; acted as dist. ct. judge on several occasions; commanded N.S.W. Irish Rifles, 1898; served in S. African war, 1900-1901; reed. comm. of major in Impr. army in recognition of his services, 1902; chief judicial offr. and mem. of exec. and legis. couns., Papua, Sept., 1904; ag. administrator, 8th Apr., 1907; and lieut.-gov., in addition to duties of judge, 1908.

MURRAY, JOHN MURRAY.—B. 1888; ed. Victoria Coll., Univ. of Cape of Good Hope, and Worcester Coll., Oxford; B.A., LL.B. (Cape of Good Hope), B.A. (Oxon.); advoc., sup. ct. of S. Africa, 1913; K.C., 1932; judge sup. ct. (Transvaal Prov. div.), 1937.

NANKIVELL, HOWARD.—B. 1893; ed. Christ's Hosp., clk., col. secretariat, Jamaica, 1911; asst., 1912; 2nd cls. air mech., R.F.C., 1916; 2nd lieut., R.F.C., 1916; lieut., tech. I. confee., 1926; attd. for temp. duty, C.O., 1926; sec., salaries comn., Jamaica, 1928; ag. olk., privy coun., 1928; asst. col. sec., Trinidad, Apr., 1929; ag. col. sec., on various occasions, 1929-37; dep. col. sec., Jan., 1930; sec., intercol. confee. on trade relations with Canada, 1930; sec., Trinidad sir bd., 1930; ag. gov.'s dep., Jan., 1932 and Aug., 1934; chmn., unemployment bd., 1933; do., nutrition comn., 1936; sec. for lab., Sept., 1937; treas., Cyprus, 1938.
NAPIER, HON. JOHN MELLIS, LL.B.—B. 1882; ed. City of London Schl. and Adelaide Univ.; admitted a practitioner of sup. ct., 1903; K.C., 1922; judge sup. ct. of S. Australia since Feb., 1924.
NAPIER, SIR WALTER JOHN, KT. BACH. (1909), D.C.L. (Oxon.).—Barrister-at-law, Lincoln's Inn; atty.-gen., S. Sttlnts., 1907-1909; mem., W. African lands comn., 1912.

NASH, HERBERT FREDERICK WILLIAM.—B. 1901; entered C.O., 26th Nov., 1917; apptd., after compet. exam., asst. clk. and assigned to C.O., 29th Dec., 1919; cler. offr., 1st Jan., 1920; higher cler. offr., 17th Sept., 1929.

NASH, HON. WALTER.—B. 1882; ed. St. John's Schl.; mem., N.Z. H. of R. since 1929; min. of finance, min. of cust., min. of stamp duties, 1935, also min. of marketing, 1936; mem., national indus. confee., 1927; has represented N.Z. at various confees. overseas, including 2nd. internatl. lab. confee., Geneva, 1920, confees. of inste. of Pacific relations, 1927 and 1933, confee. of Br. commonwealth relations, 1933, and Imp. Confee., 1937; conducted trade mission overseas, 1936-7.

NASON, WILFRED PAUL.—B. 1894; ed. Christ's Hosp.; served with forces, France and Russia, 1915-19; asst. audr., Tanganyika Territory; do., Nigeria, 1928; audr., Leeward Is., 1932; audr., Trinidad, 1935; do., Zanzibar, 1938.

NATHAN, LT.-COL. SIR MATTHEW, G.C.M.G. (1908), K.C.M.G. (1902), C.M.G. (1899).—B. 1882; entd. R.E., 1880; capt., 1889; maj., 1898; lt.-col., 1907; served in Nile expedit., 1885; Lushai expedit. (medal with clasp), 1889; sec. col. def. comntee., 1895; adminstrd. govt. of S. Leone in 1899; gov., Gold Coast, Oct., 1900; gov., Hong Kong, 21st Sept., 1903; gov., Natal, 1st May, 1907; sec. to G.P.O., London, 1910; chrmn. of bd. of inland revenue, 1911; under sec. to lord-lieut. of Ireland, 1914; permanent sec. to ministry of pensions, 1916; gov., Queensland, 1920-25.

NATTRASS, ROLAND MARSHALL, B.Sc. (Agr.), Lond., Ph.D., Lond., D.I.C., Dip. Agr., Wye.—B.1895; ed. Nottingham High Sch.; asst. to cotton specialist, Tanganyika Territory, 1922; advisory mycologist, Western Prov.; Bristol Univ., Long Ashton Research Stn., 1924; senr. mycologist, miny. of agr., Egypt, 1928; mycologist, (now plant pathologist), Cyprus, 1931; ag. entomologist, June-Oct., 1937; snr. plant pathologist, Kenya, 1938.

NATTRISS, ERNEST ALFRED, O.B.E. (1934).—B.1882; ent. C.A. shipping agents, 1898; apptd. to C.A. office, 1912; dep. head, shipping dept., 1922; apptd. shipping offr. to the "Discovery Committee," 1927; head, shipping dept., 1933; do., gen. store dept., 1935.

NAVARATNAM, SARAVANAMUTTU SAMUEL, B.Sc. (Lond.).—B. 1894; demstr., govt. tech. schls., Ceylon, July, 1917; cadet, Dec., 1921; attd., Matara kach., Dec., 1921; ditto, Ratnapura kach., May, 1922; extra office ast. to govt. agt., Prov. of Sabaragamuwa, Sept., 1923; pol. mg., Matale, May, 1924; ditto, Chilaw, Feb., 1926; ast. comsrr., excise, Aug., 1927; dist. judge, Kegalle, Mar., 1930; ast. attnt. offr., July, 1931; sec. to min. of lab., industry and commerce, Oct., 1931; dep. contr., lab., Aug., 1933; ast. land comsrr., Dec., 1933; ast. settlmt. offr., June, 1934.
NEALE, CHARLES NORMAN.—Commsr. for road transp. and tramways, N.S.W., Aug., 1937.
NKAME, ARTHUR ROBERT LUNGLBY.—B.1892; B.S.A. pol., 1912-19; ast. asst. Tanganyika Territory pol., 1920; supt., 1928; ag. dep. comsrr., 1933-34.

NEAVE, JOHN RICHARD, M.C.—B. 1893; ed. King's Coll., Lond.; cadet, S.S., Oct., 1919; attd., res. office, Malacca, Nov., 1919; ast. advr., Kota Tinggi, Feb., 1925; res., Labuan, Dec., 1927; offr., cls. III, Jan., 1930; 2nd ast. sec. NEILL, J. H.—Med. offr., E. Africa Prot., Mar., 1919; ditto, Uganda, July, 1920.

Kedah, Aug., 1932; senr. ast. supt., surveys, Malaya, 1935; ch. survr., S.S. and F.M.S., 1938.

NEILL, JAMES SCOTT, B.A., Barrister-at-Law.—B. 1889; ed. Trinity Coll., Dublin; clas. schol., 1911; 1st sen. mod. hist. and pol. sci., 1912; Victoria prizeman, Kings Inns., Dublin; cadet, Fiji, 1914; clk., leg. and exec. coun., Oct., 1915; dist. comsrr., 1916; regtr., sup. st. and off. recr., 1922; as. atty.-gen. and mem. exec. and leg. coun., July, 1922 to July, 1923; ag. prin. ast. col. sec. and agt.-gen., immigr., Jan., 1924 and Sept., 1924; mem., bd. examna., Fijian language and ag. editor, Indian newspaper "Radjut"; seconded sp. duty under Tongan govt. and apptd. mem., privy coun. of Tonga, Aug., Sept., 1924; comsrr. of assize, circuit ct., June, 1925; ct. of review, income tax, Nov., 1925; Br. agt. and cons.-gen., Tonga, Jan., 1927; he also a dep. comsrr. for W. Pacific and a judi. comsrr. for matrimonial causes; seconded for sp. duty, Ocean Is., Dec., 1930 to Mar., 1931; special duty in Pitcairn Island, May and June, 1937; admstr., Dominica, 1937.

NEILSON, HARRY ROSS, M.B., Ch.B., D.P.H.—B. 1879; ed. Bedford Med. Schl. and Aberdeen Univ.; med. offr., Uganda, 1912; temp. capt., Uganda med. staff, 1914; health offr., 1921; senr. do., 1924; ag. D.D.S.S. for various periods, 1926-32.

NELSON, MAJOR JOHN BARRETT, M.O. and 2 Bars, M.A. (Oxon.), M.A. (Glas.).—B. 1896; ast. inspr., schls., Perak, July, 1921; ast. mast., govt. NESBITT, ROBERT HENRY, J.P., F.C.I.S. (Eng.)—B. 1883; ed. London, Eng.; Australian trade comsrr. to N. Z., 1934-37; chmn., milk bd. of N.S.W., 1937.

NESER, FRANCOIS, B.A.—B. 1895; 3rd grade clk., Lands, Pretoria, 1914; 2nd grade clk., 1916; 1st grade clk., 1919; senr. clk., 1924; prin. clk., 1931; pub. serv. inspr., grade II, 1934; under sec. for lands, 1937.

NEVIN, HENRY MILLER, M.A., M.B., B.Ch., B.A.O. (Univ. of Dub.), D.T.M., D.T.H. (L'pool), D.P.H. (Univ. of Dub.).—B. 1896; ed. NEWBOLD, CHARLES DEMORRE, B.A. (Oxon.).—B. 1909; called to bar, Gray's Inn, 1931; ed. Lodge Schl., Barbados and Keble Coll., Oxford; prin. offr. and 2nd dep. marshal, Trinidad, Jan., 1936; relieving mag., 1937.

NEWBOLT, CAPT. A. F.—B. 1893; ed. NEWBOLT, ALEXANDER THEODORE, M.O.—B. 1896; ed. Kingswood and Exeter Coll., Oxford; cadet, F.M.S., Nov., 1920; attd. NEWILL, COMMANDER JOSEPH BERNARD, D.S.O., R.N. (ret.).—B. 1887; harbrmr., Malacca, 1922; dep. harbrmr., Hong Kong, 1926.

NEWMAN, WILLIAM AUGUSTIN, M.B.E.—B. 1873; ed. Sydney High Schl.; ent. New South Wales pub. serv., 1891; served with Australian forces, S. C. of A., and sec. to fedl. exec. coun., 1913; sec. royal commr. on navy and defence admnstr., 1916; ch. clk., Australian defence dept., 1918; admnstr., Island of Nauru, 1927.

NEWSAM, NEVILLE.—B. 1898; ed. Harrison, Coll., Barbados; 2nd ast. analyst, Br. Guiana, 1922; 1st ast. analyst, 1933; ag. govt. analyst, 1936.
NEWTON, ALEXANDER STAFFORD, L.D.S. (Liv.), R.A.M.C.—B. 1896; mil'y. serv., 1914-19; dental surg., Tanganyika Territory, Sept., 1926.

NEWTON, SIR FRANCIS JAMES, K.C.M.G. (1919), C.M.G. (1892), C.V.O. (1911).—B. 1887; ed. at Rugby and Univ. Coll., Oxon.; B.A., 1890; barrister, Inner Temple; lieut., 3rd batt. Honduras, 1896; ditto, Barbados, 1901; treas., S. Rhodesia, 1903; ret., 1919; col. sec., 1st S. Rhodesian ministry, 1st Oct., 1923; high commnr. for S. Rhodesia in London; ret., 1930.

NEWTON ISAAC, F.R.C.S., (Edin.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.), M.R.C.S. (Eng.).—B. 1897; olin. ast., Charing Cross hosp., 1921; 2nd health offr. at port, Hong Kong, 1925; visiting med. offr. Kwong Wah hosp., 1926; med. offr. in ch. Kowloon hosp., 1927; M.O.-in-ch., govt., civ. hosp., 1930; in ch., Univ. surg. clinic in adln., 1932; med. offr. in ch., Victoria hosp., 1932; med. offr. in ch., govt. civ. mental and Tsan-Yuk hosps., 1936; do., Univ. surgical clinic, Mar., 1936; M.O.-in-ch., Queen Mary Hosp., May, 1937.

NGATA, HON. SIR APIRANA TURUPA, KT. BACH. (1927), M.A., LL.B.—B. 1874; ed. Waimatatini Native Schl., Te Aute Coll., and Canterbury Univ. Coll.; mem., house of reps., N.Z., since 1905; mem., exec. coun., repg. Maori race, 1909-12; min., native affirs., min., Cook Is., and mem., exec. coun. representing Maori race, 1928-34.
NICHOLAS, HON. HAROLD SPRENT.—B. 1877; M.L.C., N.S. Wales, 1932; judge, sup. ct., Feb., 1936.

NICHOLLS, HON. SIR HERBERT, K.O.M.G. (1927).—B. 1868; LL.B., Tasmania; called to the bar, 1892; atty.-gen. and min. of educn., Tasmania, 1903-4; puisne judge, Tasmania, Jan., 1909; chief justice, 1914; also lieut.-gov.; resgd., 1937.

NICHOLS, JAMES EDWARD.—B. 1882; ast. engr., Lagos rly., Feb., 1907; ast. engr., Gold Coast rlys. (open lines), May, 1911; offr. in charge, Togoland Milly. rlys., 1916-17; ch. engr., G. Coast rlys., Jan., 1920.

NIIOOL, ANDREW, A.M.Inst.C.E., A.M.I. Struct.E.—B. 1898; ed. Aberdeen Gram. Schl. and Robert Gordon's Tech. Coll., Aberdeen; engr., P.W.D., Hong Kong, 1921; exec. engr., Port Works Office, 1938.

NICOLE, J.—War serv. 1914-19; ag. sub. instr., pol., B.
