W., Demerara and ohmn., canals, polder authority, July to December, 1934.

NICOLL, MAJOR FREDERICK ALAN BENSON, O.B.E., R. of O. (1921).—B. 1883; ed. St. Edward's Schl., Oxford; Imp. Yeo. S. African War, 1900-02; Natal mtd. pol., 1903-09; Natal native rebellion, 1906; dist. supt., pol., Uganda, 1910; R. Sussex Regt., 1914-20 (France, Belgium and Palestine, army of occupation, Germany, and plebiscite troops, Danzig); ment. in desp.; O.B.E. NICOLL, JOHN FEARNS.—B. 1899; ed. Carlisle Grammar Schl. and Pembroke Coll., Oxford; war serv., France, 1918; cadet, Br. N. Borneo civ. serv., Dec., 1920; ast. dist. offr., Dec., 1922; cadet, Tanganyika Territory, Sept., 1926; ast. dist. offr., 1928; seconded, sec., 1930-31, 1933-34, 1935 and 1936; ag. ast. ch. sec. and elk. of couns., 1935; seconded for duty as ast. prin., C.O., 29th Apr., 1937; seconded as ag. dep. col. sec., Trinidad, Oct., 1937; gov'ts. dep., Apr.-May, 1938; ag. col. sec., Feb.-June, 1938; dep. col. sec., Sept., 1938; ag. col. sec., Nov., 1938.

NIGHTINGALE, PERCY HERBERT, B.A.—B. 1907; ed. Monkton Combe and Christ's Coll., Cambridge; cadet, Fiji, 1930; clk. to couns., 1932; prvte. sec. to O.A.G., 1933; dist. commr., 1933; asst. sec., secret., 1936-38; re-designated dist. offr., 1938.

NIHILL, J. H. B., K.C., M.A., M.C.—B. 1892; ed. Felsted and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; 2nd cl. hist. tripus, pts. I and II; pres., Union Soc'y, 1914; barrister-at-law, Inner Temple; 2nd lieut., Munster Fusiliers, 1914-18; investigating offr., Min. of Lab., joint industrial couns. divn., 1919; priv. sec. to Sir Wm. NIXON, FRANK ASHLEY.—B. 1879; Transvaal civ. serv., 1902; Union civ. serv., 1910; ch. clk., dept. of mines and industries, 1919; under-sec., dept. of mines and industries, 1924; chmn., mining leases bd., 1934.

NIXON, R., M.B., Ch.B. (L'pool), D.T.M. (L'pool), D.P.H. (L'pool).—Tempy. lieut., capt. and major, R.A.M.C. (S.R.), 1917-19; med. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Oct., 1921; senr. sanitation offr., 1928; asst.-dir., med. services, 1936.
NOAD, FREDERICK MITCHELL.—B. 1895; ed. prvte. (Ascham Coll.) Berkhamsted and St. Lawrence; W.A.F.F., 1915-20; admtrv. offr., cls. III, Nigeria, 1930; do., cls. II, 1936; do., cls. I, 1937.

NOAD, JOHN ELLIOTT MONCRIEFF, O.B.E.—Ed. R. Naval Coll., Osborne and Dartmouth and Training Cruiser, H.M.S. "Cornwall"; midshipman, R.N., Aug., 1910; H.M.S. NOALL, W. E.—B.1880; apptd., after compet. exam., clk. of the 2nd div. of the civil ser., and assigned to the C.O., June, 1898; promoted, with gratuity, Jan., 1911; conf. clk., 1st Mar., 1916; minor staff clk., 8th Sept., 1917; sec. to visual instr. comttee, 1st Jan., 1918; sec., "Discovery" comttee, 3rd Mar., 1937.

NOBBS, CAPT. HOWARD.—B. 1891; ed. University Coll., and Inst. of Educn., Lond.; B.Sc. (Lond.), 1913 (hons. in chem.); M.Sc. (Lond.), 1919; A.I.C. (1918); F.I.C. (1923); 2nd lieut., Northamptonshire Regt., Apr., 1914; lieut., 1915, capt., 1916; war serv. in France, 1st battn., Northamptonshire Regt. 1914-16 and with R.E., 1916-18; chem. mast., R. NOBLE, G. S. P., M.B., Ch.B. (Glas.), Certif. Lond. S.H.T.M. (distinc.).—B. 1897; mil. serv., 1916-18; med. offr., Tanganyika Territory, 1925.

NOBLE, CAPT. JAMES MORTON, A.M.I.C.E., Chtd. Civ. Engrn.—B. 1890; asst. engrnr., F.M.S., Nov., 1919; do., Kedah, Jan., 1920-Feb., 1924; exec. engrnr., F.M.S., Nov., 1928; ag. senr. exec. engrnr., various occasions, 1929-31 and 1934; senr. exec. engrnr., July, 1935; ag. state engrnr., Pahang, Jan., 1938.

GORDON.—B. 1880; entered army (Royal Welsh Fus.), 1898; served in Boer war, 1899-00 (Queen's medal with clasp); Tugela heights, relief of Ladysmith, Cape Colony, Orange Free State and Transvaal; seconded for serv., N. Force, 1914; lieut., 1916 served on Caro expdn., B. Honduras, 1916; instructor to 2nd B. Honduras contingent, 1916; capt., 1918; supt. of prisons, Grenada, Jan., 1920.

NORMAN, ROSS JUNNER, M.C., D.Sc., D.I.C., F.G.S., M.I.M.M.—Ed. Melbourne Univ. and R. Coll. of Sci.; war serv., France and Italy, 1914-18; mem., coun. of inst. of Mining and Metallurgy, London, asst. geologist, and geologist Gold Coast geol. survey, 1920; dir., Sierra Leone geol. survey and ch. inspr., mines, 1927; dir., Gold Coast geol. survey, Oct., 1930.
NORRIS, J. A., C.M.G.(1936)—B. 1872; ed. Ballygunny and Williamstown, Ireland; acct. & treasry., Victoria, Mar., 1914; audr.-gen., Mar., 1919.

NORRIS, JOHN WILSON A.C.G.L., M.Inst.C.E., A.M.I.Mech.E.—B. 1890; ed. Lond. Univ. Imp. Coll. Sc. and Technology; active service B.E.F. (France) 1914-19, major R.R., mentioned in despatches; joined C.A. as engr. inspr., 1919; asst. engr., 1920; visited Gold Coast 1924; services loaned to consult. engr. reporting on Takoradi Harbour; seconded for duty as asst. engr. Gold Coast harbours staff, Takoradi 1924; rejoined C.A. 1926; dep. to ch. insp. engr., 1929.

NORRIS, VICTOR HUGH, L.M.S.—B. 1893; ed. Raftes Inst. & Coll. of Medicine, S'pore; asst. surgn., S'pore, Sept., 1915; ag. med. offr., various occasions, 1917-24; asst. med. offr., Sept., 1925; dep. med. offr., Oct., 1926; med. offr., S.S., Dec., 1930; do., P. Wellesley, June, 1933.

NORTH, ROLAND ARTHUR CHARLES, B.A. (Oxon.).—B. 1889; ed. Blundell's and Balliol Coll., Oxford; cadet, Hong Kong, Nov., 1913; seconded for serv. with Samoa govt., 1921; studied Japanese in Japan, 1922; 2nd asst. sec. for Chinese affrs., 1923; ch. asst. to sec. for Chinese affrs., 1923 and 1930; ag. sec. for Chinese affrs. 1927, 1928, 1929 and 1931; extra aast. col. sec., Feb., 1931; 2nd aast. col. sec. and dep. clk., coun., June, 1931 and Jan., 1934; prin. ast. col. sec. and clk., couns., in 1933, 1934 and 1935; 1st cls. cadet offr., Apr., 1935; ag. col. sec., Mar.-Nov., 1936; O.A.G., Aug.-Sept., 1936; sec., Chinese affrs., Oct., 1936; ag. col. sec., Apr.-Oct., 1937 and in Feb., 1938.

NORTHCOTE, SIR GEOFFREY ALEXANDER STAFFORD, K.C.M.G. (1935), C.M.G. (1931).—Ed. at Blundells Schol. (Newte and Huish exhibns.) and Balliol Coll., Oxford M.A. (hons.), ast. collir., E. Africa Prot., May, 1904 dist. comnrs., 1909; ast. col. sec., 1921; ch. sec., N. Rhodesia, 1927; col. sec., Gold Coast, 1930; gov., and commdr-in-ch., Br. Guiana, 1934; do., Hong Kong, 1937.

NORTHCOTE, MAJOR LEONARD AUGUSTUS STAFFORD.—B. 1879; ed. Dulwich Coll.; 2nd lient, Royal Fusiliers, 1900; res. of offrs., Feb., 1914; served in Gallipoli, Egypt, Salonika and Palestine, 1914-19; local commdt. of pol. and ast. comnrs., Cyprus, 1919 (title changed to supt., pol., Mar., 1938); passed exam. in mod. Greek; ag. comnrs., for various periods 1924-27 and 1929-34; ag. dep. ch. commdt., pol., in 1936 and 1937.

NORTHCOTE, R. C.—Ed. Blundells and S. African Schol. of Mines and Technology, Johannesburg; Rhodesian admtn., B.S.A. pol., 1914-16; Rhodesian Native Regt., 1917-19; German E. Africa campaign (ment. in desp., mily. med.); ast. polit. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Feb., 1921; dist. offr., Feb., 1932.

NORTHCROFT, HON. ERIMA HARVEY, D.S.O. (1919), V.D.—B. 1884; ed. Hokitika dist. high schl., Well. Coll., and Auckland univ. coll., N.Z.; joined N.Z. pub. trust offr. as cadet for short period; admitted to bar, 1907; served overseas, 1916-19 with N.Z.F.A., with rank of lt.-col.; dep. judge-advca.-gen., 1927-33; judge-advoc.-gen. with rank of col., 1933-35; judge, sup. ct. of N.Z., 1935.

NORTHCROFT, LESLIE ARTHUR, B.A. (Can-tab).—B. 1901; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Dec., 1923; attd. to secretariat, Jan., 1924; ditto, Putalam kach., Jan., 1925; ditto, N’Eliya kach., July, 1925; office ast., Jaffna kach., May, 1926; temp. attd., C.O. Sept., 1928 to Mar., 1929; addnl. ast. col. treas., Apr., 1929; ag. ast. gov’t. agt., Matare, Oct., 1929; ast. stintl. offr., Nov., 1929; ast. ch. sec., Apr., 1935.

NORTHBY, MAJOR-GENERAL SIR EDWARD; G.C.M.G. (1922), K.C.M.G. (1918), C.B.—B. 1868; served with Hazara expdn., 1891; Miranzai expdn., 1891; Isazai expdn., 1892, in S. African war, 1899-02; and in the war of 1914-18; extra A.D.C. to H.M. the King, 1915; brig.-gen. (temp.) commdg. Nyasaland Rhodesia field force, 1918-19; lieut.-col. (brevet col.), 1915; King’s R.R.C., 1911; gov. E. Africa Prot. and high comnrs., Zanzibar Prot., July, 1918; assumed govt., Jan., 1919; ret., Aug., 1922.

NORTH-HUNT, HAROLD.—B. 1894; ed. Ipewich Schol. and St. Catharine’s Coll., Cambridge (exhib.); war serv., 1914-20; (ment. in desps.); cadet, F.M.S., Mar., 1920; 2nd ast. dist. offr., July, 1920; ast. dist. offr., Jan., 1921; 2nd ast. adviser, Apr., 1922; offr., cls. IV, Aug., 1925; offr., cls. III, Aug., 1930; ag. ast. advr., supt., lands and regis., titles, Kelantan, Aug., 1933; offr. cls. II., Aug., 1935; regis.-gen., statistics, S.S. and F.M.S., Aug., 1935; supt., rubber control S’pore and Penang and regis., textile quotas, S.S. and F.M.S. in addn., 1937.
NORTHMORE, HON. SIR JOHN ALFRED, K.C.M.G. (1932), LL.B.—B. 1865; judge, sup. ot. W. Australia, 1914; ch. just., 1931; lieut-gov., 1932-33.

NORTHROP, ALBERT WILLIAM.—B. 1890; Imp. cust. and excise serv., 1912-23; supervr., cust., Tanganyika Territory, Dec., 1923; senr. collr., cust., N. Rhodesia, Apr., 1929; dep. contr., cust., 1931; dep. commr., cust., Kenya and Uganda, Oct., 1936; commr., do., 1938.

NORTON, CUTHBERT HUGH, M.B.E. (1938).—B. 1878; ed. pte. schls. and Weymouth Coll.; ent. govt. ser., Br. Guiana, 1913; ast. comnry., taxation, 1914; 3rd gde., do., 1917; 2nd gde., do., 1920; 1st gde. do., 1927; seconded as census comnrs., Sept., 1930 to Dec., 1931; 1st gde. offr., dist. adminstr., 1932; ch. comnry. and dist. commnrs., Georgetown and East Bank, Demerara dist., Jan., 1934; poor law comnrs., Jan., 1936; chmn., poor law comnrs., July, 1936; dist. comnrs., W. Demerara and chmn., canals polder authority, 1936.

NORTON, HENRY JOHN STEPHEN, O.B.E. (1938), M.B.E. (1934).—B. 1896; cler. ast., Gibraltar, 1913; crane and wharf clk., 1914; treas. clk., 1914; passed compet. exam., 1917; clk.-in-charge, impts. and expts., and clk. to food contr., 1914 to 1920; dep. ast. shipping master, 1921; ast. shipping master, 1922; sec. and treas., sailors’ home, 1920; clk.-in-charge port dept. and ag. Trinity agt., in 1924-26; ast. sec., col. sec.’s office, 1927; pvte. sec. and interpreter to gov., 1929.

NORVAL, WILLIAM DIXON.—B. 1893; ed. Wepener, O.F.S.; clk., dept. of lands, 1913; clk., dept. of educn. (Union), 1919; pvte. sec. to min. of interior, educn. and pub. health, 1924; senr. clerk, dept. of agr., 1926; mining comnrs., Namaqualand, 1929; do., Barberton, 1930; do., Barberton and Pilgrims Rest, 1931; under-sec. for lab., 1932.
NORWOOD, LEONARD ROBERT OSWALD.—B. 1896; ed. Lincoln Coll., Oxford; supt., educn. dept., N. Prova., Nigeria, 2nd Nov., 1921.

NOSWORTHY, HON. SIR WILLIAM, K.C.M.G. (1929).—B. 1867; ed. privately and at Boys High Schol., Christchurch, N.Z.; mem., N.Z. parlmt., 1908-28; jun. whip for reform party and later sen. whip; sen. whip for Nat. govt.; min. of agr., 1919-26; min. of immigrn., 1920-28; min. of finance and stamp duties, 1925-26; P.M.G. and min. of tels., 1926; min. of external affrs., 1926-28.

NOTT, CHARLES ROBERT HARLEY, M.A.—B. 1904; ed. Marlborough and Christ’s Coll., Camb.; cadet, Fiji and W. Pacific services, Mar., 1926; col. sec.’s office, Fiji, Apr., 1926; passed cadet, Mar., 1928; clk., exec. and leg. couns., Mar., 1928; ag. A.D.C. and priv. sec. to gov., 1930; ch. clk., col. sec.’s office, Jan., 1931; ag. 2nd ast. col. sec., for various periods, 1932-35; ag. prin. ast. col. sec., 1936; ag. chmn., mining bd., 1936; dist. comnrs., 1937; ast. sec., secretariat, 1938.
NOTT, MELVILLE CHARLES.—B. 1882; under sec. and perm. head, dept. of atty.-gen. and just., N.S.W., 1930.

NOTTINGHAM, CAPT. ERIC CARO, M.C. 1918.—B. 1891; ed. Kingston Schol.; Great War, 1914-1919, Egypt, Gallipoli, France, Flanders, Rhine Army of Occupation; wounded; ment. in desps., 1918; N. Nigeria pol., 1919; Nigeria pol., 1930; ag. ast. inspr.-gen., various periods, 1932-35; ast. comnrs., 1936; ag. dep. comnrs., various periods, 1936-38; comnrs., pol., Gold Coast, 1938.

NUNN, GEOFFREY NOEL NAPIER.—Ed. Bradfield and Magdalene Coll., Camb.; B.A. (hons.). cadet, Gold Coast, July, 1929; asst. dist. comsnr., 1930; seconded, Gambia, Dec., 1931; ag. comsnr., 1932; 2nd lieut., R.A.R.O., July, 1929.

NUNN, MAJOR REGINALD LEWIS, D.S.O., M.Inst.C.E., M.Inst.W.E.—B.1892; ed. Brighton Coll.; waterwks. engr., Trinidad and Tobago, Apr., 1921; res. engr., Br. Guiana, Nov., 1924; asst. dir., P.W.D., Nigeria, Feb., 1927; dep. col. engr., P.W.D., S.S., Aug., 1930; dep. D.P.W., S.S., Jan., 1932; seconded for aerodrome planning, July-Nov., 1934; offi. mem., exec. coun., S.S., June, 1935; dir. civ. aviation, Malaya, Sept., 1935; D.P.W., S.S. and advr., pub. wks., Malay States, Nov., 1935.

NUROCK, MAX, O.B.E. (1932), M.A. (Dublin)—B. 1893; mily. serv., 1915-19; asst. pvt. sec., Palestine, 1920; junr. asst. sec., 1922; asst. sec., 1926; sec. to standing comte., for commerce and industry, 1928; ag. asst. ch. sec. on various occasions and once as ch. sec.; clk., exec. coun. in addn., 1934; asst. ch. sec., Uganda, 1937; ag. dep. ch. sec., July-Dec., 1937 and Mar.-Nov., 1938.

NURSE, SYDNEY HARRINGTON—B. 1889; ed. Harrison Coll.; ent. civ. serv., Barbados, 1905; clk., pol. mag.'s ct., dist. "D," 1919; ag. pol. mag., various dists., on several occasions 1922-36; pol. mag., dist. "E," July, 1936.

NUTMAN, FREDERICK JOHN, A.R.C.S., M.Sc., D.I.C.—B. 1904; ed. Newton Abbot and R. Coll. of Sci., Lond.; timber mycologist, forest products research lab., Oxford, 1926; plant physiologist, E. African agr. research stn., Amani, Tanganyika Territory, 1928.

OAKES, ALFRED CHARLES GEORGE.—B. 1878; ed. privately; clk., surv.-gen'l. office, Cape Town, 1897; native affrs. dept., 1908; on active serv., 1917-18; mag., Taunye, Bechuanaland 1919; Sutherland, 1923; Willowmore, 1927; Craddock, 1929; Bethal, 1932; senr. addl. mag., Durban, 1932; mag., Potchefstroom, 1936.

OAKES, ARTHUR PODMORE, M.B.E. (1936), M.M.—B. 1892; Europ. War, 1914-18; N. Staffordshire Regt., 1912; Northamptonshire Regt., 1917-28; K.A.R., Nyassaland and Somaliland, 1921-26; pay and qtrmr., Somaliland pol., 1926; dep. oomdt., pol. and dir., prisons, 1928; ag. comsnr., pol. and dir., prisons, various occasions, 1928-1938.

OAKESHOTT, BRIAN FRANCIS—B. 1900; ed. Berkhamsted Sch., St. George's Sch., Harpenden; pol. prob., S.S., Aug., 1919; asst. supt., July, 1923; i/o gambling suppression br., S'pore, July, 1927; C. of P., Trengganu, Sept., 1934.

OAKLEY, PHILIP DOUGLAS, C.B.E. (1936), M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.), D.T.M. (L'pool).—B. 1883; med. offr., W.A.M.S., Gold Coast, Jan., 1911; senr. med. offr., July, 1926; asst. dir., med. serv., Aug., 1928; dep. dir., Feb., 1932; dir. med. and any. services, Sierra Leone, 1933.

O'BRIEN, ARTHUR JOHN RUSHTON, C.M.G. (1929), M.C., M.B., Ch.B. (Edin.), D.P.H., B.Hy. (hons.), (Durham), Certif. L.S.T.M. — B.1883; joined W.A.M.S., 1911; served in Great War, 1915-19; awarded M.C. and bar, twice ment. in despr., and recvd. thanks of S. of S. for services in Cameroons; surg. specialist, Gold Coast, 1921; R.M.O., Gold Coast hospital, 1923; sec. to C.A.M.S. comtee., Dec., 1929; visited West Indies at request of S. of S., 1930; assumed duty assn., C.A.M.S., comtee., June, 1930; asst. med. advr., C.O., 1931; ch. med. advr., do., 1938.

O'BRIEN, BRYAN JUSTIN.—B. 1902; ed. Uppingham and Queen's Coll., Oxford; 1st cl. hona. mods.; 2nd cl. lit. hum.; asst. sec., col. sec's. office, Cyprus, Apr., 1927, ag. clk., exec. coun., for various periods, 1927-31; clk., exec. coun., Aug., 1931; ag. ch. asst. sec. for various periods, 1927-36; ag. asst. comsnr., Jan. to Mar., 1928; ag. col. sec., Dec., 1933; admstve offr., asst. sec. Jan., 1935; comsnr., July, 1936; joint edr., Handbook of Cyprus, 1930 edn.

O'BRIEN, JOHN.—B. 1895; ed. John Lyon's Harrow; Artists' Rifles, 1914; 2nd lieut. Duke of Cornwall's Light Inf'y., France, Salonika, 1915-18; seconded 2nd Bn., K.A.R., 1918-20; admstve offr., Nyassaland, 1920; dep. provl. commr., Zanzibar, 1937.

O'CONNELL, BASIL MORGAN BENEDICT, Lieut. R.N. (Ret.), R.N.C.—B. 1900; ed. Corpus Christi Coll., Cambridge; pol. probr., F.M.S., Dec., 1920; crim. regisy., S.F.S. & F.M.S., ag. asst. commr., pol., Jan., 1923; asst. commr. pol., F.M.S., May, 1925; adjt., fedl. headqrs., Sept., 1930; dep. commr., pol., F.M.S., Oct., 1935; ag. commr., pol. and aliens immigrn. offr., Kedah, Feb., 1936.

O'CONNOR, ALEXANDER JAMES, Dipl. Forestry (Capo.).—Cik., conserv. of forest's office, W. conservancy, 1903; forester, Uitvlugt, W. conservancy, 1905; asst. dist. forest offr., Tokai, 1908; seconded to gen. coun., Transkei, 1908; asst. dist. forest offr., Knysna, 1909; ditto, working plans, Midland conservancy, 1910; asst. dist. forest offr., Woodbush, Transvaal conservancy, 1910; dist. forest offr., Woodbush, 1918; ditto, Pietersburg, 1924; ag. conserv., foresta, Natal conservancy, 1926; 2nd grade conserv. of forests, Natal conservancy, 1927; prin. fores. offr. (forestry inspr.), Transvaal and O.F.S., 1932; ch. forest offr. (forestry inspr.), T'vaal and O.F.S., 1934; 1st grade conserv., foresta, T'vaal, 1935; dep. dir., forestry, 1936.

O'CONNOR, CHARLES ANTHONY.—B. 1883; ed. Royal Coll., Mauritius; supt., Reduit gardens and plantations, forests and gardens dept., Mauritius, Sept., 1904; ch. overseer, dept. of agr., Aug., 1913; enlisted, 2nd Artists' Rifles, Aug., 1917; reed. comsnr., Royal Munster Fus., Sept., 1918; inspr. of plantations, Zanzibar Port., 1919; agrl. instr. and inspr., co-op. credit societies, Mauritius, Jan., 1923.

O'CONNOR, Dermot Patrick Joseph, M.C., B.L.—B. 1891; ed. Portmouth Grammar Sch. and R. Mily. Coll., Sandhurst; mily. serv., 1910-12, 1914-18; ment. in despr.; Fiji constab., 1913; asst. dist. offr., Nigeria, 1920; dist. offr., 1928; admstive offr., cl. II, 1936; do., cl. I, 1937.

O'CONNOR, HUGH JOSEPH, F.Z.S.—B. 1887; Lieut. Gambia Artillery (V.), 1910; mem. of navigation and piloting board, Gambia, 1914; asst. comsnr. of police, G. Coast, Jan., 1916; ag. staff instructor, Mar., 1916, and June, 1918; ag. comsnr. of police for various periods in 1917, 1921 and 1922; O.C. Togoland mil. police, and O.C. Togoland prisons, 19th Aug., 1919; capt., G. Coast Rifles, 1922; comsnr. of police, Jan., 1923.

O'CONNOR, JOSEPH.—B. 1896; ed. St. Mary's Coll., Trinidad; clk., goods dept., Ry., Jan., 1914; clk., gen. man. office, July, 1914; 6th clk., col. secret., Jan., 1919; 6th ols. clk., do., Jan., 1920; 5th do., May, 1920; 4th do., Mar., 1922; 3rd do., Sept., 1925; 2nd do., Jan., 1928; 1st do., Jan., 1930; senr. clk., do., Jan., 1931; 2nd asst. col. sec., Mar., 1936; ag. asst. col. sec., Apr.-Sept., 1936 and May-Oct., 1937; ag. dep. col. sec., June, 1938.
O'CONNOR, MARTIN GEORGE, B.A. (Trin. Coll., Dub.).—B. 1899; ast. mast., educn. dept., Hong Kong, 1925.

O'CONNOR, MICHAEL PATRICK, M.D., Ch.B., B.A.O., Cert. L.S.T.M.—B. 1896; ed. St. Joseph's Coll., Ireland and Univ. Coll., Dublin; war serv., 1916-20; M.O., Malaya, Feb., 1927; demonstr. in pub. health, Coll. of Med., S'pore, Feb., 1935.
O'DONOGHUE, LAURENCE.—B. 1895; ed. Newcastle Schi., Co. Tipperary; R.I.C., 1917-22; staff sergt.-major, Jamaica constab., Feb., 1922; sub-instr., Feb., 1931; 3rd cls. instr., Nov., 1932.

OFARRELL, EDWARD JOSEPH.—Ed. Belvedere Coll. and Trinity Coll., Dublin; solr., supt., ot., Ireland, 1905; dep. regis., sup. ct., Kenya, 1924; dep. offr. recr. for Coast Prov. and ex-officio agt. at Mombasa for sub. trustee, Kenya, 1930; registr., sup. ot., and of E. Africa of ot. appl., 1938; ed., E. A. law repts., 1933.
OGG, ROBERT CUTHBERT, M.B., Ch.B.—B. 1901; rel. med. offr., Basutoland, 1927; med. offr., 1929.

OGILVIE, DUNCAN COLLINGWOOD, M.C., M.R.C.S. (Eng.), I.R.C.P. (Lond.), L.M.S.S.A. (Lond.).—B. 1892; ed. Bedford Schi. and Middlesex Hosp.; war serv., R.A.M.C., 1915-19; M.O., min. of pensions, Dec., 1919 to Oct., 1920; D.M.O., Fiji, Oct., 1920; resigned, Dec., 1924; reassigned, M.O., Leeward Is., Dec., 1931; D.M.O., Cyprus, Sept., 1936; D.M.O., Leeward Is., 1937.

O'GRADY, JAMES JOSEPH, M.C., M.B., B.Ch., B.A.O. (Dublin), D.M.R. & E. (Cantab.).—B. 1894; med. offr., gen. hosp., K. Lumpur, Mar., 1926; med. offr., Batu Gajah and i/c X-ray dept., Ipoh, Sept., 1931; med. offr., Ipoh and i/c X-ray dept., Mar., 1935; health offr., E. Pahang, 1937; do., Prov. Wellesley, 1938.

O'GRADY, WILLIAM MICHAEL, B.L.—B. 1885; ed. Wesley Coll., Dublin, admitted solr., Ireland, 1908; mily. serv., Egypt, 1916; Salonika, 1916-17; Palestine, 1917-18; Egypt, 1919; called to bar, Ireland, 1920; mag., Tanganyika Territory, 1920; ag. prison judge, high ct., for various periods, 1927-37.

OKE, HARRIS RENDELL, C.M.G. (1839), M.C.—B. 1891; ed. Bishop Feild Coll. St. John's, Newfoundland; stud.-at-law, 1909-14; The Royal Newfoundland Regt., 1914-15; The Royal Scots, 1915-20; served France, 1916-18 (M.C. and ment. in desps., 1918); served N. Russian Relief Force, 1919 (bar to M.C.); admstv. offr., Nigeria, 1920; transf'd, to admstv. serv., the Gambia, 1932; ag. col. sec., May-Aug. and Dec., 1933; ag. gov., Jan.-Apr., 1934; senr. asst. col. sec., Apr., 1934; col. sec., 1934; ag. gov., various periods, 1935-38.
OLDAKER, A. A.—B. 1901; ed. Lancing and Balliol Coll., Oxon., admstv. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Jan., 1924.

OLIPHANT, GEORGE MITCHELL.—B. 1905; ed. Morgan Acad., Dundee and Glasgow Univ.; admitted solr., Jan., 1926; ast. land offr., Tanganyika Territory, 1930; ast. admr.-gen. and offr. recr., N. Rhodesia, 1935; regtr., high ct., sheriff, regtr., companies, patents, trade marks and designs, regtr.-gen., births, deaths and marriages, 1936; admstv. gen. and offr. recr., 1937.

OLIPHANT, JOHN STANLEY, M.B., Ch.B. (Ed.), D.P.H., Cert. of L.S.T. Med.—Ed. George Watson's Coll., Edin. Acad. and Edin. Univ.; med. offr., Nigeria, 1927; med. offr. (health), Sierra Leone, 1933; senr., do., 1938.

OLIVER, Geo. A.—B. 1898; ent. G.P.O., Dec., 1914; apptd. asst. clk., Apr., 1916; wireless operator, R.N.V.R., Jan., 1917 to Apr., 1919; cler. offr., 1st Jan., 1920; transf'd. to C.O., June, 1923; accompanied U.K. delegation to Imp. Economic Conf., Ottawa, 1932.
OLIVER, WALTER REGINALD BROOK, D.Sc., F.R. Soc. N.Z.—B. 1883; ed. Tauranga High Schi. and Victoria Univ. Coll., N.Z.; ent. N.Z. cust. dept., 1900; Dom. museum staff, 1920; dir., 1928.

OLIVIER, 1ST BARON (created 1924), Rt. Hon. Sir SYDNEY OLIVIER, P.C. (1924), K.C.M.G. (1907), C.M.G. (1898), B.A.—B. 1859; ed. at Tonbridge schi.; open exhib., Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford; 2nd class classical mode, 1878; 2nd class in final classical schi., 1881; apptd., after compet. exam., clk. in the office of the S. of S. for cols., 17th Apr., 1882; honourably mentioned for Cobden prize essay, Oxford, 1883; ag. col. sec., Br. Honduras, Oct., 1890, to Apr., 1891; ag. auditor-gen., Leeward Is., Oct., 1896, to Feb., 1896; priv. sec. to the Earl of Selborne, Sept., 1896; sec. to the Roy. comm. to inquire into condition of W. Indies, Dec., 1896; 1st class clk., June, 1897; sent to Washington on sp. serv. in connect. with W. Indian reciprocity negotiations, 1898; col. sec., Jamaica, 1900-1904; ag. gov., 1900, 1902, and 1904; returned to C.O. as a prin. clk., Oct., 1904; gov. of Jamaica, Apr., 1907; assumed govt., May, 1907; represented West Indian Colonies at the Coronation of H.M. King George V., 1911; perm. sec., bd. of agriculture and fisheries, 1913; mem. of comtes. for settm. of ex-service men within the Empire, 1917; ast. compt. and audr., exchequer and audit dept., 1917; S. of S. for India, 23rd Jan. to 7th Nov., 1924.

O'MORCHOE, TH. (CAPT. ARTHUR DONEL MACMURROGH).—B. 1892; ed. St. Andrew's Coll. and Trinity Coll., Dublin; joined Leinster Regt. (Royal Meaths), Aug., 1914; served in the great war; regtl. signalling offr., 1915; ast. adjt., 1916-17; A.D.C. to maj. gen. Hon. E. J. Montague Stuart-Wortley and camp commdt., 65th divn., 1917-18; provost marshal's dept., 1918-22; junr. pol. offr., Zanzibar, Jan., 1924; pub. pros., 1924-25; supt., pol., Feb., 1926; ag. supt., C.I.D., Nov., 1925 to May, 1926; adjt., 1928; ag. ast. commdt., 1930; A.D.C. to Br. res., Aug., 1930; pvte. sec., May, 1931; comsnr., pol., Gold Coast, 1934.

ONRAET, RENE HENRY DE SOLMINIHAC.—B.1887; pol. prob., S. Stiltns., Dec., 1907; passed final exam. in Hokkien, July, 1910; passed final exam. in Malay, Aug., 1911; ast. supt. of pol., July, 1912; ag. supt. of pol., Singapore, Jan., 1919; ast supt. of pol., Singapore, Mar., 1919; supt., pol. and dir. crim. intell., S.S., Mar., 1922; ch. pol. offr., S'pore, Dec., 1925, and June 1931; dir., crim. intell., S.S., Oct., 1926; dep. comsnr., pol., Perak, F.M.S., Oct., 1931; ag. inspr. gen., pol., S.S., Mar., 1932; ch. pol. offr., Perak, Oct., 1933; inspr.-gen., pol., S.S., Oct., 1935.

ORLOFF, CEDRIC JAMES, B.A. (Lond.).—B. 1906; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Oct., 1930; attd., secretariat, Oct., 1930; attd., Kalutara kach., July, 1931; office asst., Hambantota kach., Oct., 1932; do., Nuwara Eliya kach., Apr., 1936; pol. mag., Puttalam, Nov., 1937.
O'REGAN, JOHN WILLIAM HAMILTON, B.A. (Oxon.).—B. 1913; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Oct., 1935; offr. cls. II, Oct., 1937; priv. sec. to gov., Mar. 1938.

O'REGAN, HON. PATRICK JOSEPH.—B. 1869; ed. Victoria Univ. Coll., N.Z.; mem., N.Z. H. of R., 1893-99; mem. Westport harbr. bd., 1893-1901; mem., Victoria Univ. Coll. coun., 1898-1900; admitted solic., 1905; chrmn., Wellington conciliation bd., 1906-08; pvt. legal practice until apptd. judge, N.Z. sup. ct. and ot. of arbitrn., 1937.

O'REILLY, HARRY ALLAN OSWALD.—B. 1893; called to bar, Lincoln's Inn, 1924 (cert. of hon.); dep. regisr. gen., Trinidad, 1934; atty.-gen. and registr., sup. ct., St. Vincent, 1936; atty.-gen., Leeward Is., 1938.

ORME, REGINALD FRANCIS PLATT, M.C.—B. 1896; ed. Eastbourne Coll.; Royal Sussex Regt., 1915-20; India, 1916-19; admstv. offr., cls. IV, Nigeria, 1921; cls. III, 1929; cls. II, 1936; ag. sec., N. Provs., Mar.-Apr., 1937 and June, 1938.

ORR, SIR CHARLES WILLIAM JAMES, K.C.M.G. (1928), C.M.G. (1921).—B. 1870; ed. Bath Coll. and Woolwich Acad.; 1st coman., Royal Artillery, Feb., 1889; capt., Sept., 1899; maj., 1904; ret., Sept., 1908, served with Chitral relief force, 1895 (medal with clasp); Indian frontier, 1897 (clasp); Tirah expdn. force, 1897-99 (clasp); China war, 1900-01 (medal); S. African war, 1900-02 (medal, 2 clasps); 3rd cls. res., N. Nigeria, Feb., 1903; 2nd cls. res., 1904; 1st cls. res., 1908; ag. sec. to adminstr., N. Nigeria, May to Sept., 1907; ch. sec., Cyprus, Feb., 1911; adminstr. govt., July to Oct., 1911, and Apr. to Aug., 1913; ret. on pension on account of ill health, Feb., 1917; attd. W.O., Int. branch, 1918-19; col. sec., Gibraltar, June, 1919; ag. gov., on several occasions; gov. and commdr.-in-ch. of the Bahamas, 1926-1931; ret.

ORR, LIONEL ALEXANDER WILLIAM.—B. 1905; ed. Avoca Schl., St. Andrew's Coll., and Trinity Coll., Dublin, B.A., LL.B.; solr., sup. ct., L.F.S.; asst. comanr., lands, Gold Coast, Aug., 1929 to Aug., 1932; asst. admtr.-gen., N. Rhodesia, Aug., 1932; ag. admtr.-gen., ag. offi. recvr., ag. registr., high ct., ag. registr. of companies, ag. registr. of patents, trade marks and designs, ag. registr. of deeds, Mar.-June, 1934; dep. regisr., high ct., Tanganyika Territory, Oct., 1934; registr., do., 1936; ch. registr., sup. ct., Palestine, Apr. 1938.

ORETT, WILLIAM ARTHUR.—B. 1892; sub-inspr., Jamaica constab., 1913; R.I.C. depot, 1914; army serv., R. Inniskilling Fusiliers, 1914-19; 3rd cls. inspr., Jamaica constab., 1918; 2nd cls. do., 1920; 1st cls. do., 1931; ag. dep. inspr. gen. in 1934 and 1937; dep. comanr., pol., Trinidad and Tobago, July, 1938.

OSBORNE, MAJOR D. R.—B. 1879; ed. Prince Alfred Coll., Adelaide, S. Australia, Adelaide Univ. (mental and moral phil.) and Christ's Coll., Cambridge; B.A., law; called to the bar, Inner Temple, 1909; practiced at bar until 1914; on mily. serv., 1914-19; et. martial offr., Nov., 1918 to May, 1920; in Ireland as A.L.O. until Apr., 1922; stip. and circuit mag., Bahamas, June, 1923; ag. ch. just., Aug. to Sept., 1923 and from July to Nov., 1924; ag. reg.-gen. in addn. June to July, 1924.

OSTLER, HON. SIR HENRY HUBERT (Kt. Bach, 1939), K.C. (1926), LL.B. (1904).—B. 1876; ed. Christ's Hosp. and Victoria Univ. Coll.; assoc. to New Zealand ch. just., 1903-06; edr., N.Z. Law Repts., 1906-10; 1st asst. crown law office and crown pros., 1910; ag. solr.-gen., 1913; in private practice, 1915-25; judge, N.Z. sup. ct., 1926; examr. in law for N.Z. Univ. for many years; fellow N.Z. Univ. senate, 1916-20.

O'SULLIVAN, TIMOTHY ADOLPHUS, M.A., M.R.S.T.—B. 1894; ed. King's Hosp. and Trinity coll., Dublin; asst. mast., col. for blind, Worcester, St. Christophers, Eastbourne, Ranelagh sch., Athlone, 1913-15; Insns Ct. O.T.C. and R.A., 1915-19; asst. mast., Raffles inst., Singapore, 1919; inspr., schls., Pahang, Feb., 1922; do., Malacca, Nov., 1924; do., Selangor, Mar., 1927; senr. educn. offr. (A.), S.S., 1931; inspr., schls., Penang, Aug., 1931; senr. educn. offr., sp. gr. Malaya, Nov., 1936; inspr. of schls., Perak, July, 1936.

OSWELL, HENRY THEODORE WARREN.—B. 1896; ed. Dulwich Coll. and Worcester Coll., Oxford; war serv., 1916-18; cadet, F.M.S., April, 1920; passed cadet, June, 1922; offr., class V, Apr., 1923; asst. dist. offr., T. Malim, Feb., 1925; asst. contrlr., lab., Seremban, Aug., 1926; offr., cls. IV, Apr., 1927; dist. offr., Ulu Langat, Nov., 1929; prot., lab., Kedah, Mar., 1930; offr., cls. III, Apr., 1932; offr., cls. II, Apr., 1937; on temp. trans. to Mauritius as prot., imigrts., poor law comanr. and dir. of lab., Mar., 1938.

OWEN, GEORGE DOUGLAS, C.M.G. (1935).—B. 1887; ed. Highgate Schl. (Eng.); entd. civ. ser., Trinidad, 16th Jan., 1907; 3rd cls. col. sec.'s office, Aug., 1915; asst. sec., local defence comte., 1914-1915; ag. sec., ditto, 1915-1916; sec. to educn. coman., June, 1914 to Nov., 1916; sec., West Indian Court of Appeal Conf., 1916; priv. sec. to Sir W. S. Knaggs in 1910, 1912 and 1916; to Sir G. R. Le Hunte, 1913-1915; to Sir J. R. Chancellor, 1916, 1920 and 1921; served with H.M. Forces, 1916-19; ch. and conf. clk., col. secretariat, and clk. of legia. coun., May, 1920; priv. sec. to Sir T. A. V. Best, 1921 and to Sir S. Wilson, 1923-24; ag. 2nd asst. col. sec. for several periods since 1921; asst. col. sec., clk. to exec. coun., combined act. and ct. of policy, Br. Guiana, Feb., 1925; ag. col. sec. on various occasions, 1925-30; col. sec., Barbados, Sept., 1931; ag. gov. in 1932, 1933, 1935 and 1937; col. sec., Br. Guiana, 1938.

OWEN, WILFRID HERBERT, B.Sc.Tech., A.R.I.B.A., Chart. Arch., A.M.T.P.I., R.I.B.A. Dip., Town Planning.—B. 1896; ed. St. John's Cheetham, Manchester, Manchester Grammar Schl. and Manchester Univ.; archt., P.W.D., Hong Kong, 1925.
OWEN, HON. WILLIAM FRANCIS LANGER, K.C.—B. 1896; judge, sup. ct., N. S. Wales, Oct., 1937.
PADDON, CYRIL EVANS, A.C.A.—B. 1899; war surv., 1918-19; asst. audr., S.S., May, 1929; ag. sr. asst. audr., S.S., Oct. to Dec., 1930; asst. audr., Penang, Nov., 1931; do., S'pore, Oct., 1931.

PAGE, RT. HON. SIR EARLE CHRISTMAS GRAFTON, P.C., G.C.M.G. (1938), M.B., Ch. M. (Syd.).—B. 1880; ed. Sydney High Schl. and Sydney Univ. (George Allen Maths. Scholarship and John Harris Scholarship for anatomy and physiology); served in European War with A.I.F. as surg. specialist to No. 3 Australian casualty clearing station, France, and No. 3 Australia gen. hosp., mayor of S. Grafton, N.S.W., 1918; pres., N. Coast Development League, 1918; el. mem., H. of R., C. of A., for Cowper, Dec., 1919; treas., C. of A., 1923-29; leader of country party in Fedl. parl., 1930; min. for commerce and dep. leader, Lyons min., 1934; also min. for health from Nov., 1937; min. for commerce, Nov., 1938; visited England, 1936, 1938.

PAGE, SIDNEY MAYNARD—Transvaal constab., Oct., 1900; clk., mgte. off., July, 1902; asst. mgte., Mar., 1913; rgtg., Mar., 1918; pub. serv. inspr., Nov., 1924; addnl. mag., Pretoria, Mar., 1928; mag., Pietersburg, Sept., 1928; ditto,
DURBAN, Feb., 1930; do., Johannesburg, Mar., 1933.

PALETHORPE, RONALD FLINT.—B. 1891; ed. Hereford Schol. and Keble Coll., Oxford; ast. mast., St. George's Schol., Windsor Castle, 1913; ast. dist. commnr., E.A.F., 5th Aug., 1914; ag. dist. commnr., Kenya, 1926; res. mag., 1929; ag. puisne judge, Nov., 1937.

PALLISTER, RICHARD ALAN, M.D., B.S. (Durham), M.R.C.P., D.T.M. & H. (Lond.)—B. 1903; ed. Rutherglen Coll., Univ. of Durham Coll. of Med.; M.O., Malaya, Aug., 1927; ast. prof. of med., Coll. of Med., S'pore, Sept., 1935; ag. assoc., do., June, 1937; ag. prof. of med., July, 1937.

PALMER, HAROLD.—B. 1895; apptd., after compet. exam., clk. of the 2nd div. of the civ. ser.; apptd. to national health insurance audit dept., 13th Jan., 1913; on military service, 2nd May, 1918, to 26th Feb., 1920; assigned to C.O., 8th Mar., 1920; second ast. acctnt., 5th Nov., 1928.

PALMER, Sir HERBERT RICHMOND, K.C.M.G. (1933), C.M.G., C.B.E.—Ed. Oundle Schol. and Trinity Hall, Camb. (class. schol.), M.A., LL.B.; barrister-at-law, Middle Temple, 1904; ast. res., N. Nigeria, Oct., 1904; commr., native rev., N. Nigeria, 1911; rev. mission to Anglo-Egyptian Sudan, 1912; supervisor, native rev., Nigeria, 1914; ag. res., Kano Prov., Nigeria, 1915-16; res., Bornu Prov., Nigeria, 1917; visited Anglo-Egyptian Sudan from Bornu via Wadai and Darfur in connection with educn. proposals, 1918; ag. lieut.-gov., N. Prov., Nigeria, May-Dec., 1921; ag. res., Sokoto Prov., Apr. to May, 1922; lieut. gov., N. Prov., Nigeria, May, 1925; gov., Gambia, 1930; gov. and commdr-in-ch., Cyprus, 1933; ret. 1939.

PALMOUR, FREDERICK JOHN CLIFFORD, F.S.I.—B. 1884; ed. Daventry Schol. and Queen's Coll., Oxon.; survr., Can. Pac. Rly., 1911; valuer, valn. dept., I.R., 1913; adjt., R.E., ord. surv. office, Southampton, 1918; land agent, lands branch, W.O., 1920; ex. offr., land dept., Nigeria, 1928; land offr. and survr., Gambia, 1934.
PALTRIDGE, FREDERICK, A.R.C.S.C.I., R.Sc. (Eng.) London U., Hon. B.Sc., N.U.I.—Ast. tel. engrnr., P.&T., Gold Coast, June, 1928; tel. engrnr., July, 1932; senr. do., July, 1937.

PANES, JOHN NELSON.—B. 1888; ed. Dean Close Schol. and Hertford Coll., Oxford (M.A. hon.); mast., King Edward VI's Schol., Norwich, 1914-22; ast. mast., King's Coll., Lagos, Nigeria, 1922; ast. educn., S.P., Nigeria, 1926; ag. ch. inspr., educn., 1930-31; prin., King's Coll., Lagos, 1931; ch. inspr., educn., 1936; ag. ast. dir., educn., S. Provs., 1937.

PANET, LIEUT.-COL. CHARLES LOUIS.—B. 1870; ed. Ottawa Univ.; ent. civ. serv., Canada, 1889; sec., Canadian defence commte., 1898; priv. sec. to late Sir F. W. Borden, min. of militia and defence, 1904-12; sec., dept. of militia and defence from 1907; pres., bd. of enquiry, re claims of applicants for the fenian raid volunteer bounty, 1912-15; pres. of pensions and claims bd.; ment. in desp. for serv. rendered in Can., 1914-1918.
PAPAFIO, Mercy Kwalee Quartey.—Ed. Accra Grammar Schol. and in England; 6th grade teacher, 1911; 5th grade do., 1912; 2nd cls. do., 1919; 1st cls. do., 1920; headmistress 1921.

PARK, ALEXANDER DALLAS, C.M.G., (1932).—B. 1882; ed. Waitaki Pub. Schol., Waimate High Schol. and privately; served with N.Z. forces in S. Africa; N.Z. pub. wks. dept., 1900-18; agr. dept., 1919; inspr., pub. serv. commnr.'s office, 1920; sec., marine, 1922; ast. pub. serv. commnr., 1923; ast. sec., treas., 1925; sec., 1930, also finan. adv., 1931; joint managing dir., N.Z. mortgage corp., 1935; joint managing dir., N.Z. state advances corp., 1936.

PARK, CAPT. EDWARD NIGEL, O.B.E. (1933), M.C.—B. 1889; ed. Aldenham Schol.; European War, 1914-17; lieut. 6th (Serv.) Batt., Border Regt., M.C., desps.; capt., Somaliland Camel Corps, 1917-20; operns. against Mullah, desps.; dist. police offr., Somaliland, 1920; dist. commr., Somaliland, Feb., 1922.

PARK, JAMES MILLER.—B. 1883; ed. Glenavy State Schol., Waimate High Schol.; joined N.Z. land and income tax dept., 1902; apptd. section clk., 1919; assessor, 1924; ch. assessor, 1928; dep. commnr., 1932; commnr., 1937.

PARKE, JOSEPH.—B. 1888; R.I.C., July, 1908-May, 1913; inspr., pol., F.M.S., May, 1913; ag. ast. commnr., pol., Nov., 1923; ast. commnr., detect. br., Perak, Nov., 1924; dir., crim. intell. br., K. Lumpur, Nov., 1927; do., F.M.S., June, 1932; dep. commnr., pol., F.M.S., Oct., 1932; dir., crim. intell., F.M.S., Oct., 1934; ch. pol. offr., Perak, Mar., 1938.

PARKE, CAPT. L. S.—Coman., 7th Batt., Hampshire Regt., 1909; Coldstream Guards, 1911; on war ser., Uganda and India, 1913-18; selected by W.O. for serv. with Yemen Infy., Aden, 1919-20; cadet, Zanzibar pol., 1923-25; ast. finan. advr. and comdt., pol., govt. of Bahrain, Persian Gulf, 1926-32 (twice acted as advr. to govt.); 1st grade inspr., Mauritius pol., 1933.

PARKER, JAMES T.—Cert. teacher, Leeward Is.; dist. postmr. Bethesda, 1901; schlrm., govt. boys' reformatory schol., 1903; clk. and storekeeper, P.W.D., and clk. to road bd., Antigua, 1906; 1st govt. offr., Virgin Is., 1915; inspr. of weights and measures, Virgin Is., 1917.

PARKER, LANCELOT—B. 1879; clk., cust., Joh'burg, 1902; survr., Cape Town, 1915; served R.F.A. in Great War; cust. investigation offr. for Union in New York, 1926; collr. cust. and shipping mast., Port Elizabeth, 1930; do., Durban, 1933.

PARKER, PELHAM.—B. 1888; Mediterranean and Egyptian Expedy. Forces, 1915-20 (Gallipoli, Salonika, Palestine and Egypt); 2nd lieut. (Gen. List), 1917; lieut., 1918; miny. of P.W., Egypt (Sudan irrign. serv.), 1921-22; ast. acctnt., P.W.D., Cyprus, Oct., 1929; ag. acctnt., June to Sept., 1930; seconded to treas., Nov., to Dec., 1930; ast. acctnt., 1st gr., Jan., 1932; ch. acctnt., Nov., 1934; ag. registr., co-op. and co-op. credit socs., June-Oct., 1936; ag. dep. treas., various periods 1936-38.

PARKES, EDWARD, M.V.O., I.S.O.—Ent. Tasmanian pub. serv., 1906; clk. of exec. coun., 1925; sec. to premier, 1925; offl. sec., Tasmanian agt.-gen.'s office, London, 1927-28; under sec. and ch. electoral offr., 1930; state organizer, visit of H.R.H. the Duke of Gloucester, 1934; mem., salmon and freshwater fisheries commn., 1925-35.

PARKHILL, SIR ROBERT ARCHDALE, K.C.M.G. (1936).—El. to H. of R., C'wealth of Australia, 1927; min. for home affrs. and transport, Lyons' ministry, 1932; P.M.G., 1932-34; min. for defence, 1934; ret. from cabinet, 1937.

PARKINSON, Sir Arthur Charles Cosmo, K.C.B. (1938), K.C.M.G. (1935), C.M.G. (1931), O.B.E., M.A. (1910).—B. 1884; ed. at Epsom and Oxford; class. demy, Magdalen, 1903-7; 1st cls. mods., 1906; 1st cls. lit. hum., 1907; apptd. after compet. exam., 1st cls. olk., acctnt-gen.'s dept., Admiralty, Feb., 1908; transf'd. as 2nd cls. clk., C.O., 19th Apr., 1909; sec.; man. comtce. of imperial bureau of entomology; sec. to comtce. apptd. to investigate relationship between wild animals and trypanosomiasis, 1913-1914; asst. priv. sec. to Mr. Lewis Harcourt, 5th Oct., 1914, to Mr. Bonar Law, 27th May, 1915, to Mr. Walter Long, 11th Dec., 1916; 1st cls. clk., 8th Dec., 1917; on military service from 16th May, 1915 to 26th May, 1919; prin., 1st Apr., 1920; priv. sec. to Lord Milner, 1st Oct., 1920; asst. sec., 1st Oct., 1925; order of the Brilliant Star of Zanzibar, cls. III, 1929; asst. under-sec. of state, 15th Aug., 1931; perm't. under sec. of state for the cols., 2nd July, 1937.

PARMELEE, James Grannis, O.B.E.—B.1875; inspr., iron and steel bounties, Sydney, Nova Scotia; trade and commerce, Canada, 1902; olk., dept. T. and C., Apr., 1911; on mily. serv., 1914-19 (twice ment. in desps.); head clk., Apr., 1919; ch. clk., Jan., 1921; asst. dep. min. T. and C., Apr., 1923; on sp. duty to U.K., 1928; dep. min., trade and commerce, Nov., 1931.

PARNALL, John.—Solr.; asst. aud., E. Africa Prot., 1915; asst. aud. in charge, Zanzibar, Nov., 1915; ag. treas., Zanzibar, Sept. 1919 to July, 1920; registr. and offl. assignee, H.B.M. ct., Zanzibar; registr. of patents, designs, and trade marks, Feb., 1920; ag. admstr.-gen. and registr. of docs., Apr. to Oct., 1922; asst. admstr.-gen., Tanganyika Territory, Sept., 1923; ag. admstr.-gen., Oct., 1923 to Jan., 1924; admstr.-gen., pub. trustee, registr. of docs. and land offr., Zanzibar, May, 1924; ag. atty-gen., June-Dec., 1929; also offl. assignee, registr., patents, designs and trade marks, Jan., 1934; ag. treas., Nov., 1934.

PARR, Hon. Sir Christopher James, G.C.M.G. (1935), K.C.M.G. (1925), C.M.G. (1914).—B. 1869; ed. Auckland Gram. Schol. and Auckland Univ. Coll.; barrister-at-law; mem., N.Z. parlmt., 1914-26; min. of educn., 1920-26; min. of health, 1920-23; min. of just., 1923-26; P.M.G. and min. of tele., 1925-26; high commr. for N.Z. in England, 1926-29 and 1934-36; M.L.C. and leader of leg. coun., 1931-33.
PARRY, J. H. B.A. (Cantab.), M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.)—Temp'y. comn. in Indian med. serv., Mar., 1915 to Mar. 1919; med. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Mar., 1919; senr. med. offr., 1928.
PARRY, Reginald Francis Scarlett.—Ed., St. John's Schol., Leatherhead, and Lincoln Coll., Oxford; jnr. supt., educn. dept., N. Nigeria, Jan., 1920; ag. ch. inspr., S. Provs., 1934.
PARRY, Hon. William Edward.—B. 1878; ed. pub. schls., N.S.W.; mem., N.Z. royal comms. on mines, 1911; mem., N.Z. H. of R. since 1919; min. of internal affrs., 1935.
PARSONS, Hon. Sir Herbert Angas, K.T. Bach. (1936), LL.B.—Judge of sup. ct., S. Australia, 1921.

PARSONS, Thomas Reginald.—B.1898; entd. savings bank dept., G.P.O., June, 1914; apptd. after compet. exam., ast. clk., Apr., 1916; served in R.N. Apr., 1917-Apr., 1919; trans. to C.O. as cler.-offr., Sept., 1922; higher grade cler.-offr., Jan., 1938.

PARTRIDGE, George James, O.B.E. (1937), B.A. (1915), M.A. (1921), Barrister-at-Law, Middle Temple (1927).—B. 1893; ed. Christ's Hospital and Cambridge; open exhib. (maths.), and Essex County Scholar, Queens' Coll., 1912-14; 2nd cls. maths. tripes, part I., 1913, B.A. (War), 1916; temp. comn., 1914-18; on active serv. in France and German E. Africa; 1914-15 Star; ment. in desp.; asst. polit. offr., 1st grade, provisional admstrn., German E. Africa, Dec. 1918; priv. sec. to ag. admstr., Aug. to Dec., 1919; asst. sec., Oct., 1919; clk. to exec. coun., Sept., 1922 to Sept., 1923; senr.-asst. sec., Aug., 1925; ag. ast. ch. sec., in 1927, 1928 and 1929; asst. ch. sec., Apr., 1930; ag. dep. ch. sec. for various periods, 1930-33; 1st ast. ch. sec., 1934; ag. prov. comn., 1937; ag. admstrv. sec. Jan.-Apr., 1938; prov. comn., 1938.
PASCHALIS, Neoptolemos, O.B.E. (1937), K.C. (1931).—B. 1880; barrister-at-law, Middle Temple, 1902; solr.-gen., Cyprus, Mar., 1927; ag. atty.-gen. for various periods, 1927-38.

PASKIN, Jesse John, B.A., M.C.—B. 1892; ed., King Edwards Schl., Stourbridge and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; 1st cls., maths. tripes, pt. I., 1913; on mily. serv., Aug., 1914 to Mar., 1919 (Worcestershire Regt., T.F. and Machine Gun Corps); Croix de Guerre, 1918; transf'd. to T.F. res. with rank of major, Jan., 1920; apptd. unier reconstruction scheme, junr. higher divn. clk., miny. of transport, Jan., 1920; ast. prin., C.O., 29th Mar., 1921; ast. prte. sec. to S. of S., 10th Dec., 1928; prin., 4th Sept., 1929.

PASSFIELD, 1st Baron (Cr. 1929) Rt. Hon. Sidney James Webb, P.C., 1924, LL.B. (Lond.), Hon. Doc., Polt. Economy (Munich), etc.—B. 1859; ed. London and abroad; ent. W.O. by open compet., 1878; survr. of taxes, 1879; C.O., cls. I by open compet., 1881; resigned, 1881; sometime hon. lect. on polt. economy at City of London Coll. and Working Men's Coll. and subsequently hon. prof., public admstrn., London School of Economics, 1912-27; M.P. (Seaham Divn.), 1922-29; pres., Bd. of Trade, Jan.-Nov., 1924; sec. of state for dom. affrs. and the cols., June, 1929; has served on numerous royal comms. and deptl. comtces.; author of many works on politics and economics.

PASSINGHAM, Frank Selwood, M.B.K. (1937).—B. 1883; ed. Goldsmiths' and Morley Colls., Lond.; hors. medallist in typography, City and Guilds of Lond. Inst.; govt. printer, Mauritius, Feb., 1914; also supt., bookbinding works and stat. stores, Apr., 1914; supt. of printing, Jamaica, Mar., 1924; supt., govt. printing office, Cyprus, Oct., 1930.

PATENAUBE, Joseph Oscar, I.S.O.—Ed. Longueil Coll., Quebec; clk., Dom. govt. stationery office, 1889; ast. supt., Dom. govt. stationery, Ottawa, 1899; supt., stationery, 1911; contr. purchases dept., pub. printing and stationery, 1920; King's printer of Canada, 1933.

PATERNER, Henry Sibbald.—B. 1890; ed. Winchester Coll. (schol.) and Magdalen Coll., Oxford (exhib.), B.A., 1913; cadet, F.M.S., Nov., 1913; seconded for war serv., Mar., 1915; active serv. in France and England, 1915-19; ment. in desp.; demob. with rank of major, Aug., 1919; ast. dist. offr., Aug., 1919; seconded as ast. adviser, Trengganu, July, 1920; supernum. offr., cls. IV., Nov., dist. offr., Dec., 1923; offr., cls. III., Nov., 1925; offr., cls. II., Nov., 1930; ast. admr., Kedah, Apr., 1932; offr. cls. IB, Sept., 1934; P.M.C., Penang, Nov. 1935;

PATERSON, RALPH STANLEY WATSON, M.C.—B. 1894; ed. Perth Acad. and Tech. Coll., Dundee; val. dept., inland rev., 1913; R.E., 1915; county survr., Zetland, 1919; engr., P.W.D., Hong Kong, 1924; tech. secy., P.W.D., 1932.

PATTERSON, JOHN ROBERT.—B.1892; B.A. (Cantab.); ast dist. offr., Nigeria, 1915; ag. senr. res., Bornu Prov., 1925; adm. offr., c.l.s., Aug., 1930; prin. ast. sec., Oct., 1930; ag. dep. ch. sec., 1932; sec., N. Provs., 1935; staff grade, 1936.
PATTON, JOHN, M.B., Ch.B., B.A.O. (Belf.), D.P.H.—B. 1896; temp. med. offr., Basutoland, 1936; med. offr., 1937.

PAUL, ALEXANDER JOHN.—Silver medallist, City and Guilds; serv. London Telephone Service, posts and tels., Ceylon, Nigeria and Gold Coast; Ceylon engrnr., 1914; tel. engrnr., Gold Coast, 1933; senr. engrnr., 1937; ag. ch. engrnr., 1936; ag. engrnr.-in-ch., 1938.

PAUL, ERNEST VICTOR.—B. 1890; ed. Taonui pub. schl., Palmerston North High Sch.; N.Z. state advances office, 1907; pub. trust office, 1919; acctnt., printing and stationery dept., 1927; govt. printer, 1937.
PAUL, GEORGE GRAHAM, LL.B. (Edin.), M.A. (St. Andrews).—B. 1887; advoc., Scottish bar, 1910; puisne judge, Nigeria, 1933.

PAUL, ROBERT JAMES.—B. 1888; sub.-inspr., constab., Trinidad, Sept., 1912; 2nd lieut., Trinidad local forces, Jan., 1913; pay and qr-mtr., constab., and senr. supply and transport offr., Trinidad local forces, Nov., 1914, to Oct., 1916, and from Apr. to May, 1917; ag. inspr. of constab. and capt. local forces, Aug., 1915; ast. supt., fire brigade, Nov., 1914, to Nov., 1915, and from Feb. to Mar., 1919; ag. supt., fire brigade, Nov., 1915, to Sep., 1916; J.P. for the colony; ag. chief of police, comdt. local forces, supt. of prison, St. Vincent, Aug., 1919; J.P. for the colony; supt. of pol., Nyassaland, Mar., 1923; ag. contr., pol. and prisons, May, 1925 to Sept., 1926; ag. ch. comntr., pol. and ch. inspr., prisons, in 1926, 1929, 1932 and 1936.

PAULIN, GEORGE.—B. 1885; ed. Canterbury Coll., Univ. of New Zealand, B.Sc., 1906; B.Eng., 1908; pupillage with govt. rly., New Zealand, with city engrnr., Christchurch, and with Selwyn county engrnr. and engrnr.-in-chief, city of Dunedin drainage; engrnr.-survr., P.W.D., N.S. Wales, 1910; dist. engrnr., Fiji, 1912; res. engrnr., Suva harbr. wks., Fiji, 1915.
PAUW, JACOBUS CORNELIUS, M.A.—B. 1892; ed. Victoria Coll., Stellenbosch, S.A. Teachers' Coll., Columbia Univ., New York, U.S.A.; inspr., schl., Transvaal, 1934; sec., Transvaal educn. dept., 1937.
PAVLIDES, STELIO.—B. 1892; barrister-at-law, Gray's Inn, 1916; crown coun., Cyprus Dec., 1927; ag. solr.-gen., for various periods, 1928-38.
PAWAN, JAMES LENOX, M.B.E. (1934), M.B., Ch.B., Edin.—B. 1887; ent. med. serv., Trinidad, Jan., 1913; dist. med. offr., Mar., 1916; bacteriologist and pathological regnr., Nov., 1923.
PAYNE, ERNEST JOHNS.—B. 1876; undersec., dept. of lab. and industry, N.S.W., June, 1918; pub. trustee, July, 1924; chmn., pub. serv. bd., Jan., 1933.

PAYNE, RALPH LAVINGTON.—B. 1886; ed. privately; col. med. office, Grenada, 1903; supervsr., cust., Nigeria, 1911; adm. offr. (N.P. secretariat), 1915; on active serv., 1917-19; resumed duty, 1919; ag. prin. ast. sec., Nigerian secretariat, Apr., 1930 to Aug., 1931; dep. res., July, 1934; adm. offr., cl. I, 1935.

PEAKE, HAROLD GORDON, B.Sc. (hons.), Lond., A.C.G.I., M. Inst. C.K.—B. 1890; ed. Eltham Coll. and Imp. Coll. of Science and Tech., London; adm. engrnr. staff, Rosyth dockyard, 1912; ast. engrnr., P.W.D., F.M.S., 1914; mily. wks. services, India, 1919; engrnr., P.W.D., Nigeria, 1924; senr. exec. engrnr., 1923; D.P.W., Zanzibar, 1933.

PEARCE, Rt. Hon. Sir GEORGE FOSTER, K.C.V.O. (1927), P.C. (1921)—Senator for Western Australia in commonwealth parlmt.; sec. of trades coun. at Perth, 1896; pres., 1899; returned to first Commonwealth Senate, 1901; re-elected, 1906, and elected chmn. of comtess.; min. of defence, C. of A., Apr., 1910 to June, 1913; at. Imp. Conf., 1911; min. of defence Sept., 1914; rep. Australia at the Washington Disarmament Conf., 1921; min. for home and territories in Bruce ministry, 1923; vice-pres., exec. coun., June, 1926; min. of defence and govt. leader in senate (Lyons' ministry), 1932; resigned min. of defence and apptd. min. for external affrs. and min. in ch., territories, 1934; defeated at g.e., 1937; ret. from cabinet, Nov., 1937.

PEARCE, HENRY JOSEPH, M.C., M.Inst. Struct.E., L.R.I.B.A.—B. 1887; ed. Castle House Schl., Weymouth and Weymouth Tech. and engrg. Schl.; ast. engrnr., Hong Kong, 1919; dep. head, bldg. ord. office, 1925; exec. engrnr., bldgs. ord. office, 1930.

PEARCE, PHILIP GEORGE.—B. 1888; ed. Mt. Albert Schl. and Coromandel dist. High Schl., N.Z.; joined N.Z. valuation dept., 1904; justice dept., 1919; lands and survey dept., 1921; mines dept., 1921; inspr., pub. serv. comntr.'s office, 1926; under-sec., native dept. and native trustee, 1933-35; comntr., stamp duties, and sec., lands and deeds, 1935.

PEARSON, COLIN BATEMAN, M.A. Cantab.—B. 1889; capt., R.A.R. of O., Durham L.I.; called to bar, Middle Temple, 1924; European mag., Tonga, Sept., 1928; ag. land ct. judge, 1930; pol. mag., Gold Coast, Oct., 1932; ag. circuit judge, Ashanti and N. Territories, May to Sept., 1934; ag. puisne judge, sup. ct., on various occasions, 1935-37; title changed to dist. mag., 1935; ast. judge, prot. ct., Nigeria, 1938.

PEARSON, JAMES RAJ, C.I.E.—B. 1871; schol., St. Paul's Schol. and Clare Coll., Cambridge; ent. I.C.S., 1892; held various Indian appts., 1892-1926; ret., I.C.S., Aug., 1926; sec. for Indian affrs., Fiji, 1927.

PECK, EDWARD FINCH, M.R.C.V.S., B.V.Sc., (Tor.), D.T.V.M.—B. 1901; ed. Sedbergh, Univ., Toronto, Liverpool and Edinburgh; farming, Canada, 1920; inspr., health of animals br., Canada, 1929; vety. offr., Nigeria, 1931; vety. and agr. offr., Somaliland, 1935; ch. do., 1938.
PECK, MAJOR GEORGE EDGAR, M.C., R.E., A.M.I.C.E.—B. 1875; ed. City of London Engrng. Coll.; engrnr. and man., water works dept., Barbados, 1923; dir. P.W., 1934.

PEDLER, FREDERICK JOHNSON, M.A.—B. 1908; ed. Watford Grammar Schl. and Gonville and Caius Coll., Cambridge; major schol., 1927-30; Goldsmith's exhib., 1929; cls. I. divn. I, hist. tripods, pts. I. and II; B.A., 1930; Schuldhamb plate prizewinner; apptd. after compet. exam., ast. prin., C.O., Oct., 1930; seconded to prov. admnstr., Tanganyika Territory, Jan., 1934 to Jan., 1936; pvt. sec. to perm. U.S. of S. for the col., 11th Jan., 1936; ag. prin., 31st May, 1937; seconded as sec. to Lord Privy Seal, 1937-38.

PEDLOW, JOSEPH HOWARD, B.A. (N.U.I.), Barrister-at-Law (Middle Temple).—B. 1889; ed. Queen's Coll., Galway; cadet, S.S., Dec., 1912; Chinese Prot., Penang, Jan., 1913; at Amoy, to study Hokkien, Sep., 1913; Chinese Prot., S'pore, Mar., 1916; treasy. for custodian enemy property and war loan work, May, 1916; collr. of war tax, S'pore, in addition, Mar. to June, 1918; pol. mag., S'pore, Aug., 1919; supernm. offr., cls. IV, Dec., 1919; asst. to atty.-gen., S'pore, Dec., 1920; ag. dep. treas., S'pore, Jan., 1921; offr. asst. prot. of Chinese, S'pore, June, 1921; pol. mag., Penang, Oct., 1921; mem., rent assessment bd., Penang, and munic. commnr., George Town, Nov., 1921 and Oct., 1924; cls. III, Nov., 1924; prot. Chinese, Penang, Dec., 1924; called to bar, Middle Temple, 1924; dep. pres., munic. commnr., George Town, Penang, (cls. IB.) in addn., Oct., 1926; registr., sup. ct., in addn., Mar., 1927; ag. dep. pub. pros., Penang, June, 1929; do., S'pore, Sept., 1929; offr., cls. II, Dec., 1929; stamp and probate offr., S'pore, Dec., 1930; ag. solr. gen., S.S., 1931-34; dist. judge, S'pore, 1933; offr., cls. I.B., Dec., 1933; ag. atty.-gen., S.S. and mem., exec. and leg. couns.; solr.-gen. S.S., Oct., 1934; ag. judge, F.M.S., Dec., 1935; puisne judge, S.S., 1936.
PEDRAZA, R.—Asst. dist. commnr., E.A.P., Aug., 1914.

PEEL, SIR WILLIAM, K.C.M.G. (1931), K.B.E. (1928), C.M.G. (1926), B.A., 1896, M.A., 1931—B. 1875; ed. Queens' Coll., Cambridge, 11th wrangler, math. tripos., 1896; cadet, S. Sttlmts., Oct., 1897; passed fin. exam. in Malay, Dec., 1899; dist. offr., Prov. Wellesley, Dec., 1901; ditto, Tampin, F.M.S., June, 1908; seconded as pres., mun. commnr., Penang, Jan., 1911; offr., Class III, but remained seconded, Jan., 1912; ag. res. councillor, Penang, in addn., Feb. to Oct., 1917; pres., mun. commnr., Singapore, July, 1918; offr., Class II, F.M.S., Jan., 1919; joint passage contrlr., Malaya, in addn., Feb., 1919; seconded as food contrlr., Malaya and chmn., shipping cte., May, 1919; chmn., European unemployment comte., in addn., Feb., 1921; contr., lab., F.M.S. and S. Sttlmts., Feb., 1922; Br. adviser, Kedah, Oct., 1922; res. coun., Penang, July, 1925; ch. sec., F.M.S., June, 1926; offr., administering govt., S.S. and high commnr., Malay States, May to June, 1927; on sp. duty, London, Mar., 1928; gov. and commdr-in-ch., Hong Kong, Mar., 1930; ret., 1935; Kt. of Grace, Order of St. John of Jerusalem; hon. fellow, Queens Coll., Camb., 1932.

PEET, JAMES HERBERT, O.B.E. (1938)—B. 1882; clk., audits office, Bahamas, 1904; 4th clk., P.O., 1907; 2nd clk., C.S.O., 1909; 3rd clk., treasy. dept., 1910; ch. clk., P.O., 1913; ch. clk. and asst. examg. offr., customs, 1914; compr. of cust., Apr., 1921; ag. recvr.-gen., treas. and temp. mem., exec. coun., on various occasions, 1925-28; ag. postmr., Sept., 1928; has held numerous other ag. appts.; postmr., Sept., 1928.

PEGG, HUGH HANDLEY.—B. 1889; ed. Ironville Schi., Derbyshire and pvtevly; asst. land survr., P.W.D., Hong Kong, 1914; asst. engnr., Jan., 1921; mem., Kowloon communication comteee., 1924; dep. head, rds. office, Jan., 1925; exec. engnr., Dec., 1932.
PELLISSIER, S. H., B.A.—B. 1887; ed. Grey Univ. Coll.; vice prin. Boshof, 1914; prin., Ficksburg, 1917; dir., educn., O.F.S., Dec., 1926.
PELLY, RUSSELL STEELE.—B. 1910; ed. Mercer's Schi. and Edin. and Oxford Univs.; B.Sc. (Forestry Edin.), 1931; asst. conservator (forests), Br. Hond., Oct., 1932; do., Sierra Leone, 1937.
PEMBLETON, EDGAR STANLEY, B.A. (Oxon).—B. 1888; asst. res., N. Nigeria, 1911; res., 1928; senr. res., Aug., 1934; sec., N. provs., Oct., 1936-May, 1937; ag. ch. commnr., do., Mar.-Apr., 1937.

PENDLEBURY, CAPT. HENRY MAURICE, F.R.E.S., F.Z.S.—B. 1893; ed. Shrewsbury Schi. and R. Coll. of Sc., London; 2nd lieut., Kings Shropshire Ld. Infnty., Sept., 1914; lieut., Feb., 1915; capt., July, 1915; demob., Sept., 1919; syst. entomologist, F.M.S., Apr., 1921; curator, Selangor museum, Jan., 1932; hon. dy. game warden, Selangor, in addn., Sept., 1934; dir., museums, F.M.S., 1937.

PENGILLEY, ERNEST EDGAR.—B. 1897; ed. Christ's Hosp. (exhbr.) and Exeter Coll., Oxford (class. schol.); lieut., 23rd Batt., Royal Fusiliers, 1917-19; cadet, F.M.S., Jan., 1921; passed cadet, July, 1922; offr., cls. V, Jan., 1924; asst. dist. offr., Rembau, Feb., 1924; tempy. attd. for duty in C.O. as asst. prin., Nov., 1925-May, 1926; asst. collr., land rev., K. Lumpur and dep. regis., titles, Selangor, June, 1926; offr., cls. IV, Jan., 1928; dist. offr., P. Puteh, Kelantan, Aug., 1928; asst. dist. offr., Ipoh, May, 1929; offr., cls. III, Jan., 1933; dist. offr., K. Pilah, Sept., 1935; offr., cls. II, Jan., 1938.

PENNEFATHER, CHARLES EDWARD DE FONBLANQUE.—B. 1883; apptd., Fiji, 1910; dist. commnr., 1914; ag. ch. pol. mag., 1918; ag. registrar-gen. on various occasions; seconded as ag. agt. and consul, Tonga, 1921-22.

PENNEFATHER-EVANS, JOHN PENNEFATHER.—B. 1894; pol. probr., F.M.S., Oct., 1914; A.C.P., Jan., 1918; adjt., fed. hqrs., June, 1921; ag. comdt., pol. depot, Mar., 1924; offr., supt. pol. cir., Taiping, Apr., 1926; ag. commnr., pol., Kedah, Mar., 1931; supt., pol. S.S., Sept., 1932; comdt., pol. depot, S'pore, Dec., 1934; dir., sp. br., Aug., 1935; ag. ch. pol. offr., S'pore, in addn., June, 1938.
PENNINGTON, ARTHUR LAWRENCE, R.A.—B. 1901; ed. Newbury Gram. Schi. and Keble Coll., Oxford; asst. dist. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Sept., 1925.

PENTZ, HERBERT FREDERICK, M.C., M.M.—B. 1886; clk., col. sec.'s office, Cape, 1903; clk., Cape adminstr., 1910; ch. clk., ditto, 1928; pu. serv. inspr., 1930; served in S. African infy. brig. m Great War (overseas); under sec. for pub. ads., Aug., 1931; prov. sec. Transvaal, Apr., 1934.

PEPPYS, WALTER EVELYN, C.M.G. (1938).—B. 1880; ed. at Malvern and Brasenose Coll., Oxford; B.A., 1907; M.A., 1910; cadet, F.M.S., 1908; passed cadet, June 1911; cls. V., Jan., 1912; dist. offr., Jelebu, Apr., 1912; do., Pasir Puteh, June, 1915 and Jan., 1918; offr., cls. IV., F.M.S., Jan., 1918; offr., class III., Nov., 1920; fed. sect. for sp. duty, Aug., 1921; offr., cls. II., Nov., 1925; asst. sec. to gov't., F.M.S., Nov., 1925; offr., cls. II., commnr., lands and mines, Johore, Jan., 1928; offr., cls. I.A., under sec. to gov't., F.M.S., Mar., 1930; supt., govt. monopolies, S.S., Mar., 1932; and commnr., trade and cust., F.M.S. in addn., Apr., 1932; commnr., cust. and excise, S.S. and F.M.S., July, 1932; mem., S.S. trade commn., in addn., Feb., 1933; gen. advr., Johore, 1933.

PERCY, MAJOR GEORGE REGINALD, M.C., A.M.I.C.E., F.S.I., Chtd. Civ. Engnr., Chtd. Survr.—B. 1890; asst. engnr., P.W.D., F.M.S. May, 1919; exec. engr., Feb., 1928; sr. exec. engr., whk. and bldgs., June, 1932; exec. engr., Malacca, Jan., 1934; munici. engr., in addn., Feb., 1934; senr. exec. engr., Jan., 1935; ag. munici. sec., Malacca, in addn. Dec., 1935; ag. Settlement engr., S'pore, Jan. 1938.

PERCY, H. VANE (formerly Baumgartner).—B. 1883; ed., Bedford Sch.; clk., land settlt. dept., O.R.C., Oct., 1902; clk. and typist audit dept., Dec., 1902; clk., audit dept., July, 1904; examr. of accts., audit dept., Jan., 1908; jnr. astt. treas., G. Coast, Jan., 1914; astt. treas., Apr., 1917; ag. ch. astt. treas., Nov., 1919; ag. dep. treas. on several occasions, 1921-28; dep. treas., 1929; ag. treas., for various periods, 1930-37; acctn. gen., July, 1937.

PERDUE, CECIL GRAHAM.—B. 1891; ed. Trin. Coll., Dublin; pol. prob., Hong Kong, Nov., 1912; passed in Chinese, 1916; attd. to Punjab pol., 1916-16; passed in Urdu, 1916; temp. 2nd lieut., Indian army res., 1916-19; J.P., 1920; astt. supt., pol., 1920; astt. census offr., 1921; dir., crim. intell., 1930; divnl. supt., pol., 1930; dep. inspr.-gen., pol., Apr., 1936.

PERERA, BENJAMIN FRANKLIN, B.A.(Lond.).—B. 1901; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Dec., 1925; attd., Colombo kach., Jan., 1926; ditto, Anuradhapura kach., Apr., 1926; office astt. Puttalam kach., Oct., 1927; pol. mag., Kalutara, Apr., 1928; office astt. Kandy kach., Nov., 1928; addnl. pol. mag., Colombo, Aug., 1930; addnl. astt. govt. agt., Matara, Aug., 1930; addnl. astt. govt. agt., Galle, in addn., Mar., 1934; sec., min. for home affrs., Dec., 1935.

PERERA, CONRAD BONIFACE PETER, B.Sc. (Lond.).—B. 1903; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Feb., 1927; attd., Badulla kach., Mar., 1927; attd. Anuradhapura kach., Oct., 1927; office astt., Puttalam kach., Apr., 1928; ditto, Ratnapura kach., May, 1930; addnl. astt. govt. agt., Colombo, June, 1933; addnl. pol. mag., Colombo, Nov., 1933; astt. sttlmt. offr. May, 1934.
PERERA, LESLIE SIMON BERNARD, B.Sc. (Lond.).—B. 1910; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Jan., 1935; offr., cls. II, Jan., 1937; office astt., Feb., 1937; astt. govt. agt., Kurunegala, June, 1938.

PERERA, THEODORE DUNCAN, LL.B. (Lond.).—B. 1895; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Aug., 1919; attd. Kurunegala kach., Aug., 1919; Jaffna kach., Dec., 1919; pol. mag., Avisawella, July, 1921; astt. comsrr. of stamps, July, 1923; pol. mag., Pt. Pedro, Oct., 1926; astt. comsrr. stamps, Nov., 1926; astt. col. sec., Jan., 1929; astt. to income tax adviser, Sept., 1929; sec. to min. for agr. and lands, July, 1931; astt. comsrr. income tax, Feb., 1932; comsrr. income tax, estate duty and stamps, Dec., 1937.

PEREZ, JOSEPH LEON MATHIEU, LL.B.—B. 1896; ed. St. George's Coll., Weybridge, Surrey; called to bar, Middle Temple, 1917; dep. regisr.-gen., Trinidad, Oct., 1927; ag. regisr. gen., regisr., sup. ct. and marshal in 1928 and 1929; ag. solr.-gen., in 1929, 1932, 1934 and 1935; mag., St. George West, 1930; do., Victoria, 1933; astt. law offr., 1934; ch. mak., Trinidad, 1936; ag. puisne judge, Oct., 1936-Aug., 1937, and May-Nov., 1938.

PERRIES, HERMAN ERIC, B.A. (Cantab), B.Sc. (Lond.).—B. 1906; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., 1929; office astt., Anuradhapura kach., Aug., 1934; astt. sec. to min. for agr. and lands, Mar., 1936; admnr. sec., dept. of income tax, estate duty and stamps, and clk. to the bd. of review, July, 1937.

PERKINS, HON. JOHN ARTHUR, M.P.—M.L.A., N.S.W., Goulburn, 1921-26; mem., Eden-Monaro, H. of R., C'wealth of Aust., 1926-29 and from 1931; astt. min., Lyons min., Jan.-Oct., 1932; min. for interior, Oct., 1932-Oct., 1934; astt. min., Nov., 1937; min. for trade and customs, Nov., 1938.

PERKINS, LEICESTER GRANT.—B. 1890; ed. privately and Harrison Coll., Barbados; clk., post office, Barbados, Jan., 1909; 5th clk., audit office, Mar., 1911; 2nd clk., registn. office, Nov., 1913; clk. to master in chancery, May, 1914; 4th clk., savings bank, Jan., 1915; 2nd-lieut., Imperial Army, Mar., 1917, served during war in Egypt and Palestine with 2nd B.W.I. Regt.; 1st-lieut., Oct., 1918; 1st clk., Barbados savings bank, Aug., 1919; 2nd grade supvr. of cns., Nigeria, Feb., 1920.
PEROMBELON, EDGAR.—Ag. P.M.G., Mauritius, July, 1935; P.M.G., Nov., 1935.

PEROWNE, STEWART HENRY, Offr. of Order of St. John of Jerusalem (1931); M.A. (Cantab).—B. 1901; lect., govt. Arab Coll., Jerusalem, 1927; adminstr. offr., Palestine, 1930; junr. astt. sec., 1930; seconded as press offr., 1931; astt. dist. commr., 1932; astt. sec., lieut.-gov.'s office, Malta; political offr., Aden, 1937; seconded to B.B.C., Aug., 1938.

PERRY, SIR ALLAN, KT. BACH. (1904); M.D. (Durham); D.P.H. (Lon.); M.R.C.S. Eng.; L.S.A. Lond.—B. 1860; prin. civ. med.offr.and inspr.-gen. of hosps., Ceylon; prin. of Ceylon med. coll., Dec., 1897; major R.A.M.C. (retired); mem. municipal coun., Colombo; late pres. Ceylon branch Br. med. assoc.; del. for Ceylon to Internat. Leprosy Conf., Bergen, 1909; ret., 1915.

PERRY, B. C. G.—B. 1886; apptd. after compet. exam., clk. of the 2nd div. of the civ. serv., and assigned to C.O., 9th Apr., 1906; cler. offr., higher grade, 1st Apr., 1922; regisr., C.O., 7th Sept., 1931.

PERRY, GEORGE McREDDIE, M.B., C.M., Glasgow, and St. Mary's hosp., London, F.R.G.S.—Astl. coll. surg., Lagos, Oct., 1893; ag. coll. surg., Feb. to Mar., 1894; ag. astt. col. sec., May, 1894; med. offr. to expedn. to Idanai mountains; sen. astt. coll. surg., Aug., 1895; ag. coll. surg., 1895.
PERSSE, CAPT. EDMUND MATURIN, M.C. (1918), F.R.G.S., R. of O., W. Yorks. Regt.—B. 1890; ed. Bedford Mod. Sch.; astt. dist. commr., Uganda, Oct., 1919; dist. commr., Apr., 1925.

PETERKIN, EDMUND MURRAY.—B. 1886; agrl. astt., dept. agr., Barbados, 1909; agrl. instr., dept. sci. and agr., Br. Guiana, 1915; hort. supt., bot. gdns., 1917; clk. and sec., bd. agr., 1918; ag. astt. bot. and mycologist, 1922-23; agrl. supt., 1928; investigated rice conditions, Br. Honduras, 1933.
PETERS, BERNARD R., M.B.E., A.M.I.C.E.—Ast. engr., E.A.P., 1914-15; exec. engr., Tanganyika, 1919; ag. dep. D.P.W. in 1926 and 1927; D.P.W., Nyasaland, Aug., 1928.

PETRIDES, SIR PHILIP BERTE, KT. BACH. (1936)—B. 1881; ed. Dulwich Coll. and Brussels; called to the bar, Middle Temple, 1906; joined Midland Circ. and Birmingham and cent. crm. court sess., 1906; legal adviser and crown prosecutor, Seychelles, Jan., 1916; ex-officio mem. exec. and legis. couns.; ag. chief just., July, 1916 to Mar., 1917, May, 1918 to Oct., 1918, and from Dec., 1918 to Nov., 1919; temp. empd., C.O., Feb. to Apr., 1920; ch. just., Seychelles, June, 1920; admstd. govt., Mar., 1921 to Sept., 1922; atty.gen., Nyasaland Prot., 1924; ag. judge, May to Dec., 1925; puisne judge, Nigeria, Sept., 1926; ch. just., Mauritius, May, 1930; do. Gold Coast, Dec., 1935.
PETTMAN, P. A. H.—B. 1896; acct., P.W.D., Zanzibar, 1925; asst. treas., Uganda, 1935; asst. acct.-gen., 1937; ag. senr. acctnt., Feb.-Sept., 1938.

PHANG, JOSEPH—B. 1892; lands and mines dept., Br. Guiana, ag. govt. survr., 1918; govt. survr., 1923; land offr., Essequibo river and dep. navig. offr., 1925; sub-warden, Nos. 3 and 4 mining dist., 1933; 1st grade survr., 1938.

PHEAR, HAROLD WILLIAM, M.M.—B. 1891; ed. Chord Schl.; war serv., 1914-19; prob. inspr., pol., Singapore, Dec., 1919; dist. inspr., pol., Aug., 1921; asst. supvr., cust., F.M.S. and ag. supvr., expt. duties, Singapore, Aug., 1923; supt., cust., prev. br., Selangor, Nov., 1923; do., N. Sembilan, Apr., 1924; dep. commr., trade and cust., N. Sembilan, Jan., 1931; head, prov. ser., Singapore, Feb., 1932; J.P., Singapore, Mar., 1932; dep. supr., rubber control, S'pore, June, 1934; asst. supr., petroleum rev., July, 1934; dep. commr., cust. and excise, Selangor and Pahang, May, 1935; ag. senr. dep. commr., F.M.S. Dec., 1936.

PHILLIPS, ARTHUR, M.A. (Oxon.), B.A. (Lond.), Barrister-at-Law (Middle Temple).—B. 1907; ed. Highgate Schl. and Trinity Coll., Oxford (schol.); dist. offr., Kenya, July, 1931; ag. res. mag., 1933-35; crown coun., Sept., 1936.
PHILLIPS, C. H., L.M.S.S.A. (Lond.).—Tempy. lieut., R.A.M.C., Jan., 1915 (General List); med. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Dec., 1920.

PHILLIPS, HAROLD ERNEST.—B. 1877; clk. to auditor, B. Honduras, Oct., 1892; 5th clk., treasy., customs and P.O. dept., June, 1893; 4th clk., Feb., 1894; 3rd clk., treasy. and cust. dept., May, 1897; 2nd clk., June, 1898; 2nd clk., C.S.O., Sept., 1900; ch. clk. and clk. of couns., Jan., 1906; priv. sec. to gov. Sir E. Swayne, 1909-10; to gov. Collett, 1911-13; to ag. gov., Walter, 1916; ag. commr. of currency, Feb., 1911; ag. treasur., collr. of cust., 1911-14 and 1917-20; ag. col. sec., 1920; Br. Honduras del. to Canada-W. Indies Confce., Ottawa, June, 1920; asst. col. sec., Apr., 1921; commr. and judge, Turks and Caicos Is., June, 1923.

PHILLIPS, WILLIAM JAMES ELLERY, M.C., M.B., B.S., D.T.M. & H., Lond.—B. 1891; ed. Prince Alfred Coll., Adelaide and Adelaide Univ.; on war serv., major A.A.M.C., 1915-19; med. offr., Malayan med. serv., 1923; dist. med. offr., Cyprvs, Feb., 1935; ag. bacteriologist, Feb.-Apr., 1936; S.M.O., dept. of health, Palestine, 1937.

PHILLIPSON, SYDNEY, M.A. (Manchester).—B. 1892; on mily. serv., Oct. 1915; cadet, Ceylon civil serv., Nov., 1919; attchd. to Kandy kach., Dec., 1919; pol. mag., Matara, 1922; office asst., Kurunegala kach., July, 1924; asst. contr., rev., Jan., 1925; addnl. asst. col. treas., Nov., 1929; addnl. asst. col. treas., June, 1930; contr., estab., July, 1931; contr., supplies and finance, July, 1932; contr., estab., Feb., 1934; ag. dep. finan. sec., May-Nov., 1935; ag. dep. finan. sec., Mar., 1937.
PHILP, DAVID.—B. 1893; asst. acct., F.M.S. rly., Dec., 1919; ag. dep. acct., in 1930, 1932, 1934 and 1935; ag. asst. sec. to gen. man., June, 1931; expend. acct., Aug., 1935.
PICKERING, SIR GEORGE HUNTER, Kt. Bach. (1932).—Town mag., K.A.P., 1910; ag. 2nd puisne judge, 1915; puisne judge, 1917; ag. ch. just., Apr. to Nov., 1927; ch. just., Zanzibar, 1928; ret., 1932.
PICOT, FRANK R.—Dir. N.Z. govt. internal marketing divn., 1937.
PIDDUCK, H. B.—Ed. Harper Adams agrl. coll., Univ. of Wales; Shropshire Yeomanry, 1914-19; science mast., Dominica Grammar Schl., 1922; headmstr., 1923; agr. offr., 1929.

PIEGROME, JOSEPH CHARLES, M.C.—B. 1896; lieut., Northumberland Fusiliers, 1915; France, 1916-18; wounded, 1916; asst. commr., pol., Gold Coast, Sept., 1921; commr., 1927; title changed to supr., 1937; ag. dep. commr., Apr.-Sept., 1938.

PIENAAR, ANDRIES GUSTAF EBELAN.—Ck. of ct., Lichtenburg, July, 1902; ag. pub. prosec., Feb., 1903; pub. prosecutor, June, 1903; ch. clk. and ag. asst. res. mag., Nov., 1905; addnl. asst. res. mag., Krugerdorp, Sept., 1906; res. mag. and native commr., Waterberg, Nov., 1910; res. mag., Ermelo, Apr., 1912; senr. mag., Germiston, May, 1920; pub. serv. inspr., Sept., 1921; mag., Louis Trichardt dept. of just., Aug., 1924; sec. for int., Dec., 1924; chmn., pub. serv. comm., Aug., 1926; chmn., Union tender and supplies bd., June, 1934.

PIENAAR, ANDRIES JACOBUS, K.C., B.A., LL.B.—Ed. Muir Coll., Uitenhage (Cape) and S.A. Coll., Cape Town; advoc., sup. ct. of S. Africa; addl. legal advr., Cape Prov. admntr., 1929; war serv., S.W. Africa and E. Africa, 1914-19; parly. draughtsman, parl. of Union of S. Africa, 1936.

PIENAAR, LIEUT-COL. FILIPPUS ROCHE, D.T.D., O.B.E., R. of O., Union Defence Force—Civ. serv., S.A. Republic; ment. in despa., Great War; admitted sworn translator, sup. ct.; accredited rep. of Union of S. Africa to League of Nations; H.M. cons.-gen. in Mozambique for the Union of S. Africa, 1934.
PIERS, FRANCIS A.—B. 1882; sub-inspr., Basutoland mounted pol., 1910; inspr., 1923; supr., pol., 1936.

PIGGOTT, SIR FRANCIS TAYLOR, KT. BACH (1905).—Barrister-at-law; employed by F.O. on special service in 1887; legal adv. to prim. min., Japan, Nov., 1887; sec. to att.-gen. Sir C. Russell (afterwards Lord Russell of Killowen) during Behring Sea arbitrn., 1893-4; proc.-gen., Mauritius, 1894; ag. ch. just., 1895 to 1897; ch. just., Hong Kong, Apr., 1905; ret., 1912; recd. thanks of coun. of govt. for ser. in connection with planters loans, 1902; author of "Nationality and Nationalization," "Exterritoriality," "Extradition," "Foreign Judgments and Jurisdiction," "Law of Tort," and "Imperial Statutes applicable to the Colonies."

PILBEAM, S. A.—B. 1899; entd. C.O., Jan., 1915; passed asst. clk.'s exam., July, 1916; on military service from 18th July, 1917, to 10th Mar., 1919; assigned to C.O. as asst. clk., Apr., 1919; cler. offr., 1st Jan., 1920; ag. cler. odr. higher grade, 10th Oct., 1925.

PILGRIM, EDWARD OLIVIER.—B. 1886; ed. Lodge Schl., Barbados; B.Sc. (Lond.), hon. physics; asst. mast., Queen's Coll., Br. Guiana, 1905; ag. prin. various occasions; mem., bd. of land survrs., exams., 1916.
PILKINGTON, CHARLES WILLIAM McDONALD.—B. 1877; clk., cust., Cape Town, 1896; ch. clk., Port Elizabeth, 1930; do., Pretoria, 1933; collr., cust., Johannesburg, 1934.

PILLING, HENRY GUY, C.M.G. (1932).—B. 1886; ed. King's Sch., Ely, and Keble Coll., Oxford; M.A., 1911; cadet, Fiji, 1907; passed cadet, 1910; 2nd grade dist. commr., 1914; mem., Indian Franchise Comm., 1920; ag. asst. col. sec., 1920; asst. col. sec., Jan., 1921; ag. sec., W. Pacific high commr., Mar., 1924; sec., W. Pacific high commr., 1926; asst. to high commr., W. Pacific, 1929; col. sec., Br. Honduras, Dec., 1929; ag. gov. in 1930 and 1931; dep. col. sec., Kenya, 1933; gov. and commr.-in-ch., St. Helena, 1936.
PIMM, SURG. LIEUT. SYDNEY RICHARD
GERALD, R.N.—Radiologist, col. hosp., Port of Spain, Trinidad, Mar., 1937.

PINDER, RICHARD FRANCIS, (B. of O.).—B. 1898; ed. St. Bees, Cumberland; served, European War, Flanders, Germany, 1916-19; asst. audr., Sierra Leone, May, 1920; ag. audr., Nov.-Dec., 1920; asst. audr., Gold Coast, Nov., 1929; ag. audr., Feb.-May, 1930; sen. asst. audr., Uganda, Aug., 1931; ag. audr., July-Dec., 1932; audr., Fiji, Solomon Is., Prot., Gilbert and Ellice Is., Col., Aug., 1934; do., N. Rhodesia, 1938.

PINKERTON, WILLIAM JAMES DUNLOP, A.M.I.C.E., Chartered Civil Engr.—B. 1890; asst. engr., P.W.D., F.M.S., Apr., 1914; exec. engr., Ulu Selangor, July, 1923; sr. exec. engr., in addn., Jan., 1930; offict. as sr. exec. engr., Kinta, in addn., Nov.-Dec., 1930; senr. drainage and irrig. engr., Malay States, Jan., 1932; irrig. engr., Kelantan, Jan., 1932; asst. advr., drainage and irrig., Malay States, Nov., 1936; ar. dir., drng. and irrig., S.S. and advr., do., Malay States, July, 1936.

PINNEY, CAPT. CHAS. ROBERT, M.C. (1917).—B. 1883; ed. Essendon Grammar Sch.; draughtsman, lands dept., Papua, 1906; on war serv., A.I.F., 1914-20; wounded; transf'd. R. of O., C.M.F., 1920; ch. draughtsman, Papua, 1920; staff survr. in addn., 1924; ag. commr., lands, etc., 1927; commr., lands, dir., mines, dir., agr., dep. chmn., land bd., mem. exec. and leg. couns., July, 1930; adminstr., Norfolk Is., July, 1932.

PIERIE, D. A.—B. 1886; clk. Burgher camps, Transvaal, 1903; treasv., 1903; prin. clk. treasv., Union of S. Africa, 1920; sec. mily. pensions bd., treasv., 1920; ch. clk., grade II, 1928; ch. clk., grade I, 1932; dep. commr., pensions, 1936.
PIRIE, JAMES HENRY.—B. 1893; ed. St. Paul's Sch., Darjeeling, India; asst. storekeeper, Nigerian Eastern Rly. constr., 1931; storekeeper, Nigerian rly. open lines, 1931-37; stores aspt., do., 1937.

PIROW, OSWALD.—B. 1890; ed. South Africa, Germany and England; admitted barrister, 1913; K.C., 1924; ent. parl't. of the Union of S. Africa, 1924; min. of justice, June, 1929; min. rlys. and harbrs. and min. defence, Mar., 1933; World Econ. Conf., London, 1933; min., commerce and industry, and defence, 1938.

PITBLADO, DAVID BRUCE, M.A.—B. 1912; ed. Strand Schl. and Emmanuel Coll., Camb. (schol. 1930-34); hist. tripus part 1 and law tripus part 2; B.A., 1933; hist. tripus part 2, cls. 1, 1934; supervisor in hist., Emm. Coll., 1934-35; apptd. after compet. exam., asst. prin., D.O., Oct., 1935; asst. pvt. sec. to S. of S. for Dom. Affrs.: (Mr. Malcolm MacDonald) 1st July, 1937.

PITCAIRN, ANDREW.—B. 1894; ed. Edinburgh Coll. of Agr.; on war serv., 1915-19 (France, Mesopotamia and Palestine); lieut., R.F.A.; ment. in despa.; dist. agrl. offr., Tanganyika Territory, June, 1921; asst. dir., agr., Cyprus, Mar., 1929; ag. dir., agr., on various occasions, 1930-37; ag. dir., animal husbandry, June to Sept., 1930, and June-Sept., 1932.

PITMAN, CHARLES ROBERT SENHOUSE, D.S.O. (1917), M.C. (1916).—B. 1890; ed. Royal Naval Schl., Blundell's and R.M.C., Sandhurst; att'd. R. Warwickshire Regt.; 27th Punjabis; capt., 1915; war serv., Egypt, 1914-15; France, 1916; Mesopotamia, 1916-18 (wounded, despa. twice, M.C., D.S.O.); Palestine, 1918-21; gen. staff, 1919-21; game warden, Uganda, 1925; seconded to N. Rhodesia for sp. duty, 1931-33.

PITT, WALTER JAMES, B.A.—B. 1878; ed. Downing Coll., Cambridge (modern and medieval language tripus); ent. Middle Temple, Jan., 1919; passed Bar final exam., Dec., 1920; headmr., Cape Coast Govt. Schl., 1919; inspr. of schls., 1912; prov. inspr. of schls., 1914; att'd. to polit. serv., Gold Coast, Aug., 1914; asst. dist. commr., July, 1917; dist. commr., Jan., 1921; ag. prov. commr., W. Prov., Ashanti, Feb.-Nov., 1920.

PITTMAN, WILLIAM.—B. 1878; ed. S. African Coll. and Wadham Coll., Oxon; B.A. (Univ. S.A.), M.A. (Oxon); called to bar, Middle Temple, 1903; admitted to Cape (1904) and Transvaal (1905) bars; dean of faculty of law, Univ. S.A., 1918-25; K.C., 1924; judge, sup. ct., S. Africa eastern dista. local divn., 1925; mem., delimitation comm., July, 1927 and Mar., 1932.

PLANT, GEORGE FREDERIC, C.B.E. (1927).—B. 1877; ed. Manchester Gram. Schl. and Brasenose Coll. (scholar, 1896); 1st cl. class. mods., 1898; B.A., 1900; cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., 1901; off. asst., Kurunegala, 1902; off. asst., Galle, 1904; pol. mag., Avissawella, 1905; dist. judge, Ratnapura, Apr., 1906; ditto, Matara, Dec., 1906; asst. govt. agt., Kalutara, May, 1909; temporarily employed in C.O. London, May, 1915, to Dec., 1918; sec., overseas settlement comte., Jan., 1919; permanently transferred to Home civ. serv., Sept., 1919; mem., overseas settlement delegtn. to Canada, 1924; asst. sec., D.O., 11th Nov., 1935.

PLATT'S, SHKLLEY ARDEN.—B. 1899; ed. Bedford Schl.; passed army entrance exam.; "King's India cadetship," 1917; gazetted 2/9th Gurka Rifles; 3rd Afghan War, 1919; att'd. 2/11th Gurka Rifles and 4/50th Burma Rifles, Burma, 1921; cadet, admstive service, Tanganyika Territory, May, 1926; asst. dist. offr., July, 1928; dist. offr., 1938.
PLAYFORD, HON. THOMAS, M.P.—B. 1896; served in A.I.F. in great war; el. parl't., 1933; commr., crown lands, min. of repatriation and irrig., 1938.

PLOWMAN, CLIFFORD HENRY FITZHERBERT, C.M.G. (1937), O.B.E. (1928).—B. 1889; ed. at King's Schl., Ely and Trin. Coll., Camb.; B.A. (class. tripus); asst. dist. commr., E. Africa Prot., Mar., 1912; pol. offr., Turkana Expdn., 1915 (A.G.S. Med. with clasps); ag. offr. in charge, Northern Frontier Dist., 1919-20; seconded to F.O. as H.B.M. Vice-Consul, Harar, Abyssinia, 1920; H.B.M. Consul on transfer Harar Consulate to C.O., 1922; transf'd., Somaliland, 1922; H.B.M. chargé d'affaires, Addis Ababa, Dec., 1925 to Jan., 1926; mem., mission to Abyssinia for coronation of Emperor, 1930; 2nd cls., Star of Ethiopia; asst. commr. and pol. offr., Br. Somaliland; Ethiop. bndy. comm., 1931-33; sec., govt. of Somaliland, 1933; ag. comsnr., May-Oct., 1934; ag. gov., May-Oct., 1936 and Oct., 1937-Jan., 1938; has passed E. Africa Prot. law exam. and in Swahili, Boran Galla and Amharic languages.

PLUNKETT, JOHN OLIVER, M.I.C.E., Chtd. Civ. Engnr.—B. 1888; 2nd lieut., R.G.A., Dec., 1914; demob. with rank of capt., Sept., 1919; asst. engnr., P.W.D., F.M.S., Apr., 1920; do., S.S., Sept., 1922; exec. engnr., Nov., 1929; exec. engnr., F.M.S., Oct., 1933; senr. exec. engnr., Mar., 1936.

PLYMOUTH, 2nd Earl of (cr. 1905) and 15th Baron Windsor (cr. 1829) IYOR MILES WINDSOR-CLIVE.—B. 1889; ed. Eton and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; D.L., Worcestershire; lord-lieut., Glamorganshire, 1923; capt., hon. corps of gentlemen-at-arms, 1925; M.P., Ludlow divn., Shropshire, 1922-23; parly. under-sec. of state for Dominion affrs., Jan., 1929; kt. of grace of St. John of Jerusalem; parly. sec., min. of transport, Sept., 1931; parly. under sec. of state for the cols., 30th Sept., 1932; do., F.O., 1st, Sept., 1936.

POLE-EVANS, ILLTD.BULLE, C.M.G.(1921), M.A. (Cantab.), D.Sc. (Wales), F.L.S.—B. 1879; ed. Cowbridge Gram. Schl. and Univs. of Wales and Cambridge; mycologist, Transvaal, 1905; rep. Transvaal govt. at 4th Internat. Botanical Congress, Brussels, 1910; ch., divn. of mycology and plant pathology, 1911; ch., divn. of botany and plant pathology, 1912; sp. mission to P. E.
