African Assocn. for Advancement of Science, 1920; ch., divn. of botany, horticulture and entomology, 1927; ch., divn. of plant industry, 1929.

POLLARD, ARNOLD.—B. 1893; ed. Heles Schl., Exeter; active serv., R.N.A.S. and R.N.R., 1915-19; col. audit dept., Feb., 1920; asst. audr., Tanganyika Territory, Apr., 1920; asst. audr., Kenya, May, 1924; ag. senr. audr., audr. on several occasions in Tanganyika and Kenya; audr., Windward Is., Jan., 1926; senr. audr., Uganda, Jan., 1929; audr., Trinidad, June, 1931; do., N. Rhodesia, 1935; do., Hong Kong, 1938.

POLLOCK, CAPT. JAMES HURY HAMIL (R. of O.), O.B.E. (1939).—B. 1893; ed. Royal Schl., Armagh; war serv., 1914-20; Gallipoli, Macedonia, Egypt and Palestine; men. in despatch; staff capt., O.E.T.A.; inspr., grade 3, Palestine, July, 1920; dep. gov., grade 3, Ramallah, Oct., 1920; admstv. offr., Nigeria, Sept., 1923; asst. sec., secr., Lagos, Dec., 1927; admstv. offr., Palestine, Jan., 1930; asst. dist. commr., 1932.

POOLE, EDWARD HUMPHREY LANE.—Ed. Rugby and Balliol Coll., Oxford, B.A.; ent. B. S. A. Co.'s admstr., N. Rhodesia, 1913; asst. nat. comsnr., 1915; nat. comsnr., 1919; asst. mag., 1926; title changed to distr. offr., 1928; ag. dir., nat. educn., 1934-1935; prov. comsnr., 1936; mem. and sec., native reserves commn., E. prov., 1924-25; various publications on native history and customs.
POOLEY, CHARLES EDWARD.—B. 1903; apptd. after compet. exam., cler. offr., C.O., 22nd Oct., 1920; cler. offr., higher grade, Aug., 1933.

POPE, GEORGE WILLIAM, L.L.M.R.C.P., and S.I., D.P.H., R.C.P. and S.I.—B. 1891; med. offr., Kenya, 1920; 2nd health offr., port med. dept., Hong Kong, 1924; ag. asst. bacteriologist, 1924; asst. M.O.H., 1925; M.O.H., 1926; health offr., 1936.

POPE, LAWRENCE HENRY.—B. 1896; ed. Uppingham; served European War, 1915-19 (France and N. Russia); asst. P.S. to gov't., S. Australia, Sir W. E. E. A. Weigall, K.C.M.G., 1920; P.S. to gov't., Tasmania, Sir W. L. Allardyce, G.C.M.G., 1921; col. audit dept., 1923; asst. audr., Fiji, 1923; ag. audr., 1924-28; asst. audr., Tanganyika Territory, 1928; do., Nigeria, 1935; senr. asst. audr., 1936; audr., Fiji and W. Pacific, 1938.

POPE, STEAD.—Br. post office, Feb., 1903; dist. survr., post and tels., Gold Coast, Dec., 1913; on active serv., Togoland Field Force, Aug., 1914; and later in charge posts and tels., Togo land to May, 1915; imp. army serv., Sept., 1915-19; lieut., R.E. and R.A.F.; dist. survr., Gold Coast, June, 1919; div. survr., Jan., 1922; P.M.G., Nyassaland, Mar., 1930; rep. Nyassaland air mail confce., Salisbury, 1934; African post and telecommunications confce., Pretoria, 1935; and confce. re interlocking Cent. African services, Salisbury, 1936; P.M.G., Gold Coast, Jan., 1937.

POPHAM, SIR HENRY BRADSHAW, K.C.M.G. (1938), C.M.G. (1935), M.B.E. (1918).—B. 1881; ed. Tombride Schl.; coman., 13th Light Inf'y, 1900; served, S. African War (2 med.); seconded, G. Coast regt., W.A.F.F., 1906; priv. sec. and A.D.C. to ag. gov'r., G. Coast, Mar. to Aug., 1909; col. sec.'s office, Jan., 1910; priv. sec. to ag. gov'r., Feb. to June, 1911; ag. ch. ast. col. sec. and clk. of couns., July to Aug., 1913; ag. senr. ast. col. sec., Oct., 1913; dist. comanr., Jan., 1914; seconded to Togoland polit. admstr., Nov., 1914; senr. ast. col. sec. and clk. of couns., Jan., 1920; ag. ch. ast. col. sec., Sept., 1920 to Feb., 1921; dep. prov. comsnr. and ag. comsnr., W. Prov., Feb., 1921; prov. comsnr., Apr., 1922; ret'd. (invalided) May, 1923; comanr., Cyprus, Oct., 1925; admstr., Dominica, 1933; mem., exec. coun., Leeward Is., 1933; gov. and commdr.-in-ch., Windward Is., 1937.

POPHAM, AIR CHIEF MARSHALL SIR HENRY ROBERT MOOR BROOKE, G.C.V.O. (1935), K.C.B. (1927), C.B. (1919), C.M.G. (1918), D.S.O. (1915)—B. 1878; ed Haileybury, Sandhurst and Staff Coll.; ent. Army, 1898; capt., 1904; R.F.C., 1912; ht. maj., 1913; maj., 1916; R.A.F., 1919; served European War, 1914-19; commdt. R.A.F. Staff Coll., 1921-26; air offr. commdg. fighting area air defences, Gt. Britain, 1926-28; air offr., commdg. Iraq, commd., 1928-30; commd., Imp. Defence Coll., 1931-33; air offr. commdg.in-ch. air defence, Gt. Britain, 1933-35; prin. air A.D.C. to the King since 1933; I.G., R.A.F., 1935-36; gov. and commdr.-in-ch., Kenya and high commd. for transport, 1936.
POPPLEWELL, GEOFFREY DOUGLAS, R.A. (Oxon.).—B. 1904; cadet, provincial adminstr., Tanganyika Territory, April, 1927; asst. dist. offr., May, 1929; ag. dist. offr., various periods, 1931-35.
PORT, FRANCIS GEORGE RICHARD.—B. 1892; ed. Truro Grammar Schl.; asst. acctnt., Nigeria rly. open lines, 1920; acctnt., 1928; personal asst. to gen. man., 1936; prin. admstv. asst., Apr., 1938.

PORTELLY, JOHN, M.D., D.P.H., D.T.M. and H. (Eng.), Certif. Lond. Schl. of Hygiene and T.M.—B. 1889; ed. govt. secondary schl. and Univ. Malta; med. offr., gen. hosp., Penang, S.S., Mar., 1922; med. offr., S. Kedah, and ast. supt. prisons, in addn., Jan., 1925; med. offr., in ch. gen. hosp., J. Bahr, Apr., 1927; port health offr., S'pore, Feb., 1929; ag. senr. health offr., Penang, Apr., 1929; offr. rep. on L. of N. Interchange Tour for port health offr. in the East, June, 1930; ag. senr. health offr., Johore, Sept., 1932. agiar.-gen., births and deaths, Johore, Oct., 1932; upperscale offr., grade A, Nov., 1936.
PORTER, GEORGE EDWARD JOSEPH, M.R.C.S. Engl., L.R.C.P.—B. 1909; ed. Flores Coll., Valletta, Malta, Douai Sch., Woolworth and t. Mary’s Hosp., Paddington; D.M.O., Cyprus, ‘eb., 1938.
PORTER, REGINALD VERNON HERBERT—B. 900; ed. Choltenham and Wadham Coll., Oxford, B.A. forestry, Indian Army, 1918-22; astt. conserv., forests, Kenya, Oct., 1929.
PORTER, ROBERT ALEXANDER, M.A., M.B., Ch., B.A.O.—B. 1906; ed. Foyle Coll., Londonderry, Trinity Coll., Dublin, Queen’s univ., Belfast, and R.C.S., Edin.; D.M.O., Cyprus, Nov., 1936.

POSTMUS, JOHANNES.—B. 1877; ed at Sneek, Holland; gen. man., Netherlands Bank of S.A., 1920-26; dep.-gov., S. African Reserve Bank, 1927-31; gov., S. African Reserve Bank, Jan., 1932; World Econ. Conf., London, 1933.

POTT, GIDEON ELLIOT.—B. 1909; ed. St. Andrew’s Grahamstown, Edinburgh Acad. and Trinity Hall, Cambridge; grade II olk., Basutoland, 1935; astt. dist. commr. (cadet), 1937; registr., res. commr.’s ot., 1937; astt. dist. commr., 1937.

POTTINGER, JOSEPH HECTOR, M.B., Ch.B., D.P.H., D.T.M. & H. (L’pool).—B. 1898; on war serv., 1915-18; med. offr., health, W. African med. staff, Gold Coast, 1928; astt. gov’t. med. offr. of health, Br. Guiana, 1932; senr. med. offr., Palestine, 1935.

POTTS, JOHN WILFRED, I.S.O. (1937).—B. 1832; served in Bechuanaland Prot. pol., 1912-15; clk., 1915; ag. astt. res. mag., 1920; ag. res. mag., 1920 and 1922-23; passd civ. ser. lower law exam., 1919; passed 2nd grade Seawans exam., 1922; sub. inspr., Bech. Prot. pol., 1924; inaprr., Mar., 1927; res. mag., Apr., 1929; dist. commr., 1937.

POULIER, REGINALD SYDNEY VERNON.—B. 1894; B.A., Lond.; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Aug., 1919; pol. mag., Matale, Feb., 1923; addnl. dist. judge, Mannar and Mullaitivu, Oct., 1924; addnl. astt. gov’t. agt., Colombo, Dec., 1925; commr., requests, Kandy, Oct., 1927; astt. gov’t. agt., Mullaitivu, Nov., 1928; astt. gov’t. agt., Colombo, Oct., 1931; do., Mannar, Oct., 1934; do., Kegalla, Aug., 1936; ag. gov’t. agt., Prov. of Sabaragamawwa, Apr., 1937.

POULTON, ARTHUR STANLEY.—B. 1900; ed. R. Mily. Acad., Woolwich; R.A., 1918; Iraq campaign, 1920; Somaliland Camel Corps (K.A.R.), 1927; commr., 3rd grade, Br. Somaliland, 1931; astt. dist. offr., 1932.

POULTON, WILFRED FRANK, C.B.E. (1935).—B. 1888; ed. Bishop’s Stortford Coll. and Royal Vety. Coll., London; vety. offr., Uganda, Jan., 1914; war serv. in E. Africa, 1914-17; dist., vety. services, Uganda, Feb., 1925.

POUSSETTE, HENRY RIVINGTON.—B. 1872; ed. Sarnia Pub. Sch., Upper Can. Coll., Royal Mily. Coll., Kingston, and Sch. of Prac. Science, Toronto; capt., S. African Constab., 1901, and thereafter in same corps throughout S. African war; dist. commr., and pub. pros., S. Africa; ent. serv. dept. of trade and commerce, Can., as trade commr., Durban, S. Africa, 1909; trade commr., Buenos Aires, 1910; enlisted for activeserv., 1914; returned Can., 1919; inspr., trade commr. serv.; sp. trade commr., India, 1919; dir., comml. intelligence serv., Ottawa, 1921; trade commr., Trinidad, 1924.
POWELL, HARRY CRESCWELL, A.M.Inst.T.—B. 1881; mily. serv., France, 1915-19; traffic supt., Jamaica govt. rly., Jan., 1921; dir., do., Oct., 1930.

POWER, HON. CHARLES GAVAN, K.C., B.A., LL.L. (Quebec South).—B. 1888; ed. in Loyola Coll., Montreal; grad., 1907; B.A., LL.L., Laval Univ.; called to bar, 1910; enlisted Jan., 1915; served overseas, twice wounded, invalided from serv., 1918; capt. and ag. major, M.C.; el. to H. of C., Canada, Dec., 1917; re-el., g.e., 1921, 1925, 1926, 1930 and 1935; min. of pensions and nat. health, Mackenzie King’s cabinet, Oct., 1935.

POWER, FRANCIS WILLIAM.—Ed. Stonehurst Coll.; Indian Army, 1917; seconded, I.C.S. as polit. res., Perim Is., 1922-23; col. audit dept., Apr., 1924; astt. audr., Gold Coast, June, 1924; ag. dep. audr., July, 1928; audr., Windward Is., Feb., 1929; senr. astt. audr., N. Rhodesia, 1936; ag. audr., Oct., 1937-Sept., 1938.
POWER, GERALD, B.A.—B. 1891; ed. Denstone Coll. and Queen’s Coll., Oxford; supt., educn., N. Nigeria, Jan., 1914; astt. dir., educn., Gold Coast, May, 1920; dir., ditto, Feb., 1933.
POWERS, HON. SIR CHARLES, K.C.M.G., (1929).—Justice of the high ct. of Australia, 1913; ret.

POYTON, ARTHUR HILTON.—B. 1908; schol. of Marlborough Coll. (1918-23) and Brasenose Coll., Oxford (1923-7); 1st cls. honours, moda., 1925, 2nd cls. hon., lit. hum., 1927; M.A., 1933; apptd. astt. prin., dept. of scientific and industrial research after compet. exam., 31st Oct., 1927; transf. to C.O., 1st Oct., 1929; joint sec., Col. Development Advisory Commt., 1st Apr. to 30th Sept., 1930; sec., do., 1st April, 1932; pte. sec. to partly. under sec. for cols. (Sir R. Hamilton), 18th April, 1932 and to (Lord Plymouth), 30th Sept., 1932; seconded to Sierra Leone adminstr., Apr., 1933-35; prin., D.O., 9th Sept., 1933; do., O.O., 4th Nov., 1935.

POYSER, KENNETH ELLISTON, D.S.O., K.C. (Barbados).—B. 1882; ed. Shrewsbury and Merton Coll., Oxford; called to bar, Inner Temple, 1906; served, European War, 1914-20, Yorks. L.I., and Loyol N. Lanc. Regt. (deeps thrice, D.S.O., 1917); puisne judge, Leeward Islands, Sept., 1920; atty.-gen., Barbados, June, 1925; atty.-gen., Uganda, 1928; puisne just., Ceylon, 1933; ch. just., F.M.S., 1938.

PRAIN, LT.-COL. SIR DAVID, KT. BACH. (1912): C.M.G. (1912); C.I.E. (1906); M.A., M.B., LL.D., F.R.S. E., F.L.S., F.R.S.—B. 1857; ed. Aberdeen gram. schl. and Univ. of Aberdeen and Edinburgh; demonstrator of anatomy, coll. of surgeons, Edin., 1882-3, and univ. of Aberdeen, 1883-1884; entd. Indian med. serv., 1884; curator, Calcutta herbarium, 1887-1898; prof. of botany, med. coll., Calcutta, 1896-1905; director, botanical survey of India, and astt. of royal botanic garden, Calcutta, 1898-1906; trustee of the Indian Museum, 1898-1904; sec. bd. of scientific advice for India, 1903-1904; mem. of coun. of various scientific societies; director of royal botanic gardens, Kew, 1905; ret., 1922.

PRANKERD, HORACE ARTHUR, B.A.(Cantab.).—B. 1883; ed. at Ipswich and Queen’s Coll., Cambridge (scholar, 2nd class, tripes), B.A., 1905; baccalaureat (B.L. 1st part) Lyons Univ., France, 1908; astt. sec., N. Nigeria, Oct., 1910; astt. res., 1911; ag. astt. schla., July, 1912 to Apr., 1913; called to bar, Gray’s Inn, Apr., 1921; promoted cls. I, grade I, admstive serv., Feb., 1928.

PRASAD, MUBARI, B.Sc. (Allahabad), Barrister-at-law, Middle Temple—B. 1888; cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., Dec., 1912; addtl. office asst. to govt. agt., N.C. Prov., Aug., 1914; addtl. dist. judge, Anuradhapura, Aug., 1915; office asst. to govt. agt., N.C. Prov., Dec., 1915; pol. mag., Chilaw, Apr., 1917; ditto, Panadure, Apr., 1919; office asst. Jaffna kach., Mar., 1920; dist. judge, Anuradhapura, May, 1921; pol. mag.; Kalutara, May, 1922; ditto, Gampola, Mar., 1923; dist. judge, Tangalla, Oct., 1924; dep. fiscal, Colombo, Feb., 1926; dist. judge, Matara, June, 1927; comsrr. stamps, July, 1932; dist. judge, Kurunegala, Oct., 1933; asst. govt. agt., Kalutara, Oct., 1935; govt. agt., E.P., Mar., 1936.

PREEDY, CAPT. BERNARD, M.C.—B. 1894; ed. Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford; M.A. (Oxon.); European mast., Taiping, Sept., 1922; headmast., gov't. English schl., Kajang, Oct., 1927; do., Clifford sch., K. Kangsar, Feb., 1930.

PRENDERGAST, HON. JAMES EMILE PIERRE.—Ed. at Seminary of Quebec, and Laval Univ., Quebec; B.A., 1878; LL.B., 1881; returned for Le Verandrye, Manitoba, Aug., 1885; again Dec., 1886; on formation of cabinet by Hon. Thomas Greenway, Jan., 1888, was given portfolio of prov. sec.; re-elected in said constituency; elected for Woodlands, 1888; judge of sup. ct., N.W.T., 1902; ditto, Saskatchewan, 1907; puisne judge, court of King's Bench of Manitoba, 1910; ch. just., Dec., 1929.

PRENTIS, JOHN BERNARD.—B. 1904; ed. Epsom Coll. and Guy's Hosp.; admitted solr., sup. ct. of judicature, Eng., 1932; priv. prac. Hong Kong, 1932-35; junr. legal offr., Hong Kong, July, 1935; J.P., 1935; ag. ast. land offr. and dep. regtr., marriages, July, 1935; ast. cr. solr., Dec., 1935; ag. ast. land offr. and dep. regtr., marriages in addn., Jan., 1936; ag. offr. reor. and regtr. trade marks and letters pat., Mar., 1936; ag. dep. regtr., sup. ct., Aug., 1938.
PRESCOTT, ARTHUR STANLEY.—Apptd. after compet. exam., supplementary clk., supreme ct., Gibraltar, 1896; 3rd cls. clk., 1899; ast. regtr. and deputy clk. of arraigna, 1911.

PREITHEROR, EDWARD OWEN, M.C., B.A.; LL.B.—B. 1896; called to bar, Gray's Inn, 1921; admstr. offr., 1922; ast. commr., lands, 1924; ag. commr., lands, 1924; ast. sec., S. Provs., 1924; pol. mag., 1928; ag. ch. regtr., sup. ct. on various occasions; crown coun., 1930; ag. solr. gen. on various occasions.

PRETORIUS, ABRAHAM JOHANNES LODEWIJKUS, D.T.D., M.C., Croix du Guerre, A.R.S.M.—B. 1884; ed. Grey Coll., Bloemfontein; R. Schol. of Mines, London; Anglo-Boer war, 1899-1902; commdt. gen.'s staff; Great War, capt. and adjt., 3rd Mounted Brig., 1914-15 (German S.W. Africa); capt. 2nd mounted brig., German E. Africa, 1916-17; Union of S. Africa civ. serv.; insp. mines, 1912-32; depy. govt. mining engr., 1933-37; chmn., miner's phthisis prevention comtee, 1933-37; chmn., hl. of directors., govt. miners' training schlk., 1937.

PRETORIUS, Jan Lodewyk.—B. 1878; ed. at Grey Coll., Bloemfontein; joined O.P.S. repub. civ. serv., Mar., 1897; ast. mag., Edenburg, 1902; Vrede, 1904; Lindley, 1908; det. ast. mag., Dewetsdorp, Feb., 1910 to Oct., 1913; Reitz, Nov., 1913; mag., Reitz, 1918; Hoopstad, Feb., 1919; Boshof, Oct., 1919; Frankfurt, Oct., 1923; Senekal, June, 1929; Vereeniging, Feb., 1930; Lichtenburg, Mar., 1933; Port Elizabeth, Feb., 1934; Pretoria, Mar., 1936.

PRETTY, ERIC ERNEST FALK.—B. 1891; ed. Harrow, and Demy of Magdalen Coll., Oxford; cadet, S.S., Nov., 1914; passed cadet, June, 1917; ag. collr. of land revenue, Johore Bahru, Sept., 1917; offr., cls. V., S.S., Dec., 1917; 2nd lieut. and ast. adjt., J.V.R., May, 1918; supernny. offr., cls. IV., Nov., 1921; Br. res., Brunel, Mar., 1923; offr., Cls. III., Nov., 1926; dist. offr., Krian, June, 1928; offr., cls. II., Nov., 1931; ag. under-secr. to govt., F.M.S., Apr., 1937; offr. cls. I.B., Apr., 1937; fin. comsrr., and aud. gen., Johore, Dec., 1937.

PRICE, CHARLES ROY.—B. 1893; ed. Welling ton Schl., Somerset and University Coll., London; B.A. (war), 1919; unattached list, Territorial Force, Jan. to Aug., 1915; Royal Garrison Arty., Sept., 1915 to Dec., 1918; served in France, June, 1916 to Sept., 1918; apptd. under reconstruction scheme, ast. prin., C.O., 1st Mar., 1921; pvt. sec. to perm. under sec. for dom. affrs., 6th July, 1927 (ag. from 6th June, 1927); temp. ag. prin., D.O., 22nd July, 1927; pvt. sec. to partly. under sec. for dom. affrs., Jan., 1928; ast. sec., Imp. wireless and cable confce., Jan.-July, 1928; ag. prin., D.O., 15th Sept., 1929; U.K. delegn. to L. of N. assembly, 1928 and 1932; U.K. delegn. to London naval confce., 1930; attd. to sec., Imp. confce., 1930; U.K. delegn. disarmament confce., Geneva, Feb.-Dec., 1933.

PRICE, REGINALD WILLIAM.—B. 1892; ed. Newport High Schl.; war serv., R. Gloucesters Hussars Yeomanry and R.F.A., 1914-19, in Egypt, Gallipoli, Mesopotamia and Palestine; audit offr., min. of finance, Iraq, 1920; acctnt., P.W.D. Tanganyika Territory, 1925; dep. ch. acctnt., 1926; ch. acctnt., 1935; ch. acctng. offr., P.W.D., Palestine, 1938.
PRICE, THOMAS JOHN, B.Sc., Dip. Ed. (Sheffield)—B. 1900; ast. mast., Hong Kong educn. dept., 1928.

PRICE-JONES, CAPT. WILLIAM.—Served during Great War in France; awarded M.C., 1917; ast. dist. comsrr., Gold Coast, Aug., 1919; dist. comsrr., Apr., 1924; called to bar, Gray's Inn, Nov., 1926; pol. mag., 1928; ag. puisne judge, sup. ct., Sept.—Oct., 1933; ag. ch. regtr. and sheriff, various occasions, 1933-37.

PRIESTMAN, HAROLD EDDEY, C.M.G. (1937), B.A. (Dublin).—B. 1888; mil. serv., 1914-19; temp. admin. offr., min. of lab., 1919; ast. dist. offr., Nigeria, 1921; attched. to C.O., 1930; seconded as ag. col. sec. and senr. ast. col. sec., Gambia, 1931; resumed duties, C.O., 1932; resumed substantive appt., Nigeria, 1933; admin. sec. to high comn. for Basutoland, Bech. Prot. and Swaziland, 1935.

PRING, HARRY.—B.1883; ed. Watford Schl. and King's Coll., London; Imp. serv., 1898-1946; col. sec.'s office, Capetown, 1903; dept. of interior, Pretoria, 1910; ch. clk., 1925; under sec., 1927; pub. serv. comsrr., 1931.

PRINGLE, LEONARD DUNCAN, M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P.—B.1905; med. offr., F.M.S., 1931; Hosp. Korg, 1932; ag. radiologist, Jan.-Aug., 1933; M.O. in ch. of officers' families, Victoria Gen. infec. diseases hosp., Victoria mortuary and remand home, 1934; med. offr. in ch., civ. hosp., 1935; med. offr., Kowloon hosp., Apr., 1936; ag. radiologist, Apr., 1937.
PRITCHARD, NEIL.—B. 1911; ed. Liverpool Coll. and Worcester Coll., Oxford (hist. schl., 1929-32); hist. schol., 1st cls.: B.A., 1933; apptd., after compet. exam., ast. prin., D.O., Oct., 1933.

PROUT, SIR WILLIAM THOMAS, K.C.M.G. (1928), KT. Bach. (1924), C.M.G. (1903), O.B.E., M.B., C.M. (Edin.)—Ast. poor law med. offr., Mauritius, 1885; surg. supt., immigrant ship "Dundee," 1887; asst. col. surg., Gold Coast, 1888; dist. comsnnr., Gold Coast, 1890; col. surg., Gambia, 1893; ag. chief mag., Gambia, 1893; med. offr. in charge of base hosp. during campaign against Fodav Sila; col. surg., S. Leone, 1895, title altered to med. offr., 1901; retired, 1906; fellow of roy. med. soc., Edin., and corrpdng. mem. of the epidemiological soc. of Lond.; lect. schl. of trop. med., Univ. of Liverpool; med. adviser to C.O.; mem. of advisory, med. and sanitary comttee. for Tropical Africa, C.O.; senr. med. adviser to C.O., 1924; author of article "Yaws" in "Diseases of Warm Climates" (Davidson); Fil. Voloulous (Archives de Parasitologie, May, 1901); "Filariasis in S. Leone" (Brit. Med. Journal, 1902); and other papers.

PRYER, JOHN HAWKES.—B. 1894; ed. Bedford Harpur Trust; mech. transport, B.E.F., France, 1915-18; cadet pilot, R.A.F., 1918-19; asst. storekeeper, Gold Coast rly., Sept., 1920; asst. ch. do., Aug., 1929; ch. do., Mar., 1932.
PUCKERIN, MERVYN ALLYNNE.—B. 1891; entd. civ. serv., Barbados, 1909; war serv., 1916-19; supt., govt. indust. schls., 1924; sov., Glendairy prison, 1932; supt., prisons, Trinidad, 1938.

PUCKRIDGE, GEOFFREY MARTIN.—B. 1896; ed. Exeter Schl.; 2nd lieut., Devon Regt., T.F., Dec., 1913; capt., Dec., 1914; served in France with 5th West Yorks regt.; seconded to Royal Flying Corps, July, 1917; seconded to Inter Allied Aeronautical Comn. of Control, Germany, 1920; resigned comn., Sept., 1921; asst. dist. comsnnr., Gold Coast, 1921; dist. comsnnr., 1925; asst. col. sec., 1928.

PUDNEY, ERIC WILLIAM, A.C.A.—B. 1903; asst. acct., Gold Coast, Dec., 1930; asst. treas., Apr., 1934; ag. dep. treas., June-Sept., 1936; asst. treas., Mauritius, Oct., 1936; ag. treas., Feb.-Oct., 1938.

PUDSEY, FAWCETT.—B. 1882; Assoc. M.I.M.E.; Assoc. M.I.C.E.; ed. Hymer's Coll., Hull, and Repton Schl.; apprentice with Messrs. Rose, Downs and Thompson, Ltd., Hull, 1900-1905; engineering asst., dock engrn.'s staff, N.E. Rly., 1905-1911; engaged on survey work, Catalonia, Spain, for the Pearson Engineering Corporation, New York, 1911; in charge of opening of the Hesse quarry and crushing plant of the British Portland Cement Manufacturers, Ltd., 1912-1914; asst. D.P.W., B. Guiana, 1914; ag. D.P.W., Feb., 1916; D.P.W., Palestine, Dec., 1927.

PULLICINO, SIR PHILIP, KT. Bach (1934), B.Litt., LL.D.—B. 1888; ed. St. Ignatius' Coll., Malta; matric., Malta Univ., 1900; B.Litt., 1903; LL.D., 1907; advoc. for poor, 1918; judge, 1923; pub. pros. and treasy. coun., 1929; atty.-gen., 1937.

PULLON, BETRAN.—B. 1882; asst. printer, N. Rhodesia, 1921; ag. govt. printer, 1924-25; asst. govt. printer, 1925; ag. govt. printer for various periods during 1927-29, 1931 and 1932; govt. printer, 1932.

PURCELL, HUBERT KENNEDY, O.B.E. (1937).—B. 1884; ent. Crown Agents office, Oct., 1901; asst. cashier, July, 1914; head of pay dept., Dec., 1920; dep. head of pay and checking dept., Nov., 1921; dep. head, pay dept., 1924; dep. head, gen. dept. and dep. ch. clk., 1930; dep. head, gen. stores dept., Jan., 1933; head, pay dept., May, 1933; head, gen. dept. and ch. clk., Nov., 1933.

PURCELL, VICTOR WILLIAM WILLIAMS SAUNDERS.—B. 1896; ed. Bancroft's Schl. and Trinity Coll., Camb.; B.E.F., France, 1915-18; cadet, F.M.S., Apr., 1921; attd., Chinese prot., K. Lumpur; passed cadet, July, 1924; offr., cls. V, July, 1923; inspr., Chinese schls., S.S., and asst. dir., eduon., June, 1924; asst. prot., Chinese, Penang, July, 1925; dep. regiar., sup. ct., Sept., 1926; offr., cls. IV, July, 1927, dep. regiar. and asst. offr., assignee; 2nd asst. dir., eduon. (Chinese), S.S., Aug., 1930; offr., cls. III, July, 1932; mag., Seremban, July, 1934; offr., cls. II, July, 1937.

PURLL, FREDERICK JOHN.—B. 1891; conf. shorthand writer, B. of T., Aug., 1909; conf. clk. to gen. man., lab. exchange dept., B. of T., Oct., 1909; conf. shorthand writer to S. of S., H.O., Jan., 1911; cler. offr., 21st Jan., 1925; accompanied the Rt. Hon. L.S. Amery, M.P. (S. of S. for Dominion Affairs) on his tour of the Dominions July, 1927-Feb., 1928; transferred to C.O., June, 1928; accompanied Br. economic mission to Australia, Aug., 1928-Feb., 1929; seconded for serv. with Newfoundland R. Coman., Mar.-Oct., 1933; seconded for serv. with Palestine R. Coman., Nov., 1936-June, 1937; seconded as office asst., secretariat, Cyprus, 1938.

PURVES, ALEXANDER BRUCE, M.I.C.E.—B. 1890; ed. Kirkcaldy and George Watson's Coll., Edin.; survr., P.W.D., Hong Kong, 1914; asst. engrn., Tytam Tuk scheme, 1915; asst. water engrn., 1916; engrn., Shing Mun Valley scheme, in addn., 1923-29; dep. water engrn., 1925; exec. engrn. in ch. waterwks., 1930.

PUTTICK, LEONARD JAMES.—B. 1888; ed. at High Schl. and Huguenot Coll., Wellington and S. African Coll., Cape Town; served Anglo-Boer war, 1900-01; draughtsman, Swasiland, 1910; elk. of lands, 1916; ch. clk., 1919; ag. regiar. and mast., sp. ct. and sheriff, 1919; sec., Swasiland-Portuguese bndry. coman., 1922; sec., undeveloped minerals coman., 1927; ag. dept. asst. comntr., 1925 and 1930; beacon inspr., 1927; ag. govt. sec. in 1927 and 1928; asst. govt. sec., 1928; regiar. and mast. sp. ct., sheriff, regiar. births, marriages and deaths and comntr., protocols, 1932; ag. govt. sec. and finan. sec., Mar.-Sept., 1936.

QUASHIE-IDUN, SAMUEL OKAI, M.A. (Cantab.).—B. 1902; ed. Mfantsipim Schl., Gold Coast and Selwyn Coll., Cambridge; called to bar, Inner Temple, Jan., 1927; practised before sup. ct., Gold Coast, Apr., 1927 to Feb., 1936; dist. mag., Gold Coast, Feb., 1936; ag. ch. regiar. on various occasions.

QUIGLBY, EUGENE PATRICK, M.C.—B. 1889; served six years R.I.C.; joined army, 1914; 2nd lieut., R. Dublin Fusiliers, 1915; served in France, Egypt and Palestine; organised pol., C.I.D., Palestine, 1919; D.C.P., grade I, 1921; dist. supt., pol., Apr., 1926; dep. comdt., C.I.D., 1930.

RAE, SIR JAMES STANLEY, KT. Bach (1933)—B. 1881; articled and conf. clk. to Sir W. R. Davies, Kt., when atty.-gen., Bahamas; called to the bar, Bahamas, Apr., 1904; called to the bar, Middle Temple, Jan., 1919; ag. coroner for the Is. of New Providence from 1904, confirmed in 1911; J.P., 1910; ag. atty.-gen., Jan., 1910; ag. stip. and circ. mag., 1910-11, and on other occasions; dist. comntr., Belize, B. Honduras, 1914; offr. rec. in bankruptcy, offr. admstr. and J.P. for the col., 1914; regiar.-gen., Nov., 1915, in conjunction with duties as dist. comntr.; dir. of P.O.G. fund, 1916; custodian of enemy property, Sept., 1916; press censor during war; pvt., B. Hond. Territorial Force; attd. to staff as mily. censor; atty.-gen., St. Vincent, Apr., 1919; cross of Chevalier of the Order of the Crown, Belgium, for relief services during war, May, 1919; ag. ch. just., St. Vincent, June to Oct., 1919; atty.-gen., St. Lucia, Sept., 1920; ag. ch. just., June, 1921 to Feb., 1922 and from Sept., 1922 to June, 1923; ch. just., St. Vincent, June, 1923; ag. admstr., June, 1926 to Feb., 1927; ch. just., Grenada, 1927; revised and consolidated laws, 1926; ch. just., Leeward Is., Apr., 1931; ret. 1938.
RAE, NORMAN.—B. 1892; entd. Crown Agent's office, May, 1911; asst. head of dept., May, 1919; dep. head, stores dept., Oct., 1932; dep. ch. clk., July, 1935.

RAEBURN, COLIN, O.B.E. (1934), D.Sc., F.G.S.—B. 1894; ed. Hamilton Acad. and Glasgow Univ.; lieut., Cameron Highlanders, 1915-19; Salonika, 1915-18; geologist, Scottish Spitzbergen syndicate, 1919; geologist, geo. survey, Nigeria, 1920-29; asst. dir., 1929; seconded Cyprus, 1937; author of "Alluvial Prospecting."

RAIKES, H. R.—Ed. Tonbridge Sch., Dulwich Coll. and Balliol Coll., Oxford (Williams exhib.), Abbot Schol., 1911, final hon. schl. of nat. science (chem.), class I, 1914; B.A., 1914; M.A., 1919; served European War, 1st Batt., The Buffs, R.F.C. and R.A.F., 1914-1919 (A.F.C.); fellow, Exeter Coll., Oxford, 1919-27; sub-rector, 1924-27; demonstr. and treas., Balliol and Trinity Coll. laboratory, 1920-27; ch. instr., Oxford Univ. Air Squadron, 1925-27; wing commander, R.A.F.O. since 1926; prin., Univ. of Witwatersrand, Johannesburg, 1928; publs.—papers in Jnl. of Chem. Soc., and Faraday Soc.
RAINFORD, HUME FREDERICK.—B. 1902; ed. Dublin Univ.; B.A. and hons. B.A.I., civ. engnr., 1925; staff survr., Tanganyika Territory, 1927; computer, survey dept., Uganda, 1937.

RAJA, KAMARALAMAN IBNI RAJA MANSUR.—B. 1892; sthnt. offr., Seremban, June, 1910; offr. cl. V, Jan., 1927; offr. cl. IV, Jan., 1931; dist. offr., Krian, Aug., 1934; offr., cl. III, Jan., 1936; state treas., Perak, Oct., 1937.

RAJA MUSA, BIN RAJA HAJI BOT, Barrister-at-law (Inner Temple).—B. 1897; Malay probr., Oct., 1910; Malay asst., grade III, July, 1914; do., grade II, July, 1917; Malay offr., grade I, July, 1920; asst. dist. offr., U. Langat, Nov., 1921; offr. cls. V, Jan., 1924; offr. cls. IV, Jan., 1928; offr. cls. III, Jan., 1933; on ap. duty, England, 1936; registr., sup. ct. K. Lumpur, Apr., 1937; ag. judge, sup. ct., F.M.S., Mar., 1938.

RAJA SALIM, BIN RAJA MOHAMED YUSUF.—B. 1890; ed. Malay Coll., K. Kangsar; sthnt. offr., F.M.S., Dec., 1907; Malay offr., grade II, Jan., 1913; do., grade I, July, 1915; offr. cls. V, M.C.S., Oct., 1929; asst. dist. offr., B. Gajah, Sept., 1939; offr. cls. IV., Oct., 1933; mag., Taiping, Nov., 1937; offr., cls. III., Oct., 1938.

RAJA UDA, BIN RAJA MUHAMMAD.—B.1894; Malay probr., Oct., 1910; Malay asst., C.S.O., grade III, Sept., 1913; do., grade II, July, 1917; Malay offr., grade I, July, 1920; offr. cls. V, asst. dist. offr., Kuantan, Jan., 1924; offr. cls. IV, Jan., 1928; dep. chmn., sany. bd., K. Lumpur, Sept., 1930; offr. cls. III, Jan., 1933; dist. offr., K. Selangor, Mar., 1933; collr., land rev., K. Lumpur, Aug., 1937, and dist. offr., Ulu Langat in addn.

RAJENDRA, MURUGRYSSEN, B.A. (hons.) Lond.—B. 1911; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv. Dec., 1934; office asst., Hambantota kach., Apr., 1935; att office of dir., med. and sany. services, and asst. sec. to min. for health, June, 1936; do. and sec. to do., June, 1937.
RAKE, EUSTACK OLPHERTS.—B. 1886; ed. Bradfield and Pembroke Coll., Cambridge, B.A.; asst. dist. commr., Gold Coast, Jan., 1914; prov. commr., 1930.

RALSTON, JAMES.—B. 1890; M.A. (Glas.), 1911; asst. mast., educn. dept., Hong Kong, 1914; seconded for mil. serv., 1918-19; headmast., Indian Sch., Hong Kong, 1925; 2nd mast., King's Coll., 1928; headmast., Ellis Kadodoo Sch., 1929 and 1933; ditto, Wontai Sch., 1930; external examr. in method, Hong Kong Univ., 1930; ag. inspr., schls., 1931 and 1935; offr., J.P., 1932; headmr., Cent. Br. Sch., 1934; inspr., Eng. schls., 1936; dir., evening inst., 1936.

RAMAGE, RICHARD OGILVY.—B. 1896; ed. Edinburgh Acad.; R.E. 1914-19 (capt. T.A.R. of O.); admstv. offr., cls. IV, 1920; polit. offr., anti-tsetse duties, 1926; asst. sec., Nigerian secretariat, 1929; clk. to exec. and leg. couns., 1930-32; prte. sec. to O.A.G., and to gov., 1931; ag. prin. asst. sec., Nigerian secretariat, 1932 and 1933; ag. prin. asst. sec., S. Prov., do., 1933-34; ag. sec., S. Prov., 1934-35; admstv.serv., cls. II, 1934; personal asst. to lieut.-gov., Malta, 1936; clk., exec. coun., 1936; nom. mem. (temp.), exec. coun., 1936; title changed to asst. to lieut.-gov., Malta, 1938; nom. off. mem. exec. coun. 1938; ohmn. comtece. on finances of Malta, 1938.

RAMBAUT, ARTHUR EDMUND, B.A.—B.1885; ed. Magd. Coll. Sch., and B.N.C., 1920; 2nd cls. math. moda., 1905; 4th cls. bot., 1908; dipl. forestry, 1909; asst. con. forests, F.M.S., Nov., 1909; on mil. duty; 2nd lieut. North. Fus. (T.F.), 1916; lieut., 1916; temp. capt. 1916; served in France; intel. corps., 1917 (France, Belgium, Germany); rtnd. to F.M.S., Aug., 1919; dep. conserv., forests, Perak, South, Nov., 1926; conserv., forests, Jan., 1930; do., Johore, July, 1936.
RAMMELL, JAMES CUTHBERT.—B. 1894; ed. Worcester and Worcester Coll., Oxford, M.A.; dipl. forestry; war. serv., 1915-19; asst. conserv., forests, Kenya, 1920; sr. asst. do., Sept., 1929.

RANASINHA, ARTHUR GODWIN, B.A. (Lond.)—B. 1898; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Jan., 1921; attd. to Kegalla Kach., Mar., 1921; attd., Jaffna kach., Oct., 1922; pol. mag., Pt. Pedro, Mar., 1923; ditto, Balapitiva, Nov., 1923; ditto, Jaffna, June, 1926; dist. judge, Avisawella, Apr., 1928; ditto, Badulla, Jan., 1930; sec. to min. for agr. and lands, June, 1933; pub. trustee, Nov., 1936.

RANKINE, ADAM, M.C., M.B., Ch.B. (Glas.) D.P.H., Major, R.A.M.C.T.—B. 1891; prim. med. offr., Palestine, 1920; senr. med. offr., 1922; dep. dir., med. services, 1932 & 1934; dep. surg. gen., Trinidad, Apr., 1935; dir., med. services, Mar., 1936.

RANKINE, SIR RICHARD SIMS DONKIN, K.C.M.G. (1932), C.M.G. (1919).—B. 1875; clk. treasury, Fiji, Feb., 1894; priv. sec. to Sir George O'Brien, Sept., 1897 to 1901; acted clk. to ex. coun. Sept., 1899 to Mar., 1900; appld. acct. native taxes, treas., 1901; priv. sec. to Sir Henry Jackson, July, 1902; priv. sec. to Sir E. im Thurn, Oct., 1904; conf. sec., Jan., 1905; sec. to consul-gen. for W. Pacific; clk. of exec. coun.; ag. sec., W. Pacific high comm., June, 1905 to June, 1906, and from Nov., 1907 to Dec., 1908; asst. col. sec. and asst. recvr.-gen., Jan., 1909; recvr.-gen. and commr. of stamps, pres. of marine bd., mem. exec. and legis. coun., 1910; ag. col. sec., Nov., 1913 to Nov., 1914; gov'r's dep. in, June, 1914: commr. of currency, Dec., 1914; ag. agt.-gen. of immigrn. (joint), Mar., 1915; ag. col. sec., Aug., 1915 to May, 1916; gov'r's deputy, Aug., Oct., Dec., 1915; ag. col. sec. on various occasions, 1917-19; on sp. mission to India as personal rep. of the govr. in connection with emigrn. of Indians to Fiji, Oct., 1919 to June, 1920; ch. sec., Nyamaland Prot., July, 1920; ag. gov., on various occasions, 1921-24 and in 1926; ch. sec., Uganda, Aug., 1927; admstd. govt., June to Oct., 1928; Br. res., Zanzibar, 1930; ret., 1937.

RANSOM, HON. SIR ETHELBERT ALFRED, K.C.M.G. (1935).—B. 1868; ed. Lower Hutt state schl., N.Z.; mem., N.Z. H. of R. since 1922; min. of pub. wks., 1928-30; min. of lands and commnr., state forests, 1930; min., same offices, coalition min., 1931-36.

RAWLINGS, WILLIAM.—B. 1893; commr., water conservation and irrign. commn., N.S.W., May, 1935.

RAYMOND, LEONARD WILLIAM.—B. 1892; ed. Horwell Gr. Schl. and Dunheved Coll., Launceston; Royal Coll. Sci.; B.A. (Lond.), B.Sc. (Lond.) 1912, hons. 1915) A.R.C.S. (1915), A.I.C. READ, ALEXANDER LLEWELLYN, M.V.O. (1934), I.S.O. (1937).—B. 1877; ent. pub. serv., S. READ, SIR HERBERT JAMES, G.C.M.G. (1935), K.C.M.G. (1918), C.B. (1914), C.M.G. (1907).—B. 1863; M.A., Brasenose Coll., Oxford; 1st class math. moda., and 1st class math. finals; clk., higher div., W.O., Feb., 1887; 2nd class clk., C.O., Jan., 1889; asst. priv. sec. to Mr. Chamberlain, Sept., 1896; priv. sec. to Mr. (afterwards Sir E.) Wingfield, Feb., 1898; int. cls. clk., Oct., 1898; principal clk., Apr., 1905; British delegate at African liquor confce. at Brussels, 1899; one of the Brit. delegates on boundy. comm. at Berlin for the settm. of Anglo-German frontier in E. Africa, 1906; one of the Brit. plenipotentiaries at African liquor confce. at Brussels, 1906; mem. of hon. man. comte. of sleeping sickness bureau, 1908; chmn. of deptmtl. comte. to enquire into W. African med. staff, 1909; visited E. Africa, 1911-12; sen. mem., C.O. delegation at Paris Peace Confce., 1919; asst. under-sec. of state, March, 1916; regis. of the Order of Saint Michael and Saint George; govt., Mauritius, 1924 to 1930; hon. mem., managing comte., Imp. Inst. of Entomology, 1931; hon. mem., managing comte., Imp. Mycological Inst., 1932.

READ, JOHN GORDON.—B. 1886; B.S.A. pol., 1909-13; clk., N. Rhodesia pol., 1913-16; clk., dist. adminstr., 1916-17; asst. native commr., 1917; native commr., 1923; dist. offr., 1926; prov. commr., 1937.

REAY, CHARLES STUART DE CAIROS.—B. 1895; apptd., Fiji, 1914; ag. clk. to couns. and ag. ch. clk., col. sec.'s office, 1926; dist. commr., 1927.

REAY, CAPTAIN, GEORGE HENRY NOEL, A.M.I.E.E., D.F.H.—B. 1889; asst. telegr. engr., F.M.S., Jan., 1914; war serv., 1915-19; elec. inspr., F.M.S., Nov., 1920; elec. engr., P.W.D., Singapore, July, 1922.

REECE, JOSEPH GRANT.—B. 1885; 4th astt., trig. survey, Trinidad, 1904; 2nd do., 1906; qual. land survr., 1906; topo. astt., 1921; 1st astt., lands and surveys, 1930; dep. survr.-gen., and dep. sub-intendant, 1937; dep. dir. surveys and dep. sub-intendant, Crown lands, 1938.

Harcourt when chancellor of exchequer, 1893-5; and afterwards from 1896-8; atty.-gen., Bahamas, 1897; administered the gvt., 1901; ag. ch. just., 1902; King's advocate, Cyprus, Oct., 1902; atty.-gen., Hong Kong, 1906; K.C., 1908; ag. ch. just., 1909-10; ch. just., Hong Kong, 1912; chmn. of gen. mily. serv. tribunal, 1918; chmn., pub. serv. coman., Hong Kong, 1919; chmn., exec. comtee. on the visit of H.R.H. (Alta., Canada).—B. 1887; on mil. serv., 1914-19; admstve. branch, A.I.P., Air Miny., 1918-22; astt. mast., educn. dept., Hong Kong, Mar., 1922; lectr., Hong Kong Univ., 1925-33; 2nd mast., Queen's Coll., 1931 and 1933; headmnr., Wantsee schl., 1934; do., Kadoorie schl., 1936; do., Yaumati schl., 1938.

REID, C. B. B., M.B., Ch.B. (Edin.).—Capt., R.A.M.C., 1917-19; med. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Feb., 1919.

REILLY, JOSEPH OLIVER.—B. 1891; Imp. postal serv. 1911; war serv., R.F.A., 1914-19; postmr., Tanganyika, 1920; postmr., Nyassaland, 1924; ag. acct., tel. engrnr. and survr., P. and T., Nyassaland, 1932; ag. P.M.G., Nyassaland, 1935; P.M.G., Br. Guiana, July, 1936.

RICE, HARRY PATRICK, O.B.E. (1934). — B. 1886; S.A. constabl., 1906-15; K.M., 1916-19; asst. aupt., pol., Kenya, Nov., 1919; supt., 1926; dep. commdt., pol. (C.I.D.), Palestine, 1932; dep. inspr.-gen., 1936.

RICE, J. A., B.A., LL.B., (T.C.D.) — B. 1904; ed. Clongowes Wood Coll., Ireland; admitted solt., sup. ct., I.F.S.; asst. comanr., lands, lands dept., Gold Coast, 1927; ag. comanr., lands, Apr.-Dec., 1935, Sept., 1937-Apr., 1938.
RICH, HON. SIR GEO. EDWARD, K.C.M.G. (1932). — Senr. puisne just. of the high court of Australia, Apr., 1913.
RICHARDS, ARTHUR COURTNEY. — B. 1896; 2nd asst. gov't. printer, Ceylon, Sept., 1920; asst. gov't. printer, Apr., 1923; gov't. printer, Apr., 1932.

RICHARDS, SIR ARTHUR FREDERICK, K.C.M.G. (1935), C.M.G. (1933), B.A. (Oxon.). — B. 1885; ed. Clifton Coll. and Christ Ch., Oxford; cadet, F.M.S., Nov., 1908; offr., cls. RIDDELL, WALTER ALEXANDER, M.A., PH.D.—Supt., trades and lab. dept. of pub. wks., Ontario govt., 1916; dep. min. of lab., Ontario 1919; ch. of agrl. serv., Internat. Lab. Office, L. of N. Jan., 1920; Dominion of Canada advisory offr. to L. of N., Geneva, 1925.

RIDDELL, HON. WILLIAM RENWICK.—B. 1852; ed. Cobourg, Ont., Coll. Inst. and Victoria Univ., Toronto; lecturer in maths, Normal Schl., Ottawa, 4 years; called to bar, 1883; Q.C., 1897; bencher, Law Soc., Upper Can., since 1892; judge, high ct., Ont., King's Bench divn., 1906; judge, appellate divn. (second div. ct.), 1913; hon. mem., American Bar Assoc. and of Bar Assocs. of several states; F.R. Hist. Soc., England; F.R.S.C., Can.; LL.D., Toronto, Yale, McMaster, Lafayette, Rochester, Wesleyan, North Western Univ.; L.H.D., Syracuse Univ.; J.U.D., Trinity Coll. (Toronto); author of several legal and hist. works; has contrib. extensively to hist., scientific and literary juls.

RIDGEWAY, THE RT. HON. SIR J. WEST, G.C.B. (1906), G.C.M.G. (1900), K.C.S.L., P.C. (Ireland).—B. 1844; ret. from the army with the rank of col., 1889; under-sea., foreign dept., India, 1881-5; commr. for delimitation of Afghan frontier, 1885-7; under-sea. to lord-lieut. of Ireland, 1887; lieut.-gov. of the Isle of Man, 1893; gov. of Ceylon, Dec., 1895-1903; chmn. of comm. sent to S. Africa on Transvaal constitution, 1906; mem. of hon. man. comtes. of sleeping sickness bureau, 1908; chmn., cf. dir., Br. N. Borneo Co., 1910.
RIDGWAY, T. R. J.—Asst. supt., pol., Kenya, Dec., 1923; supt., pol., 1935.

RIGBY, WILLIAM EDWARD, M.C.—B. 1887; ed. Bury Gram. Schl.; served in France, Aug., 1916-Mar., 1918 and June, 1918-Nov., 1919; cadet, S.S., Nov., 1920; attd., Chinese Prot., Penang, Dec., 1920; passed cadet, Nov., 1923; offr., cls. V., Nov., 1923; asst. supt. govt. monops., Spore and asst. contrlr., rubber, Mar., 1926; offr., cls. IV., Nov., 1927; 3rd mag., Spore, June, 1930; inspr., prisons, in addn., Sept., 1931; relief asst. sec. to govt., F.M.S., Nov., 1932, offr., cls. III.; asst. Malayan estabmt. offr., Aug., 1933; ag. 1st asst. Malayan estab. offr., May, 1936; offr., cls. II., Nov., 1937; ag. dep. finan. sec., S.S., Mar., 1938.

RIGNELL, JOHN HERBERT.—B. 1886; Scots Guards, 1904-25; mily. prov., staff corps, 1911; warrant offr., cl. I., 1920-25; physical training instr., Trinity Coll., Kandy, Ceylon, 1915-19; physical training instr., Achimota Coll., Gold Coast, Sept., 1925.
RIMINGTON, CAPT., G.B., M.C.—Asst. dist. oomnr., Kenya, May, 1920; dist. offr., 1923.

RINFRET, HON. FERNAND, B.A., M.P.—B. 1883; ed. St. Mary's Coll., Quebec (B.A.); ed. St. James divn., Montreal bye-el., 1919; re-el. 1921, 1925, 1926 and 1930; sec. of state, Sept., 1926; re-el. by accl., Nov., 1926; mayor, Montreal, 1932-34; re-el., H. of C., 1935; sec. of state, Oct., 1935.

RINFRET, HON. THIBAUDEAU.—B. 1879; ed. St. Mary's Coll. (B.A., 1897), McGill Univ. (B.C.L., 1900); called to Quebec bar, 1901; K.C., 1912; min. of roads in Quebec Govt., 1910-25; prof., munic. law and law of public utilities, McGill Univ.; judge, sup. ct., Canada, Sept., 1924.

RING, JULIUS, B.Sc. (Eng.) (Birm.), F.S.I., Chart. Surveyor, A.M.I.Struc.E.—B. 1892; ed. Workshop Coll., Notts.; engnr., P.W.D., Hong Kong, 1925; assessor, rates, 1928; 1st ass. doc., 1933; assessor, rates, 1938.

RIPPON, STEWART Ross, M.R.O.V.S.—B. 1896; lieut., R.A.V.C., Jan., 1917; serv. in Salonica, Macedonia, and with Army of Black Sea; capt., Jan., 1918; demob., Jan., 1921; vety. offr., Gold Coast, May, 1921.

RISLEY, SIR JOHN SHUCKBURGH, K.C.M.G. (1922), K.C. (1921), C.B. (1912)—B. 1867; ed. at Marlborough and Magd. Coll., Oxford (classical exhibitioner); 2nd class classical mods., 1888; 2nd class jurisprudence, 1890; open studentship, Inns of Court, 1892; called to the bar, 1893; M.A. and B.C.L., 1894; author of "The Founding of St. Stephen's Golf Club," 1893; "The Law of War," 1897; "Notes and Echoes," 1902; editor of Dale's "Clergyman's Legal Handbook," 7th edition; joint editor of Waterlow's "Companies Acts Manual," 11th and 12th editions; legal assistant, C.O., March, 1901; legal adviser, 19th May, 1911.
RITCHIE, CAPT. A. T. A., O.B.E. (1936), M.C., B.A. (Oxon)—Game warden, Kenya, 1923; ch. game warden, F.M.S., 1937; game warden, Kenya, 1938.

RITCHIE, HON. SIR GEORGE, K.C.M.G., M.L.C.—B. 1864; el. fordist of Alexandra to H. of A., S. Australia, 1902; el. N. dist., leg. coun., 1924; commsnr. pub. wks., 1914; has occupied some time or other every portfolio in govt.; at present chief sec., min. of health, and min. of mines.

RITCHIE, THOMAS MARSHALL.—B. 1895; war serv., 1914-19 (2nd lieut. flying offr., R.A.F., R. Scots Fusiliers); foreman of wks., P.W.D., Gold Coast, 1925; inspr. of wks., 1928; mast., govt. tech. schl., 1930; prin., middle boarding schls., July, 1938.

ROBERTS, ALFRED ADRIAN, B.A. (Cape), B.A., LL.B. (Cantab).—B. 1890; called to bar (M.T.), 1916; advoc. Pretoria, 1916-19 and 1929-35; regs., Transvaal Univ. Coll., 1919-29; chmn., govt. comm., on accountancy, 1934; author of "A Guide to Voet"; edr. of "Weasels on Contract"; law advr. to Union Govt., 1935.

ROBERTS, ARTHUR HALLAM, B.A. (Cantab), Barrister-at-law.—B. 1888; ed. Aldenham schl. and Jesus Coll., Camb.; called to bar, Inner Temple, Nov., 1914; cadet, Fiji, Jan., 1916; priv. sec. to gov., Mar., 1915; ag. lat. cls. clk., col. sec's office and clk. to exec. and leg. coun., Jan., 1916; passed cadet, June, 1916; priv. sec., Oct., 1916; 2nd grade dist. commr., Oct., 1916; ag. crown solr., July, 1917; assigned dist. of Savu Savu, Aug., 1917; act. serv., June, 1918; dist. commr., Navua, Dec., 1919; resigned, Oct., 1920; el. mem., leg. coun., 1923-26; regis., sup. ct., taxing master, curator, pub. trustee and offl. recr. in bankruptcy, Mar., 1927; ag. atty.-gen. in 1931, 1932, 1935 and 1936; ch. pol. mag., Aug., 1931; mag. and crown coun., Zanzibar, Aug., 1936; ag. atty.-gen., 1937.

ROBERTS, GEORGE, I.S.O. (1939), F.I.H.E., J.P.—B. 1885; ent. Admy., 1902; seconded as exec. engrnr., Falkland Is., 1925; col. engrnr., 1928; mem., leg. coun., 1929; title of office altered to dir., pub. wks., 1931; mem., exec. coun., 1933.

ROBERTS, GEORGE WILLIAM PEARSON, M.B.E. (Mil), B.Sc. (Lond.), A.C.G.I., A.M. Inst. C.E.—B. 1893; ed. Univ. Coll. Schl. and London Univ.; mil. serv. (France), 1915-19, capt.; joined C.A. as engrnr. inspr., 1920; asst. engrn., 1926; dep. to ch. inspr. engrn., 1935.

ROBERTS, JOHN EDWIN THEOPHILUS.—Ed. Mico Coll., Jamaica; head teacher, St. Mary's Schl., St. Kitts; head teacher, Road Town Wes. Schl., Virgin Is.; clk. to commr., Virgin Is., Mar., 1917; ag. educnl. dist. offr., Oct., 1919-Feb., 1920; educnl. dist. offr., Mar., 1920; clk. to mag. and bailiff, sup. ct., Apr., 1921; consent. to admstr. oaths, July, 1921.

ROBERTS, JOHN HOLLAND, B.A., M.I.M.E.—B. 1886; ed. Tonbridge Schl., 1900-04 and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge (B.A. mech. sciences); asst. engrnr., P.W.D., Perak, 1912; asst. engrnr., P.W.D., F.M.S., 1914; exec. engrnr., 1921; senr. exec. engrnr., 1930; ag. state engrnr., Perak, 1933; senr. exec. engrnr., K. Kangar, June, 1933; do., Pahang, Dec., 1935; state engrnr., grade II, Feb., 1936.
ROBERTS, JOHN REGINALD, A.M.Inst.C.E., A.M.Inst.W.E.—B. 1893; exec. engrnr., Nigeria, 1921; water wks. engrnr., 1927; senr. hydraulic engrnr., Gold Coast, 1929; dep. D.P.W., 1937.

ROBERTS, JOHN RICHARD, A.M. Inst. T.—B. 1892; ed. Winchcombe County Schl. and Grammar Schl.; G.W. Rly., 1908-21; mily. serv., France, 1915-19; dist. stationmr., Tanganyika Territory rly., 1921; traffic inspr., 1925; asst. traffic supt., 1928; traffic supt., 1935; ag. traffic man., May, 1937 to Feb., 1938.
ROBERTS, M. A. W., B.A., M.B., B.Ch., B.A.O. (Trinity Coll., Dublin), F.R.C.S. (Ireland).—Med. offr., Kenya, July, 1929.

ROBERTS, PHILIP HAMER.—Prison offr., Br. Guiana, Nov., 1901; served penal settlmt., Massaruni, and at Essequibo and Georgetown county prisons; clerical asst., Georgetown and Essequibo prisons; gaoler, G. Coast, Mar., 1911; keeper, James Fort prison, June, 1911.
ROBERTS, WALTER.—B. 1896; ed. Bedford Modern Schl.; Europ. war, 1915-19; asst. commissary, Br. Guiana, 1925; asst. inspr. dista., 1927; asst. dist. commnr., 1935; inspr., lab., 1938.
ROBERTS, W. P.—B. 1893; Out Island commnr., grade IV, Bahamas, 1923; grade III, 1926; grade II, 1927.
ROBERTS, CAPT. WILLIAM HOWARD.—B. 1887; asst. engrnr., F.M.S., June, 1920; exec. engrnr., June, 1929; do., P.W., Dec., 1933; do., S'pre, Oct., 1934; ag. senr. exec. engrnr., Feb., 1936.

ROBERTS, WILLIAM RODDICK.—B. 1880; war serv., three years, nine months, R.N. and R.N.A.S.; gaol tech. instr., prisons dept., Gold Coast, Jan., 1921; mast., Accra tech. schl., Oct., 1923; seconded to prisons dept., Oct. to Nov., 1923; housemast., Apr., 1926.

ROBERTS-WRAY, KENNETH OWEN.—B.1899; R. Mily. Acad., Woolwich, 1917-18; commnd., R. Arty., Jan., 1918; served in France, 1918; ret., 1920; Merton Coll., Oxford (lstols. jurisprudence, 1922); M.A.; certif. of hon., bar final exam., 1923; called to bar, Jan., 1924; S. Eastern circuit; professional legal clk., Miny. of Health, Oct., 1926; ag. asst. ch. clk., Jan., 1929; 2nd asst. legal adviser, D.O. and C.O., 1st July, 1931; part author of "The Law of Collisions on Land."

ROBERTSON, ALGAR RONALD WARD.—B. 1902; ed. Dean Close Schl., Cheltenham; asst. treas., Gold Coast, Feb., 1929; capt., G.C. Defence Force; dep. treas., Br. Guiana, Jan., 1936; ag. col. treas., May-Dec., 1936; capt., B.G. militia, 1937.
ROBERTSON, COLIN BROWN.—B. 1803; ed. Leven higher grade; war serv., 1914-19; exec. engrnr., P.W.D., Hong Kong, 1920.

ROBERTSON, IAN DONALD, A.M.I.C.E., Chartered Civil Engrnr.—B. 1886; asst. engrnr., P.W.D., F.M.S., Jly., 1914; asst. engrnr., P.W.D., irrigation br., Aug., 1916, Aug., 1919 and Nov., 1921; dir., wks., Kelantan, Jan., 1923; exec. engrnr., Jan., 1926; sr. exec. engrnr., Jan., 1933.
ROBERTSON, KENNETH STRUAN, B.Eng.—B. 1899; ed. Bedford Schl. and Univ. of Sheffield; engr., P.W.D., Hong Kong, 1924.

ROBERTSON, Sir W. C. F., K.C.M.G. (1927), C.M.G. (1919).—Ed. Trin. Coll., Dublin; exhibitioner, 1885 and 1887; classical scholar, 1888; classical moderator, 1889; inspr., schls. G. Coast, 1898; dir., educn., 1899; ag. comptr., cust., 1899; compiled census rept., 1901; ag. sec., native affairs, June to Nov., 1905; prov. commnr., Nov., 1905; sec. for native affairs, May, 1907; ag. col. sec., July to Sept., 1908; chief astt. col. sec., Jan., 1909; ag. col. sec., in 1909, 1910, 1911, 1912, 1913; deputy gov., Jan., 1910; dep. gov., Dec., 1912, and April to May, 1913; ag. gov., May to Aug., 1914; dep. gov., Aug. to Sept., 1914; col. sec., Gibraltar, Feb., 1915; lieut.-gov. and chief sec. to gov't., Malta, May, 1917; offr. admstg. gov't., Malta, for various periods, 1920-25; gov.'s dep., in Oct., 1920; lieut. gov. by comm. under new constitution, Apr., 1921; gov.'s dep., for various periods in 1920 and 1922; hon. LL.D., Malta Univ., 1925; gov't., Barbados, 1926; attended C.O. conferees, 1927 and 1930; ret., 1932.
ROBERTSON, WILLIAM, I.S.O. (1937).—B. 1875; ed. Palmerston Dist. High Schl., N.Z.; N.Z. dept. of lands and survey, 1892; inspr., 1919; astt. under-sec., 1925; under-sec., 1931.

ROBERTSON, WILLIAM MACFARLANE, B.Sc. (Edin.).—B. 1897; ed. Edin. Univ.; medallist (equal) and 1st cls. hons. intermediate forestry (including sylviculture) and geology; enlisted Scottish Horse, May, 1916; on active serv., W. Yorks. Regt., 1917-18 and with the army of occupation, Germany, until Feb., 1919; astt. conserv., forests, Nigeria, 1922; conserv., forests, 1926; do., Sierra Leone, 1936.

ROBINS, REGINALD EDWIN, C.M.G. (1938), O.B.E. (1932), A.M.Inst.T.—B. 1891; ed. Queen Mary's Schl., Basingstoke; ent. G.W.R. serv., 1908; sp. training under G.W.R. training scheme for offrs., 1920; London Schl. of Economics, 1919-24; Brunel medallist; Kenya and Uganda rly.s., 1925; gen. man., Tanganyika rly.s., and ports services, Apr., 1936.

ROBINSON, Capt. ARTHUR GORDON, B.Sc. (Eng.), M.Inst.C.E.—B. 1887; ed. St. Asaph gram. schl., N. Wales and Birmingham Univ.; astt. engr., pub. works dept., F.M.S., June, 1912; seconded, Kedah, July, 1912; B.E.F., France, Mar., 1917-Jan., 1919; capt., commanding Indian labr., lieut., R.E.; exec. engr., pub. works dept., F.M.S., June, 1921; sen. exec. engr., pub. works dept., F.M.S., July, 1928; ch. hydraulic engr., 1930; astt. advr., drainage and irrign. Malay States, Jan., 1932; dir. drainage and irrign., S.S. and advrs., do. F.M.S., Nov., 1935.

ROBINSON, AUBREY CHARLES.—B. 1875; ed. Lancing Coll., and Hertford Coll., Oxford; B.A.; called to the bar, Inner Temple, Nov., 1901; practised at the bar, Jamaica, 1901 to 1911; commnr., and judge of grand ct., Cayman Is., Jan., 1913; compld. revised edition of Laws of Cayman Is. (publcn. deferred during the war); regir.-gen., Bahamas, Jan., 1918; perf. duties of S. and C. mag. in 1918 and 1919; regir.-gen., Trinidad, Aug., 1919; ag. regir., supt. ot. and marshal, June to Sept., 1920; res. mag., Jamaica, Sept., 1923; regir., sub. ct. and regir.-gen., Trinidad, Nov., 1928; 2nd puisne judge, Aug., 1931.

ROBINSON, GEORGE GILMOUR.—B. 1894; ed. Repton and Trinity Coll., Oxon., B.A., M.A.; war serv., 1914-19 (France); called to bar, Inner Temple, 1924; practised, 1924-30; res. mag., Kenya, 1930; puisne judge, N. Rhodesia, 1938.

ROBINSON, KENNETH ERNEST.—B. 1914; ed. Monoux Schl., Walthamstow and Oxford; open schl., Hertford Coll., 1931-36 and state schl., 1931-36; hons. schl., philosophy, politics and economics, ols. I., 1934; B.A., 1934; hons. schl., mod. hist., ols., I., 1936; awarded Beit senr. research schl. in col. history, 1936 but resigned on appt. to C.O., 1936; appt. after compet. exam., astt. prin., C.O., Oct., 1936.

ROBINSON, MONTGOMERY BLAIR.—B. 1878; B.A., Trinity Coll., Dublin, 1901; LL.B., Univ. of Cape of Good Hope, 1909; clk. lawdept., Cape, 1903; mag., 1921; ch. clk., dept. of just., 1929; addnl. mag., Pretoria, June, 1931; mag. Wynberg, Nov., 1935; mast., sup. ct., Pretoria, Jan., 1937.

ROBINSON, S.—B. 1893; ed. Barnsley Gram. Schl. and Univ. of Sheffield; B.A., 1914; let ols. hons. mod. langs.; M.A., 1919; on milv. serv., 1914-19; apptd. under reconstruction scheme, junr. clk., bd. of cust. and excise, Sept., 1919; astt. prin., 1st Jan., 1920; transf'd to C.O. as astt. priv. sec. (appts.) to successive secs. of State, 1923-24; astt. prte. sec. to Lord Passfield, Sept., 1929; prin., 1st Oct., 1930.

ROBINSON, Lieut-Col. Sir THOMAS BILSE, K.O.M.G. (1913), K.T. BACH. (1910), K.B.E. (1918).—B. 1853; formerly major in command of 1st Queenslanders, and senior offr. in Cent. Queensland; man. and dir. of various public companies; J.P., Queensland, 1887; pres. of Rockhampton chamber of commerce; Queensland commnr. for Franco-British exhibn.; agt.-gen. for Queensland, 1910; mem. of comtee. for settm. of ex-serv. men within the Empire, 1917.

ROBISON, Lionel McDOWALL, B.A. (Manchester).—B. 1886; lect., govt. training coll., Colombo, Ceylon, 1909; vice-prin., June, 1912; inspr., schls., Oct., 1915; army serv., July, 1918; astt. dir., educn., July, 1924; dep. dir., July, 1929; sec. to min. for educn., Feb., 1932; dir., educn., Jan., 1936.

ROCK, Alfred Milton.—B. 1888; ag. 3rd outdoor offr. and harbmr., treasry., Dominica, 1913-15; clk. and interpreter to mag., dist. "K." Mar., 1915; clk., mag., regir. and provost marshall "A," commnr. of oaths, dep. coroner, Montserrat, Sept., 1915; clk. to mag., dist. "A" and "B," dep. coroner, Antigua, Nov., 1920; senr. clk., regir.'s office, clk., sup. ct. law libry., "A" commnr. of oaths, dep. coroner, Dominica, May, 1927; prin. for duty as regir. and provost marshal, regir. of titles, admir. of estates and regir.-gen. of births, marriages and deaths, Dominica, Apr., 1929.

ROCK, John CHRISTOPHER WILBERFORCE.—B. 1882; Ceylon civ. serv., Nov., 1905; addnl. pd. mag., Hambantota, July, 1906; office astt. Ratnapura kach., Jan., 1908; pol. mag. Panadura., Dec., 1908; ditto, Point Pedro, Nov., 1911; dist. judge, Tangalla, Oct., 1912; dist. judge, Kalutara, Aug., 1920; dep. commnr., excise, May, 1921; dist. judge, Matara, Apr., 1925; dep. commnr., stamps, July, 1927; regir.-gen., Sept., 1930; excise commnr., June, 1931; inspr. offr., magistrates' courts, Feb., 1933; regir.-gen., June, 1933; regir.-gen., dir. of commerce intelligence, regir. of motor cars, and dir. of stats., June, 1933; offr., ols. I., grade I.; dir. of commerce and industries, and in addn. dir. of stats. and food contrlr., June, 1938.

LODD, A. J.—B. 1886; apptd., after exam., t. library attendant, C.O., 11th Dec., 1905; rary attendant, 18th Feb., 1911; supplemen- y elk., col. audit dept., Apr., 1914; on mil. v. from 5th Aug., 1914 to 1919; regar., Ist rr., 1928.

RODDA, GEORGE CHARLES, M.B.E. (1919), R.A.N.Z., F.I.A.N.Z.—B. 1878; ed. Stafford hl., Hokitika dist. high schl. and Victoria inv. coll., N.Z.; ent. N.Z. govt. insur. dept., 1914; treas., 1920; ofr. in ch., war expenses, 1914-19; sec., local govt. loans bd., 1927-30; astt. c., treas., 1930; sec., treas., revcr.-gen., asymr.-gen., 1935; also chmn. local govt. loans bd., finan. advr. to govt., and ex officio dir., reserve bank, 1935; astt. N.Z. deleg., world economic confce., 1933; mem., N.Z. deleg., to imp. Confce., 1937.

RODGER, ROBERT, A.M.Inst.C.E., A.M.I. (eech. E., A.M.Inst.M. & Cy.E., M.I.Struct.E., R.G.S.)—B. 1893; ed. Wishaw High Schl., Vishaw Tech. Schl. and Glasgow Tech. Coll. (affld.); on active serv. with Lanarkshire Yeo., Lowld., Mtd. Bde., Scot., T.F.) 1914-30; dist. engrnr., P.W.D., Palestine, 1920; road engrnr., P.W.D., Gold Coast, on "Tarmet" scheme, 1924; road engrnr., P.W.D., Nigeria, on road develop- ment schemes, 1926; road engrnr., P.W.D., Cyprus, in ch., reconstruction scheme, 1930; astt., D.P.W., various occasions, 1931-36; ag. astt., D.P.W., in 1936; senr. exec. engrnr., P.W.D., Nigeria, 1937; ag. astt. D.P.W., 1937.

RODRIGO, EDMUND.—B. 1889; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Dec., 1912; office astt. to govt. agt., W. Prov., May, 1915; mun. mag., Colombo, Oct., 1915; pol. mag., Puttalam, July, 1917; office astt. to govt. agt., Prov. of Sabaragamuwa, Sept., 1918; dist. judge, Batticaloa, Apr., 1920; ag. dist. judge, Matare, May, 1922; astt. commr. excise, Sept., 1924; dep. commr., excise, July, 1927; contr., finance and supplies, July, 1931; astt. govt. agt., Puttalam, July, 1932; govt. agt., N.W.P., Oct., 1935; ag. dir., agr., Mar., 1937.

RODWAY, RUPERT, B.A. (Cantab.)—B. 1908; ed. King's Coll., Cambridge (math. tripes, pt. I, econ. tripes, pt. II); col. audit dept., Nov., 1931; astt. audr., Sierra Leone, Dec., 1931; do., Nigeria, May, 1934; do., Br. Guiana, Jan., 1936; ag. audr., May, 1936-Apr., 1937, Aug. and Dec., 1937.

RODWELL, SIR CECIL HUNTER, G.C.M.G. (1934), K.C.M.G. (1919), C.M.G. (1909).—Ed. Eton and King's Coll., Camb., B.A., 1897; serv. on staff of high commr. for S. Africa since Dec., 1900; Imperial sec., Mar., 1904; gov., Fiji, and high commr., W. Pacific, 1918; gov. and commdr.-in-ch., B. Guiana, 1925; gov. and commdr.-in-ch., Southern Rhodesia, Sept., 1928; ret., Dec., 1934.

RODWELL, REGINALD MORTON HASSEL, B.A.—B. 1906; ed. Marlborough Coll. and Oxford Univ. (Worcester Coll.); called to bar, Inner Temple, 1929; practised at English bar; dist. mag., Gold Coast, Sept., 1938.

ROE, ROBERT JESTIN, M.R.C.V.S. (Williams Memorial Prize), D.V.S.M.—B. 1897; ed. Drogheda and R. Vety. Coll., Ireland; war serv., 1915-16; vety. surg., Cyprus, June, 1921; passed exam. in mod. Greek, Jan., 1923; del. to Imp. Agric. Research Confce., 1927; ag. dir., animal husbandry, July-Oct., 1928; title changed to ch. vety. offr., Jan., 1930; rep. govt. Internat. Dairy Cong., Copenhagen, 1931.

ROGERS, HOWARD, B.A., LL.B.—B. 1891; ed. Rhodes Univ. Coll. and S.A. Coll.; apptd. pub. serv., 1911; admitted advoc., sup. ct., 1921; war serv., German W. and E. Africa, 1914-17; nat. commr., Pretoria, 1932; ch. nat. commr. (northern areas), Apr., 1936; contr., native settlms., Aug., 1936; author of "Native Administration in the Union of S. Africa" (1933).

ROGERS, FRANCIS MERVYN.—B. 1890; student, R. Botanic Gardens, Kew, 1915; milly. serv., 1916-19; sub-foreman, Kew, 1919-20; head gar- dener, Amani, Tanganyika Territory, 1920; supt., plantations, E. African agric. research sta., Amani, 1927.

ROGERS, HON. NORMAN McLEOD.—B. 1894; ed. Amherst pub. and high schls. and Acadia Univ.; served Great War; B.A., Acadia Univ., 1919; cl. to Rhodes scholarship at Oxford; B.A. (hist., polit. sci. and law); prof., hist., Acadia Univ.; admitted to Nova Scotia bar; see, Rt. Hon. Mackenzie King in his capacity as pres., P.C., 1927; prof., polit. sci., Queen's Univ., 1929; coun. for govt. of Nova Scotia before R. commn. of econ. enquiry, 1934; cl. M.P., Canada, g.e., 1936; min. of lab., Mackenzie King cabinet, 1936.

ROGERS, PHILIP.—B. 1914; ed. Manchester Hulme Grammar Schl. and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; senr. school, of Emmanuel, 1935-36; mod. languages tripes pt. I (German cls. II, divn. I, French cls. II, divn. II), hist. tripes, pt. II, (cls. I), economics tripes, pt. II (cls. I, divn. II); apptd. after compet. exam., astt. prin., C.O., Oct., 1936.

ROGERSON, WALTER JOHN LANCASTRSHIRE, B.A., Cantab.—B. 1889; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Dec., 1912; addtl. office astt., Anuradhapura, June, 1914; office astt. to govt. agt., Badulla, Sept., 1914; pol. mag., Matale, Jan., 1915; office astt. to govt. agt., Kandy, May, 1917; pol. mag., Avisawella, Jan., 1921; ditto, Kandy, Feb., 1921; dist. judge Kegalla, Sept., 1922; pol. mag., Colombo, July, 1923; astt. govt. agt., Colombo, Nov., 1925; addnl. govt. agt., N.W.P., Feb., 1930; astt. govt. agt., Nuwara Eliya, Apr., 1931; addnl. govt. agt., W. Prov., Sept., 1933; govt. agt., S.P., Oct., 1934; chmn., muncl. coun., Kandy, Nov., 1935; govt. agt., N.W.P., Mar., 1936; do., S.P., Nov., 1937.
