Rlys., 1924; run. supt., 1927; traffic man., Nov., 1930; transportation man., 1933; ag. gen. man., F.M.S. rlys., Apr., 1934.

SANDFORD, FOLLIOTT HUGH BLAKELOCK.—B. 1889; ed. Rugby and Magdalen Coll., Oxford, (demy and Sheppard exhib.) M.A.; barrister-at-law; asst. dist. commr., Uganda, 1913; dist. commr., 1923; senr. dist. offr., 1933; prov. commr., Dec., 1935.

SANDFORD, GEORGE RITCHIE, C.M.G.(1938), O.B.E. (1924), Barrister-at-Law, Inner Temple, 1924.—B. 1892; ed. Christ's Hosp. and Queens' Coll., Camb., B.A. and wrangler, 1914; asst. dist. commr., E. Africa Prot., Feb., 1915; priv. sec. to gov., 1918; junr. asst. sec. and clk. of couns., Sept., 1922; ag. senr. asst. sec., Mar. to Sept., 1923; resig., 1925; clk. to leg. coun., Jan., 1926; dep. treas., 1931; treas. and currency offr., Tanganyika Territory, 1936; finan. sec., 1937.

SANDFORD, THOMAS FREDERICK, C.M.G. (1935), M.B.E., B.A.—B. 1886; ed. Marlborough Coll. and Oriel Coll., Oxford; asst. native commr., N. Rhodesia, Sept., 1910; native commr., Apr., 1913; served in E. African campaign, 1915-19; ag. dist. commr., 1927; ag. prov. commr., in 1929, 1930 and 1931; prov. commr., Aug., 1932; temp. mem., leg. coun., 1932 and 1935; senr. prov. commr. and mem., exec. and leg. couns., 1936.

SANDY'S, MELVILL KEVENER TRELLAWNY, M.A. (Oxon.).—B. 1890; on mily. serv., Aug., 1914; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Nov., 1919; attd., Ratnapura kach., Nov., 1919; office asst. to govt. agt., N.W. Prov., Mar., 1922; dist. judge, Anuradhapura, July, 1925; pol. mag., Kurunegala, Oct., 1926; ag. agt., Hambantota, May, 1927; ag. dist. judge, Tangalla, June, 1927; asst. govt. agt., Uva, July, 1927; asst. settlmt., officer, Jan., 1929; asst. govt. agt., Kalutara, Oct., 1931; do., Mannar, Nov., 1931; do., Trincomalee, Mar., 1933; do., Matale, Nov., 1935; dist. judge, Badulla, Oct., 1936; ag. govt. agt., N.P., May, 1937; chmn., mun. coun., Kandy, and addnl. asst. govt. agt., Nov., 1937.

SANKERALLI, ESAU JIMSHED, M.B., Ch.B., B.A.O. (Queen's University, Belfast), D.T.M. (Liverpool), L.M. (Dublin), D.P.H. (Belfast).—B. 1898; govt. med. offr., Trinidad, 1923; asst. res. surg., col. hosp., 1929; junr. med. inspr., health, Aug., 1935; M.O.H., S.W. dist., Jan., 1936.

SANSON, CHARLES HENRY, C.M.G. (1938)—B. 1886; ed. Plymouth and Munnamead Coll.; barrister-at-law, Middle Temple, 1923; pol. prob., 1905; regir. of criminals, F.M.S. and S.S., 1910; asst. commr. of pol., Ipoh, 1911; adjt., F.M.S. pol., 1913; commr. of pol., Johore, 1921; supt. pol., S'pore but cont. former appt., Nov., 1924; ch. pol. offr., S'pore, Dec., 1929; offg. ch. pol. offr., Perak, Nov., 1930; commr., pol., F.M.S., Oct., 1931.

SARAVANAMUTTU, PAIKIASOTHY.—B. 1894; B.A., Lond.; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Aug., 1919; attd., Colombo, kach., Aug., 1919; attd. to Matale Kaoh., June, 1920; addnl. dist. judge, Badulla, Mar., 1921; pol. mag., Dandagamuwa, Sept., 1921; ditto, Kurunegala, June, 1922; addnl. asst. govt. agt., Colombo, Mar., 1923; addnl. asst. dir., educ., Dec., 1925; asst. govt. agt., Mullaitivu, Dec., 1926; dist. judge, Kegalle, Mar., 1929; dist. judge, Kalutara, Apr., 1932; asst. govt. agt., Kalutara, Apr., 1933; rubber contr., May, 1934; tea contr. in addn., Apr., 1935.
ARGEANT, CAPT. R. G., M.B.E. (1928), A.I.N.A., F.R.G.S.—Port offr., Kenya, Jan., 1917; port capt., K.U.R. & H., 1925.

SAUNDERS, MAJOR ALAN, O.B.E. (1932), M.C.(1917).—B. 1886; ed. Christ's Hosp.; Indian pol., 1906-14; Great War, 1914-20; France, Flanders, Palestine; wounded twice; three times ment. in despe.; dep. inspir. genl., Palestine pol., 1926; King's pol. med., Sept., 1929; commr., Nigeria pol., 1936; inspir.-gen., pol. and prisons, Palestine, Nov., 1937.

SAUNDERS, GODFREY ESTRIDGE.—B. 1884; offr. of cust., Cape Town, 1901; 1st grade examnt., Mossel Bay, 1915; prin. clk., Pretoria, 1923; senr. inspir., Johannesburg, 1931; surv., cust., Port Elizabeth, 1933; investigation offr., London, 1933; ch. inspir., Pretoria, 1936; asst. dep. commr., Pretoria, 1937; dep. commr., 1938.

SAUNDERS, PHILIP HENRY NORMAN.—B. 1890; ed. Queen's Coll., Br. Guiana; successively 6th, 5th, 4th, 3rd and 2nd cls. clk., P.W.D., Br. Guiana, 1911-24; sec., bd. of examiners for land surveyors, 1917; sec., bd. of industrial training, 1930; acctg. clk., P.W.D., 1931; sec., advisory comttee. on sea defences, 1932; sec., sea defence bd., 1933; sec., prisoners' aid comttee., 1935.

SAUNDERS-JONES, DAVID WILLOUGHBY.—B. 1899; ed. Christ Coll., Brecon; Indian Army, 1917-23; on service in Waziristan, Egypt and Palestine; wounded in Palestine, 1919; asst. dist. commr., Zanzibar, Feb., 1924; ag. pvtc. sec., Br. res. in 1925 and from Aug., 1930-May, 1931; ag. prov. commr., Pemba, June, 1930 and July, 1932-Feb., 1933; asst. sec. and pte. sec. to Sultan, 1936-38.

SAVAGE, ALFRED WILLIAM LUNGLAY.—B. 1903; Crown Agents office, 1920; seconded to treasy., N. Rhodesia, 1925; asst. treas., 1928; mun. coun. audr., 1930 and 1931; sec. taxn. oomm., 1934; dep. treas., Fiji, 1935; sec., pub. serv. reorganisation comttee., 1936; acct. gen. and dep. treas., 1937; ag. financial sec. and col. treas., and ex-officio M.L.C., 1937; chmn., agric. bank comttee., 1938; offl. M.L.C., 1938; ag. financial sec. and col. treas., July-Sept., 1938.

SAVAGE, RT. HON. MICHAEL JOSEPH, P.C. (1936).—B. 1872; mem., N.Z. H. of R. since 1919; leader of labour party, 1933; prime min., min. of external affrs. and native min., 1935; rep. N.Z. at coronation, 1937; leader, N.Z. delegn. to Imp. Confee., 1937.

SAVARY, JOHN LEWIS HENRY WILLIAM.—B. 1884; col. schol., Trinidad, 1901; Gray's Inn, 1902; prizeman in evidence, procedure and criminal law exam., 1904; barrister-at-law, Nov., 1905; practised at bar, Trinidad, 1906-24; retainer from Trinidad govt. as junr. coun. to atty.-gen., Jan., 1916-Dec., 1923; ag. solr.-gen., in 1921, 1923 and 1923; regis. and marshal and regis. gen. and prin. regis., West Indian ct. of appeal, Jan., 1925; ag. puisne judge in 1925 and 1926; puisne judge, Br. Guiana, 1928; ag. ch. just. in 1932, 1934 and 1936; puisne judge, Gold Coast, June, 1937.

SAWARD, CAPT. SIDNEY CARMON, M.C. (1916), M.M. (1916).—B. 1889; fellow of surveyors' inst.; served with 509 (1-1) Lond. Field Coy., R.E., 6th divn.; home serv., Aug. to Dec., 1914; France and Germany, Dec., 1914 to June, 1919; Ireland, June to Aug., 1919; commdg., cadre, 12th Field Co., R.E.; commd., Sept., 1915; lieut., June, 1916; capt., Oct., 1918; ag. major, Dec., 1918 to Jan., 1919; ment. in desps., Mar., 1919; survr., Gold Coast, Nov., 1919; field supervisor Apr., 1924; ag. dep. survr.-gen. on various occasions, 1926-32; ag. survr.-gen., Feb.-June, 1927; asst. survr.-gen., Apr., 1929; ag. survr.-gen., Mar.-Aug., 1934, and Mar.-Aug., 1936; dir. of surveys, 1938.

SAXTON, SIDNEY WARING.—Ed. Toulouse (France), and Dean Close Sch., Cheltenham; paymr. cadet, R.N., July, 1906; paymr.-lieut. comdr., Mar., 1918; 1st cls. French interpreter lent to C.O. and apptd. asst. dist. comanr., G. Coast, Sept., 1914, but did not take up apptnt., having resumed duty in H.M. Navy for the period of the war; assumed duties as junr. asst. col. sec., G. Coast, Apr., 1919; ag. asst. col. sec., May, 1919; ag. dist. comanr., Accra, and ag. trav. comanr. for Accra-Akwapim and Biriyim dists., Oct., 1919; invalided, H.M. Navy, 1920; passed Tropical African service course (all subjects, 2nd part); barrister-at-law, Middle Temple, 1925; promoted pay commdr., ret. list, 1927; dep. prov. comanr., 1930; prov. comanr., 1938.

SAYERS, ELDER FRANK.—B. 1889; ed. King's Coll. Sch., Wimbledon, St. John's Coll., Cambridge, and Grenoble Univ., B.A., LL.B. (Cantab.); barrister-at-law (Inner Temple), Apr., 1913; passed lower and higher stand. exams. in Tonno, and lower stand. exam. in Koranko; war service, E. Africa, 1918; asst. dist. comanr., Sierra Leone, Jan., 1915; dist. comanr., Aug., 1922.

SAYERS, GERALD FLEMING.—B. 1893; ed. Westminster and Christ Church, Oxford; B.A. (hon.) 1914; asst. dist. comanr., E. Africa Prot., Feb., 1915; ag. senr. asst. sec., Nov., 1919 to July, 1920; asst. sec., Tanganyika territory, July, 1920; ag. senr. asst. sec., for various periods 1920-24; clk. to exec. coun., Sept., 1923; attd. to C.O., Aug., 1924 to Sept., 1925 and from July to Sept., 1930; olk., leg. coun., 1926-32; ag. asst. ch. sec., on various occasions, 1927-1931; asst. ch. sec., 1932; asst. ch. sec., Nyasaland Prot., 1933; ag. ch. sec., Feb., 1934; dep. ch. sec., Tanganyika Territory, May, 1935; mem., exec. and leg. couns.; ag. ch. sec., in 1935 and 1937; admstr. sec., 1937; edr., Handbook of Tanganyika Territory, 1930.

SCAMMELL, ERNEST HENRY.—B. 1873; ed. privately; sec. various business concerns, 1891-1907; visited Australia, engaged in business and journalism in Canada, 1907-1913; organizing sec., Canadian Peace Centenary Association, under the auspices of the govt. of Canada, 1913; asst. cable censor, dept. of militia and defence, Canada, 1915; sec., mil. hosps. comanr., Canada, 1915; asst. dep. min. and sec., dept. of soldiers' civil re-estabmt., Canada, 1918; negotiated reciprocal agreements between Canadian, British and United States Govts., regarding treatment and training of Naval and Military Forces resident in these countries, 1919; sec., pensions divn., dept. of pensions and national health, 1931; Fellow of the Chartered Institute of Secretaries (London); Fellow of the Royal Colonial Institute (London); mem. of the Exec. of the Canadian Peace Centenary Assoc.

SCHARFF, JOHN WILLIAM, B.A., M.D., B.A.O., B.Ch., D.P.H. (T.C.D.), D.T.M. & H. (Lond.).—B. 1895; surgn. sub.-lieut., R.N.V.R., July, 1915; health offr., S.S., Nov., 1920; lect. in biology, King Edward VII Coll. of Medicine in addn., May, 1921; hon. sec. for Malays of the Far Eastn. Assoc. of Trop. Med., Aug., 1921; health offr., Malacca, June, 1926; sr. health offr., Penang, May, 1928; offl. deleg. to 8th cong. of Far Eastn. Assoc. of Trop. Med., Bangkok, Dec., 1930; superscale offr., grade A, Jan., 1935; ch. health offr., S'pore, July, 1935.
SCHERMBRI, JUDGE SALVATORE, LL.D.—B. 1881; matric., Malta Univ., 1899; LL.D., 1907; ag. judge, Malta, 1936; judge, 1937.

SCHMIDT, CARL FRIEDRICH, B.A.—Civ. serv. law olk., law dept., Cape, May, 1893; control and audit office, July, 1893; asst. examnr. of accts., Jan., 1894; examnr., May, 1898; ch. examnr., July, 1907; inspr. of accts., Jan., 1909; prin. olk., Apr., 1912; sec. educn. dept., Bloemfontein, 16th Aug., 1915; ag. dir. of educn., O.F.S., July, 1918; dir. of educn., O.F.S., June, 1920; sec. for interior, Sept., 1926; contr. and audr.-gen., 1929; rector, Pretoria univ., 1935.

SCHMITT, LOGIS JOHN, A.I.O.A., F.C.A.A.—B. 1885; ed. Victoria Parade coll., Australia; asct. for some years; sec., N.Z. bd. of trade price investig. tribunal, 1919; investig. acct., bd. of trade, 1921; sec. to advry. coun., N.Z. sec., Wembley exhib., 1924; dep. govt. commnr., Dunedin internat. exhib., 1926; advry. acct., industries and commerce dept., 1927; mem. (rep. employers), N.Z. ct. of arbit., 1928; N.Z. govt. tourist and trade commr. in Australia, 1930-35; sec. and gen. man., N.Z. dept. of industries and commerce, tourist and publicity, 1935.

SCHNEIDER, GEORGE ALBERT WILHELM.—B. 1884; ed. Pretoria High Schl. and S.African Coll., Cape Town; solr., sup. ct., Transvaal; civ. serv., Transvaal, 1907; ch. olk., dept. of lands, 1931; under-sec. for agr., 1935.

BOROCH, AUGUST ARNOLD.—B. 1883; ed. Berne, Switzerland; graduated, Leiden, LL.D. and called to bar, Middle Temple, 1910; ent. Union pub. serv., 1911; priv. sec. to min. of justice, 1913-16; professional asst. to atty.-gen. (Transvaal), 1916-24; solr.-gen., Cape Eastern Dist., Aug., 1924; law adviser and Dutch draughtsman, dept. of just., Pretoria, Sept., 1925; senr. law adviser, Oct., 1931.
SCHOKMAN, CLIVE CONRADE.—B. 1897; 4th supt., prisons, Ceylon, Jan., 1924; 1st do., Apr., 1928; inspr.-gen., prisons, Mar., 1932.

SCHOLEFIELD, GUY HARDY, D.Sc. (Lond.), O.B.E. (1919).—Ed. Tokomairio High Schl. and Univ. of N.Z. (Bowen and Macmillan Brown prizeman) and Univ. of London; ch. librarian, gen. assembly liby. and contrlr., dominion archives, N.Z., 1926.

SCHOLEFIELD, WILLIAM.—Dep. olk., res. mag.s. ct., Jamaica, Mar., 1913; olk., res. mag.s. ct., Feb., 1917; asst. comanr. and mag., Turks and Caicos Islands, July, 1919; ag. comanr., ditto, Sept. to Oct., 1920; ag. res. mag., Jamaica, on various occasions, 1922-26.

SCHOLLES, ALWYN DENTON, M.A. (Hons. Cantab.).—B. 1910; ed. Cheltenham Coll. and Selwyn Coll., Cambridge; called to bar. Inner Temple, 1934; practised at English bar, Central Crim. Ct., Midland circuit and Birmingham, Lincoln, Leicester, Northampton, Warwick, Nottingham, Bedford and Derby City and County sessions; dist. mag., Gold Coast, Apr., 1938.

SCHREINER, OLIVER DENEYS.—B. 1890; ed. Rondebosch H. Schl., S.A. Coll. Schl., S.A. Coll. (Cape Town), Trin. Coll., Camb. (fellow 1916); barrister, Inner Temple; M.C., 1916; advoc., Johannesburg, 1920-37; K.C., 1935; judge, supr. ct., S.A., T.P.D., 1937.

SCHUMANN, THEODOR E. WERNER, B.A., M.So., Ph.D.—B. 1896; ed. Stellenbosch, S.A.; Gottingen, Germany and Yale, U.S.A.; lect. in physics and maths., Rhodes Univ. Coll., Grahamstown, 1918-19; and at Stellenbosch, 1920-21; dir., fuel research inst. of S. Africa, June, 1931, regrd., Feb., 1933; ch. meteorologist, Union of S.A., Sept., 1933.

SCILUNA, HANNIBAL P., M.B.E. (1935), Hon. M.A. (Oxon.) (1938), L.P.—B. 1880; ed. St. Ignatius' Coll., Malta; matric., Malta Univ., 1897; ent. Malta civ. serv., 1902; legal procurator, 1905; sec. and regis., Malta Univ., 1913-20; rep. of govt. emigr. dept. in devastated regions, France, 1920; attd., ministry of justice, 1921; esquire, Order of St. John of Jerusalem (Rome), 1910; Officier d'Academie (France), 1921; mem. of the Societe Academique d'Histoire Internationale de Paris, 1923; libr., Malta public library, July, 1923; ag. dir. of museum, 1938.
SCOTLAND, ERICA HELENOR.—Ent. C.O. as typist, July, 1913; shorthand writer, Aug., 1918; cler. offr., 9th Mar., 1922.

SCOTT, MAJOR CHARLES ALEXANDER.—B. 1893; ed. Edinburgh Univ.; M.A. (Edinburgh); European mast., S.S., Apr., 1923; headmast., Rangoon Rd., schl., S'pore, Aug., 1932; inspr., schl., N. Semilan, June, 1934; headmast., King George V schl., Seremban, Feb., 1935; ag. headmast., English Coll., J. Bahrul, Oct., 1936.

SCOTT, HENRY HAROLD, M.D., London Univ.; F.R.C.P., London; F.R.S., Edin.; D.P.H.; M.R.C.S., Eng.; D.T.M., & H. (Camb.); Fell. of Royal Soc. of medicine; Fell. of Royal Inst. of preventive medicine; Fell. of Soc. of tropical medicine and hygiene.—Ed. at St. Bart's and St. Thomas's hosps.; Univ. Colls. London and Copenhagen; prosector, 1894; ass't demonstrator in Materia Medica, 1895; ass't house physician, 1897; house physician, 1898, all at St. Thomas's hosp.; res. med. offr., Teignmouth hosp., 1898; serv. in S. African War (Queen's medal with 5 clasps); med. offr. in charge bacteriological and X-Ray depts., mil. hosp., Maritzburg, 1903; M.O. in charge Fort Napier hosp. for women and children, 1904; res. med. offr., Kingsbury and Neasden isolation hosp., 1910; bacteriologist to Willesden Dist. Coun., 1910; govt. bacteriologist and fermentation chemist, Jamaica, 1910; is also pathologist to Kingston gen. hosp.; mem. of med. coun., board of examiners, quarantine board, and cent. board of health, Jamaica; during 1915-1917, seconded for war service; bacteriologist at Command Laboratory, Aldershot, 1915; in charge No. XIII, mobile laboratory, 1916; pathologist and med. specialist, (Cambridge hosp.), Aldershot, 1916; govt. pathologist, dir., serum inst. Hong Kong, 1919; lect. on path. and med. jurisprudence, Hong Kong Univ., 1919; Milner research fellowship, L.S.H. and T.M., 1925; lect., trop. disease, Westminster hosp. med. schl., 1926; examr., D.M.T. and H. Eng., 1927; med. sec., col. med. research comte, 1928.

SCOTT, SIR JOHN, K.B.E. (1932), C.M.G. (1923).—B. 1878; ed. Bath Coll. and King's Coll., Cambridge (B.A.); cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., Nov., 1901; prin. asst. col. sec., Apr., 1917; dep. ch. sec. to govt., Nigeria, Feb., 1921; ch. sec., Tanganyika Territory, Jan., 1924; admstd. govt. for various periods, 1924-29; col. sec., S. S., 1929; admstd. govt., various occasions, 1929-31; ret., Aug., 1933.
SCOTT, MARGARET.—Ed. Sherbourne Schl. for Girls; V.A.D. nurse, Brit. R. Cross, 1915-19; housekeeper's dipl., Edin. Sch. of Domestic Econ., 1920; matron, Aclimata, 1925.

SCOTT, R. R., M.C., M.B., B.S. (Durham), M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.), D.P.H. (Lond.).—R.A.M.C. (S.R.), Aug., 1914; ment. in desps., 1919; med. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Mar., 1919; senr. med. offr. of health, Apr., 1924; dep. dir., sany. serv., 1932.

SCOTT, WALTER RICHARDSON.—B. 1899; ed. Durham, 1922; pol. probationer, Hong Kong, 1922; passed final exam. in Chinese, 1924; asst. supt. pol., 1924; J.P., 1925; attd. Indian pol. and passed in Urdu, 1926; passed Punjabi, 1928; pvt. sec. to O.A.G., in addn., 1928; ditto to gov. in addn., Jan. to Mar., 1929; hon. A.D.C., to gov., 1929; ag. dir., crim. intell., 1932-36; supt., pol., 1933; divnl. supt., pol., 1936.

SCRIMGEOUR, COLIN GRAHAM.—B. 1903; ed. Gisborne pub. schl., N.Z.; home-missioner, Methodist Ch., 1923; city missioner, Auckland, 1926-32; dir., Friendly Road broadcasting statn., 1932-36; contrlr., N.Z. comm. broadcasting serv., 1936.

SCRIVENER, RUPERT FREDERIC.—B. 1890; assoc., R. Schl. of Mines, dipl., Imp. Coll. Science, assoc., I.M.M., assoc., Inst. of Mining and Metallurgy; survey work, England and Alaska, 1911-14; comsrd. serv., 1914-20; capt., 1918; served in Gallipoli, Egypt, Mesopotamia, India and Palestine; commanded, 29th Rly. Coy., Sappers and Miners, I.A.; asst. ch. engnr., Palestine rly., 1920; engnr., ways and wks., Palestinerly., 1924.

SCRIVENOR, T. V. B. 1908; ed. King's Schl., Canterbury and Oriel Coll., Oxford; B.A., 1930; apptd. temp. asst. prin., C.O., Oct., 1930; dist. offr., Tanganyika Territory, 1933; asst. dist. offr., Palestine, 1937.

SCUPHAM, MAJOR WILLIAM ERIC HALSTEAD, M.C.—B. 1893; ed. St. Paul's Schl.; army, 1914-19; dipl., anthropology, Cambridge, 1926; asst. polit. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Jan., 1920; dist. offr., Mar., 1931; dep. prov. comr., Jan., 1934; prov. comr., Oct., 1936; senr. prov. comr., Dec., 1937; asst. dep. ch. sec., 1930 and 1937; ag. adminis. sec., 1938.

SCULLIN, RT. HON. JAMES HENRY, P.C.—El. to Commonwealth H. of R. for Corangamite, Victoria, 1910; defeated, gen. el., 1913; M.H.R. for Yarra, Victoria, since 1922; prime min. of the Commonwealth of Australia, 1929-31.

SCULLY, HUGH DAY.—B. 1883; ed. pte. and pub. schls. and Parkdale Collegiate Inst., Toronto, Univ., of Toronto; 1st cls. hom. and gold med. in polit. sc. and Alexander MacKenzie fellowship, 1906; commr., excise, Canada, 1932; commr., cust., 1933.

SEAGER, BASIL WILLIAM.—B. 1898; ed. abroad; mily. serv., 1916-22; Br. pro-consul, Jeddah, Saudi Arabia, (Br. agency and consulate, later Br. Lega.), 1926-33; lent to Aden govt. as temp. polit. offr., during Anglo-Yemeni treaty negotiations, 1933-34; reverted to Jedda as Br. pro-consul, Mar.-Aug., 1934; polit. offr., Aden, Sept., 1934; ag. polit. sec. for various periods in 1935-38.

BRAGOR, ERNEST GOLDFINCH, M.B.E.—B. 1886; clk. to res. commr., New Hebrides, 1902; inspr. of native labourers, Br. residency, New Hebrides, 1909; ag. astt. to res. commr., 1911-12 and 1915-16; joint ag. native advocate, Oct., 1915, to Dec., 1922; ag. astt. to res., Nov., 1919 to Feb., 1920 and Sept. to Dec., 1921; commdt., constab., Aug., 1922; dep. commr., W. Pacific (New Hebrides), Nov., 1922; in ch. of accts., May to Aug., 1923; ag. astt. to res. commr. on various occasions, 1923-38; dist. agt. and lab. inspr., Apr., 1923; agt., Efate dist. and commdt., pol. (Br. divn.) Apr., 1925.

SEDGWICK, P. C. M.—B. 1911; ed. St. Lawrence Coll., Ramsgate, Brasenose Coll., Oxford and Queen's Coll., Cambridge; cadet, Hong Kong, Sept., 1934; passed Cantonese, 1937; 3rd astt. col. sec., Aug., 1937; admstr. astt., urban coun., Sept., 1937.

SEDGWICK, RICHARD ROMNEY.—B. 1894; ed. Westminster and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; schol., Trinity Coll., 1912; 1st cl. hist. tripes, pt. I, 1914 and pt. II, 1915; Gladstone prize, 1914; elected fellow of Trinity Coll., 1919; M.A., 1919; apptd. under reconstruction scheme astt. prin., Min. of Labour, 1919; astt. prin., C.O., 1922; pte. sec. to parly., under sec. of State, D.O., 23rd Sept., 1929; prin., D.O., 1st Oct., 1930; offl. sec. to high commr. for U.K. in Australia, Sept., 1938.

SEED, CHARLES WILLIAM STANLEY.—B. 1898; ed. St. John's (R.C.) Coll., Wonesham, Surrey; Artists' Rifles, 1916; 2nd lieut., 12th London Regt., 1917; served in France, 1918-19; (G.S. and Victory Meds.); col. audit dept., July, 1921; astt. audr., Nigeria, Sept., 1921; astt. audr., Nyasaland, Dec., 1926; ag. senr. astt. audr. in 1927, 1928 and 1929; audr., Seychelles and Somaliland, July, 1930; also pte. sec. to gov., Seychelles, 1931-33 and to O.A.G., July-Sept., 1933; senr. astt. audr., S.S. and F.M.S., 1936; ag. dep. audr., Nov., 1936.

SEKL, GEORGE FREDERICK.—B. 1895; ed. Macolesfield Grammar Schl. and Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford (schol.); on milly. serv. Cheshire Regt. in Gallipoli, Egypt and Palestine; apptd. to air mlny., 28th July, 1919; seconded for serv. in C.O., as astt. prin., 9th Jan., 1922; pte. sec. to parly. under secy. of state for the Cola. (Mr. Wm. Lunn, M.P.), 10th June 1929; prin., 17th Sept., 1929.
SEHEULT, JOSEPH EMANUEL.—B. 1881; entd. civ. serv., Trinidad, Mar., 1902; ch. clk., dept. of agric., Jan., 1916; astt. censor, 1914; censor, Apr., 1918; man.-sec., agrl. bank, Jan., 1928.

SEHEKULT, LEO GABRIEL, A.C.G.I., A.M. Inst. C.E.—B. 1886; astt. dist. offr., pub. works, Trinidad, Mar., 1911; 2nd grade dist. offr., Sept., 1916; 1st grade dist. offr., June, 1917; gen. astt. to astt. D.P.W., Jan., 1925; exec. engr., July, 1930; divnl. engr., Jan., 1936.

SELBORNE, 2nd EARL OF (creat. 1882), VISCOUNT WOLMER, BARON SELBORNE (U.K.), K.G. (1909), P.C. (1900), G.C.M.G. (1905), TST RT. HON. WM. WALDEGRAVE PALMER.—B. 1859; ed. at Winchester and at Univ. Coll., Oxford; B.A. (1st ast. hist.), 1882; astt. priv. sec. to Mr. Childers, Chanc. of Excheq., 1882-86; was mem. of parlmt. for R. Hants, 1886-92, and for W. Edinburgh City, 1892-96; succeeded his father, 1896; Under S. of S. for Cola., June, 1895; chmn. Pacific cable comtee., 1896; mem. of Pacific cable comtee., 1899; first lord of the admiralty, Nov. 12th, 1900; high commr. for S. Africa, and gov. and commr.-in-chief of Transvaal and O.R.C. 1905 to 1910; ceased to be gov. of O.R.C. on grant of resp. govt., June, 1907; pres. of bd. of agric. and fisheries, 1915-16.
SELBY, JOHN ALEXANDER ROBERTSON, M.R. Ch.B. (Edin.)—B. 1900; health offr., (venereal dis.); med. dep., Hong Kong, 1930.

SELWYN-CLARKE, PROV. SELWYN, M.C. M.D., B.S. (Lond.), F.R.O.P. (Lond.), M.R.C.S. (Eng.), D.P.H. (Eng.) with distinc., T.M.T. and H. (Lond.), barrister-at-law.—B.1893; ed. St. Bartholomew's Hosp. and Lond. Univ.; med. offr., W.A.M.S., Oct., 1919; med. offr., health W.A.M.S., Gold Coast, Nov., 1919; senr. med. offr., W.A.M.S., Oct., 1924; awarded Langley Memorial Prize, 1924; dep. dir., san. serv., May to Aug., 1927; dep. for Gold Coast to Yellow Fever confce., Dakar, Senegal, 1928; visited U.S.A. and Canada at invitation of Rockefeller Foundation, 1928; astt. dir., san. serv., June, 1929; called to bar, Gray's Inn, 1929; awarded Médaille d'Argent des Épidémies for work in connection with outbreak of yellow fever in W. Africa, 1929; ch. health offr., F.M.S., 1930; astt. dir., med. serv., Gold Coast, Jan., 1932; dep. dir., health serv., 1933; ag. dir., med. serv. in 1934 and 1936; G.C. dep. to Pan-African Health Confce., Johannesburg, 1936; dep. dir., health serv., Nigeria, 1936; dir., med. services, Hong Kong, 1937.

SEMPLE, J. M., B.A., M.B., B.Ch., B.A.O. T.C.D., L.M. (Rotunda).—Sandhurst, 1915; lieut., R. Irish Rifles, 1915-18; med. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Mar., 1922; med. offr., Zanzibar, Oct., 1924; ag. S.S.O., July, 1925 to Oct., 1926; ag. D.D.S.S., Feb. to Aug., 1928; and Nov., 1928 to Aug., 1929; senr. med. offr., Uganda, 1934.
SEMPLE, HON. ROBERT.—B. 1876; ed. Sofia Schol., N.S.W.; mem. N.Z. H. of R., 1918-19 and since 1928; min. of pub. wks. and min. of transp., 1935.

SENEVIRATNE, LEOPOLD JAMES DE SILVA, LL.B., Barrister-at-law, Lincoln's Inn.—B. 1899; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Nov., 1923; attd. SENNETT, CECIL WILLIAM ARTHUR—B. 1890; ed. Plymouth Coll., Edinburgh Univ. and non-collegiate, Oxford (B.A., 1913); cadet, S.S. 1914; astt. regir., impts. and expts., 1917; astt. supt., govt. monopolies 1920; astt. dist. offr., 1920; offr., cl. IV., Nov., 1921; astt. supt., govt. monopolies, Penang, Nov., 1923; dep. regir., sup. ct. and astt. offr. assignee, Penang, Jan., 1925; dist. offr., Dindings, Mar., 1926 (offr., cl. III.); 2nd mag., astt. dist. judge and coroner, Penang, Nov., 1926; collr., land rev., Penang, Apr., 1927 and Mar., 1930; offr. assignee and astt. regir., coys., Penang, July, 1929; offr., cl. II., Nov., 1931; dist. offr., L. Perak Apr., 1933; 1st mag., Ipoh, Oct., 1933; legal advr., Trengganu, Dec., 1936; commr., lands, S.S., Mar., 1938.
SERTSIOS, B. D., O.B.E. (1931).—B. 1876; ed., Athens; Ph.D., University of Athens; B.A., Greek College, Serres; barrister-at-law, Middle

TEMPLE, 1906; judge, dist. ct., Cyprus, 1909; ass't king's advoc., June, 1928; title changed to ass't atty.-gen., May, 1925; ag. solr.-gen., Apr. to May, 1927; puisne judge, Oct., 1927; ag. ch. just., July-Sept., 1934.

SETON, CLAUD RAMSAY WILMOT, M.C. (1918).—B. 1888; ed. Framlingham Coll., Leatham, Margate and Univ. Coll., London; admitted solr., sup. ct., England, 1910; on mily. serv., 1914-19; wounded, 1918; twice ment. in despatches; called to bar, Gray's Inn, 1928; pres., dist. ct., Jaffa, Palestine, 1919; judl. adviser, Transjordan, Oct., 1926; Order of Istighal, 2nd cls.; pres., dist. ct., Haifa, 1931; puisne judge, Jamaica, 1935; puisne judge, Jamaica, 1935; publication "Legislation of Trans-Jordan, 1918-1930."

SEVIGNY, HON. ALBERT, K.C.—B. 1881; ed. Nicolet and Valleyfield (Quebec) Colls. and Laval Univ., B.A., L.L.B.; advoc., city of Quebec; elec. to H. of C., Canada, g.e. 1911; dep. speaker, 1915; speaker, 1916; min. of inland rev., 1917; defeated at g.e., 1917; mem. of the P.C. for Canada; judge, superior ct., Quebec, Mar., 1921.

SKWELL, CHARLES YULE BLACKWOOD.—B. 1888; govt. roads dept., New Zealand, 1906; surv., rev. survey dept., Perak, Oct., 1908; surv., 1st grade, Apr., 1909; asst. sup't., surveys, Kedah, Aug., 1917; sup't., rev. surveys, N. Sembilan, Jan., 1926; ditto, Selangor, July, 1929; senr. sup't., surveys, S'pore, May, 1933.

SEYMOUR-HALL, CHARLES F.R.S.A., F.R.E.S., M.R.A.S., F.Z.S.—B. 1897; prob. flight offr., R.N.A.S., 1917; flight sub-lieut., R.N., 1917; lieut., R.A.F., 1918; ment. in despatches, 1918; capt., H.M.A.Z. 31, 1918; demob., 1919; asst. dist. coms'n., Zanzibar, 1921; ag. 2nd ast. sec. and priv. sec. to ag. Br. res., 1923-4; ag. ast. admn. gen., 1926-27; ag. ast. sec., pvt. sec. to Br. res., elk. of couns. and edr., off. gaz., 1928; ag. prov. coms'n. (with seat on leg. coun.), 1929, 1930, 1931, 1932 and 1934; dist. offr., Tanganyika Territory, 1935; assisted in revision of Madan's Swahili Dictionary, 1931.

SHACKLES, DEER HOLMES.—B. 1900; ed. R.N.C., Osborne and Greshams Schls., Holt; solr.; asst. coms'n., lands, Gold Coast, 1929; dep. regiar., high ct., Tanganyika Territory, 1933; ag. regiar., 1933; regiar., high ct., Uganda, 1934.
SHAFTAIN, FRANK WALTER.—B. 1891; joined Hong Kong pol., 1912; sub. inspr., 1923; inspr., 1929; ch. inspr., 1935; col. pol. long serv., med., 1937; asst. sup't., pol., 1938.

SHAND, KEITH RAE.—B. 1881; ed. S. Andrew's Coll., Grahamstown, S. Africa; Assoc. M.Inst.C.E., 1913; irrign. dept., Union S. Africa, Apr., 1917; circle engnr., Dec., 1924; ch. constr. engnr., July, 1933; ast. dir., Dec., 1937.

SHARP, COMMBR. GEORGE ARTHUR COXALL, D.S.C., R.N. (ret.).—B. 1891; ed. R. Naval Colls., Osborne and Dartmouth; war serv., 1914-18; ret., 1920; marine offr., Tanganyika, 1927; marine supt., Takoradi, Gold Coast, 1936.

SHARP, GEORGE FREDERICK.—B. 1888; ed. Univ. Coll. Schi., and King's Coll., London; compt. and acct.-gen.'s dept., G.P.O., 1907-12; ast., postal dept., S. Nigeria, 1912-14; acct. posts and tels. dept., N. and S. Provs. of Nigeria, 1914-19; ch. acct., posts and tels., Nigeria, 1920; on mily. serv., London Rifle Brig., 1908-12; Nigerian Land Contgt., 1914-18; ch. acct., P. and T., Nigeria, 1920; ag. dep. P.M.G. in 1928-30; ret., Oct., 1930; reappointed col. postmr., Barbados, July, 1931 and in 1934.

SHARP, WILLIAM JOSEPH CURRAN.—B. 1888; ed. Gram. Schi., Maldon and Regent St. Polytechnic; war serv., 1915-19, R.E. Signal Sect.; U.K.P.O., 1904-19; sup't., P.O., S.S., May, 1921; sup't., teleg., Penang, Apr., 1924; ast., Penang, Nov., 1930; sup't., Malacca, Apr., 1932; do., Selangor and Pahang, Feb., 1934; do., Malacca, Sept., 1934.
SHARPE, H. B.—Ast. dist. coms'n., Kenya Colony, Apr., 1919; dist. coms'n., 1932.

SHAW, BARNABAS JAMES DOLD.—B. 1877; clk., cust., Port Elizabeth, 1897; senr. olk., Pretoria, 1915; prin. clk., 1920; ch. olk., Cape Town, 1928; collr., cust. and S.M., E. London, 1932; do., Johannesburg, 1934; do., Port Elizabeth, 1934; do., Cape Town, 1935.

SHAW, FREDERIC LLOYD, B.A. (Hons.) Lond.—B. 1892; ag. headmast., English Coll., Johore Bahru, Feb., 1921; headmast., govt. Lumpur, Aug., 1929; sup't., educn., Johore, 1936.

SHAW, GEORGE INGRAM, M.M., M.B., Ch.B. (Glasgow), D.T.M. and H. (Eng.)—B. 1896; M.O.H. Malaya, 1928; M.O., med. dept., Hong Kong, 1934; M.O. in oh., Vict. hosp., Jan., 1935; do., Vict. gaol, inf. dis. hosp., officers' families (H.K.), remand home and pol. urg., H.K., 1935; M.O. H.K. prison, 1938.
SHAW, JOHN HENRY DE BURGH, B.L.—B. 1887; ed. Clifton Schi., Dalkey, Dublin and Corrig Schi., Kingstown; ast. dist. coms'n., Sierra Leone, May, 1921; dist. coms'n., May, 1928.

SHAW, JOHN VALENTINE WISTAR.—B. 1894; ed. Repton Schi.; service with R.E., 1914-19; ast. dist. coms'n., Gold Coast, 1921; dist. coms'n., 1925; ast. col. sec., 1928; seconded for duty at C.O., Mar.-Sept., 1930; ast. sec., grade F, Palestine, 1935; senr. ast. sec., 1938.
SHAW, ROBERT PHILLIP, A.R.I.B.A. (Chartered Architect).—B. 1888; ed. Bathgate Acad. and Heriot Watt Coll., Edinburgh; architect, P.W.D., Hong Kong, 1920; exec. engnr., 1937.

SHEEHAN, JOHN JOSEPH.—B. 1896; ed. Christian Schls., Dublin Univ. and Univ. V., Mar., 1923; dep. contrlr., rubber exp'ts., Johore, in adln., Aug., 1923; offr., cls. IV, Mar., 1927; dist. offr., Temerloh, Jan., 1930; offr., cls. III, Mar., 1932; dist. offr., K. Pilah, Nov., 1933; offr., cls. II, Mar., 1937; pub. trust. and offr. admtr., F.M.S., Nov., 1937; contr., rubber, Malaya, July, 1938.

SHELLEY, JAMES, M.A. Camb., Dipl. in Educn., Camb.—Ed. Bablake schl., Coventry and Christ's Coll., Camb.; lect. in educn., Manchester Univ., 1910-14; prof. of educn., Southampton Univ. Coll., N.Z., 1920-36; dir., broadcasting, nat. broadcasting serv., N.Z., 1936; served R.F.A., hon. rank major, 1916-20; prof., emeritus, Canterbury Univ. Coll., N.Z., 1937.
SHELLISH, ALEXANDER JOHN.—B. 1881; jun. clk., postal dept., Cyprus, 1902; asst. island postmr., 1921; postmr.-gen., 1934.

SHELWELL-WHITE, GROFFREY HENRY, Barrister-at-Law.—B. 1897; ed. Whitgift Schi.; comand. with R.F.A. in India, Mesopotamia and Persia, 1916-18; asst. pol. offr., Mesopotamia, 1918-20; personal asst. to Sir Arnold Wilson, ag. siv. comsmr., 1918; Iraq rebellion, 1920; A.D.C., Zanzibar, 1921; priv. sec. to res., ag. 1st asst. sec., secretariat, elk. to prot. coun. and edr. off., gaz. at various periods; higher standard Swahili, 1925; pres., Pemba Swahili exam. bd., 1929; dist. regstr., Pemba, 1929 and 1930; ag. res. mag., Pemba, 1929 and 1930; called to bar, 1930; pyte. sec. to Sultan, 1930-35; asst. sec., secretariat, 1930-35; clk., exec. and leg. couns., 1931-35; edr., Offl. Gaz., 1931-33; trades licensing commr., 1934-35; ag. asst. ch. sec., 1934; ag. asst. ch. sec. and prot. comsmr., 1935; Brilliant Star of Zanzibar, 1936; ch. asst. sec., Gibraltar, 1936; ag. col. sec., May-Sept., 1937; fin. advcr. Trans-Jordan, 1938; author of "A Guide to Zanzibar."
SHELTON, C. F., M.D., B.S., D.T.M. & H., M.R.C.P.—B. 1888; civil surgeon, Serbian red cross, 1913; surgeon lieut., 1914-19; med. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Sept., 1921; junr. med. specialist, 1934.

SHELTON, JAMES, F.I.C.—B. 1886; ed. City and Guilds Coll.; asst. rech. and analytical chem. in the sc. and tech. dept., Imp. Inst., G. Britain, Mar., 1909; 2nd asst. govt. analyst, Singapore, Feb., 1915; chem., geol. dept., F.M.S., Feb., 1917; in charge of geol. dept., June, 1917; on sp. duty at Inst. Med. Resch., Jan., 1923; in charge, trade and cust. lab., Mar., 1930 and July, 1933; ag. ch. chem., Inst. Med. Resch., F.M.S., Mar., 1931 and Feb., 1935; ch. chem., Inst. Med. Resch., F.M.S., Oct., 1937.

SHEPPARD, CHARLES HENRY, O.B.E. (1932).—B. 1897; European War, 1914-1918; ment. in desps.; Kurdish gendarmerie, 1919-21; inspecting offr., Iraq pol., 1922-31; Iraq gallantry med., 1932; asst. commdt., pol., Somaliland, 1931; pol. offr., Aden, 1938.

SHEPSTONE, FRANCIS ALLAN, K.C.—B. 1879; ed. Harrow; rly. constrn., Pretoria-Pietersburg, 1898-99; Anglo-Boer war, 1899-1901; barrister, Inner Temple, 1903; Transvaal bar, 1907; Natal, 1908; farming, 1912-22; S.A.H.R., 1918; solr., 1922-29; Natal bar, 1930; ag. judge, native high ct., 1932; K.C., 1934; judge, native high ct., 1934.
SHERGOLD, F. V.—B. 1900; apptd. after compet. exam., asst. clk., C.O., Apr., 1918; on mily. serv., 1918 to 1920; cler. offr., 1st Jan., 1920.

SHERIDAN, SIR JOSEPH, Kt. Bach. (1932), LL.D. (Dublin).—B. 1882; ed. Castleknock Coll. and Trin. Coll., Dublin; called to bar, Ireland, 1907; went Connaught circuit; judicial clk. SHERINGHAM, ANTHONY ILEX.—B. 1890; ed. Brighton Coll.; supt., Ceylon pol., 1909-31; on active serv., France 1917-18 (lieut., 8th Hussars); comsmr., pol., and comsmr., prisons, Zanzibar, Apr., 1931.

SHERLOCK, DAVID THOMAS JOSEPH, K.C. (1922), B.A.T.C.D., M.B.E. (Mil.).—B. 1881; judl. comsmr. and offl. mem., leg. coun., N. Borneo, Dec., 1920; ch. just., Feb., 1926; ret., Aug., 1935; judge, ct. of appl., Jamaica, 1936.
SHERRARD, PETER JAMES CORBY, B.A. (Cantab).—B. 1913; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Oct., 1936; office asst., July, 1938.

SHERWELL, ARNOLD GUY.—B. 1891; ed. St. Edward's Sch. and Brasenose Coll., Oxford; M.A. (schl. of jurisprudence, hon. cl. SHIELDS, DR. T. DRUMMOND, M.C., M.P.—B. 1881; ed. Edinburgh Univ. (M.B., Ch. B.); served European War as offr. R. SHILL, A. T.—B. 1896; on active serv. with Rifle Brig. and Dorset Regt., 1915-17; wounded; Imp. cust. and excise dept., 1919; also dep. supt., mercantile marine, recor. of wreck, regiar. shipping, Lascar transfer offr., etc. asst. recer. gen., Gambia, Aug., 1928; ag. asst. col. sec., Apr. to Oct., 1929 and from July, 1931; contr., food and fuel, May, 1931; supervisor, cust. and excise dept., Mauritius, Apr., 1932.

SHIPWAY, WALTER ROBERT, M.B.R. (1924).—B. 1879; apptd., after compet. exam., 2nd div. clk., and assigned to C.O., Dec., 1896; minor staff clk., 1911; regsr. of col. laws, 1st Apr., 1922; staff offr., promotions, 19th Nov., 1928.

SHIRLEY, EVELYN PHILIP SEWALLIS, O.R.E.—B. 1900; ed. St. Columba's Coll. Rathfarnham, Dublin and R. Mily. Coll., Camberley; joined R. Irish Fusiliers, Dec., 1918; N. Persia and Iraq, 1920; seconded, K.A.R., Jan., 1923; 2/1st K.A.R., Tanganyika and Somaliland Camel Corps; 3rd grade comsmr., Somaliland, Sept., 1929; asst. dist. offr. 1932.

SHORLAND, CHRISTOPHER WILLIAM, B.A., Barrister-at-Law.—B. 1902; ed. Wellington and Exeter Coll., Oxford; cadet, F.M.S., 1924; offr., cl. V, Dec., 1927; offr., cl. IV, Dec., 1931; offr., cl. III, Dec., 1936; dist. offr., Kuantan, July, 1936; dep. contr., labour, Kedah, Apr., 1938.

SHREWSBURY, HERBERT SUTCLIFFE, F.I.C., F.C.S.—B. 1878; ed. Newchurch Grammar Sch., Nottingham Univ.; asst. govt. analyst, Trinidad, 1910; prin. asst. govt. analyst, 1912; govt. analyst, 1920; author of various papers on org. and analytical processes, and on agrl. and forensic chemistry.

SHUCKBURGH, SIR JOHN EVELYN, K.C.M.G. (1922), C.B. (1918).—B. 1877; ed. Eton and King's Coll., Cambridge; B.A., 1899; M.A., 1906; apptd. jur. clk., store dept., I. O., Oct., 1900; transf. to judl. and pub. dept., Mar., 1901; joint editor, "India Office List," Oct., 1901; priv. sec. to perm. under-sec. of state, July, 1902; senr. clk., polit. dept., Mar., 1906; asst. sec., polit. dept., Oct., 1912; sec., polit. dept., Mar., 1917; ag. asst. under sec. of state, C. O., 1st Mar., 1921; asst. under sec. of state, 1st Apr., 1924; dep. under sec. of state, 15th Aug., 1931.

SHUTE, GEOFFREY GAY, C.M.G. (1939), B.A. (Cantab.).—B. 1892; ed. St. Andrew's Sch., Eastbourne, Uppingham and Clare Coll., Cambridge; ast. dist. offr., Nigeria, 1915; cl. I., admstve. serv., 1930; prin. ast. sec., S. Provs., 1931; sec., S. Provs., 1933; staff grade, 1936; ag. ch. omsnr., S.P., 1937-38.

SIDEBOOTHAM, JOHN BIDDULPH, B.A. (1914), M.A. (1920).—B. 1891; ed. (Sir J. Maffey) to W. Indies, 1936.

SIDLEY, WILLIAM HENRY FRANCIS, B.A. (Trinity Coll., Dublin).—Cadet, R.I.C., Aug., 1908; 3rd cls. dist. inspr., Oct., 1908; 2nd cls., Feb., 1910; 1st cls., Aug., 1919; counsr., Nov., 1920; served in R.I.C. until disbandment of that force, July, 1922; depot inspr. SIMMS, HENRY, O.B.E. (1936), M.V.O. (1917), A.M.I.C.E. (1913).—B. 1881; ast. engr., Gold Coast, 1908; ast. engr., Sierra Leone, 1912; D.P.W., Cyprus, 1919; D.P.W., Jamaica, 1926.

SIMPKINS, ALICE LOUISE, A.O.G.I., A.M. Inst.C.E., A.M.Inst.M.E.—B. 1901; ed. Eastbourne Coll. and City and Guilds Engng. Coll.; ast. engr., P.W.D., Nigeria, 1926; exec. engr., 1933; superintending engr., P.W.D., Cyprus, June, 1937; D.P.W., 1937.

SINCLAIR, HON. JOHN EWEN.—B. 1879; ed. local common schls.; mem. of swine comsn. which visited Europe in 1909; unsucc. cand., legis. ass., P.E.I., 1908; al. to H.C., g.e., 1917; re-el., 1921; min. without portfolio in King admn., 29th Dec., 1921; senator, June, 1930.

SINCLAIR-BURGESS, MAJOR-GEN. SIR WILLIAM LIVINGSTONE HATCHWELL, K.B.E. (1935), KT. Bach. (1934), C.B., C.M.G. (1918), D.S.O. (1916)—Lieut., N.Z. Arty. with rank brig.-gen., Aug., 1917-19; major, N.Z. staff corps, 1919; lieut.-col., Aug., 1919; arty. staff offr., cent. commd., Nov., 1919; G.S.O. (1) at G.H.Q., Apr., 1922; dir., mily. training and intell., Jan., 1924; ch. of gen. staff, Apr., 1924 with temp. rank col. on staff; ag. commdt., N.Z. mily. forces, Sep.-Dec., 1925; temp-brig., June, 1928; major-gen. and apptd. commdt. N.Z. mily. forces, Apr., 1931 to Apr., 1937; Croix d'officier de Legion of Honour; awarded American D.S.M.; six times ment. in des.

SINEL, ROY, A.R.A.N.Z.—Ed. Auckland Gram. Schl.; joined N.Z. lands and survey dept., 1904; health dept., 1915; war serv., 1915-19; pub. trust office, 1919; acctnt., wheat control bd., 1920; ch. acctnt., native trust, 1921; ch. inspr., employment div., lab. dept., 1934; supt., nat. provident fund and reglar. friendly socs., 1936.

SISNETT, SIR HERBERT KORTRIGHT DONNELL, KT. BACH. (1927).—Ed. at Harrison Coll., Barbados; called to the bar, Inner Tem., May, 1896; practised at bar, Barbados, June, 1897, to Feb., 1898; ag. junior pol. mag., Bridgetown, Barbados, Sept. to Nov., 1897; ag. res. mag. for St. Ann's, June to Dec., 1901; ag. priv. sec. to Sir A. Hamming, May to June, 1902, and priv. sec. to Mr. Olivier, the ag. gov't., June to Nov., 1902; clk. of cts., St. James, Jamaica, Apr., 1903; ag. res. mag., St. James and Trelawny, Sept., 1903, do., St. Elizabeth, Mar., 1904, do., Manchester, Apr. to June, 1904, do., St. James and Trelawny in 1904, 1905 and 1906; registr.-gen., Br. Hond., and dist. comanr. of Belize, Apr., 1907; ag. atty.-gen. and ag. ch. just., Br. Hond., on several occasions; J.P. for Br. Hond., 1907; stip. mag., B. Guiana, Mar., 1913; held inquiry into Rose Hall coolie riots, 1913, and was highly commended by S. of S.; ag. atty.-gen., 1919-20; seconded, puisne judge, Jamaica, 1920; 1st puisne judge, 1921; ch. just., Br. Hond., 1922; compiled Br. Hond. sup. ct. rules, 1926; apptd. arbitrator by gov'ts. of U.S.A. and Guatemala in case of P.W. Shufeldt v. the gov't. of Guatemala.

SITTA MPA LAM, CATHIRAVELU, B.A. (Cantab.), Barrister-at-law (Middle Temple).—B. 1898; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Feb., 1923; attd., Kalutara schl., Mar., 1923; ditto, Kandy kach., Mar., 1925; office asst., Puttalam kach., Aug., 1926; pol. mag., Matara, Apr., 1928; ditto, Puttalam, Oct., 1929; asst. govt. agt., Kurunegala, Nov., 1929; dist. judge, Kegalle, July, 1931; asst. govt. agt., Kurunegala, July, 1932; do., Batticaloa, May, 1934; do., Mannar, May, 1937.
SKAN, D. A., M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P., D.T.M., B.T.H. (Liv.).—B.1899; war serv., R.N.V.R., 1918-19; med. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Apr., 1926.

SKEETE, CECIL CARRINGTON, B.A. (Cantab.).—B. 1900; ed. Harrison Coll., Barbados and Jesus Coll., Cambridge; 3rd class hon. nat. sci. tripes pt. I., 1922; dipl. course, Schi. of Agr., Cambridge, 1922-23; asst. dir., agr., Barbados, Sept., 1923; ag. dir. agr., in 1924 and 1925; asst. dir., sci. and agr., Sept., 1925; ag. dir., sci. and agr., on various occasions, 1926-29.

SKELTON, OSCAR DOUGLAS, M.A., Ph.D.—B. 1878; ed. Orangeville and Cornwall High Schls., Queen's Univ. and Univ. of Chicago; counsellor, dept. of external affrs., 15th July, 1924; under-sec. of state for external affrs., 30th Mar., 1925.

SKENE, CHARLES EDWARD.—B. 1889; ed. St. Paul's Schi. and Wren's; asst. dist. comsnr., Gold Coast, Nov., 1912; seconded for serv. in col. sec.'s office, 1912-13; dist. comsnr., G. Coast, Nov., 1912; dist. sec.'s office, 1912-15; priv. sec. to ag. gov., 1914; ag. dist. comsnr., Ashanti, 1916-18; dist. comsnr., Oct., 1918; ag. pol. mag., Dec., 1922; dep. prov. comsnr., July, 1927; prov. comsnr., Sept., 1928; ag. sec., native affrs. Apr.-June, 1930 and Apr. to Oct., 1932; ag. dep. col. sec., June-Sept., 1930.
SKILTON, CAPT. FRANCIS LISNEY.—B. 1889; asst. supt., excise, F.M.S., Mar., 1924; supt., Oct., 1927; supt., cust., marine and chandu, Kelantan, Jan., 1937.

SKINNER, ARTHUR DONALD WILLIAM.—B. 1890; ed. Bedford Schi.; 4th olk., col. sec.'s office, Antigua, Aug., 1914; sub-inspr. of schools, St. Kitts-Nevis, Apl., 1915; o/c cadet corps, St. Kitts-Nevis, 1917; ag. inspr. of schls., Leeward Is., Apr., 1918 to Feb. 1920; clk. to comsnr., clk. of couns. and educn. dist. offr., Montserrat, Feb.-Oct., 1920; 1st clk., col. sec.'s office, clk. of couns., Antigua and supt., govt. printing office, Leeward Is., Nov., 1920; ag. asst. col. sec. and ag. clk. of couns. Leeward Is. on various occasions 1921-24; bursar, Achimota Coll., Gold Coast, 1927; ag. prin., Prince of Wales Coll., July to Sept., 1929; asst. col. sec., Sept., 1931; ag. clk., couns. May to June, 1932.

SKINNER, HUGH WELBY.—B. 1894; war serv., 1914-19; served in France, India, Mesopotamia; capt., T.A. res., gen. list, R.A.; appt. col. audit dept., 1923; asst. audr., Nyasaland, 1923; asst. audr., Nigeria, 1926; col. audit, 1930; asst. audr., Gold Coast, 1935; joint audr., Somaliland and Aden, 1937.

SKINNER, LESLIE ERNEST.—B.1890; Gloucestershire R.F.A., 1903 to 1910; B.S.A. Pol., 1910 to 1913; E.A.P. pol., Sept., 1913; asst. supt., 1915; staff offr. to inspr.-gen. of pol. and prisons, Kenya Colony and Uganda Prot., 1920 to 1922; supt., pol., Nyasaland, 1922; ag. ch. comsnr., pol. and ch. inspr., prisons, 1923 to 1924; dep. comsnr., pol., Zanzibar, 1924; ag. comsnr., pol. and comsnr., prisons on various occasions, 1925-38.

SKINNER, ROBERT BURNELL, M.B.E. (1933).—B.1893; ed. Bedford Modern Schi.; held various minor appts., Leeward Is., 1910-20; clk. to admr., and clk., couns., St. Kitts-Nevis, Nov., 1920; ch. clk. to admr. and clk., couns., Dominica, Nov., 1924; sec., loan bd., May, 1926; mem., Roseau town bd., Apr., 1927; comsnr., income tax, May, 1927; priv. sec. to Sir Eustace Fiennes, Nov., 1927 to June, 1929; ag. treas., Dominica, June to Sept., 1929; offl. mem., exec. and leg. couns., Dominica, June to Sept., 1929; O.A.G., Montserrat, Feb., 1930; ag. treas. and offl. mem., exec. and leg. couns., May to Sept., 1930; treas., Antigua and fedl. treas., Mar., 1931; offl. mem., fedl. exec. coun. and gen. leg. coun.; mem., exec. and leg. coun., Antigua; dep. treas. and comsnr., income tax, Trinidad, Mar., 1934; ag. treas., Aug.-Dec., 1936.

SLATER, SIR ALEXANDER RANSFORD, G.C.M.G. (1933), K.C.M.G. (1924); C.M.G. (1916); C.B.E. (1918).—B. 1874; ed. at King Ed. schi., Birmingham, and Emm. Coll., Camb. (scholar); B.A. 1897 (30th wrangler); cadet, Ceylon, Nov., 1898; extra off. asst. govt. agt., W. P., Aug., 1899; 2nd asst., P.M.G., Mar., 1900; ag. 2nd asst. col. sec., Dec., 1901; on spec. duty at camp for prisoners of war Diyalatwala, July, 1902; ag. 2nd asst. col. sec., Jan., 1903; clk. legis. coun., May, 1904; dist. judge, Badulla, Apl., 1906; additional asst. col. sec. and clk., legis. couns., Apl., 1907; on special deptn. to Straits and F.M.S., Jan., 1908; 2nd asst. col. sec., Feb., 1908; ag. prin. asst. col. sec., Dec., 1909; dep. collr. of cust., Colombo, Sept., 1910; joint comsnr. to report on salaries of pub. serv., Nov., 1911; priv. asst. col. sec., Sept., 1912, and clk. to exec. coun.; col. sec., G. Coast, 18th Aug., 1914; on sp. duty in Togoland, 1st to 28th Sept., 1915; ag. gov'r., G. Coast on several occasions; gov'r. and comdr.-in-chief, Sierra Leone, Apr., 1922; gov'r. and comdr-in-chief, Gold Coast, 1927; capt.-gen. and gov.-in-ch., Jamaica, 1932; resigned, 1934.

SLREP, CAPT. ARTHUR, B.Sc. (Manchester).—B. 1894; cadet, F.M.S., Feb., 1920; attd. land office, Kelantan, Feb., 1920; passed cadet, Nov., 1921; asst. supt., lands, Kelantan, Mar., 1923; asst. dist. offr., Mar., 1925; dist. offr., Aug., 1926; offr., cls. III, Mar., 1931; 2nd asst. sec. to gov't., F.M.S., May, 1931; offr., cls. II, Mar., 1936; ag. finan. advr. and treas., F.M.S. in addn., Jan., 1937; ag. dep. fin. sec., F.M.S., Feb., 1938.
SLEIGH, JOHN MANSON, B.S., A.M.I.C.E.—B. 1899; ed. Aberdeen Gram. Schi. and Glasgow Univ.; asst. engr., P.W.D., Nigeria, 1926; senr. exec. engr., 1937.

SLEIGHT, GEORGE FREDERICK, M.A. (New Zealand), Ph.D. (London).—B. 1906; ed. Waipuani and Univ. Coll., Wellington, New Zealand and London Univ.; headmr., Normal Schi., Cyprus, Aug., 1937 (title changed to principal 1938).

SMALL, SIR ALEXANDER SYM, K.B.E. (1934), C.M.G. (1937), M.A., B.Sc. (Glas.).—B. 1887; ed. Glasgow Univ.; cadet, F.M.S., Jan., 1911; offr., cls. V, sec. to res., N. Sembilan, Mar., 1914; offr., cls. IV, Jan., 1919; offr., cls. III, Jan., 1923; town planning admstr., K. Lumpur, Dec., 1926; offr., cls. II, finan. comsnr. and admin. gen., Johore, June, 1927; offr., cls. Ia, finan. advr. and treas., F.M.S., Dec., 1930; treas., S.S., June, 1932; col. sec., S.S., Dec., 1935; O.A.G., S.S., and ag. H. Cr., Malay States, Apr.-Sept., 1936, and from Jan., 1939.

SMALLEY, JAMES THORNTON, M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.).—B. 1892; late clinical asst., asst. house surg., out-pat. offr. and house surg., Guy's hosp.; clinical asst., Evelina hosp., London; R.M.O., Colony hosp., Suva, Fiji. D.M.O., Ra; D.M.O. and M.O.H., and port med. offr., Levuka; stip. mag., Levuka, 1910-1913; med. offr., Kowloon and New Territories, Hong Kong, med. offr., Kowloon-Canton rly., and med. offr. in charge, pub. mortuary, Kowloon, 1913; lectures in Pharmacology and Therapeutics, and examn., physiology, Hong Kong Univ.; J.P., 1919; visiting med. offr., Kwong Wah hospital; med. offr. in charge, Lai Chi Kok branch prison; surg. lieut., Hong Kong Volunteer Corps; med. offr. govt. civil hosp., 1923; ag. prin. civ. med. offr., Nov. 1927 to Jan., 1928; ag. dep. dir., med. and sanry. services, Feb., 1928 to Feb., 1929; senr. med. offr. and in ch., Kowloon hosp., Jan., 1931; ag. D.D.M.S., June, 1937 and in addn. D.M.S., July, 1937; ag. D.D.M.S., Mar., 1938.

SMALLWOOD, MARGARET, M.B., B.S., Grad. L.S.T.M. and Hyg. Trin. Coll., Melbourne Univ.—B. 1898; lady med. offr., inf. wel. centre, Ipoh, May, 1924; do., hosp., Ipoh, Nov., 1927; do., women's hosp., K. Kangar, Dec., 1933 and Nov., 1935; do., hosp., Ipoh, Sept., 1934; do., Johore Bharu, Dec., 1937.

SMALLWOOD, THOMAS NORMAN, J.P.—B. 1892; ed. Shrewsbury (England), Wellington (N.Z.), and privately; war serv., 1914-17; service with Auckland savings bank, 1908-35; joint managing dir., N.Z. mortgage corp., 1935; joint managing dir., N.Z. state advances corp., 1936.

SMART, ARCHIBALD GULPH HOLDSWORTH, M.B.E. Kedah, Oct., 1920; health offr., Kedah, Nov., 1921; sr. health offr., Penang, May, 1927; ditto, Perak, May, 1928; State med. and health offr., Selangor and regisr. gen. births and deaths, F.M.S., Jan., 1932; mem., S.S. and F.M.S. med. coun., Feb., 1932; ret.; senr. med. offr., St. Vincent, 1930; asst. med. advr., C.O., 1938.

SMART, HAROLD PHILIP.—B. 1899; ed. Michaelhouse Diocesan Schl., Natal, Glen Schl. of Agr., O.F.S., (Farmers' Weekly gold medallist) Transvaal Univ. Coll., Pretoria, (Farmers' Weekly schl.), B. So. Honduras, 1928; senr. agr. offr., Tanganyika, 1938.

SMART, LESLIE MASON, C.B.E. (1936)—B. 1889; asst. supt., Kenya and Uganda Rly., Apr., 1919 to Mar., 1927; dep. traffic man., Tanganyika rly., Mar., 1927; gen. man., Gold Coast rly. and hbr. authority, Takoradi hbr., Oct., 1933; gen. man., F.M.S.rlys., 1938.

SMART, W. IRVING.—B. 1875; ed. Upper Canada Coll., Toronto; graduated from Queen's Univ.; engaged on surveys for the Dom. govt., 1897-98; ent. rly. serv., 1900; prof., rly. engnr. and opern., McGill Univ., Montreal, 1907-14; sp. engrnr., Canadian Nat. Rlys., 1923-28; gen. supt., trans., Western region, C.N.R., 1928-30; dep. min. of rlys. and canals, Dom. of Canada; also govt. dir., Canadian Nat. Rlys., 1930.

SMARTT, FITZPATRICK FORBES PERCY.—Ed. Denstone Coll.; probationer, col. audit dept., July, 1921; asst. audr., Br. Honduras, Oct., 1921; asst. audr., N. Rhodesia, May, 1925; ditto, Tanganyika Territory, 1934; senr. asst. audr., Apr., 1937.
SMIT, DOUGLAS LAING.—B. 1885; Cape civ. serv., 1903; pub. progr., 1912; mag., 1927; under sec. for just., Pretoria, 1933; sec., nat. affrs., 1934.

SMIT, HAROLD PRINGLE.—B. 1883; joined Cape civ. serv., Jan., 1901; office of dir. of mil. intell., May-Aug., 1902; Transvaal law dept., Sept., 1902; ch. clk. to atty.-gen., Transvaal, July, 1908; addl. mag., Benoni, Sept., 1914; war stores comm., 1915; mag., Bloemhof dist., Dec., 1915; ch. clk., dept. of just., Dec., 1917; pub. serv. inspr., Feb., 1922; sec. for S.W. Africa, Apr., 1923; dep. admstr., May-Oct., 1924 and July-Dec., 1928; sec. for lands, Feb., 1934; contr. and audr.-gen., Nov., 1935.

SMITH, LIEUT. ARTHUR BERTRAM, F.R.G.S.—B. 1889; ed. R. Masonic Schl., Buahay; sub. lieut., R.N.R., Oct., 1914; ag. lieut., Jan., 1915; served in 8th submarine flotilla, 1914-15; Baltic submarine flotilla, 1915-17; Russian Order of St. Anne, 3rd cls. (with swords), 1915; Russian Order of St. Vladimir, 4th cls. (with awards), 1916; commanded submarine C 14 (N. Sea), Mar. to Nov., 1918, and various surrendered German submarines, Nov., 1918 to Mar., 1919; port offr., Dar-es-Salaam, Mar., 1919; harbmr. and supt. of lighthouses, Trinidad, Feb., 1926.

SMITH, ALLAN CHALMERS, B.A., M.C.—B. 1893; ed. SMITH, A. H. W., M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.), D.P.M. (Univ. of Lond.)—Med. supt., St. Ann's lunatic asylum, Trinidad, Nov., 1934; med. supt., ment. hosp., Br. Guiana, 1937.

SMITH, B. O.—Cler. asst., post. dept., Br. Guiana, Aug., 1898; sec., leave and pensions comm., 1920; sec., deptl. salaries comte., 1921; gradually promoted to 1st cls. clk. and sec. currency comsrs., July, 1923; transf'd to col. secretariat, Apr., 1925; acctnt., sup. ct., Oct., 1925; ch. acct., G.P.O., Dec., 1932; ag. P.M.G., 1935-36.

Borneo serv., Aug., 1913; asst. dist. offr., May, 1916; mag., 1st cls., Jan., 1918; dist. offr., July, 1919; asst. govt. sec., Oct., 1926; ag. under sec., July, 1929; ag. res., May, 1930; res., Jan., 1932; ag. govt. sec., Mar., 1934; addl. sess. judge, May, 1934; ag. judge, high ct., June, 1934; res., Jan., 1936; judge, high ct., Sept., 1936; gov., N. SMITH, CLIVE ALKXANDER.—B. 1903; ed. Malton Grammar Schl.; R. Navy, 1919; sub. inspr., Jamaica constab., Dec., 1924; 3rd cls. inspr., May, 1930.

SMITH, CYRIL HILTON, A.M.I.C.E.—B. 1902; ed. Cheltenham Coll.; survr., P.W.D., Bermuda, 1923-24; divn. engrnr., Bermuda rly., 1928-31; asst. engrnr., P.W.D., 1932-37; 1st wks. asst.,

SMITH, HON. DAVID STANLEY, LL.M.—B. 1888; ed. Southland Boys' High Sch., Wellington Coll. and Victoria Univ. Coll. (N.Z.); on serv. with N.Z. exped. force, 1917-18; barrister, solio., and notary pub. till apptd. judge, N.Z. sup. ct., 1928; examin. in law, univ. of N.Z., 1920-27; appointee of U.S.A. as non-national mem., perm. comm. to settle disputes between U.S.A. and Peru; chmn., native affrs. comm. SMITH, E. A.—B. 1896; ent. E.I.O., Dec., 1911; apptd. after compet. exam., asst. clk., Dec., 1913; on mily. serv., Dec., 1915 to Jan., 1919; cler. offr., 1st Jan., 1920.

SMITH, EDWARD PAUL.—B. 1884; Cape mounted pol., June, 1901; clk. to regr. deeds, Cape Town, 1903; clk. to high sheriff, C.T., 1907; 2nd gr. clk., pub. ser. comm., 1913; on mily. serv., Great War, 1915-18 (wounded); 1st gr. clk., ditto, 1919; 1st gr. clk., treas., 1920; senr. clk., P.S.C., 1922; prim. clk., ditto, 1927; ch. clk., ditto, 1930; pub. ser. inspr., 1931; under sec., dept. of commerce and industries, 1936.
SMITH, EMILE HAMEL, L.R.C.P., L.R.C.S., L.F.P. and S., Glas.—B. 1879; ent. med. serv., Trinidad, Sept., 1909; dist. med. offr., Dec., 1912.
SMITH, ERNEST ALLEN.—B. 1896; war serv., 1914-19; asst. audr., Gold Coast, 1924; audr., Leeward Is., 1927; senr. asst. audr., N. Rhodesia, 1932; ag. audr., 1934; dep. audr., Gold Coast, 1936.

SMITH, ERNEST GARDINER, M.A., LL.B.—Advoc.; prizeman in constit. law and hist.; asst. dist. commr., S. Nigeria, Aug., 1908; asst. pol. mag., Jan., 1911; 2nd grade mag., Nigeria, Jan., 1914; 1st grade mag., Jan., 1914; transf. to legal dept., Sept., 1914; ag. solr. gen., Aug., 1920 to Apr., 1921 and Aug., 1921 to Mar., 1922; circuit judge, Ashanti and N. SMITH, ERNEST JOHN, F.C.I.S.—B. 1888; ed. E. London Tech. Coll.; mily. serv., Gold Coast Volunteers, 1914-18; asst. acct., constrn., Gold Coast rly., 1920; ch. acct., 1922; dep. ch. constrn. acct., Nigerian rly., 1929; asst. ch. acct., 1930; ch. acct., 1933; ag. gen. man., Nigerian rly., open lines, May-June, 1937.

SMITH, JAMES.—B. 1882; stock inspr., Basutoland, 1911; sub.-inspr., Basutoland mounted pol., 1917; inspr., 1926; supt., pol., 1936.
