SMITH, JAMES LANDRETH.—B. 1870; draftsman, P.W.D., St. Lucia, July, 1891; draftsman, survey off., Sept., 1893; warden, and supt. of water-wks. under St. George's town bd., Apr., 1895 to Aug., 1901; chief overseer of roads and works, Grenada, Aug., 1901; asst. supt. of wks., Apr., 1902; commr. of crown lands, June, 1904; survr., crown lands, 1904; ag. supt., wks., June, 1906 to Jan., 1907; supervisor, govt. cotton gin- nery, Oct., 1912; col. engr., Oct., 1918 to Aug., 1920; supt., pub. wks., St. Kitts-Nevia, Sept., 1920.
SMITH, CAPT. JAMES PATTERSON.—B. 1893; ed. Madras Coll. and St. Andrew's Univ.; adm. offr., cls. IV., Nigeria, 1920; cls. III., 1928; cls. II., 1937; ag. res., Dec., 1937-June, 1938.

SMITH, JOHN, O.B.E. (1933).—B. 1883; ed. New Vety. Coll., Edinburgh (M.R.C.V.S.), and Liverpool Univ. (D.V.H.); vety. offr., N. Rhodesia, June, 1913; resigned, Apr., 1916; active serv., 1916-19; capt.; reapt., Oct., 1921; ag. ch. vety. offr., Nov., 1921-Mav., 1922; ch. vety. offr., May, 1922; mem., leg. coun.; ag. sec. for agr., Apr., 1925; J.P.; dir., animal health, Nov., 1929.

SMITH, JOHN NOEL.—B. 1886; ed. at Summer Fields, Oxford, Radley Coll. and Hartfell Coll., Oxford; 2nd lieut., 3rd (Res.) Batt., Oxford and Bucks. Light Infantry, 1909; seconded for serv. as asst. res., N. Nigeria, Nov., 1910; add., Nigeria Regt., Cameroons, Mar., 1915; station mag., N. Provs., Mar., 1919; dist. offr., Jan., 1921; ag. senr. res., Sept., 1923.
SMITH, JOSEPH MARIO, O.B.E. (1927), M.R.C.V.S.—Vet. inspr., min. of agric., Egypt, 1912-14; lieut., R.A.V.C., 1914; mily. service, 1914-19; major, 1919; ch. vety. offr., Palestine, July, 1920.
SMITH, L. J. D.—B. 1893; ed. Rastriek Grammar Sch.; U.K. cust., 1914-16; mily. serv., 1917-19; supt., cust., Tanganyika Territory, 1919; senr. supervsr., cust., 1935.

SMITH, LEO ANDREW, A.R.S.M., Assoc. Inst. M.M.—B. 1886; inspr., mines, Perak, June, 1916; asst. warden, mines, June, 1925; sr. inspr., mines, Larut, Feb., 1931; asst. warden, mines, Selangor, Aug., 1932; ditto, Perak, Mar., 1933 and Nov., 1934; ag. warden, mines, Perak, Sept., 1933.

SMITH, LESLIE STEPHEN.—B. 1898; entd. regisy. of friendly societies, 16th Jan., 1914; apptd. after compet. exam., asst. clk., 27th Apr., 1916; on mily. serv., Jan., 1917 to Feb., 1918; trans. to savings bank dept., G.P.O., May, 1919; trans. to C.O., June, 1923; higher grade cler. offr., 1st Mar., 1930; asst. sec., col. development advsy. comte; staff offr., 1938.

SMITH, LINDSAY LEE.—B. 1870; ag. clk. to comnr., Turks Is., 1894, 1896, 1900, and 1903; confirmed 1st July, 1903; ag. acctnt. and clk. (collr. of customs, postmr., cashier and regir. of shipping) comnr.'s office, June to Sept., 1904, and May to Aug., 1906; acctnt. and clk. (collr. of cust., postmr., cashier, and regir. of shipping), Jan., 1907; gen. man., savings bank, 1st July, 1907; ag. asst. comnr., Dec., 1910 to Jan., 1911, and from Sept., 1912 to Apr., 1913.
SMITH, MALCOLM GEORGE, M.A.—B. 1904; inspr., schls., Gold Coast, Dec., 1928; ag. a.st. dir., educn., Oct., 1936; senr. inspr., schls., July, 1937.

SMITH, NORMAN LOCKHART, C.M.G. (1937).—B. 1887; ed. at Sedbergh and Queen's Coll., Oxford (Hastings scholar); B.A. 1910; cadet, Hong Kong Off., 1910; ag. asst. dist. offr., (north), 1912; ag. 2nd A.R.G., 1913; J.P. 1913; ag. asst. col. sec. and clk. of couns., 1913; ag. 1st asst. to sec. for Chinese affairs, 1913; ag. asst. postmstr.-gen., 1914; ag. postmstr.-gen., 1915; seconded for military service 1916-19; ag. 2nd pol. mag., 1919, 1920 and 1923; recd. Chinese order of Wan-hu, 5th cls., 1920; supt., impta. and expts., 1921; attd., Geneva opium confce., 1923; head of army. dept., 1924; P.M.G., Oct., 1928; prin. asst. col. sec. and clk. of couns., 1931; dir., educn., 1933; sec., Chinese affrs., and mem., exec. and legis. couns., Mar., 1934; ag. col. sec., 1935; O.A.G., Sept.-Oct., 1935 and Apr.-Oct., 1937; col. sec., 1936.

SMITH, REGINALD HOPKINSON, O.B.E. (1935).—B. 1898; ed. Chatham House, Ramsgate; served in the Royal Naval Divn., Oct. to Dec., 1914; H.A.C., Oct., 1915 to Oct., 1917; Indian Army R. of O., Nov., 1917 to Aug., 1919; Aden Field Force, July, 1918 to July, 1919; dist. pol. offr., Somaliland, July, 1919; dist. offr., Jan., 1931.

SMITH, ROBERT KNOWLTON, LL.B., K.C.—B. 1887; ed., St. Francois Xavier Univ., Antigonish, N.S.; grad. Dalhousie Law Schl.; admitted to Nova Scotia bar, Mar. 1911; K.C., Jan., 1929; crown pros., Cumberland County, N.S. Aug., Oct., 1925; mayor, Amherst, 1923; el. to H. of C., Canada, Oct., 1925, re-elec. at g.e., 1926 and 1930; dep. min. of marine, Aug., 1935.
SMITH, SIDNEY W.—B. 1902; apptd., after compet. exam., asst. clk., C.O., Oct., 1919; cler. offr., 1st Jan., 1920; higher grade cler. offr., 29th Oct., 1930.

SMITH, STEPHEN JOHN.—B. 1887; N.Z. post and tel. dept., 1903; internal affrs. dept., 1913; sec., Samoan admistr., 1915; ch. clk., external affrs. dept., 1920-30; ch. clk., Cook Is. dept., 1920-28; sec., 1928.

SMITH, TRAFFORD, M.A.—B. 1912; ed. City Boys Schl., Leicester and Trinity Coll., Cambridge, 1930-34 (state schl., senr. schl., Jeston Exhibr.); B.A., 1933 (1st cls. pts. I and II mod. and med. languages tripes); apptd., after compet. exam., asst. prin., C.O., 1935; asst. prts. sec. to S. of S. for the cols. (Mr. Ormsby-Gore), 24th Dec., 1937.

SMITH, WILLIAM FORGAN, M.L.A. (Queensland).—B. and ed. in Scotland; M.L.A., Queensland since 1915; successively min. without portfolio, sec. for pub. wks. and sec. for agr. and stock; premier and treas., June, 1932.

SMITH, WILLIAM HARDEN, B.A., M.B., B.Ch., B.A.O. (T.C.D.), L.M. (Rot.), D.P.H. (R.C.P. and S.I.), D.T.M. (Calcutta).—Ed. Trinity Coll., Dublin; surg. sub-lieut., R.N.V.R., 1918; med. offr., Zanzibar, Nov., 1925; ag. D.D.S.S., on various occasions, 1930-33; med. offr., Tanganyika Territory, 1935.

SMITH-FOLLETT, ROBERT REDVERS, B.Sc., A.R.C.S.—B. 1901; ed. Skinners Schl. and Impl. Coll. Sci. and Tech. (B.Sc. hons. chem.); Impl. Col. Trop. Agr., Trinidad, 1925; research ass't to prof., chem. and soil sci., 1927; chemist-ecologist att'd to col. comte., Br. Guiana, 1929; chemist-ecologist, dept. agr., Br. Guiana, 1930.

SMUTS, GEN. THE RIGHT HON. J. C., P.C. (1917), C.H., K.C.—B. 1870; ed. at Victoria Coll., Stellenbosch; B.A., Cape Univ., 1891; Ebbden scholar at Christ's Coll., Camb.; double first in law tripes, 1894; barrister, Cape Col.; state-attorney, S. African Republic, 1898; served with Boer forces during S. African war, and was in comd. of Boer forces in Cape Col. during the latter part of the campaign; mem. of head comte. of Het Volk; col. sec., Transvaal, 1907; mem. of Imp. Conf. on Naval and Military Defence, 1909; min. of finance and defence, Union of S. Africa, 1912; min. of defence, 1915; in command of troops in E. Africa, 1916; mem. of British war cabinet; represented S. Africa at peace confce. at Paris, 1919; prime min. and min. of native affrs., Union of S. Africa, 1919-24; M.P. for Standerton, 1924; leader, opposition, 1924-33; pres., Br. Assn. for Advancement of Science, 1931; min. of just. and dep. prime min., coalition govt., 1933; leader, S. African delegation, World Economic Confce., 1933.

SMYLY, SIR PHILIP CHAMPTON, KT. BACH. (1905).—B. 1866; ed. at Trin. Coll., Dub., B.A., LL.B.; called to the bar, King's Inns, Dub., 1888; LL.D., 1891; called to the bar, Gray's Inn, 1902; Queen's advoc., S. Leone, 1895; atty.-gen., 1896; ch. just., S. Leone, 1901; ch. just., Gold Coast, 1911; ret., 1929.

SNATH, PAUL ALFRED TURNER, M.B. (Toronto), M.C.P. & S. (Ontario), Cert. London S.T.M., D.P.H. (Toronto), M.D. (Toronto).—B. 1898; med. offr., W.A.M.S. (Gold Coast), 1923; resigned, 1927; prov. lieut., Canadian army medical corps, 1927; lieut., 1928; capt., 1929; Canadian O.T.C. (U. Toronto contingent), major (med.), R.C.A.M.C.(N.P.), 1931; asst. govt. M.O. H., Br. Guiana, 1937.

SNEILL, EDWARD WALTER.—B. 1879; asst. post office, Kingwilliamstown, Mar., 1900; asst. G.P.O., Cape Town, Nov., 1900; clk., acct.; branch, July, 1903; 1st cls. clk., Apr., 1912; income tax office, Pretoria, Sept., 1914; senr. clk., income tax office, Cape Town, July, 1915; senior clk., rev. office, Johannesburg, Mar., 1918; prin. clk., ditto, Jan., 1921; ch. clk., income tax, head office, inland rev. dept., Pretoria, Aug., 1921; asst. compt., inland rev., Sept., 1929; compt., inland rev., June, 1938.

SNELLING, ARTHUR WENDELL.—B. 1914; ed. Ackworth Schol. and Univ. Coll., London; B.Sc. (economics) 1st cls., hon., 1934; study group sec., Royal Inst. of Internat. Affairs., 1934-36; appld. after compet. exam., asst. prin., D.O., Oct., 1936.
SNYMAN, NICOLAAS.—Prof. asst. to surv.-gen., O.F.S., 1920; surv.-gen., O.F.S., Dec., 1931; ditto, Natal, May, 1934; do., Transvaal, Dec., 1937.
SOERTZ, FRANCIS JOSEPH, K.C., Barrister-at-Law (Gray's Inn).—B. 1886; held several appts. as pol. mag., crown coun., dist. judge and commr. of assize, Ceylon, 1932-33; puisne just., Nov., 1936.

SOLLIS, CLIFFORD GEORGE, M.A.—B. 1895; ed. Bristol Grammar Schol. and St. John's Coll., Oxford, asst. mast., Bukit Zahrah Sch., Johore Bahru, Dec., 1920; ag. inspr., schls., Pahang, Oct., 1926; do., Penang, Mar., 1928; supth., educn., Kedah, Dec., 1930; inspr., schls., Selangor, Aug., 1931; senr. insp., Eng. schls., Hong Kong, 1937.
SOLOMON, SAUL.—B. 1875; ed. Bedford Grammar Schol. and Lincoln Coll., Oxford; M.A.; called to bar, Lincoln's Inn, 1900; K.C., Union of S. Africa, 1919; judge, sup. ct., Transvaal prov. divn., 1927.

SOMASUNTHARAM, KANDIAH.—B. 1897; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Dec., 1921; attd., Kalutara kach., Dec., 1921; ditto, Kurunegala kach., Jan., 1922; ditto, Colombo kach., Dec., 1922; office asst. to rubber contr., Mar., 1923; office asst., Galle kach., Aug., 1926; 5th asst. col. sec., Nov., 1926; 4th asst. col. sec., May, 1928; addnl. asst. gov't. agt., Colombo, Jan., 1929; addnl. asst. contr., rev., Nov., 1929; asst. gov't. agt., Kurunegala, July, 1931; dist. judge, Kegalla, July, 1932; sec., min. for lab., industry and commerce, June, 1933; dep. contr., lab., May, 1934; contr., finance and supply, Jan., 1936.

SOMERS, CECIL GEORGE.—B. 1896; served Dardanelles and E. Mediterranean with H.M. Transports, 1914-18; gov't. pilot, Kenya, 1924; asst. port offr., Zanzibar, 1929; ag. port offr., on various occasions, 1929-37; asst. port and cust. offr., Jan., 1938; ag. port offr., Mar., 1938; port and cust. offr., Sept. 1938.

SOPER, MAJOR EDWARD JOSEPH, (R. of O.), V.D.—B. 1889; engng. acct., F.M.S. klys., Sept., 1923; asst. acct., Jan., 1925 and June, 1931; fincl. offr., engng. dept., July, 1928; do., gr. I., Sept., 1928; rev. acctnt., Aug., 1935.

SOPER, JOHN ROBERT PHILPOTT, B.A. (hons.), Cantab.—B. 1904; ed. Tonbridge Schol. and Pembroke Coll., Cambridge (schol.); headmaster, agrl. prim. schl., Zanzibar, Sept., 1926; seconded for duty as agrl. offr., 1926; agrl. offr., 1928; do., S.S. and F.M.S., 1936.
SOUTHALL, COLIN LAMBERT.—B. 1898; ed. Sidcot Schol.; R.F.C. 1915-19; B.Sc. (1st cls.) Birmingham; govt. chem., Fiji, 1924-28; asst. analyst, Straits Sttlnts., 1928; govt. analyst, Nigeria, 1936.

SOUTHBOROUGH, 1ST BARON (creat. 1917), RT. HON. SIR FRANCIS JOHN STEPHENS HOPWOOD, P.C. (1912), G.C.B. (1916), G.C.M.G. (1908), K.C.B. (1901), K.C.M.G. (1906), C.B. (1896), C.M.G. (1893).—B. 1860; ed. at Louth by Canon W. W. Hopwood; admitted a solr., 1882; asst. law clk., B. of T., 1885-1888; asst. solr., 1888-1892; priv. sec. to pres. of B. of T., 1892; sec., rly. dept., 1892-1901; perm. sec., 1901; perm. under-sec. of S. for the colonies, 1907-1910; employed on different occasions upon offl. missions to U.S.A., Canada, and Newfoundland; Brit. deleg. to internat. rly. congress in London, 1886; and in Paris, 1900; hon. sec. to chmn. of select comtees of H. of C. on Jameson raid, 1897; mem. of London traffic comtn., 1903; visited S. Africa as mem. of Transvaal and O.R.C. constitutions comtn., 1906; mem. of comtns. on canals and waterways, 1906; and on ocean freights and shipping "rings," 1906; accompanied H.R.H. the Prince of Wales on his visit to Quebec, 1908; mem. of Royal comtn. on electoral reform, 1909; regar. of the Order of St. Michael and St. George, 1907-1909; sec. of the Order, 1909-1911; accompanied H.R.H. the Duke of Connaught on his visit to S. Africa in connection with the opening of the first parlmt. of the Union of S. Africa, 1910; vice-chmn. of the Development comtn., 1910; specially apptd. to act as under sec. of state for the colonies during the period of the Imperial Conf., 1911; additional civil lord of the Admiralty, Jan., 1912; sec. to the Irish Convention, 1917.

SOUTHORN, SIR WILFRED THOMAS, K.C.M.G. (1938), K.B.E. (1933), C.M.G. (1927).—B. 1879; ed. Warwick Schol. and Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford (M.A.); hon. LL.D. (Hong Kong Univ.), 1936; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Nov., 1903; off. asst. to gov't. agt., N. Prov., Oct., 1904; N.C. Prov., May, 1906; dist. judge, Tangalla, Apr., 1907; landing survr. customs, Colombo, July, 1909; asst. gov't. agt., Matale, Nov., 1911; priv. sec. to gov', Aug., 1914; dep. collr. of customs and landing survr., Colombo, Sept., 1915; 2nd asst. col. sec., Dec., 1919; prin. asst. col. sec., July, 1920; prin. collr., cust. and chmn., Colombo port comtn., Dec., 1923; mem., leg. coun., 1923 and exec. coun., 1925; col. sec., Hong Kong, May, 1926; O.A.G., for various periods, 1927-35; gov' and commdr-in-ch., Gambia, Oct., 1936.
SPAFFORD, HAROLD HAMILTON, B.A.—B. 1889; milv.-serv., France, 1917-18; demob., 1921; inspr. schls., Gold Coast, Jan., 1928; senr. inspr. do., 1938.

SPARROW, HERBERT ROBERT, A.M.I.E.E.—B. 1889; asst. elec. engrnr., F.M.S., Mar., 1920; elec. engrnr., K. Lumpur, Sept., 1927; ag. dep. ch. elec. engrnr. and inspr. in addn., various periods, 1928-31; senr. elec. engrnr., Selangor, Sept., 1935; ag. advr., elec. Malay States, Mar., 1937; advr., do., 1938.

SPARROW, WILLIAM LABARTE.—B. 1904; ed. Trinity Coll., Dublin; pol. probationer, Oct., 1924; asst. supt., pol., Hong Kong, 1927; seconded to Weihsiaiwei as junr. dist. offr., Jan., 1928; passed in Mandarin and Cantonese; attd. Punjab pol., 1931-2; passed in Urdu, 1933; supt., pol., 1936.

SPEDD, SIR EDWIN ARNEY, K.T. BACH. (1911).—M.A.; LL.B.; B. 1869; ed. at Rugby, and Trin. Coll., Camb.; major scholar and senior exhibtr., Rugby schl.; scholar and prizeman of Trin. Coll., Camb. 1st cls. class. tripes, 1890; 2nd cls. law tripes, Part I., 1891; called to the bar, Inner Temple, June, 1893; dist. comsnr. G. Coast, 1899; atty.-gen., Lagos, 1900; edited a revised edition of Lagos laws, 1901; has acted on numerous occasions as ch. just. and also as col. sec., from Jan., 1905, to Mar., 1906; atty.-gen., S. Nigeria, May, 1906; edited revised edtn. of laws of S. Nigeria, 1907; ch. just., N. Nigeria, 1908; retired.
SPEIRS, R. C., M.B., Ch.B. (Edin.), D.T.M. (Liv.)—B. 1901; med. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Apr., 1926.

SPENCE, JOHN, C.M.G. (1936), B.A., LL.B. (Syd. Univ.)—B. 1878; mem. pub. serv. bd., 1924-28; chmn., N.S.W. budget comtes., 1928; chmn. N.S.W. taxation investgtn. comtes., 1933-35; audr.-gen., N.S.W. and trustee, Australian museum since, 1928.

SPENCE, LOUIS PERIVAL.—B. 1895; olk., treas., St. Vincent, 1914; 1st cls. rev. and port offr., 1919; ch. rev., excise and port offr., 1927; ch. inland rev. offr., 1936; ag. treas., collr., cust., etc. on several occasions, 1934-37; col. treas., collr. cust., etc., Jan., 1938; ag. admr. in 1938.

SPICER, ROY GODFREY BULLEN, C.M.G. (1934), M.C. (1918).—B. 1889; ed. Cole Court and St. Paul's; probationary asst. supt. pol., Ceylon, Nov., 1909; asst. supt., 1911; supt., 1915; on war serv., 1915-19; (lieut., The Carabiniers); wounded, Oct., 1918; returned to Ceylon pol., 1919; supt., grade I., 1923; comsnr. pol., Kenya, June, 1928; inspr.-gen., pol. and prisons, Palestine, 1930.

SPILLER, JOHN WYATT, C.M.G. (1938), M. Inst. C.E.—B. 1878; ed. Wellington Schl. (Som.); served in engnr. dept., G.W.R., as pupil, survr., and draughtsman, new wks. asst. and divtn-engnr. 1896 to 1910; joined engnr. dept., Crown Agents, 1910; services lent to Miny. of Munitions, 1916; tech. adviser to dir., Munitions Overseas Transport, 1917; returned to C.A., 1919; dep. ch. engnr., 1919; visited Ceylon, F.M.S. and S. Stlntts., 1920; ch. engnr. (designs), 1922; visited Br. Guiana and Trinidad, 1923; acted for one year as gen. man., and ch. engnr. rly., F.M.S., 1926.
SPOONER, ARTHUR CASWELL.—B. 1906; ed. Epsom Coll.; cadet, Gold Coast, 1929; asst. dist. comsnr., 1930.

SPOWERS, JAMES HOWARD.—B. 1889; authorised survr., Queensland, Oct., 1911; survr., F.M.S., Nov., 1913; asst. supt., surveys, Jan., 1919; do., trig. surveys, F.M.S., Nov., 1922; do., rev. surveys, Sept., 1924; ag. supt., surveys, Johore, Jan., 1931; do., Kedah, Oct., 1932; supt., surveys, F.M.S. and S.S., June, 1933; do., rev. surveys, Selangor and N. Sembilan, Nov., 1935.
SPURLING, ANTONY CUTHBERT, M.A. (Oxon.), Barrister-at-Law (Inner Temple).—B. 1906; asst. legal advr., Miny. of Health, 1934; res. mag., Kenya, 1936.

SQUIBB, FRANCIS LEOPOLD.—B. 1895; ed. Towcester Schl.; served Manchester Rgt., 1914-19; Imp. war graves comm., 1920-22; entd. R. botanic gardens, Kew, 1922; asst. supt., botanical and forest dept., Hong Kong, 1924; asst. curator and agrl. offr., Dominica, 1926; ag. agrl. supt., Apr. to Nov., 1931; asst. dir., agr., Seychelles, 1932; dir., Mar., 1934; mem., ex. and leg. couns., Apr., 1934; agric. offr., Gold Coast, 1939.
SRIKHANTA, MAYILVAKANAM, B.A. (Lond.).—B. 1913; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Jan., 1937.

STACPOOL, GEORGE WENTWORTH.—Asst. comsnr., lands survey dept., Gold Coast, 1925; transf'd. to lands dept., 1927; ag. dep. comsnr., July, 1928-Apr., 1929; ag. comsnr., lands, in 1932 and 1933; comsnr., lands, Oct., 1933.

STACY-MARKS, LIEUT.-COMMDR. N.J., R.D., R.N.R.—B. 1893; ed. H.M.S. "Worcester" served R.N., 1914-20; demob., 1920; 2nd offr., lake steamers, K.U.R. & H., 1920; asst. to port man., Mombasa, 1928; port man., 1933.

STAFFORD, FRANK EDMUND, O.B.E. (1934).—Active serv. with Queen's Regt., 1914-19; Iraq civ. adminstr., Feb., 1919; asst. finan. advr., 1921; dep. finan. secy., Jan., 1923; finan. sec., 1923; asst. treas., Nigeria, 1936; senr. asst. sec., 1937; ag. prin. asst. sec., 1937; prin. asst. sec. (finance br.), 1938.

STANHOPE, REGINALD ALFRED BROWNING, B.V. Sc. (Melb.), M.R.C.V.S., Melb. Univ. and Royal Vet. Coll., Lond.—B. 1919; vet. surg., F.M.S., May, 1920; ag. dir., vety. reach. and vety. advr., Malaya, July, 1936.

STANLEY, SIR HERBERT JAMES, G.C.M.G. (1930), K.C.M.G. (1924), C.M.G. (1913).—B. 1872; ed. at Eton and Balliol Coll., Oxford (M.A.); priv. sec. to H.M. min. resident at Dresden and Coburg, and British vice-consul at Dresden, 1897-1902; asst. priv. sec. to first Lord of the Admiralty, 1906-1908; priv. sec. to Lord Pres. of the Coun., 1908-1910; priv. sec. to Viscount Gladstone, gov.-gen. of Union of S. Africa, 1910; sec. to gov.-gen., 1913; resident comsnr., Southern and Northern Rhodesia, 1st Apr., 1916; Imperial sec. and acctnt. to high comsnr. for S. Africa, 1918; first gov. and commdr.-in-chief, N. Rhodesia, 1st Apr., 1924; gov. of Ceylon, Aug., 1927; H. M. high comsnr. for S. Africa, and first high comsnr. for the U.K. in the Union of S. Africa, Apr., 1931; gov. and commdr.-in-ch., S. Rhodesia, 8th Jan., 1935.

STANLEY, ROBERT CHRISTOPHER STAFFORD.—B. 1899; ed. Westminster Schl. and R.M.A., Woolwich; R. G. A., 1918-21; on war serv., Palestine, 1918; admstve. offr., Nigeria, May, 1925; do., Cyprus: asst. sec., Dec., 1935; comsnr., Mar., 1936; ch. asst. sec., Mar., 1938; ag. col. sec., Apr.-Aug. and Sept., 1938.

STANLEY, W. B., C.M.G. (1923), M.B.E. (1918), Barrister-at-Law, Gray's Inn, 1921.—Served with 1st Border regt. occupation of Crete, 1898; S. Africa, 1899-1900 (med. and 4 clasp); 2nd lieut., W. India regt., 1900; lieut., 1901; ag. adjt., 3rd W. India regt., Gambia expdn., 1901. (med. and clasp); ag. trav. comsnr., Gambia May and June, 1901; trav. comsnr., Gambia, Aug., 1901; A.D.C. and priv. sec. to ag. gov., Gambia, Aug. to Oct., 1901; capt., 1902; passed course of survey, schl. of mil. eng., Chatham, 1905; resig. comsnr., 1906; capt., 3rd Yorks regt., 1906; qualified in native language; dist. comsnr., S. Leone, 1910; prov. comsnr., May, 1920; ag. col. sec., S. Leone, on various occasions, 1918-20 and 1922-23; gov.'s dep. on several occasions.
STAPLES, JAMES WEBSTER.—B. 1902; apptd. after compet. exam., asst. clk., G.P.O., Oct., 1919; cler. offr., 1st Jan., 1920; transf'd. to C.O., Mar., 1923.

STAPLES, RAYMOND RHODES.—B. 1897; M.A.; dip. agr. (Cantab.); botanist, Union of S.A., 1922; agr. economist, Tanganyika Territory, 1929; ag. dep. dir., agr., 1930; res. offr. (pasture), vety. dept., 1931; botanist, 1935; ecological survey, Basutoland, 1937.
STAPLEY, FRANK R.—B. 1895; mily. serv., Sept., 1914 to Dec., 1919; temp. clk., C.O., Apr., 1920; apptd. cler. offr., 4th July, 1922; higher grade cler. offr., 17th Feb., 1930.
STARK, PATRICIA YOUNG.—Asst. mistress, educn. dept., Hong Kong, 1912; head mistress, Peak Sch., 1920; do., Belilos Pub. Sch., 1934.
STATHAM, HON. SIR CHARLES ERNEST, KT. BACH. (1926).—Mem., N.Z. H. of R., 1915-35; speaker, 1923-36; M.L.C., 1936.

STEAD, KINGSLEY WILLANS, C.B.E. (1933), O.B.E. (1928).—B. 1883; ed. at King Edward's, Birmingham; apptd., after open compet. exam., ast. in int. cust., Cardiff, Mar., 1908; Harwich, Oct., 1906; ast. collr. of cust., Larnaca, Cyprus, dep. harbmr. and tide surveyor, Apr., 1908; collr. of cust. and excise, Apr., 1910; passed prelim. exam. in modern Greek, June, 1910; reg. of trade marks, June, 1911; passed lower standard exam. in modern Greek, June, 1912; ast. cable censor, Aug., 1914-Apr., 1919; cable censor, May-July, 1919; pub. cust. of enemy property, Jan., 1919 to Mar., 1920; refugee commr., Larnaca, May, 1919 to Apr., 1920; reg. of patents, Aug., 1920; ch. collr. of cust. and excise, Oct., 1921; inspr. of fisheries, 1921; Médaille de la Reconnaissance Française, 3rd cls., 1922; ag. commr., Larnaca for various periods in 1922, 1923 and 1924; reg. of companies, July, 1922; ch. supt., Port of Larnaca, June, 1922; reg. of companies, July, 1922; reg. of Cyprus ships, Jan., 1923; reg. of Br. ships, Jan., 1923; dir. cust., excise, and trade, Palestine, July, 1924; ag. reg. gen., Aug. to Dec., 1924; reg. of trade marks and patents, Apr., 1928; ag. reg., companies and co-op. societies, Apr.-Dec., 1928.

STRAD, ROBERT J. C.—B. 1880; ast. gen. publicity agt., colonization dept., C.P.R., 1913; gen. publicity agt., colonization dept., C.P.R., 1917; dir. publicity, dept. of immigr. and colonization, Ottawa, 1919; has written several books.
STEED, L. F.—B. 1897; on mily. serv., Sept., 1914 to Mar., 1919; temp. clk., O.S.O., Feb., 1920, apptd. after compet. exam., cler., offr., O.S.O., July., 1922.

STEED, ROGER HERBERT, M.I.C.E., Chtd.civ. engrnr.—B. 1894; ed Dulwich Coll.; war serv., 1915-19; ast. engrnr., P.W.D., Malaya, Feb., 1924; supt., stores & furniture, S'pore, Oct., 1926; ag. ex. engrnr., S'pore, Nov., 1929; ex. engrnr., S'pore, Feb., 1933.

STEELE, Lieut.-Commr. ARTHUR DAVENET, R.N.R. (Retd.).—B. 1893; ed. Yale Coll., Rangoon; cadet H.M.S. "Worcester," 1908-10; H.M. ships, 1914-18 (Dover Patrol and Destroyers based on Devonport and Portsmouth); harbmr. and marine supt., Gambia, 1930; hydrographic surveying course, 1930; el. younger brother, Trinity House, 1933; capt. of port, Gibraltar, reg. of shipping and Trinity agt., 1934.

STEELE, HENRY WILLIAM.—B. 1875; ed. Grenada Gram. Schl. and Dulwich Coll.; called to the bar, Inner Temple, Apr., 1899; admitted Grenada bar, July, 1899; mag., dist. F., Dominica, Jan., 1916; ag. mag. and reg. gen., dist. E., Dominica, for various periods, 1917-20; ag. crown atty. and mem. of exec. and legis. coun., St. Christopher and Nevis, Oct. to Dec., 1918; ag. crown atty. and mem. of the exec. and legis. coun., Dominica, Feb.-Mar., 1917, and July-Oct., 1920; pol. mag., Grenada, Oct., 1930; ag. atty.-gen., Apr., 1931.

STEELE-PERKINS, Wing Commander, ALFRED HORACE STEELE (R.A.F. retd.), O.B.E.—B. 1887; ed. Eastmans R. Naval Acad.; R.N., 1906-16; R. Naval Air Serv., 1916-18; R.A.F., 1918-28; Fleet Air Arm, 1928-31; comte. of Imp. Defence, 1931-33; H.O., 1933-38; C.O., 1938; air raid precautions offr., Hong Kong, 1938.
STEPHEN, HON. EDWARD MILNER.—B. 1870; judge, sup. ct., N.S.W., June, 1929.

STEPHENS, MAJOR FRANCIS TRANT, O.B.E. (1919), M.C., King's Police Med.—Served in S. African war, 1900-02; joined B.S.A. pol., Rhodesia, 1903; command., 1907; served with Rhodesian column in S.W. Africa and E. Africa, 1914-16; seconded to 1st K.A.R. for service in K. Africa, 1916-20; 4 times ment. in despa.; ch. commr. pol., and ch. inspr. of prisons, Nyasaland Prot., July, 1920.

STEPHENSON, ERNEST FORTUNATUS, A.M.I.E.E., M.A.M.I.E.E., Chtd.Elec.Engnr.—B. 1891; ast. elec. engrnr., F.M.S., 1948; ag. dep. ch. elec. engrnr., 1929; elec. engrnr., 1933; sr. elec. engrnr., Sel., Oct., 1937.

STEPHENSON, JOHN EVERARD, O.B.E. (1935).—B. 1893; ed. at Winchester Coll. and Oxford; passed intermediate exam. in laws, London Univ., 1911; scholar, New Coll., Oxford, 1912; 1st cls., class. mods., 1914; called to the bar, 1923; on military service, 28th Aug., 1914 to 28th Feb., 1919; B.A. (war), 1919; tempy. 2nd cls. clk., C.O., 12th Sept., 1919; apptd. under reconstruction scheme, 2nd cls. clk., C.O., 12th Jan., 1920; ast. prin., 1st Apr., 1920; ast. sec. to S. of S., 18th June, 1920; ag. prin., 1st Mar., 1923; attd., secretariat, Imp. Economic Confce., 1923; prin., 12th Dec., 1924; U.K. delegation to L. of N. Assembly, 1925; astd., secretariat (economic sec.), Imp. Confce., 1926; int. sec., confce. on operm. of Dom. legislation, 1929; admstve. ast. sec., Imp. Confce., 1930; ast. sec., 1st Apr., 1936; ast. under sec., 1938.
STEPHENSON, WILLIAM RALPH EVANS, M.A. (N.Z.).—Col. audit dept., Mar., 1936; ast. audnr., Hong Kong, Apr., 1936.
STEVENS, HON. BERTRAM SIDNEY BABENSDALE.—B. 1889; M.L.A., Croydon, N.S.W. from Oct., 1927; ast. treas., 1927-29; treas., N.S.W., 1929-30; premier and treas., 1932.

STEVENS, GEORGE ALEXANDER, B.A.—B. 1901; ed. Bancroft's Schl., Woodford, Essex and Queen's Coll., Oxford; foundation schol. in mod. hist., 1919; 2nd cls. hon., mod. hist.; B.A., 1923; ast. mast., Achimota, Gold Coast, 1925.

STEVENS, WILLIAM OSWALD, M.A., Oxon.—B. 1891; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Nov., 1914; attached to Jaffna kach., Dec., 1914; addtl. pol. mag., Jaffna, in addn. May, 1915; ast., Batticaloa Kachoheri, Oct., 1915; pol. mag., Matale, Aug., 1918; ditto, Avinawella, Jan., 1923; ditto, Kandy, Mar., 1923; ditto, Colombo, Feb., 1925; pol. mag., Kurunegala, Sept., 1927; ast. govt. agt., Matale, Nov., 1932; dep. contr., lab., Dec., 1935; offr. cls. 1, grade II, May, 1936; govt. N.C.P., Mar., 1938.

STEVENSON, CECIL YOUNG, M.I.C.E., A.M.I.Mech.E.—B. 1886; ed. Queen's Coll., Belfast; lieut., R.E., 1910; ast. engrnr., P.W.D., E. Africa, Jan., 1913; capt., R.E., 1915; adjt., 1916; served with B.E.F., France, Aug., 1914 to Sept., 1916; Indian Ex. Force and Aden Field Force, 1917-19; "Moss Star," ment. in despa.; exec. engrnr., German & Africa, July, 1919; ag. D.P.W., Apl. to Dec., 1920; ag. dep. D.P.W., on various occasions, 1921-30; D.P.W., 1931; ag. D.P.W., 1931-33; D.P.W., Jan., 1934.

STEVENSON, DUNGAN.—B. 1899; ed. Lincoln Sch., Edin. Univ., B.Sc. (Forestry), 1923; 2nd lieutenant, R.E., 1918; lieutenant, 1919; France, 1918-19; asst. conserv., forests, Br. Honduras, Jan., 1924; dep. conserv., forests, Apr., 1926; ag. conserv., Apr.-Oct., 1927; senr. asst. conserv., forests, N. Rhodesia, 1929; asst. conserv., forests, Gold Coast, Apr., 1934; sr., do., July, 1937.

STEVENSON, MAJOR HUBERT CHADDOCK, C.M.G. (1938), O.B.E. (1934), M.C.—B. 1888; ed. Glengorse, Meads, Eastbourne and Harrow; war serv., 1915-19 (commanded batty., R.F.A.); admstve. offr., cls. IV., Nigeria, 1920; asst. sec., S. Provs. secretariat, 1926; ag. prin. asst. sec., do., 1927; asst. sec., do., 1927; ag. senr. asst. sec., Nigerian secretariat, 1929; ag. res., 1933-34; admstve. serv., cls. I., 1934; ch. commr., Ashanti, 1936.
STEVENSON, NEIL STUART.—B. 1902; ed. Lincoln Schl. and Edin. Univ., B.Sc. (Forestry), 1924; asst. conservator, forests, Br. Honduras, Nov., 1924; conservator, Jan., 1930.

STEVENTON, L. E., A.M.Inst.T.—B. 1897; ed. County Schl., Wolverton and Elstow Schls., Bedford; on mily. serv. in E. Africa, Egypt, Salonika and Asia Minor; asst. divn. supt., Nigerian rly., 1928; ag. aspt., line, open lines, Nov., 1936-Mar., 1937; ag. ch. traffic supt., Apr.-Sept., 1937; traffic man., Tanganyika rly., 1938.
STEWART, AUGUSTUS GEORGE, M.D., F.R.O.S. (Edin.).—Med. supt., Durban hosp., 1922.
STEWART, CHARLES DUNCAN, B.Sc. (Lond.).—B. 1888; supt., meteorological br., survey dept., S.S. and F.M.S., May, 1929.

STEWART, CAPT. JOHN LIVINGSTONE, M.C., B.Sc., M.R.C.V.S.—B. 1894; enlisted Scottish Horse, 1914; 2nd lieut., Argyll and Sutherland Highlanders; served with B.E.F., France, wounded, M.C., Br. War and Victory med.; ment. in desps.; vety. offr., Gold Coast, Dec., 1923; prin. vety. offr., 1930.

STEWART, DOUGLAS ROR, C.M.G. (1929).—B. 1886; native dept., Fiji, 1905; cadet, 1906; passed cadet, 1909; stip. mag., 1910; 2nd asst. col. sec., 1912; asst. native commr., 1914; asst. sec. for native affrs., 1916; asst. col. sec., 1917; prin. do., 1921; dep. commr., Western Pacific, 1925; col. sec., Barbados, 1928; O.A.G., in 1927, 1929, 1930; admstr., St. Kitts-Nevis, 1931, O.A.G., Leeward Is. in 1935, 1936, 1937.

STEWART, MAJOR MALCOLM JOHN.—B. 1893; 2nd lieut., Lovat Scouts Yeomanry, 1914; Indian Army, 1916-22; capt., 1919; Impl. Sch. of Instr., Zeitoun, Cairo, 1919-21; cov. offr., coy. commdr., 53rd Sikhs F.F.; asst. treas., Tanganyika Territory, 1923; ag. senr. ass't. treas., 1930, 1931, 1932, 1933; ag. treas. and ch. of cust., Somaliland, Sept., 1933-Mar., 1934; treas. and collr., cut., mag., regiar-gan. and harbour, Falkland Is., 1935; maj. and O.C., F.I.D.F., 1935; ag. col. sec., Apr., 1936 and June-Dec., 1937; dep. for gov., May and July, 1936; dep. treas., Sierra Leone, 1938.
STEWART, NEIL, M.M.—B. 1893; Royal Highlanders (Black Watch), 1915-19 (M.M.) (men in desps.); asst. supt., pol., Kenya, Sept., 1919; supt., 1927; King's pol. med., 1935.

STEWART, WILLIAM.—B. 1881; ed. Waimate Dist. High Schl. and Victoria Univ. Coll., N.Z.; entd. N.Z. lands and survey dept., 1899; commr., crown lands and ch. survr., Christchurch, 1926; valuer-gen., 1934.

STEWART, WILLIAM GORDON, B.Sc. (Eng.), A.M.I.C.E., Chtd. Civ. Engrnr.—B. 1893; asst. engrnr., P.W.D., F.M.S., Nov., 1920; do., Johore, Sept., 1925; exec. engrnr., Nov., 1929; do., F.M.S., May, 1930; do., S.S., Aug., 1934; senr. exec. engrnr., Sept., 1936.

STETYTLER, JOHANNES COENRAAD VAN WYK.—B. 1891; ed. Boys High Schl., Paarl, Grey Univ. Coll., Bloemfontein; dept. of int., Sept., 1913; dept. of just., Feb., 1915; mag., Phillips town, Feb., 1930-35; mem., farmers' assistance bd., May, 1935; soc. state advances recoveries office, Sept., 1935.
STIRLING, JOHN WIGHTMAN.—B. 1885; med. offr., Basutoland, 1912; prin. med. offr., Bech. Prot., 1936.

STOCKDALE, SIR FRANK ARTHUR, K.C.M.G. (1937), C.M.G. (1932), O.B.E. (1928), M.A. (Can tab.), F.L.S.—B. 1883; ed. Wisbech and Magdalene Coll. Camb.; Holmes exhibtn., Mag. Coll., Camb., 1901; B.A. (1st cls. Nat. Sc. Trip.) 1904; M.A. 1911; mycologist and lecturer in agric. science, Impl. dept. of agric. for the West Indies, May, 1905; on sp. serv. to Trinidad in connection with diseases of coconuts, 1906; scient. sec. to W.L. agric. confere., 1907 and 1908; asst. dir., dept. of science and agric., and govt. botanist, B. Guiana, Aug., 1908; dep. chmn., bd. of agric., B. Guiana, 1909; mem., agric. sch. comrn., 1909; mem., banana comrn., 1910; on sp. serv. to Dutch Guiana in connection with banana industry, 1910; sec., tobacco comrn., 1911; comrnr. for Br. Guiana at Int. rubber exhibtn., London, 1911; dir., agric., Mauritius, 1912; vice-president, bd. of agric., 1913; mem., coun. of govt., 1913; regist., co-operative credit societies, July, 1913; mem., bd. of dirs., Mauritius Inst., 1914; mem. of irri gation comte., 1914; visited Rodrigues, 1914; dir., agr., regist., co-op. societies and vice-pres., agric. soc., Ceylon, 1916; chmn., exec. comte., Ceylon rubber research scheme, 1916; del. to Imp. entomological confere., London, 1920; mem., coun., Ceylon Univ. Coll., 1921; M.L.C., 1921; del. to Imp. botanical confere., London, 1924; mem., bd. of management, tea research inst., 1925; visited tea dists., N. India with Ceylon delegation, 1925; asst. agric. adviser to S. of S., Mar., 1929; editor "Tropical Agriculturist" and author of several repts., etc., on trop. agr.

STOCKS, ARTHUR HUDSON, C.M.G. (1937)—B. 1889; ed. Heath Gram. Schl., Halifax and Oriel Coll., Oxford; B.A. (Oxon); war service, E. Africa, 1917-18; passed progress exam. in Mende; asst. dist. comrnr., Sierra Leone, Apr., 1914; priv. secy. to ag. gov., 1916-17; dist. comrnr., Aug., 1922; senr. dist. comrnr., Jan., 1931; prov. comrnr., 1932.
STONE, THOMAS HENRY, M.B.E., B.Sc., M.I.R.Sig.E., F.P.W.I. (London).—B. 1888; Messrs. Belliss & Morecom Ltd. 1903-12; S.E. & C.R., 1912-13; draughtsman, K.U.R. & H., 1913; ch. draughtsman, 1926.

STONEHAVEN, 1ST VISCOUNT (cr. 1938), Rt. Hon. Sir John Lawrence Baird, Bart., P.C. (1922), C.M.G. (1925), C.M.G., 1904, D.S.O. (1915), J.P., D.L.—B. 1874; ed. Eton and Christ Church, Oxford; ent. diplomatic service, Vienna, 1896; Cairo, 1898; Abyssinia, 1899; pvt. sec. to Sir U. Geratin, under-sec., pub. wks., Egypt, 1900-02; ag. agt. and cons. gen., Abyssinia, 1902; polit. offr., Br. E. Africa and Abyssinia frontier survey, 1902-3; polit. offr., Abyssinian army in Somaliland, 1903-4 (med. and clasp, desps., C.M.G.); 2nd sec., Paris, 1904-6; Buenos Aires, 1906-8; served as intelligence offr., European war, 1914-15 (desps., D.S.O.); M.P., Rugby, 1910-22; Ayr Burghs, 1922-26; parly. mem., Air Bd., 1916-18; parly. under sec., R.A.F., 1918; parly. under sec., H.C., 1919-22; min. of transport, 1922-24; 1st commr. of wks., 1924; gov. gen. and commdr.-in-ch., Commonwealth of Australia, 1925-31.

STOODLEY, EDWIN ALAN.—B. 1890; ed. Cranleigh Sch.; col. audit dept., Mar., 1915; asst. audr., Nigeria, May, 1915 (seconded to E. Africa, mily. audit, 1916-19, asst. audr., Somaliland, 1919); dep. audr., Gold Coast, July, 1933; ag. audr., May, 1934.

STOOKE, GEORGE BERESFORD, F.R.G.S., F.R.Econ.S., F.R.A.I.—B. 1897; ed. Bath; paymr. cadet, R.N., Jan., 1914; asst. naval sec., Malta Dockyard, 1915; asst. sec., rear admr. Sir D. R. L. Nicholson, 1917; do. to Adm. Sir L. Bayley, 1918; transf., emergency list of offrs., R.N., 1919; cadet, Sarawak civ. serv., 1920; asst. sec., 1924; dist. offr., Kenya, 1925; ag. clk. of leg. coun., 1931; clk., exec. coun. in addn., 1932; asst. treas., Mauritius, 1933; dep. treas., Kenya, 1936; dep. ch. sec., 1938; author of various papers on anthropological subjects.

STOREY, HAROLD HAYDON, M.A., Ph.D. (Cantab.).—B. 1894; ed. Cambridge Univ.; mily. serv., 1914-19; mycologist, dept. of agr., Union of S. Africa, Aug., 1922; plant pathologist, E. African agrl. research stn., Amani, Tanganyika Territory, Feb., 1928.

STORRS, SIR RONALD, K.C.M.G. (1929), K.T. BACH (1924), C.M.G. (1916), C.B.E. (Mil.) (1919).—B. 1881; ed. Temple Grove, Charterhouse and Pembroke Coll., Cambridge; classical scholarship, Charterhouse and Pembroke Coll., Cambridge; 1st cls. class, tripos, 1903; ent. min. of finance, Egypt, 1904; occupied posts in secretariat, finance, dept. of mines (sec.), cust. admin. and audit dept., Egypt, 1909; served with Sir E. Gorst, Lord Kitchener, Sir H. MacMahon and Sir R. Wingate; asst. polit. offr. to Anglo-French polit. mission, E.E.F., 1917; liaison offr. for the mission in Baghdad and Mesopotamia (desps.); secretariat of war cabinet, autumn, 1917; mem., Comité, Conservation des Monuments Arabes, Cairo, also comte., Palestine exploration fund; mily. gov. of Jerusalem, 1917-20; civ. gov., Jerusalem, Jaffa, Judaea and Southern Palestine; founder, Pro-Jerusalem Soc., Jerusalem schl. of music, Jerusalem chamber of commerce; chmn., Palestine local govt. comm., chmn., Palestine bd. of higher studies, vice-pres., Palestine Oriental Soc.; Knight of Grace of the Order of St. John of Jerusalem; comdr. of the Crown of Italy and St. Saviour's of Greece; gov. and commdr.-in-ch., Cyprus, Sept., 1926; do., Northern Rhodesia, Nov., 1932; ret., 1934; rep. Cyprus at 17th cong. of orientalists, Oxford, 1928; rep. Cyprus at C.O. confce., 1930; joint edr., Handbook of Cyprus, 1930 edn.

STOURTON, I. H. E. J.—B. 1901; ed. Stonehurst Coll., Lancs.; prob. inspr., pol., Mauritius, Oct., 1921; A.D.C. and pvt. sec. to O.A.G., Apr., 1922-Mar., 1923; inspr., Jan., 1924; ag. dep. inspr.-gen., July-Dec., 1932; ch. of pol., Bermuda, 1933.

STOUTE, CYRIL EUSTACE.—B. 1882; clk., dept. of agr., Barbados, Nov., 1898; after various promotions, apptd. 1st grade clk., and ch. clk., C.S.O., and clk., leg. coun., Feb., 1925; ag. col. sec., for various periods, 1926-32; man., govt. savings bank, 1932.

STOWELL, EDGER DE LA MOTHE, B.A.—B. 1893; ed. Selburgh Sch.; schlr., Corpus Christi Coll., Camb.; asst. mast., F.M.S., Mar., 1921; asst. mast., Penang Free Sch., Nov., 1924; instr., Penang Normal Cls. in addn., July, 1925 and Oct., 1931; headmr., High Sch., B. Mertajam, Jan., 1927 and Dec., 1930; sr. normal instr., Penang in addn., Apr., 1937; asst. inspr., schlr., Sept., 1931; supt., educn., Kedah, Oct., 1938.

STOYLE, HERBERT BLIN, A.M.I.Mech.E.—B. 1888; ed. Queen Elizabeth's Grammar Sch., Credilton, Battersea Polytechnic and L. and S.W.R.; Stan Creek rly., Br. Honduras, 1914; ag. dir., pub. wks., 1919-20; ag. dist. comsrr., Stan Creek, 1920; J.P.; asst. loco. supt., Tanganyika rly., 1921; dist. loco. supt., 1923; loco. supt., K.U.R.H., 1933.

STRACHAN, ANDREW ANDERSON, A.M.I.R.E., Chtd. Elec. Engr.—B. 1896; ed. St. Andrew's Univ., B.Sc. (St. Andrews); asst. tel. engr., P. & T. dept., F.M.S., Dec., 1924; engr., Selangor dist., Sept., 1932; sr. engr., Penang & Prov. Wellesley, Mar., 1936.

STRACHEY, SIR CHARLES, K.C.M.G. (1929), C.B. (1920).—B. 1862; apptd., after compet. exam., 2nd cl. jun. clk., P.O., 2nd Apr., 1885; 1st cl. jun. clk., 1st May, 1885; sec. Uganda rly. comtce., 10th Sept., 1895; 1st cl. clk., C.O., 20th Nov., 1898; prin. clk., 1st Jan., 1907; delegate on Anglo-German Boundary Com. (Yola-Chad), Mar., 1906; ditto, Anglo-French (Niger-Chad), May, 1906; ditto, Anglo-German (Yola to see), Oct., 1909; mem., W. African lands comtce., 1912; plenipotentiary at Brussels Internat. Liquor Conf., 1912; travelled in Nigeria, 1914; mem., Br. delegation, Paris Peace Confce., Jan. to Sept., 1919; Br. rep. on internat. comtce., for preparation of conventions on (1) African liquor traffic, (2) arms traffic, (3) revision of Berlin and Brussels Acts, signed at St. Germain, 10th Sept., 1919; asst. sec., 1st Apr., 1920; asst. under sec. of state, 12th Dec., 1924; ret., Sept., 1927.
STRADBROKE, 3RD EARL OF (cr. 1821).

GEORGE EDWARD JOHN MOWBRAY ROUS, BARON ROUS (cr. 1796), VISCOUNT DUNWICH (cr. 1821), K.C.M.G. (1920), C.B. (1904), C.V.O., A.D.C., D.L., J.Y.—B. 1862; ed. Harrow and Cambridge; col., 1st Norfolk Royal Garrison Arty. Volunteers, 1888-1908; col., 3rd (howitzer) brig., E. Anglian divn., R.F.A., 1908-17; col., 27th brigade, R.F.A., Jan. to Nov., 1917; gov., Victoria, 1920-26.

STRAHAN, FRANK, C.V.O., C.B.E., B.A., LL.B.—Sec., prime min's. dept., C'wealth of Australia; sec., Australian delegn. to the Imp. conf., 1923; gov't. dir., Amalgamated Wireless (A/sia) Ltd.; sec., Br. Australian and N. Z. Antarctic research expdn.

STRATFORD, RT. HON. JAMES, P.C. (1938).—B. 1869; ed. St. Aidan's Coll., Grahamstown, Cape Colony and Exeter Coll., Oxford; M.A., B.C.L., Oxon; mem., Inner Temple; called to the bar, England, 1898; Transvaal, 1902; practised, Johannesburg; K.C., 1912; judge, sup. ct., Transvaal prov. division, 1921; judge of appeal, 1927; ch. just., Union of S. Africa, 1938.

STREAK, M. J.—B. 1879; ed. Grey Coll., Bloemfontein; C.S., L.L.B.; asst. mag., Jacobsdal, 1900; Hoopstad, 1903; Heilbron, 1906; Senekal, 1910; Harrismith, 1913; mag., Bothaville, 1916; adnl. mag., Bloemfontein, 1919; mag. and mining comsrr., Namakualand, 1928; adnl. mag., Pretoria, 1930; mag., Ermelo, 1931; 1st civ. mag., Johannesburg, 1932; mag., Pt Elizabeth, Mar., 1936; do., Pietermaritzburg, Oct., 1936; ap. A' mag., Durban, Aug., 1937.

STREET, BRIG. HON. GEOFFREY AUSTIN, M.C., M.P.—Served A.I.F., 1914-19; brig.-major, 1917; lt.-col. commdg. 4th Light Horse, 1932; in commd. 3rd Cav. Bde., 1936; temp. brig., 1938; el. to H. of R. for Corangamite, Victoria, g.e., 1934, 1937; parly. sec. for defence, July, 1938; min. for defence, Lyons, Min., Nov., 1938.
STREET, HENRY EASTWOOD, D.E.C., A.F.I.A.—B. 1882; under sec. and perm. head, local gov't. dept., N.S.W., May, 1931; under sec. and perm. head, wks. and local gov't. dept., Feb., 1936.
STREET, HON. KENNETH WHISTLER—B. 1890; judge, sup. ct., N.S. Wales, Oct., 1931.

STREET, HON. SIR PHILIP WHISTLER, K.C.M.G. (1928).—B. 1863; puisne judge, N.S. Wales; judge in bankruptcy and probate jurisdictions, Feb., 1907; ag. ch. just., July, 1924; ch. just., 1925; ret. as ch. just., Jan., 1934; lieut.-gov., 1930.

STRICKLAND, 1st BARON (or. 1928), SIR GERALD STRICKLAND, COUNT DELLA CATENA, G.C.M.G. (1913), K.C.M.G. (1897), C.M.G. (1889).—B. 1861; ed. Oscott Coll., Birmingham; lient. C.U.R.V., and elected mem. coun. of gov't., Malta, 1886; B.A. and LL.B., Trin. Coll., Camb., honours law tripos; attended col. conf., 1887, on behalf of Malta; called to the bar, Inn. Temp., and pres. cholera comen., Malta, 1887; thanked by gov't. for services in negotiations with the Holy See; unoffic. mem., ex. coun., and ag. asst. sec. to gov't., 1889; vice-pres., bd. of health, pres., comtese of privileges, ch. sec. to gov't., and major, Royal Malta militia, which he established, 1889; re-organized Malta rly., 1891; planned breakwater, 1894; pres., coun. of Malta univ., 1900; chmn., mil. and civ. drainage bd.; mem. comte., on Malta naval reserves; gov. and comdr-in-chief, Leeward Is., 1902; estab. central factories for sugar and cotton in Antigua, 1903; gov., Tasmania, 23rd July, 1904; read scientific papers as pres. of Royal Society, 1904; gov., W. Australia, 6th Apr., 1909; mem. of W. Aust. Inst. of engrns.; gov'r., New South Wales, 25th Nov., 1912; assumed gov't., 14th Mar., 1913; ex-officio gov'f. of Norfolk Is., 1913-14; codified the laws of Norfolk Is., and negotiated the transfer of the dependency to the Commonwealth; hon. col., 86th W. Australia Infantry Regt.; mem. of Inst. of local gov't. engrns., N.S. Wales; held dormant coman. to administer C. of A. in absence of gov'-gen.; retired, 1917; prime min. of Malta, 1927.
STRINGER, HON. SIR THOMAS WALTER, KT. BACH. (1928).—B. 1856; ed. high schl., Christchurch, New Zealand; barrister and solr., K.C., 1908; judge of the sup. ct., New Zealand, 1914-27.

STRONACH, JOHN CLARK, B.A., B.A.I.—B. 1887; ed. St. Andrews Coll., Dublin, Dublin Univ. and Eng. Sch. T.C.D.; asst. engrn., Indian pub. wks., 1911; exec. engrn., 1919; New Delhi constrn., 1913-26; seconded to Kenya as supdg.-engnr. (bdgs.), 1926-29; transf'd. Kenya, 1930; ag. dir., P.W., 1930 and 1934; D.P.W., 1936.
STRONG, ERNEST EDWARD.—B. 1879; stock inspr., Basutoland, 1912; sub.-inspr., Basutoland mounted pol., 1915; inspr., 1929; supt., pol., 1936.

STRONG, THOMAS ARTHUR.—B. 1899; ed. Edin. Univ.; B.Sc. (For.) Edinburgh; asst. conserv., forests, F.M.S. Sept., 1921; do., Johore, May, 1926; dep. conserv., forests, June, 1931; conserv., forests, Mar., 1936.

STRONGE, SIR HERBERT CECIL, KT. BACH. (1930), K.C., Ireland.—B. 1875; ed. Falmouth Schl., and Trin Coll., Dublin; prizes classics and English literature; B.A.; called to the bar, King's Inn, Dublin, 1900; joined N.E. circuit, 1901; stip. mag., Bahamas, 1911; ag. atty.-gen., July to Aug., 1914, Sept. to Nov., 1914, and from May to Sept., 1915; also acted as chief just. in Jan., 1915; chief just. Tonga, 1917; reed. thanks of Tongan gov't. for services in 1918 epidemic; seconded for sp. duty as pres. of comen., in Fiji, 1921; ch. just., Leeward Is., 1925; ch. just., Cyprus, 1931; ret., 1938.

STROTHER-STEWART, ROBERT, M.A., B.C.L., B.Litt., Durham Univ., Gladstone prizeman.—Admitted solr., sup. oct., 1905; 2nd lieut. the Tynebouth R.G.A. (T.F.), 1913; A.D.C. to gen. offr. commdg. Tyne garrison, 1914-15; intelligence offr., Tyne garrison, 1916-17; ment. for valuable services; asst. competent mily. authority, Tyne garrison, 1917-19; called to bar, Inner Temple, 1919; major, R.A., T.F., R. of O., 1919-27; M.P. for Stockton-on-Tees, 1923-24; mag., county of Victoria, Trinidad, Apr., 1927; temp. asst. legal adviser, C.O., July to Dec., 1929; legal adviser to gov. of Malta, Jan., 1930; mem., privy and nominated couns. and coun. of health; examr. in English lit. and hist., Univ. of Malta; chmn. comte. to enquire into motor transport, 1930; dep. gov., July-Aug., 1932; chmn., traffic control bd., 1930-32; chmn., trade union council claims bd., 1932; puisne judge, Gold Coast, 1933; ag. ch. just., various occasions, 1935-38.

STRUTHERS, ERNEST ANDREW, M.B., Ch.B., D.P.H. (St. Andrew's), D.T.M. & H. (Eng.). Cert. L.S.T.M.—B. 1901; ed. Harris Acad., Univ. Coll., Dundee, St. Andrew's Univ. and Lond. Univ.; M.O., Malaya, Mar., 1926; ag. sr. health offr., Johore, June, 1936.

STUART, REV. CYRIL EDGAR, M.A.—B. 1892; ed. Dulwich Coll., Repton and S. John's Coll. and Ridley Hall, Cambridge; B.A., 1914; M.A., 1920; mily. serv., 1914-19, Salonica, 1916-19, deacon, 1920; priest, 1921; curate, St. Mary's, Hendy Rise, 1920; chaplain and lect., Ridley Hall, Cambridge, 1920-24; chaplain and librarian, Achimota, Gold Coast, 1925.

STUBBS, CAPT. EDWARD CARLETON.—B. 1881; ent. R.N., 1895; lieut. (navigating offr.), 1903; commdr., 1916; squadron navigating offr., First Battle Squadron, 1920-23; asst., King's harbrmr., Portsmouth, 1923-25; in command, H.M.S. "Agamemnon," 1925-27; ret. with rank of capt., Mar., 1927; master attendant, Colombo, Apr., 1927.

STUBBS, SIR REGINALD EDWARD, G.C.M.G. (1928), K.C.M.G. (1919), C.M.G. (1914).—B. 1876; ed. at Radley and Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford (exhibitioner); 1st cl. classical mods., 1897; 1st cl. lit. hum., 1899; B.A., 1899; M.A. (1920); apptd., after compet. exam., 2nd cl. clk., C.O., Jan., 1900; ag. 1st cls. clk., May, 1907; 1st cls. clk., Dec., 1910; visited S. Sttlmts., F.M.S. and Hong Kong to inquire into question of revision of civ. ser. salaries, 1910-11; mem. of West African lands comte., 1912; col. sec., Ceylon, Jan., 1913; O.A.G., Jan. to Oct., 1913, Dec., 1915 to Apr., 1916, and Mar. to Sept., 1918; gov., Hong-Kong, 16th June, 1919; assumed gov't., 30th Sept., 1919; Order of the Rising Sun of Japan, 1st cls., 1921; hon. LL.D., Hong Kong, 1926; hon. fellow, Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford, 1926; capt.-gen. and gov. in ch., Jamaica, 1926; gov. and commdr.-in-ch., Cyprus, Dec., 1932, do., Ceylon, 1933; ret., 1937; joint editor of C.O. List, 1910-1912; edited "Lucas' Historical Geography of the British Colonies," vol. i., 2nd edition, 1906.
STUCKEY, REGINALD ROBERT, C.M.G. (1932). A.I.A.—Pub. actuary, S. Australia, 1914; under treas. since 1923; chmn., state bank bd.; chmn., pub. serv. superannuation fund bd.; pub. debt commnsmr.

STUPART, SIR ROBERT FREDERIC, K.T. BACH. (1914).—B. 1887; ed.; priv. schl.; Upper Can. Coll.; ent. Can. meteorological serv., Nov., 1879; for some years was sen. inspr. and probability offr.; Toronto observ.; in charge of ch. station, Hudson's Straits, 1884-86; supt. and dir., meteorological serv. of Dom. and of magnetic observ., 28th Dec., 1894; fellow, Royal Soc., Can., 1901; pres., Royal Astron. Soc., Can., 1902-03; pres., Royal Can. Inst., 1906-07; mem., internat. meteorological comte., 1907 and 1919; pres., St. George's Soc., Toronto, 1919-20; ret., 1929.

STURGES, ROBERT SIMON MANSFIELD, O.B.E. (1929).—B. 1893; ed. Radley and Christ Ch.; astt. pol. offr., Iraq civ. admin., Apr., 1920; external oriental sec. to high commr., Iraq, Feb., 1921; astt. sec., Apr., 1921; polit. sec., Oct., 1924.

STURROCK, HON. FREDERICK CLAUD, M.P., Min. without Portfolio, M.I.Mech.E.—B. 1882; pres., asm., of chambers of commerce of S. Africa, 1924; mem., educn. adminstr. comm., 1924; mem., Port Elizabeth harbr. enquiry commn., 1925; chmn., main reef road commn., 1928; M.P., Turffontein div. of Johannesburg, 1929; mem. provl. finance commn., 1933.
STUTTAFORD, RICHARD.—B. 1870; M.P. for Newlands, and Claremont, Union of S. Africa from 1924; min. without portfolio, 1933; min. of interior and pub. health, 1936.

SUDBURY, EDWIN RONALD, M.A. (Oxon.), Barrister-at-law (Middle Temple).—B. 1897; on mily. serv., Jan., 1916; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Aug., 1921; attd. to Kurunegala Kach., Sept., 1921; attd., Nuwara Eliya kach., Nov., 1921; extra office astt. to govt. agt., C. Prov., Jan., 1925; pvt. sec. and A.D.C. to O.A.G., Apr., 1925; office astt. to govt. agt., Cent. Prov., Feb., 1927; astt. govt. agt., Kandy, Nov., 1928; on sp. duties in connexion with new constitution, Jan., 1930; admstive sec., income tax dept., June, 1932, elk., bd. of review in addn.; admstive sec. amalgamated depts. of income tax, estate duty and stamps, and elk., bd. of review, Oct., 1933; sec., elec. bd. in addn., July, 1936; sec. to gov., Jan., 1937.

SUKUNA, JOSEVA LALABALAVU VANAALALI, B.A. (Oxon.), Barrister-at-Law, Middle Temple.—ed. Wanganui, N.Z. and Wadham Coll., Oxford; held various appts., col. secretariat and native offices, Fiji 1907-12; served in France with the Foreign Legion and awarded the Mily. Med., 1914-16; ag. astt. sec. for native affrs. and aide-de-camp, 1916; cadet, Fiji, 1917; served in France with Fiji Labour Corps, 1917-18; called to bar, 1921; passed cadet, 1921; native lands com. snr., 1922; ag. dep. registr., sup. ct., 1922; astt. commn. for Fiji, Br. Empire Exhibn., 1924; ag. chmn. native lands comm., 1927; dist. commn., Lau, 1932; non-native mem., leg. coun., 1932; govt. rep., coronation, 1937; native M.L.C., 1937.
SULLIVAN, HON. DANIEL GILKS, J.P.—B. 1882; ed. Marist Bros. schl., Christchurch, N.Z.; mem., N.Z. H. of R. since 1919; min. of rly. and min. of industries and commerce, 1935.

SULLIVAN, JOHN ALEXANDER, K.C., B.C., B.A., LL.B.—B. 1879; capt., R. of O. barrister-solr. and atty., former vice-pres., Montreal bar; mem., gen. coun., Quebec bar; mem., bd. of examrs., do.; el. to H. of C., Canada, July, 1930; dep. P.M.G., Aug., 1935.

SUMNER, JAMES A.W.—B. 1908; ent. L.P.S., May, 1919; apptd. after compet. exam. cler. offr., London telephone serv., Apr., 1921; transf., C.O., Oct., 1923; seconded for serv. with Rhodesia-Nyasaland R. Comsn., Apr., 1938.

SURRIDGE, BREWSTER JOSEPH.—B. 1894; ed. Felsted Coll., and Downing Coll., Cambridge; B.A.; served, N. Lancs. regt., 1914-17; astt. sec., ch. secs. office, Cyprus, Mar., 1918; ag. ch. astt. sec., Jan. to Apr., 1919; local commt., police, Apr., 1919; ag. A.D.C., July, 1920; ag. astt. sec., Oct., 1920; ag. commn., Aug., 1921; ag. astt. sec., Sept., 1922; commn., Jan., 1924; admstive offr.; commn. and regiar., co-op. and co-op. credit socs., Jan., 1935; regiar., co-op. and co-op. credit socs., Mar., 1936.

SURRIDGE, ERNEST REX EDWARD.—B. 1889; ed. Felsted and St. John's Coll., Oxford; mod. hist. (hons.); B.A., 1922; lieut., 7th Batt., The Duke of Cornwall's Light Infy., 1917-20; France and Germany; astt. dist. offr., Tanganyika Territory, 1924; seconded to secretariat, 1925-26; aide-de-camp and pvt. sec. to ag. gov., 1928; seconded to C.O. as astt. prin., Sept., 1930-Feb., 1933; seconded to secretariat, 1933; ag. dep. ch. sec., 1935; astt. ch. sec., 1936; seconded to C.O. as prin., July-Oct., 1938.

SURVEYER, HON. EDWARD FABRE.—B. 1875; ed., Ste. Marie Coll., McGill and Laval Univ.; called to bar, 1896; vice-pres., Jun. Bar Assoc., 1902-03; pres., 1903-04; mem. of coun. of Quebec bar, 1904-05; lectr., law, McGill Univ. since 1905; judge, superior ct., Quebec, Jan., 1920; edr. of practice repts., Que., since 1898; also of pocket eds. of civ. code and code of civ. procedure (the latter in both languages); pres., Can. Club, Montreal, 1909-10; vice-pres., l'alliance Francaise, Montreal.
SUTCLIFFE, MAJOR A. W., D.S.O., M.C., B.Sc. (Durham).—Ast. dist. commn., Kenya, May, 1920.

SUTHERLAND, HON. DONALD MATHEWS, P.C. (Can.), M.B., D.S.O.—B. 1879; 1st el. to rep. S. Oxford in Ont. legis., g. e., May, 1902; re-el., g. e., Jan., 1905; dir., colonization and immigr., Ontario, Mar. 1909; el. at g. e., Dec., 1921; re-el., g. e., 1925 and 1930; min. of national defence in Bennett cabinet, Aug., 1930; re-el. by accl. after assuming office, Aug., 1930.
SUTTON, GEORGE, A.M.C.T.—B. 1902; ed. Accrington Grammar Schl. and Tech. Schl., Manchester Coll. of Technology; astt., govt. tech. schl., Gold Coast, June, 1927; ag. prin., do., 1938.

SUTTON, GEORGE GERHARD, K.C., LL.B.—B. 1880; ed. Dale Coll., King William's Town, Union of S. Africa, Manchester Grammar Schl. and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; called to bar, Middle Temple, June, 1903; admitted to bar, sup. ct., Cape Town, Oct., 1903; K.C. for Union of S. Africa, Sept., 1921; ag. judge, Cape prov. divn., on various occasions, 1922-28; judge, Cape prov. divn., Feb., 1929.

SWABEY, CHRISTOPHER.—B. 1906; ed. Winchester Coll. and Edinburgh Univ.; Younger silver med. for forestry, 1927; B.Sc. (forestry), 1928; astt. conservator, forests, Trinidad, 1928; forestry offr., Jamaica, 1937.
SWAIN, EDWARD HAROLD, FULCHER.—B. 1883; dipl. forestry (N.S.W.), M.I.A.W.A.; forestry commn., N.S.W., 1930.

SWAINE, CAPT. DAVID RICHARD, M.A. (Wales), M.C.—B. 1889; astt. mast., Penang Free Schl., Sept., 1913; on mily. serv., 1915-19; twice men. in deeps, M.C., astt. mast. Penang Free Sch., Feb., 1920; sr. normal instr., Penang, in addn., Mar., 1920; headmast., Northam Rd. Sch., Dec., 1924; European mast., ols. IB; prin., King Edward VII Sch., Taiping Sept., 1929; ag. ch. inspr., Eng. schs., S.S. & F.M.S., Sept., 1934; prin., Raffles Inst., S'pore, June, 1936; supt., educn., Johore, Oct., 1935; ag. ch. inspr., English schs., S.S. and F.M.S., July, 1937.
SWAN, GEORGE.—B. 1897; asst. govt. marine survr., Hong Kong, 1924; Hong Kong del. to safety of life at sea confee., Simla, 1931.

SWANZY, H. V. L.—B. 1915; ed. Wellington and New Coll., Oxford (open exhib., 1933); Gibbs schol. in mod. hist., 1935-36; 1st ols. hons., schol. of mod. hist., 1936; appt. after compet. exam., asst. prin., C.O., Oct., 1937.

SWEET-ESCOTT, SIR E. B., K.C.M.G. (1904), C.M.G. (1895).—B. 1857; ed. Somersetshire Coll., Bath, and at Balliol Coll., Oxon.; exhibitioner, Worcestershire Coll., Oxon., June, 1876; 2nd classical mods., June, 1878; 3rd class in mod. hist. finals, June, 1880; B.A., July, 1880; M.A., 1911; classical prof. at the Roy. Coll., Mauritius, June, 1881; précis writer, col. sec.'s office, Feb., and 2nd asst. col. sec., Apr., 1886; ag. col. sec., 1889; ag. col. sec., Br. Honduras, Mar., 1893; admtd. the govt. of that col., Apr. to Nov., 1893; col. sec., Br. Honduras, 1894; admtd. govt., Apr. to Nov., 1896, again in 1897; ag. 1st class clk., C.O., Jan., 1898; admstr. of the Seychelles Is., Aug., 1899; assumed govt., Nov., 1899; gov. and comdr.-in-chief, Oct. 1903; gov., Br. Honduras, Apr., 1904; gov., Leeward Is., Jan., 1906; recd. coronation medal, 1911; gov., Fiji, and high comnr., W. Pacific, March, 1912; assumed govt., July, 1912; ret., 1918; rep. of Fiji at Br. Empire Exhibn., 1924.

SWETTENHAM, SIR FRANK ATHELSTANE, G.C.M.G. (1909), K.C.M.G. (1897), C.H. (1917), C.M.G. (1886).—B. 1850; cadet, S. Sttlmnts., July, 1870; passed final exam in Malay, July, 1872; asst. collr. of land rev. for Penang and Prov. Wellesley, Aug., 1872; collr. of land rev., July, 1873; J.P. and mag. and comnr., ct. of requests, Penang, May, 1874; sent on special missions to Perak in Jan., June, and July, 1874; sent to reside with the Sultan of Selangor, Aug., 1874; asst. rea., Selangor, Dec., 1874; took charge of the residency in Perak on the murder of the rea., Nov., 1875; dep. comnr., Perak, Nov., 1875; ment. in desps.; asst. col. sec. for native states, Mar., 1876; asst. col. sec., July, 1881; Br. rea., Selangor, Sept., 1882; comnr. for S. S. and Malay States, Calcutta Exhibn., 1883-84; ag. Br. rea., Perak, Mar., 1884, to Jan., 1886; exec. comnr. for S. S. and Malay States, Col. and Ind. Exhibn., 1886; Br. rea., Perak, 1889; res. gen., Malay States, Jan., 1896; admtd. govt. S. S., Feb., 1901; gov., 1901; ret. 1904; chmn. of royal comm. on Mauritius, 1909.
SWIFT, EDMUND HAROLD, M.A., A.I.C.A.—B. 1893; under sec., dept. of pub. wks., N.S.W., 1934; under sec. and compt., accts., treasy., N.S.W., 1938.

SWINBOURNE, MAJOR CHARLES AUGUSTUS, O.B.E. (1918).—B. 1884; elk. audit.dept., N.S.W. govt.rly., May, 1900; lieut., Australian Rifle Regt., Sept., 1906; elk., pay and qtrmr., with rank of sub-inspr., Fiji constab., Oct., 1909; capt.; Commonwealth mil. forces, 1st Jan., 1910, comdg. offr., Fiji cadet corps, Feb., 1911; mem. of bd. of survey, Suva, Feb., 1911; supervising offr., Fiji rifle assoc., Apr., 1911; attd. 1st Loyal North Lancs. Regt., Mar.-June, 1914; accompanied Fiji 1st contingent to Europe as comdg. offr., Jan., 1915; J.P., Suva, Sept., 1915; comdt. of Fiji Defence Forces with rank of major, Sept., 1916; A.D.C. to gov., Oct., 1916; ment. by Army Council for services in connection with defence of Fiji; on active serv., July, 1917; tempy. capt., Loyal North Lancs. Regt., Aug., 1917; regmtl. musketry offr., 3rd Batt., L.N.L. Regt., Jan., 1918; dist. offr., Gilbert and Ellice Is. Col., Feb., 1920; dep. comnr., W. Pacific, Sept., 1920; dist. offr., Ellice Is., Aug., 1922; 1st dist. offr., Jan., 1928; senr. admstive. offr., July, 1931; ag. res. comnr. in 1928 and 1932.

SWORDER, HERALD HOPE.—B. 1887; survr., topo. br., F.M.S., June, 1921 and Jan., 1925; asst. supt., trig. br., July, 1924; offr. i/c, topo. br., Feb.-Dec., 1932; asst. supt., rev. br., Perak, Nov., 1933; ag. sr. asst. supt., rev. surveys, Kinta, 1935; dep. ch. survr., Jan., 1938.

SYDENHAM-CLARKE, FREDWAY SEYMOUR SYDENHAM, B.A. (Cantab.).—B. 1913; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Oct., 1936; attd., ch. sec.'s office, Aug., 1937; extra office asst., Mar., 1938; office asst., May, 1938.

SYKES, EDWIN LEONARD.—B. 1914; ed. Leys Schol. and Trinity Coll., Cambridge (senr. schol., 1936); 1st ols. history tripes., pt. I, 1935 and pt. II, 1936; B.A., 1936; appt. after compet. exam., asst. prin., D.O., Oct., 1937.

SYKES, GEORGE ROGNVALD, M.A. (Liv.).—B. 1889; cadet, S. Sttlmnts., Nov., 1913; ag. 3rd mag., Penang, July, 1914; attd. censor's office, S'pore; passed cadet, 1917; ag. asst. prot. of Chinese, S'pore, Apr., 1917; asst. regiar. impts. and expts. (war trade dept.) S'pore, June, 1917; ag. asst. prot. of Chinese (in addition) Mar., 1918; ceased to perform duties in war trade dept., ag. asst. prot. of Chinese, S'pore, Sept., 1918; ag. 3rd mag., S'pore, in addn., Dec., 1919; ag. 2nd asst. prot. of Chinese, S'pore, Jan., 1920; supernmy. offr., ols. IV., Nov., 1920; asst. prot. Chinese, S'pore, Sept., 1924; cls. III., Nov., 1925; prot., Chinese, Kedah, Dec., 1926; offr., cls. II, prot., Chinese, Perak, Nov., 1930; ag. immigrn. offr., S.S. and F.M.S., Feb., 1936; ag. res. coun., Malacca, Nov., 1936; comnr., trade and customs, Johore, April, 1938.
SYME, JAMES KEIR GODFREY.—B. 1903; ed. Durham Schl.; cadet and asst. dist. comnr., Gold Coast, 1928.

SYMES, LIEUT.-COL. SIR GEORGE STEWART, G.C.M.G. (1939), K.C.M.G. (1932), K.B.E. (1928), C.M.G., D.S.O.—B. 1882; ed. Malvern Coll. and Sandhurst; ent. Army, 1900; serv. S. African War (Queen's med., 2 clasps); Aden, 1903-04 (deeps., D.S.O.); attd., Egyptian Army, 1905 and Sudan govt., 1907; Blue Nile expedn., 1908 (med.); promoted major, 1915 and brevet lt.-col., 1917; A.D.C. to Sir达尔 and gov.-gen. of the Sudan, 1906-07; asst. dir. of intelligence, 1908-12; pte. sec. and sec., gov.-gen.'s coun. (Sudan), 1913-16; European War (3 times ment. in desps., med., brevet lt.-col.); attd., staff of high comnr., Egypt, 1917-19; attd., miny.of int., Egypt, 1919-20; dist. gov., Palestine, Sept., 1920; ch. sec., 1925; admstd. gov., Palestine, July to Sept., 1925 and May to Sept., 1927; accredited rep. to mandates comnr., 1926 and 1928; res. and commdr.-in-ch., Aden, 1923; gov. and commdr.-in-ch., Tanganyika Territory, 1931; gov.-gen., Sudan, 1933.

SYMONS, CLAUDE TREVINE.—B. 1879; B.A., Oxon., F.R.G.S.; vice-principal, govt. training coll., Ceylon, Aug., 1908; asst. govt. analyst, June, 1912; ag. govt. analyst, in addn. Dec., 1916; govt. analyst, June, 1919; ag. regis., med. coll. in addn., May to Dec., 1922.

TADMAN, JOHN LADBROOKE, B.A. (Oxon).—Ed. Norwich Schol. and St. Edmund Hall, Oxford (Goldsmiths' Exhibn., 1929); col. aud. dept., Feb., 1931; aast. audr., Uganda, Apr., 1931; aast. audr., Mauritius, Feb., 1935; ag. audr., June, 1936-Mar., 1937, and Aug.-Dec., 1938.

TAIT, HUGH NIMMO, C.M.G. (1937)—B. 1888; ed. at Clifton and St. John's Coll., Camb.; 1st cls. Math. Tripos, Parts I. and II.; 1st cls. Nat. Science Tripos, Part I.; apptd. after compet. exam., clk., chief sec.'s office, Ireland, Oct., 1911; 2nd cls. clk., C.O., Mar., 1912; ag. 1st class clk., 20th July, 1917; on mil. service from 27th Sept. to 9th Dec., 1918; sec. to Rhodesian Comsn., 1919-20; prin., 1st Apr., 1920; ag. asst. sec., D.O., 4th Sept., 1929; asst. sec., 24th Jan., 1930.

TAITT, HORACE VIBART.—B. 1887; ed. at Harrison Coll., Barbados; Island scholar, 1906; 3rd oir. class B.A., 1909; M.A., 1917; educn. offir., educn. dept., B. Guiana, 1914; ag. 2nd aast. inspr. of schools in 1914, 1915 and 1916; ag. 3rd cls. clk., educn. off., Jan.-Apr., 1917; ag. class. mast., Queen's Coll., Apr.-July, 1917; ag. aast. mast., do., Mar., 1930; aast. mast., 1934.

TALBOT, CHARLES HARRY VINCENT.—B. 1902; ed. Dulwich Coll. & St. John's Coll., Oxford; asst. ch. sec.'s office, Bermuda, July, 1925; asst. col. sec., Feb., 1937; ag. col. sec., Mar.-Oct., 1937 and July-Oct., 1938.
TAMBAYAH, GODFREY PRABAD, B.A. (hons.) Lond.—B. 1914; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Jan., 1937.
TAMWORTH, IAN PHILIP, B.A. (Forestry).—B. 1913; ed. Wellington Coll., and Magd. Coll., Oxford; asst. supt., botan. and for. dep., Hong Kong, 1937.

TATHAM, BERNARD MEABURN.—B. 1891; ed. Rossall Schol.; aast. acount., P.W.D., Uganda, May, 1921; ch. acount., Apr., 1922; aast. treas., Uganda, June, 1926, (with seniority from May, 1921); senr. aast. treas., Sept., 1934; senr. aast. treas., Gold Coast, Aug., 1936; ag. dep. treas., Sept., 1936-Apr., 1937; ag. treas., May-July, 1937; ch. aast. acount.-gen., July, 1937; ag. acount.-gen., Aug.-Sept., 1937, and April-Nov., 1938.

TAYLOR, ARTHUR JOHN COLLINS.—B. 1892; ed. Caterham Schol.; pol. dept., Hong Kong, 1913; acount., 1920, 1921-31 and 1932-35; inspr. stores, P.W.D., Nov., 1920; ag. cashier, treas., 1931-32; ch. acount., Kowloon Canton rly., 1935.

TAYLOR, ARTHUR PERCY, M.C., 1914 (Mons) Star.—B. 1892; ed. Colchester Royal Grammar Schol.; called to bar, Gray's Inn, 1926; served with R.N.V.R., R.N.D., R.N.A.S. and R.A.F., 1914-19; severely wounded, 1917; aast. acount., P.W.D., Gold Coast, Jan., 1920; aast. commit. Northern Territories constab., Sept., 1920; transfed. to Gold Coast pol. as aast. comnr., pol., Nov., 1923; oomsnr., pol. and in charge, C.I.D., 1926; seconded to Ashanti judiciary as ag. pol. mag., May, 1927; pol. mag., sup. ct., Gold Coast, June, 1928; crown coun., Sept., 1932; ag. solr.-gen. in 1933.

TAYLOR, CHARLES JAMES, B.Sc.—B. 1912; ed. Shanghai Pub. Schol. for Boys and Univ. of Edinburgh (B.Sc. forestry, 1934, postgrad. botany course, 1934-35, med. in Indian and col. forest trees); Imp. Forestry Inst., Univ. of Oxford, 1935-36; aast. conserv. of forests, Gold Coast, Sept., 1936; Gold Coast local forces, 1936.

TAYLOR, EVAN NUTTALL, Barrister-at-law, Inner Temple.—B. 1898; ent. R.M.A., 1916; 2nd lieut., R.F.A., 1918, France; R. of O., 1920; cadet, F.M.S., May, 1921; attch. dist. office, Raub, June, 1921; passed cadet, Jan., 1923; offr., cls. V, May, 1924; aast. dist. offr., Rembau, Dec., 1925; offr., cls. IV, May, 1928; aast. dist. offr., L. Perak, Dec., 1928; ag. aast. offr. assignee and regis. companies, S'pore, Nov., 1930; offr., cls. TAYLOR, ROBERT STANLEY, O.B.E. (1933), M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.), M.A., M.D., B.C., D.P.H. (Cantab.), Cert., S.T.M.—B. 1875; ed. TAYLOR, ROBERT WALTER, C.M.G. (1933), C.B.E. (1928), O.B.E. (1919).—B. 1883; ed. TAYLOR, ROYDEN ERNSOLIPPE, M.B.R. (1937)—B. 1889; ent. civ. serv., Grenada, Oct., 1905; seconded for war serv., Sept., 1915 to May, 1919; J.P., 1919; regisr., sup. ct., Grenada, Mar., 1931.

TAYLOR, WILLIAM CECIL, LL.B., B.A. (Manchester).—B. 1902; ed. Bolton Sch. and Manchester Univ.; cadet, S.S., Dec., 1924; offr., cl. V., Dec., 1927; offr., cl. IV., Dec., 1931; ag. astt. atty.-gen., Nov., 1933; offr., cl. III., Dec., 1936.

TEMPANY, HAROLD AUGUSTIN, C.B.E. (1933), D.Sc.(Lond.), F.I.C., F.C.S.—B.1881; ed. privately and Univ. Coll., London; asst. gov. chemist, Leeward Is., 1903; ag. gov't. chemist and supt. of agric., July to Dec., 1906, and Jan. to Nov., 1909; gov't. chemist and supt. of agric., Nov., 1909; J.P., Antigua, 1910; off. mem. legis. coun., Antigua, Mar. to July, 1912; represented Leeward Is. at 3rd Internat. Congress of Trop. Agric., June, 1914, and at rubber and tropical products exhibtn., June-July, 1914; chmn. of trustees Antigua pub. library, 1916; mem., Antigua Defence Reserve, 1905 to 1916; dir. of agric., Mauritius, 1917; nominated M.L.C., Mauritius, May, 1917; mem., bd. of dirs., Mauritius Inst., Jan., 1918; reported on S. African sugar industry, 1917; organised gov't tobacco factory, 1920; sugar experiment station, 1921; agril. coll., 1923-24; dairy, 1923; prin., Mauritius Agril. Coll. in addn. to substantive appt., 1924; reported on local sugar industry, 1926; organising vice-pres., Mauritius agr. and indus. exhibtn., 1926; chmn., various comtes.; dir. agr., S.S. and F.M.S., Jan., 1929; pres., bd., R.R.I., Malaya, Apr., 1929; visited Java as del., 4th Pacific Sol. Cong., June, 1929; prin., schl. of agr., Malaya, in addn., May, 1930; chmn., comtes., rice cult., Malaya, July, 1930; del., confee. of col. dirs., of agr., Lond., July-Dec., 1931; dir. agr., S.S., and advr. agr., M.S., Jan., 1935; author of numerous papers and repts. on agril. chem., cane sugar production, agril. educn. and trop. agr.
TEMPLE, ALFRED JOHN BROOMHALL.—B.1912; ed. Denstone Coll.; 16/5th Lancers, 1931-32; Palestine pol., 1933-36; sub-inspr., pol. Ceylon, 1936-38; supdt., pol., Somaliland, 1938.
TEMPLEK, JAMES TREVOR.—B. 1900; asst. conserv., forests, Kenya, 1938.

TENISON, EDWARD HERON RYAN, B.A. (Oxon.)—B.1898; on mil'y serv., Apr., 1917; cadet, Ceylon, Nov., 1921; attd., Kandy Kach., Dec., 1921; office astt., Anuradhapura kach., Feb., 1924; ditto, Badulla kach., June, 1925; ditto, Batticaloa kach., Nov., 1926; pol. mag., Matale, Dec., 1927; astt. gov't. astt. Badulla, Jan., 1929; addnl. astt. gov't. astt., Colombo, Nov., 1931; pol. mag., Colombo, Nov., 1932; addnl. astt. ch. sec., Nov., 1935; comsnr., development of agril. marketing, Nov., 1936; dep. comsnr. for dev. of agric. marketing, Sept., 1937; attd. office of food contr., Apl., 1938.

TETLEY, ALBERT EDWARD MARRIOTT, B.Sc. (Civil Eng.), A.S.A.C.E.—B. 1898; ed. S. African Coll. High Schl. and Witwatersrand Univ.; served in G.E.A. campaign; astt. engrnr., irrig. dept., Union of S. THERON, COL., FRANCOIS HENRI.—B. 1891; ed. THOMAS, Rt. Hon. JAMES HENRY, P.C. (1917), M.P., J.P., LL.D., Cambridge.—B. 1875; served as cleaner, fireman and driver, G.W.R.; has served in turn as organiser, astt. sec., gen. sec. and pres., Nat. THOMAS, LESLIE ARDING.—B. 1891; ed. Portsmouth Gram. THOMAS, Sir SAMUEL JOYCE, Kt. Bach. (1935).—B. 1875; ed. King's Coll., London; Inglis schl. and early English Text Soc'y prizeman; assoc. (with distinc.) Indian ct. of appl., 1921-23; 2nd puisne judge, Trinidad, Mar., 1923; chmn., oil and water bd., 1923; ag. 1st puisne judge on various occasions, 1923-26 and in 1928; puisne judge, Kenya, 1929; ch. just., F.M.S., Oct., 1933; ret., Oct., 1937.

THOMAS, THOMAS JOHN, M.C., M.A. (Oxon.)—B. 1893; ed. Univ. Coll. of Wales and Jesus Coll., Oxford; European mast., F.M.S., July, 1923; ag. headmast., High Schl., Malacca, Apr., 1930; do., Penang Free Schl., Aug., 1933; headmast., Sultan Abdul Hamid Coll., Alor Star, Dec., 1936.

THOMAS, Sir THOMAS SHENTON WHITELEGGE, G.C.M.G., (1937), K.C.M.G.(1931), C.M.G.(1929), O.B.E. (1919).—ed. at Leatherhead and Queen's Coll., Camb. Africa civ. serv. comm., 1918; astt. ch. sec., Uganda, Jan., 1919; ag. ch. sec., June, 1919 to Apr., 1920; chmn., Ugandas development comm., 1919; gov's. dep., Jan.-Feb., 1920; prin. astt. sec., Nigerian secretariat, Mar., 1921; ag. dep. ch. sec., Apr.-Dec., 1921; dep. ch. sec., Jan., 1924; ag. ch. sec., in 1925 and 1926; gov's. dep. on various occasions, 1925-26; col. sec., Gold Coast, 1927; gov., Nyasaland, 1929; gov. and commdr.-in-ch., Gold Coast, 1932; gov. and commdr.-in-ch., Straits Stltm. and high commr., Malay States, 1934.
