THORNE, RICHARD ALFRED.—B. 1902; apptd. after compet. exam., asst. clk., sec.'s office, G.P.O., 24th Oct., 1919; cler. offr., 1st Jan., 1920; transf. to C.O., 15th Oct., 1920; cler. offr., higher grade, 29th Oct., 1930.

THORNE THORNE, RICHARD JOHN PERCY, M.A. (Cantab.)—B. 1900; ed. Charterhouse and Christ's Coll., Cambridge; served R.A.F., 1918; 2nd lieut., The Buffs, 1920; lieut., 1922; Tropical African services course, Cambridge, 1926-27; cadet, Uganda Prot., 1927; priv. sec. to govnr., Uganda, 1928-29; asst. dist. offr., 1929; dist. commr., 1934-36; lieut. R. of O., K.A.R., 1933; capt., 1935; admstve. offr., Cyprus; asst. sec., Aug., 1936; clk., exec. coun., Dec., 1936; ag. ch. asst. sec., Aug., Dec., 1937 and Apr., June, 1938; liaison offr. i/c Cyprus inf. office, London, Sept., 1938.

THORNTON, BENJAMIN BECKETT, F.I.A.C.—B. 1890; war serv., 1914-19; clk., K.U.R. & H., 1922; ag. asst. ch. acctnt., Jan.-Nov., 1924; constrn. acctnt., 1925; workshops acctnt., 1930; senr. asst. do., 1932.

THORNTON, LIEUT.-COL. (TEMP. COL.), SIR EDWARD NEWBURY, K.B.E. (1919), M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.), D.P.H. (Camb.), S.A.M.C., commdr. of the Order of St. John of Jerusalem (1924).—B. 1878; ed. Cheltenham Coll. and London Hosp.; asst. med. offr., Imp. Yeomanry Hospitals, Anglo-Boer war, 1900-01; plague serv., Punjab, 1902-03; ent. civ. serv., Cape Colony, 1903; additional M.O.H., Cape Colony, 1906; med. inspr. of hosp. and charitable insts., and ch. local govt. inspr., Cape, 1910; major, med. br. sec. I, Union Res. of Offrs. in German S.W. Africa, 1915; (ment. in desps.); Lieut.-Col., S.A.M.C. and O.C. S. African mil. hosp., Richmond, Surrey; mem., comtee. of management, and surg. to Queen Mary's aux. conval. hosp. for limbless soldiers, Roehampton, and mem., comtee. management, Queen Mary's aux. conval. hosp. for facial injuries, Sidcup; chmn., exec. comtee. on vocational training of soldiers in hosp., Lond. dist.; represented S. Africa, Inter Allied Conf. on disablement problems arising out of the war, 1916-20; senr. aast. health offr. for the Union, dir. of med. services, Union Defence Forces, and chmn., cent. housing bd. for Union, 1920; sec. for pub. health and ch. health offr. for Union, 1932.

THORNTON, GEORGE EDWIN, M.B.E. (1932).—B. 1899; war. serv., 1916-17 (E. Africa); clk., N. Rhodesia, 1917; ag. aast. comsrr., taxes for periods, 1926, 1927 and 1928; aast. comsrr., taxes, Apr., 1928; comsrr., income tax, Apr., 1932; ag. treas., 1933; ch. acctnt. and comsrr., income tax, 1937.

THORNTON, HUGH HOLCOMBDELEY, C.M.G. (1920), C.V.O. (1920).—B.1881; ed. Kelly Coll., Tavistock, and at Christ Church, Oxford; priv. sec. to Viscount Milner, as mem. of the War Cabinet without portfolio, Dec., 1916 to Apr., 1918; as sec. of state for war, 1918-19; as sec. of state for the colonies, 14th Jan., 1919; apptd. crown agt. for the colonies, 1st Oct., 1920.

THORNTON, RUSSEL WILLIAM, C.B.E. (1936).—B.1881; ed. Graaff Reinet and Elsenburg Schl. of Agr., Cape Prov. (dipl. with hon.); served in Anglo-Boer War as Scout and intelligence offr.; joined dept. of agr., Cape Prov. field crops and experiment and research section, 1908; offr.-in-ch., agrl. research branch, agrl. dept., Cape prov., 1908; prin., Grootfontein Schl. of Agr. and experiment station, 1910; sent by govt. to England, Europe and Australia to study agrl. educon., experiment station organ. etc., took charge of expndn. to W. Central Africa, in connection with ostrich industry of Union, 1912; active serv. in France, 1917; resumed charge of Grootfontein Schl. of Agr., 1919; dir., field and animal husbandry, Oct., 1923; dir., native agr., dept. of native affrs., Apr., 1929; dir., agr., Basutoland, and agrl. advr. to H. Cr., 1934.

THOROGOOD, WILLIAM JESSE, M.C. — B. 1896; ed. Latymer Upper Schl. and Trinity Hall, Camb.; war serv., 1915-18; cadet, F.M.S., Apr., 1921; attd., Chinese prot., Ipoh; passed cadet, Sept., 1923; offr., cls. V, Apr., 1924; aast. contrlr., lab., Seremban, Aug., 1924; aast. dist. offr., Gopeng and Kampar, Feb., 1926; aast. sec. to res., Selangor, June, 1927; offr., cls. IV, Apr., 1928; aast. contrlr., lab., Klang; aast. contrlr., lab., Penang, May, 1931; ag. emigrn. comsrr. for Malaya, May, 1932; offr., cls. III, Apr., 1933; mag., K. Lumpur, Apr., 1937.
THORPE, CYRIL EDWARD, B.Sc., A.M.I.C.E. — B. 1899; aast. engrnr., P.W.D., Nigeria, 1924; senr. exec. engrnr., 1935.

TIBBITT, CAPT. ERIC MONTAGUE (R. of O.).—B. 1896; served in European War, 1914-18; priv. sec. to gov., Br. Honduras, Nov., 1920; ag. clk., exec. coun., Br. Hond. Nov., 1922; aast. audr., Nigeria, Aug., 1924; audr., Br. Hond., Feb., 1928; capt., Br. Hond. Defence Force, Apr., 1928; senr. aast. audr., Uganda, Oct., 1934; audr., Cyprus, 1937.

TICKLE, ARTHUR GEORGE WARNHAM, F.R.I.B.A., F.S.I.—B. 1887; ed. Holy Trinity, Bansbury, Polytechnic and Royal Acad., London; aast. engrnr., P.W.D., Hong Kong, 1913; exec. engrnr., 1920; tech. sec., D.P.W., 1923-4 and 1928-32; J.P., 1931; aast. D.P.W., 1932; ag. D.P.W., 1933 and 1936.
TILLMAN, JOHN, M.B., Ch.B. (Edin.).—B. 1901; temp. med. offr., Somaliland, 1936; med. offr., 1937.

TILNEY, CHARLES EDWARD, M.A. (Oxon.).—B. 1909; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Jan., 1932; attd., office of dir., med. and san. services, Feb., 1932; attd., Galle kach., Sept., 1932; office aast., Ratnapura kach., June, 1933; admstve. sec., dept. of med. and san. services and sec. to min. for health, Mar., 1934; aast. comsrr., lands, Mar., 1937.

TINDALL, BENJAMIN ARTHUR.—B. 1879; ed. Victoria Coll., Cape Prov.; B.A., LL.B.; called to bar, 1903; practised, Pretoria; K.C., 1919; presided, sp. ct., Swaziland, 1914; judge, sup. ct., Transvaal prov. divn., Dec., 1922; judge pres.; Transvaal prov. divn., 1937; judge of appeal, 1938.

TINWORTH, WILLIAM LEONARD, A.M.Inst.T.—B. 1885; mil. serv., India and Mesopotamia, 1917-19; aast. acctnt., F.M.S. rly., 1921; ag. dep. acctnt., 1926, 1929 and 1930; ag. acctnt., pub. wks. dept., 1929; dep. ch. acctnt., Tanganyika rly., 1931; ag. ch. acctnt., 1933; ch. acctnt., 1936; ag. gen. man., 1938.

TISDALE, EDWARD GORDON ST. CLAIR, M.C.—B. 1895; ed. Bedford Schl. and Selwyn Coll., Cambridge; served in France 1st. Monmouth Regt., 1915-18; aast. dist. comsrr., E.A.P., Oct., 1919; ag. comsrr., mines, Kenya, 1933 and 1936.

TISDALE, HERBERT GEORGE, B.Sc., A.R.C.Sc., A.M. INST. C.E., A.M.I.E.E.—B. 1879; ed. Bedford Schl. and Imp. Coll. of Science (Whitworth schol.), eng. inspr., C.A., 1907; aast. engrnr., engng. contracts dept., 1913; dep. head, engng. contracts dept., 1926.

TITCHENER, HENRY IAN HERRICK.—B. 1900; entd. C.O., Feb., 1916; apptd., after compet. exam., asst. clk., C.O., 24th Apr., 1918; cler. offr., 1st Jan., 1920; higher grade cler. offr., 13th July, 1928; aast. librarian, 14th Jan., 1929.

TODD, CEDRIC LINDLEY.—B. 1897; ed. Sherborne Schl.; served in European War, Western fronts, 1914-18 and Rhine Army, 1919-21; aast. audr., Nyasaland, Nov., 1921; aast. audr., Tanganyika Territory, Feb., 1929; senr. audr. audr., 1933; dep. audr., Apr., 1937; ag. audr., Oct.-Apr., 1938.

TODD, CHARLES DOUGLAS—B. 1901; aast. treas., Tanganyika Territory, 1923-25; resigned; re-apptd., 1929; ag. treas. and ch. of cust., Somaliland, June-Oct., 1938; treas., Antigua and fedl. treas., Leeward Is., 1936; aast. col. sec., Gold Coast, 1938.

TODD, DUDLEY C.—B. 1906; ed. St. Kitts Nevis Grammar Schl. and Lodge Schl., Barbados; clk. and cotton inspr., agrl. dept., St. Kitts, 1929; ch. san. offr., country districts, 1930; clk. and storekeeper, P.W.D., 1933; 2nd lt., defence force, 1935; ag. ch. rev. offr. and harbr. mast., 1937.

TODD, RONALD RUSKIN.—B. 1902; ed. County High Schl. and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge (schol.); B.A. (hons.), 1924; M.A., 1928; cadet, Hong Kong, 1924; passed Cantonese, June, 1927; aast. to sec., Chinese affrs., 1927; aast. aupt., impts. and expts., 1930; ch. aast. to sec., Chinese affrs., 1933; chmn., urban coun., 1936.

TOLFREE, WILLIAM NORMAN.—B. 1892; ed. Dudley Coll., N. Stafford Regt. and R.A.F., 1913-20; impnr., schls., S. Prova., Nigeria, 1921; ag. ch. inspr., Dec., 1930-May, 1931; senr. audr., 1938; prin., May, 1938.
TOLMIE, GEORGE MACKENZIE, B.Sc. (Edin.).—B. 1904; ed. Inverness Royal Acad., Edinburgh Univ. and Imp. Forest Inst., Oxford; aast. conservator, forests, Gold Coast, 1926.

TOMLINSON, SIR GEORGE JOHN FREDERICK K.C.M.G. (1934), C.B.E. (1928), M.A. (Oxon); Barrister-at-law.—B. 1876; ed. Charterhouse and Univ. Coll., Oxford; Transvaal educ... dept., 1903-04; asst. res., N. Nigeria, 1907; seconded to Gold Coast as dir., educn., 1909; reverted to N. Nigeria, 1911; 3rd cls. res., 1911; ag. res., 1912; 2nd cls. offr. (change of title), 1914; 1st cls. dist. offr., 1918; ag. sec., N. Prova., 1919; asst. sec., native affrs., 1921; ag. sec., do., 1921 and 1925; staff grade (superint.), 1925; ret., 1928; asst. under sec. of state, C.O., 1st Oct., 1930.

TOMLINSON, JOHN.—Asst., P.O., Port Elizabeth, 1900; 1st cls. asst., comml. br., Midlands dist., 1912; senr. asst., do., 1914; divnl. comml. supt., comml. br., Johannesburg, 1922; ch. supt., Johannesburg, 1923; 2nd grade ch. clk., telephone man., Johannesburg, 1929; ag. ch. clk., ch. clk.'s office, Pretoria, 1929; ch. clk., 1929; contr., stores, Pretoria, June, 1930; ag. asst. under sec., head office, Pretoria, 1932-33; contr., post office propaganda, 1936.

TOMLINSON, THOMAS FOWLER, M.C., B.Sc., A.M.I.C.E.—B. 1884; ed. Queen's Royal Coll., Trinidad, and Leeds University; asst. engrnr., constrn., Trinidad govt. rly., 1911; served with R. Engnrs., France, 1915-19; res. engrnr., constrn., Trinidad govt. rly., 1920; temp. D.P.W., Virgin Is., 1925; dist. engrnr., P.W.D., Br. Guiana, 1927; ch. engrnr., govt. rlys., Trinidad, 1928; supt., Trinidad govt. rly., 1933.

TOMLINSON, SIR THOMAS SYMONDS, K.T. BACH (1925).—B.A. Trin. Coll., Camb.; B. 1877; called to the bar, Inner Temple, 1901; town mag., Zanzibar, May, 1907; asst. judge of H.B.M. ct. for Zanzibar, Jan., 1911; ag. judge, Feb. to Nov., 1916; judge (now ch. just.), 11th Oct., 1919; seconded as legal adviser of the civil admtn. of the occupied terry. of Ger. E. Africa, Feb., 1918; ag. atty.-gen., Tanganyika Territory, Dec., 1920 to July, 1921; prepared revised edn. of the laws of Zanzibar, 1922; 2nd cls., Order of the Brilliant Star, Zanzibar, 1923; ret., 1928.

TONGUE, EDWIN, M.M.—B. 1898; pol. probr., S.S., Jan., 1925; asst. supt., pol., June, 1929; asst. supt., pol., detec. br., S'pore, June, 1930; dep. commr., estate duties, in addn., July, 1934; collr., estate duty, S.S., Jan., 1936; offr.-in-ch., stamp office, in addn., Apr., 1936.

TONGUE, ERIC DAUNCY, O.B.E. (1934).—B.A., LL.B.—Ed. Taunton Schl. and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge (schol. and exhib.).; asst. dist. commr., Uganda, October, 1913; dist. commr., Aug., 1923; lab. commr., May, 1930; reverted dist. offr. on abolition of post, Oct., 1931; senr. dist. offr., Dec., 1935; prov. commr., 1937.

TONKIN, SYDNEY, B. Eng. (Liverpool), A.M.I.C.E., A.M.I.M.E., Chtd. Civ. Engrnr., Chtd. Mech. Engrnr.—B. 1886; asst. engrnr., P.W.D., F.M.S., June, 1920; exec. engrnr., Dec., 1925; do., F.M.S., govt factory, June, 1929; senr. exec. engrnr., June, 1936.

TOPP, W.M., I.S.O. (1937).—B. 1884; Gambia govt. serv., 1901-07; S. Nigeria govt., 1907-08; N. Nigeria govt., Oct., 1908 to Sept., 1910; transf. to the Gambia, Sept., 1910; 1st clk., col. sec.'s office, Nov., 1911; ch. clk., June, 1913; sp. cls. apptnt., Jan., 1920; ag. clk., leg. couns. on various occasions, 1916-22; asst. col. sec., Jan., 1924; offr.-in-charge, secretariat, July-Aug., 1925; J.P. and commr., ct. of requests, July, 1925.

TORY, GEOFFROY WILLIAM.—B. 1912; ed. King Edward VII Schl., Sheffield, and Queen's Coll., Cambridge (mod. languages schol., 1931-35); med. and mod. languages tripes, pt. 1 cls. I, pt. 2 cls. I; B.A., 1934; apptnt., after compet. exam., asst. prin. D.O., Oct., 1935.

TOOTHILL, JOHN DOUGLAS, C.M.G. (1938).—B. 1888; ed. Blundell's Schl.; Ontario Agr. Coll. (Toronto Univ.); B.Sc., Agr., 1910; Cornell Univ., U.S.A., Graduate Schl., 1913, 1914; Harvard Univ., D.Sc., 1922; agt. and expert, U.S. Bureau of Entomology, 1910-11; entomologist, entomological branch, Ottawa, 1911-24; seconded as dir., Levuana campaign, Fiji, for two years, Nov., 1924; ag. dir., agr., Fiji, May, 1925; dir., Levuana campaign and agr., 1926; dir., agr., Uganda, June, 1929.

TOUGHIER, ROBERT ALEXANDER HUNTER.—B. 1891; ed. Royal Belfast Acad. Inst. and Queen's Univ. of Belfast, B.A. (1914); army, 1914-19; war ser., Dardanelles, Egypt and Syria, ment. in desps.; capt., gen. R. of O., 1919; soi., Ireland, 1920; asst. land offr., Tanganyika Territory, 1929; asst. commr., lands, Gold Coast, 1937.

TOUSSAINT, JAMES REGINALD.—B. 1879; clk., gen. clerical serv., Ceylon, Mar., 1898; ag. off. ast., Matara kach., Jan., 1917; sec., Buddhist temporalities ord. comm., and extra off. ast. to dir., educn., Nov., 1918; apptd. to civ. serv., July, 1919; addnl. ast. commr. of stamps, Sept., 1921; ast. commr., stamps, Mar., 1930; dep. commr., stamps, July, 1931; dep. fiscal, Colombo, Oct., 1934; office ast., Kurunegala kach., Feb., 1935; dep. fiscal, Colombo, June, 1935.

TOVEY, DUNCAN LORIMER PATTISON.—B. 1903; ed. Clifton Coll. and Trinity Hall, Cambridge; M.A.; asst. priv. sec. (appts.) to S. of S., Mr. L. C. M. S. Amery, Dec., 1926; to Lord Passfield, June, 1929; apptd. ast. prin., C.O., Oct., 1930; prin., 4th Nov., 1937.

TOWNLEY, CHARLES HENRY.—B. 1892; served with field coy., R.E.'s (T.) in Egypt, Gallipoli, France, Belgium and Germany 1914-19; ast. govt. printer, Nigeria, May, 1920; senr. supt., Aug., 1926; ag. govt. printer, May, 1927 and Nov., 1929; govt. printer, Gold Coast, 1931.

TRATMAN, EDGAR KINGSLEY, L.D.S., M.D.S.—B. 1899; ed. Clifton Coll. and Bristol Univ.; prof., dental surgery, coll. of med., S'pore, Oct., 1929; mem., dental bd., F.M.S., Apr. 1931; do., S.S., July, 1931.

TREMLETT, CAPT. JOHN DANIEL SCHWEDER, M.C., M.R.C.V.S.—B. 1886; ed. Harrow Schl. and R. Vety. Coll., London; war serv., Gallipoli and France, 1915-18; M.C., 1917; vety. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Dec., 1920; senr. vety. offr., Apr., 1923.

TREVOR, IVAN BERNARD.—B. 1900; ed. Tyttenhanger Lodge, Seaford; Rossall exhib., 1915 (not taken up); cadet, Midland rly., 1917; traffic ast., Kowloon-Canton rly., Hong Kong, Dec., 1924; ag. traffic supt. and ch. storekeeper in addn., 1925 and 1927; ag. ch. acct., 1928; passed Cantonese, 1928; traffic man. and ch. storekeeper, 1932; capt., H.K. Vol. Def. Corps., 1935.

TRINICK, COMDR. GEORGE WILLIAM, R.D., R.N.R.—B. 1889; merchant serv., 1904-13; extra master's R.O.T. cert.; R.N., 1914-19 (H.M.S. "Diana," Home waters, near and far East, H.M.S. "Temeraire," North Sea and Russia); marine offr., Nigeria, 1922; lieut. comdr., 1924; comdr., on retired list, 1934; ag. dir., marine, 1935 and April, 1937; prin. marine offr., 1935.
TRIPE, WILLIAM BORROWDALE.—Ed. Christ's Coll., Christchurch, N.Z., Victoria Univ. Coll. and Balliol Coll., Oxford; ast. dist. offr., Tanganyika Territory, July, 1929.

TROUGHTON, EDWARD PERCEVAL — B. 1899; ed. Forest Sch.; R.N.A.S. and R.A.F., 1917-18; asst. to dist. commr., Basutoland, 1920; asst. to finan. sec., 1923; asst. treas., Tanganyika Territory, 1926; senr. asst. treas., Sept., 1935; dep. treas., Mar., 1937; acct.-gen. and currency offr., Aug., 1937.

TROUGHTON, JOHN F. G., M.B.E. (1936).—B. 1902; ed. St. Andrew's Coll., Dublin and Trinity Coll., Dublin; foundation schol. in matha.; B.A. (senr. moderatorship and gold med. in ment. and moral philosophy), 1924; LL.B. (1929); admstve. cadet, Kenya, 1926; dist. offr., 1928; on sp. duty, Kenya land comm., 1933; ag. clk., leg. coun., and clk., exec. comm., 1933; seconded to C.O., 1936; seconded to B.B.C. as Empire news edtr., 1936; on sp. duty, C.O., 1938.

TROWELL, JAMES HENRY ATKIN, A.M.I.M.E. — B. 1891; inspr., machinery, Selangor, Negri Sembilan and Pahang, Nov., 1920; do., Ipoh, Oct., 1922; ag. licensing offr., automobiles, Perak South, in addn., Mar., 1924; ag. ch. inspr., machinery, F.M.S., in addn. June, 1932; inspr., machinery, Ipoh, and ag. ch. inspr., machinery, F.M.S., in addn., Apr., 1933.

TRUPHET, JOHN SYDNEY.—B. 1883; ed. Roan Schl. and King's Coll., London; entd. Crown Agent's office, 1901; asst. head, stores dept., 1919; do., insurance and checking dept., 1920; do., shipping dept., 1922; dep. head, shipping dept., 1927; dep. head, stores dept., 1931; head, do., 1933; head, shipping dept., 1936.

TRUSTED, SIR HARRY HERBERT, K.T. BACH. (1938), K.C.—B. 1888; ed. Ellesmere Coll. and Trin. Hall, Camb.; called to bar, Inner Temple, 1913; served overseas (Duke of Cornwall's L.I. and staff, East Indies), 1914-19; puisne judge, Leeward Is., 1925; atty.-gen., 1927; commr. to revise the federal acts, Leeward Is., 1928; K.C., Leeward Is., 1929; atty.-gen., Cyprus, Dec., 1929; temp. attd., legal advisory staff, C.O., Aug., 1931; atty.-gen., Palestine, 1932; ch. just., 1936.

TRUTER, SIR THEODORUS GUSTAFT, K.B.E. (1924), C.M.G. (1918).—B. 1873; ed. Boys Pub. Schl., George; Normal Coll., Capetown; Diocesan Coll., Rondebosch; Cape civ. ser., Dec., 1892; chief examr., accts., 1895; lieut., Robert's Horse, 1899-1902 (med. and four clasps); asst. mag., Pretoria, Apr., 1901; mem., spec. criminal ct.; mag., Ermelo, Dec., 1904; mag., Standerton, Apr., 1908; prov. sec., Transvaal, July, 1910; commr., S. African pol., Oct., 1910; J.P., Union of S. Africa; ret., 1928.

TUCKER, D.E.A., M.C.—B. 1896; ed. at St. Francis de Sales Fathers, Givisiez (Fribourg) and Florimont Coll., Lancy (Geneva), B.-es-Lettres (1re partie) Grenoble Univ., July, 1914; Wilts. Regt. and M.G.C., 1914-20; 2nd lieut., June, 1917; H.M. cust., U.K., 1923; inspr.-in-ch., preventive serv., Tanganyika cust., 1929.

TUCKER, KEITH RAVENSCROFT, C.B.E. (1932), F.L.A.A.—B. 1890; articled pupil to Messrs. Yarrow & Co., Glasgow, 1906-08; entd. engnr. dept., office of crown agts. for the colonies, Mar., 1908; transf. to col. audit dept., cent. office, June, 1913; asst. audr., G. Coast, Sept., 1913; served with Togoland Field Force, Aug.-Sept., 1914 (mentd. in despa.); lieut., G. Coast Volrs., Apr., 1915; asst. audr., Nyasaland Prot., June, 1916; capt., Nyasaland Field Force, 1919; despatched Pretoria, on milv. audit duties, Oct., 1918 to Apr., 1919; dep. treas., Nyasaland, Dec., 1921; treas., Jan., 1924; ag. ch. sec., and dep. to gov. various occasions; ag. gov., Feb., 1934; finan. sec., N. Rhodesia, Feb., 1938; ag. ch. sec., June-Sept., 1938; chmn., agric. advisory and maize control bds.

TUCKER, RICHARD WILLIAM ETHELBERT, M.A. (Cantab.), B.Ed., (Capetown).—B. 1892; ed. Wilsons Grammar Schl. and Selwyn Coll., Cambridge; Istcl., nat. sci. tripos pt. I (hons. chem. and zoology) 1913; Ist cln. nat. sci. tr pos. pt. II (zoology) 1914; asst., S. African museum, Cape-town, 1914-21; entomologist, dept. of agr., Union of S. Africa, 1921-28; entomologist, Barbados, 1928; publins. include various papers on Araneae and Acari, and economic papers on control of Acariad and insect pests.

TUDHOPE, CUTHBERT DREYDEN.—B. 1893; ed. S. Andrew's Coll., Rhodes Univ. Coll. and S.A. Coll.; active serv., 1914-19; B.Sc.(mining engnr.) Cape, 1911; asst. inspr. mines, 1929; dep. inspr., 1933; inspr., mines, 1937.
TUDOR, DUDLEY HEMING.—B. 1904; ed. Cranleigh schl.; acct., bankruptcy dept., S.S., May, 1930; acct. pub. wks. and excise dept., Penang, Jan., 1937; asst. treas., Penang, in addn., Apr., 1937.

TULL, JOHN CADMAN.—M.D. (McGill), F.R.C.P. (Lond.).—B.1884; capt., Canadian Army Med. Corps (mentd. in despa.), France, 1915-19; pathologist, Rangoon gen. hosp., 1919; med. offr., gen. hosp., Penang, Jan., 1921; pathologist, Penang, July, 1921; lect., pathology, S'pore June, 1926; govt. pathologist, S'pore, Aug., 1926; deleg. from King Edward VII Med. Coll. to Intern. cong. on cancer, London, May, 1928; deleg., leprosy confere., Manila, Dec., 1930; pathologist, Cyprus, 1936.

TUPPER, HENRY DE BEAUVIORE, A.M., R.N., Capt., R.N. TURBET, CHARLES RUPERT, B.V.SC., M.R.C.V.S.—B. 1897; served, A.I.F., 1915-19, Egypt and France; vety. offr., Fiji, 1923; senr. vety. offr., 1930; ag. dir., ag., Fiji, 1937 and provisional offr. M.L.C.

TURBETT, IVAN JOLIFFE TUFNELL, K.C.—B. 1890; ed. Trinity Coll., Dublin; barrister-at-law, King's Inn, Dublin, 1913; cadet, Fiji, 1914; ag. dist. commr., 1915; ag. crown solr., 1916; ag. ch. pol. mag., 1917; pol. mag., Gambia, 1918; ag. legal adviser, 1919-22 and in 1924; ag. judge, sup. ct. on various occasions; solr.-gen., Sierra Leone, 1926 ag. atty.-gen. in 1926, 1927 and 1928; atty.-gen., 1933.

TURGEON, WILLIAM FERDINAND ALPHONSE, K.C., B.A.—B. 1877; ed. at New York city and Laval univ., Montreal; barrister; atty.-gen. of Sask., 23rd Sept., 1907; elec. to Sask. leg., 1907; re-elec., 1908, 1912 and 1917; atty.-gen. and prov. sec., 1915; judge, ct. of appeal, Sask., 14th Mar., 1921.

TURNER, MAJ. CLARENCE ROY, A.M.I.Mech.R., Chartered Mech. Eng.—B. 1891; ed. Brompton Pub. Schl. and Schl. of Mines, Adelaide, S. Australia; Australian Imp. Forces, 1914-19; Gallipoli and France (mentd. in despa.); majr., Apr., 1917; now majr., Reg. Army R. of O.; asst. loco. supt., Gt. Southern of Spain rly., Nov., 1919 to Aug., 1921; asst. loco. supt., rly. constrn., Nigeria, Jan., 1923; asst. loco. supt., Gold Coast rly., Jan., 1925; dist. loco. supt., Jan., 1927; run. supt., Jan., 1932; ch. mech. engnr., Feb., 1937.

TURNER, ERNEST EDGAR.—B. 1890; elk. post office, Sheffield, 1894-1900; served in S. African war and in S. African constab. (medal and 5 clasps); depot sergt-major, R.N.W. mounted police, Canada, 1908-1916; coronation medal, 1911; lieut., Bahamas police and defence force, 1916; capt., 1917; major and offir. com. local forces, comdt. of police, provost marshal, and inspr. of prisons, June, 1919; ch. of pol., St. Lucia, May, 1923; ch. of pol., Grenada, Jan., 1927.

TURNER, ERNEST NELSON.—B. 1884; ed. Merchant Venturers Coll., Bristol; served in Gt. War, Egypt and Western Front, 1915-18; sub-instr., armed constab., Br. Solomon Is., 1918; offr.-in-commd., 1919; ag. dist. offr., San Cristoval, 1919; passed final exam. in law, 1921; ag. dist. offr., Nggela, 1923; dist. offr., Nggela, conj., 1934.
TURNER, HENRY.—B. 1882; ed. Oamaru South Pub. Sch., N.Z.; N.Z. pub. trust office, 1899; asst. pub. trustee, 1923; under-sec. of defence, 1930.

TURNER, JAMES POGSON.—B. 1871; clk., pub. library, St. Kitts, 1890; clk., regir.'s off., St. Kitts, 1897; comsnr. to administer oaths, St. Kitts, 1899; clk. to mag., dist. "C," St. Kitts, May to Oct., 1905; 1st clk., regir.'s off., Antigua, May, 1906; comsnr. to admst. oaths, Antigua, May, 1906; clk. regir. of jt. stock companies, Nov., 1906; dep. coroner, dists. "A" and "B," Antigua, Apl., 1916; dir. of P.O.G. fund, Jan., 1917; regir. and prov. marshal, Antigua, and chief regir., Sept., 1917; comsnr. income tax, July, 1923; M.L.C., Antigua, Mar., 1926; addnl. mag., dist. A., Antigua, Jan., 1928; M.L.C., Antigua, Mar., 1929; J.P., May, 1930; comsnr. sup. ct., Oct., 1931.

TUTE, SIR RICHARD CLIFFORD, KT. BACH. (1937).—B. 1874; ed. St. Peter's Royal Sch., York and London Univ.; barrister-at-law, Middle Temple; passed open compet. exam. for Indian civ. serv., 1898; dist. and sessions judge, Allahabad, Bareilly, Meerut and other dists., from 1907; served throughout European war, retiring in 1919 with rank of major, R.G.A.; dist. judge, Galilee dist., Palestine, 1919; pres., dist. ct., Samaritis, 1920; pres., land ct., Samaritis, 1921; pres., land ct., Jerusalem, 1925; ch. just., Bahamas, 1931; admst. govt. in Aug., 1932; author of "A Commentary on the Ottoman Land Code," and (as joint author) a metrical translation of Omar Khayyam; retd., 1939.

TWEEDSMUIR, 1st Baron (cr. 1935) Rt. Hon. JOHN BUCHAN, P.C. (1937), C.H. (1932), M.A. (Oxon.), hon. D.C.L. (Oxon.), hon. LL.D. (Glasgow and St. Andrews), J.P. (Oxfordshire), D.L., J.P. (Peebleshire).—B. 1878; ed. Glasgow Univ. and Brasenose Coll., Oxford (schol., 1895, M.A., hon. fellow); Stanhope historical essay, 1897; Newdigate prize, 1898; 1st cl. lit. hum., 1899; pres., Union, 1899; barrister-at-law, Middle Temple, 1901; pvt. sec. to Lord Milner, 1901-03; on headqrs. staff Br. Army in France, 1916-17 (temp. lieut.-col., offr. of the Crown of Belgium and the Crown of Italy); dir. infrn., 1917-18; M.P., Scottish Univa., 1927-36; lord high comsnr., church of Scotland, 1933 and 1934; gov.-gen. of the Dominion of Canada, 2nd Nov., 1936; author of many novels and of historical and other works.

TYLER-SMITH, H. A., A.M.I.C.E., A.M.I.M.E.—B. 1887; B.Sc. (Eng.) with 1st cls. hons., civil engrg., Liverpool Univ., 1909; 2nd lieut., R.E. (ap. res.) 1910; asst. engrg., P.W.D., Kenya, 1911; exec. engrg., 1917; capt., R.E. (S.R.), 1917; D.P.W., Gambia, Dec., 1920; asst. D.P.W., Nigeria, 1930; dir., wks. and transport, Trinidad, May, 1934; mem. leg. coun.

TYNDALE-BISCOE, CECIL JULIAN, M.C.—B. 1895; ed. Glenalmond, Trent and Jesus Coll., Camb.; war ser., R.A. 1914-18 (France and Palestine); M.C., 1916; instr. Imp. Sch. of Gunnery, 1918; polit. offr. Nazareth; passed Indian civ. serv. exam., 1919; supt., educn., Tanganyika, 1924; ag. dep. dir., educn., 1932; ag. dir., do., May-Nov., 1932 and Feb.-Sept., 1935; dir., native educn., N. Rhodesia, 1936.

TYNDALL, ARTHUR, A.M.Inst.C.E., M.Am. Soc.C.E., M.N.Z.Soc.C.E., solr., sup. ct., N.Z.—B. 1891; ed. Blue Spur Schi., Lawrence dist. high schl., Otago univ. and Massachusetts inst. of technology; joined N.Z. pub. wks. dept., 1909; asst. engnr., 1913; engnr.-in-ch., pub. wks. dept., W. Samoa, 1920; highways engnr., N.Z., 1924; under-sec., mines, 1934, also dir., housing constrn., 1936.

TYRELL, SIR FRANCIS GRAHAM, K.B.E. (1934), C.M.G. (1928).—B. 1876; ed. Pembroke Coll., Oxford (B.A.); cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., Oct., 1900; ag. priv. sec. to H.E. the governor, Aug., 1901; priv. sec. to H.E. the offr. admstg. the govt., Apr., 1902; off. asst. to govt. agt., Cent. prov., Jan., 1903-Dec., 1904; seconded asupt. of pol., N.W. prov., Aug., 1905, cent. prov., Mar., 1907; dist. judge, Badulla, Dec., 1908; asst. govt. agt., Matale, Feb., 1909; dep. collr. of customs, Nov., 1911; on mil. duty, Jan., 1915; govt. agt., N.W. Prov., May, 1921; prin. collr., cust., Apr., 1926; mem., exec. and leg. couns.; contr., rev., June, 1927; dep. ch. sec., July, 1931; ch. sec., May, 1932; O.A.G., Sept.-Dec., 1933 and June-Nov., 1935; ret., 1937.

TYRWHITT-DRAKE, ROBERT HAMILTON.—B. 1901; ed. St. Andrews Coll., Grahamstown, S. Africa, Magdalen Coll., Oxford and Rhodes Univ. Coll. Grahamstown; computer, survey dept. Kenya, 1927; dist. survr., Kenya, 1930; dir. land survey and registr., Zanzibar, 1936.

TYSOE, CAPT. WILLIAM, D.S.O. (1918), O.B.E. (1936), M.C. (1918), French Croix de Guerre (1918), (ment. in desps., 1918), Warserv., 1914-18, France; North Russia, 1919; B.S.A. pol., 1920; N. Rhodesia pol., and N. Rhodesia Regt., Dec., 1920; lab. offr., July, 1938.

UPSDELL, GEORGE EDGAR SKYNNER, M.A. (Cantab.). H.C.F., C.I.E.E.—B. 1886; asst. mast., educn. dept., Hong Kong, 1919; headmr., Indian Schl., 1931; Yaumati Schl., 1932; Cent. Br. Schl., 1935; av. headmast., King's Coll., 1937; senr. mast., 1938.
UPTON, THOMAS HAYNES, O.B.E., M.Sc. M.C.E., M.Inst.C.E.—B. 1889; pres. Metropolitan water, sewarge and drainage bd., N.S.W., 1930.
URICH, LEO GOTTFRIED WILFRED, M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.), D.C.M. & H.—B. 1899; med. supt., Chacachacare leper settlmt., Trinidad, Jan., 1933; M.O.H., 1937.
URQUHART, WILLIAM, B.Sc. (Edin.), A.M.I.C.E.—B. 1901; asst. engnr. K.U.R. & H., 1926; snr. asst. engnr., 1935.
USHER, CAPT. C. G., M.C.—Asst. dist. comsnr., Kenya, May, 1920; dist. offr., 1923; ag. sec., secretariat, 1927 and 1931.

USHER, WALTER WHITBRAD.—B. 1886; ed. at Marist Bros. Coll., Uitenhage; served Anglo-Boer War, 1901, S.W. Africa, 1914-15, Flanders, 1916-18; clk., Swaziland, 1910; regir. and master, ap. et. of Swaziland, and Sheriff 1916; dep. asst. comsnr., 1919.
UTTLEY, DENYS PLURAT, B.A.—B.1906; ed. St. John's, Leatherhead and Christ's Coll., Cambridge; col. audit dept., May, 1930; ast. audr., Tanganyika Territory, Aug., 1930; audr., Br. Honduras, 1938.

UTTLEY, KENNETH HARRISON, M.A., M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P., M.D., B.Ch. (Camb.), D.T.M. & H. (Lond.).—B. 1901; med. offr., Hong Kong; ast. med. offr., Victoria Hosp., 1929; med. offr. in ch., Kowloon and New Territories, Lai Chi Kok Prison, Kowloon mort.; ast. med. offr., Kowloon Hosp., in ch. offrs.' families, Kowloon, and pol. surg., Kowloon, 1930; med. offr., ditto, 1933; M.O. in ch., govt. civ., mental and Tsan Yuk hosps., 1936; M.O. in ch., Kowloon hosp., June, 1937.

VAITHIANATHAN, KANTHIHA, B.Sc. (Lond.)—B. 1896; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., June, 1923; attd., Colombo kach., July, 1923; ditto Ratnapura kach., May, 1924; ag. office asst. ditto, Sept., 1924; extra office asst., ditto, Apr., 1925; pol. mag., Dandagamuwa, Sept., 1925; ditto, Avissawella, February, 1926; office asst., Galle kach., Nov., 1926; addnl. ast. govt. agt., Hambantota, Dec., 1928; office asst., Colombo kach., Mar., 1929; addnl. ast. govt. agt., Colombo, Nov., 1929; ast. col. sec., Mar., 1930; sec. to min. for home affrs., July, 1931; sec. to min. for commun. and wks., Oct., 1936; ast. govt. agt., Colombo, Aug., 1937.

VALENTINE, DOUGLAS JAMES, M.C., M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.), M.B., B.S. (Lond.), D.P.H. (Lond.), D.T.M. and H. (Eng.).—B. 1892; ed. Palmer's Schl. and Lond. Hosp.; temp. comen., K.O.S.B., 1914-16; R.A.M.C., 1917-19; med. offr., med. dept., Hong Kong, 1919; in ch., civ. hosp., 1925-28; in ch., Kowloon hosp., 1929; in ch., Victoria hosp., 1930; ag. D.D.M.S.S., 1931, 1932, 1935 and 1936; M.O. in ch., civ. and ment. hosp., Jan., 1934; ag. D.D.M.S., Feb., 1936; D.D.M.S., Oct., 1936; ag. D.M.S. and M.L.C., June, 1937-Feb., 1938.

VALLADARES, ERIC.—B. 1908; ed. Dan. Stewart's Coll., Edinburgh; olk., transp. dept., Br. Guiana, 1924; cler. ast., med. dept., 1926; prob. offr. of cust., 1927; 6th cls. clerk., col. sec.'s office, 1932; 5th do., 1933; cl., leg. coun., May, 1936.

VAN COPPENHAGEN, FREDERICK PASCHOUDE.—B.1883; 3rd grade elk., deeds, Capetown, 1904; 2nd grade elk., 1912; 1st grade elk., 1918; 2nd grade examr., 1919; prin. elk., deeds, Pretoria, 1933; regar., deeds, Kingwilliamstown, 1935; ast. regar., deeds, Capetown, 1937; regar., deeds, Bloemfontein, 1938.

VAN BROEKHUIZEN, DR. H. D.—B. 1872; ed. Ficksburg, Senekal, Grey Coll., Bloemfontein, Victoria Coll., Stellenbosch, Edinburgh, Utrecht and Leyden; D.D. Leyden; min., Dutch reformed ch., Pretoria, before and after Anglo-Boer War; M.P., Pretoria, 1924-33; mem., S.A. acad.; historical soc., Leyden and Utrecht; min. plenip., for Union S. Africa, The Hague, Aug., 1933.

VAN DER BYL, PAUL ANDRIEUS.—B.1888; ed. Boys' High Schl., Wynberg, and Boys' High Schl., Stellenbosch; grad. at Victoria Coll., Stellenbosch (now the Univ.) in 1909 with hons. in botany; M.Sc., 1913; D.Sc., 1915; dept. of agr., Union of S. Africa, 1911; in ch., Natal herbarium and plant pathological laboratories, Durban, Natal, 1916; prof., plant pathology Univ. of Stellenbosch, 1921; prin., Stellenbosch-Elsenburg Coll. of Agr., 1928; author of papers on S. African fungi and plant diseases.

VAN DER LINGEN, PIETER MOLL, B.A.—B. 1879; ed. Kroonstad and Stellenbosch; headmr., Jeppie educn. institn., Johannesburg, 1903; do., Middelburg secondary schl., 1908; inspr., educn., Transvaal, 1913; pub. serv. comsnr., 1934.
VAN DER MERWE, HERMANUS ZULCH, B.A.—B. 1885; ed. at Ceres High Schl. and Victoria Coll., Stellenbosch; inspr., schla., 1912; sec., educn. dept., Cape Prov., 1934.

VAN DER SPUY, LT.-COL. ADRIAN JOHANNES, M.B., Ch.B. (Glasgow), D.P.H. (Capetown).—B. 1889; ed. Boys' High Schl. and Victoria Coll., Stellenbosch, Glasgow Univ. and Capetown Univ.; H.S. and H.P., Western Infmy., Glasgow, 1914-16; capt., S.A.M.C. (Overseas), 1916-20; served in France and Belgium, 1918; Br. War and Victory medals; O.C. mil. hosp., Wynberg, as capt. (temp. maj.), S.A.M.C. (A.C.F.), 1920; maj., S.A.M.C., (Permit Force), 1923; defence headqrs. (med. services sect.), 1926; O.C. mil. hosp., Roberts' Heights and senr. med. offr., Roberts' Heights, 1926; transf'd. to dept. of public health as ast. health offr. for Union, 1927; lt.-col., R. of O. (med. br.) U.D.F., 1927; dep. dir., med. services, U.D.F., attd. to Staff Corps from R. of O. (in addn. to civ. duties as ast. health offr. for Union), 1927.

VAN DER SPUY, MICHIEL LOUW.—2nd gr. compr., Natal, Feb., 1913; ch. draughtsman and compr., Apr., 1914; prof. ast., Apr., 1917; survr.-gen., Natal, Sept., 1931; do., Pretoria, May, 1934; do., Capetown, Dec., 1937.

VAN LANGENBERG, WILLIAM JAMES ALBERT, B.Sc. (Lond.), A.C.G.I.—B. 1906; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Dec., 1929; attd., Ratnapura kach., Jan., 1930; attd., Auradhapura kach., May, 1930; ag. office ast., Nuwara Eliya kach., Oct., 1931; adm. sec. to dir., med. and san. services, and sec. to min. for health, Feb., 1936; ast. govt. ast., Batticaloa, June, 1937.

VAN NIEKERK, CHRISTIAAN ANDRIES.—B. 1874; ed. pub. schl., Wellington and Victoria Coll., Stellenbosch; commdt. for Kroonstad in Anglo-Boer War (wounded); del., peace confce., Vereeniging, 1902; mem., compensation comm., till 1903; mem., leg. assem., O.F.S., 1907; leg. assem., Union, 1910-24; mem., pensions grievances comm., chmn., sp. pensions bd., 1925; mem., Union senate since 1924; pres., 1930.

VAN RYNEVELD, MAJOR-GEN. SR. PITRE HELPEBUS ANDRAS, K.B.E.(1920), D.S.O. (1918), M.C. (1916), B.A., B.Sc.—B.1891; ed. Grey Coll. Schl., Grey Univ. Coll., Bloemfontein, Imp. Coll., Univ. of London; 2nd lieut., 7th Loyal N. Lance Regt., Aug., 1914; lieut., R.F.C., Apr., 1915; detachment C.O., Senussi campaign, Egypt, 1915-16; flight comdr., Palestine, 1916; ditto, Salonika, 1916; squad. comdr., London night defences, 1916-17; squad. comdr., No. 17 squad., B.E.F., 1917; severely wounded; wing comdr., XI. (Army) Wing, 2nd Army, 1917-19; ditto, Army wing, Rhine army of occupation, 1919; ment. in despr. six times, "1914-15" Star, Brit. War med., Victory med., Order of the Nile 4th cls., Croix de Chevalier, Legion d'Honneur, France, Croix d'Officier, Ordre Leopold avec Croix de Guerre, Belgium; S. African liaison offr., Ar Miny., 1919-21; lieut.-col. (temp. col.), Permanent Force Union Defence Forces, Feb., 1920; director of air services, U.D.F., Feb., 1920; col., S.A. staff corps, Nov., 1929; O.C. troops, Roberts Heights and commdt., S.A. Milv. Coll. in addn. to duties as dir., air services, Dec., 1929; bre. gen. and apptd., ch., gen. staff, May, 1933; maj.-gen., Apr., 1937.

VAN SCHALKWIJK, JOHANNES ZACHARIAS, B.A.—B. 1883; ed. Bloemfontein and Stellenbosch; astt. Bethlehem; vice-prin., Ficksburg, 1906; do., Normal Practising Schi., Bloemfontein, 1910; prin., model schi., Bloemfontein and vice-rect., Normal Coll., 1913; inspr. of schls., O.F.S., Nov., 1917.

VAN SCHALKWIJK, L. M. A. N., B.A. (Cape), Ph.D. (Amst.).—B. 1888; ed. Normal and S. African Coll., Cape Town, also in Holland, Germany and Austria inspr. of institutns., Union ed. dept., 1921; organising inspr., Union dept. of educn. since 1922; organiser, child welfare and spec. ed., 1925; supt., welfare and probation servs., 1937.
VAN STON, JAMES JOHN, M.I.Mar.E.—B. 1894; ed. Diocesan Boys' Schi., Hong Kong; survr., ships, S.S., May, 1923; do., S'pore, May, 1927; do., Penang, Feb., 1932; do., S'pore, Oct., 1935.
VAN STON, JAMES JOHN, M.R. Mar.E.—B. 1896; ed. Robert Gordon's Coll., Aberdeen; war surv., 1914-19; survr., ships, S.S., Jan., 1929.
VAN ZEYLEN, FREDERICK CORNELIUS.—B. 1885; mech. and elec. engnr., draughtsman, harbr. engnr.'s office, Nassau, Bahamas, 1923; astt. civ. engnr., 1925; dep. civ. engnr. 1925; D.P.W., May, 1928.

VAN ZIJL, HENDRIK STEFANUS, LL.B. (Camb.), B.A., LL.B. (Cape).—B. 1876; ed. Victoria Coll., Stellenbosch, and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; advoc., sup. ct., Cape Town, 1903; M.P., Cape of Good Hope, 1905-10; parly. draftsman, Union of S. Africa, 1910-15; K.C., 1919; judge, sup. ct., Union of S. Africa, 1920; chmn., delimitation comm., July, 1927 and Mar., 1932; judge-pres., Cape prov. divn., 1935; chmn., S.W. Africa constitution comm., 1935-36.

VAN ZYL, GIDEON BRAND.—B. 1873; ed. Normal and South African Coll., Capetown; atty.-at-law and notary pub., 1898; conveyancer, 1897; astt. dir., war recruiting, 1918; dep.-chmn., comtes., Union of S.A. H. of A., Jan., 1934.

VAN ZYL, JACOB ERNEST SIBERT.—B. 1885; prob. clk., R.M., Hanover, 1904; clk., deeds, Vryburg, 1904; clk., R.M., Calvina, 1905; clk., master's off., C.T., 1906; 2nd gr. clk., Union educn. dept., 1910; 1st gr. clk., do., 1914; senr. elk., do., 1917; 2nd gr. prin. elk., do., 1920; 1st gr. prin. elk., 1921; 2nd gr. ch. elk., 1925; 1st gr. ch. elk., 1928; pub. serv. inspr., 1931; under sec., educn., July, 1934.

VAN ZYL, JACOBUS PETRUS, D.Phil., M.A.—B. 1890; teachers' dipl., 1910, Victoria Coll., Stellenbosch; vice-prin., secondary schl., 1911-12; resch. astt. to 1918; agrcl. chem., Univ. Göttingen; lect. in chem., Glen schl. of agr., 1919; resch. offr., Ondersteepoort, 1920; resch. chem., div. of chem., Pretoria, 1927; astt.ch., div. chem. services, dept., agr. and forestry, 1934; ch., 1936.

VAN ZYL, L. J., B.A.—B. 1887; ed. Boys' High Schi., Wellington, and Victoria Coll., Stellenbosch; head, High Schls., Vredefort, Smithfield, Ladybrand, 1910-21; inspr., educn., 1921-35; sec., educn., O.F.S., 1935.

VASKESS, HENRY HARRISON, O.B.E.(1938).—B. 1891; clk., Western Pacific high comm., Apr., 1911; on mil'y. serv., June, 1915; clk. in ch., accnts., W. Pacific, Sept., 1919; ch. elk. and acctnt., Apr., 1920; ag. sec. to W. Pacific high comm., for short periods in 1920, 1922, 1925 and 1927-29; sec., Western Pacific high comm., Dec., 1929.

VASSALLO, EDWIN P., L. S. & A.—B. 1891; ed. Flore's Coll. and Malta Univ.; grad. as land survr. and architect, 1914; elec. for 7th divn. in leg. assm., Oct., 1921; re-ele., June, 1924 and Aug., 1927; min. for pub. wks., 16th Aug., 1927; edr. "The Malta Times and Progress" until Aug., 1927.

VASSALLO, S., F.R.C.S. (Edin.).—Ed. Malta Lyceum and Malta Univ.; M.D., 1913; F.R.C.S. (Edin.), 1928; govt. exhibt. for science, 1906-1907; ditto for medicine, 1909-1913; post-graduate work at Univ. Coll. Hosp. and London Hosp.; clinical astt., St. John's Hosp., W., and Royal Ear Hosp.; supernumerary med. offr., Leeward Is., 1914; D.T.M. and H. (Lond.), certif., L.S.T.M. (Duncan med.) ; temp. comm., R.A.M.C. (ment. in desps.); med. offr., Uganda, 1919; res. surg. offr., Zanzibar Prot., 1925; senr. specialist offr., 1935; 4th cls. Order of Brilliant Star of Zanzibar, 1931; 3rd cl. do. 1938.

VAUGHAN, JOHN HENRY, M.C.—B. 1892; ed. Eastbourne Coll. and Corpus Christi Coll., Cambridge, B.A. (hist. tripods), 1914; served with H.M. Forces, 1914-21; M.C., Arras, 1917; astt. dist. comm., Zanzibar, 1921; priv. sec. to Br. res. and clk. of the coun., July, 1924; ag. regis., high off., 1927; called to bar, Inner Temple, 1929; res. mag., July, 1929; ag. judge in 1931, 1932 and 1933; astt. atty.gen., 1933 and 1934; ag. atty-gen. in 1934 and 1935; commr. for revision of laws, 1934; solr.-gen., Tanganyika Territory, 1936; ag. atty-gen., Nov., 1936 to Oct., 1937; author of "The Dual Jurisdiction in Zanzibar" (1935).

VEAL, CYRIL JOHN DOUGLAS.—B. 1893; ed. S.A. Coll. Schi. and S.A. Coll., Cape Town, war serv. with Union forces in W. Africa (1914) and Imp. forces in E. Africa (1915-18); Union Defence Force, 1912-26, and on R. of O. with rank of capt.; joined mines dept., 1925, as astt. inspr.; dep. inspr., 1929; inspr., 1935.
VERASA WMY, JOHN ALOYSIUS.—B. 1891; barrister-at-law (Middle Temple), 1913; ag. stip. mag., Br. Guiana, 1922; stip. mag., 1928.

VENABLES, OSWALD ERIC, B.A. (Dublin).—B. 1891; ed. Campbell Coll. and Trinity Coll., Dublin; cadet, S.S., Nov., 1914; on active ser., June, 1917; offr., cls. V, Nov., 1917; offr., cls. IV, Nov., 1921; 2nd mag., S'pore, 1923; 2nd astt. sec. (A), col. secret., S'pore, 1924; Br. res., Brunei, 1926; offr., cls. III, Nov., 1926; mag., Seremban, 1927; dist. offr., Upper Perak, 1929; collr., land rev., K. Lumpur, 1931; offr., cls. II, Nov., 1931; Br. advr., Perlis, July 1932; dist. offr., K. Kangar, May, 1936; offr., cls. Ia., Jan., 1937; ag. under sec. to govt., F.M.S., Aug., 1938.

VENNING, GUY, M.B.E. (1929).—B. 1879; apptd., after compet. exam., astt. clk., and assigned to local govt. bd., May, 1899; after further compet. exam., apptd. clk. of 2nd div. of the civ. ser., and assigned to W.O., 8th Jan., 1900; transf. to C.O., 16th Apr., 1903; conf. clk., 1st Apr., 1919; staff offr., 9th Jan., 1928.

VENOUR, LOUIS STEPHEN DOUGLAS HAMILTON.—B.1885; ed. pvty.; capt., 5th batt., Middx. Regt.; astt. commr., pol., Gold Coast, Aug., 1914; ag. staff instr., in 1916 and 1919; commr., pol., Jan., 1917; King's Pol. Med., 1922; ag. commr., pol. C.I.D., June to Nov., 1928; senr. commr., pol., 1929; ag. inspr.gen., pol., on various occasions, 1925-33.

VERITY, JOHN.—B. 1892; barrister-at-law, copyist, treasy. and cust., Br. Honduras, 1908; 3rd cls. clk., registr. gen.'s office, 1910; 2nd cls. clk., atty. gen's office, 1912; resign., 1913; called to bar. Middle Temple, 1918; dep. clk., cts., Jamaica, 1918; res. mag., 1925; clk., cts., 1920; ag. judge, Kingston ct., 1927 and 1934; chmn., commr. of enquiry, lands dept., 1936; ag. judge, sup. ct., Turks and Caicos Is., 1936; ag. judge, high ct., Jamaica, 1936; 2nd puisne judge, Br. Guiana, 1936; ag. ch. just., Nov., Dec., 1936; 1st puisne judge, 1937; ag. atty.-gen., July 1937; ag. ch. just., May-Sept., 1938.
VERNALL, RICHARD JOHN, A.R.I.B.A., M.I.Struct.E.—B. 1901; ed. Marling Sch., Strand, archt., P.W.D., Hong Kong, 1925; ag. tech. sec., P.W.D., 1934; Lieut., H.K.N.V.F.
VERRALL, ROBERT JOHN WYNDHAM.—B. 1896; ed. Warninglidg and Cologne Coll.; B.S.A. pol., 1920; N.R. pol., 1928; ast. supt., 1929; supt., 1931; dep. commr., 1937.

VIADER, RENE, M.B.E. (1930).—B. 1879; ed. R. Coll., Mauritius; ent. civ. serv., Mauritius, Nov. 1899; registr., col. sec's office, May, 1919; finan. ast., P.W.D., Mar., 1923; ch. clk., col. sec's office, Apr., 1925; 2nd ast. col. sec., Jan., 1926; ag. ast. col. sec., 1925-30 and Apr., 1932.

VICKERS, WILLIAM JOHN, M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.), D.P.H. (Camb.), D.T.M. & H. (Camb.), Certif. L.S. of H. and T.M.—B. 1898; ed. King Edward's Gram. Schl. and Birmingham Med. Sch.; Gt. War, Br. Expdy., Feb., 1916-19; res. staff, gen. hosp., B'gham, 1923-25; med. offr., dist. hosp., K. Lumpur, Sept., 1925; health offr., Selangor E., May, 1926; on cholera duty in Perak, Sept. 1927; health offr., K. Lumpur, June, 1928; ag. ar. health offr., Kedah, Dec., 1934 and Feb., 1935; enr. med. offr., dept. of health, Palestine, 1938.

VICKERS-HAVILAND, L. A. W.—B. 1895; lieut., R. Fusiliers, 1914-18; seconded, 2nd K.A.R., 1918; ast. polit. offr., 1920; admstv. offr., 2nd grade, 1921; ast. dist. offr., 1926; astd., secretariat, Tanganyika Territory, 1930; dist. offr., 1931; seconded as ast. to dir., agr., 1932-33.

VIGNE, CHIDLOW.—B. 1900; M.Sc. (Yale), B.A. (Oxon.), dip. for (Oxon.) Worcester Coll.; 2nd lieut., R.A.F., 1918; ast. consvr., forests, Gold Coast, Mar., 1922; commonwealth fund fellow, 1930-32, Yale Univ.; sviliculturist, Gold Coast, Oct., 1933; sr. ast. consvr., forests, July, 1937.

VILJOEN, PHILIPPUS RUDOLPH, M.C., M.R.C.V.S., Dr. Med. Vet. (Berne).—B. 1889; ent. dept. of agr. as ast. vety. bacteriologist, Onderstepoort, Pretoria, 1913; vety. research offr., Onderstepoort and Vryburg, 1914; seconded to dept. of defence, Oct., 1914; retd. to dept. of agr., June, 1915; resigned, Jan., 1919; reapptd. dept. of agr. as senr. research offr., Onderstepoort, 1920; sub-dir., vety. research and prof. in vety. medicine, Transvaal Univ. Coll., Apr., 1922; dep. dir., vety. services, 1927; under-seo., agr., Jan., 1931; sec., agr., May, 1933; chmn., national marketing coun., 1937.
VINCENT, STEWART LKE.—B. 1902; ast. supt., pol., Kenya, Nov., 1926; supt., Feb., 1936.
VINCENT, WILLIAM HENRY, L.M.S.S.A., (Lond.)—B. 1886; ed. Colet Court, St. Paul's Sch., St. Mary's Hosp., and London Univ.; dist. med. offr., Fiji, 1915.

VISCHER, HANNS, C.M.G. (1934), C.B.E. (1918)—B. 1876; ed Switzerland and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; ast. res., N. Nigeria, 1903; res., 1905; dir., educn., 1908-18; lieut., Gen. List, 1915; capt., 1915; major, 1916; Étoile Noire, 1917; Légion d'Honneur, 1918; Crown of Italy, 1918; Crown of Belgium, 1918; demob., 1919 with rank of major in G.R.; sec. S. of S.'s advisory council, c. in Trop. Africa, Nov., 1923.
VISSER, DANIEL HENDRIK.—Entered Cape pub. serv., 23rd Mag. mag. and ag. civil commr., 1903; Cape House of Assembly, 5th Asst., House of Assem., Union Nov., 1910; clk., House of Assem., 1920.
VISSER, DR. THOMAS CHRISTIAN.—Union assem., to 1929; mem., Union chmn. of comtees, 1930.

VLIELAND, CHARLES ARCHIBALD.—1890; ed. Exeter Sch. and Balliol (schol.); lat. clc. hon., nat. sci., F.M.S., 1914; ast. sec. to res., Sel 3rd ast. sec., fedl. secretariat, 1919 offr., 1920-21; sec. to res., N. Semi pvt. sec. to O.A.G., June, 1925; attd. office, S'pore, Nov., 1925; offr., cls., 1926; ag. dist. offr., B. Padang, Jul., Sept., 1928; dist. offr., K. Selangor, 1931; census, Malaya, Mar., 1932 II, Dec., 1931; economy offr., S.S. ad Aug., 1932; ag. under sec., S.S., Feb., cls. Ib., Oct., 1934; finan. commr. a gen., Johore, July, 1935; food contr., July, 1938.

VOIGT, FREDERICK CARL MARTIN LL.B.—B. 1879; clk., dept. of just., 1902; sec. to min. of nat. affr., 1910; senr. affrs. dept., Pretoria, 1912; dept. of just., 1913; ast. mag. Krugersdorp, 1916; prov. atty. gen's. office, Bloemfontein, 1921; Cape Town, 1926; pub. serv. inspr., 1929; prov. sec., Cape adminstr., 1930; prov. local govt. inspr. and contr., educn., Mar. 1933.
VOLLET, DENYS HENRI RENE.—B. served in Great War; med. offr., Basutoland.

VON BONDE, CECIL, Ph.D., M.A., F.R.S.—Ed. Univ. of Capetown; senr. lect. Zoology, Univ. of Capetown, 1918; ast. "Purcell Memorial Prize" for Zoology in ag. prof., Zoology, Univ. of Capetown, 1925; fisheries survey and govt. marine biologist, S. Africa, 1928; seconded for marine survey to confere. of E. African govs. in Zanzibar, 1928.

VOSS, GEORGE DU TOIT, K.C., B.A.—Advocate of the Sup. Ct., of S. Africa.—B. asst. regisr., sup. ct. of S. Africa (Transvaal divn.), 1918; regisr., high ct. of S.W. A. 1919; atty.-gen., S.W. Africa, 1925; sol. Eastern dists., Cape Prov., 1931; law adv. dept. of just., Nov., 1931.

WACHER, LAWRENCE FORD, M.B.E. (1918) B. 1884; ed. Waldon House Sch., Herne Kent Coast Coll. and Agric. Coll., Wye; m staff, Wye Coll., 1910; agr. offr., Basutoland 1911; war serv., 1915-19; 2nd lieut., H.C.B. 1915; lieut., 1916; ag. capt. and adjt. 2nd Div., 1918; returned to Basutoland, 1920.
WADDELL, CECIL JAMES, B.SC., A.M.I.C.E.—B. 1896; ed. Allan Glen's Sch., Glasgow Univ.; war ser., 1915-18; eng. P.W.D., Hong Kong, 1925; seconded, Kowk Canton rlv., 1931-32.
WADDINGTON, HARRY, B.Sc.—B. 1896; mily. serv., 1915-18; ast. dist. offr., Nig. 1920; barrister-at-law (Middle Temple); dist. of 1928; ast. judge, prot., etc., 1934.

WADDINGTON, E.J., C.M.G. (1935), O.B. (1919).—Ast. dist. commr., E.A.P., Dec., 19 ag. prov. comsnr., Jubaland, Nov., 1919; senr. amt. sec., secretariat, Kenya, Feb. to Sept., 1923; clk. of couns., Sept., 1923; asst. sec., gov't. confce., 1926; res. comsnr., Mombasa, 1928; col. sec., Bermuda, 1932; do.; Br. Guiana, 1935; gov. and commdr.-in-ch., Barbados, 1938.

WADE, Sir A. de V., Kt. Bach (1937), C.M.G. (1935), O.B.E. (1931).—Ed. Lancing Coll., exhibtbr. and Keble Coll., Oxford; scholar; M.A.; asst. dist. comsnr., E. Africa Prot., March, 1912; ag. dep. ch. native comsnr., Kenya, 1926; prin. asst., native affrs. dept., Jan., 1928; ch. native comsnr., 1932; ag. col. sec., 1933; col. sec., 1934; ag. gov't., 1935 and 1936.

WADE, Capt. T. G. Rochfort, M.A., F.R.G.S.—B. 1901; ed. King William's Coll., Isle of Man, and St. Columba's Coll., Dublin; B.A., Trinity Coll., Dublin, 1926; M.A., 1930; inspr., schls., St. Lucia, May, 1931; C.O., St. Lucia Volunteer Forces; headmast., Grammar Schl., St. Vincent, 1938.

WADIA, Siavak Hirji, B.A. (Bombay), B.A. (Cantab.),—B. 1890; cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., May, 1915; attd., Jaffna Kach., June, 1915; addtl. pol. mag., Jaffna, in addn., Oct., 1915; finan. asst. to chrmn., munic coun., Colombo, Jan., 1918; office asst. to gov't. agt., Prov. of Sabaragamuwa, Mar., 1920; pol. mag., Avissawella, June, 1920; asst. contr., rev., Apr., 1922; addnl. asst. contr., rev., Aug., 1925; asst. comsnr., stamps, Jan., 1926; asst. contr., rev., Nov., 1926; dist. judge, Kurunegala, Sept., 1931; asst. gov't. agt. Matara, Dec., 1932; offr., cls. I, grade II, Mar., 1936; excise comsnr., Mar., 1938.
WADLEY, Walter Joseph Durham, M.A.—B. 1903; ed. City of London Schl.; class schol., Lincoln Coll., Oxford; inspr., schls., Gold Coast, Sept., 1926; senr. inspr., schls., July, 1937.

WAIRWRIGHT, William Frederick, O.B.E.—B. 1881; Indian pol., 1899; accompanied Sir Charles Yate on polit. mission to Makran, S. Persia; transf. to Bengal pol., 1901 on sp.; famine opera., 1906-08; serv. in Gt. War, Egypt and Palestine, 1916-20; major, Territorial Reserve (Yeomanry); dep. dist. comdt. of pol. Palestine, 1920; dist. supt., pol., Palestine, 1926.

WAKEFIELD, Arthur John, B.Sc., N.D.A., N.D.—B. 1900; ed. Brewood Grammar Schl., Harper Adams Agrl. Coll., Reading and Edinburgh Univ.; 2nd lieut. Shropshire Light Infy., 1918; vety. dept., N. Rhodesia, 1923; dist. agrl. offr., Tanganyika Territory, 1924; senr. agrl. offr., 1930; ag. dir., agr., in 1933 and 1935-6; dep. dir., agr., 1935; dir., agr., 1938.

WAKEFIELD, Brig.-Gen. Hubert Stephen, O.B.E. (Mil.)—1919—B. 1883; ed. privte. schl. and Ellesmere Coll.; joined Cape Mtd. Riflemen, 1901; S.A. war, 1899-02; (Queen's med., 3 clasps); lcn., C.M.R., 1912; lieut., 1st regt., S.A.M.R., 1913; opera. in G.S.W. Africa, 1914-15; adjt., 1st Regt., S.A.M.R., 1915-16; capt., 1st S.A.Infy. (Overseas), 1917; opera. France and Belgium, 1917-18; capt., 1st Res. Batt., S.A. Infy. and seconded for sp. employment, 1918; D.A.Q.M.G. and temp. major, 1918; opera., Persia, 1918-19; ment. in desps., '1914-15' Star, Br. War and Victory med.; on staff of O.C., Union garrison troops, Cape Peninsula, with temp. rank of capt., 1921; D.A.A.G. (later staff offr.) defence hdqrs., Pretoria, 1921; capt., 1922; posted S.A. Staff Corps, 1923; maj., do., 1929; O.C., No. 1 mil. dist., Cape Town, with temp. rank of lt.-col., 1931; lt.-col., S.A. Staff Corps, 1931; O.C., No. 4 mil. dist., Johannesburg, 1932; adjt.-gen., U.D.F., 1934; temp. col., 1934; col., 1935; brig.-gen., Sept., 1937; dep. ch., gen. staff, retaining appt. of adjt.-gen., Sept., 1937.

WAKEFIELD, Thomas Cleave, M.B., Ch.B. (Edin.), L.M., Rotunda Hosp., Dublin, grad., London S.T.M. (with distinc.).—B. 1893; med. offr., F.M.S., Aug., 1926; med. offr., European hosp., K. Lumpur, Feb., 1929; med. offr. in ch., Tan Took Seng Hosp., S'pore, Oct., 1935; med. offr., Batu Gajah, Feb., 1936.

WAKEHURST, 2nd Baron (Cr. 1934), Capt. John de Vere Loder, K.C.M.G.—B. 1895; ed. Ktn.; 4th R. Sussex Regt. and Intell. Corps, 1914-19; served in Gallipoli, Egypt, Palestine (deeps.); olk., F.O., 1919-21; M.P., E. Leicester, 1924-29; do., Lewes, E. Sussex, 1931-36; gov. and commdr.-in-ch., New South Wales, 1937.

WALCOTT, Edward Keith, K.C. (1937)—B. 1892; ed. Harrison Coll., Barbados; war serv., 1915-18; solr., 1920; called to bar, Middle Temple, 1921; solr.-gen., Barbados, 1930; ag. att.-gen., various periods, 1930-34; also M.H.A.; atty.-gen., July, 1936.
WALCOTT, James Eyre, M.B., Ch.B., D.T.M. & H.—B. 1894; ed. Harrison Coll., Barbados and Glasgow Univ.; govt. bacteriologist and pathologist, Barbados, 1934.

WALKER, Alan Ernest, A.M.I.E. Aust., Chartered Engrn., Aust.—B. 1906; ed. Bendigo High schl. and Bendigo schl. of Mines, Victoria, Australia; tempy. asst. engrn., P.W.D., Cyprus, May, 1936; ag. divnl. engrn., various periods, 1936-38; asst. engrn., Ost., 1938.

WALKER, Geoffrey, F.C.R.A. (1913)—B. 1889; ed. Market Harborough Gram. Schl. and Lond. Univ.; apptd. after competitive exam., 2nd divn. clk., Jan., 1908; apptd. after competitive exam., examr., Exchequer & Audit dept., 1911; on mily. serv., 2nd City of Lond. Yeomanny, 1914-19; seconded, K.A.R., 1917-19; ment. in desps.; audr., E. & A.D., 1920; senr. do., 1924; dep. dir., audit, 1933; regional offr., unemploy.ment assst. bd., 1934; offr., col. acct., C.O., 1936; hon. treas., civ. serv. sports coun. since 1931.
WALKER, George Blyth, M.B., Ch.B. (Edin.), D.P.H. (Edin.), certif. of London S.T.M.—B. 1899; med. offr., Nigeria, 1922; M.O.H., 1924; senr. health offr., 1927; dep. dir., health service, 1938.
WALKER, Henry James, M.B.E.—B. 1891; home civ. serv., 1907-14; Uganda rly., 1914; office asst. to ch. engrn., 1928.

WALKER, Hubert Edmund, O.B.E. (1937), M.I.C.E.—B. 1891; asst. engrn., P.W.D., Nigeria, 1919; ag. senr. exec. engrn., 1926; suplt., native adminstr. wks., 1929; dep. D.P.W., 1935; ag. D.P.W., 1937 and 1938.
WALKER, Ivan Lawrence.—B. 1883; ch. inspr., dept. of lab., Union of S. Africa, Nov., 1924; under-sec. for lab., Sept., 1929; sec. for lab., May, 1932.
WALKER, J. W., M.B., Ch.B. (Glas.).—B. 1897; mily. serv., 1916-18; med. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Sept., 1929.

WALKER, Capt. Reginald David, M.C., A.R.C.S., D.I.C., M.Inst.C.E., M.Inst.T., Chtd. civ. engrn.—B. 1892; ed. R. Coll. Science, and City and Guilds Engng. Coll., Whitworth Exhibr., 1913; field cov., R.E., France, Salonika, Egypt, 1915-18; capt., 1917; R.E., Egypt, 1918-20; asst. engrn., F.M.S. Rlys., 1920; ag. dist. engrn., 1923; dist. engrn., grade II, 1925; mntce. asst. engrn., ways and wks., 1926 and 1932; dist. engrn., grade I, 1929; ag. sec., gen. man., F.M.S. rlys., 1932; ag. estate and survey offr., F.M.S.R., 1933; man. and ch. engrnr., Kowloon-Canton Rly., Hong Kong, 1934; maj., H.K. Vol. Defence Corps, 1935.

WALKER, REGINALD PLUMER, A.M.I.T.—B. 1891; travelling audr., F.M.S., Rly., 1914-18; ast. acct., do., 1918; dep. acct., Jan., 1926; ag. chief acct., various periods, 1926-34; ch. acct., Kenya and Uganda rlys. and harbors, 1935.
WALKER, RICHARD, A.R.C.A. (Lond.)—Ed. Royal Coll. of Art; art supt., S.S., Dec., 1923.

WALKER, ROBERT PHILIP SEBASTIAN, M.A., (Oxon.).—B. 1898; astd., Eng. Coll., Johore, Nov., 1920; astd., govt. Eng. schl., Muar, Feb., 1921; Eng. coll., Johore Bahrn, June, 1922; Raffles Inst., S'pore, Apr., 1924; Malay coll., K. Kangsar, May, 1924; govt. Eng. schl., Alor Star, Aug., 1926; King Edward VII Sch., Taiping, Sept., 1926; headmast., Eng. schl., Johore, Dec., 1930; prin., King Ed. VII schl., Taiping, Oct., 1931; senr. educn. offr., grade A, Malayan educn. serv., 1937.
WALKER, WILLIAM ALEXANDER RIDDELL.—B. 1906; ed. St. Alban's Schl. and Glasgow Univ.; cadet, Gold Coast, 1930; ast. dist. comsrr., 1931.

WALKINGSHAW, RICHARD, M.C., M.B., Ch.B. (Glasgow), D.T.M., D.T.H. (L'pool), D.O.M.S. (Lond.), F.R.C.S. (Edin.)—B. 1896; ed. Airdrie Acad.; M.O., Malaya, July, 1927; ag. surg., Penang, Nov., 1930; ag. surg., Perak, Dec., 1937.

WALL, ERNEST, M.C., A.M.I.C.E.—B. 1896; ed. Worcester Royal Grammar Schol.; R.E., 1915-19; capt.; ast. engrnr., P.W.D., Nyassaland, 1921; exec. engrnr., 1929; ag. D.P.W., May, 1931 to Feb., 1932 and 1934-35; senr. exec. engrnr., P.W.D., Nigeria, 1937.
WALL, FRANCIS JOSEPH.—B. 1900; supt., prisons, Trinidad, Nov., 1932.

WALLACE, GEORGE BRUNTON, B.Sc. (agri.), Ph.D.—B. 1899; ed. George Heriot's Schol., Edinburgh; mily. serv. R.G.A., 1918-19; B.Sc., 1922; Ph.D., 1925; senr. demonstr. in agr. and forest entomology, univ. of Edinburgh, 1922-23; ast. in plant pathology, bd. of agr. for Scotland, 1924-25; miscroscopist, game pres. dept., Tanganyika territory, 1925; plant pathologist, dept. of agr., 1927.

WALLACE, HORACE BLAKE.—Natal civ. serv., 1906; mily. serv., native rebellion, 1906; German S.W. and E. Africa, 1914-16; mag., Mobasa, 1918; add. mag., Durban, 1923; mag., Pinetown, 1923; Greytown, 1929; Ikopo, 1930; add. mag., Johannesburg, 1937.

WALLACE, JOHN LAWRENCE LESLIE, M.B., Ch.B. Edin., 1903, M.D., 1909, F.R.A.C.P., 1938.—B. 1876; ast. phys., Crichton-Dumfries, 1906-10; N.S.W. mental hosps. dept., 1910; med. supt., Callan Park, 1922; inspr.-gen., mental hosp., 1935.
WALLACE, SIR LAWRENCE AUBERT, K.B.E. (1918); C.M.G. (1910)—Acting admstr., N.E. Rhodesia, May, 1907; ag. admstr., N.W. Rhodesia, Jan., 1909; admstr., Northern Rhodesia, 1911; ret., 1921.

WALLACE, THEODORE DAVID, B.A. (Cantab.), LL.B. (Dublin), Barrister-at-Law (King's Inn).—B. 1906; ed. St. Andrew's Coll., Dublin, Gonville and Caius Coll., Cambridge; practised, S.-Western circuit, 1930-34; crown coun., Kenya, 1934; ag. solr.-gen. and King's proctor and nom. mem., leg. coun., Sept., 1936-Mar., 1937 and from Mar., 1937; ag. atty.-gen., 1938.
WALLER, GEORGE RONALD, M.B., Ch.B. (Ed.) D.P.H. (Eng.), Cert. of L.S.T. Med.—Med. offr., Nigeria, 1924; senr. health offr., 1928; A.D.M.S. (health), Sierra Leone, 1938.
WALLINGTON, HAROLD GEORGE, M.A. (Cantab.).—B. 1899; ast. mast., educn. dept., Hong Kong, Jan., 1924; headmnr., Yaumati schl., 1936.
WALLIS, KENNETH.—B. 1901; ed. Epworth Coll., Ryland Univ. Coll., Nottingham; B.Sc. (Lond.), 1923; A.I.C., 1924; ast. govt. analyst, Trinidad and Tobago, 1929; govt. analyst, Br. Guiana, 1933.

WALMSLEY, CUTHBERT HORNER, M.C., M.Inst.C.E., M.Inst.M. and Cy.E., M.Inst.W.E.—B. 1882; ed. Eccles Grammar Schol.; Sudan P.W.D., 1906; Kenya, Magadi water wks., 1913; war serv. in B.E.A., G.K.A. and P.R.A.; ment. in deeps, 1916, 1917, 1918; ent. col. serv., 1922; ast. engrnr., P.W.D., 1922; ag. contracts offr., 1924; exec. engrnr., 1925; ag. ast. dir. in 1926 and 1930-31; ag. hydraulic engrnr., 1931-32; ag. suptdg. engrnr. in 1933 and 1934; senr. exec. engrnr., 1937; ag. D.P.W., 1937.

WALSH, DUDLEY J. C.—B. 1896; ed. Merchant Taylors Schol.; served with H.A.C. in France, 1914-15; 2nd lieut., Middlesex Regt., 1915; W. African Regt., Sierra Leone, Nigeria, 1916-17; transf., 2nd K.A.R. and served in E. Africa 1918-21; Somaliland campaign, 1920; dist. pol. offr., Somaliland, Feb., 1922; dist. offr., Jan., 1933.

WALSH, CAPT., (Hony.) EDGAR STANLEY.—B. 1893; ed Bradford Grammar schl. and Downing Coll., Cambridge; mily. serv., 1914-19, mentd. in deeps; admstive. offr. cls. IV, Nigeria, 1919; cls. III, 1927; ag. res., Apr.-June, 1936; admstive. offr., cls. II, 1937.

WALSH, PATRICK JOSEPH STANISLAUS, Ph.D., K.C.—B.1872; ed. Clermont and Paris (France), Innsbruck (Tyrol), Trinity Coll., Dublin; called to bar, King's Inns, June, 1898; K.C., Feb., 1910; Ph.D. (metaphysics and ethics, ancient languages and comparative philology, maths.); junr. and senr. crown pros., N.W. circuit and winter assizes, 1906-20; war serv., 1915-1918; ag. county st. judge for two circuits, midland counties; dep. recorder, Galway; dep. county st. judge for counties Kerry, Tipperary and West Riding of Cork; pres., Br. pensions appl. tribunal (Ireland), Mar., 1920-May, 1929; pres., dist. ots. Cyprus, 1929-31; ch. just., Seychelles, 1931; O.A.G., Seychelles, Dec., 1931-May, 1932.

WALTER, A., O.B.E. (1935), F.R.A.S.—Joined magnetic and meteorological dept., royal observatory, Greenwich, 1892; ast. director, Royal Alfred Observatory, Mauritius, Apr., 1897; statistical adviser to Mauritius Royal comrn., 1909; census comrn., Mauritius, 1911 and 1921; dir., Royal Alfred Observatory, May, 1911 to 1921; ast. col. sec., Mauritius, Nov. to Dec., 1918; food, trade and export contr., Mar., 1920 to Jan., 1922; ag. prot. of imigrts. and poor law comsrr., Jan., 1922; mem. of coun. of govt., 1922; headed deputation to India for the re-opening of emigr. to Mauritius, 21st Dec., 1922; statistician, Kenya, 1926; statistician to govs'. confere., 1926; dir., Br. E. African meteorological serv., 1928; author of "Sugar Industry of Mauritius"; Insurance of crops against cyclones; editor, Mauritius almanac.

WALTERS, ARTHUR WILLOUGHBY.—B. 1900; ed. Cordwallis Prep. Schol., Michaelhouse; secret. elk., Swaziland, 1919; dist. adminstr., Swaziland, 1920; treasy. elk., Swaziland, 1922; transf. as treasy. elk., grade I, to Basutoland, 1928; ag. finan. sec. in 1933, 1936 and 1937; ast., 1934.

WALTERS, JOHN RADLEY, B.A. (Cantab.)—B. 1888; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Nov., 1911; att. Kandy kach., Dec., 1911; att. Galle kach., May, 1912; asst. pol. mag., Negombo, Nov., 1912; office asst., Galle kach., Nov., 1913; pol. mag., Gampola, May, 1915; pol. mag., Karunegala, Dec., 1920; asst. govt. agt., Mullaitivu, Sept., 1921; dist. judge, Nuwara Eliya, July, 1923; asst. govt. agt., Kegalla, Feb., 1924; dist. judge, Matare, Oct., 1926; asst. govt. agt., Trincomalee, Mar., 1927; ag. govt. agt., Badulla, Apr., 1928; asst. govt. agt., Trincomalee, June, 1929; ag. govt. agt., N.W.P., Apr., 1931; do. E.P., Nov., 1932; addnl. P.M.G., July, 1933; P.M.G., Sept., 1933; offr., cls. I, gle. I, 1938.

WALTON, BRYAN STAGG.—B.A. (Oxon.); cadet, S.S. Nov., 1913; ag. asst. dist. judge and 4th mag., S'pore, Mar., 1914; asst. censor, Nov., 1914; 2nd lieut, S'pore V.R., Jan., 1916; ag. asst. supt. govt. monops., S'pore, Nov., 1916; offr., cls. V., Apr., 1917; 2nd lieut., R.G.A. (R.), Dec., 1917; ag. asst. supt. govt. monops., Penang, Aug., 1919; offr., cls. IV., Nov., 1920; dist. offr., K. Selangor, May, 1924; offr., cls. III., Nov., 1925; supt., lands, Kelantan, Aug., 1926; 2nd mag., Penang, Dec., 1928; ag. collr., land rev. and asst. treas., Malacca, May, 1929; cls. II., Nov., 1930; 1st asst. sec. (A), S.S., Aug., 1932; chmn., sany. bd., Kinta, Jan., 1934; ag. advr., land office, Kedah, May, 1936.

WALTON, HON. SIR EDGAR HARRIS, K.C.M.G. (1911).—M.L.A., Cape, for Port Elizabeth, since 1898, and in Union parlmt. since 1910; treas. gen., Feb., 1904, to Feb., 1908; mem. of S. African National Convention in connection with S. Africa Bill, 1909; is proprietor and editor of Eastern Province Herald; high commr. for Union of S. Africa, London, 1921-25.

WALTON, SIR GEORGE O'DONNELL, KT. BACH. (1925).—B.1871; matriculated London Univ., 1891; called to the bar, Middle Temple, 1893; practised at the bar, Barbados, 1894 to 1902; acted on several occasions as pol. mag. and judge of asst. ct. of appeal, Barbados; apptd. pol. mag., dist. "F," Barbados, Dec., 1902; lieut. of M.I., Barbados volunteers; ag. chief just. St. Lucia, 18th May to Oct., 1908, and from March to Oct., 1911; mag. and cor., dist. "C," St. Kitts, Nov., 1911; ag. 2nd puisne judge, Leeward Is., on various occasions, 1911-15; atty.-gen., St. Lucia, May, 1915; ag. adminstr. on several occasions in 1915 and in 1916 and 1917, ag. chief just., from Oct., 1916 to Mar., 1919; atty.-gen., B. Honduras, Mar., 1919; ag. chief justice, May to Nov., 1919; ch. just., Grenada, July, 1921; 1st puisne judge, Trinidad, Apr., 1926; ret., 1931.
WALWYN, ALGERNON EDWARD VERE.—B. 1888; ed. Bath Coll. and Peterhouse, Camb.; asst. dist. offr., N. Provs., Nigeria, Apr., 1915; rea., Sept., 1932; sec., N. Provs., 1933; staff grade, 1937.

WALWYN, VICE ADMIRAL SIR HUMPHREY THOMAS, K.C.S.I. (1923), C.B. (1923), D.S.O. (1916).—B. 1879; ed. H.M.S. Britannia, Dartmouth; went to sea, H.M.S. Camperdown, 1895; gunnery lieut., 1904; comdr., 1912; H.M.S. Warspite, 1915-17; capt., 1916; in comm., destroyer flotillas and senr. offr., Mediterranean destroyers, 1923; dir., gunnery divn., naval staff, admv., 1924-26; commanded H.M.S. Valiant, 1926; flag offr. commodg. R. Indian Navy, 1928-34; rear-adm., 1928; vice-adm., 1932; ret. list, 1934; gov. and commdr.-in-ch., Newfoundland, 1936.

WAMSLEY, R. W., B.A., LL.B.—B. 1880; clk., civ. commr.'s office, Kimberley, 1903; inland rev. dept., 1911; prin. clk., 1921; recr., rev., Port Elizabeth, 1921; recr., rev., Bloemfontein, 1931; ch. rev. offr., 1932; recr., rev., Cape Town, 1933; asst. commr. for inland rev., June, 1938.
WAN, IU-SHING, B.Sc. (Lond.).—B. 1892; inspr., vernacular schls., Hong Kong, 1928.

WANN, MAJOR JAMES, B.A. (Oxon.).—B.1890; ed. Dulwich Coll. and New Coll., Oxford; mily. serv., 1915-1919; ment. in desps.; asst. dist. offr., Nigeria, 1920; dist. offr., 1928; admstve. offr., cls. II, 1936.

WARD, ERSKINE RUEUL LA TOURETTE, B.A.—B. 1900; ed. Harrison Coll., Barbados and Oxford Univ.; called to bar, Middle Temple, 1924; pol. mag., dist. "A," Barbados, 1931; ag. regist., and judge, asst. ct. of appl., various periods, 1932-33; judge, petty debt ct., Bridgetown, 1936; regir., 1938.

WARD, ESPIRE FRANCIS, M.D., B.Ch., B.A.O.—Ed. Epsom Coll. and Queen's Univ., Belfast; med. offr., W.A.M.S., Sierra Leone, 1912; trans., Gambia, 1918; trans., G. Coast, 1920; on special service for C.O., Oct., 1915 to Feb., 1916; temp. licent., gen. list, Sept., 1914 to Mar., 1916; attached Cameroons Expdy. Force, 1914-15; priv. secy. to ag. gov., Gambia, 1918 senr. med. offr., July, 1927; author of "Beri-Beri, Etiology, Symptoms, and Treatment." "Favourite Prescriptions" (2nd edition), "Deaths from Lightning Stroke" (Br. Med. Jnl.)

WARD, E. R.—B. 1902; ed. King's Coll., Taunton and Coll. of Estate Management, Lincoln's Inn Fields; cadet, Nigeria, 1926; asst. dist. offr., 1929; priv. sec. to lieut.-gov., S. Provs., Nov. 1930-May, 1931; ditto to gov., July, 1931-Mar., 1934.
WARD, FRANK DERRINGTON, F.R.I.B.A., M.R.S.A.I.—B. 1885; ed. Kent Coll., Canterbury; P.W.D., S.S., Aug., 1920; govt. architect, S.S., Nov., 1928; registr., bd. of archts., S.S., Jan., 1932.

WARD, JOSEPH CORBET, O.B.E.—Lieut. (afterwards capt.), L.V., S. Africa, 1900-02 (Queen's medal and 4 clasps, King's medal and 2 clasps); Transvaal civ. ser., 1902-04; Br. E. Africa, Sotik expdn. (med. and clasp), 1905; prin. regist., docs., land dept., E.A.P., 1905; attd. 3rd K.A.R., 1914; post. commdt., lines of commun., 1916-18; staff offr. for Br. E. Africa, 1918; temp. lieut. col. (desps. 3 times, 3 war medals); staff offr. and intell-offr., Kenya, 1920-25; sec., office of H.M. Eastern African Dependencies, London, 1926.

WARD, LENNOX, K.C. B.A. (lit. and phil.), LL.B., 1908 (Univ. Cape of Good Hope).—Called to bar, 1909; prof. asst., atty.-gen., Cape, 1910; prof. ast., solr.-gen., 1914; atty.-gen., mandated territory, S.W. Africa, 1919; solr.-gen., 1925; atty.-gen., Natal, 1926; judge-pres., native high ct., 1933.

WARD, LEONARD KEITH, D.Sc. (Adelaide), B.A., B.E.—Ed. Sydney Grammar Schl. and Brisbane Gram. Schl., entd. Univ. of Sydney, graduated in arts, 1900, and in engnr., 1903; lect. in geology, mineralogy, petrology and mining geology, Kalgoorlie schl. of mines, W. Australia, 1903-7; asst. govt. geologist and inspir., mines, Tasmania, 1907-11; govt. geologist, S. Australia, Jan., 1912; also dir. of dept. of mines.

WARD, WILFRED ARTHUR.—B. 1892; ed. privately and at Christ's Hosp.; served, France with Civil Service Rifles, 15th London Regt., as lance-corp.; 2nd lieut., 3rd Lancs. Fusiliers May, 1915; seconded, M.G.C., France, Salonica and Palestine; M.C. and promoted capt.; cadet, F.M.S., Mar., 1920; passed cadet, Jan., 1922; asst. dist. offr., Krian, Mar., 1922; offr., cls. V., Nov., 1919; offr., cls. IV., Nov., 1923; dist. offr., Port Dickson, Apr., 1926; offr., cls. III., Nov., 1928; dist. offr., Temerloh, July, 1929; 2nd asst. sec. to govt., F.M.S., Feb., 1930; ag. assist. treas. F.M.S. and state treas., Selangor, Dec., 1930; offr. cls. II., Nov., 1933; pub. trustee and offr. admstr., F.M.S., Mar., 1934; ag. sec. to res., Selangor, Nov., 1936; offr., cls. II., Aug., 1937.

WARD, WILLIAM ERNEST FRANK, B.A., B.Litt.—B. 1900; ed. Mercer's Schl., London, Dulwich Coll. and Lincoln Coll. Oxford; open hist. schol., Lincoln Coll., 1918; 2nd cls. hona., B.A., mod. hist., 1922; B.Litt., 1923; Goldsmith's exhib., mod. hist., 1921; Ridley Hall, Cambridge; mast., Achimota, Gold Coast, 1924.

WARDLE, JAMES MOREY, B.Sc., C.E., M.E.I.C.—B. 1888; ed. Br. Columbia Pub. Schls. and Queen's Univ., Kingston, Ont.; B.Sc., 1912; engrnr. on locn. and constrn., Canadian N. Pac. Rly., Br. Columbia; highway engrnr., nat. parks, Canada, dept. of intr., 1914-18; supt., Banff nat. park, Alta., 1918-21; ch. engrnr., nat. parks, Canada, 1921-35; western supervsr., nat. parks, 1932-35; dep. min., dept. of intr., Ottawa, 1935; dir., surveys and engnr. br., dept., mines and resources, 1936.

WARE, THOMAS WALTER, M.B., Ch.B., Brist., D.P.H. (Lond.)—B. 1890; med. offr., Brist.; med. offr., med. dept., Hong Kong, in ch., subord. staff families, Kennedy Town infec. dis. hosp. and asst., Victoria hosp., 1927; in ch., civ. hosp., 1928; visiting med. offr., Chinese hosps. and dispensaries, 1930; 2nd port health offr. and inspr., immigrts., Sept., 1932 and Nov., 1933; ag. port health offr., etc., 1933; visiting health offr., Nov., 1934; health offr., Kowloon and New Territories, June, 1937; ag. port health offr., and inspr., immigrts., Aug., 1937 to Aug., 1938.

WARNER, BRODICK ASHTON, C.M.G. (1939).—B. 1888; ed. Winchester Coll.; asst. dist. commnr., Uganda Prot., 1912; lab. commnr., 1922; 1st grade administrative offr., 1923; senr. asst. sec., 1924; dep. prov. commnr., 1929; ag. dep. ch. sec., Oct., 1931; prov. commnr., June, 1933; late capt., 5th batt., City of London Regt. (T.F.); capt., Territorial Force Reserve.

WARREN, EDGAR HERBERT, M.B.E. (1930).—Served with 28th I.Y., S. African war, 1902 (medal with 2 clasps); S. African constab. (civil), 1903-1905; lieut., Transvaal H.A., 1907; clk. in customs, Nyasaland Prot., 1908; offr. of customs, 1909; also immigr. offr., 1913; principal immigr. offr., May, 1914; asst. contr., cust., Apr., 1920; ag. comprtr., cust., July, 1922; comptr., cust., Mar., 1925; ag. treas., June, 1933.
WARREN, FREDK. DRUMMOND.—B. 1881; stock inspr., Basutoland, 1914; sub-inspr., Basutoland mounted pol., 1922; war serv., 1915-19; supt., pol., 1936.

WARREN, JOHN JOSEPH.—B. 1889; R.I.C., 1910-13; inspr., pol., F.M.S., May, 1913; asst. commnr., pol., Mar., 1921; dir., crim. intell., May, 1926; head, prev. serv., S'pore, Nov., 1927; asst. supt., govt. monops., S.S., Sept., 1929; senr. asst. supt., govt. monops., S.S., July, 1932; senr. dep. commnr., excise, 1934; ag. commnr., cust. and excise, S.S. 1935.

WARRINGTON, J. C.—B. 1887; educ., Marlborough and Trin. Coll. Camb.; B.A., 1908; served in France with R.E. (Signals) 1914 to 1918; twice mentd. in despa.; French Croix de Guerre; asst. dist. commnr., G. Coast, 1919; dist. commnr., Apr., 1924.

WARRINGTON, JOHN FRANCIS STANLEY TAMAR.—B. 1885; B.S.A. pol., 1909-13; S. Rhodesia civ. serv., 1913-14; clk., dist. adminstr., N. Rhodesia, 1914; asst. native commnr., 1916; native commnr., 1920; dist. offr., 1923; ag. dep. prov. commnr., 1934; ag. prov. commnr., 1937; prov. commnr., May, 1937.

WASE-BAILLEY, BASIL NOEL VICTOR M.D. (Edin.), D.T.M. & H. (Eng.), 1926; D.P.H. (Eng.), 1929.—B. 1899; med. serv., B. Guiana, 1926; G.M.O., pub. hosp.; ag. gent. beact., 1926; asst. G.M.O.H., personal ast. to surg.-gen. and dep. port health offr., 1927; govt. med. offr. of health, surg. gen.'s dep. and port health offr., 1931; immigrn. agt.-gen., regtr.-gen., 1935; ag. chmn., poor law commrs., 1935; dep. ch., local gov't. bd., 1937; ag. surg.-gen., 1937; ch. med. offr., Barbados, 1938.

WATERER, RALPH RONALD, M.A. (Cantab.).—B. 1902; passed B.A. in estate management and dipl. forestry, Cambridge Univ.; Imp. Forestry Inst., Oxford; asst. conserv., forest. lst gr., Cyprus, Oct., 1928; conserv., forest., 1938.

WATERMEYER, ERNEST FREDERICK.—B. 1880; ed. Graaff Reinet Coll., Stellenbosch Boys Schl., Bath Coll. and Caius Coll., Cambridge; maths. schol., Caius Coll., 1899; maths. tripos, 1902; law triplos, 1903; called to bar, Inner Temple, Apr., 1904; pres., income tax sp. ct., 1919; ag. puisne judge, Cape Prov., divn., sup. ct., 1920; puisne judge, C.P. divn., sup. ct., 1922; judge of appl., 1937.

WATERMEYER, THEODORE HEINRICH.—B. 1879; ed. Graaff Reinet Coll., Old Gymnasium, Stellenbosch, Bath Coll., Bath and Mason's Univ. Coll. (now Birmingham Univ.), M.Inst.C.E.: app't, Cape govt. rlys., 1902; asst. ch. civ. engrnr., 1920; rep. S. African Rlys. at Internat. Rly. Congress, Rome, 1922; asst. gen. man., Bloemfontein, 1925; asst. gen. man. for Cape suburban electrification, Capetown, and consulting engrnr., 1927; asst. gen. man., Johannesburg, 1928; gen. man., 1933.

WATERS, HAROLD BERTRAM B.A., Oxon.—B. 1896; ed. Royal Gram. Schl., Guildford and St. Edmund Hall, Oxford; served in The Queen's (Royal West Surrey) Regt. and Signal Serv., R.E., 1914-19; supr., agric., Nigeria, Mar., 1921; sent to F.M.S., Java and Sumatra to report on oil-palm industry, 1926; dep. asst. dir., agric., Nigeria, Apr., 1927; commnr., Canadian national exhibn., 1928; dep. dir., agr., Gold Coast, Dec., 1928; dir., agr., Kenya, 1933.

WATKINS-PITCHFORD, H. O., M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P. (Lond.), D.T.M. & H.—B. 1900; ed. St. George's Coll., Surrey; St. Thomas's Hosp. Med. Schl.; E. Africa med. serv., Zanzibar, Feb., 1925; med. offr., Kenya, 1934.

WATSON, ANDREW D.—B. 1882; ed. common schls., Baddeck Acad., Dalhousie Univ., B.A., 1906; ent. civ. serv., Canada, dept. of insurance, Feb., 1906; actuary, 1914; assoc., Inst. of Actuaries, Lond., 1909; Assoc., Actuarial Soc. of America, 1913; Fellow, 1919; contrib. to jnl. of Inst. of Actuaries and transactions of Actuarial Soc.

WATSON, DONALD FRANCIS, O.B.E., (1926).—B. 1883; 2nd clk., admstr.'s office, Seychelles, 1899; dispensary and clk., med. dept., 1900; clk. to local bd. of health, quarantine and cemetery comtee., 1900; man., govt. pharmacy, 1901; ag. supt., lunatic asylum, May to Oct., 1920; ag. treas., collr. of customs, chmn., marine bd., and chmn., profiteering comte., July, 1920 to Apr., 1921; contr., local clearing office, June, 1921; treas., postmr., collr. of cust., comnr. of income tax, man. of savings bank, regis. of shipping, Apr., 1923.
WATSON, ERNEST SIBLES, B.Sc. Tech.—B. 1902; ed. Cent. High Schl., Manchester and Coll. of Technology, Manch.; B.Sc., Tech., 1923; asst. mast., Achimota, Gold Coast, 1925.

WATSON, H.C., M.B., B.Ch., B.A.O., R.U., Ireland.—B. 1882; ed. Campbell Coll. and Queen's Coll., Belfast; Transvaal civ. serv., July, 1909; physn. supt., Fort Beaufort ment. hosp., 1919; Alexandra instn., Cape Town, 1921; Bloemfontein ment. hosp., 1923.

WATSON, HON. HERBERT GORDON, I.S.O. (1922).—Ck., gov. of Zululand's office, 1896; ag. sec. for Zululand at different periods, 1896; olk., gov.'s office, Natal, Dec., 1896; transf. Cape civ. serv. and apptd. ck., gov.'s office, Mar., 1901; ch. ck., July, 1904; ck., exec. coun., Apr., 1904 to Apr., 1907; ag. priv. sec. to offr. admgt. govt., major-gen. E. S. Africa, May, 1910; sec. to prime min. and ck., exec. coun., Feb., 1916; ck., exec. coun., Union of S. Africa, 1922; adminstr., prov. of Natal, Feb., 1928.

WATSON, JAMES GILBERT.—B. 1889; ed. Kgl. Preuss. Forstakademie, Eberswalde, Germany; supt., govt. plantations, F.M.S., Feb., 1913; asst. conserv., forests, F.M.S., Oct., 1913; dep. conserv., forests, research, F.M.S., Nov., 1926; conserv., forests, Jan., 1930; ag. dir. forestry, S.S. and advt., forestry, M.S., June, 1937.
WATSON, NOEL DUNCAN, B.A. (Oxon.).—B. 1916; ed. Bradford Grammar Schl. and schol. of New Coll., Oxford; admstv. offr. (asst. sec., col. sec.'s off.), Cyprus, Oct., 1938.

WATSON, OSWALD, L.S.—B. 1891; survey cadet, N.S.W. and N. Guinea, Sept., 1910-14; milt. serv., survey sec., R.E. and topo. sec., A.I.F., 1915-18; lic. land survr., Australia and N.Z., Sept., 1919; survr., S.S., Apr., 1920; do.
