WATSON, P. C. M.—Treasy. asst., E.A.P., May, 1914; senr. asst. treas., Kenya, Jan., 1922; ag. dep. treas., Nov., 1922 to Mar., 1924; prin. asst. treas., 1930; acct.-gen., Sept., 1938.

WATSON, WILLIAM LAW, A.M.I.C.E., M. Coun. Inst. Loco. Engrs.—B. 1883; ed. Grammar Schl. and Robert Gordon's Coll. (Abdn.); loco. dept., G.N.S.R., 1899; loco. dept., G.W.R., 1905; engrg. design dept., Crown agents, 1912; min. of munitions, 1915; dep. head, engrg. design dept., C.A., 1919; dep. ch. engrg. (mech.), 1928; ch. engrg. (contracts), 1935.
WATT, CECIL JAMES.—B. 1899; under secy., dept. of pub. health, N.S.W., 1930.

WATT, MICHAEL HERBERT, C.B.E. (1935), M.D. (N.Z.), D.P.H. (N.Z.).—B. 1887; ed. Otago Boys' High Schl. and Otago Univ. (N.Z.); med. offr. of health, 1917; asst. ch. health offr., 1920; dep. dir.-gen. of health, 1925; dir.-gen., 1931.

WATT, SIR THOMAS, K.C.M.G. (1912); C.M.G. (1906).—B. 1867; ed. at priv. schl. and Glasgow Univ.; admtd. a solr. to Scottish cts.; practised in Natal; served as subaltern in Imperial L.I., 1900; apptd. capt. and offr. commdg. Newcastle Town Guard, and ment. in desps.; M.L.A., Natal, 1901-1910; formerly min. of just. and educn., Natal; min. of posts and telegraphs and pub. wks., Union of S. Africa, 1912; min. of pub. wks. and interior, 1916; min. of pub. health, 1919; min. of posts and telegraphs and of public wks., 1921-24.

WATTS, CLIVE.—B. 1893; 2nd lieut., Northumberland Fusiliers, Sept., 1915; lieut., July, 1917; capt., June, 1918; ment. in desps., and wounded, European War; acctnt., treasy., Cyprus, 1920; acctnt. to pub. custodian and Cyprus contr. in addition, Apr., 1921 to May, 1923; ag. asst. treas., for various periods in 1924, 1925 and 1927; ag. asst. sec., col. sec.'s office in 1926 and 1927; ag. dep. treas., July, 1927 to Jan., 1928; dep. treas., Jan., 1928; ag. treas., for various periods, 1928-33; offl. recor. in bankruptcy, Mar. to June, 1931; Br. del. of Evcaf., Nov., 1933; treas., Mauritius, 1934.

WATTS, GUY TRACY.—B. 1892; ed. Clifton Coll.; barrister-at-law, Lincoln's Inn, Apr., 1914; war serv., 1915-18; crown counsel, Nigeria, Mar., 1925; pol. mag., Calabar, 1928; atty.-gen., Bahamas, Jan., 1930; chmn., bd. of educn., mein., leg. coun.; ag. ch. just., in 1930, 1931 and 1932; puisne judge, Mauritius, 1933.

WAUCHOPE, MAJOR-GEN. SIR ARTHUR GRENFELL, G.C.B. (1939); G.C.M.G. (1933), K.C.B. (1931), C.B. (1923), C.M.G. (1917), C.I.E. (1919), D.S.O. (1900).—B. 1874; ed. St. Ninian's, Moffat and Repton; 2nd lieut., 4th Argyll and Sutherland Highlanders, 1893; 2nd Batt. The Black Watch, 1896; capt., 1901; served in Boer War (desp., Queen's med. with clasp, King's med. with two clasps); European War, 1914-18; major, 1914; commanded 2nd Batt. Black Watch in France and Mesopotamia, 34th Brigade and temporarily 1st divn., Indian Corps (desp.); bvt.-lieut.-col., afterwards col., 1922; major-gen., 1922; mily. mem., Overseas Sttltm. delegation to Australia and N.Z., 1923; ch. of Br. sec., mily. inter-allied comsn. of control, Berlin, 1924-27; G.O.C., 44th Home Counties divn., T.A., 1927; G.O.C., Northern Ireland dist., 1929; high commr. and commr.-in-ch., Palestine, 1931-38.

WAYLAND, EDWARD JAMES, C.B.E. (1938).—B. 1888; ed. Central Foundation Schl., City of London Coll., R. Coll. of Science and R. Schol. of Mines; nat. sci. studentship (C.L.C.), 1906; Natnl. schol. (R.C.S.), 1907-10; post grad. & Marshall research schol., 1910; A.R.C.S., M.Inst. M.M.; F.G.S., London, etc.; Bigeye med., geol. soc., Lond., 1933; Victoria med., R.G.S., 1935; asst. min. survr., Ceylon, 1912; war serv. 254 Tunnelling Coy., R.E.; forward demolition reconnaissance affr. (IVth Army); ag. O.C., 3rd Water Boring Sec., R.E., apptd. to Uganda, Oct., 1918; dir., geological survey, 1928.

WAYNE, RICHARD ST. JOHN ORMEROD.—B. 1904; schol. of Eton, 1917; Trinity Coll., Cambridge, 1923; M.A., 1930; asst. sec., col. sec.'s office, Cyprus, May, 1927; ag. priv. sec. to O.A.G., on various occasions, 1927-29 and 1932; ag. ch. asst. sec. in 1930 and 1933; ag. comnr., in 1931, 1934 and 1935; admstv. offr.; asst. sec., Jan., 1935; comnr., Mar., 1935.

WEATHERHEAD, HENRY DOUGLAS, M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.), D.T.M. & H. (Eng.).—B. 1899; served in Great War, 1916-19; senr. res. surv., B'dos gen. hosp., 1926; M.O., Lee-ward Is., Dec., 1926; asst. surg., Br. Guiana, Feb., 1931; med. supt., Holberton hosp., Antigua, 1933; senr. M.O., St. Lucia, Nov., 1933.
WEAVING, L. A.—Aast. dist. commnr., Kenya, June, 1921; ag. sec., 1930.

WEBB, AMBROSE HENRY.—B. 1882; ed. Clifton Coll., Bath Coll. and Trinity Coll., Oxford, 1899 (class. schol.); 1st Victoria prizeman and John Brooke schol., King's Inns, Dublin, 1908; called to bar, 1909; K.C., 1920; pres., dist. ct., Samaria, 1921; puisne judge, Kenya, 1933; ch. just., Sierra Leone, 1937.

WEBB, CECIL RICHARD, O.B.E. (1926), M.C.—Aast. traffic manager, Uganda rly., 1914; traffic man., Tanganyika Territory rly., Oct., 1919; gen. man., govt. rly., Sierra Leone, June, 1923; gen. man., Palestine rly., 1930.

WEBB, OLIVER STANLEY, M.C., M.Inst., M. & Cy., E., M. I. Struct. E. Chtd. Struct. Engrn.—B. 1889; ed. Kingswood Coll., Grahamstown and Rhodes Univ. Coll., S. Africa; sapper, R.E., Dec., 1914; mentd. in despatches, commdg. 31st divan. signal co., R.E., Jan., 1919; asst. engrn., P.W.D., S.S., Feb., 1920; exec. engrn., Jan., 1927; senr. exec. engrn., Feb., 1936.
WEBB, HON. PATRICK CHARLES.—Mem., N.Z. H. of R., 1913-18 and since 1933; min. of mines, 1935.

WEBB, WILLIAM LESLIE.—B. 1888; ed. Christ Coll., Blackheath and Guy's Hosp.—M.B. (hons.), B.S., Lond.; D.P.H., Lond.; M.R.C.S., Eng.; L.R.C.P., Lond.; med. offr., Uganda, 1913; superv., venereal diseases, 1913; temp. capt., Uganda med. serv., 1914-16; temp. capt., R.A.M.C., 1918-19; senr. med. offr., 1923; spec. offr., med. supt., Mulago hosp., 1926; dep. dir., med. serv., Uganda, 1928; dir., med. services, Zanzibar, 1934.

WEBLEY, H. L.—B. 1884; ed. pvtly., Bloxham; W. African pol., Feb., 1912; served in European war, 1915-19; asst. commnr., pol., S. Nigeria, Sept., 1919; seconded to act as commnr., pol., inspr., prisons and sheriff, Gambia, May, 1920; asst. commnr., pol., Gambia, 1920; ag. commnr., pol., etc. also as pvt. sec. and A.D.C. on several occasions, 1920-29; ag. crown pros. on several occasions, 1920-29; commnr., pol., inspr., prisons and sheriff, June, 1929; King's pol. med., 1934.

WEBSTER, GEORGE FREDERICK.—B. 1899; asst. polit. offr., German E. Africa provisional admnstr., Oct., 1916; adminstr. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Feb., 1922; ag. senr. commnr., Dar-es-Salaam dist., Jan. to Dec., 1925; dep. prov. commnr., Jan., 1926; offr. mem., leg. coun., Dec., 1926; ag. sec., native affrs. and mem., exec. coun., Jan. to Apr., 1927; ag. prov. commnr., Feb., 1928; prov. commnr., junr. grade, May, 1929; senr. prov. commnr., Nov., 1934; offr. mem., leg. coun., 1935.

WEBSTER, GEORGE HENRY, O.B.E. (1937).—B. 1887; Br. post office, 1903-14; war serv., 1915-20; twice ment. in despatches; asst. treas., Kenya, July, 1920; asst. audr., Kenya, Apr., 1923; ch. storekeeper, P.W.D., 1929; ch. acctnt., P.W.D., Nov., 1929.

WEBSTER, CAPT. JOHN SUTTON, O.B.E. (1939), M.B., Bach. Surg. (Vict. Univ. Manch.). D.P.H., D.M.R.E., Barrister-at-law, Middle Temple.—B. 1884; med. offr., S.S., July, 1909; grade I, Apr., 1910; med. offr. for surg. wk., gen. hosp., Penang, July, 1910; ola. IV, Jan., 1914; med. offr., P.W., Sept., 1914; W.O., Apr., 1915 (mentd. in despatches); prof. of med., Coll. of Med., S'pore, Sept., 1922; offr. deleg., 6th Cong. Far Eastn. Assoc. of Trop. Med., Tokyo, Sept., 1925; radiologist, S.S., Apr., 1927; ag. ch. med. offr., S'pore, in addn. in 1930 and 1935.

WEDDERBURN, MAXWELL MACLAGAN, C.M.G. (1935).—B. 1883; ed. George Watson's Coll., Edinburgh, and Edinburgh Univ. (M.A.), cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., Dec., 1906; pol. mag., Point Pedro and Chavakachcheri, Apr., 1908; Matara, Aug., 1909; Kurunegala, June, 1909; asst. land settlmt. offr., Jan., 1911; asst. land settlmt. offr. and special offr. under "Waste Lands Ordece," Aug., 1912; asst. censor, Aug., 1914; asst. land settlmt. offr. and special offr. under "Waste Lands Ordece," Oct., 1914; asst. censor, Nov., 1914; asst. gov't. agt., Nuwara Eliya, Sept., 1915; asst. settlmt. offr., July, 1920; rubber contr., Mar., 1925; gov't. agt., N.C. Prov., Sept., 1925; addnl. gov't. agt., S. Prov., Nov., 1928; gov't. agt., S. Prov., Apr., 1929; cls. I, grade I, 1931; gov't. agt., W. Prov., Mar., 1931; ag. ch. sec., Dec., 1932; offr. apptd. to report on poor relief, Aug., 1933; dep. ch. sec., Sept., 1934; ag. ch. sec., Oct., 1934-Mar., 1935 and June-Nov., 1935; ch. sec., Jan., 1936; O.A.G., June-Nov., 1937.

WEGENER, WILLIAM FREDERICK, M.Inst.C.E.—B. 1897; ed. Crosley Schil., Halifax; Fleetwood L. & Y. & L.N.W. Joint rly., marine dept., 1914; draughtsman, C.M.E. dept., Horwich L. & Y. rly., 1919; power plant supt., loco. works, C.M.E. dept., Horwich L.M.S. rly., 1922; works man., loco. dept., F.M.S. rly., July, 1928; asst. ch. mech., engrn., Oct., 1932; ch. mech. engrn., F.M.S. rly., Oct., 1937.
WEIGALL, CECIL EDWARD, K.C., B.A. (Oxon.), LL.B. (Syd.)—B. 1870; solr. gen. and asst. law offr., N.S.W., 1930.

WEIGALL, LIEUT.-COL. SIR WILLIAM ERNEST GEORGE ARCHIBALD, K.C.M.G. (1920).—B. 1874; ed. Wellington Coll., Royal Agric. Coll. (gold medallist); major (ret.), 3rd Batt., Northants Regt.; served in S. Africa, 1902; M.P. (U.I.), Horncastle Div., Lincs., 1911-1920; capt. Lincolnshire Yeomanry, and lieut.-col. and inspr. Q.M.G. services, Eastern Comd., 1914-17; mem. of national salvage council; dep. chmn., surplus govt. property disposal bd.; mem., select comtees on national expenditure; mem., national agric. coun.; pres. agric. organisation soc.; dep. chmn., fedn. of county agric. comtees; J.P., Kent and Lincs.; gov., S. Australia, 1920-22.

WEIR, ANDREW HERON WILSON, B.Sc. Edin.—B. 1893; ed. Viewpark Schil., Edinburgh and Edinburgh Univ.; on mil. serv., 1914-19 (R.F.A., U.K., India and Mesopotamia); men. in despatches; asst. conserv., forests, Nigeria, Aug., 1920; conserv., forests, col. forest serv., May, 1935; ag. ch. conserv., forests, Nigeria, July, 1935-July, 1936; author of "Gum Arabic in Bornu Province," and "Report on Gum Arabic in Anglo-Egyptian Sudan."

WEISBERG, HYMAN, B.A. Cantab.—B. 1890; 1st cls. math. tripus.; cadet, F.M.S., Nov., 1914; passed cadet, June, 1917; asst. dist. offr., Nov., 1919; dist. offr., May, 1920; mag., July, 1922; ag. asst. treas., F.M.S. and state treas., Selangor, in addn., Apr. to June, 1929; addl. pub. trustee's office, Dec., 1930; asst. sec. to gov't., F.M.S., offr. cls. II, Nov., 1931; ag. under sec. to gov't., F.M.S., June, 1933; offr. cls. II, July, 1935; treas., S.S. Dec., 1935; finan. sec., S.S., 1937.
WELCH, T. B.—B. 1898; ed. Brighton Coll.; asst. loco. supt., Nigerian rly., 1923; asst. divnl. supt. (loco.), 1927.

WELDON, LEO FRANCIS, A.M.Inst.C.E.—B. 1896; ed. Stonehurst and Univ. Coll., London Univ.; lieut. R.F.A., 1915-19; asst. engrnr., Min. of Transport, 1924-26; temp. asst. engrnr., P.W.D., Cyprus, 1931; asst. engrnr., 1936; ag. divnl. engrnr., P.W.D., June to Sept., 1932.

WELLS, THOMAS BOSTOCK, A.M.Inst.T.—B. 1888; ed. Lincoln Cathedral Choir Schl.; stattnmr., F.M.S. rly., Sept., 1913; asst. traffic inspr., Tampin, Sept., 1915; wharf supt., Prai, Oct., 1916; asst. traffic man., July, 1918; asst. traffic man., July, 1926; senr. asst. traffic man., July, 1927; asst. sec. to gen. man., F.M.S. rly., Jan., 1932.

WELMAN, JOHN BARTHROPPE.—B. 1896; ed. Southey Hall, Worthing, Chateau de Rots near St. Germain-en-Laye; milly. serv. in Egypt, S. Arabia and Mesopotamia, 1914-19; admvte. offr., cls. IV, Nigeria, 1920; cls. III, 1928; ag. sec., N. Provs., Nov.-Dec., 1935; admvte. offr., cls. II, 1936.

WELSH, ALEXANDER SIMPSON, K.C.—B. 1881; Cape civ. serv., 1899; B.A. (Cape), 1904; LL.B. (Cape), 1907; prof. assist. to crown prosecutor, Johannesburg, 1914; prof. asst. to atty.-gen., Cape, 1918; crown pros., Johannesburg, 1921; law advr., 1926; atty.-gen., Transvaal, 1929.

WELSH, GORDON JAMES.—B. 1894; ed. Cathedral Schl. and Simon Langton Schl., Canterbury; war serv., 1914-19; India, Aden and Mesopotamia, as pvt., 1/4th Batt., The Buffs and 2nd lieut., The Queen's Own Royal West Kent Regt.; clk. to gov., Tanganyika Territory, Feb., 1921; ch. clk., Apr., 1923; asst. sec. to govt., Somaliland, Mar., 1929; ag. dist. commr., Bureau dist., Aug., 1929; asst. sec. in ch., secretariat on various occasions, 1929-1934.
WENTWORTH, MAURICE FRANK GERARD, B.A.—B. 1908; ed. Haileybury and Univ. Coll., London (teacher's dipl., Lond.); inspr., schl., Aug., 1930.
WERE, MARY JOSEPHINE, O.B.E., L.R.C.P., S.L., D.P.H., Royal Coll. Surgns., Dublin—Lady med. offr., dist. hosp., K. Lumpur, May, 1921; do., infant welfare centre, May, 1923.

WEST, HAROLD.—B. 1882; ed. Christ's Coll., Blackheath; land survr., P.W.D., Hong Kong, 1910; lieut., R.G.A., 1917-19; 1st cls. land survr., 1920; prin. land survr., Kowloon and New Territories, 1922; 1st asst. supt., crown lands, 1926; asst. supt., crown lands, 1932; supt., crown lands and surveys, 1938.

WESTON, BERTRAM JOHN, M.Sc. (Agr.) S. Africa, M.A. (Cantab.), F.R.H.S.—B. 1907; ed. Ashford Grammar Schl. and Sidney Sussex Coll., Camb.; horticulturist (now supt., agr.), Cyprus, 1931; sec. asst. commr., Dec., 1937.

WESTON, ERNEST ARTHUR, C.M.G. (1918).—B. 1880; B.A. Cantab., 1902; served with R.E. during Great War, 1914-19; capt., 1914; lieut.-col., 1916; asst. dist. commr., S. Nigeria, 1917 and served in various posts under Nigerian govt. until 1927 when retd. on med. grounds; on staff of Imp. Economic Comtes., 1927-31; commr., Cayman Is., Dec., 1931; commr., Cyprus, 1933.

WESTON, FREDERICK THOMAS.—B. 1898; ed. Ebw Vale (Mon.) and Port Talbot (Glam.) secondary schls. and Univ. Coll., Cardiff, B.A. (hons.) Wales, 1929; dipl. ed., 1930; asst. mast., Queen's Coll., Br. Guiana, 1935.

WESTON, HARRY CHARLES, B.So. (Eng.), A.M.I.C.E.—B. 1894; ed. Chatham House Schl., Ramsgate, Goldsmiths Training Coll., Univ. of Lond.; Glasgow Univ., and Royal Tech. Coll., Glasgow; war serv., 1914-19; ch. asst. engrnr., Zanzibar, 1921; res. engrnr., Dar-es-Salaam, 1926, prin., Acro Tech. Schl., May, 1929.

WHEBELL, CHARLES JOHN.—B. 1887; joined Royal North West Mounted Pol., Canada, Dec., 1912; proceeded overseas with 1st Canadian contingent, Sept., 1914; on war serv., 1914-19; "1914-15" Star, Victory and Gen. Serv. Meds., Meritorious Serv. Med., Croix-de-Guerre (Belgian), ment. in desps., Apr., 1917; rejoined R.N.W. M. Pol., Canada, May, 1919; asst. comdt., pol., Bahamas, Apr., 1921; ag. comdt., June, 1921 to Apr., 1923 and from May to Dec., 1924; comdt. of pol., instr. of prisons, provost marshal and ch. of fire dept., Aug., 1926.

WHINDUS, ERNEST JAMES.—Warserv., S.W. and E. African campaigns, 1914-17; clk., treas., N. Rhodesia, 1917; asst. treas., 1922; senr. asst. treas., 1935; ag. dep. treas., 1935 and 1936; contr., stores and transp., 1937; contr., stores and transp., 1938.

WHISKARD, SIR GEOFFREY GRANVILLE, K.C.M.G. (1933), O.B. (1923), C.M.G. (1931).—B. 1886; ed. St. Paul's Schl. and Wadham Coll., Oxford; 1st cls. mods., 1907; 1st cls. lit. hum., 1909; ent. H.O., 1911; asst. prta. sec. and subsequently prin. pty. sec. to successive secs. of state, 1915-20; asst. sec., ch. sec.'s office, Dublin Castle, 1920-22; asst. sec., C.O., 1st Apr., 1924; vice-chmn., overseas settlement c'tee., 4th Sept., 1929; asst. under sec. of State, D.O. in addn., 1st Jan., 1931; accompanied U.K. delegation to Imp. economic confce., Ottawa, 1932; high commr. in the C'wealth of Australia for H.M.'s govt. in the U.K., 1936.

WHITCOMBE, SIDNEY F.—B. 1899; ent. C.O., July, 1915; apptd., after compet. exam., asst. clk., C.O., 1917; on mil. serv., Jan., 1918 to Apr., 1920; cler. offr., 1st Jan., 1920; cler. offr., higher grade, Apr., 1927; dep. reglar., Nov., 1931.
WHITE, ALBERT FREDERICK JOHN.—B. 1906; ed. Trinity Coll., Dublin; cadet, Br. Solomon Is. Prot., 1930; passed cadet, 1931; dist. commr., Fiji, 1934; re-designated dist. offr., 1938.

WHITE, ALBERT GEORGE.—B. 1901; apptd. after compet. exam., cler. offr., H.M.S.O. Mar., 1920; transf'd. to C.O., Mar., 1928; accompanied U.K. delegn. to Imp. economic confce., Ottawa, 1932; seconded as reglar. on staff of high commr. in S. Africa for H.M.'s govt. in the U.K., 1934-37.

WHITE, ARTHUR REGINALD, M.C.—B. 1886; ch. draftsman, Kelantan, May, 1912; on milly. serv. 1915-18; asst. supt., surveys, Kelantan, Nov., 1918; supt., surveys, Jan., 1923; supt. rev. surveys, F.M.S. and S.S., July, 1929; supt., surveys, Malacca, Mar., 1931; ag. asst. survr.-gen., F.M.S., Apr., 1933; senr. supt., surveys, Perak, Aug., 1935; dep. survr.-gen., F.M.S. and S.S., Nov., 1937.
WHITE, GEORGE, B.Sc. (Eng.), A.M.I.Mech.E., Chart. Mech. Engrnr.—B. 1898; capt., T.A.R.O.; prin., junr. tech. schl., Hong Kong, 1932; do., trade and tech. schls., 1935.

WHITE, HUGH FORTESCUE MORRBY.—B. 1891; ed. Malvern Coll. and St. John's Coll., Oxford; asst. dist. offr., Nigeria, 1915; admvte. offr., I, Sept., 1932; prin. asst. sec., 1936; ag. dep. ch. sec., Sept., 1936-Feb., 1937 and July-Dec., 1937; admvte. offr., staff gde., 1937; ag. ch. commr., S.P., 1938.

WHITE, JAMES BOWIE, A.M.Inst.C.E., A.M. Inst.W.E.—B. 1903; ed. R. Tech. Coll., Glasgown and Glasgow Univ.; engr., African Lakes Corporation, Nyasaland, 1924-28; asst. engr., P.W.D., Zanzibar, 1930; do., P.W.D., Uganda, 1937.
WHITE, Lt.-Col. Thomas Walter, D.F.C., M.P., V.D.—El. to H. of R., Commonwealth of Australia for Balacava (Vic.), 1929; min. for trade and cust. Lyons ministry, 1933 and Jan., 1933-Nov., 1938.

WHITE, Rt. Hon. Sir William Thomas, P.C. (1920), G.C.M.G. (1935), K.C.M.G. (1916), B.A.—B. 1866; ed. High Schls., Oakville and Brampton, Ont., Univ. of Toronto (B.A.), graduate of the Ontario law school; apptd. manager National Trust Co., Ltd., Toronto, 1899; subsequently general manager and vice-pres. of the same institution; mem. of the Privy Council for Canada and min. of finance in Mr. Borden's cabinet, Oct., 1911; ditto in National govt., Oct., 1917; re-elec. at g.e., Dee., 1917, for Leeds-Brockville; resig. portfolio, July, 1919.

WHITE, W. V. C.—B. 1897; on mily. serv., 8th Sept., 1914 to 14th Dec., 1915; discharged through wounds, 17th Feb., 1916; ent. Miny. of Food, 18th Mar., 1918; appt. cler. offr., C.O., 24th Apr., 1922; higher grade cler. offr., July, 1938.

WHITELEY, Gerald Charles, C.M.G. (1937).—B. 1891; ed. Bradford and Jesus Coll., Oxford; B.A.; (Oxon.); 2nd cls. hons. moda., 3rd cls. lit. hum.; asst. dist. offr., Nigeria, 1915; ag. sec., Muri prov., 1926; admive. offr., cls. I., Apr., 1930; ag. dep. ch. sec., 1931; ag. ch. sec., June, 1931; dep. ch. sec., 1934; ag. ch. sec. various occasions, 1934-37; gov't. dep. in 1936, 1937 and 1938; ag. ch. sec., 1938.

WHITFIELD, Lionel Digby.—B. 1899; ed. Mountjoy Schl., Dublin; M.A. (T.C.D.), M.R.S.T.; European mast., S.S., Aug., 1922; headmast., govt. English schl., K. Pilah, normal cls. instr. in addn., Jan., 1928; ag. inspr. schls., 1928; ag. headmast., Anderson schl., Ipoh, May, 1936.
WHITFORD, Hon. Stanley R.—B. 1878; M.L.C. S. Australia, since 1922; ch. sec., min. of agr., and commiss. of forest lands, 1930-33.

WHITLAMSMITH, Gerard Kime, B.A.—B. 1903; ed. Westminster and Christ Ch., Oxford; 2nd cls. hons. hist., 1925; admive. offr. (cadet), Tanganyika Territory, 1926; asst. dist. offr., 1928; seconded secret. in 1932-37.

WHITLEY, Major Norman Henry Pownall, M.C., B.A. (Cantab.). Barrister-at-Law, Inner Temple.—B. 1883; called to bar; N.Circuit, 1907; on war serv., 7th Manchester Regt., T.F., 1914-18; demob. with rank of major, Jan., 1920; dep. pub. pros., S.S., June, 1921; puisne judge, S.S., Feb., 1930; judge, F.M.S., Feb., 1930; puisne judge, S'pore, Nov. 1930; ag. sr. puisne judge, Penang, Apr., 1931; puisne judge, S'pore, Apr., 1932; do., Penang, Mar., 1934; ag. ch. just., Jan., 1936; ch. just., Uganda, 1938.
WHITLEY, W., B.A. (Oxf.), A.I.C.—B. 1902; analytical chem., Tanganyika Territory 1928.

WHITTING, Charles Edward Jewel.—B. 1900; ed. Haileybury and St. John's Coll., Oxford; supt., educ. dept., N. Provs., Nigeria, July, 1922; senr. educ. offr., Palestine, 1931; supt. educ., Nigeria, 1933.
WHITTINGDALE, William.—B. 1885; ch. draughtsman and compr., surveys, Cape, Mar., 1917; prof. asst., Sept., 1920; 1st grade prof. asst., trig. survey, Nov., 1926; dir., trig. survey, May, 1933.
WHITTINGHAM, Thomas William.—B. 1901; ed. Masonic Schl., Buahay; sub-insp. pol., Br. Guiana, 1924; ex. officio J.P., 1925; dist. inspr., 1927; ag. commr., N.W. dist., 1929; county inspr., Dec., 1932.

WHITTLE, Reginald Alfred, M.C.—B. 1896; ed. Manchester Grammar Schl. and Queen's Coll., Oxford (schol.); 1st cls. math. moda., 1919; B.A., 1920; 2nd lieut., Manchester Regt., 1916; served in France, 1917; invalided from Army, 1918; asst. dist. commr., Uganda, 1921; asst. sec., 1922; senr. asst. sec., 1932; asst. ch. sec., Jan., 1937; ag. dep. ch. sec., Nov., 1936-June, 1937.

WHITWORTH, Stanley Howarth.—B. 1898; ed. Melbourne Univ., Royal Vet. Coll., Lond., Univ. of Zurich; vet. serv., Kenya, Mar., 1920-Dec., 1930; do., Gold Coast, and min. of agril. and fisheries, Jan., 1931-Aug., 1933; dir., vet. resch. and vet. advr., Malaya, Sept., 1933.

WHYATT, John.—B. 1905; ed. Stonyhurst and Balliol Coll., Oxford; B.A. (hons.); barrister-at-law, Inner Temple, 1927; asst. crown sol., Hong Kong, 1937; land offr., Mar., 1937; ag. asst. atty.-gen., June, 1937.

WHYBRO, Charles, B.A., LL.B.—B. 1902; ed. Berkhamsted Schl., London Univ., Schol. of Economics, and Christ's Coll., Cambridge, 1921-23; economic tripoe, pt. I, 3rd cls., 1922; law tripoe, pt. II, 3rd cls., 1923; supt., educ., Tanganyika Territory, 1926.

WHYTE-SMITH, Thomas Stodart.—B. 1883; ed. Merchiston Castle Schl. and Edinburgh Univ.; writer to signet, 1906; 2nd lieut., the Royal Scots, 1913; lieut., 1916; caps., K.A.R., 1918; served in Nyasaland and Portuguese E. Africa, 1917-19; 2nd asst. crown sol., Hong Kong, Dec., 1923; J.P., 1924; asst. crown sol., 1926; ag. mag., Kowloon, 1929 and 1930; asst. crown sol., 1930; ag. land offr. and registr., marriages, 1934; land offr. and registr., marriages, 1935.

WICKHAM, Capt. Dennis Henry.—B. 1896; ed. Winchester Coll. and New Coll., Oxford; Connaught Rangers, Sept., 1914; served B.E.F., France, 1915-18; mentd. in despatches; transfd. to Queen's Royal Regt., 1922; served with 5th K.A.R., Kenya, 1922-25; admive. offr., Kenya col.; seconded to Somaliland, Apr., 1929.

WICKHAM, Dudley Lee Borrett.—B. 1909; ed. Br. Guiana and Barbados; cler., asst. secretariat, Br. Guiana, Apr., 1927; asst. clk., leg. coun., Oct., 1930; 4th cls. clk., secretariat, Jan., 1933; pte. sec. to O. A. G., May to Oct., 1933; ag. clk., leg. coun., June and July, 1933; has been secy. to various comms. and bds.; clk., leg. coun., Oct., 1935; asst. dist. commr., 1938.

WICKHART, W. A. F.—B. 1888; clk. crown agents' office, 1906; asst. head, gen. dept., Mar., 1922; do., stores dept., July, 1931; do., head, shipping dept., Nov., 1934; asst. sec., W. African currency bd., June, 1914; secy., Dec., 1918.

WICKRAMASINGHE, Reginald Hugh M.A. (Cantab.), B.Sc. (Lond.).—B. 1907; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Nov., 1930; office asst., Anuradhapura kach., Feb., 1932; do., Kegalla kach., Aug., 1934; do., Mannar and Mullaitivu kachs., Nov., 1935; do., Anuradhapura kach., Mar., 1936; asst. sec. to min. for agr. and lands, Oct., 1936; admin. sec., cent. bd. of agr. and add. to office of dir., agr., Aug., 1937.

WICKREMESINGHE, Cyril Leonard.—B. 1890; cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., Apr., 1912; att. Badulla kach., Apr., 1912; Matara kach., Oct., 1912; office asst. to govt. agt., Uva, July, 1913; addtl. pol. mag., Colombo, Negombo and Avissawella, June, 1916; pol. mag., Kalutara, Mar., 1917; pol. mag., Jaffna, Mar., 1920; addnl. astt. govt. agt., Colombo, May, 1921; astt. govt. agt., Manuar, Mar., 1923; dist. judge, Nuwara Eliya, Nov., 1925; astt. govt. agt., Puttalam, Oct., 1926; ditto, Kalutara, Mar., 1928; ag. govt. agt., N.C.P., July, 1931; addnl. commr., lands, Sept., 1934; commr., lands, Apr., 1935.

WIDDUP, COLONEL CHARLES PONSONBY, V.D.—B. 1884; clerical astt., treasy., B. Guiana, 1902; 6th cls. clk., lands and mines dept., 1903; ag. sub-inspr. of police, 1903; sub-inspr. of police, 1904; dist. inspr. of police, 1908; passed Hindustani exam., 1909; county inspr. of police, 1914; dep. inspr. gen. and supt., fire brig. B. Guiana, Dec., 1920; internat. prison confinee, 1920; ag. inspr.-gen. pol. and commdt., local forces in 1924, 1926, 1927, 1930 and 1933; ag. inspr., prisons in addn., July, Nov., 1933; King's pol. med., 1934; inspr.-gen. pol., and comdt., local forces, 1937; title changed to commr.-pol., 1938.

WIGG, LEONARD G.T.—B. 1900; ed. Bishop's Stortford Grammar Schl. and Oxford Univ.; army, 1918; Rhodes schol., 1921; B.A. (forestry), B.A. (hons. nat. sci.), dip. forestry (distinct.), astt. conserv., forests, Tanganyika, 1924; secnded as silviculturist, Zanzibar agr. dept., 1932.

WIGGETT, ARCHIBALD DOUGLAS.—B. 1884; law and income tax depts., Cape Col., 1901-10; census office, 1911; accts. br. dept. of int., 1912; income tax dept., 1914; inland rev. dept., 1916; recr., rev., Kimberley, 1931; accts., 1931; recr., rev., E. London, 1932; ch. clk., income tax, Feb., 1933; recr., rev., Pretoria, 1933; ch. rev. offr., June, 1934.

WIGLEY, WILFRID MURRAY, O.B.E. (1931).—Ed. Derby schl.; held various clerical appts. under govt., St. Kitts-Nevis, Feb., 1895, to Apr., 1898; called to the bar, Mid. Temp., Jan., 1901; practised at bar of Leeward Is. for some years; ag. admstr., St. Kitts, puisne judge, Leeward Is. and atty.-gen., on various occasions; crown atty., St. Kitts-Nevis, 1918; 1st puisne judge, Leeward Is., Jan., 1935; ch. just., 1937.
WILBY, GEORGE STAINBURN, B.A. (Hons. (Oxon).—B. 1905; supt., educn., Nigeria, 1928; astt. mast., educn. dept., Hong Kong, 1935.

WILFORD, SIR THOMAS MASON, K.C.M.G. (1930), K.C. (1929).—B. 1870; ed. Christ Coll., Canterbury, N.Z.; M.P. for Wellington Suburbs, 1900 and for Hutt from 1903-29; chmn., Wellington harbr. bd., 1908-9 (mem. 10 years); min. of just., marine, etc., in national govt., two years; leader of opposition and liberal party, 1920-25; min. of just. and defence, 1928-29; high commr. for N.Z. in London, 1929-33.
WILKIN, B. O., M.B., Ch.B., D.P.H. (Edin.).—B. 1889; mily. serv., 1914-20; med. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Aug., 1925.

WILKINSON, GODFREY—B. 1901; ed. Hertford Coll., Oxford; B.A. (Oxon.); dip. forestry, R.N. Colls.; astt. conserv., forests, F.M.S., Oct., 1922; dist. forest offr., May, 1923; ag. conserv., forests, Kedah, Nov., 1930; state forest offr., Pahang, Mar., 1934; dist. forest offr., K. Kangsar, Dec., 1934.
WILKINSON, HAROLD HOWARD, M.A. (Dublin).—B. 1894; ed. Trinity Coll., Dublin; admstve. offr., cls. IV, Nigeria, 1921; cls. III, 1924; cls. II, 1936; ag. rea., in 1935, 1936 and 1937.

WILKINSON, HENRY CAMPBELL.—B. 1893; B.A. (Yale), M.D. (Columbia), D.H., D.T.M. & H. Caneb. Presbyterian Hosp., New York City, 1919-21; med. supt., King Edward VII hosp., Bermuda, 1922-6; senr. med. offr., 1927; author of "Adventurers of Bermuda."

WILKINSON, PAUL BIDDULPH, M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P., Lond., M.B., B.S., Lond., M.R.C.P., Lond.—B. 1901; W.A.M.S., 1930; 2nd med. offr., St. Helena, 1934; ag. senr. med. offr., 1936; med. offr., Hong Kong, 1937.

WILLAN, HAROLD CURWEN, M.C., B.A. (Oxon.), Barrister-at-Law, Inner Temple.—B. 1896. ed. Kendall Gram. Schl. and Jesus Coll., Oxford (open exhibr.); lieut., R.G.A.; M.C., June, 1917; cadet, F.M.S., Nov., 1920; passed cadet, Jan., 1923; offr., cls. V, Oct., 1923; dist. offr., Jelebu, Nov., 1923; astt. dist. offr., K. Kangsar, Aug., 1925; offr., cls. IV, Oct., 1927; examur. in law for State of Perak, Nov., 1927; ag. registr., dist. judge and 1st mag., Malacca, Jan., 1932; offr., cls. III, Oct., 1932; ag. dep. legal advr., F.M.S., July, 1934; solr.-gen., Kenya, 1936; ag. atty.-gen., 1937.

WILLBOURN, ERIC STEWART, M.A. (Cantab.), F.G.S.—B. 1889; astt. geologist, F.M.S., May, 1914; 2nd lieut., R.E. (Signal.), Apr., 1918; demob., May, 1919; ag. geologist, F.M.S., Nov., 1922-Jly., 1923 and Jly., 1927-Feb., 1928; dir., geolox. survey, F.M.S., Dec., 1931.
WILLOCK, HON. JOHN COLLINGS.—B. 1879; M.P., Western Australia, 1917; min. for rlys. and just., 1924-30 and 1933-36; premier of W.A. since 1936.

WILCOCKS, MAJOR JAMES LUGARD, D.S.O., M.C.—B. 1893; ed. Cheltenham Coll. and R.M.C., Sandhurst; 2nd lieut., The Black Watch, Feb., 1912; A.D.C. to G.O.C., N. Army, India, July, 1913; A.D.C. to G.O.C. Indian Army Corps, France, Sept., 1914; (twice ment. in despatchs); brig. maj., 1916; brig. maj., Bermuda, Mar., 1919; ret., Feb., 1922; (placed on R. of O.), A.D.C., Kenya, Feb., 1925; ag. sec., native affrs. dept., May, 1928 to Sept., 1930; pvt. sec. to ag. gov., 1930-31; astt. commr., prisons, Apr., 1932; commr., prisons, 1933; do., Hong Kong, 1938.

WIMBUSH, Samuel Humphrey.—B. 1903; ed. Haileybury and St. John's Coll., Oxford, B.A., dip. forestry; asst. conserv., forests, Kenya, Oct., 1925.

WINCHESTER, John Wishart, M.D., Ch.B. (St. Andrews), D.M.R.E. (Camb.), Cert. L.S.T.M. (with dist.),—B. 1901; ed. Harris Acad., Dundee and St. Andrews Univ.; M.O. Malaya, Sept., 1927; port health offr., S'pore, Apr., 1929; med. & health offr., Labuan, Jan. 1929; M.O. and ast., radiological dept., S'pore, May, 1933; radiologist, Sel., Jan., 1935.

WINDHAM, RALPH, M.A., LL.B. (Cantab.).—B. 1905; ed. Wellington Coll. and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; 1st class hon.; law trips; called to bar, 1930, (Lincoln's Inn, Buchanan prizeman); legal draftsman, Palestine, May, 1935.
WINDRUM, JAMES EDWARD.—B. 1895; cadet, Fiji, 1921; passed cadet, 1924; dist. commr., 1925; ag. ch. pol. mag., 1929.
WINSER, CYRIL LEIGH, C.M.G. (1928), M.V.O. (1920).—Priv. sec. to govt. of S. Australia since May, 1916.

WINSON, HERBERT WILLIAM, F.I.S.A.—B. 1890; ed. Trifins Schol., Kingston-on-Thames, Surrey and Schol. of Accountancy, Glasgow; head acctnt., Usain Gisha Rly., Kenya, 1921-23; ch. acctnt., 1923-26; acctnt. and audr., rly. dept., Mauritius, 1927-29; ch. acctnt., 1929; ag. gen. man., Dec., 1934 to Sept., 1935; prin. ast. to ch. acctnt., Nigerian Rly., open lines, Apr., 1938.

WINSON, VICTOR HANOTT, B.Sc., A.K.C., M.I.E.E.—B. 1887; ed. Tiffins Schol., Kingston-on-Thames and King's Coll., London (1st cls. hon., elect. engrg.); 2nd cls. engrg., G.P.O., engrg. dept., Oct., 1909; ast. engrg., July, 1911; supdt., posts and tels., Malacca, Sept., 1913; war serv., Aug., 1917-Apr., 1919; supdt., posts and tels., Jan., 1922; senr. engrg., posts and tels., S.S. and F.M.S., Oct., 1926; senr. engrg., posts and tels., Penang, July, 1928; ditto, F.M.S. and S.S., Oct., 1930; ast. engrg.-in-ch., P. and T., S.S. and F.M.S., Dec., 1930; engrg.-in-ch., do., 1933; dir., tels. and phones, and engrg.-in-ch., P. and T., S.S. and F.M.S., Jan., 1934.

WINSTEADT, SIR RICHARD OLAF, K.B.E. (1935), C.M.G. (1926), M.A., Oxon., D. Litt., Oxon.—B. 1878; ed. at Magdalen College School and New Coll., Oxford; 2nd cls. mods.; 2nd cls. Lit. Hum., M.A.; cadet, F.M.S., Nov., 1902; passed cadet, 1904; class V., Jan., 1910; sec. to comtese. for Malay studies in addn., Sept., 1913; class IV., confirmed dist. offr., Dec., 1913; federal examr. in Malay in addn., Dec., 1913; class III., ast. dir., educn. S.S. and F.M.S., June, 1917; ag. chmn., sany. bd., Kinta, June to Aug., 1919, in addn.; cls. II., Nov., 1919; fed. examr. in Malay, S. Stilts., and F.M.S., June, 1920; seconded to act as prin., Raffles' Coll., S'pore, June, 1921; offg. as sec. to high comtnr. in addn.; Fed. examr. in Malay in addn., Mar., 1923; dir., educn., S.S. and F.M.S., cls. Ia, July, 1924; chmn., postal serv. comtese. in addn., July, 1924; chmn., tech. educn. comtese. in addn., Nov., 1925; rep., F.M.S., govt. at C.O. confoc., Apr. to July, 1927; chmn., comtese., med. research, S.S. and F.M.S. in addn., Aug., 1928; chmn., Malay delegation to 4th Pacfic serv. cong., Batavia, May, 1929; rep. F.M.S. at C.O. confoc., June, 1930; gen. advr., Johore, Oct., 1931; ret., 1935; author of an "English-Malay Dictionary" (3 vols.), a "Malay Grammar" (Clarendon Press), "Colloquial Malay," a "Malay Reader" (Clarendon Press), "Malayan Memories," "Shaman, Sawa and Sufi," a study of Malay magic (London), editor and part author of "Malaya" (London, 1922), and author of various pamphlets published by the F.M.S. govt. on Malay subjects; editor of many Malay folk-tales and (with R. J. Wilkinson, C.M.G.) of Pantun Melaya.

WINSTON, V. A.—B. 1903; ag. 3rd cls., post office, Dominica, 1920; ast. cls., P.W.D., Sept., 1920; jnr. audit, cls., Sept., 1924; ag. audit cls., Jan.-Apr., 1927; senr. cls., treasy., St. Kitts-Nevis, 1934.
WIRASINHA, VICTOR LLOYD, B.A. (Hons.) Lond.—B. 1913; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Jan., 1935; offr. cls. II, 1937.

WISE, WILFRED, M.Inst.C.E., O.B.E. (1936), B.Sc. (Hons. Lond.), M.I.Mun.Cy.E., A.C.G.I.—B. 1886; ed. privtly, and City and Guilds of London Cent. Tech. Coll., Univ. of Lond.; ast. engrg., P.W.D., Fiji, May, 1921; ag. comtnr., wks., Aug., 1923; comtnr., wks., Jan., 1926; on sp. duty, Tonga, May-July, 1930; chmn., cent. road bd., M.L.C.; chmn., town planning comte., and beaconing comte., 1930; chmn., traffic control bd., 1934; title changed to D.P.W., 1936; offr. M.L.C., 1937.

WISERMAN, R. A.—B. 1886; ed. Monmouth Gram. Schol. and Oxford; math. demy, Magdalen Coll., 1905-1909; 2nd cls. math. mods., 1906; 2nd cls. hist., 1908; 2nd cls. jurisprudence, 1909; apptd. after compet. exam., cls., sec.'s dept., Admiralty, Mar., 1910; 2nd cls. cls., C.O., Moh., 1911; sec. to overseas prize disposal comte., Nov., 1914; temporarily served at ministry of shipping, Jan., 1917 to Mar., 1919; sec. to international shipping comte., Feb., 1917; sec. to inter-allied chartering comte., May, 1917; priv. sec. to parly. U.S. of S. for the Cols. (Mr. Amery), 1st Apr., 1919; 1st cls. cls., 1st Jan., 1920; prin., 1st Apr., 1920; accompanied parly. U.S. of S. for the Cols. (Hon. E. F. L. Wood) on visit to W. Indies, Dec., 1921-Feb., 1922; rep. U.K. govt. at W. Indian confoc., Ottawa, June-July, 1925; ast. sec., 18th Sept., 1927; D.O., 1928.

WODEHOUSE, Lieut.-Gen. Sir JOSCHLINE HENRAGE, C.G.B. (1913), K.C.B. (1908), C.B. (1889), C.M.G. (1890).—B. 1852; ed. at R.M.A., Woolwich; comtnr. in R.A., 1872; served in Zulu war, 1879; Afghan war, 1880; Soudan, 1883-1894; gov. of Eng. frontier, 1887-1894; commd. "I" battery, R.H.A., 1894-1898; with Malakand field force (severely wounded), 1898; in action with Egyptian forces at Argin; infantry div. at Toski; Presidency dist., 1898-1899; Secunderabad, 1900-1; Lahore, 1902; Rawal Pindi, 1903; ag. in comd. of W. Comd., 1905; Zulu war (medal and clasp); Soudan war (medal, 3 clasps); N.W. Frontier (medal and clasp); 2nd cls. Medjidiye; 2nd cls. Osmanieh; Egyptian bronze star; Order of Palm, French Govt.; gov. of Bermuda, 1907-1908; late G.O.C. Northern Army, India.

WODEHOUSE, R. E., O.R.E., M.D., D.P.H.—B. 1883; ed. pub. schls. and Toronto Univ.; on mil. serv., Canada, France and England, 1914-19; prov. dist. med. offr., Fort William and Port Arthur in 1912 and again in 1919; exec. sec., Canadian Tuberculosis Assoc., 1921; dep. min., dept. of pensions and national health, Dom. of Canada, 1933.

WODEMAN, GUY STANLEY.—B. 1886; ed. Rossall schl. and Jesus Coll., Camb. (M.A.); cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Nov., 1909; office ast. to govt. agt., Sabaragamuwa, Oct., 1910; pol. mag., Matale, Feb., 1912; ast. comtnr. of excise attd., the office, excise comtnr., Apr., 1913; ast. comtnr., excise, Cent. Div., Jan., 1914; ast. govt. agt., Kandy, Apr., 1915; astd. ch. censor's office, Sept., 1915; ast. govt. agt., Trincomalee, Apr., 1916; ditto, Colombo, Oct., 1918; ditto, Nuwara Eliya, Dec., 1919; ditto, Kegalla, Dec., 1921; ditto, Matara, Mar., 1923; dep. collr., cust., Apr., 1925; dep. comtnr., excise, Nov., 1926; comtnr., excise, Sept., 1927; govt. agt., S.P., Nov., 1930; prin. collr., cust., and chmn.. Colombo port comm., Nov., 1931; ag. dep. ch. sec., Dec., 1934-Mar., 1935 and June-Nov., 1936; dep. ch. sec., Jan., 1937; ag. ch. sec., May-Oct., 1937.

WOLFE, HENRY, O.B.E. (1935), M.Sc.(Agric.), Cornell Univ., U.S.A.—B. 1887; tech. asst., dept. of agr., S. Africa, 1916; dep. dir., agr., Tanganyika Territory, 1920; ag. dir., agr., Feb. to Dec., 1921 and Mar. to Nov., 1924; E. Africa campaign 1916-17; dep. dir. agr. (plant industry), Kenya, 1931; ag. dir., agr., 1933 and 1937.

WOLLASTON, ALFRED CHRISTOPHER.—B. 1893; ed. Elmhurst Sch.; asst. sup't., P. & T. dept., F.M.S., Oct., 1923; sup't., Penang, July, 1928; do., Spore, Sept., 1934; personal asst. to dir.-gen., Feb., 1936.

WOMACK, OSWALD CEDRIC, P.A.S.I.—B. 1892; ed. St. Paul's Sch., and Leeds Univ.; temp. capt., R.A.S.C. and Rifle Brig., 1915-20; asst. sup't., surveys, Ceylon, 1921-24; 2nd cls. survr., P.W.D., Hong Kong, 1924; land survr., do., 1932.

WOOD, BERNARD READER.—B. 1890; ed. Tonbridge Sch., Sid. Suss. Coll. Camb.; M.A., dipl. in forestry; asst. conserv. Indian forest serv., 1914; dep. conserv., 1918; conserv. of forests, Br. Guiana, 1925; reverted to Indian forest serv., Oct., 1927; conserv., forests, Br. Guiana, Jan., 1928; mem., bd. of examrs., Hindi and Urdu, Apr., 1928; ag. off. mem., leg. coun., Aug., 1928; chinm., local govt. bd., 1929-30; off. mem., exec. coun., Apr., 1930; do., leg. coun., Jan., 1931; J.P., Aug., 1932.
WOOD, FRANCIS WILLIAM.—B. 1887; ed. Queen Elizabeth Gram. Sch., Wakefield; 2nd cls. survr., P.W.D., Hong Kong, 1914; land survr., 1921.

WOOD, HARRY.—B. 1895; ed. Bearne Sch., and Tech. Sch., Newton Abbot; G.W.R., 1910-14; R.E., 1914-20; S. American Rly., 1920-27; Crown Agts., 1927-32; asst. loco. sup't., K.U.R. and H., 1933; dist. loco. sup't., cls. I., 1938.

WOOD, HARRY EDWIN.—B. 1881; ed. Manchester Grammar Sch., and Owens Coll., Manchester. (B.Sc., 1902, M.Sc., 1905); asst. lect., dept. of physics, Manchester Univ., 1905; ch. asst., Transvaal meteorological dept., 1906; ch. asst., Union observatory, Johannesburg, 1912; served, German E. Africa campaign, 1916; Union astronomer, Jan., 1928.

WOOD, JOHN, C.M.G. (1938), M.I.C.E.—B. 1880; ed. State schl., Timaru, and Timaru Boys High schl.; entd. N.Z. pub. wks. dept., 1900; asst. engr., 1904; ag. res. engr., Nelson, 1905; engr.-in-ch., Arthur's Pass tunnel constrn., 1907; engr.-in-ch., Westport dist., 1909; dist. engr., N. Auckland, 1912; inspgr. engr. for N.Z., 1920; asst. engr.-in-ch., 1934; under-sec. and engr-in-ch., pub. wks. dept. and chmn., main highways bd., 1936.

WOOD, LESLIE WILLIAM.—B. 1890; ed. Merchant Taylors' schl. and Queen's coll., Camb.; 1st cls. math. tripos, pt. I.; 2nd cls. math. tripos., pt. 2; B.A., 1912; asst. dist. commnr., Gold Coast, Aug., 1914; ag. dist. commnr., 1914-20; asst. col. sec., Jan., 1921; ag. clk. of couns. for various periods, 1922 and 1929-32; senr. asst. col. sec., 1930; ag. prin. asst. col. sec. in 1931 and 1932.

WOOD, CAPT. REGINALD CECIL MERVYN.—B. 1889; E. African Expedy. Force, 1915-17; seconded, K.A.R., 1918; K.A.R. staff, 1919; adjt., 3rd K.A.R., 1919-22; ooy. commdr., 1923; asst. treas., Kenya, 1923; estab. offr., secretariat, Mar., 1928.
WOOD, RONALD MACKAY, B.Sc. (Eng.) (Manchester), A.M.Inst.C.E., Chld. Civil Engr.—B. 1906; ed. Bradfield Coll. and Manchester Univ.; engr., P.W.D., Hongkong, 1928.

WOOD, S. T.—B. 1889; ed. Upper Canada Coll., and R. Mily. Coll., Kingston, Ontario; inspr., R.N.W.M. pol., Nov., 1912; supt., Oct., 1931; asst. commr., Oct., 1933; ag. dep. commr., Feb., 1938; commr., Mar., 1938.

WOODHOUSE, LIONEL GILBERT OLLEYET.—B. 1888; supernum. survr., Ceylon, Aug., 1905; 3rd gde. survr., May, 1908; asst. supt. survrs., Jan., 1910; mily. survr., Oct., 1918-Mar., 1919; supt., block survrs., Feb., 1928; asst. gen., Feb., 1932; dep. survr-gen., May, 1936; survr-gen., June, 1937.

WOODYING, JOHN CONRAD—B. 1901; ed. Harrison Coll., and Codrington Coll., Barbados; B.A., Durham; clas. hon., 1922; M.A., 1927; senr. asst. mast., Gram. Sch., St. Kitts, Aug., 1922; ag. headmr., Montserrat, and St. Kitts, various periods, 1927-33, mem., Middle Temple, Nov., 1933; ag. postmr., St. Kitts, Dec., 1935; reglar. and provost marshal St. Kitts and addl. mag., dists. O.D.H.K., Dec., 1936; mem., pub. tenders comttee, and income tax comttee, May, 1936; called to bar, Middle Temple, Jan., 1938; ag. mag., dist. D., St. Kitts, June, 1938.
WOODMAN, RAYMOND HENRY, B.Sc., Eng. (Lond.), Dipl. Civ. and Munic. Engng., Univ. Coll., Lond.—B. 1903; engr., P.W.D., Hong Kong, 1929.

WOODMAN, WILLIAM JAMES.—B. 1872; ed. pte. schl., Winchester Coll. and St. Mary's Hosp.; M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P., L.S.A.; attd. to R.A.M.C. at Fort Pitt Hosp., Chatham, during S. African War; London Sch. of Trop. Med. exam., 1919; med. offr. and radiologist and dist. comntr., Br. Honduras, 1910-15; med. offr., hosp., gaol, etc., Hong Kong; seconded for serv. in war, 1918-19; capt., R.A.M.C. (T.), in charge of sanitary company, Salonica and Constantinople; in charge, Belize hosp., Jan. to June, 1923; Bahamas med. offr., Out Islands, June to Sept., 1928; ag. res. surg. and radiologist, Bahamas, Sept., 1928-Aug., 1929; ag. dir. med. services, Aug. to Nov., 1929.

WOODWARD, WILLIAM, B.Sc. (Eng.), A.M. Inst.C.E.—B. 1892; ed. Nuneaton Gram. Schl. and Birmingham Univ.; served Gt. War, 1915-19; engr., P.W.D., Hong Kong, 1919; ag. exec. engr., waterwks. constrn., 1930, 1932-5 and 1937-38; ag. exec. engr., waterwks. maint., 1936.

WOOLLEY, CHARLES CAMPBELL, C.M.G. (1937), O.B.E. (1934), M.C.—B. 1893; on mily. survr., Aug., 1914; cadet, Ceylon civ. survr., May, 1921; attd., Kandy Kach., June, 1921; 4th asst. col. sec., Feb., 1924; 3rd asst. col. sec., Mar., 1925; temp. attd. C.O., 1925 to June, 1926; addnl. asst. col. sec., Sept., 1926; 3rd asst. col. sec., Oct., 1926; asst. govt. agt., N'Elwa, May, 1927; asst. col. sec. and clk., leg. coun., Sept., 1928; prin. asst. col. sec. and clk., exec. coun., Nov., 1930; sec. to gov., July, 1931; col. sec., Jamaica, Aug., 1935; ch. sec., Nigeria, 1938.

WORKMAN, JOHN EDWARD—B. 1897; served Gloucestershire Regt., King's Liverpool Regt. R.F.C., R.A.F., 1915-1918 (Mesopotamia, India and Egypt); dist. inspr. and intell. off., R.I.C., 1920, platoon commdr., R. Ulster sp. constab., 1921; asst. commr., pol., Gold Coast, Jan., 1925; commr., Jan., 1933; commr., pol., Fiji, 1937; comdt., Fiji defence force, at M.L.C., 1937.

WORLEY, NEWNHAM ARTHUR.—B. 1892; ed. Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge, B.A., 1913; barrister-at-law, Inner Temple; cadet, F.M.S., Nov., 1914; asst. contr. of lab., Kampar, July, 1917; asst. dist. offr., Ipoh, May, 1920; asst. prot. of Chinese, Negri Sembilan and ag. dep. contr. of lab., Seremban, Aug., 1920; asst. prot. of Chinese, Perak, Dec., 1921; asst. dir. of educn. (Chinese), F.M.S., June, 1922; offr., cls. III, Nov. 1926; asst. dir., educn. (Chinese), F.M.S. and S.S., in 1927 and 1930; 1st mag., Ipoh, Nov., 1931, offr. cls. II; regiar., sup. ct., K. Lumpur, May, 1932; D.P.P., S'pore, Mar., 1934; offr. cls. IB, Jan., 1935; solr. gen., S'pore, Mar., 1936; ag. atty.-gen., in 1936 and 1937.

WORLEDGE, JOHN LEONARD.—B. 1895; ed. St. Paul's Sch.; active service in France, Sept., 1914 to Sept., 1918; ment. in despatches, Ireland, 1919; asst. audr., Kenya, Apr., 1920; asst. audr. in charge, Zanzibar, May 1920 to Feb., 1921; ag. senr. asst. audr., Kenya, Mar., 1923; senr. asst. audr., Nov., 1925; seconded to Seychelles, Oct., 1925 to Jan., 1926; on sp. duty, Seychelles, Oct.–Nov., 1929; audr., Br. Guiana, May, 1933; audr.-gen., Jamaica, 1936.
WORRELL, CECIL THEOPHILUS WILFRED EVERARD.—B. 1894; called to bar, Gray's Inn, 1916; mag., St. Patrick, Trinidad, Jan., 1932; do., St. George East, Jan., 1934; asst. law offr., 1936.

WORSLEY, RICHARD ROBERT LE GETT Ph.D. (Lond.), D.I.C., A.R.C.S., B.Sc.—B.1899; ed. St. Paul's and B. Coll. of Sci.; R.E.O.C.B., 1918-1919; sub. dir., chem. sect., miny. of agr., Egypt, 1926; biochemist, E. African agr. research sta., Amani, Tanganyika Territory, 1928.

WORTH, HELEN ISOBEL (née Robertson), M.A., M.D., D.C.O.G.—Lady med. offr., K. Kangsar, Mar., 1927; do., Teluk Anson, May, 1927; do., K. Lumpur, May, 1928; do., K. Pilah, Dec., 1930; do., Penang, Aug., 1935.
WORTHINGTON, GRAHAM.—B. 1902; ed. St. Michael's Crewe; asst. gov't. printer, Sierra Leone, May, 1928; ag. gov't. printer in 1928-33; supt., printing dept., Gold Coast, May, 1933.

WORTLEY, EDWARD JOCKLYN, C.M.G. (1935), O.B.E. (1926), M.B.E.—B. 1884; ed. Jamaica Coll.; course in agric. and agric. chem. at Jamaica, govt. lab. and sp. course at the Ontario agric. coll.; fellow of the chem. socy., 1906; jnr. asst. chem., Jamaica, 1902; asst. chem., 1905; lect. in agric. sci., 1906; headmar., govt. farm schl. and stock farm, 1909; comsnr. for Jamaica, Canadian Nat. Exhibn., Sept., 1910, and Sept., 1912; dir. of agr., Bermuda, 1913-20; food comsnr., 1917; hon. sec., internat. comm. on potato industry, 1919; visited Canary Is. on behalf of Bermuda govt., 1920; dir. of agr., Nyassaland, Dec., 1920; dir. agr., Trinidad, 1930; author of "Companion to Blackie's Tropical Readers," "Poultry Diseases," and "Foods and Food Products of Jamaica."

WRIGHT, MAJOR ANDREW BARKWORTH, C.B.E. (1932), M.A., M.C. (1917), Bar, 1918, R. of O.—B. 1895; ed. Haileybury and Jesus Coll., Cambridge (schol.); served in European War with Suffolk Regt., 1914-19; major, 1918; asst. sec., ch. sec's. office, Cyprus, June, 1922; asst. priv. sec. to O.A.G., June to Oct., 1923; ag. priv. sec. to high comsnr., and ag. priv. sec. and A.D.C. to O.A.G., on various occasions, 1924-26; ag. col. sec. for various periods, 1927-37; ch. asst. sec., col. sec's. office, Jan., 1930; ag. comsnr., Mar.–June, 1932; ag. treas., Aug.–Nov., 1934; ag. Br. del. of Evkaf, 1934; admstve. offr.: ch. asst. sec., Jan., 1935; col. sec., Apr., 1937; ag. gov., July-Aug., 1938.

WRIGHT, ARTHUR ALBAN, C.M.G., (1937), B.A.—B. 1887; ed. St. Edmund's Sch., Canterbury; open exhibn., St. John's Coll., Oxford, 1906; lieut., 3rd Bn. Bedfordshire Regt. (S.R.), 1909; 2nd cls. hons. schl., mod. hist., Oxford, 1910; B.A., 1910; cadet, Fiji, 1912; ag. sub-inspr., constab., 1912; passed cadet, 1914; 2nd grade dist. comsnr., 1915; dist. comsnr., Nadi, 1915; do., Colo East, 1916; on mily. duty, 1917; 2nd lieut., 5th Bn. Rifle Brigade, 1917; on active serv., France, 1917; dist. comsnr., Macuata, 1920; ditto (1st grade), Nadroga and prov. comsnr., Nadroga and Colo West, 1922; ditto, Colo North, 1923; ag. asst. col. sec., Jan., 1927; asst. col. sec., Jan., 1928; nom. mem., leg. coun., 1929; 1st asst. col. sec., Jan., 1931; sec. for native affrs., Mar., 1932; mem., exec. and leg. couns.; ag. col. sec. in 1932, 1933, 1934 and 1935; gov't. dep. on several occasions, 1932-35; ag. gov., Mar.–Aug., 1935; admstr. and col. sec., St. Vincent, June, 1936; admstr., St. Lucia, July, 1938; ag. gov., Windward Is., 19th Sept.–1st Oct., 1938; gov't. dep., 17th–19th Sept. and 2nd–4th Oct., 1938.

WRIGHT, CHARLES HAROLD.—M.A., F.I.C., F.C.S.; B. 1880; scholar, Sidney Sussex Coll., Cambridge; B.A. (Nat. Sc. Tripod), 1902; M.A. 1916; asst. analyst and science master, Trinidad, Apr., 1908; ag. gov't. analyst and prof. of chemistry, Trinidad, on two occasions; agric. chemist, Fiji, Mar., 1914; ag. dist. comsnr., Kadavu, July–Aug., 1918; govt. chem., Fiji, Jan., 1920; senr. agr. chem., Nigeria, June, 1923.

WRIGHT, OWEN FRANKLIN.—B. 1887; R.I.C. depot, 1908; sub-inspr., Jamaica constab., Nov., 1908; 3rd cls. inspr., June, 1911; 2nd cls. inspr., Aug., 1919; 1st cls. inspr., June, 1924; inspr.-gen., pol., Nov., 1932.

WRIGHT-NOOTH, CAPT. RODNEY GEORGE, M.C. (1916), A.M.I.C.E.—B. 1884; ed. St. Paul's Schl., stud. engnr. faculty, King's Coll., London, 1904-08; 2nd lieut., R.E. (S.R.), 1909; engr. on survey and constrn. of Magadi rly. and water wks., 1911-14; R.E., 1914-22; served, Kenya, German E. Africa and Jubaland; severely wounded twice, ment. in despatches, M.C.; div. offr., R.E., Londonderry and Enniskillen Divs., Ireland, Apr., 1920 to Apr., 1922; capt., Reg. Army, R. of O., 1922; supt. pub. wks., Antigua, July, 1922; mem., leg. coun., Antigua, Aug., 1922; asst. D.P.W., B. Guiana, Dec., 1926; ag. D.P.W. in 1927, 1929 and 1931; dep. D.P.W., N. Rhodesia, Nov., 1931; ag. D.P.W. in 1933, 1934 and 1935; res. engr. in ch., constrn. of Lusaka, Sept., 1933-Aug., 1935; col. engr., St. Lucia, Feb., 1936.

WUPPERMAN, RUDOLF CARL, M.B., Ch.B., Edin.—B. 1883; entered med. serv., Trinidad, Oct., 1909; asst. res. surg., colonial hosp., Port of Spain, Oct., 1914; res. surg., col. hosp., May, 1923; dist. med. offr., 1929.
WURTH, WALLACE CHARLES, LL.B. (Syd.).—B. 1896; asst. under-seo. and industrial regis., dept. of lab. and industry, N.S.W., Aug., 1932; mem., pub. service bd., Jan., 1936.

WYATT, ALBERT WILLIAM.—B. 1900; ed. Leamington Coll. and R.M.C., Sandhurst; lieut., Hampshire Regt., 1919-28; 2nd K.A.R., 1923-26; cadet, Tanganyika Territory, Sept., 1926; asst. dist. offr., Oct., 1928; ag. dist. offr., various periods, 1930-38.

WYATT, WILLIAM HENRY.—B. 1892; ed. Lond. City & Guilds Inst.; war serv., 1914-19; H.M.S.O., Gr. Br., Aug., 1922; supt., printing Palestine, Mar., 1923; dep. supt. govt. ptg. office, S.S., July, 1933; supt. ptg., dept., F.M.S., July, 1935.

WYNNE, MERVYN LLEWELYN.—B. 1891; ed. Corrig Sch., Ireland, Ellesmere Coll., Shropshire, and in Hanover, Munich and Edinburgh; Malayan pol. serv., 1911; ast. comsnr., pol., Selangor, 1915; 2nd lieut., Rifle Brigade, 1916; served in France 1917-18; comdt., pol. depot, Kuala Lumpur, 1920; comanr., pol., Trengganu, 1923; dep. comanr., pol., and ag. dir., crim. intell., F.M.S., 1928; comsnr., pol., Johore, 1929; dir., crim. intell., S.S., June, 1931; dir., ap. br., S.S., June, 1931 and Nov., 1933; offg. ch. pol. offr., Perak, Mar., 1933; dir., ap. branch, S.S., Nov., 1933; senr., supt., and senr. dep. comanr., Malayan pol. serv., 1936; ag. ch. pol. offr., S'pore, June, 1936.
WYNNE, COMDR. WALTER ROBERT MORANT, R.N. (Retd.).—B. 1885; King's harbr. mast., Bermuda, 1924-26; harbr. and shipping mast., Barbados, 1928.

WYNNE-JONKS, EDWARD IRVINE.—B. 1895; ed. Oxford and Univ. of Wales; cadet, Hong Kong, July, 1920; dist. offr., South, June, 1922; attd. col. secretariat, Nov., 1922; passed final exam., Feb., 1923; dist. offr., North, May, 1923; ditto, South, Dec., 1926; attd., col. secretariat, July, 1928; pol. mag., Kowloon, 1928; extra asst. col. sec., Nov., 1928; 2nd asst. col. sec., and elk. of couns., in 1929; dist. offr., North, 1930; 2nd pol. mag., Feb., 1932 and Nov., 1934; pol. mag., Kowloon, 1934 and Mar., 1935; P.M.G., Dec., 1936.

YATES, GEO. W.—B. 1872; ed. London pub. and high schls.; ent. civ. serv. of Ont. as priv. sec. to various mins., from Feb., 1899 to Oct., 1911; accompanied Hon. F. Cochran, Ont. min. of lands, forests and mines, to Ottawa on latter accepting portfolio of min. of rlys. and canals in Borden govt.; cont. priv. sec., min. of rlys. and canals until formation of union govt., 1917, when became ch. priv. sec. to Sir Robt. Borden; ast. deputy minister of railways and canals, 1920.

YATES, JOSEPH MERVYN ST. JOHN, B.A.—B. 1878; ed. Winchester Coll. and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; called to the bar, Inner Temple, May, 1903; northern circuit, 1903-15; H.M. Forces, 1915-19; regisr.-gen., Bahamas, Feb., 1920; ag. stip. mag., Mar., 1920; ag. atty.-gen., Nov., 1920; ag. ch. just., Feb.-Mar., 1921; ag. stip. and circuit mag., Mar., 1921 to Sept., 1922; seconded for serv. as ag. asst. to atty.-gen., Jamaica, Sept., 1922; res. mag., Jamaica, 1923; ag. atty.-gen. in 1923 and 1924; ag. judge, Kingston et al., Nov., 1924 to Oct., 1925; ag. puisne judge, Oct., 1925; circuit judge, Ashanti and N. Territories, Gold Coast, 1926; puisne judge, Gold Coast, 1928; ag. ch. just. on various occasions, 1934-38.
YOUNG, ALFRED ERNEST, M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.), I.D.S. (Eng.).—B. 1891; med. offr., Basutoland, 1924.

YOUNG, SIR ALFRED KARNEY, KT. BACH. (1923), K.C., B.A.—Ed. Magdalen Coll., Oxon; called to the bar, In. Temp., May, 1889; 2nd clk., col. sec.'s office, Br. Honduras, Oct., 1890; ag. dist. mag., Toledo dist., Aug., 1891; priv. sec. to Sir A. Maloney, and clk. of councils, 1892; priv. sec. to admnstr. of the govt., 1895; ag. regisr.-gen., 1895-97; dist. comanr., Orange Walk, 1898; regisr.-gen., June, 1898; ag. supt. of police, May to Nov., 1899; J.P. for ool.; crown prosecutor, legal adviser, and police mag., Seychelles, Sept., 1903; atty.-gen., B.C. Africa Prot., Mar., 1906; stip. mag., Trinidad, 1908; ch. just., Seychelles, Aug., 1909; admnstd. govt., Apr. to Nov., 1911, and in Dec., 1912; atty.-gen., Fiji, May, 1914; ag. chief just., Jan., 1916 and from Mar., 1919 to May, 1920; ch. just., Leeward Is., Feb., 1921; chief ust., Fin., 1923; ret. June, 1929.

YOUNG, CAPT. SIR ARTHUR HENDERSON, G.C.M.G. (1916), K.C.M.G. (1908), K.B.E. (1919), C.M.G. (1897).—B. 1854; late 27th Inniskilling; ed. at Edin. Acad., Rugby, and Sandhurst; local comdt., mil. pol., Kyrenia, Aug., 1878; ast. comanr., Paphos, Nov., 1878; comanr., Paphos, Sept., 1879; ditto, Famagusta, Jan., 1882; dir. of survey and prin. forest offr., 1892; mem. lega. coun., 1892; ch. sec., 1894; admnstd. the govt. July, to Dec., 1895, Jan. to Apr., 1898; sent on special mission to St. Vincent in connctn. with relief work, Nov., 1902; col. sec. S. Sttlmts., June, 1906; admnstd. govt., Feb. to May, 1909; ch. sec., F.M.S., 1910; gov., S. Sttlmts., Aug. 1911; assumed govt., Sept., 1911; also high comsnr. for Malay States and for Brunei and Brit. Agt. for N. Borneo and Sarawak; ret. Nov., 1919.

YOUNG, HON. SIR FREDERICK WILLIAM, KT. BACH. (1918), LL.B.—M.H.A., S. Australia, 1899; comsnr. of crown lands and immigr., 1912-14; agt.-gen. for S. Australia, 1915-18; mem. of comittee for attitl. of ex-service men within the Empire, 1917; M.P. (Co. U.), Swindon, 1918.

YOUNG, HENRY ALFRED, K.C.—Ed. Edin. Univ.; called to the bar (Scotland), July, 1899; ast. crown advocate, E. Africa Prot., 22nd June, 1906; ast. atty.-gen., 1913; senr. res. mag., 1917; ag. atty.-gen. on various occasions; pol. mag., Nigeria, Jan., 1922; puisne and circuit judge, Sierra Leone, 1930.

YOUNG, MAJOR SIR HUBERT WINTHORP, K.C.M.G. (1934), KT. Bach. (1932), C.M.G. (1929), D.S.O. (1918).—B. 1888; ed. Eton and R. Milt. Acad., Woolwich; comand., R. Garrison Arty., 1904; Indian Army, 1908; capt., 1913; major, 1919; ast. censor, Army headqrs., Simla, July, 1914 to Feb., 1915; N.W. Frontier, Feb.-Dec., 1915(1914-15 Star); ast. polit. offr., Mesopotamia, Dec., 1915 to Aug., 1917; dep. dir., local resources, Mesopotamia, Aug., 1917 to Mar., 1918 (ment. in desps.); gen. staff offr., 2nd grade, Hejaz operations, Mar. to Oct., 1918 (ment. in desps., D.S.O., 3rd class Order of Nahda); pres. local resources bd., Damascus, Oct., 1918; seconded to F.O., Feb., 1919 to Mar., 1921; seconded to C.O., Middle East Dept. as ast. sec., Mar., 1921 to Dec., 1926; accompanied Mr. Churchoil to Cairo confere., Apr., 1921; visited Palestine and Iraq on sp. serv., Sept., 1921 to Jan., 1922; sp. serv., Lausanne for Turkish peace treaty., Jan., 1922; and at Brussels and Geneva for Iraq frontier dispute on various occasions during 1925 and 1926; ret. from army and appd. col. sec., Gibraltar, Feb., 1927; couns. to high comsnr., Iraq, Sept., 1929; ag. high comsnr., on four occasions; envoy extraordinary and min. plenipotentiary, Baghdad, Oct., 1932; gov. and commdr.-in-ch., Nyassaland Prot., 1932; do., Northern Rhodesia, Mar., 1934; do., Trinbald and Tobago, 1938.

YOUNG, HON. SIR JAMES ALEXANDER, K.C.V.O. (1935).—Mem. N.Z. parl., 1911-35; min. of health, 1926-28; min. of industry and commerce, 1928; min. of health, min. of immigr., in coalition miny., 1931-35; min. internal affrs., 1933-35.

YOUNG, JAMES ANDERSON, M.C., M.B., Ch.B. (Edin.), Certif. L.S.T.M.—B. 1889; ed. St. Andrews and Edinburgh Univ.; late house surg.; Derbyshire R. Infirmary; served with R.A.M.C. (T.F.), 1915-19; granted hon. rank of major on resignation; med. offr., Somaliland, Sept., 1919, asst. bacteriologist, Nigeria, 1927.

YOUNG, SIR MARK ATTCHISON, K.C.M.G. (1934), C.M.G. (1931).—R. 1886; ed. Eton and King's Coll., Camb. (B.A.); cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., Nov., 1909; off. asst. to govt. agt., N. Prov., Aug., 1910; ditto, Kandy, Nov., 1911; ag. asst. govt. agt., Mullaitivu, Jan., 1913; 4th asst. col. sec., Nov., 1913; ag. 3rd ditto, Feb., 1914; 2nd ditto and clk. of leg. coun., Sep., 1914; 3rd asst. sec., col., Oct., 1914; on mil. duty, 1915-1919; 3rd asst. col. sec., Jan., 1920; 2nd asst. col. sec., July, 1920; asst. govt. agt., Hambantota, Mar., 1922; prin. asst. col. sec. and clk., exec. coun., Mar., 1923; col. sec., Sierra Leone, 1928; ag. gov. in 1929 and 1930; ch. sec., Palestine, 1930; admtd. govt. for various periods, 1830-32; gov. and commdr.-in-ch., Barbados, June, 1933; do., Tanganyika Territory, 1938.

YOUNG, ROBERT MARCUS, B.A., B.A.I. (Elect.) A.R.C.S.I.—B. 1900; ed. Mountjoy Schi., and Trinity Coll., Dublin; European mast., S.S., Aug., 1924; headmast., govt. Eng. schl., Alor Star, Nov., 1929; ag. supt., edcn., Kedah, in addn., Feb. to Mar., 1933; headmast., govt. Eng. schl., K. Pilah, Mar., 1935; do., High schl., B. Mertajam, May, 1938.

YOUNG, W. A., M.A., B.M., B.CH. (Oxon.), B.Sc. (War.), Lond., M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.), D.T.M. & H. (Lond.), cert., Lond. Schi. of Trop. Med. (with distinction, 2nd place), late exhibr., Jesus Coll., Oxford.—War serv., France, 1916-18; clin. asst., children and ear depts., St. Thomas' hosp., 1922; R.M.O., Ealing hosp., 1922-23; East Africa med. serv. (Zanzibar), Sept., 1923; ag. R.S.O., Zanzibar, 1927, 1930 and 1933; M.O.H. and ag. senr. med. offr., 1935; S.M.O., Tanganyika Territory, 1936.

YOUNG, WILLIAM, M.B., Ch.B. (Glas.), certif. L.S.T.M.—B. 1892; med. offr., F.M.S., Nov., 1921; med. offr., K. Lipis, Nov., 1922; ditto, Taiping, Oct., 1924; ditto, Larut, May, 1925; ditto, B. Gajah, Mar., 1927; ditto, B. Pahang, May, 1928; ag. sr. med. offr., Pahang, Nov., 1928; med. offr., Larut, Aug., 1929; ag. ch. med. offr., soc. hyg., F.M.S., May, 1930; ag. state, med. and health offr., Pahang, Jan., 1932; ch. med. offr.; Malacca, Oct., 1935; superscale offr., grade A., Sept., 1936.
YOUNG, WILLIAM ARTHUR, B.Sc., M.B., B.S. (Lond.), M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P., D.T.M. and H.—B. 1890; prof. of bacteriology, Coll. of Medicine, Spore, Mar., 1927.

YOUNG, SIR WILLIAM DOUGLAS, K.B.E. (1919); C.M.G. (1907).—Ed. at Charterhouse; clk. in govt. sec.'s office, Br. Guiana, Jan., 1877; ch. clk., 1889; ag. priv. sec. on several occasions, 1877-1893, to govrs. of Br. Guiana, Trinidad, and G. Coast; ag. asst. gov't. sec., Br. Guiana, 1892-3-4-5; ag. gov't. sec., Dec., 1892; ag. govt. agt. N.W. dist., 1895; asst. col. sec., Mauritius, 1895; ag. col. sec., 1896-7-8, 1900-01; admtd. govt., Mauritius, Aug., 1897; comsr., Turks and Caicos Islands, 1901; admtr., Dominica, 19th Apr., 1906; ag. gov'r., Leeward Is., 1909; admtr., St. Lucia, 1913; ag. gov', Windward Is., May to Dec., 1914; govt., Falkland Is., Dec., 1914; assumed govt., May, 1915; ret., 1920.
YOUNG, WILLIAM MARTIN.—B. 1890; N. British Rlyw., 1906-20; dep. ch. acct., Ceylon govt. rlyw., 1921; ch. acct., Palestine rlyws., 1927.
YU WAN, B.A. (Oxon.).—B. 1890; inspr., vernacular schls., Hong Kong, 1922; mem., bd. of exam., 1923.

ZIAI, MUSTAFA FAUAD.—B. 1888; barrister-at-law, Gray's Inn, 1922; inspr., Cyprus mily. pol., Nov., 1906; judge, dist. ct., Apr., 1913; puisne judge, Oct., 1927; ag. ch. just., Feb.-Mar. and May-Sept., 1938.
